questionText:
Identify the type of sentence:

How efficiently the inspection team completed the verification.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Exclamatory

• 'How efficiently the inspection team completed the verification.' — ഈ വാക്യം 'How' കൊണ്ട് തുടങ്ങുന്നു, inspection team-ന്റെ efficiency-യോടുള്ള അഭിനന്ദനം/അതിശയം പ്രകടിപ്പിക്കുന്നു.
• Exclamatory sentence = വികാരം, അതിശയം, അഭിനന്ദനം പ്രകടിപ്പിക്കുന്ന വാക്യം — 'How' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'What' ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• Pattern: 'How + adverb + subject + verb' = Exclamatory — ഇതാണ് ഈ sentence-ന്റെ exact structure.
• PSC-ൽ 'How/What' കൊണ്ട് തുടങ്ങുന്ന sentences-ന്റെ type identify ചെയ്യൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — Exclamatory vs Interrogative confusion ഓർക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  How + adj/adv + Subject + Verb = Exclamatory
  What + (a/an) + adj + noun + Subject + Verb = Exclamatory
• Sentences-ന്റെ 4 types:
  - Assertive: ഒരു fact/statement — 'She works hard.'
  - Interrogative: ചോദ്യം — 'Does she work hard?'
  - Imperative: command/request — 'Work hard!'
  - Exclamatory: വികാരം/അതിശയം — 'How hard she works!'
• Interrogative vs Exclamatory: 'How did they do it?' (Interrogative — answer expect ചെയ്യുന്നു) vs 'How efficiently they did it!' (Exclamatory — emotion express ചെയ്യുന്നു, auxiliary flip ഇല്ല)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Exclamatory sentence identification — 'How/What' pattern PSC-ൽ regular ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. How beautiful this painting is!
  2. What a wonderful opportunity this is!
  3. How efficiently the team completed the task!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Assertive
• Assertive = ഒരു fact/statement plain ആയി പറയുന്ന sentence. 'How efficiently...' = emotion/admiration express ചെയ്യുന്നു, plain statement അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sentence-ൽ question mark ('?') ഇല്ലാത്തതിനാൽ statement ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, Assertive choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Imperative
• Imperative = command/request, usually verb കൊണ്ട് തുടങ്ങും — 'Go!', 'Please sit down.' 'How efficiently...' ഒരു command അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Imperative-ഉം Exclamatory-ഉം subject കാണാതിരിക്കാം, students confuse ചെയ്യും — 'How' = Exclamatory signal ആണ്.

❌ (C) Interrogative
• Interrogative = ചോദ്യം — answer expect ചെയ്യുന്ന sentence. 'How efficiently...' ഒരു ചോദ്യം അല്ല, admiration express ചെയ്യുകയാണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'How' കൊണ്ട് Questions-ഉം തുടങ്ങും ('How did you do it?') — പക്ഷേ 'How + adj/adv + S + V' (auxiliary flip ഇല്ലാതെ) = Exclamatory.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HOW + quality + subject = WOW! = Exclamatory!' — 'How beautiful!' / 'How efficiently!' = WOW feeling express ചെയ്യുന്നു = Exclamatory. WOW = Exclamatory ആണ്!
• Quick test: ചോദ്യം ആണോ? No → Not Interrogative. Command ആണോ? No → Not Imperative. 'How/What' + emotion = Yes → Exclamatory!
• PSC exam strategy: 'How + adjective/adverb + subject + verb' (auxiliary flip ഇല്ലാതെ) കണ്ടാൽ = always Exclamatory. ഉടൻ D choose ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText:
Choose the correct option:

The delegation travelled by ______ air due to the urgency of the meeting.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) no article

• 'Travel by air' — transport-ന്റെ mode indicate ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'by + transport noun'-ൽ article ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — ഇത് ഒരു fixed rule ആണ്.
• 'by air, by train, by bus, by sea, by road, by rail' — ഇവ എല്ലാം zero article ഉള്ള fixed phrases ആണ്.
• Article ചേർത്താൽ meaning മാറും: 'by the air' = standard English അല്ല — wrong idiom ആകും.
• PSC-ൽ 'by + transport' zero article rule ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — ഈ pattern ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: by + transport noun = NO article (zero article) — ഇത് fixed rule ആണ്, exceptions ഇല്ല.
• Zero article transport list:
  by air | by train | by bus | by car | by sea | by road | by rail | by boat | by plane | by taxi
• Article ഉള്ള vs ഇല്ലാത്ത comparison:
  - 'Take A bus' (ഒരു specific trip = a/an) ✓
  - 'Travel by bus' (transport mode = no article) ✓
  - 'The bus is late' (specific bus = the) ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Zero article with 'by + transport' — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• Similar zero article patterns: 'in hospital', 'at school', 'at home' — ഇവ zero article.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The delegation travelled by air to reach on time.
  2. He goes to work by train every day.
  3. They prefer travelling by sea rather than by air.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a
• 'By a air' — 'air' vowel sound കൊണ്ട് തുടങ്ങുന്നതിനാൽ 'a' doubly wrong ആണ്. ഇനി 'by + transport' = zero article, അതുകൊണ്ടും wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Blank fill ആണ്, article വേണം എന്ന് feel ആകും — 'a' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) an
• 'By an air' — 'air' vowel sound ആണ്, 'an' form correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'by air' = fixed idiom, article തന്നെ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Air' vowel sound → 'an air' correct feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ 'by + transport' = zero article rule override ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) the
• 'By the air' — 'the' ഉപയോഗിച്ചാൽ specific/definite ആകും, ഇത് standard English അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The' = definite, formal sound ആകും — correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BY + transport = BARE! No clothes (article) needed!' — 'by air, by train, by bus' — all BARE, no a/an/the!
• Transport list: by air · by train · by bus · by car · by sea · by road · by rail — ഇവ memorize ചെയ്യൂ.
• PSC exam strategy: 'by ___' + transport word കണ്ടാൽ = 'no article' option choose ചെയ്യൂ. Vowel ആണോ consonant ആണോ എന്ന് നോക്കേണ്ടതില്ല — always zero article!

questionText:
Choose the correct verb form:

Neither the training module nor the supplementary material ______ available on the portal.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) is

• 'Neither the training module NOR the supplementary material _____ available' — 'Neither...nor' correlative conjunction ആണ്.
• 'Neither...nor' rule: verb, NOR-ന്റെ ശേഷം വരുന്ന subject-നനുസരിച്ച് (nearest subject rule) agree ചെയ്യണം.
• NOR-ന്റെ ശേഷം 'the supplementary material' = singular noun → verb = 'is' (singular).
• ഈ rule PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ചോദിക്കുന്ന subject-verb agreement rule ആണ് — ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Correlative conjunctions (nearest subject rule):
  Neither A nor B → verb agrees with B
  Either A or B → verb agrees with B
  Not only A but also B → verb agrees with B
• ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
  - Neither the boys nor the girl IS here. (girl = singular → is) ✓
  - Neither the girl nor the boys ARE here. (boys = plural → are) ✓
  - Neither the module nor the material IS available. (material = singular → is) ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Neither...nor' subject-verb agreement — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും popular agreement question.
• Other agreement rules:
  - A and B = plural verb
  - Each/Every/Either/Neither alone + singular noun = singular verb
  - 'A as well as B' = verb agrees with A (first subject)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Neither he nor his friends were present.
  2. Neither the students nor the teacher was ready.
  3. Either the manager or the staff members are responsible.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) are
• 'Are' = plural verb. NOR-ന്റെ ശേഷം 'supplementary material' = singular → 'is' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: രണ്ട് subjects ഉണ്ട് (module + material) → plural ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, 'are' choose ചെയ്യും. Neither...nor = nearest subject rule ഓർക്കൂ!

❌ (C) were
• 'Were' = past plural — tense wrong (sentence present tense ആണ്) + number wrong (singular needed).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Are'-ന്റെ past form ആണ്, 'are' choose ചെയ്ത് past form-ലേക്ക് shift ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) have been
• 'Have been' = Present Perfect plural — number-ഉം (plural) tense-ഉം (perfect) — രണ്ടും wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have been available' = formal/complete sound ആകും, educated choice ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Neither...NOR → NOR-ന്റെ NEIGHBOR (nearest subject) wins!' — NOR-ന്റെ ശേഷം വരുന്ന word = verb decide ചെയ്യുന്ന word.
• Short cut: 'Neither X nor' cover ചെയ്യൂ. ബാക്കി: 'The supplementary material _____ available' = singular = IS.
• PSC exam strategy: Neither...nor / Either...or question കണ്ടാൽ — 'NOR/OR-ന്റെ ശേഷം' ഉള്ള subject ആണ് verb decide ചെയ്യുന്നത്. Singular → is/was, Plural → are/were.

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag:

Let us reconsider the proposal, ______ ?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) shall we

• 'Let us reconsider the proposal, _____?' — 'Let us' = suggestion/invitation ആണ്.
• 'Let us' (Let's)-ന്റെ question tag = always 'shall we?' — ഇത് ഒരു fixed rule ആണ്, exceptions ഇല്ല.
• PSC-ൽ 'Let's' question tag ഒരു common trick question ആണ് — 'shall we?' ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Let's / Let us → shall we? (always! fixed rule!)
• Normal question tag rules:
  - Positive statement → Negative tag: 'She is smart, isn't she?'
  - Negative statement → Positive tag: 'He isn't here, is he?'
• Special cases (PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും):
  - Let's → shall we?
  - Imperative (Go!) → will you? / won't you?
  - I am... → aren't I?
  - Nobody/Nothing → are they? / is it?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common grammar topic — special cases frequent ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Let's play cricket, shall we? ✓
  2. Let's have dinner, shall we? ✓
  3. Let's go home, shall we? ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) will you
• 'Will you?' = imperative sentences-ന്റെ tag — 'Close the door, will you?' ഇതുപോലെ.
• 'Let us' = suggestion, imperative അല്ല → 'shall we?' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will' future-related ആണ്, proposal reconsider = future action ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (C) do we
• 'Do we?' = present simple auxiliary — question tags-ൽ 'do' ഇങ്ങനെ standalone ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Let's'-ന് 'do we?' ഒരിക്കലും ശരിയല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'We' subject ആണ് → 'do we?' feel ആകും — Let's = shall we? fixed rule ഓർക്കൂ.

❌ (D) don't we
• 'Don't we?' = negative form — 'Let us' = positive statement, normal rule apply ചെയ്താൽ negative tag feel ആകും, പക്ഷേ Let's special exception ആണ് = 'shall we?'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Positive → negative tag normal rule — 'don't we?' logical feel ആകും, special exception ignore ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LET'S = SHALL WE go together? LET'S ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ SHALL WE ഉണ്ട്!' — Let's = together suggest ചെയ്യൽ = shall WE? Always WE!
• PSC Special Tag Cheat Sheet (ഈ 4 ഓർക്കൂ): Let's → shall we? | Go! (imperative) → will you? | I am → aren't I? | Nobody → are they?
• PSC exam strategy: 'Let's' / 'Let us' കണ്ടാൽ — 'shall we?' ഉള്ള option ഉടൻ choose ചെയ്യൂ. 3 seconds-ൽ answer!

questionText: Identify the correct sentence:
optionA: The director had the assistant to prepare the summary.
optionB: The director had the assistant prepare the summary.
optionC: The director had the assistant prepared the summary.
optionD: The director had the assistant preparing the summary.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The director had the assistant prepare the summary.

• 'Have + object + bare infinitive (V1)' = causative structure — ഒരാൾ മറ്റൊരാളെക്കൊണ്ട് ഒരു കാര്യം ചെയ്യിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Had the assistant prepare' = director assistant-നെ ഉപയോഗിച്ച് summary prepare ചെയ്യിച്ചു — ഇതാണ് ശരിയായ causative structure.
• 'Prepare' = bare infinitive (to ഇല്ലാതെ) — have/had-ന്റെ ശേഷം bare infinitive ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.
• PSC-ൽ causative 'have' structure ഒരു important correction question ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: have/had + object + bare infinitive (V1) = Causative structure
• Causative verbs comparison:
  - HAVE + object + bare infinitive: 'I had him fix it.' (neutral)
  - GET + object + to-infinitive: 'I got him to fix it.' (effort involved)
  - MAKE + object + bare infinitive: 'I made him fix it.' (force/compulsion)
• Common errors:
  - 'had the assistant TO prepare' → WRONG ('to' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല have-ന് ശേഷം)
  - 'had the assistant prepared' → WRONG (past participle ആകില്ല — passive sense മാത്രം)
  - 'had the assistant preparing' → WRONG (-ing form ആകില്ല)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Causative 'have' structure correction questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She had her secretary book the tickets. ✓
  2. He had the plumber fix the leaking pipe. ✓
  3. The teacher had the students write an essay. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) had the assistant TO prepare
• 'To prepare' = to-infinitive — 'have' causative-ന്റെ ശേഷം to-infinitive ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Get'-ന്റെ ശേഷം to-infinitive: 'got him to prepare' ✓. 'Have'-ന്റെ ശേഷം bare infinitive: 'had him prepare' ✓.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'to prepare' = standard infinitive form — grammatically correct feel ആകും.

❌ (C) had the assistant PREPARED
• 'Prepared' = past participle — causative 'have'-ൽ object-ന്റെ ശേഷം past participle ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് passive sense-ൽ ആണ് ('I had my car repaired'). ഇവിടെ assistant ആണ് prepare ചെയ്യുന്നത് (active), passive structure wrong ആകും.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'had prepared' = Past Perfect feel ആകും, grammatically correct sound ആകും.

❌ (D) had the assistant PREPARING
• 'Preparing' = present participle — causative 'have'-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും -ing form ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Continuous form = action ongoing ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, correct ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAVE = BARE! Have causative-ൽ verb BARE ആയി നിൽക്കും — to ഇല്ല, -ed ഇല്ല, -ing ഇല്ല!' — 'had him prepare' (bare) ✓
• Causative family: HAVE + bare | GET + to | MAKE + bare — HAVE-ഉം MAKE-ഉം bare, GET-ന് മാത്രം 'to'.
• PSC exam strategy: 'had the assistant ___' കണ്ടാൽ → bare infinitive option choose ചെയ്യൂ. 'to prepare' = GET-ന്, 'have'-ന് bare 'prepare' ആണ്.

questionText: Ravi ______ tea every morning.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) drinks

• 'Ravi _____ tea EVERY MORNING' — 'every morning' = signal word for Simple Present tense (habitual/routine action).
• ഓരോ ദിവസവും ചെയ്യുന്ന routine ആണ്, ഇപ്പോൾ ഈ moment-ൽ നടക്കുന്ന action അല്ല.
• Ravi = 3rd person singular → verb-ൽ 's' ചേർക്കണം: 'drink' → 'drinks'.
• PSC-ൽ signal word-നനുസരിച്ച് tense identify ചെയ്യൽ ഏറ്റവും popular tense question ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Signal word ആദ്യം നോക്കൂ → Tense decide ചെയ്യൂ!
• Tense signal words (PSC must-know list):
  - Every day/morning/week → Simple Present
  - Yesterday/ago/last → Simple Past
  - Tomorrow/next week → Simple Future
  - Now/at this moment/currently → Present Continuous
  - Already/just/ever/never/yet → Present Perfect
  - Since/for (ongoing) → Present Perfect Continuous
• Simple Present formula: He/She/It/Ravi = verb + s/es | I/We/They = verb
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Signal word → Tense identification PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common tense question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She reads the newspaper every morning. ✓
  2. He drinks coffee every day. ✓
  3. They play cricket every Sunday. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) is drinking
• 'Is drinking' = Present Continuous — ഇപ്പോൾ ഈ moment-ൽ നടക്കുന്ന action-ന്. 'Every morning' = habitual action → Simple Present.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ഇപ്പോൾ tea കുടിക്കുന്നു' = continuous feel ആകും, 'every morning' signal word ignore ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) drank
• 'Drank' = Simple Past — കഴിഞ്ഞ ഒരു specific time-ൽ ചെയ്ത action-ന്. 'Every morning' = present habit, past action അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ravi' = known person, story context = past ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (D) has drunk
• 'Has drunk' = Present Perfect — ഭൂതകാലത്തിൽ complete ആയ action. 'Every morning' = routine, Present Perfect signal words ഒന്നും ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has drunk' = formal/correct sound ആകും, 3rd person singular 'has' ശരി feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EVERY = EVERYDAY habit = Simple Present!' — 'Every morning, every day, every week' = routine = Simple Present. ഈ equation ഓർക്കൂ: EVERY = ROUTINE = SIMPLE PRESENT.
• Signal word family: every day/week/morning → Simple Present | now/currently → Present Continuous | yesterday/ago → Simple Past.
• PSC exam strategy: Blank fill question-ൽ ആദ്യം signal word identify ചെയ്യൂ. 'Every morning' കണ്ടാൽ = Simple Present = 'drinks' (3rd person singular, 's' ഉള്ളത്).

questionText: The plant shed its leaves. (Identify the Possessive Pronoun)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) its

• 'The plant shed ITS leaves' — 'its' = possessive pronoun/adjective — plant-ന്റേതായ (belonging to the plant) leaves.
• 'Its' = possessive form: 'it'-ന്റെ possession indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'its leaves' = plant-ന്റെ ഇലകൾ.
• Question-ൽ Possessive Pronoun identify ചെയ്യാൻ ആവശ്യപ്പെടുന്നു — 'its' = ഇതാണ് sentence-ലെ possessive word.
• 'Its' (possessive) vs 'it's' (it is) = different meaning — ഈ confusion PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Possessive Pronoun = ownership/belonging indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന word
• Possessive pronouns family:
  I → my/mine | You → your/yours | He → his
  She → her/hers | It → its | We → our/ours | They → their/theirs
• Possessive Adjective vs Possessive Pronoun:
  - Possessive Adjective: noun-ന് മുൻപ് വരും: 'its leaves', 'my book'
  - Possessive Pronoun: alone നിൽക്കും: 'The book is mine', 'The leaves are its'
• Important: 'its' (possessive, no apostrophe) ≠ 'it's' (it is/has, apostrophe)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of speech identification — possessive pronouns/adjectives ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The dog wagged its tail. ✓
  2. The company lost its reputation. ✓
  3. The tree shed its leaves in autumn. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) plant
• 'Plant' = Noun (subject of the sentence) — possessive pronoun അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Plant' = main character of the sentence, important word ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (B) shed
• 'Shed' = Verb (past tense) — action word ആണ്, possessive pronoun അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Shed' = unusual word, notice ചെയ്ത് choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) The
• 'The' = Definite Article — possessive pronoun അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The' = common, important word ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ITS = IT'S property! — its leaves = plant-ന്റെ property. Possession = ITS!'
• 'Its' vs 'it's' confusion: 'Its' = belonging to it (no apostrophe). 'It's' = it is (apostrophe = short form). PSC-ൽ ഈ confusion trap ആകി വരും.
• PSC exam strategy: Possessive pronoun identify ചെയ്യൽ question-ൽ — ownership/belonging indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ് answer. 'its, his, her, their, our, your' = possessive pronouns/adjectives.

questionText: Keerthana won't be late, ______? (Use suitable question tag)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) will she

• 'Keerthana won't be late, ______?' — Negative statement ആണ് → Positive question tag വേണം.
• 'Won't be' = negative (won't = will not) → positive tag = 'will she?'
• Subject = 'Keerthana' = feminine singular → pronoun = 'she'.
• Auxiliary verb 'won't' (will not) → tag-ൽ positive form 'will' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Positive statement → Negative tag (-n't)
  Negative statement → Positive tag (no -n't)
• Question tag formula: auxiliary verb + pronoun
  - Subject pronoun identify ചെയ്യൂ (Keerthana → she)
  - Auxiliary verb identify ചെയ്യൂ (won't → will)
  - Negative → Positive: 'will she?'
• Common auxiliary verbs in tags:
  is/are/was/were | will/would | can/could | do/does/did | has/have/had
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequent grammar topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She won't come, will she? ✓
  2. He isn't ready, is he? ✓
  3. They didn't inform, did they? ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) will not she
• Question tag-ൽ contracted form (won't) ഉപയോഗിക്കണം — 'will not she' = uncontracted, wrong form.
• Standard English tag = 'won't', 'isn't', 'didn't' — full form question tag-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Won't = will not' expansion ആണ്, full form ശരി ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (C) would she
• 'Would' = conditional/past modal — 'won't' = 'will not' (future/general), 'would' ആകില്ല.
• Tag-ൽ statement-ൽ ഉള്ള same auxiliary-ന്റെ form ഉപയോഗിക്കണം: won't → will.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Won't' → 'would' feel ആകും, polite form ആണ് correct ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും.

❌ (D) wouldn't she
• 'Wouldn't she' = negative tag — statement negative ആണ്, tag positive ആകണം (Negative → Positive rule).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Won't' → 'wouldn't' similar sound, negative → negative feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'NEGATIVE → POSITIVE! POSITIVE → NEGATIVE! — Seesaw rule!' — Statement negative, tag positive. Seesaw-ൽ ഒരു side down → other side up!
• Two-step process: (1) Statement negative/positive ആണോ? → Tag opposite. (2) Subject = pronoun (Keerthana=she). → 'will she?'
• PSC exam strategy: 'Won't' കണ്ടാൽ = negative → positive tag = 'will'. Subject 'Keerthana' = she. Answer = 'will she?'. 5 seconds!

questionText: Now we shall discuss ______ our future prospects.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) no preposition

• 'Now we shall discuss ______ our future prospects.' — 'discuss' = transitive verb, direct object directly take ചെയ്യുന്ന verb ആണ്.
• 'Discuss' = preposition ഉപയോഗിക്കാതെ directly object take ചെയ്യും: 'discuss our prospects' (correct), 'discuss ABOUT our prospects' (wrong!).
• 'Discuss about' = common Indian English error — standard English-ൽ 'discuss' + object (no preposition).
• PSC-ൽ 'discuss about' error ഒരു popular correction question ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: DISCUSS = transitive verb → NO preposition needed, object directly follows!
• Verbs that do NOT take prepositions before their objects:
  - Discuss (not 'discuss about')
  - Describe (not 'describe about')
  - Reach (not 'reach to')
  - Enter (not 'enter into')
  - Emphasize (not 'emphasize on')
  - Mention (not 'mention about')
  - Resemble (not 'resemble with')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Discuss about' / 'reach to' errors PSC-ൽ popular correction questions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. We discussed our plans. ✓ (NOT: discussed about)
  2. They reached the station on time. ✓ (NOT: reached to)
  3. She entered the room quietly. ✓ (NOT: entered into)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) about
• 'Discuss about' = wrong — 'discuss' directly object take ചെയ്യും, 'about' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Talk ABOUT' = correct (talk is intransitive). 'Discuss' = transitive, 'about' wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Talk about', 'speak about', 'think about' patterns familiar → 'discuss about' correct ആണ് feel ആകും. ഏറ്റവും common Indian English error ആണ് ഇത്!

❌ (B) of
• 'Discuss of' = incorrect — 'discuss'-ന് 'of' preposition ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Think of', 'speak of' patterns → 'discuss of' feel ആകും.

❌ (C) for
• 'Discuss for' = incorrect — 'discuss'-ന് 'for' preposition ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Prepare for', 'look for' patterns → 'discuss for' feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DISCUSS is DIRECT! — No middleman (preposition) needed!' — Discuss directly talks to its object. 'Discuss our plans' — Direct! No 'about', no 'of'!
• Zero preposition verbs list (PSC must-know): discuss · mention · describe · reach · enter · emphasize · resemble.
• PSC exam strategy: 'discuss' verb കണ്ടാൽ → 'no preposition' option ഉടൻ check ചെയ്യൂ. 'Discuss about' = always wrong in standard English. Indian English trap ആണ്!

questionText: We are going to bake the bread. (Change into Passive Voice)
optionA: The bread is going to be baked.
optionB: The bread was going to be baked.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) The bread is going to be baked.

• Active: 'We are going to bake the bread.' — 'be going to' = future intention express ചെയ്യുന്ന structure.
• Active → Passive transformation:
  Active: We ARE GOING TO BAKE the bread.
  Passive: The bread IS GOING TO BE BAKED.
• 'be going to' passive formula: is/am/are + going to + be + past participle (V3).
• 'Baked' = past participle of 'bake'. 'The bread' = passive subject.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active tense = Passive tense (ALWAYS same tense!)
  be going to (active) → be going to be + V3 (passive)
• 'be going to' passive formation:
  - Active: Subject + am/is/are + going to + V1 + Object
  - Passive: Object + am/is/are + going to + be + V3
• Passive voice general rules:
  - Active object → Passive subject
  - Active subject → drops or becomes 'by + agent'
  - Tense preserved: Active tense = Passive tense
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Active-Passive transformation PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. We are going to build a school. → A school is going to be built. ✓
  2. She is going to write the report. → The report is going to be written. ✓
  3. They are going to announce the results. → The results are going to be announced. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) The bread WAS going to be baked.
• 'Was going to' = past tense — Active 'are going to' = present tense. Tense mismatch ആണ്.
• Active tense = Passive tense rule: 'are going to' (present) → passive = 'is going to be' (present), 'was' (past) ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'baked' = past form → 'was' = past, ഒരുമിച്ചു ചേരും feel ആകും.

❌ (C) The bread HAS BEEN baked.
• 'Has been baked' = Present Perfect Passive — Active 'are going to' = future intention, Present Perfect ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has been baked' = common passive structure, correct sound ആകും.

❌ (D) The bread WILL BE baked.
• 'Will be baked' = Simple Future Passive — 'be going to' = intention/plan, 'will' = prediction. Meaning different ആണ്. 'Are going to' → passive = 'is going to be', 'will' ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will be baked' = future passive, correct sound ആകും — 'be going to' structure preserve ചെയ്യണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GOING TO stays! — Passive-ൽ going to remove ചെയ്യരുത്! Be + going to + BE + V3!' — 'are going to bake' → 'is going to BE baked'. 'going to' stays, just add 'be' + V3!
• Active-Passive formula: 'be going to + V1' → 'be going to + BE + V3'. Tense same, add 'be' before V3.
• PSC exam strategy: Active-ൽ 'be going to' കണ്ടാൽ — Passive-ൽ 'going to be + V3' ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യൂ. Tense change check ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText:
Pick out the antonym of the underlined word

The rules and regulations were __strictly__ enforced.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) lightly

• 'The rules and regulations were strictly enforced.' — 'strictly' = കർശനമായി, ശക്തമായി.
• Antonym = opposite meaning: 'strictly' ↔ 'lightly' = ലാഘവത്തോടെ, ശക്തിയില്ലാതെ.
• 'Strictly enforced' = rules കർശനമായി നടപ്പാക്കി. 'Lightly enforced' = rules ലാഘവത്തോടെ/അലക്ഷ്യമായി നടപ്പാക്കി.
• PSC-ൽ antonym questions-ൽ synonyms-നെ options-ആയി ഇടും — ഈ trap ഓർക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Antonym = OPPOSITE meaning — strictly (കർശനമായി) → lightly (ലാഘവത്തോടെ)
• 'Strictly' word family:
  - Strictly (adverb) → Strict (adjective) → Strictness (noun)
  - Synonyms: firmly, rigidly, harshly, severely, sternly
  - Antonyms: lightly, loosely, mildly, gently, leniently
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms/Antonyms PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common vocabulary questions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The law was strictly enforced. (Antonym: The law was lightly enforced.) ✓
  2. She spoke strictly to the students. ✓
  3. The rules were applied lightly in practice. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) harshly
• 'Harshly' = കഠിനമായി — ഇത് 'strictly'-ന്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Antonym question-ൽ synonyms ഇടും — ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common PSC trap!

❌ (B) firmly
• 'Firmly' = ഉറപ്പോടെ — ഇതും 'strictly'-ന്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Firmly' sounds opposite-ish, പക്ഷേ actually similar meaning ആണ്.

❌ (C) rigidly
• 'Rigidly' = കർക്കശമായി — ഇതും SYNONYM ആണ്. 'Rigidly' = 'strictly' = same meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 3 synonyms + 1 antonym — students synonyms-ൽ confuse ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'STRICTLY = SERIOUSLY heavy. LIGHTLY = light as a feather. Heavy ↔ Light = Strictly ↔ Lightly!' — ഭാരമേറിയ enforcement ↔ ഭാരം കുറഞ്ഞ enforcement.
• PSC Antonym Trap Alert: ഈ question-ൽ 3 options (A, B, C) = synonyms ആണ്! PSC ഇങ്ങനെ ചെയ്യും — ഒരു correct antonym + 3 synonyms. Always find the OPPOSITE.
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ question word-ന്റെ meaning ആദ്യം: 'strictly' = കർശനമായി → opposite = ലാഘവത്തോടെ = 'lightly'. Synonyms eliminate ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText:
Fill up using the appropriate word from the brackets

A _______ of cattle lying under the tree

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) herd

• 'A _____ of CATTLE lying in the meadow' — cattle-ന്റെ collective noun = 'herd'.
• 'A herd of cattle' = cattle-ന്റെ ഒരു കൂട്ടം — ഇത് ഒരു fixed English usage ആണ്.
• PSC-ൽ animal collective nouns ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'cattle = herd' ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ easy mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Collective Noun + of + Animal = specific group name
• Animals-ന്റെ collective nouns (PSC important list):
  - Cattle/Elephants/Horses → a herd
  - Sheep/Birds → a flock
  - Lions → a pride
  - Wolves/Dogs → a pack
  - Fish → a school/shoal
  - Bees → a swarm
  - Ships → a fleet
  - Ants → a colony
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A herd of cattle grazed in the field. ✓
  2. A flock of birds flew over the lake. ✓
  3. A pride of lions rested under the tree. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) flock
• 'Flock' = sheep, birds-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം — cattle-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flock' = animals-ന്റെ general group feel ആകും, cattle-ന് apply ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) fleet
• 'Fleet' = ships/vehicles-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം — animals-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Fleet' sounds formal/correct, uncommon word ആണ്, correct ആണ് feel ആകും.

❌ (D) swarm
• 'Swarm' = bees/insects-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം — cattle-ന് ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Swarm' = large group feel ആകും, cattle = large group ആണ് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CATTLE HEARD together in a HERD! — Cattle = Herd, sheep = Flock, bees = Swarm, ships = Fleet!' — Each animal has its specific group name.
• PSC Collective Noun family (ഈ 5 ഓർക്കൂ): Cattle=Herd | Sheep/Birds=Flock | Lions=Pride | Wolves=Pack | Bees=Swarm.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Cattle' കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ 'herd' — ഒരിക്കലും flock/fleet/swarm അല്ല!

questionText:
Fill up the blank with the correct phrasal verb.

The meeting was _______ due to the Manager's illness.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) put off

• 'The meeting was _______ due to the Manager's illness' — meeting postpone ചെയ്തു = 'put off'.
• 'Put off' = postpone ചെയ്യുക, later-ലേക്ക് മാറ്റുക — 'meeting was put off' = meeting postpone ചെയ്തു.
• Manager-ന്റെ illness = reason, meeting later date-ലേക്ക് move ചെയ്തു.
• PSC-ൽ phrasal verbs-ന്റെ meaning ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'put off = postpone' ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ easy mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: PUT + preposition = completely different meaning!
• PUT family phrasal verbs (PSC must-know):
  - Put off = postpone ചെയ്യുക (meeting put off = postponed)
  - Put on = wear ചെയ്യുക / start ചെയ്യുക ('put on a coat')
  - Put up = accommodate ചെയ്യുക / tolerate ചെയ്യുക ('put up with nonsense')
  - Put out = extinguish ചെയ്യുക ('put out the fire')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും. 'Put off' = postpone — very common.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The match was put off due to rain. ✓
  2. Don't put off your work until tomorrow. ✓
  3. The meeting was put off to next week. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) put on
• 'Put on' = wear ചെയ്യുക (clothes), start ചെയ്യുക — meeting 'put on' ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put on' = start/begin feel — meeting start ചെയ്തു? No, postpone ചെയ്തു.

❌ (C) put up
• 'Put up' = accommodate ചെയ്യുക, tolerate ചെയ്യുക — meeting postpone-ന്റെ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put up' sounds like 'fix/arrange' — meeting arrange ചെയ്തു feel ആകും.

❌ (D) put out
• 'Put out' = extinguish ചെയ്യുക (fire), inconvenience ചെയ്യുക — meeting cancel/extinguish feel ആകും, പക്ഷേ 'postpone' = 'put off' ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put out' = cancel feel ആകും, postpone-ന്റെ near meaning ആണ് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PUT OFF = കൊടുക്കുന്നത് OFF ചെയ്യുക = postpone! Meeting OFF ചെയ്തു = Put off!' — OFF = cancel/postpone. Put OFF = postpone.
• PUT family cheat sheet: put OFF=postpone | put ON=wear | put UP=tolerate | put OUT=extinguish — ഈ 4 ഓർക്കൂ.
• PSC exam strategy: 'postpone/delay' meaning ഉള്ള context കണ്ടാൽ = 'put off'. 'put on/up/out' = completely different meanings.

questionText:
Replace the word underlined with its synonym.

Boby's success was the result of __perseverance__

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) persistence

• 'Boby's success was the result of PERSEVERANCE.' — 'Perseverance' = ക്ഷമയോടെ, give up ചെയ്യാതെ ഒരു കാര്യം തുടർന്നുകൊണ്ടിരിക്കൽ.
• Synonym = same/similar meaning word: 'Perseverance' ≈ 'Persistence' = തുടർച്ചയായ effort, give up ചെയ്യാതെ ഉള്ള dedication.
• 'Perseverance' = 'Persistence' = same core meaning: continuous effort despite difficulties.
• PSC-ൽ synonym questions-ൽ similar-sounding words trap ആയി വരും — meaning-based choice ചെയ്യൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Synonym = SAME meaning word — Perseverance = Persistence = dedication without giving up
• 'Perseverance' word family:
  - Perseverance (noun) → Persevere (verb) → Perseverant (adjective)
  - Synonyms: persistence, determination, tenacity, diligence, steadfastness
  - Antonyms: laziness, giving up, inconsistency, fickleness
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും. Abstract nouns — perseverance, persistence — popular choices.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Her perseverance helped her achieve her goals. ✓
  2. Success requires persistence and hard work. ✓
  3. Boby's persistence led to his remarkable success. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) intelligence
• 'Intelligence' = ബുദ്ധി, smart ആയിരിക്കൽ — 'perseverance' = effort/dedication ആണ്, intelligence അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Success = intelligence result ആണ് general feeling — 'intelligence' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) talent
• 'Talent' = natural ability, gift — 'perseverance' = hard work/effort, natural talent അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Success = talent result ആണ് common belief — 'talent' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) luck
• 'Luck' = ഭാഗ്യം, chance — 'perseverance' = deliberate, consistent effort, luck അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Success = luck' common feeling — 'luck' choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PERSEVERANCE = PERSIST + ANCE = persistence! — Both words have the same root PERSIS (to continue). Per + sist = through + stand = stand through difficulties!' ഒരേ root, ഒരേ meaning.
• Etymology: Latin 'perseverare' = to abide by strictly. 'Persistence' = Latin 'persistere' = to persist. Both = 'continue despite difficulties'.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Perseverance' = 'not giving up despite difficulties' → synonym = word with same meaning. 'Persistence' = same. Intelligence/talent/luck = different concepts.

questionText: Pick out the correct prefix to get the opposite of the word __manage__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) mis

• 'Pick out the correct prefix to get the opposite of manage' — 'mismanage' = badly manage ചെയ്യുക, incorrectly manage ചെയ്യുക.
• Prefix 'mis-' = wrongly, badly — 'mis' + 'manage' = 'mismanage' = poorly/incorrectly manage ചെയ്യുക.
• 'Mis-' prefix = wrong/bad direction ആണ് — manage-ന്റെ opposite direction ആണ്.
• PSC-ൽ prefix questions ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'mis-' = wrongly/badly ഓർക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: mis- = wrongly/badly + verb = doing it incorrectly
• 'mis-' prefix words (PSC important list):
  mis + manage = mismanage (badly manage)
  mis + understand = misunderstand (wrongly understand)
  mis + behave = misbehave (badly behave)
  mis + spell = misspell (wrongly spell)
  mis + treat = mistreat (badly treat)
  mis + lead = mislead (wrongly lead)
• Other prefix comparison:
  - un- = not (unhappy, unkind, unable)
  - dis- = not/opposite (disagree, dishonest, disappear)
  - non- = not (non-fiction, non-stop)
  - mis- = wrongly/badly (mismanage, misspell)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Prefix questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He mismanaged the company's funds. ✓
  2. She misunderstood the instructions. ✓
  3. The teacher often misspells this word. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) un
• 'Unmanage' = standard English word അല്ല — 'un-' + 'manage' common usage ഇല്ല.
• 'Un-' = adjectives-ഉം some verbs-ഉം negative ആക്കും: unhappy, unkind, unable.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Un-' = common negative prefix → 'unmanage' correct feel ആകും.

❌ (C) dis
• 'Dismanage' = standard English word അല്ല — 'dis-' + 'manage' combination ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Dis-' = common prefix: disagree, dishonest, disorganize — പക്ഷേ 'dismanage' ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Dis-' = very common negative prefix → 'dismanage' correct feel ആകും.

❌ (D) non
• 'Nonmanage' = standard English word അല്ല — 'non-' + 'manage' combination ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Non-' = nouns-നൊപ്പം: non-fiction, non-stop, non-member.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Non-' = not prefix → 'nonmanage' correct feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MIS = MISTAKE! — Mismanage = managing with a MISTAKE. mis- prefix = mistake/wrong way!' — Mis + manage = manage-ൽ mistake = mismanage.
• Prefix family cheat sheet: mis=wrongly | un=not (adj) | dis=not/opposite | non=not (noun).
• PSC exam strategy: 'manage-ന്റെ opposite' ചോദിക്കുമ്പോൾ — 'mismanage' = real English word. 'unmanage', 'dismanage', 'nonmanage' = not real words. Real word = correct answer!

questionText: What is the meaning of "Bona fide"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) In good faith / genuine

• 'Bona fide' = Latin phrase — 'bona' (good) + 'fide' (faith) = 'in good faith' / genuine ആണ്.
• 'Bona fide' = genuinely, authentically, without deception — 'ആത്മാർഥമായ, യഥാർഥ'.
• ഉദാഹരണം: 'a bona fide student' = a genuine/real student, not a fake.
• PSC-ൽ Latin/foreign phrases ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'bona fide = genuine' ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Bona fide = Latin = 'good faith' = genuine/authentic
• Common Latin phrases (PSC important list):
  - Bona fide = genuine, in good faith
  - Viva voce = oral examination
  - Vice versa = the other way around
  - Status quo = existing state of affairs
  - Post mortem = after death (examination)
  - Alma mater = educational institution one attended
  - Per capita = per person
  - De facto = in reality/practice
  - Ad hoc = for a specific purpose only
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin/foreign phrases PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും. 'Bona fide' popular question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is a bona fide expert in the field. ✓
  2. This is a bona fide offer, not a scam. ✓
  3. We need bona fide documents for the application. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Illegal
• 'Bona fide' = genuine/legal — 'Illegal' = law-ന് എതിരായ. Exact opposite meaning ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Latin word = confusing, 'illegal' = law-related feel → choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) Temporary
• 'Temporary' = ക്ഷണിക, short-term — 'bona fide' = genuine/authentic, temporary feel ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Latin word = formal/legal feel → 'temporary arrangement' feel ആകും.

❌ (D) Immediate
• 'Immediate' = ഉടനടി, without delay — 'bona fide' = genuine, urgency/time ആണ് meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Latin phrase = official/urgent context feel → 'immediate' choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BONA FIDE = BONE of FIDELITY = genuine loyalty/faithfulness!' — 'Fide' = fidelity (loyalty/faith). BONA = good. BONA FIDE = good faith = genuine!
• Latin phrase family (ഈ 5 ഓർക്കൂ): Bona fide=genuine | Viva voce=oral exam | Vice versa=other way | Post mortem=after death | Per capita=per person.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Bona fide' കണ്ടാൽ = 'genuine/in good faith'. Latin phrases-ൽ etymology clue ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ use ചെയ്യൂ — 'fide' = faith, always!

questionText: What is the correct expansion of "LASER"?
optionA: Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission Radiation
optionB: Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation
optionC: Light Amplified by Stimulated Emitted Radiation
optionD: Light Amplification through Stimulated Emission of Rays

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation

• LASER = Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation — ഇതാണ് correct full form.
• L=Light, A=Amplification, S=Stimulated, E=Emission, R=Radiation — ഓരോ letter-ഉം specific word represent ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'of Radiation' = option B-ൽ ഉണ്ട്, option A-ൽ ഇല്ല (Emission Radiation = wrong, 'of' missing).
• PSC-ൽ common abbreviations ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — LASER exact expansion ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: LASER = L+A+S+E+R — Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation
• Common Science/Technology abbreviations (PSC important):
  - LASER = Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation
  - RADAR = Radio Detection And Ranging
  - SONAR = Sound Navigation And Ranging
  - LED = Light Emitting Diode
  - CPU = Central Processing Unit
  - GPS = Global Positioning System
  - AI = Artificial Intelligence
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Abbreviations PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും. LASER popular question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The surgeon used a laser to perform the operation. ✓
  2. LASER technology is used in various industries. ✓
  3. RADAR and SONAR are both detection technologies. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission Radiation
• 'of' missing ആണ് — 'Emission OF Radiation' ആണ് correct, 'Emission Radiation' ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: A-ഉം B-ഉം almost same ആണ് — tiny difference ('of') notice ചെയ്യാതിരിക്കും.

❌ (C) Light Amplified by Stimulated Emitted Radiation
• 'Amplified' (past participle) = wrong, 'Amplification' (noun) ആണ് correct.
• 'Emitted' = wrong, 'Emission' (noun) ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Verb forms sound similar, noun/verb distinction notice ചെയ്യാതിരിക്കും.

❌ (D) Light Amplification through Stimulated Emission of Rays
• 'through' = wrong, 'by' ആണ് correct. 'Rays' = wrong, 'Radiation' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Through' = 'by' similar preposition feel, 'Rays' = 'Radiation' similar concept feel.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'L-A-S-E-R: Light A Slow Emission of Radiance — No! Just: LIGHT AMPLIFICATION STIMULATED EMISSION RADIATION!' — L=Light, A=Amplification, S=Stimulated, E=Emission, R=Radiation. Read it as a sentence!
• Key distinction: Option A vs B — 'of' ഉണ്ടോ? 'Emission OF Radiation' (B=correct) vs 'Emission Radiation' (A=wrong). Always check prepositions!
• PSC exam strategy: Abbreviation questions-ൽ options carefully compare ചെയ്യൂ. 'LASER = by Stimulated Emission OF Radiation' — 'of' = must remember!

questionText: What is the one-word substitute for "a person who believes in the doctrine of pleasure as the chief good of life"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Hedonist

• 'A person who believes in the doctrine of PLEASURE as the chief good' = Hedonist.
• 'Hedonist' = pleasure ആണ് life-ൽ ഏറ്റവും പ്രധാനം എന്ന് believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — 'ആനന്ദ-തത്വവാദി'.
• Greek root: 'hedone' = pleasure → Hedonist = pleasure-seeker, pleasure doctrine follower.
• PSC-ൽ one word substitutes ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'hedonist = pleasure believer' ഓർക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Hedonist = pleasure doctrine believer (Greek 'hedone' = pleasure)
• Life-philosophy related one word substitutes (PSC important):
  - Hedonist = ആനന്ദം/pleasure-ൽ believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ
  - Ascetic = pleasures all give up ചെയ്ത് severe discipline follow ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ (opposite of hedonist!)
  - Stoic = pain/pleasure-ൽ emotions show ചെയ്യാത്ത ആൾ
  - Idealist = ideal/perfect world-ൽ believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ
  - Atheist = ദൈവം ഇല്ല എന്ന് believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He is a hedonist who lives for pleasure. ✓
  2. The ascetic monk gave up all worldly pleasures. ✓
  3. A stoic person remains calm in all situations. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Ascetic
• 'Ascetic' = pleasure all give up ചെയ്ത്, severe self-discipline follow ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — Hedonist-ന്റെ OPPOSITE ആണ്!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ascetic' = philosophical term ആണ്, sound important ആകും — pleasure = Ascetic feel ആകും, exact opposite ആണ്.

❌ (C) Stoic
• 'Stoic' = pain or pleasure-ൽ emotions show ചെയ്യാത്ത ആൾ — pleasure doctrine-ൽ believe ചെയ്യുന്നത് അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Stoic' = philosophy word ആണ്, pleasure-related ആകും feel ആകും.

❌ (D) Idealist
• 'Idealist' = ideal/perfect conditions-ൽ believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — pleasure doctrine-ൽ believe ചെയ്യുന്നത് അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Idealist' = positive/good things believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ = pleasure believe ആകും feel.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HEDONIST = HEDONE (pleasure in Greek). He-DONE it for pleasure! HE DONE everything for PLEASURE = HEDONIST!' — Greek root 'hedone' = pleasure. ഓർത്തിരിക്കൂ!
• Philosophy family (opposites ഓർക്കൂ): Hedonist (pleasure-seeker) ↔ Ascetic (pleasure-giver-upper) | Stoic (no emotions) | Idealist (ideal world)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Pleasure as chief good' = HEDONIST. 'Pleasure give up' = ASCETIC. 'No emotions' = STOIC. Context clearly indicate ചെയ്യും — pleasure doctrine = Hedonist.

questionText: Choose the correctly spelt word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Occurrence

• 'Occurrence' = ഒരു കാര്യം സംഭവിക്കൽ, an event that happens — correct spelling ആണ്.
• Key spelling: O-C-C-U-R-R-E-N-C-E — double 'c' AND double 'r' ഉണ്ടെന്ന് ഓർക്കൂ.
• 'Occur' + 'rence' = 'occurrence' — 'occur'-ൽ double 'r' add ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ (suffix '-ence' ചേർക്കുമ്പോൾ) double 'r' maintain ചെയ്യണം.
• PSC-ൽ long words-ന്റെ spelling ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'occurrence' popular choice ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: OC-CUR-R-ENCE — double C, double R, -ence ending!
• Spelling breakdown:
  - oc + cur + rence
  - Double C: oCCurrence ✓
  - Double R: occurRRence ✓
  - Ending: -ence (not -ance, not -ense)
• Doubling rule: single-syllable verbs ending in consonant-vowel-consonant → double the final consonant before suffix:
  occur → occurring, occurred, occurrence (r doubles)
  begin → beginning (n doubles)
  run → running (n doubles)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions PSC-ൽ regular. Double letters frequently tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Such an occurrence is very rare. ✓
  2. The occurrence of the event surprised everyone. ✓
  3. It was a strange occurrence indeed. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Occurence
• Double 'r' missing ആണ് — 'occurRence' → single r = wrong.
• Correct: occuRRence (double r). Wrong: occurence (single r).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Quick reading-ൽ correct ആണ് feel ആകും — double r notice ചെയ്യില്ല.

❌ (C) Occurencee
• Double r missing + extra 'e' at end — double wrong.
• Correct: occurrence. Wrong: occurencee (both errors).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Extra letters ചേർക്കൽ = trap option.

❌ (D) Occurrance
• 'rrance' = wrong ending — 'rrence' ആണ് correct (-ence, not -ance).
• 'Occur + rance' = not a word. 'Occur + rence' = occurrence (correct).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ance' suffix common ആണ് (performance,urance) → 'occurrance' correct feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'oc-CUR-R-ence — CUR has a double R tail! Like a dog that CURRS with two Rs!' — CURR (double r) is in the middle of OCCURRence. Both C and R double!
• Spelling checklist: (1) Double C? YES — oCCurrence ✓ (2) Double R? YES — occuRRence ✓ (3) Ending -ence? YES — occurrence ✓ (not -ance, not -ense)
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ options carefully compare ചെയ്യൂ. 'Occurrence' = double c + double r + -ence. ഒരു letter miss ആകിയാൽ wrong!

questionText: Which word means "having a harmful effect"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Pernicious

• 'Having a HARMFUL EFFECT' = Pernicious — ദോഷകരമായ, നാശം ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന.
• 'Pernicious' = causing serious harm/damage, especially in a gradual or subtle way — 'അപകടകരമായ, ദോഷകരമായ'.
• Latin root: 'perniciosus' = destructive (per = thoroughly + nex = death/destruction).
• PSC-ൽ one word substitutes vocabulary ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'pernicious = harmful' ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Pernicious = harmful/destructive (Latin 'perniciosus' = causing destruction)
• 'Harmful' related one word substitutes (PSC important):
  - Pernicious = seriously harmful, damaging
  - Perilous = dangerous, risky
  - Detrimental = causing harm/damage
  - Noxious = harmful, poisonous
• Confusion alert: Pernicious vs Perilous
  - Pernicious = harmful effect (gradual/subtle damage)
  - Perilous = dangerous situation (immediate risk)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Smoking has a pernicious effect on health. ✓
  2. The pernicious influence of drugs destroyed his life. ✓
  3. The drug has pernicious side effects. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Perilous
• 'Perilous' = dangerous, risky — immediate danger indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു, 'having a harmful effect' = ongoing/gradual harm.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Pernicious' vs 'Perilous' — similar starting letter (P), similar feel. 'Perilous' = dangerous, 'pernicious' = harmful effect. Subtle difference!

❌ (C) Precious
• 'Precious' = വിലയേറിയ, highly valued — 'harmful' = opposite meaning ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Precious' = 'Per-' prefix word → 'Pernicious' prefix-ഉം 'per-' ആണ്, same prefix = similar feel ആകും.

❌ (D) Perceptive
• 'Perceptive' = sharp observation ability ഉള്ള, insightful — 'harmful' meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Per-' prefix + long word = educated/correct sound ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PERNICIOUS = PER-NICIOUS = thoroughly (per) harmful (nicious ~ noxious)! Like venom (VENOM is PERNICIOUS)!' — 'Per' = thoroughly, 'nicious' = harmful. Thoroughly harmful = pernicious!
• P-word confusion buster: Pernicious=harmful | Perilous=dangerous | Precious=valuable | Perceptive=observant — each P-word has its own unique meaning.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Harmful effect' = PERNICIOUS. 'Dangerous situation' = PERILOUS. Subtle difference: harm (ongoing effect) vs danger (immediate risk). Context clue: 'having a harmful EFFECT' = pernicious.

questionText:
Complete the sentence with the correct option.

If you had gone there, you ______

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) would have met

• 'If you had gone there' = Past Perfect clause — Third Conditional ആണ്.
• Third Conditional = past-ൽ happen ആകാതിരുന്ന ഒരു situation-ന്റെ imaginary result.
• 'You didn't go → so you didn't meet' — reality. Conditional = what WOULD have happened.
• 🔑 Formula: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Three Conditionals:
  1st: If + Present Simple → will + V1 (possible future)
     'If it rains, I will stay home.'
  2nd: If + Past Simple → would + V1 (unlikely/imaginary present)
     'If I had money, I would buy it.'
  3rd: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3 (impossible past — didn't happen)
     'If you had gone, you would have met him.'
• Third Conditional clues: 'had gone / had studied / had tried' in if-clause.
• Result clause: would have + V3 (past participle).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional — PSC-ൽ most tested conditional type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If she had studied, she would have passed.
  2. If they had left early, they would have caught the train.
  3. If I had known, I would have helped.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) would meet
• 'Would meet' = Second Conditional result — but if-clause has Past Perfect ('had gone'), not Past Simple.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would + V1' familiar pattern — students mix 2nd and 3rd conditional.

(C) will meet
• 'Will meet' = First Conditional result — present/future. If-clause Past Perfect-മായി match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will' = future, meeting in future feel ആകും — but tense structure wrong.

(D) will have met
• Future Perfect form — conditionals-ൽ 'will have + V3' ഇങ്ങനെ use ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have met' part correct but 'will' wrong modal for 3rd conditional.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: '3rd Conditional = 3 PASTS — had gone / would have / met (V3)'
  If + HAD + V3 → WOULD + HAVE + V3
• Shortcut: See 'had + V3' in if-clause → answer is always 'would have + V3'.
• PSC pattern: Third conditional = regret about past. 'Had gone' in question = 'would have met' in answer. Guaranteed!

questionText: Pick out the correctly spelt word from the following.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Receive

• Correct spelling: R-E-C-E-I-V-E
• Golden rule: 'I before E except after C'
  — after C → 'ei' (receive, deceive, ceiling)
  — elsewhere → 'ie' (believe, achieve, relief)
• 'Rec-EIVE' — after 'c', so E comes before I: E-I not I-E.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'I before E, except after C'
  - After C → EI: receive, deceive, ceiling, perceive, conceive
  - Elsewhere → IE: believe, achieve, relief, grief, piece, field
• Exceptions to remember (PSC trap words):
  - weird, seize, neither, leisure, foreign — 'ei' but not after 'c'
  - their, vein, reign — 'ei' for other reasons
• Etymology: Latin 'recipere' — re (back) + capere (to take)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: IE/EI spelling rule — PSC-ൽ repeatedly tested.
• Common '-eive' words (all after C → EI):
  receive, deceive, perceive, conceive, achieve (exception — no C!)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I received a letter from the PSC office.
  2. She achieved her goal through hard work.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Recieve
• 'IE' after C — wrong! After C it must be 'EI'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students apply 'I before E' blindly without the 'except after C' part.

(C) Reseive
• 'S' instead of 'C' — completely wrong spelling.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Phonetically, 'c' sounds like 's' in 'receive' — so some write 's'.

(D) Resieve
• Both 's' instead of 'c' AND wrong IE/EI order.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double error — two mistakes combined to confuse.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'I before E except after C — reCEIve, deCEIve, CEIling!'
  After C → always EI. No exception for these words.
• PSC spelling family: receive / deceive / perceive / conceive — all -CEIVE (EI after C).
• Quick check: Is there a 'C' just before the IE/EI? YES → use EI. NO → use IE (believe, achieve).

questionText:
Fill in the blank using the appropriate form of the verb.

The government decided to abolish ______

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) rationing

• 'Decided to abolish ______' — 'abolish' ഒരു verb ആണ്, അതിന്റെ object വേണം.
• 'Abolish rationing' = ration system നിർത്തലാക്കുക.
• 'Rationing' (noun/gerund form) = ration ചെയ്യൽ — the system of rationing.
• 'Decided to abolish' + noun object = 'rationing' (the practice/system).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: decide + to + V1 (infinitive) — 'decided TO abolish'
  Then 'abolish' takes an OBJECT (noun): abolish + what? = rationing (the system)
• Gerund as noun: '-ing' form used as noun:
  - 'Rationing' = the act/system of rationing
  - 'Swimming is good' (subject)
  - 'I enjoy swimming' (object)
  - 'The government abolished rationing' (object of verb)
• Verbs followed by infinitive (decide, want, plan, hope, refuse, agree):
  decide TO go, want TO eat, plan TO study
• Verbs followed by gerund (enjoy, avoid, finish, suggest, keep):
  enjoy swimming, avoid eating, finish working
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Infinitive vs Gerund after specific verbs — PSC-ൽ regular question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The government decided to abolish rationing.
  2. She enjoys swimming every morning.
  3. He refused to accept the offer.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) ration
• 'Ration' = noun (a fixed portion) or verb. 'Abolish ration' grammatically possible but 'abolish rationing' (the system) more natural/correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ration' is familiar word — students pick it without thinking about 'rationing' as a system.

(C) rations
• 'Rations' = plural portions of food. 'Abolish rations' = remove food portions, not the system.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Plural form feels more complete — but meaning slightly different.

(D) rationism
• Not a real English word — invented distractor.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ism' suffix makes it sound like a valid concept word.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DECIDE TO → then what? A THING (noun)!' Abolish WHAT? The SYSTEM = rationing.
• Key pattern: decide/plan/want/hope + to + V1 → then object can be a gerund-noun.
• PSC tip: When verb is followed by 'to + V1' and then another blank, think — is it a verb or a noun needed? Here 'abolish' needs a noun object = rationing.

questionText:
Select the appropriate question tag.

I am good, _______

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) aren't I?

• 'I am good' = POSITIVE statement, auxiliary = 'am', subject = 'I'.
• Positive → Negative tag needed.
• Special exception: 'I am' positive → tag is 'aren't I?' (NOT 'amn't I?' which doesn't exist in standard English).
• 'I am good, aren't I?' — ഇത് standard English-ലെ accepted form.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Special 'I am' tags:
  'I am' (positive) → 'aren't I?' ← EXCEPTION (amn't = not standard)
  'I am not' (negative) → 'am I?' ← regular positive tag
• Why 'aren't I?' not 'amn't I?':
  - 'Amn't' is not accepted in standard British/American English
  - 'Aren't I?' is the universally accepted exception
• General tag rule:
  Positive → negative tag | Negative → positive tag
  Same auxiliary in tag as in statement
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'I am' exception tag — PSC favourite because it's a known exception.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I am your friend, aren't I?
  2. I am not wrong, am I?
  3. She is coming, isn't she?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) am I?
• 'Am I?' = positive tag — used for NEGATIVE statements ('I am not..., am I?').
• Here statement is positive ('I am good') → negative tag needed.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Am' is in statement, so 'am I?' feels right — but polarity wrong.

(B) amn't I?
• 'Amn't' = not standard English. Grammatically invalid in formal usage.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Logically derived (am + n't = amn't) but not accepted usage — classic trap.

(D) isn't I?
• 'Isn't' = third person singular. 'I' never takes 'is/isn't'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Isn't it?' is common tag — students accidentally write 'isn't I?' forgetting subject-verb rule.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'I AM → AREN'T I? — AM becomes ARE for the tag!' Just remember this one exception.
• The two 'I am' tags — write them down once and memorize:
  I am → aren't I? | I am not → am I?
• PSC shortcut: See 'I am' (positive) in question → answer is 'aren't I?' — no thinking needed!

questionText: Choose the correct plural form of the word LOUSE.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Lice

• 'Louse' = ഒരൊറ്റ പേൻ (a single parasitic insect).
• Plural = 'Lice' — irregular plural, '-ouse → -ice' pattern.
• Same pattern: Mouse → Mice, Louse → Lice.
• 'Louses' or 'lices' — both wrong. Only 'lice' is correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: -ouse → -ice (irregular plural)
  Mouse → Mice | Louse → Lice
• Irregular plural groups — PSC favourites:
  - -ouse → -ice: mouse/mice, louse/lice
  - -oot → -eet: foot/feet, tooth/teeth
  - -an → -en: man/men, woman/women
  - -ild → -ildren: child/children
  - -ox → -oxen: ox/oxen
  - Same form: sheep/sheep, deer/deer, fish/fish
  - Latin origin: cactus/cacti, syllabus/syllabi, radius/radii
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Irregular plurals — PSC-ൽ animal/insect plurals frequently tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The child had lice in her hair.
  2. Mice are found everywhere in old buildings.
  3. The oxen pulled the cart.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Louses
• Regular '-s' added — wrong for irregular noun.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Regular plural rule applied to irregular noun — most common mistake.

(C) Lices
• 'Lice' is already plural — adding '-s' makes it double plural (wrong).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students know 'lice' is related but add '-s' unnecessarily.

(D) Lousen
• Not a real English word — invented distractor.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-en' suffix exists (oxen, children) so 'lousen' sounds plausible — but wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MOUSE-MICE, LOUSE-LICE — they rhyme because they're the same family!'
  One mouse, many mice. One louse, many lice. Same pattern!
• Irregular plural pairs PSC loves: mouse/mice · louse/lice · foot/feet · tooth/teeth · child/children · ox/oxen
• PSC strategy: When you see irregular nouns — never add just '-s'. Think: does it follow a special pattern?

questionText: The antonym of 'mitigate' is ______

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Aggravate

• 'Mitigate' = ദുരിതം/കഷ്ടം കുറയ്ക്കുക — to make less severe, reduce suffering.
• 'Aggravate' = കൂടുതൽ വഷളാക്കുക — to make worse, intensify.
• Exact opposites: mitigate (reduce) ↔ aggravate (increase/worsen).
• PSC context: 'Measures to mitigate flood damage' vs 'Actions that aggravate the situation'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Mitigate = reduce/lessen ↔ Aggravate = worsen/intensify
• Word families:
  - Mitigate → Mitigation (n) → Mitigating (adj: mitigating circumstances)
  - Aggravate → Aggravation (n) → Aggravating (adj)
• Etymology:
  - Mitigate: Latin 'mitigare' = to soften (mitis = soft + agere = to make)
  - Aggravate: Latin 'aggravare' = to make heavier (ad + gravis = heavy)
• Synonym groups:
  - Mitigate = alleviate, lessen, reduce, relieve, ease, soften
  - Aggravate = worsen, intensify, exacerbate, inflame, compound
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Mitigate/Aggravate antonym pair — PSC-ൽ multiple times tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The medicine helped mitigate the pain.
  2. His rude reply only aggravated the situation.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(B) Alleviate
• 'Alleviate' = reduce/lessen — SYNONYM of mitigate, not antonym!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Classic PSC trick — put a synonym as wrong option to confuse students who know mitigate = reduce.

(C) Stubborn
• 'Stubborn' = വാശിയുള്ള — completely unrelated to mitigate.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Random distractor for students who don't know mitigate's meaning.

(D) Lessen
• 'Lessen' = another synonym of mitigate — same meaning, not opposite.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Two synonyms (alleviate + lessen) placed as wrong options — tests if student knows antonym vs synonym.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AGGRAVATE = GRAVE situation!' — aggravate makes things GRAVE (serious/worse).
  MITIGATE = MITI-gate = opens the gate for relief!
• PSC antonym trap alert: When you see synonyms of the word in options (alleviate, lessen) — eliminate them! Antonym = opposite.
• Word pair: Mitigate ↔ Aggravate — learn them as a pair, like optimism/pessimism.

questionText:
Use the correct preposition.

She is good ______ Mathematics.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തරം: (B) at

• 'Good at' = fixed preposition collocation — skill/subject-ൽ നല്ലത് ആകുക.
• 'She is good AT Mathematics' — ഇത് standard English expression.
• 'Good at' = ability/skill in a subject or activity.
• PSC context: 'He is good at English.' 'She is good at cooking.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: good AT (skill/subject) — always 'at', never 'in/on/for'
• Fixed preposition collocations — PSC must-know:
  - Good AT (skill): good at maths, good at cooking
  - Interested IN (topic): interested in science
  - Married TO (person): married to Raju
  - Afraid OF (thing): afraid of dogs
  - Responsible FOR (task): responsible for the project
  - Angry WITH (person): angry with him
  - Angry AT (situation): angry at the delay
  - Proud OF: proud of her achievement
  - Fond OF: fond of music
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Fixed preposition collocations — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequent preposition question type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is good at painting.
  2. He is not good at remembering names.
  3. Are you good at Mathematics?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) on
• 'Good on' — not a standard English collocation.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'On' is common preposition, random choice.

(C) in
• 'Good in' — sometimes heard informally but NOT standard. 'Interested IN' is correct, not 'good in'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'In Mathematics' sounds natural (we study IN mathematics) — but 'good IN' wrong.

(D) for
• 'Good for' = beneficial for something ('Exercise is good for health') — different meaning entirely.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Good for' is a valid phrase but means 'beneficial', not 'skilled at'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AT = Aiming at a Target' — when you're GOOD AT something, you HIT the target!
• PSC preposition collocations cheat sheet:
  good AT · interested IN · married TO · afraid OF · responsible FOR · proud OF · fond OF
• Exam strategy: Memorize these 7 fixed pairs — they cover 80% of PSC preposition questions!

questionText:
Choose the correct article.

I saw _______ one-eyed man.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) a

• 'One-eyed man' — 'one' = 'w' sound (wun) — consonant SOUND ആണ്.
• Article rule: 'a' = before consonant SOUND, 'an' = before vowel SOUND.
• 'One' starts with 'w' sound (not 'o' sound) → 'a one-eyed man' ✅
• Similarly: 'a university' (yu- sound), 'a European' (yoo- sound) — vowel letter but consonant sound.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Article depends on SOUND, not SPELLING
  'a' = before consonant SOUND
  'an' = before vowel SOUND (a, e, i, o, u sounds)
• Tricky cases:
  - 'one' = /wʌn/ (w-sound) → a one-eyed man ✅
  - 'university' = /juːnɪ/ (y-sound) → a university ✅
  - 'hour' = /aʊər/ (vowel sound, h silent) → an hour ✅
  - 'honest' = /ɒnɪst/ (vowel sound, h silent) → an honest man ✅
  - 'European' = /jʊərə/ (y-sound) → a European ✅
  - 'MBA' = /em-biː-eɪ/ (em sound = vowel) → an MBA ✅
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: a/an with 'one', 'hour', 'honest', 'university' — PSC-ൽ classic questions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I saw a one-rupee coin.
  2. She waited for an hour.
  3. He is an honest officer.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) an
• 'An' = before vowel SOUND. 'One' = /wʌn/ consonant sound → 'an' wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'One' starts with 'O' (vowel letter) — students see 'o' and write 'an' automatically.

(C) The
• 'The' = specific/already known. First mention of 'one-eyed man' — not specific.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: If the man was previously mentioned, 'the' would be right — but this is first mention.

(D) None of these
• An article IS needed here — 'I saw one-eyed man' without article is wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'None of these' option confuses students who are unsure between a/an.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ONE = WON (sounds like won) → starts with W sound → use A!'
  a one, a university, a European — all start with Y/W sounds despite vowel letters.
• PSC golden rule: LISTEN to the word, don't just READ it. Sound = king for articles!
• Tricky vowel-letter but consonant-sound words: one, university, European, uniform, unique → all take 'a'.

questionText: Past tense of shoot is _______

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) shot

• 'Shoot' = irregular verb — shoot → shot → shot.
• Past tense = 'shot' (not 'shooted' — that would be regular, but shoot is irregular).
• 'He shot the target.' — Simple Past of shoot.
• Same pattern as other irregular verbs: shoot/shot, sit/sat, hit/hit.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Shoot → Shot → Shot (V1 → V2 → V3 all different or V2=V3)
• Irregular verb pattern group — 'oo → o':
  - Shoot → Shot → Shot
  - Choose → Chose → Chosen
  - Lose → Lost → Lost
• Other important irregular verbs (PSC favourites):
  - Write → Wrote → Written
  - Ride → Rode → Ridden
  - Rise → Rose → Risen
  - Fall → Fell → Fallen
  - Fly → Flew → Flown
  - Blow → Blew → Blown
  - Draw → Drew → Drawn
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Irregular verb past tense — PSC-ൽ frequently asked.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The hunter shot the bird.
  2. She has shot three goals this season.
  3. He shot a film in Kerala.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) Shooted
• Regular '-ed' added to irregular verb — wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Most verbs add '-ed' for past tense — students apply regular rule to irregular verb.

(C) Shooten
• Not a real English word — invented distractor.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-en' is a past participle suffix (taken, fallen) — 'shooten' sounds plausible but wrong.

(D) Shoten
• Also not a real word — another invented distractor.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Combines 'shot' + '-en' — looks like a past participle but not standard.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SHOOT → SHOT — the 'oo' got SHOT and became 'o'!'
  Double-o (shoot) → single-o (shot) — bullet removed one 'o'!
• PSC must-know irregular verbs: shoot/shot · write/wrote · ride/rode · fly/flew · fall/fell · draw/drew
• Strategy: Never add '-ed' to these common irregular verbs — know them by heart. They come up in EVERY PSC paper.

questionText:
Choose the correct alternative.

Neither of us ______ coffee.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) likes

• 'Neither of us' = singular subject — 'neither' is always singular.
• Singular subject → singular verb → 'likes' (not 'like').
• 'Neither of us LIKES coffee' — both of us don't like, but grammatically singular.
• Rule: Neither/Either + of + plural noun → singular verb.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Neither/Either + of + noun → SINGULAR verb (always)
• Neither of us LIKES / Either of them IS / None of them HAS
• Compare:
  - Neither of us likes (singular — neither = not one of us)
  - Neither Raju nor Ravi likes (nearer subject rule — Ravi = singular → likes)
• 'Us/them/you' plural ആണ്, but 'neither of' makes it singular in meaning.
• ⭐ PSC ആවർത്തനം: 'Neither of us/them' SVA — PSC-ൽ regular question.
• Other always-singular subjects:
  - Each of the students HAS
  - Every one of them IS
  - None of them HAS (formal) / HAVE (informal — but PSC prefers HAS)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Neither of the boys has finished.
  2. Either of the answers is acceptable.
  3. Neither of us likes spicy food.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

(A) like
• 'Like' = plural verb form — 'neither of us' singular ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Us' is plural, so 'like' (plural verb) feel ആകും — but 'neither of' singular meaning ആണ്.

(B) liked
• Past tense — no past time reference in sentence. Present tense context ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Coffee dislike = habitual fact → present tense. 'Liked' = past tense, wrong.

(D) liken
• 'Liken' = compare to something ('She likened it to a dream'). Completely different word.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Looks like a verb form of 'like' — but 'liken' = compare, not enjoy/prefer.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'NEITHER = NOT EITHER ONE = ONE = SINGULAR!' 
  Neither of us = not one of us → singular → likes.
• PSC pattern: 'Neither of ___' → always singular verb. No exception.
• Quick rule family: Neither of / Either of / Each of / Every one of / None of → all SINGULAR verb!

questionText:
Choose the correct preposition given in brackets.

He searched _________ the ring everywhere.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) for

• "Search for" എന്നത് English-ൽ ഏറ്റവും commonly use ചെയ്യുന്ന collocation ആണ് — "എന്തെങ്കിലും അന്വേഷിക്കുക" എന്ന് അർത്ഥം.
• "He searched for the ring everywhere" — "അദ്ദേഹം മോതിരം എല്ലായിടത്തും അന്വേഷിച്ചു" എന്നാണ് ഈ sentence-ന്റെ Malayalam meaning.
• 'Search' എന്ന verb-നൊപ്പം എപ്പോഴും 'for' ആണ് വരേണ്ടത് — ഇത് ഒരു fixed verb-preposition collocation ആണ്, മാറ്റം ഇല്ല.
• PSC exam-ൽ "search for", "look for", "wait for" — ഇത്തരം fixed preposition combinations ആവർത്തിച്ച് ചോദിക്കാറുണ്ട്. ഇവ practice ചെയ്ത് memorize ചെയ്യുക!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Search + FOR = always! മറ്റ് prepositions ഒരിക്കലും ശരിയല്ല.
• 'Search' verb-ന് ശേഷം 'for' മാത്രമേ വരൂ — ഇത് ഒരു fixed verb-preposition collocation ആണ്.
• Verb + Preposition collocations PSC-ൽ വളരെ important ആണ് — ഇവ list ആക്കി memorize ചെയ്യണം.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Preposition collocations ഓരോ paper-ലും വരുന്നു — guaranteed topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She searched for her lost keys all morning. (അവൾ രാവിലെ മുഴുവൻ നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ട chabi അന്വേഷിച്ചു)
  2. The police searched for the missing child. (കാണാതായ കുട്ടിയെ police അന്വേഷിച്ചു)
  3. We searched for a good restaurant in the city. (ഞങ്ങൾ നഗരത്തിൽ നല്ലൊരു restaurant അന്വേഷിച്ചു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) of
• 'Of' preposition possession അല്ലെങ്കിൽ belonging indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് — "the ring of gold", "a cup of tea" ഇതുപോലെ.
• "Search of" എന്ന combination standard English-ൽ ഇല്ല — grammatically incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'in search of' എന്ന phrase ഉണ്ട് — "He went in search of the ring" ഇത് correct ആണ്. പക്ഷേ ഇവിടെ "searched" verb ആണ്, "in search" noun phrase അല്ല!

❌ (C) to
• 'To' preposition direction indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ് — "He went to school", "I talked to him" ഇതുപോലെ.
• "Search to" എന്ന combination English-ൽ ഇല്ല — ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കാറില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "looked to" vs "looked for" confusion — PSC ഇതുപോലുള്ള traps ഇടാറുണ്ട്, careful ആകുക!

❌ (D) in
• 'In' preposition place അല്ലെങ്കിൽ time indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ് — "in the box", "in 2025" ഇതുപോലെ.
• "Searched in the drawer" partial sense-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കാം, പക്ഷേ direct object-നൊപ്പം "search for" ആണ് standard.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: everyday speech-ൽ "search in" കേൾക്കാം, പക്ഷേ standard grammar-ൽ "search for" ആണ് correct form.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SEARCH FOR" — ഇത് ഒരു married couple പോലെ! ഇവർ ഒരിക്കലും വേർപിരിയില്ല. Search കണ്ടാൽ FOR automatic ആയി വരണം — "Search-For" couple!
• Word family: look FOR, wait FOR, ask FOR, care FOR, search FOR — ഇവ ഒരുമിച്ച് practice ചെയ്യുക. ഇവ ഒക്കെ same pattern!
• PSC exam strategy: Verb + Preposition questions-ൽ ആദ്യം common collocations check ചെയ്യുക. 'Search' + option കണ്ടാൽ 'for' ഉണ്ടോ എന്ന് നോക്കി ഉടനെ answer ചെയ്യുക — guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct tense form of the verb given in brackets.

Our school team ___________ the cricket match last year.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) won

• "Last year" — ഇത് Simple Past tense-ന്റെ ഏറ്റവും clear signal word ആണ്. ഇത് കണ്ടാൽ Simple Past form select ചെയ്യണം.
• "Our school team won the cricket match last year" — "കഴിഞ്ഞ വർഷം നമ്മുടെ school team cricket match ജയിച്ചു" എന്നാണ് meaning.
• 'Won' = 'win'-ന്റെ Simple Past form — win ഒരു irregular verb ആണ്: win → won → won.
• Completed action in the past → Simple Past tense — ഇത് basic rule, ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കരുത്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: LAST (year/week/month/night) → Simple Past tense! ഇത് automatic ആകണം.
• Simple Past tense: Subject + V2 (past form of verb)
• Signal words for Simple Past: yesterday, last year/week/month, ago, in 2024, then, once, when I was young
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Tense questions ഓരോ PSC paper-ലും guaranteed — signal words identify ചെയ്യുന്ന practice ചെയ്യുക!
• Irregular verb: win → won → won (V1 → V2 → V3) — same form for past and past participle.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She won the first prize last year. (കഴിഞ്ഞ വർഷം അവൾ ഒന്നാം സ്ഥാനം നേടി)
  2. They played football yesterday. (ഇന്നലെ അവർ football കളിച്ചു)
  3. He left the office two hours ago. (രണ്ട് മണിക്കൂർ മുമ്പ് അദ്ദേഹം office വിട്ടു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) had won
• 'Had won' = Past Perfect tense — ഭൂതകാലത്ത് മറ്റൊരു past action-നു മുമ്പ് complete ആയ action describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• "By the time we arrived, the team had won" — ഇതുപോലെ TWO past actions ഉള്ളപ്പോഴാണ് Past Perfect വരേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'had won' formal ആയി തോന്നുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് students ഇത് select ചെയ്യും — പക്ഷേ "last year" + single past action = Simple Past മതി!

❌ (B) wins
• 'Wins' = Simple Present tense (third person singular) — habitual/general truths describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• "She wins every match" — routinely winning describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് ഉപയോഗിക്കാം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Last year" signal word കണ്ടിട്ടും present tense answer ചെയ്യുന്നത് common mistake — time signal ഒരിക്കലും ignore ചെയ്യരുത്!

❌ (C) win
• 'Win' = base form / infinitive — ഇത് conjugated tense form അല്ല.
• Sentence-ൽ subject "Our school team" (singular) + past time signal "last year" ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് base form ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Base form options കണ്ടാൽ time signal word check ചെയ്തിട്ട് reject ചെയ്യുക.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LAST = PAST" — Last year, last week, last night കണ്ടാൽ Simple Past form ഉടനെ! ഈ rhyme ഓർക്കുക: "Last ആണോ? Past തന്നെ!" — ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Signal words family: yesterday, last __, ___ ago, once, in [past year], then, when I was young — ഇവ കണ്ടാൽ Simple Past.
• PSC exam strategy: Time signal word ആദ്യം identify ചെയ്യുക → tense decide ചെയ്യുക → answer choose ചെയ്യുക. "Last year" = Simple Past, ഇത് 2 seconds-ൽ answer ചെയ്യണം!

questionText: The children are playing, ___________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) aren't they?

• "The children are playing" — ഇത് positive sentence ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് negative question tag വേണം.
• Auxiliary verb "are" ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് tag-ൽ "aren't" (are + not contracted) വരണം.
• "The children" = third person plural → tag-ൽ pronoun "they" വേണം.
• "The children are playing, aren't they?" — "കുട്ടികൾ കളിക്കുന്നുണ്ട്, അല്ലേ?" — ഇതാണ് complete sentence-ന്റെ meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive sentence → Negative tag | Negative sentence → Positive tag
• Question tag format: [Auxiliary + n't] + [pronoun]?
• "are playing" → auxiliary = "are" → tag = "aren't they?"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags ഓരോ paper-ലും വരുന്നു — top 5 most important PSC topics!
• Auxiliary verbs and their negative contractions:
  - am/is/are → isn't/aren't
  - was/were → wasn't/weren't
  - do/does → don't/doesn't
  - have/has → haven't/hasn't
  - will → won't
  - can → can't
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is reading, isn't she? (positive → negative tag)
  2. They are coming, aren't they? (positive → negative tag)
  3. He is not sleeping, is he? (negative → positive tag)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) don't they?
• "Don't" = "do + not" → ഇത് Simple Present tense-ൽ ഉള്ള sentences-നാണ് — "They play, don't they?"
• ഈ sentence-ൽ "are playing" (Present Continuous) ആണ്, "do" auxiliary അല്ല. Tense match ആകുന്നില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'they' pronoun correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ auxiliary "don't" wrong — half-right traps PSC-ൽ common, careful!

❌ (C) do they?
• "Do they?" = positive tag — sentence positive ആണെങ്കിൽ negative tag വേണം, positive tag വരരുത്.
• Positive sentence + positive tag = grammatically wrong — ഇത് basic rule!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Positive/Negative rule confuse ആകുന്ന students ഈ option select ചെയ്യും. "Positive→Negative" rule ഓർക്കുക!

❌ (D) weren't they?
• "Weren't" = past tense (were + not) — sentence present tense-ൽ ആണ് ("are playing"), past tense അല്ല.
• Main sentence-ൻ്റെ tense = tag-ൻ്റെ tense — ഇത് iron rule, break ചെയ്യരുത്!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'they' pronoun correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ past tense "weren't" present sentence-ന് ഒരിക്കലും match ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "POS-NEG, NEG-POS" — Positive sentence → Negative tag, Negative sentence → Positive tag. ഒരു see-saw ആണ് question tag — ഒരു side up ആണെങ്കിൽ other side down! ഇത് visualize ചെയ്യുക.
• Tag formula: [Same auxiliary + n't] + [correct pronoun]? — Auxiliary main sentence-ൽ നിന്ന് copy ചെയ്യുക, tense change ചെയ്യരുത്.
• PSC exam strategy: ആദ്യം sentence positive/negative check → tag opposite → auxiliary identify → pronoun match. 4 steps, 4 seconds, guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct passive form of the sentence given below:

People will support us.
optionA: They would be supported by the people.
optionB: We will be supported by the people.
optionC: They will be supported by the people.
optionD: He will be supported by the people.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) We will be supported by the people.

• "People will support us" → Passive: "We will be supported by the people."
• Object pronoun "us" → Passive subject pronoun "We" — object pronoun 'us' subject pronoun 'we' ആകും.
• "will support" → "will be supported" — Future Simple Passive: will be + V3 (past participle).
• Active subject "People" → passive agent "by the people" ആകും.
• "ജനങ്ങൾ നമ്മെ support ചെയ്യും" → "നാം ജനങ്ങളാൽ support ചെയ്യപ്പെടും" — ഇതാണ് passive meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active Object → Passive Subject (us → we, him → he, her → she, them → they)
• Future Simple Passive formula: Subject + will be + V3 + by + agent
