explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He is engaged with many problems
• "Too many irons in the fire" = enterprises/activities refer ചെയ്യുന്നു — problems/difficulties അല്ല.
• Idiom-ൽ negative sense ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും "problems" ആണ് meaning എന്ന് directly പറഞ്ഞിട്ടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Too many" = problems feel — but idiom = too many ACTIVITIES, not problems!

❌ (B) He is having many qualities
• Idiom-ൽ qualities/talents-നെ കുറിച്ച് ഒന്നും ഇല്ല — activities/enterprises ആണ് meaning.
• "Irons in the fire" = tasks being handled, qualities/talents അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Fire" = passionate/talented feel — but idiom = overloaded with tasks!

❌ (C) He is having many drawbacks
• "Drawbacks" = shortcomings, defects. Idiom = enterprises/activities — drawbacks-മായി യാതൊരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Too many" + negative connotation = drawbacks feel — but correct meaning = enterprises overload!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Blacksmith too many IRONS = cannot focus on ONE = too many activities!" — ഈ blacksmith image ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും meaning മറക്കില്ല!
• Word family: too many irons in the fire | bite off more than you can chew | have a lot on one's plate — ഈ മൂന്ന് idioms ഒരേ theme (overloaded) — ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning ആദ്യം eliminate ചെയ്യൂ — "irons/fire" literal meaning options out. "Enterprises at the same time" = correct figurative meaning. Always check figurative sense!

questionText:
He seemed affected by __diffidence__.
(Identify the antonym of the underlined word)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Confidence

• "Diffidence" = shyness, lack of self-confidence — "ആത്മവിശ്വാസക്കുറവ്, നാണക്കേട്" എന്ന് Malayalam meaning.
• Antonym = opposite meaning → "Confidence" = self-assurance, boldness = "ആത്മവിശ്വാസം."
• "He seemed affected by diffidence" = "ആത്മവിശ്വാസക്കുറവ് അദ്ദേഹത്തെ ബാധിച്ചതായി തോന്നി."
• Memory clue: "DIF-fidence" — "DIF-ficulty with conFIDENCE" — confidence-ന്റെ opposite!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Diffidence = lack of confidence → Antonym = Confidence (remove the lack!)
• Word family:
  diffident (adjective) = shy, lacking confidence
  diffidently (adverb) = in a shy manner
  diffidence (noun) = shyness, lack of confidence
• Synonyms of diffidence: shyness | timidity | bashfulness | reticence | self-doubt
• Antonyms of diffidence: confidence | boldness | assertiveness | self-assurance
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Formal/literary vocabulary antonyms — PSC-ൽ regular feature. Diffidence-ഉം confidence-ഉം PSC pair!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Her diffidence prevented her from speaking in public. (shyness)
  2. Confidence is the exact antonym of diffidence.
  3. The diffident student rarely raised her hand in class.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Valour
• "Valour" = bravery/courage in battle — "യുദ്ധധീരത."
• Diffidence = confidence issue, bravery/cowardice issue അല്ല. Confidence ≠ Valour (close, but different dimension).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Diffident person lacks courage feel — valour = brave = antonym feel. But diffidence specifically = confidence, not battlefield courage!

❌ (C) Cleverness
• "Cleverness" = intelligence, smartness — "ബുദ്ധി."
• Diffidence = confidence-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ്, intelligence-നെ കുറിച്ചല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Shy person seems less clever feel — cleverness antonym feel ആകും. But diffidence = confidence dimension, not intelligence!

❌ (D) Seriousness
• "Seriousness" = being grave, formal, solemn — "ഗൗരവം."
• Diffidence-ഉം seriousness-ഉം entirely different dimensions — ഒന്നും ബന്ധമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Serious-ൽ shy feel ഉണ്ടെന്ന് തോന്നും — but antonym of diffidence = confidence, seriousness അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "dif-FIDENCE — FIDENCE sounds like conFIDENCE! Dif = different/opposite → CONFIDENCE!" — word-ൽ തന്നെ answer ഉണ്ട്!
• Word family: diffident/diffidence (shy) ↔ confident/confidence (bold) — ഈ pair together ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "Dif-" prefix = difficulty/opposition sense → remove the negative → confidence. Prefix trick ഉപയോഗിച്ചാൽ antonym questions easy!

questionText: I _____ (attend) the class since 10:00 am.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) have been attending

• "Since 10:00 am" = ഒരു specific time point-ൽ തുടങ്ങി ഇപ്പോഴും continue ആകുന്ന action.
• ഇപ്പോഴും class-ൽ ഉണ്ട് — action still going on → Present Perfect Continuous tense.
• Formula: have/has + been + V-ing → "have been attending."
• "I have been attending the class since 10:00 am" = "ഞാൻ രാവിലെ 10 മുതൽ class-ൽ ഉണ്ട്" (ഇപ്പോഴും).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Past-ൽ start ആയ, STILL CONTINUING action + since/for → Present Perfect Continuous
• Formula: have/has + been + V-ing
• Signal words:
  since + specific time (since 10:00 am, since Monday, since 2020) → Perfect Continuous
  for + duration (for two hours, for a long time, for years) → Perfect Continuous
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Since/for" + Present Perfect Continuous — PSC tenses-ൽ ഏറ്റവും repeat ആകുന്ന combination!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I have been studying since morning. (since = specific time)
  2. She has been working here for five years. (for = duration)
  3. They have been waiting since 9 o'clock. (since = specific time)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) have attended
• "Have attended" = Present Perfect — completed action indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• "Since 10:00 am" + still in class = action ongoing → Perfect Continuous വേണം, simple Perfect അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Since" → Present Perfect feel → "have attended" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But still ongoing = CONTINUOUS form!

❌ (B) am attending
• "Am attending" = Present Continuous — ഈ moment-ൽ നടക്കുന്നത് show ചെയ്യുന്നു, duration show ചെയ്യില്ല.
• "Since 10:00 am" = duration from a past time — Present Continuous-ന് ഇത് express ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Still attending = present continuous feel. But "since" = duration from past = Perfect Continuous!

❌ (C) had attended
• "Had attended" = Past Perfect — മറ്റൊരു past action-ന് മുമ്പ് complete ആയ action-ന്.
• "Since 10:00 am" present context ആണ് — past perfect ഇവിടെ ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Formal sound → "had attended" educated feel — but tense mismatch, action still going on!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SINCE/FOR + STILL GOING = HAVE BEEN doing!" — action start ആയ time ഉണ്ട്, ഇപ്പോഴും continue = have been + V-ing!
• Word family: have been waiting | has been studying | have been working | has been living — ഒരേ pattern, different verbs!
• PSC exam strategy: "Since" blank-ൽ കണ്ടാൽ → action still happening-ഓ check ചെയ്യൂ. Yes = have/has been + V-ing. ഈ one rule ഓർത്താൽ since/for questions always correct!

questionText:
I went for a picnic to relieve the __tedium__ of everyday life.
(Choose the synonym of the underlined word)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Boredom

• "Tedium" = ഏകതാനമായ, repeating activities മൂലം ഉണ്ടാകുന്ന mental dullness/boredom — "മടുപ്പ്, ബോർഡം."
• "I went for a picnic to relieve the tedium of everyday life" = "ദൈനംദിന ജീവിതത്തിന്റെ ഏകതാനതയ്ക്ക് ആശ്വാസം കിട്ടാൻ picnic-ന് പോയി."
• Boredom = same meaning — monotony, dullness, lack of excitement → synonym perfect match!
• Root: Latin "taedium" = weariness, disgust from repetition — ഇതാണ് tedium-ന്റെ origin.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Tedium = Boredom = Monotony — ഏകതാനതയുടെ mental feeling
• Etymology: Latin "taedium" → "taedet" = it is wearisome — Latin root synonym = boredom/weariness
• Word family:
  tedium (noun) = boredom from repetition
  tedious (adjective) = boring, monotonous, long and dull
  tediously (adverb) = in a boring/slow manner
• Synonyms of tedium: boredom | monotony | dullness | dreariness | ennui
• Antonyms: excitement | interest | stimulation | enthusiasm | thrill
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Formal vocabulary synonyms — PSC-ൽ ഇത്തരം Latin-origin words regular feature!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The tedium of the long journey was unbearable.
  2. Reading novels relieves the tedium of daily routine.
  3. She quit the job due to the tedium of repetitive tasks.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Tiredness
• "Tiredness" = physical exhaustion/fatigue — "ശരീര ക്ഷീണം."
• "Tedium" = mental boredom/monotony — physical tiredness അല്ല. ഒരാൾക്ക് bored ആകാം without being physically tired!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Long monotonous activity = tired feel → tiredness choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But tedium = MENTAL boredom, not physical fatigue!

❌ (C) Harshness
• "Harshness" = roughness, cruelty, severity — "കഠിനത, ക്രൂരത."
• Tedium = boring/dull feeling — harshness-മായി യാതൊരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Everyday life = harsh feel — harshness choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But tedium = dullness/boredom, not harshness!

❌ (D) Tension
• "Tension" = stress, anxiety, nervousness — "ഉൽക്കണ്ഠ, tension."
• Tedium = too calm/boring, tension = too agitated — actually near opposites in feel!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Relieve" keyword → "relieve tension" familiar phrase → tension choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But sentence relieve ചെയ്യുന്നത് tedium (boredom), tension അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "TEDIOUS = BORING — Ted is always BORED at work! Tedium = Ted's BOREDOM!" — Ted character image ഓർത്താൽ tedious/tedium = boredom!
• Word family: tedious (adj) | tedium (noun) | synonyms: boring, monotonous, dull, dreary — ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "Tedium/tedious" കണ്ടാൽ → ഉടനെ boredom/monotony. Never tiredness or tension. Etymology trick: Latin taedium = weariness from repetition = boredom!

questionText: Honour and glory _____ his reward.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) is

• "Honour and glory" = രണ്ട് words "and" കൊണ്ട് join ചെയ്തിട്ടുണ്ടെങ്കിലും, ഇവ ഒരേ concept represent ചെയ്യുന്ന single idea ആണ്.
• Grammar rule: "and" ൽ join ആയ two nouns single concept/idea represent ചെയ്താൽ → singular verb.
• "Bread and butter is my favourite" — ഇതുപോലെ "honour and glory" = ഒരേ reward-ന്റെ two aspects.
• "Honour and glory is his reward" = "ആദരവും മഹത്ത്വവും അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ പ്രതിഫലം ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Two nouns "and"-ൽ join → ONE concept/idea → Singular verb (is/was)
• Single concept pairs (singular verb):
  Bread and butter IS | Slow and steady WINS | Time and tide WAITS
  Honour and glory IS | Law and order IS | Thunder and lightning IS
• Two separate people/things (plural verb):
  Ram and Shyam ARE | A pen and a book ARE | Tom and Mary WERE
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Bread and butter" type SVA — PSC classic trick question, ഒരിക്കലെങ്കിലും വരും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Bread and butter is my daily food. (one food concept)
  2. Slow and steady wins the race. (one strategy concept)
  3. Tom and Jerry are cartoon characters. (two separate characters = plural)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) are
• "Are" plural verb ആണ്. "Honour and glory" single concept (one reward) represent ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ singular "is" ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Honour + glory = two words, "and" = plural feel → "are" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But single concept = singular verb!

❌ (C) have
• "Have" plural present auxiliary ആണ്. Main verb context-ൽ "is/are" വേണ്ടിടത്ത് "have" fit ആകില്ല.
• "Honour and glory have his reward" = grammatically wrong sentence structure.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "They have" = possession feel → but "reward" = identity, not possession. "Is" ആണ് ചേരുക!

❌ (D) were
• "Were" past tense plural ആണ്. Sentence-ൽ past context ഒന്നും ഇല്ല — present tense "is" ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Formal/literary sentence → past tense feel → "were" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But present tense needed here!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Bread and Butter = ONE thing on your plate = IS! Honour and Glory = ONE reward = IS!" — ഒരേ plate-ൽ ഒരേ idea = singular!
• Word family: honour and glory IS | bread and butter IS | slow and steady WINS | time and tide WAITS — PSC classic inseparable pairs!
• PSC exam strategy: "X and Y" subject കണ്ടാൽ → single concept ആണോ check ചെയ്യൂ. Inseparable idea/pair = singular verb. ഈ test ചെയ്ക്കൂ: "X and Y" = one thing feel-ഓ? Yes = IS/WAS. No = ARE/WERE!

questionText: If they had requested me, I _____ them.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Would have helped

• If-clause-ൽ "had requested" = Past Perfect tense → ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്.
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് സംഭവിക്കാതെ പോയ ഒരു unreal situation describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Reality: "അവർ request ചെയ്തില്ല" — Imagined: "ചെയ്തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ ഞാൻ സഹായിച്ചേനേ."
• Main clause formula: would have + V3 → "would have helped" = correct answer.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 (if-clause) → would have + V3 (main clause) = Third Conditional
• Three Conditional types:
  1st: If + Present Simple → will + V1 (future real possibility)
  2nd: If + Past Simple → would + V1 (present/future unreal)
  3rd: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3 (past unreal — never happened)
• If-clause-ലെ tense → main clause decide ചെയ്യും:
  "had requested" = Past Perfect → 3rd Conditional → "would have helped"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 3rd Conditional "had + V3 → would have + V3" — PSC-ൽ most tested conditional pattern!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had studied, I would have passed the exam.
  2. If she had called me, I would have come immediately.
  3. If they had left early, they would have caught the train.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Would help
• "Would help" = 2nd Conditional main clause (If + Past Simple → would + V1).
• ഇവിടെ "had requested" = Past Perfect → 3rd Conditional → "would have helped" ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Would" ശരി തന്നെ — but "have" missing! 2nd vs 3rd Conditional mix-up ആണ് ഇവിടെ trap!

❌ (B) Will help
• "Will help" = 1st Conditional main clause (future real possibility).
• "Had requested" = Past Perfect → 3rd Conditional, future tense "will" ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Helping in future feel → "will" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But past perfect if-clause = 3rd conditional = would have!

❌ (C) Will have helped
• "Will have helped" = Future Perfect tense — conditional structure-ൽ ഇത് ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ല.
• Conditional main clause-ൽ "will" അല്ല "would" ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Will have" = future complete feel → conditional context-ൽ wrong. Would have = conditional, will have = future perfect!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "HAD requested → WOULD HAVE helped — HAD triggers WOULD HAVE!" — if-clause-ൽ HAD കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ main clause-ൽ WOULD HAVE!
• Word family: 1st: will + V1 | 2nd: would + V1 | 3rd: would have + V3 — ഈ three formulas ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Conditional question കണ്ടാൽ → if-clause tense ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യൂ. "Had + V3" = 3rd conditional = "would have + V3." Automatic answer!

questionText:
Raju is __a person who builds in or works with stone__.
(Choose one word for the underlined phrase)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Mason

• "A person who builds in or works with stone" = Mason (കൽപ്പണിക്കാരൻ).
• Mason = stone, brick, concrete-ൽ work ചെയ്ത് walls, buildings build ചെയ്യുന്ന skilled worker.
• "Raju is a mason" = "Raju കൽപ്പണിക്കാരനാണ്."
• Etymology: Old French "masson" = stone worker — ഇതിൽ നിന്ന് English "mason" വന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Stone/brick worker = MASON — material identify ചെയ്തു one word choose ചെയ്ക്കൂ!
• PSC favourite craftsmen/workers one-word substitutes:
  Mason = stone/brick worker (കൽപ്പണിക്കാരൻ)
  Carpenter = wood worker (മരപ്പണിക്കാരൻ)
  Blacksmith = iron/steel worker (കൊല്ലൻ)
  Goldsmith = gold worker (തട്ടാൻ)
  Cobbler = shoe maker/repairer (ചെരുപ്പുകുത്തി)
  Potter = clay/pot maker (കുശവൻ)
  Weaver = cloth weaver (നെയ്ത്തുകാരൻ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Craftsmen one-word substitutes — PSC vocabulary-ൽ regular question, ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്താൽ easy!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The mason built a strong stone wall around the house.
  2. A skilled mason can carve intricate designs in granite.
  3. He trained as a mason and helped construct the temple.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Goldsmith
• "Goldsmith" = gold, silver-ൽ work ചെയ്ത് jewellery make ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• Stone work-മായി goldsmith-ന് ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Smith" suffix = skilled worker feel → stone smith = goldsmith? No! Goldsmith = gold only. Stone = mason!

❌ (C) Blacksmith
• "Blacksmith" = iron, steel heat ചെയ്ത് tools, horseshoes, weapons make ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• Stone work-ന് blacksmith-ൻ ആകില്ല — iron/metal ആണ് material.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Heavy manual work = blacksmith feel → but blacksmith = iron/metal, mason = stone!

❌ (D) Carpenter
• "Carpenter" = wood-ൽ work ചെയ്ത് furniture, doors, windows build ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ (മരപ്പണിക്കാരൻ).
• Stone work-ന് carpenter-ൻ ആകില്ല — wood ആണ് material.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Building/construction context → carpenter feel → but carpenter = wood, mason = stone!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "MaSon = MA(de of) Stone! Ma-SON sounds like STONE!" — mason = stone-ൽ ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന ആൾ!
• Word family: mason (stone) | carpenter (wood) | blacksmith (iron) | goldsmith (gold) | cobbler (shoes) — material → worker mapping ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: One-word substitute for craftsman → material identify ചെയ്യൂ first. Stone = mason, Wood = carpenter, Iron = blacksmith, Gold = goldsmith. Material-to-worker table ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്താൽ ഇത്തരം questions guaranteed!

questionText:
"Call the police", said the old lady to her son.
(Change into Indirect Speech)
optionA: The old lady asked her son for calling the police
optionB: The old lady commanded her son must call the police
optionC: The old lady commanded her son to call the police
optionD: The old lady commands her son to call the police

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The old lady commanded her son to call the police.

• "Call the police" = Imperative sentence (command) — direct speech-ൽ son-നോട് police-നെ വിളിക്കാൻ command ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Command indirect speech formula: reporting verb (commanded/ordered/told) + object + to + V1.
• "Said to her son" → reporting verb "commanded her son" ആകും.
• "The old lady commanded her son to call the police" = correct indirect speech structure.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Direct Command → Indirect: (commanded/ordered/told) + object + to + V1
• Sentence types in indirect speech:
  Statement: said/told that + sentence (tense backshift)
  Yes/No Question: asked if/whether + sentence
  Wh-Question: asked + wh-word + sentence
  Command/Request: commanded/ordered/told/asked + object + to + V1
• Negative command: told/ordered + object + NOT to + V1
  "Don't run" → He told him not to run.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative sentences in indirect speech — PSC-ൽ regular topic, command form ഓർക്കേണ്ടത് important!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. "Open the door." → She told him to open the door.
  2. "Don't be late." → He warned her not to be late.
  3. "Submit the report." → The boss ordered them to submit the report.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The old lady asked her son for calling the police
• "For calling" = wrong structure — command indirect speech-ൽ "for + gerund" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Correct structure: "commanded/asked + object + to + V1" ആണ്, "for + V-ing" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Ask for" = common phrase feel → "asked for calling" → but command = commanded/asked + to call!

❌ (B) The old lady commanded her son must call the police
• "Must" indirect speech-ൽ ഇങ്ങനെ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — command indirect = "commanded + object + to + V1."
• "Commanded her son must call" = grammatically incorrect structure.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Must" = strong command feel → commanded + must feel → but correct = commanded + to call!

❌ (D) The old lady commands her son to call the police
• "Commands" = present tense. Reporting verb "said" = past → backshift → "commanded" (past) ആകണം.
• Indirect speech-ൽ reporting verb past tense ആണ് → main verb-ഉം past ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Forget tense change → present "commands" use ചെയ്ക്കും. But past reporting verb = commanded!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Command → COMMANDED + object + TO do it! — C to C: Command → Commanded + To!" ഈ pattern ഓർത്താൽ മതി!
• Word family: Statements = said/told that | Questions = asked if/whether | Commands = commanded/ordered/told + to — ഈ 3 types ഒരുമിച്ച് practice ചെയ്യൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Direct speech-ൽ sentence type identify ചെയ്യൂ first. Command = commanded/ordered + object + to + V1. Tense check ചെയ്യൂ (past reporting verb = past main verb). Two steps, guaranteed mark!

questionText: Find the correct spelling from the following words.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) accommodation

• "Accommodation" = correctly spelled word — ഇതിൽ ഓർക്കേണ്ടത്: double 'c' AND double 'm'.
• a-c-c-o-m-m-o-d-a-t-i-o-n → "acc" + "omm" + "odation."
• "Accommodation" = lodging, place to stay — "താമസസ്ഥലം, വാസസ്ഥലം."
• ഈ word-ന്റെ double letters ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്താൽ spelling question-ൽ easily identify ചെയ്യാം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: acCOMModation = double-C (cc) AND double-M (mm) — TWO doubles!
• Letter breakdown: a → cc → o → mm → o → d → a → t → i → o → n
• Other words in question — what's wrong:
  ecsentric → correct: eccentric (e-cc-e-n-t-r-i-c, double-c, no 's')
  tution → correct: tuition (t-u-i-t-i-o-n, 'ui' not 'u')
  spacimen → correct: specimen (s-p-e-c-i-m-e-n, 'spe' not 'spa')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Correct spelling questions — PSC vocabulary section-ൽ guaranteed. Double-letter words PSC favourite!
• Word family: accommodate (verb) | accommodation (noun) | accommodating (adjective)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The hotel provided excellent accommodation for the guests.
  2. We need to find accommodation before the trip.
  3. The school accommodation was comfortable and clean.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) ecsentric
• Correct spelling: eccentric (e-cc-e-n-t-r-i-c).
• "Ecsentric" — 'cs' instead of 'cc' — double-c missing, wrong consonant cluster.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: /ek-SEN-trik/ pronunciation → 'ecs' sound → "ecsentric" write ചെയ്ക്കും. But correct = "eccentric" with double-c!

❌ (B) tution
• Correct spelling: tuition (t-u-i-t-i-o-n).
• "Tution" — 'ui' missing → "tu-i-tion" ആണ്, "tu-tion" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Quickly speak ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ "tution" sound ആകും — but written form = "tuition" with 'ui'!

❌ (D) spacimen
• Correct spelling: specimen (s-p-e-c-i-m-e-n).
• "Spacimen" — 'spa' instead of 'spe', extra 'a' — wrong vowel in first syllable.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Spa" sound familiar → "spacimen" write ചെയ്ക്കും. But correct = "spec-i-men" not "spac-i-men"!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "acCOMMOdation has TWO ROOMS — double-C and double-M like a hotel with extra ROOMS!" — hotel image ഓർത്താൽ cc + mm ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കില്ല!
• Word family: accommodate | accommodation | accommodating | unaccommodating — ഒരേ double-c + double-m pattern family!
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ double letters ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യൂ. accommodation = cc + mm. Most common spelling trap ഇതാണ്! ഒരിക്കൽ pattern ഓർത്താൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Varun's parents help him to __overcome__ his difficulties.
(Choose the appropriate phrasal verb of the underlined word)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) get over

• "Overcome" = ഒരു difficulty/obstacle conquer ചെയ്ത് മുന്നോട്ട് പോകൽ — "മറികടക്കൽ."
• Phrasal verb equivalent: "get over" = to successfully deal with or recover from a difficulty.
• "Help him to overcome his difficulties" = "Help him to get over his difficulties" — exact same meaning.
• "OVER" connection: over-come → get OVER — ഇരണ്ടിലും 'over' ഉണ്ട്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Overcome = GET OVER — both have OVER, both mean conquer/recover from difficulty
• "Get" phrasal verbs — PSC loves "get" family:
  get over = overcome, recover from (മറികടക്കൽ)
  get through = complete, survive a difficult period (കഴിഞ്ഞുപോകൽ)
  get along = have a good relationship (നല്ല ബന്ധം)
  get across = communicate a message (communicate ചെയ്യൽ)
  get by = manage with difficulty (ബുദ്ധിമുട്ടി manage ചെയ്യൽ)
  get up = rise from bed (എഴുന്നേൽക്കൽ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Get" phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ asked phrasal verb family!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She got over her fear of heights after therapy. (= overcame)
  2. It took months to get over the loss. (= overcome/recover)
  3. He finally got over his illness and returned to work. (= recovered from)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) get through
• "Get through" = successfully complete something, or survive a difficult period — ഒരു phase കഴിഞ്ഞുപോകൽ.
• "Overcome" = conquer/beat a specific difficulty — "get through" slightly different nuance ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Get through difficulties" = natural English feel → but "overcome" exact equivalent = "get over"!

❌ (C) get across
• "Get across" = to communicate a message or idea successfully — "idea മനസ്സിലാക്കിക്കൽ."
• Overcoming difficulties-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല — entirely different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Get across difficulties" = cross over feel → but get across = communication, not overcoming!

❌ (D) get along
• "Get along" = to have a friendly relationship with someone — "ഒരുമിച്ച് ഭംഗിയായി കഴിയൽ."
• Overcoming difficulties-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Getting along" = managing somehow feel → but correct phrasal verb = get over!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "OVER-come = GET OVER — OVER is the key! Both share OVER!" — overcome-ലെ 'over' = get over-ലെ 'over' — ഇത് ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Word family: get over | overcome | surmount | conquer | master — ഒരേ meaning family. Get over ആണ് phrasal verb!
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb question-ൽ given word-ൽ 'over/under/up/down' ഉണ്ടോ check ചെയ്യൂ. "Overcome" → over → "get over." Pattern match ചെയ്ക്കൂ!

questionText:
Sachin played well, _____ ?
(Use appropriate question tag)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) didn't he

• "Sachin played well" = Positive sentence, Past Simple tense.
• Question tag rule: Positive sentence → Negative tag ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• "Played" = past simple verb → auxiliary "did" → negative = "didn't."
• Subject "Sachin" = singular male → pronoun "he."
• Complete tag: "didn't he?" — "Sachin നന്നായി കളിച്ചു, അല്ലേ?" എന്ന് Malayalam meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive sentence → Negative tag | Negative sentence → Positive tag
• Past Simple-ന്റെ question tag:
  Positive sentence (played/went/came) → "didn't + pronoun?"
  Negative sentence (didn't play) → "did + pronoun?"
• Auxiliary verb matching:
  Present Simple → do/does (don't/doesn't)
  Past Simple → did (didn't)
  Present Perfect → have/has (haven't/hasn't)
  Future → will (won't)
  "is/am/are/was/were" → same verb, negated
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags — PSC grammar section-ൽ ഒരു paper-ഉം miss ആകാതെ വരുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She played well, didn't she? (positive → negative tag)
  2. They finished the work, didn't they? (positive → negative tag)
  3. He didn't come, did he? (negative → positive tag)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) did he
• "Did he" = positive tag. "Sachin played well" = positive sentence → negative tag ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.
• Positive sentence + positive tag = WRONG. Opposite ആണ് വേണ്ടത്!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Same tense auxiliary "did" → "did he" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But positive sentence = negative tag "didn't he"!

❌ (C) do he
• "Do he" = grammatically incorrect. "He" third person singular → "does/doesn't."
• കൂടാതെ "played" = past tense → "did/didn't" ആണ്, present "do" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Do" familiar auxiliary → "do he" feel — but "he" = does/doesn't, past tense = did/didn't!

❌ (D) don't he
• "Don't he" grammatically wrong — "he" → "doesn't" (not "don't"). "Don't" = I/you/we/they-ന്.
• Plus "played" = past tense → present tense "don't" ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Negative tag feel → "don't" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But past tense + he = didn't he!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "+sentence → -tag, -sentence → +tag! ALWAYS OPPOSITE!" — ഈ golden rule ഓർത്താൽ question tag questions guaranteed!
• Word family: played (past) → didn't | plays (present, he) → doesn't | will play → won't | has played → hasn't — tense-to-tag matching list ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Step 1: sentence positive-ഓ negative-ഓ? Step 2: tense identify. Step 3: opposite polarity tag + correct auxiliary. ഈ 3 steps follow ചെയ്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും question tag miss ആകില്ല!

questionText: 'The door must be kept locked' is an example of:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Imperative sentence

• "The door must be kept locked" = ഒരു instruction/obligation express ചെയ്യുന്ന sentence.
• "Must be kept" = ഒരു directive ആണ് — ആരോ ഇത് follow ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് ആവശ്യപ്പെടുന്നു.
• PSC classification: "must/should" ഉപയോഗിച്ച് obligation/instruction express ചെയ്യുന്ന sentences = Imperative in function.
• ഉദ്ദേശം: "വാതിൽ പൂട്ടിയിരിക്കണം" — ഇത് ഒരു rule/instruction ആണ്, simple fact അല്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Command/Instruction/Obligation express ചെയ്യുന്ന sentence → Imperative
• 4 types of sentences:
  Assertive (Declarative): fact/statement — "The door is locked."
  Imperative: command/instruction/obligation — "Lock the door." / "The door must be kept locked."
  Interrogative: question — "Is the door locked?"
  Exclamatory: strong emotion — "What a strong door!"
• Imperative forms (direct and indirect):
  Direct: "Keep the door locked." (base verb, no subject shown)
  Passive imperative: "The door must be kept locked." (same instruction, passive form)
  Modal: "You must lock the door." (must/should = obligation = imperative in meaning)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Types of sentences — PSC-ൽ regular, especially passive/modal imperatives tricky questions!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Keep the door locked. (direct imperative)
  2. Students must submit assignments on time. (modal imperative — obligation)
  3. Silence must be maintained in the library. (passive imperative — rule/instruction)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Assertive sentence
• Assertive = simple fact/statement — "The door is locked." (fact).
• "The door must be kept locked" = obligation/instruction — simple fact അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Subject "the door" ഉണ്ട് → assertive feel. But "must be kept" = instruction/obligation = imperative!

❌ (C) Exclamatory sentence
• Exclamatory = strong emotion (surprise, joy, anger) — exclamation mark (!) ഉണ്ടാകും.
• "The door must be kept locked" — emotion ഒന്നും ഇല്ല, instruction ആണ്. No exclamation mark.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Must" = strong emphasis feel → emotional? No — must = obligation = imperative, emotion അല്ല!

❌ (D) Interrogative sentence
• Interrogative = question — question mark (?) ഉണ്ടാകും, question word/inversion ഉണ്ടാകും.
• "The door must be kept locked" = no question mark, no inversion — clearly not interrogative.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easily eliminate — no question mark, sentence structure question form അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "MUST = MUST obey = IMPERATIVE! Must/should obligation sentence കണ്ടാൽ → Imperative!" — must = ആജ്ഞ = imperative!
• Word family: "Keep the door locked" / "The door must be kept locked" / "Lock the door!" — ഒരേ meaning, different forms, all imperative!
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence type question-ൽ → emotion ഉണ്ടോ = exclamatory, question ആണോ = interrogative, instruction/must/should = imperative, simple fact = assertive. ഈ 4-point check ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ sentence types guaranteed!

questionText:
Pick out the adjective from the given sentence.
I am a clever boy.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) clever

• "Clever" = "boy" എന്ന noun-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word — "ബുദ്ധിശാലിയായ."
• Adjective = noun-നെ describe/modify ചെയ്യുന്ന word.
• "What kind of boy?" → "clever boy" → "clever" ആണ് adjective.
• "I am a clever boy" = "ഞാൻ ഒരു ബുദ്ധിശാലിയായ ആൺകുട്ടി ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: NOUN-നെ describe/qualify ചെയ്യുന്ന word = ADJECTIVE
• Sentence-ലെ ഓരോ word-ന്റേയും part of speech:
  I = Pronoun (subject)
  am = Verb (linking verb — "be" verb)
  a = Article (determiner)
  clever = Adjective (describes "boy" — what kind of boy?)
  boy = Noun (the person being described)
• Adjective types PSC students ഓർക്കണം:
  Quality adjective: clever, tall, beautiful, honest (noun-ന്റെ quality describe ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  Quantity adjective: some, many, much, little
  Number adjective: two, first, several
  Demonstrative adjective: this, that, these, those
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of speech identification — PSC grammar-ൽ easy marks, ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകരുത്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is a beautiful girl. (beautiful = adjective, describes girl)
  2. He bought an expensive car. (expensive = adjective, describes car)
  3. The clever student solved the problem. (clever = adjective, describes student)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) boy
• "Boy" = Noun — person/thing-നെ name ചെയ്യുന്ന word. Adjective = noun-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word.
• "Boy" = described ആകുന്ന word. "Clever" = describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word = adjective.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Clever boy" — important word "boy" feel → noun choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But boy = noun, clever = adjective!

❌ (B) a
• "A" = Article — ഒരു type of determiner ആണ്. Articles adjective അല്ല — separate category.
• Article "a/an/the" = noun-ന് മുമ്പ് വരുന്ന special words, adjective-ൽ നിന്ന് different.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "A" noun-ന് മുമ്പ് വന്ന് modify ചെയ്യുന്ന feel → adjective feel. But article = its own category!

❌ (D) am
• "Am" = Verb (linking verb — "be" family). "I am" = subject + linking verb.
• Verb ≠ Adjective. Verb = action/state express ചെയ്യുന്നു. Adjective = noun describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Am" ഒരു important word — verb ആണെന്ന് clearly obvious, adjective question-ൽ confuse ആകരുത്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Adjective ADDs quality to a NOUN — clever ADDS quality to BOY!" — adjective = adder of qualities!
• Word family: noun (boy/girl/city) | verb (am/is/run) | adjective (clever/tall/good) | article (a/an/the) — ഈ 4 parts ഒരു sentence-ൽ identify ചെയ്യാൻ practice ചെയ്യൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "Pick out the adjective" question-ൽ → "What kind of [noun]?" question ചോദിക്കൂ. Answer ആകുന്ന word = adjective. "What kind of boy?" → "clever" → adjective. Simple test!

questionText: The gender of the word 'Duchess' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) feminine

• "Duchess" = Duke-ന്റെ ഭാര്യ, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ duchess rank hold ചെയ്യുന്ന സ്ത്രീ.
• "Duchess" clearly female → feminine gender.
• Masculine counterpart: Duke | Feminine counterpart: Duchess — royal title pair.
• "The Duchess of Cambridge" = Kate Middleton (ഉദാഹരണം) — feminine gender confirm ആകും.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "-ess" ending = almost always FEMININE! Duchess, Princess, Actress, Hostess...
• PSC favourite gender pairs:
  Duke → Duchess (ഡ്യൂക്ക് → ഡച്ചസ്)
  Prince → Princess | King → Queen | Lord → Lady | Emperor → Empress
  Actor → Actress | Poet → Poetess | Host → Hostess | Heir → Heiress
  Widower → Widow | Bachelor → Spinster | Drone → Queen (bee)
• 4 genders in English:
  Masculine = male (king, actor, duke)
  Feminine = female (queen, actress, duchess)
  Common = both male and female (teacher, student, doctor, friend)
  Neuter = non-living things (table, book, city, pen)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Gender pairs — PSC vocabulary-ൽ guaranteed question. Royal titles especially!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The Duke and the Duchess attended the ceremony. (male → female pair)
  2. The actress won the best performance award. (actor → actress)
  3. The empress ruled the kingdom wisely. (emperor → empress)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) masculine
• "Masculine" = male gender. "Duchess" = exclusively female title — ഒരു male-ന് ഒരിക്കലും "duchess" ആകില്ല.
• Male equivalent = "Duke." Female = "Duchess."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Royal/powerful title → masculine feel. But "-ess" suffix = feminine! Duchess = female only!

❌ (B) common
• "Common gender" = both male and female ആകാവുന്ന words — teacher, student, friend, doctor.
• "Duchess" = exclusively female → common gender ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Common" = general/neutral feel → safe option ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും. But duchess = women only = feminine!

❌ (C) neuter
• "Neuter gender" = non-living things, objects — table, chair, pen, city, country.
• "Duchess" = a person (female) → ഒരിക്കലും neuter ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar word → gender unsure → "neuter" (unknown) feel. But neuter = things only, not people!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "DUKe → DUCHess — Duke-ന്റെ wife = Duchess = FEMININE! '-ESS' = FEMALE!" — ess ending ഓർത്താൽ gender questions easy!
• Word family: Duke/Duchess | King/Queen | Prince/Princess | Lord/Lady | Emperor/Empress — royal pairs list ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "-ess" ending word കണ്ടാൽ → feminine gender. Duchess, Princess, Actress, Empress, Lioness — all feminine. ഈ one rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ gender questions guaranteed mark!

questionText: He is neither smart _____ brave.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) nor

• "Neither" = correlative conjunction ആണ്. ഇതിന്റെ pair ALWAYS "nor" — മാറ്റമില്ല.
• "Neither smart nor brave" = "smart-ഉം അല്ല, brave-ഉം അല്ല" — രണ്ടും negative.
• "He is neither smart nor brave" = "അദ്ദേഹം ബുദ്ധിശാലിയുമല്ല, ധൈര്യശാലിയുമല്ല."
• Neither...or / neither...and / neither...but = WRONG combinations. Neither...nor ONLY!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: NEITHER always pairs with NOR — N with N! Never mix!
• Correlative conjunction pairs (PSC most tested):
  either ... or (ഇതല്ലെങ്കിൽ അത്)
  neither ... nor (ഇതുമല്ല അതുമല്ല)
  both ... and (ഇതും അതും)
  not only ... but also (ഇത് മാത്രമല്ല, അതും)
  no sooner ... than (ഉടനെ ... ആകുമ്പോൾ)
  scarcely/hardly ... when (ഏതാണ്ട് ... ആകുമ്പോൾ)
  whether ... or (... ആണോ ... ആണോ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Correlative conjunctions — PSC grammar-ൽ must-know topic, ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ easy!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He is neither tall nor short. (neither...nor)
  2. She speaks neither Hindi nor Tamil. (neither...nor)
  3. Either you go or I go. (either...or — contrast with neither...nor)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) or
• "Neither...or" = wrong pair. "Or" = "either"-ന്റെ partner — "either...or" ✓.
• "Neither" കണ്ടാൽ → "nor" മാത്രം. "Or" ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Either...or familiar → neither...or feel. But neither = NOR, either = OR. N pairs with N!

❌ (B) and
• "Neither...and" = wrong pair — ഇങ്ങനൊരു correlative conjunction exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• "Both...and" ✓ — but neither...and = incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "And" join ചെയ്യുന്ന feel → "neither smart and brave" feel → but "and" = "both"-ന്റെ pair, "neither"-ന്റേത് അല്ല!

❌ (C) but
• "Neither...but" = wrong pair — ഇങ്ങനൊരു structure exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• "Not only...but also" ✓ — but "neither...but" = incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "But" = contrast feel → "neither smart but brave" contrast mean ആകും feel → but correlative pair = neither...NOR only!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NEITHER goes with NOR — N with N, Both start with N — they are NEIGHBOURS!" — N-N pair ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!
• Word family: either-OR | neither-NOR | both-AND | not only-BUT ALSO — ഈ 4 pairs complete list. Exam-ൽ ഇതിൽ ഒന്ന് ഉറപ്പ് വരും!
• PSC exam strategy: Correlative question-ൽ → first word identify ചെയ്യൂ → pair select ചെയ്യൂ. Neither = nor. Either = or. Both = and. Not only = but also. First word കണ്ടാൽ pair automatic!

questionText:
Give the correct verb form.
Seven hundred rupees _____ too much for this sari.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) is

• "Seven hundred rupees" = ഒരു amount of money — ഒരൊറ്റ sum ആണ്, separate rupee coins/notes അല്ല.
• Grammar rule: Money, weight, distance, time — amounts/measurements singular entity ആണ് → singular verb.
• "Seven hundred rupees is too much" = "ഏഴുനൂറ് രൂപ ഈ സാരിക്ക് വളരെ കൂടുതലാണ്."
• "Seven hundred rupees" = ഒരൊറ്റ price → singular verb "is" ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Money/Weight/Distance/Time ഒരു unit ആയി treat ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ → Singular verb
• Amounts as single unit (singular):
  Five hundred rupees IS enough. (one price)
  Two kilometres IS a long walk. (one distance)
  Three hours IS a long time. (one duration)
  Ten kilograms IS the limit. (one weight)
• Compare — when separate items (plural):
  Five rupee coins ARE in my pocket. (separate coins)
  Three hours HAVE passed. (hours passing one by one)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Money/measurement subject + singular verb — PSC Subject-Verb Agreement-ൽ classic trick!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Two hundred rupees is not enough for the trip.
  2. Ten kilometres is the distance to the station.
  3. Three weeks is a short time to learn a language.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) are
• "Are" = plural verb. "Seven hundred rupees" = one sum/amount → singular.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Rupees" plural noun → "are" plural feel. But amount as single unit = singular "is"!

❌ (C) has
• "Has" = Present Perfect auxiliary — ഇവിടെ simple statement ആണ്, Perfect tense വേണ്ടതില്ല.
• "Seven hundred rupees has too much" = grammatically incorrect structure.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Singular feel → "has" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But "has" auxiliary verb, linking verb "is" ആണ് ഇവിടെ correct!

❌ (D) has been
• "Has been" = Present Perfect Continuous/Passive. Past context ഇല്ല, ongoing situation ഇല്ല.
• Simple present statement → "is" ആണ് correct, "has been" ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Formal sound → "has been" educated feel. But simple present fact = "is"!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "AMOUNT = ONE UNIT = IS! 500 rupees is ONE price, not 500 separate things!" — amount → singular → is!
• Word family: money | weight | distance | time | measurement — ഇവ amounts ആയി use ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ALWAYS singular!
• PSC exam strategy: Subject = money/distance/time/weight amount-ഓ? → singular verb. "Rupees/kilometres/hours/kilos" amount context-ൽ = IS/WAS. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Choose the best antonym for thrifty.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) extravagant

• "Thrifty" = careful with money, not wasteful — "മിതവ്യയിയായ, ചെലവ് ചുരുക്കുന്ന."
• Antonym = opposite meaning → "Extravagant" = wasteful, spending too much — "ധൂർത്തൻ, അമിതചെലവ്."
• Thrifty person = every rupee save ചെയ്യും | Extravagant person = money unnecessarily spend ചെയ്യും.
• ഒരു word-ൽ remember: "thrifty = thrift shop = money save ചെയ്ത് ജീവിക്കൽ" → antonym = extravagant!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Thrifty = economical/careful with money → Antonym = Extravagant (wasteful/overspending)
• Word family:
  thrifty (adj) = economical | thrift (noun) = careful use of money | thriftily (adv)
  extravagant (adj) = wasteful | extravagance (noun) | extravagantly (adv)
• Synonyms of thrifty: frugal | economical | sparing | careful | prudent
• Antonyms of thrifty: extravagant | wasteful | lavish | profligate | spendthrift
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Money-related adjective antonyms — PSC vocabulary-ൽ regular. Thrifty/extravagant pair important!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is thrifty — she saves half her salary every month.
  2. He is extravagant — he spends money on things he doesn't need.
  3. Thrift is a virtue; extravagance is a vice.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) deceit
• "Deceit" = dishonesty, deception — "വഞ്ചന." Money habits-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Thrifty person = sly/deceptive feel? No — thrifty = careful with money. Deceit = entirely different!

❌ (C) turbulent
• "Turbulent" = chaotic, stormy, unstable — "കലുഷിതം." Personality/weather describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word.
• Thrifty = money habit → antonym should be money-related. Turbulent ≠ antonym of thrifty.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Opposite-of-calm feel → turbulent choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But thrifty = money habit, not calmness!

❌ (D) baffled
• "Baffled" = confused, puzzled — "കുഴങ്ങിപ്പോയ." Emotion/mental state word.
• Thrifty = spending behavior → antonym = spending behavior opposite. Baffled = mental state, ബന്ധമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Baffled = lost/confused = opposite of knowing what to do. But not antonym of thrifty!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "THRIFT store = save money. EXTRA-vagant = EXTRA spending! Thrifty ↔ Extravagant — one saves, one spends EXTRA!"
• Word family: thrifty/frugal/economical (save money) ↔ extravagant/lavish/wasteful (spend too much) — two opposite camps!
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ word-ന്റെ core meaning identify ചെയ്യൂ. Thrifty = save money → antonym = spend money = extravagant. Direct opposite dimension!

questionText:
Remember to switch off the fan, _____ ?
(Choose the appropriate tag)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Won't you?

• "Remember to switch off the fan" = Imperative sentence (command/request).
• Imperative sentences-ന്റെ question tag: "will you?" (polite request) അല്ലെങ്കിൽ "won't you?" (stronger/expectation).
• "Remember to..." = politely asking someone to do something → "Won't you?" tag ✓
• "Remember to switch off the fan, won't you?" = "ഫാൻ ഓഫ് ചെയ്യാൻ ഓർക്കുന്നുണ്ടാകും, അല്ലേ?
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Imperative sentence → Question tag = "will you?" or "won't you?"
• Imperative tag rules:
  Positive imperative (do something) → "will you?" (neutral) or "won't you?" (expectation/request)
  Negative imperative (don't do something) → "will you?"
  "Let's..." → "shall we?"
  "Let him/her/them..." → "will you?"
• Why "won't you?" for "Remember to...": speaker expects the listener will comply → "won't you?" shows that expectation.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative sentence question tags — PSC-ൽ tricky, most students confuse. "Will you / won't you" = imperative tag!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Close the door, will you? (neutral request)
  2. Please be on time, won't you? (expectation)
  3. Don't make noise, will you? (negative imperative)
  4. Let's go for a walk, shall we? (let's suggestion)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Did you?
• "Did you?" = Past Simple positive tag. "Remember to switch off the fan" = present imperative, past tense ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Remember" = past memory feel → "did you?" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But imperative tag = will/won't, not did!

❌ (C) Doesn't he?
• "Doesn't he?" = present simple tag for third person (he/she/it). Subject here = "you" (implied in imperative).
• Imperative-ൽ implied subject = "you" → tag-ൽ "you" ഉണ്ടാകണം, "he" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "He" feels formal/correct → "doesn't he?" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But imperative tag = won't you, subject = you!

❌ (D) Haven't you?
• "Haven't you?" = Present Perfect negative tag. Imperative sentence-ന് Perfect tense tag ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Remember" = experience/memory = perfect tense feel → "haven't you?" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But imperative = won't you!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "IMPERATIVE = WILL YOU / WON'T YOU? — Command ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ WILL you comply? WILL YOU!" — will/won't = compliance question for imperatives!
• Word family: Close the door, will you? | Be quiet, won't you? | Don't run, will you? | Let's eat, shall we? — imperative tag family!
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence = imperative (command/request) ആണോ check ചെയ്യൂ → tag = "will you?" or "won't you?" Let's = "shall we?" ഈ 2 rules ഓർത്താൽ imperative tags always correct!

questionText:
Choose the correct preposition.
We celebrate Independence Day _____ 15 August.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) on

• "15 August" = ഒരു specific date — day/date-ന് "on" ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Time preposition rule: specific date/day → "on" | month/year/season → "in" | exact time → "at"
• "We celebrate Independence Day on 15 August" = "നാം ഓഗസ്റ്റ് 15-ന് സ്വാതന്ത്ര്യദിനം ആഘോഷിക്കുന്നു."
• "On 15 August" ✓ — "On Independence Day" ✓ — specific day = "on" always!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: AT (exact time) → ON (day/date) → IN (month/year/season) — small to big!
• Time preposition chart:
  AT: at 3 o'clock | at noon | at midnight | at the weekend (British)
  ON: on Monday | on 15 August | on Independence Day | on Christmas Day
  IN: in August | in 2025 | in summer | in the morning/evening
• Memory: AT = pinpoint time | ON = calendar day/date | IN = longer period
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: AT/ON/IN for time — PSC-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകാതെ വരുന്ന preposition question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The match starts at 5 pm. (exact time = at)
  2. We met on Sunday. (day = on)
  3. She was born in July 1990. (month + year = in)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) in
• "In" = month/year/season/longer period — "in August" ✓ but "in 15 August" ✗.
• Specific date "15 August" → "on" ആണ്, "in" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "August" month name → "in August" familiar → "in 15 August" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But specific date = ON!

❌ (C) at
• "At" = exact clock time (at 3 pm, at noon). Dates-ന് "at" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "At 15 August" = grammatically incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Specific = "at" feel (pinpoint) → but "at" = clock time only, not dates!

❌ (D) by
• "By" = not later than, deadline — "by 3 pm" (before 3 pm). Time preposition for dates ആകില്ല.
• "By 15 August" = "ഓഗസ്റ്റ് 15-ന് മുമ്പ്" — completely different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "By" = time reference feel → but "by" = deadline, not celebration date!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "AT the clock → ON the calendar → IN the diary/almanac! Small→Medium→Large!" — time size increases: AT < ON < IN!
• Word family: at 5 pm | on Monday | on 15 August | in August | in 2025 — ഒരുമിച്ച് practice ചെയ്യൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Time blank കണ്ടാൽ → clock time = AT, day/date = ON, month/year/season = IN. "15 August" = date = ON. Instant answer!

questionText:
She was mending socks.
(Choose the correct passive form)
optionC: Socks were being mended by her
optionD: Socks have been mended by her

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Socks were being mended by her.

• Active: "She was mending socks" = Past Continuous tense (was + V-ing).
• Passive formula for Past Continuous: was/were + being + V3 (past participle).
• Object (socks) → Subject | Subject (she) → "by her" | plural socks → "were."
• "Socks were being mended by her" = "അവൾ കാൽപ്പുരകൾ തുന്നിക്കൊണ്ടിരിക്കുകയായിരുന്നു." ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Past Continuous Passive = was/were + being + V3
• Passive voice tense chart:
  Simple Present: is/am/are + V3
  Simple Past: was/were + V3
  Present Continuous: is/am/are + being + V3
  Past Continuous: was/were + being + V3
  Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3
  Simple Future: will + be + V3
• "Being" = Continuous tenses-ൽ ONLY ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു (present continuous, past continuous).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Past Continuous passive "were being + V3" — PSC-ൽ regular, "being" keyword ഓർക്കണം!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She was writing a letter. → A letter was being written by her.
  2. They were building a house. → A house was being built by them.
  3. He was repairing the car. → The car was being repaired by him.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Socks are mending by her
• "Are mending" = Present Continuous Active — passive form അല്ല. "By her" ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും active form wrong.
• Correct passive needs "being mended" — "are mending" passive form അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "By her" ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് passive feel → but "are mending" = active voice, passive form wrong!

❌ (B) Socks were mended by her
• "Were mended" = Simple Past Passive — active sentence Past Continuous ആണ് ("was mending").
• Past Continuous passive = "were being mended," Simple Past passive "were mended" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Were mended" = past passive sound → most students ഇത് choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But continuous action = "being" needed!

❌ (D) Socks have been mended by her
• "Have been mended" = Present Perfect Passive. Active sentence-ൽ Past Continuous ആണ് — tense wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Have been" = formal/complete feel → but tense mismatch. Past Continuous → "were being mended"!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Continuous tense passive-ൽ BEING കൂടെ ഉണ്ടാകും! was/were + BEING + V3 — BEING = ongoing/continuous!" — being = still happening!
• Word family: is/are being done (present cont.) | was/were being done (past cont.) — continuous passive family!
• PSC exam strategy: Active sentence-ൽ "was/were + V-ing" (past continuous) കണ്ടാൽ → passive = "was/were + being + V3." "Being" keyword missing = wrong option. Simple!

questionText: The meaning of the idiom 'at sixes and sevens' is:
optionA: Achieve two results with one effort

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) In a state of confusion

• "At sixes and sevens" = കുഴഞ്ഞുമറിഞ്ഞ അവസ്ഥ, disorder-ഉം confusion-ഉം ഉള്ള state.
• Origin: Dice game-ൽ 6, 7 highest numbers — both players simultaneously aim for these → chaos!
• "The office was at sixes and sevens" = "ഓഫീസ് മുഴുവൻ കുഴഞ്ഞുമറിഞ്ഞ അവസ്ഥയിലായിരുന്നു."
• Meaning: disorganised, in disorder, everything mixed up — "ക്രമമില്ലാത്ത, confused state.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: At sixes and sevens = confused/disordered state = "കുഴഞ്ഞുമറിഞ്ഞ അവസ്ഥ"
• Full meaning: in a state of total confusion or disorder — nothing is organised
• PSC favourite idioms about confusion/disorder:
  at sixes and sevens = in a state of confusion
  in a fix = in a difficult/confused situation
  at a loss = confused, not knowing what to do
  up in the air = uncertain, not yet decided
  all at sea = completely confused/lost
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Confusion/disorder idioms — PSC-ൽ regular. "At sixes and sevens" PSC classic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. After the manager left, the team was at sixes and sevens.
  2. The house was at sixes and sevens before the guests arrived.
  3. Without a plan, everything was at sixes and sevens.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Achieve two results with one effort
• ഇത് "kill two birds with one stone" idiom-ന്റെ meaning ആണ് — "at sixes and sevens"-ന്റേത് അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Numbers (six, seven) = achieve/score feel → two results with one effort? No! Numbers here = confusion!

❌ (C) Be highly successful
• "Be highly successful" = entirely opposite meaning. "At sixes and sevens" = confused/disordered, success അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: High numbers (6, 7) = high achievement feel → success choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But idiom = confusion, not success!

❌ (D) Do everything one can
• "Do everything one can" = "bite the bullet" or "pull out all stops"-ന്റെ feel. "At sixes and sevens" ≠ maximum effort.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 6+7 = maximum numbers = maximum effort feel → but idiom = confusion/disorder!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "6 and 7 fighting = CHAOS and CONFUSION! Six says 'me first!' Seven says 'no, me!' = utter confusion!" — numbers fighting = confusion!
• Word family: at sixes and sevens | in a fix | at a loss | all at sea | up in the air — "confusion" idiom family ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom-ൽ numbers/unusual combinations കണ്ടാൽ → literal meaning eliminate ചെയ്യൂ → figurative meaning (usually confusion/difficulty) choose ചെയ്യൂ. "At sixes and sevens" = always confusion!

questionText: Choose the correctly spelled word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Circumlocution

• "Circumlocution" = correct spelling — c-i-r-c-u-m-l-o-c-u-t-i-o-n.
• Meaning: using many words where few would do — "ചുറ്റിവളഞ്ഞ് പറയൽ, വാക്കുകൾ ധാരാളം ഉപയോഗിച്ച് simple thing പറയൽ."
• Root: Latin "circum" (around) + "locutio" (speech) = speaking around a topic = circumlocution.
• Other options-ൽ spelling errors ഉണ്ട് — A മാത്രം correct!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Circumlocution = circum (around) + locution (speech) = "around speech" = indirect/roundabout talking
• Letter breakdown: c-i-r-c-u-m | l-o-c-u-t-i-o-n → two parts joined
• Other words — spelling errors identified:
  Pavilien → correct: Pavilion (p-a-v-i-l-i-o-n, ends in "-ion" not "-ien")
  Drowcy → correct: Drowsy (d-r-o-w-s-y, ends in "-sy" not "-cy")
  Perenniel → correct: Perennial (p-e-r-e-n-n-i-a-l, ends in "-al" not "-el")
• ⭐ PSC ആavർttnam: Latin/Greek root words spelling — PSC-ൽ regular, roots ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ spelling easy!
• Word family: circumlocution (noun) | circumlocutory (adj) | circumlocute (verb — rare)
• Related "circum" words: circumference | circumstances | circumspect | circumnavigate
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Pavilien
• Correct spelling: Pavilion (p-a-v-i-l-i-o-n).
• "Pavilien" — ends in "-ien" instead of "-ion." French origin → English "-ion" ending.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: French-origin word → "-ien" French feel → but English spelling = "-ion" (pavilion)!

❌ (C) Drowcy
• Correct spelling: Drowsy (d-r-o-w-s-y).
• "Drowcy" — "-cy" instead of "-sy." Drowsy = feeling sleepy — s-y ending, not c-y.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "-cy" ending (like policy, fancy) feel → but drowsy = "-sy" ending. Sound similarity misleads!

❌ (D) Perenniel
• Correct spelling: Perennial (p-e-r-e-n-n-i-a-l).
• "Perenniel" — "-iel" instead of "-ial." Double 'n' + "-ial" ending = correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "-iel" (like Daniel) feel → but perennial = "-ial" ending. Latin root "-alis" → "-al" in English!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "CIRCUMlocution = CIRCUM (circle/around) + LOCUTION (talking) = talking AROUND the point!" — Latin roots help spell AND understand!
• Word family: circumlocution | circumference | circumspect | circumstances — "circum" = around. ഈ family ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ spelling easy!
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ → root language identify ചെയ്യൂ. Latin/Greek roots spelling = predictable patterns. "circum+locution" = around+speech = correct split. Root knowledge = spelling confidence!

questionText:
Mrs. Kamala said to Raju, "Place the dishes on the table".
(Choose the most appropriate indirect speech)
optionA: Mrs. Kamala asked Raju to place the dishes on the table.
optionB: Mrs. Kamala told Raju that he placed the dishes on the table.
optionC: Mrs. Kamala asked Raju that he could place the dishes on the table.
optionD: Mrs. Kamala told Raju that he had placed the dishes on the table.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Mrs. Kamala asked Raju to place the dishes on the table.

• "Place the dishes on the table" = Imperative sentence (command/instruction).
• Indirect speech of command: reported verb (asked/told/ordered) + object + to + V1.
• "Said to Raju" → "asked Raju" (reporting verb change for polite command).
• "Mrs. Kamala asked Raju to place the dishes on the table" = correct indirect structure ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Direct Command → Indirect: asked/told/ordered + object + to + V1
• "Said to" → reporting verb change:
  Polite command → asked + object + to + V1
  Strong command → ordered/commanded + object + to + V1
  Request → requested + object + to + V1
• Key changes in indirect speech for commands:
  Inverted commas removed | "said to" → "asked/told/ordered"
  Verb → to + infinitive form | No tense backshift for imperatives
• ⭐ PSC ആaavർttnam: Imperative indirect speech — PSC-ൽ regular. "asked/told + to + V1" pattern ഓർക്കണം!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. "Open the window." → He told me to open the window.
  2. "Please sit down." → She asked them to sit down.
  3. "Submit the report." → The boss ordered him to submit the report.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Mrs. Kamala told Raju that he placed the dishes on the table.
• "That he placed" = indirect statement form (for declarative sentences), command-ന് അല്ല.
• Command indirect speech-ൽ "that + clause" structure ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — "to + V1" ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Told that" = familiar indirect speech pattern → but commands = told/asked + to + V1, not "that"!

❌ (C) Mrs. Kamala asked Raju that he could place the dishes on the table.
• "That he could place" = wrong structure. "Asked + that + could" = non-standard/incorrect.
• Command indirect speech = "asked + object + to + V1" — "that + could" ഇവിടെ ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Could" = polite feel → but indirect command = asked + to place, not "that he could place"!

❌ (D) Mrs. Kamala told Raju that he had placed the dishes on the table.
• "Had placed" = past perfect backshift — statement indirect speech-ന്. Command-ന് "to + V1" ആണ്.
• "That he had placed" = reporting a past fact, not a command.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Tense backshift rule → "had placed" formal feel → but commands = to + V1, no tense backshift!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Command → TO do it! Asked Raju TO place — TO connects the command!" — command indirect = always TO + V1!
• Word family: asked/told/ordered/commanded/requested + object + TO + V1 — reporting verbs for commands!
• PSC exam strategy: Direct speech-ൽ inverted comma-ൽ command ഉണ്ടോ check ചെയ്യൂ → indirect = asked/told + object + to + V1. "That clause" = statements only. "To + V1" = commands only. ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed!

questionText: The meaning of the word 'alma mater' is:
optionB: The university, school or college that one formerly attended

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The university, school or college that one formerly attended.

• "Alma mater" = Latin phrase — "alma" (nourishing) + "mater" (mother) = "nourishing mother."
• ആലങ്കാരികമായി: ഒരാൾ പഠിച്ചിരുന്ന school/college/university — "വിദ്യാഭ്യാസ ജന്മഭൂമി."
• "My alma mater is Government College, Thrissur" = "ഞാൻ പഠിച്ച കലാലയം Government College, Thrissur ആണ്."
• PSC exams-ൽ Latin phrases = exact meaning ഓർക്കണം — "alma mater" = former educational institution!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Alma mater = one's former school/college/university (Latin: "nourishing mother")
• Etymology: Latin alma (nourishing, kind) + mater (mother) → institution that "nourished" your education
• PSC favourite Latin/foreign phrases:
  alma mater = one's former educational institution
  carte blanche = complete freedom to act as one wishes
  status quo = the existing state of affairs
  per se = by itself, intrinsically
  in lieu of = instead of
  bona fide = genuine, real, in good faith
  viva voce = oral examination
  post mortem = examination after death / analysis after an event
• ⭐ PSC ആaavർttnam: Latin/foreign phrases — PSC vocabulary section-ൽ regular feature!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He donated generously to his alma mater.
  2. She returned to her alma mater to teach.
  3. The alumni association of his alma mater meets every year.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Master piece
• "Masterpiece" = outstanding work of art or skill — "മഹത്തായ സൃഷ്ടി." Alma mater-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Alma" = alma = art/culture sound → masterpiece feel. But alma = nourishing (Latin), not art!

❌ (C) In the memory of
• "In the memory of" = "In memoriam" (Latin) — another Latin phrase but different meaning.
• Alma mater = living institution still existing, not a memorial.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Latin phrase = formal/memorial feel → "in memory of" choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But alma mater = your school, not a memorial!

❌ (D) Jovial companion
• "Jovial companion" = "bon vivant" or similar phrase. Alma mater = institution, person/companion അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Mater" = mother = warm/companion feel → jovial companion choose ചെയ്ക്കും. But mater here = institution (mother figure), not a person!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ALMA MATER = ALMA school (Almamater sounds like 'alma' school!) = my old school/college that FED my mind like a MOTHER!" — nourishing mother = educational institution!
• Word family: alma mater | alumni (graduates) | alumnus (male graduate) | alumna (female graduate) — same Latin root family!
• PSC exam strategy: Latin phrase കണ്ടാൽ → root words break ചെയ്യൂ. "alma" = nourishing + "mater" = mother → nourishing mother = educational institution. Latin root knowledge = PSC foreign phrase questions easy!

questionText:
I can't _____ my roommate's messiness.
(Fill in the blank using the correct phrasal verb)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Put up with

• "Put up with" = tolerate, endure something unpleasant — "സഹിക്കൽ, ക്ഷമിക്കൽ."
• "I can't put up with my roommate's messiness" = "എന്റെ roommate-ന്റെ കുഴപ്പം എനിക്ക് സഹിക്കാൻ കഴിയുന്നില്ല."
• "Messiness" (unpleasant situation) → need a phrasal verb meaning "tolerate/endure" → "put up with" ✓
• Three-word phrasal verb: put + up + with — ഒരുമിച്ച് ഒരൊറ്റ meaning!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Put up with = tolerate/endure (something unpleasant) — "സഹിച്ചുപോകൽ"
• "Put" phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും popular family:
  put up with = tolerate/endure (സഹിക്കൽ)
  put off = postpone/delay (മാറ്റിവയ്ക്കൽ)
  put on = wear clothes / gain weight / pretend (ഇടൽ / നടിക്കൽ)
  put out = extinguish / inconvenience (കെടുത്തൽ)
  put away = store in proper place (വയ്ക്കൽ)
  put forward = suggest/propose (നിർദ്ദേശിക്കൽ)
  put across = communicate/explain (മനസ്സിലാക്കിക്കൽ)
• ⭐ PSC ആaavർttnam: "Put" phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ most tested phrasal verb family!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I can't put up with his rude behaviour any longer. (= tolerate)
  2. She puts on a brave face even when sad. (= pretend)
  3. The meeting was put off until next week. (= postponed)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Put off
• "Put off" = postpone, delay — "മാറ്റിവയ്ക്കൽ." Messiness tolerate ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് "put off" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "I can't put off my roommate's messiness" = grammatically wrong/unnatural.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Put off" common phrase → "I can't put off this" feel → but put off = postpone, not tolerate!

❌ (C) Put on
• "Put on" = wear (clothes), gain weight, pretend — multiple meanings but none = tolerate.
• "I can't put on my roommate's messiness" = makes no sense.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Put on" very familiar → first choice feel → but put on = wear/pretend, put up with = tolerate!

❌ (D) Put out
• "Put out" = extinguish (fire), inconvenience someone, publish — tolerate-ന് "put out" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "I can't put out my roommate's messiness" = incorrect/unnatural usage.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Put out" = removing/dealing with something feel → but put out = extinguish, not endure!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "PUT UP WITH = Put up your hands and surrender — you TOLERATE it! You put up with it because you have no choice!" — raise hands = accept/tolerate!
• Word family: put up with (tolerate) | put off (postpone) | put on (wear) | put out (extinguish) | put away (store) — "put" family list ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "Put" phrasal verb blank-ൽ → meaning from context identify ചെയ്യൂ. "Can't stand/tolerate" context = put up with. "Postpone" context = put off. "Wear" context = put on. Context = key!

questionText: Which of the following is an adjective?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Sad

• "Sad" എന്ന word ഒരു adjective ആണ് — ഇത് ഒരു noun-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു. "She is sad" എന്ന sentence-ൽ "sad" person-ന്റെ feeling describe ചെയ്യുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് adjective ആണ്.
• Adjective identify ചെയ്യാൻ ഒരു simple test ഉണ്ട്: ആ word ഒരു noun-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നുണ്ടോ? "A sad story", "a sad face" — "sad" ഇവിടെ story-ഉം face-ഉം modify ചെയ്യുന്നു, അതുകൊണ്ട് adjective.
• "Sad" എന്ന word-ന് Malayalam-ൽ "ദുഃഖിതമായ, വിഷമം ഉള്ള" എന്നർഥം — ഒരു feeling describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective ആണ്.
• ഇതേ root word-ൽ നിന്ന് "Sadly" (adverb), "Sadness" (noun), "Sadden" (verb) ഉണ്ടാക്കാം — ഈ 4 forms ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ Parts of Speech question ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Word-ന്റെ suffix (ending) നോക്കിയാൽ Part of Speech identify ആകും!
• "Sad" root word-ൽ നിന്ന് 4 different parts of speech ഉണ്ടാക്കാം — ഇതാണ് PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ test ചെയ്യുന്ന pattern:
  - Sad → Adjective (ദുഃഖിതമായ) — suffix ഇല്ല, base form
  - Sadly → Adverb (-ly suffix ഉള്ള words usually adverbs)
  - Sadness → Noun (-ness suffix ഉള്ള words usually nouns)
  - Sadden → Verb (-en suffix ഉള്ള words usually verbs)
• Suffix Quick Reference:
  - "-ly" ending → Adverb (quickly, sadly, beautifully)
  - "-ness / -tion / -ment" ending → Noun (sadness, kindness, information)
  - "-en / -ify / -ise" ending → Verb (sadden, brighten, clarify)
  - suffix ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ / "-ful / -ous / -al / -ive" → Adjective (sad, beautiful, famous, natural)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Word family questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു — suffix identify ചെയ്ത് Part of Speech കണ്ടെത്തുന്ന questions!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She looked sad when she heard the news. (sad = adjective, "she" describe ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  2. He spoke sadly about the accident. (sadly = adverb, "spoke" modify ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  3. Her sadness was visible to everyone in the room. (sadness = noun, subject ആണ്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Sadly
• "Sadly" എന്ന word-ൽ "-ly" suffix ഉണ്ട് — ഇത് ഒരു adverb ആണ്, adjective അല്ല. "He spoke sadly" എന്ന sentence-ൽ "sadly" verb "spoke"-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നു, noun-നെ അല്ല.
• Adjective ഒരു noun-നെ modify ചെയ്യണം, ഒരു verb-നെ modify ചെയ്യരുത് — "Sadly" verb modify ചെയ്യുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് adverb ആണ്, adjective അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Sadly"-ൽ "sad" ഉണ്ടല്ലോ, അതുകൊണ്ട് adjective ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും — പക്ഷേ "-ly" ending ഉള്ള word = adverb, adjective അല്ല!

❌ (C) Sadness
• "Sadness" എന്ന word-ൽ "-ness" suffix ഉണ്ട് — ഇത് ഒരു abstract noun ആണ്. "Her sadness" = noun phrase, sentence-ൽ subject ആകാം, adjective ആകില്ല.
• Noun = ഒരു person, place, thing, or idea-നെ name ചെയ്യുന്ന word — "sadness" ഒരു feeling-നെ name ചെയ്യുന്ന abstract noun ആണ്, describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Sadness" sadness-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് adjective ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും — പക്ഷേ "-ness" ending = noun, adjective അല്ല!

❌ (D) Sadden
• "Sadden" എന്ന word-ൽ "-en" suffix ഉണ്ട് — ഇത് ഒരു verb ആണ്. "The news saddened him" = "sadden" past tense-ൽ action perform ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Verb = action, state, or occurrence express ചെയ്യുന്ന word — "sadden" meaning "ദുഃഖിപ്പിക്കുക" = action verb, adjective അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "golden, wooden" പോലുള്ള "-en" ending words adjective ആണ് — അതുകൊണ്ട് "sadden"-ഉം adjective ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും. പക്ഷേ "sadden" = "to make sad" = verb! Context നോക്കി decide ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SAD-SADLY-SADNESS-SADDEN" = Adjective-Adverb-Noun-Verb! ഈ 4 words ഒരു story ആയി ഓർക്കൂ — "SAD person (adj) SADLY (adv) faces SADNESS (noun) that will SADDEN (verb) others!"
• Word family: Happy (adj) → Happily (adv) → Happiness (noun) | Bright (adj) → Brightly (adv) → Brightness (noun) → Brighten (verb) — ഇതേ pattern ഏത് word-ലും apply ആകും!
• PSC exam strategy: Parts of Speech question കണ്ടാൽ ആദ്യം suffix check ചെയ്യൂ — "-ly" = adverb, "-ness/-tion/-ment" = noun, "-en/-ify" = verb, suffix ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ/"-ful/-ous/-al" = adjective. Suffix = instant answer! ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: Change the sentence "I know him." into passive voice.
optionC: He has been known to me.
optionD: He is being known by me.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) He is known to me.

• "Know" എന്ന verb ഒരു stative verb ആണ് — ഇതൊരു action അല്ല, ഒരു mental state describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ്. "ഞാൻ അദ്ദേഹത്തെ അറിയുന്നു" = knowing-ന്റെ ഒരു state, ഒരു action അല്ല.
• Stative verbs passive voice-ൽ "by" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — "to" ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "I know him" → passive = "He is known TO me" (not "by me").
• "I know him" = Simple Present tense, active voice → passive = "He is known to me" = Simple Present passive, tense change ആകരുത്.
• Malayalam-ൽ: "ഞാൻ അദ്ദേഹത്തെ അറിയുന്നു" → passive = "അദ്ദേഹം എനിക്ക് അറിയാം" — "to me" (എനിക്ക്) ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്, "by me" (എന്നാൽ) അല്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Stative verbs passive-ൽ "by" → "to" ആകും! "Know" passive = known TO, not known BY!
• "Know" = stative verb — action perform ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല, ഒരു state/relationship describe ചെയ്യുന്നു. Stative verbs-ൽ continuous tense ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല ("I am knowing" = wrong), passive-ൽ "to" ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Passive voice formation — Simple Present:
  - Active: Subject + V1 + Object → Passive: Object + is/am/are + V3 + to + Subject
  - "I know him" → "He is known to me"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Know" verb-ന്റെ passive voice PSC-ൽ classic trick question ആണ്! "Known to" vs "known by" — stative verb rule ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഇനി ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല.
• Stative verbs that use "to" in passive (not "by"):
  - know → known to | belong → belonged to | suit → suited to
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I know her. → She is known to me. (stative → "to")
  2. Everyone knows the truth. → The truth is known to everyone.
  3. She wrote the letter. → The letter was written by her. (action verb → "by")
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He was known by me.
• ഈ option-ൽ രണ്ട് errors ഉണ്ട് — ഒന്നാമത്, "was known" = Past Tense passive, പക്ഷേ "I know him" = Simple Present, അതുകൊണ്ട് passive = "is known" ആണ്, "was known" അല്ല.
• രണ്ടാമത്, "know" = stative verb, അതുകൊണ്ട് "by" ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത് — "to me" ആണ് correct preposition. ഈ option-ൽ tense-ഉം preposition-ഉം രണ്ടും wrong ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Passive voice = "by" ആണ് normal rule — പക്ഷേ "know" = stative verb, ഇതിന്റെ passive = "to," "by" അല്ല! Plus tense-ഉം wrong!

❌ (C) He has been known to me.
• "Has been known" = Present Perfect passive — പക്ഷേ "I know him" = Simple Present, Present Perfect അല്ല. Tense mismatch ആണ്.
• Passive voice-ൽ tense change ചെയ്യരുത് — Simple Present active → Simple Present passive, "is known" ആണ് correct, "has been known" അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Has been known to me" = formal-sounding, correct-feel ആകും — പക്ഷേ tense wrong! Simple Present → "is known," "has been known" അല്ല!

❌ (D) He is being known by me.
• "Is being known" = Present Continuous passive — "know" = stative verb, stative verbs-ൽ continuous tense ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "I am knowing" = wrong, "He is being known" = wrong — "know" stative verb ആയതുകൊണ്ട് continuous form ആകരുത്, plus "by" ഉം wrong (stative verb → "to").
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Present continuous passive = "-ing" form use ചെയ്യുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് complex/correct feel ആകും — പക്ഷേ "know" = stative, continuous passive ഉണ്ടാകില്ല, plus "by" wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "KNOW = TO! I know → known TO me. ഈ ഒരു rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ മതി — KNOW + TO = perfect pair! Action verbs use BY, but KNOW uses TO!"
• Word family: Stative verbs → know (known to), belong (belonged to), suit (suited to). Action verbs → write (written by), build (built by), make (made by).
• PSC exam strategy: "Know" passive question-ൽ — "to" കണ്ടാൽ check ചെയ്യൂ, tense correct ആണോ? "I know" = present → "is known to" = correct. Tense + preposition രണ്ടും ശരിക്ക് check ചെയ്താൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: He climbed _____ highest mountain in the region.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) the

• "Highest mountain in the region" = superlative adjective + specific reference — ഈ pattern-ൽ ALWAYS "the" ഉപയോഗിക്കണം. Superlative degree-ൽ "the" mandatory ആണ്.
• "The" = definite article — specific, unique noun-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "ആ region-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഉയരമുള്ള mountain" = ഒരേ ഒരു mountain, unique — "the" mandatory.
• Superlative degree (highest, tallest, best, most beautiful) ഉള്ള phrase-ന് മുന്നേ ALWAYS "the" ഉപയോഗിക്കും — ഇത് English grammar-ൽ fixed rule ആണ്.
• "He climbed the highest mountain in the region" = "അദ്ദേഹം ആ region-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഉയരമുള്ള മലയിൽ കയറി" — "the" = ആ specific mountain-നെ refer ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Superlative adjective (highest / best / tallest / most...) → ALWAYS "the" before it!
• "The" use ചെയ്യുന്ന situations — PSC must-know list:
  - Superlatives-ന് മുന്നേ: the highest, the best, the most beautiful
  - Unique things-ന് മുന്നേ: the sun, the moon, the earth, the sky
  - Ordinal numbers-ന് മുന്നേ: the first, the second, the last
  - Previously mentioned noun-ൽ (second mention): "I saw a dog. The dog was friendly."
  - Identifying clause-ൽ: "the man who called you," "the book I read"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Superlative + "the" — PSC Articles questions-ൽ guaranteed pattern! "the highest / best / most..." type questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is the tallest student in the class. (superlative → the)
  2. He is the best player in the team. (superlative → the)
  3. This is the most beautiful flower I have ever seen. (superlative → the)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a
• "A" = indefinite article — non-specific noun-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു, "any one of many" meaning. "A mountain" = ഏതെങ്കിലും ഒരു mountain, specific അല്ല.
• "Highest mountain in the region" = ഒരേ ഒരു specific mountain — indefinite article "a" ഈ specific, unique meaning convey ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Mountain" = common noun → "a mountain" familiar phrase feel — പക്ഷേ superlative "highest" ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ = specific mountain = "the" mandatory!

❌ (B) an
• "An" = indefinite article, vowel sound-ന് മുന്നേ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "Highest" = /h/ sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു — consonant sound, vowel sound അല്ല, അതുകൊണ്ട് "an" grammatically wrong.
• Plus "an" = indefinite, specific superlative context-ൽ wrong — "an" + consonant sound = double error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "An" = before vowel rule → "h" silent ആണോ? "Highest"-ൽ "h" pronounced ആണ് — "an hour" (silent h = ✓) vs "a/the highest" (pronounced h = "an" ✗)!

❌ (D) no article
• Article ഇല്ലാതെ = generic reference — "mountain in general" meaning. "Highest mountain in the region" = specific unique mountain, generic reference ആകില്ല.
• Singular countable noun-ന് (mountain) article mandatory — article ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ grammatically incorrect sentence ആകും.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "No article" = sophisticated choice feel — പക്ഷേ singular countable noun-ൽ article mandatory, superlative context-ൽ "the" mandatory!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "THE BEST always wears THE crown! Superlative = THE best, THE highest, THE most — THE goes with superlatives like a king's crown! Crown = "the"!"
• Word family: the highest, the tallest, the deepest, the most beautiful, the best, the worst, the first, the last — ഇവ എല്ലാം superlatives/ordinals, ALWAYS "the" with them!
• PSC exam strategy: Article blank-ൽ superlative adjective (-est / most + adj) കണ്ടാൽ → automatic "the." ഒരു second thought-ഉം വേണ്ട — superlative = "the" = guaranteed mark!

questionText: Which sentence uses a gerund correctly?
optionA: He suggested to go for a walk.
optionB: He suggested go for a walk.
optionC: He suggested to going for a walk.
optionD: He suggested going for a walk.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) He suggested going for a walk.

• "Suggest" = ഒരു verb ആണ്, ഇതിന് ശേഷം ALWAYS gerund (V-ing form) ഉപയോഗിക്കണം — "suggest + to + V1" = wrong, "suggest + V-ing" = correct.
• "Going" = gerund — "go" verb-ന്റെ "-ing" form, ഇവിടെ noun ആയി function ചെയ്യുന്നു. "He suggested going" = "going" = the thing that was suggested.
• "He suggested going for a walk" = "അദ്ദേഹം ഒരു നടത്തം നിർദ്ദേശിച്ചു" — gerund correctly used after "suggest."
• "Suggest" + infinitive (to go) = wrong — ഈ verb infinitive take ചെയ്യില്ല, gerund മാത്രം take ചെയ്യും. ഇത് ഒരു fixed grammar rule ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "Suggest" always + V-ing (gerund)! "Suggest + to + V1" = NEVER correct!
• Gerund = V-ing form used as noun — going, playing, swimming, writing.
• Verbs that take GERUND after them (PSC must-know list):
  - suggest, recommend, enjoy, avoid, finish, consider, practise
  - delay, admit, deny, risk, keep, miss, imagine, mind
• Verbs that take INFINITIVE after them:
  - want, wish, hope, decide, plan, agree, refuse, manage, offer
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Suggest + gerund" — PSC-ൽ classic question! "suggested to go" vs "suggested going" — ഇത് ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഇനി miss ആകില്ല.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He suggested going to the cinema. (suggest + gerund ✓)
  2. She recommended trying the new restaurant. (recommend + gerund ✓)
  3. They enjoyed playing cricket on the weekend. (enjoy + gerund ✓)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He suggested to go for a walk.
• "Suggested to go" = infinitive after "suggest" — ഇത് grammatically wrong. "Suggest" ഒരിക്കലും infinitive take ചെയ്യില്ല, gerund മാത്രം take ചെയ്യും.
• "Want to go, plan to go, decide to go" — ഇവ infinitive take ചെയ്യുന്ന verbs ആണ്. "Suggest" ആ list-ൽ ഇല്ല — gerund list-ൽ ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Suggested to go" = "want to go" pattern-ൽ നിന്ന് familiar feel ആകും — പക്ഷേ "suggest" ≠ "want." Suggest = gerund, want = infinitive!

❌ (B) He suggested go for a walk.
• "Suggested go" = bare infinitive (V1 without "to") — ഇതും wrong. "Go" alone = neither gerund nor proper infinitive form.
