• Grammar-ൽ "suggest + V1" = accepted structure ഇല്ല — gerund (going) മാത്രം correct. "Suggest go" = grammatically meaningless.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Go" = simple, clean look → correct feel — പക്ഷേ "suggested go" = grammar rule support ഇല്ലാത്ത structure!

❌ (C) He suggested to going for a walk.
• "To going" = "to" (infinitive marker) + "going" (gerund) ഒരുമിച്ച് ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത് — ഇത് grammatically incorrect. Either "to go" (infinitive) or "going" (gerund) — ഇവ രണ്ടും combine ചെയ്തൊരു form ഇല്ല.
• "To going" = invalid English structure — neither correct infinitive nor correct gerund form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "to going" = both forms കൂടിയതുകൊണ്ട് complete-feel ആകും — പക്ഷേ "to + V-ing" = invalid English structure!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SUGGEST and GOING — both have G! SUGGEST + GOING = G-G pair! S-G pair ഓർക്കൂ: Suggest = G-word takes G-word (gerund)!"
• Word family: Gerund verbs — suggest going, recommend trying, enjoy playing, avoid making, finish writing, consider leaving — ഇവ ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Gerund/Infinitive blank-ൽ main verb identify ചെയ്യൂ — "suggest" കണ്ടാൽ instant gerund (V-ing). Verb list ഓർക്കാൻ: "SERMA" = Suggest, Enjoy, Recommend, Mind, Avoid — ഇവ gerund take ചെയ്യുന്ന verbs! SERMA = guaranteed mark!

questionText: He had hardly finished his meal, _____ ?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) had he

• "Hardly" = negative adverb — Malayalam-ൽ "ഏതാണ്ട് ഇല്ലതന്നെ, വളരെ കുറച്ചേ" എന്ന് meaning. ഈ word sentence-ൽ ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ sentence grammatically positive ആണ്, പക്ഷേ meaning-ൽ negative ആകുന്നു.
• "He had hardly finished his meal" = "അദ്ദേഹം ഭക്ഷണം ഏതാണ്ട് കഴിഞ്ഞിരുന്നില്ല" = negative meaning — അതുകൊണ്ട് positive question tag ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Question tag rule: Negative meaning sentence → Positive tag. Auxiliary verb = "had" → positive tag = "had he?"
• "He had hardly finished his meal, had he?" = "അദ്ദേഹം ഭക്ഷണം ഏതാണ്ട് കഴിഞ്ഞിരുന്നില്ല, അല്ലേ?" — correct positive tag with correct auxiliary.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "Hardly / scarcely / rarely / never / seldom" ഉള്ള sentence → Positive tag!
• "Hidden negative" words — ഇവ sentence-ൽ ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ sentence positive look ആകുമെങ്കിലും meaning negative ആകുന്നു:
  - hardly (ഏതാണ്ട് ഇല്ലതന്നെ)
  - scarcely (ഏതാണ്ട് ഇല്ല)
  - barely (കഷ്ടിച്ച് മാത്രം)
  - rarely (വളരെ അപൂർവ്വമായി)
  - seldom (അപൂർവ്വമായി)
  - never (ഒരിക്കലും ഇല്ല)
• Question tag rule: Positive sentence → Negative tag | Negative sentence/meaning → Positive tag
• Auxiliary match: tag-ൽ main sentence-ന്റെ same auxiliary use ചെയ്യണം — "had hardly" → auxiliary = "had" → tag = "had he?"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Hardly/Never/Scarcely" question tags — PSC-ൽ tricky question! Most students "hadn't he" choose ചെയ്യും — correct = "had he" (positive tag). ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He had hardly slept, had he? (hardly → positive tag)
  2. She rarely visits us, does she? (rarely → positive tag)
  3. They never complain, do they? (never → positive tag)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) hadn't he
• "Hadn't he" = negative tag — normal rule-ൽ positive sentence → negative tag ആണ്. പക്ഷേ "hardly" = hidden negative, sentence = negative meaning → positive tag "had he" ആണ് correct.
• "Hadn't he" = "He had finished" (no hardly) type positive sentence-ന്റെ tag ആണ്. "Hardly" ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് rule flip ആകുന്നു — positive tag വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Had" auxiliary കണ്ടാൽ → "hadn't he?" natural instinct — പക്ഷേ "hardly" = hidden negative = positive tag "had he"! ഇതാണ് PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ students fall ആകുന്ന trap!

❌ (C) did he
• "Did he" = Past Simple positive tag — "he had hardly" = Past Perfect tense (had + V3), Past Simple അല്ല.
• Question tag-ൽ main sentence-ന്റെ SAME auxiliary use ചെയ്യണം — "had" auxiliary ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ tag = "had he," "did he" അല്ല. Auxiliary mismatch = wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Past tense feel → "did he" familiar — പക്ഷേ "had" = Past Perfect auxiliary, "did" = Past Simple auxiliary — tense mismatch!

❌ (D) didn't he
• "Didn't he" = Past Simple negative tag — double error: wrong auxiliary (did instead of had) + wrong polarity (negative tag instead of positive).
• "Hardly" = negative meaning → positive tag "had he" — "didn't he" = negative tag, completely wrong direction. Polarity-ഉം tense-ഉം wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Past tense + negative sound → "didn't he" familiar — പക്ഷേ tense wrong (did vs had) AND polarity wrong (negative vs positive)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "HARDLY means HARD NO! ഏതാണ്ട് 'not at all'! Hidden NO = flip the tag to YES! Hardly = hidden 'not' → positive tag!"
• Word family: Hidden negative words — hardly, scarcely, barely, rarely, seldom, never, little, few — ഇവ കണ്ടാൽ sentence = negative meaning = positive tag!
• PSC exam strategy: Question tag blank-ൽ "hardly/never/rarely/seldom/scarcely" കണ്ടാൽ → ഉടനെ positive tag choose ചെയ്യൂ. Auxiliary identify ചെയ്ത് positive form use ചെയ്യൂ — "had hardly" → "had he?", "has never" → "has he?", "rarely comes" → "does she?" ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഈ topic-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText: The antonym of "melancholy" is

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Joy

• "Melancholy" എന്ന word-ന് Malayalam-ൽ "ആഴത്തിലുള്ള ദുഃഖം, വിഷാദം" എന്നർഥം — ഇത് ഒരു negative emotion describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ്.
• "Joy" = സന്തോഷം, ആനന്ദം — ഇത് "melancholy"-യുടെ exact opposite ആണ്. Negative emotion-ന്റെ antonym = positive emotion.
• Antonym identify ചെയ്യാൻ ഒരു simple method: ആ word-ന്റെ emotion category identify ചെയ്യൂ — "melancholy" = negative/sad category, അതുകൊണ്ട് antonym = positive/happy category.
• Options-ൽ "Joy" മാത്രമാണ് positive/happy emotion — Sorrow, Gloom, Misery ഇവ മൂന്നും sad/negative emotions ആണ്. ഒരേ ഒരു positive option = correct answer.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Negative emotion word-ന്റെ antonym = positive emotion! Melancholy (sad) ↔ Joy (happy)
• "Melancholy" word family:
  - Noun: melancholy = sadness, gloom, depression
  - Adjective: melancholic = ദുഃഖഭരിതമായ
• Sadness synonyms — ഇവ PSC-ൽ antonym traps ആയി വരും:
  - Melancholy = deep sadness (ആഴത്തിലുള്ള ദുഃഖം)
  - Sorrow = grief (ദുഃഖം, വ്യസനം)
  - Gloom = darkness/sadness (ഇരുട്ട്, വ്യസനം)
  - Misery = extreme suffering (കഠിന ദുഃഖം)
• Joy synonyms — antonym group:
  - Joy = happiness (സന്തോഷം) | Elation = great joy | Bliss = perfect happiness | Delight = pleasure
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonym questions-ൽ PSC synonyms-നെ wrong options ആക്കി confuse ചെയ്യും — ഇതാണ് classic PSC trap!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. His face showed melancholy after the loss. (melancholy = deep sadness)
  2. She was filled with joy when she heard the good news. (joy = happiness)
  3. The melancholy mood lifted when spring arrived. (melancholy ↔ joy)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Sorrow
• "Sorrow" = grief, sadness = ദുഃഖം, വ്യസനം — ഇത് "melancholy"-യുടെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല. ഒരേ category-ലുള്ള words — രണ്ടും negative emotions.
• Antonym = opposite meaning, synonym = same meaning — "sorrow" = melancholy-ക്ക് similar meaning ആണ്, opposite അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Sorrow" = sadness feel → antonym feel? ഇല്ല! Sorrow ALSO = sadness = SYNONYM of melancholy, antonym അല്ല!

❌ (B) Gloom
• "Gloom" = darkness, sadness = ഇരുട്ട്, വ്യസനം — ഇതും "melancholy"-യുടെ SYNONYM ആണ്. "Gloomy weather" = ദുഃഖകരമായ കാലാവസ്ഥ — negative feeling.
• "Gloom" different-sounding word ആണ്, പക്ഷേ meaning-ൽ melancholy-ക്ക് similar — synonym, antonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Gloom" = unfamiliar word → antonym feel? ഇല്ല! Gloom = melancholy = sadness — both negative emotions = synonyms!

❌ (D) Misery
• "Misery" = extreme suffering, wretchedness = കഠിന ദുഃഖം — melancholy-ക്കാൾ intense ആണ്, പക്ഷേ still negative emotion.
• "In misery" = intense pain/sadness — still a negative emotion, antonym ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Misery" = very different sound from "melancholy" → antonym feel? ഇല്ല! Both = sadness category = synonyms! Antonym = Joy (positive)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "MELANCHOLY = heavy heart. Antonym = LIGHT heart = JOY! MEL-AN-CHOLY = very heavy, very sad. Joy = light, happy. Heavy ↔ Light = Melancholy ↔ Joy!"
• PSC antonym strategy: Options-ൽ emotion words ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ — ആദ്യം negative/positive identify ചെയ്യൂ. Melancholy = negative → antonym = positive. Options-ൽ ഒരേ ഒരു positive word = correct answer. ഇത് method ആക്കിയാൽ antonym questions miss ആകില്ല!
• Word family: Melancholy ↔ Joy | Sorrow ↔ Happiness | Gloom ↔ Brightness | Misery ↔ Comfort — ഈ pairs ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!

questionText:
Choose the appropriate collective noun.
The _____ of judges took a unanimous decision.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) bench

• "Bench" എന്നാൽ ഒരു കോടതിയിലെ ജഡ്ജിമാരുടെ കൂട്ടം എന്നാണ് അർഥം — "a bench of judges" ഇന്ത്യൻ ലീഗൽ ഇംഗ്ലീഷിൽ വളരെ സ്ഥിരമായി ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന collective noun ആണ്.
• "The bench of judges took a unanimous decision" = "ജഡ്ജിമാരുടെ ബെഞ്ച് ഏകകണ്ഠമായ ഒരു തീരുമാനം എടുത്തു" — ഇത് ഒരു court/tribunal ruling പോലുള്ള context ആണ്.
• Context clue: "took a unanimous decision" എന്നത് ഒരു ജുഡീഷ്യൽ/ട്രിബ്യൂണൽ verdict-നെ സൂചിപ്പിക്കുന്നു — ഇന്ത്യൻ കോടതികളിൽ "a two-judge bench", "a five-judge bench of the Supreme Court" എന്നിങ്ങനെ സ്ഥിരമായി കേൾക്കുന്ന പ്രയോഗമാണ്.
• Etymology: "bench" പഴയ കാലത്ത് ജഡ്ജിമാർ ഇരിക്കുന്ന ഇരിപ്പിടത്തെ (seat) സൂചിപ്പിച്ചിരുന്നു, പിന്നീട് ജഡ്ജിമാരുടെ കൂട്ടത്തെ തന്നെ കുറിക്കുന്ന വാക്കായി മാറി.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: കോടതി ജഡ്ജിമാരുടെ കൂട്ടം = bench! Court trial verdict group = jury! Competition/interview evaluation group = panel! Organization decisions = committee!
• Collective nouns for groups of people — PSC must-know:
  - a bench of judges (court/tribunal — formal ജുഡീഷ്യൽ ruling)
  - a jury of peers (court trial-ൽ സാധാരണക്കാരായ ആളുകൾ verdict കൊടുക്കുന്നത്)
  - a panel of judges/experts (competition, interview, quiz show evaluation)
  - a committee of members (ഭരണപരമായ/organizational തീരുമാനങ്ങൾ)
• More PSC collective nouns:
  - a board of directors | a staff of teachers | a crew of sailors
  - a troupe of actors | a gang of thieves | a team of players
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective noun questions PSC-ൽ regular pattern ആണ് — "bench/panel/jury/committee" confusion ഒരു ക്ലാസിക് PSC question ആണ്. Context ആണ് key — "court", "ruling", "verdict", "decision" പോലുള്ള വാക്കുകൾ കാണുമ്പോൾ bench ആണ് correct ആകാൻ സാധ്യത കൂടുതൽ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A three-judge bench of the High Court delivered the verdict. (court ruling)
  2. The jury deliberated for two days before reaching a verdict. (trial jury)
  3. A panel of experts judged the singing competition. (competition evaluation)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) committee
• "Committee" എന്നാൽ ഭരണപരമായ/organizational തീരുമാനങ്ങൾക്കായി ഔദ്യോഗികമായി രൂപീകരിച്ച ഒരു ഗ്രൂപ്പ് — "a selection committee", "a finance committee" പോലുള്ളവ. ജഡ്ജിമാരുടെ ജുഡീഷ്യൽ context-ൽ "committee" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Committee = ഭരണപരമായ body, judges = ജുഡീഷ്യൽ/evaluation group — ഇവ different contexts ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "of judges" കണ്ടാൽ ഏതെങ്കിലും ഗ്രൂപ്പ് വാക്ക് ശരിയാകുമെന്ന് തോന്നാം, പക്ഷേ committee ജഡ്ജിമാരെ ഒരിക്കലും കുറിക്കില്ല.

❌ (B) jury
• "Jury" എന്നാൽ കോടതി വിചാരണയിൽ verdict കൊടുക്കുന്ന സാധാരണക്കാരായ പൗരന്മാരുടെ കൂട്ടം ആണ് — ജഡ്ജിമാരല്ല, ജഡ്ജിയുടെ മുന്നിൽ കേസ് കേൾക്കുന്ന ആളുകൾ.
• "Jury" = trial-ൽ പങ്കെടുക്കുന്ന പൗരന്മാർ, "bench" = ജഡ്ജിമാർ തന്നെ — ഈ രണ്ടും confuse ചെയ്യരുത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Jury-യും court context-ഉം തമ്മിൽ ബന്ധം ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് ഇത് ശരിയാണെന്ന് തോന്നിക്കും, പക്ഷേ jury = ജഡ്ജിമാരല്ല, courtroom-ലെ പൗരന്മാരുടെ കൂട്ടമാണ്.

❌ (C) panel
• "Panel" = ഒരു competition, interview, quiz show എന്നിവയിൽ വിലയിരുത്തൽ നടത്തുന്ന expert-കളുടെ കൂട്ടം — ജഡ്ജിമാർ ഒരു കോടതി ruling എടുക്കുന്ന context-ൽ standard term അല്ല.
• "Panel of judges" എന്നത് competition/award ceremony context-ൽ ശരിയാണ്, പക്ഷേ ഇവിടെ "unanimous decision" എന്ന phrase ജുഡീഷ്യൽ ruling-നെ കൂടുതൽ സൂചിപ്പിക്കുന്നു, അതുകൊണ്ട് bench ആണ് കൂടുതൽ ഫിറ്റ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "panel of judges" എന്ന phrase പൊതുവേ കേട്ട് പരിചയമുള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് ഇത് സ്വാഭാവികമായി തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കാൻ തോന്നും — ഇതാണ് PSC ഇവിടെ വെച്ച trap.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "BENCH = കോടതി ബെഞ്ച്! ജഡ്ജിമാർ ഇരിക്കുന്ന ബെഞ്ചിൽ നിന്നാണ് ഈ വാക്ക് വന്നത് — 'a two-judge bench', 'a five-judge bench of the Supreme Court' എന്നിങ്ങനെ ന്യൂസിൽ സ്ഥിരം കേൾക്കുന്ന വാക്ക്!"
• Context shortcut: Court ruling/verdict → BENCH | Trial (പൗരന്മാർ) → JURY | Competition/interview evaluation → PANEL | Organization meetings → COMMITTEE. Context ആണ് key!
• PSC exam strategy: "of judges" blank-ൽ, ചുറ്റുമുള്ള വാക്കുകൾ ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക — "decision/ruling/verdict" പോലുള്ള ജുഡീഷ്യൽ വാക്കുകൾ ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ bench ആണ് സാധാരണ correct answer, competition/award context ആണെങ്കിൽ panel.

questionText: Identify the correctly spelled word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Reference

• "Reference" = correct spelling — R-E-F-E-R-E-N-C-E = 9 letters. "Refer" (verb) + "-ence" (noun suffix) = "reference."
• "Reference" = ഒരു source/person-നെ mention ചെയ്യുക, ഒരു source-ൽ നിന്ന് information എടുക്കുക — "please provide a reference" = ഒരു referral/mention.
• Stress rule: "refer" verb-ൽ stress = re-FER (second syllable), പക്ഷേ "reference" noun-ൽ stress = REF-er-ence (first syllable) — stress shift ആകുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് final consonant double ആകില്ല.
• "Referred" (past tense) = double 'r' — re-FERRED (stress on last syllable, consonant doubles). "Reference" (noun) = single 'r' — REF-er-ence (stress shifts, consonant does NOT double).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: refer → reference = stress shifts to first syllable → NO double consonant! refer → referred = stress stays on last syllable → DOUBLE consonant!
• "Reference" spelling breakdown: R-E-F-E-R-E-N-C-E = single 'f', single 'r' in middle, '-ence' ending.
• Doubling rule — consonant doubling happens when:
  - Stress is on the LAST syllable of base verb: reFER → reFERred (double r)
  - Adding suffix that starts with vowel: -ed, -ing, -al
  - BUT: when suffix shifts stress BACK → no doubling: REFerence (stress shifts)
• "-ence" vs "-ance" — PSC confusion:
  - refer → reference (-ence) | prefer → preference (-ence) | differ → difference (-ence)
  - perform → performance (-ance) | resist → resistance (-ance)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions-ൽ "-ence/-ance" confusion PSC-ൽ regular pattern ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Please provide a reference for your work. (reference = noun)
  2. He referred to the dictionary for the meaning. (referred = double r, past tense)
  3. The reference book was very helpful for the project.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Refference
• "Refference" = double 'f' error — "ref-f-erence." "Refer" base word-ൽ ഒരേ ഒരു 'f' ഉള്ളൂ, അതുകൊണ്ട് "reference"-ലും single 'f' ആണ്.
• 'f' double ആകുന്ന word: "difference" (differ + ence) = d-i-f-f-e-r-e-n-c-e — ഇതിൽ double 'f' ഉണ്ട്. "Reference" ആ pattern follow ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "difference" = double 'f' ഉണ്ട് → "reference"-ലും double 'f' ഉണ്ടോ? ഇല്ല! "Differ" = double 'f', "refer" = single 'f' — base word check ചെയ്താൽ clear!

❌ (B) Referance
• "Referance" = wrong suffix — "-ance" instead of "-ence." "Refer" → "reference" (-ence), "referance" (-ance) wrong ആണ്.
• "-ence" vs "-ance" confusion: refer/prefer/differ family = "-ence." perform/resist/appear family = "-ance."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "-ance" ending common in English (distance, balance, finance) → "referance" feel — പക്ഷേ "reference" = '-ence' not '-ance'!

❌ (D) Referrence
• "Referrence" = double 'r' error — "refer-r-ence." Stress shifts to "REF" in noun form → no consonant doubling.
• "Referred" (past tense) = double 'r' correct (re-FERRED, stress on last). "Reference" (noun) = single 'r' (REF-er-ence, stress on first) — different stress = different spelling!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Referred" has double 'r' → "reference" also double 'r'? ഇല്ല! Referred = verb (stress last, doubles). Reference = noun (stress first, no doubling)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "REFER loses its stress in REFERENCE! REF-er-ence = REF steps forward, 'r' doesn't double! Like a referee who steps back — ref steps back (stress shifts), no doubling!"
• Spelling checklist: R-E-F-E-R-E-N-C-E = single f, single r in middle, -ence (not -ance). Count the letters: 9 letters, no doubles!
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ "reference" കണ്ടാൽ — single f, single middle r, -ence ending = correct. "Refference/Referrence/Referance" = all wrong. ഒരിക്കൽ correct spelling memorize ചെയ്താൽ 3 wrong options automatically eliminate ആകും!

questionText: One who is new to a profession or activity.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Novice

• "Novice" = one who is new to a profession or activity = ഒരു profession/activity-ൽ പുതുതായി ചേർന്ന ആൾ, inexperienced person.
• "Novice" = beginners, പ്രത്യേകിച്ച് professional/skill context-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന formal word — "a novice programmer," "a novice teacher."
• Question definition: "new to a profession OR activity" = both professional and casual contexts cover ആകുന്ന word — "Novice" ഈ definition-ന് exactly match ആകുന്നു.
• Malayalam-ൽ: "Novice" = "നവാഗതൻ, പുതുതായി ഒരു profession-ൽ ചേർന്ന ആൾ" — professional context-ൽ new person.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: New to a profession/skill = Novice! Does it for free/hobby = Amateur! Army new joinee = Recruit! General learning = Learner!
• One-word substitutes — "new/beginner" category distinctions:
  - Novice = new to a profession/skill, inexperienced (formal context)
  - Amateur = does it for love/hobby, NOT for pay (opposite = professional)
  - Recruit = newly joined member of military/organization specifically
  - Learner = general term, anyone learning anything
• More PSC one-word substitutes:
  - Neophyte = novice, newcomer | Rookie = newcomer (informal)
  - Apprentice = learning under a master | Veteran = experienced person
  - Expert = highly skilled | Prodigy = exceptionally talented young person
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Novice vs Amateur vs Recruit" confusion — PSC one-word substitute classic question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is a novice in the field of journalism. (new to profession)
  2. He plays football as an amateur, not professionally. (hobby, not paid)
  3. The new recruit was assigned to basic training. (military/org new member)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Amateur
• "Amateur" = ഒരു activity payment ഇല്ലാതെ, hobby ആയി ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — "new to profession" ആണ് എന്ന് specific ആയി indicate ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• "Amateur" = professional-ന്റെ opposite (pay receive ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല) — "new" ആണ് എന്ന് ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല. An amateur can be experienced but unpaid.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Amateur = not skilled/professional feel → new to profession feel — പക്ഷേ Amateur = hobby/unpaid, not necessarily NEW. Novice = specifically NEW!

❌ (C) Recruit
• "Recruit" = specifically military/organization-ൽ newly joined person — "a new recruit" = army/police/organization context only.
• "Profession or activity" generally refer ചെയ്യുന്ന definition-ൽ "recruit" ഒതുങ്ങില്ല — too narrow, military/org specific.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Recruit = new member feel → new to profession = recruit? ഇല്ല! Recruit = military/org specific. Novice = ANY profession/activity!

❌ (D) Learner
• "Learner" = general term for anyone learning anything — too broad/vague. "New to a profession" = more specific than just "learning."
• "A learner" = ഒരു student, a child learning to walk — not specifically new to a profession. Anyone can be a learner.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Learner = not experienced yet = new to profession feel — പക്ഷേ "learner" = anyone learning. "Novice" = specifically new to a profession/skill!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NOVICE = NO VICE yet! A novice has no bad professional habits because they just started! No bad habits = brand new = novice!"
• Word distinctions: Novice (new to profession) | Amateur (not paid, hobby) | Recruit (military/org new member) | Learner (general learning). ഓരോന്നിന്റെയും context different ആണ്!
• PSC exam strategy: One-word substitute question-ൽ definition carefully read ചെയ്യൂ. "New to a PROFESSION or ACTIVITY" = novice. "Does for pleasure/free" = amateur. "Military/org context" = recruit. Definition-ന്റെ exact words = answer key — ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: What does the idiom "let the cat out of the bag" mean?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) reveal a secret carelessly

• "Let the cat out of the bag" = accidentally/carelessly ഒരു secret reveal ചെയ്യുക = "ഒരു secret പുറത്തുവിടുക — ഉദ്ദേശ്യം ഇല്ലാതെ/ശ്രദ്ധ ഇല്ലാതെ."
• Key word: "carelessly/accidentally" — intentionally reveal ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല, accidentally ആണ് — "She let the cat out of the bag about the surprise party" = surprise party-യെക്കുറിച്ച് accident ആയി പറഞ്ഞു.
• "Reveal a secret carelessly" = ഈ idiom-ന്റെ exact meaning — carelessly/unintentionally ഒരു secret പറഞ്ഞുപോകുന്നത്.
• Malayalam-ൽ: "bag-ൽ ഒളിപ്പിച്ചുവെച്ച cat-നെ (secret-നെ) പുറത്തു ചാടിക്കുക" = secret accidentally expose ചെയ്യുക.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "Let the cat out of the bag" = secret accidentally reveal ചെയ്യുക! CAT = SECRET, BAG = hiding place, CAT OUT = secret exposed!
• Idiom origin: പഴയ market-ൽ pig-ന് പകരം bag-ൽ cat വെച്ച് trick ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ cat bag-ൽ നിന്ന് ചാടിപ്പോകുന്നത് = secret exposed!
• Similar idioms — secrets category (PSC must-know):
  - "Spill the beans" = reveal a secret (accidentally/carelessly)
  - "Blow the whistle" = expose wrongdoing (deliberately)
  - "Give the game away" = accidentally reveal something
  - "Keep it under wraps" = keep it secret
  - "Mum's the word" = stay silent, don't tell
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Let the cat out of the bag" PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന idiom ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. John let the cat out of the bag about the birthday surprise.
  2. Don't let the cat out of the bag — it's supposed to be a secret!
  3. She accidentally spilled the beans about their engagement.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) To release a cat
• ഇത് literal meaning — "cat" ശരിക്കും "cat" അല്ല ഈ idiom-ൽ, "secret" ആണ്! Idioms = figurative meaning, literal meaning ഒരിക്കലും correct answer ആകില്ല.
• "Let the cat out of the bag" idiom-ൽ ഉള്ള words-നെ literal ആയി interpret ചെയ്തൊരു option ആണ് ഇത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Idiom words literal ആയി read ചെയ്യുക — "let cat out of bag" → release cat? ഇല്ല! Idioms = figurative meaning always, literal = always wrong option!

❌ (C) To say something suspicious
• "Suspicious" = doubt/mistrust create ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് idiom-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല. Secret reveal ചെയ്യുന്നതും suspicious ആകുന്നതും different ആണ്.
• Secret reveal = sometimes suspicious feel ആകാം, പക്ഷേ idiom-ന്റെ meaning = carelessly reveal a secret, suspicious ആകുക അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Secret reveal = suspicious connection feel → "say something suspicious" — പക്ഷേ idiom = specifically carelessly reveal a SECRET, not suspicious speech!

❌ (D) To drop the bag
• "Drop the bag" — ഇത് "bag"-നെ literal ആയി interpret ചെയ്ത് ഉണ്ടാക്കിയ wrong option ആണ്. "Drop the bag" = recognized English idiom ഇല്ല.
• "Let the cat out of the bag"-ൽ "bag" = figurative hiding place for secret — literal bag drop-ൽ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Bag" word idiom-ൽ ഉണ്ട് → "drop the bag" connection feel — പക്ഷേ idiom = secret reveal, bag drop-ൽ meaning ഇല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "CAT in a BAG = SECRET hidden away! CAT JUMPS OUT = SECRET is OUT! Let the cat jump out = let the secret slip out carelessly! CAT = SECRET, remember this!"
• Idiom family — secrets: let the cat out of the bag (reveal carelessly) | spill the beans (reveal) | keep under wraps (hide) | mum's the word (stay silent). ഈ 4 idioms ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning = always wrong option. Figurative meaning = correct. "Cat, bag" = literal traps. PSC top idioms list: let the cat out of the bag | spill the beans | bite the bullet | hit the nail on the head | burn the midnight oil — ഈ idioms PSC-ൽ repeat ആകുന്നു, ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed marks!

questionText:
Choose the appropriate question tag:
Please stop talking, _____________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) will you?

• "Please stop talking" = polite imperative sentence — "Please" ഉള്ള request-ൽ question tag = "will you?" ആണ്, ഇത് ഒരു fixed rule ആണ്.
• "Please" = politeness marker, ഒരു request politely ചോദിക്കുന്നു — ഇത്തരം sentences-ൽ "will you?" tag ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് listener-ന്റെ cooperation request ചെയ്യുന്നതുപോലെ ആണ്.
• "Please stop talking, will you?" = "ദയവായി സംസാരിക്കുന്നത് നിർത്തണേ, ആകുമോ?" — polite request + polite tag.
• Imperative sentences-ൽ subject ഇല്ലാത്തതുകൊണ്ട് "you" pronoun tag-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു — "will you?" = standard polite imperative tag.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "Please" ഉള്ള imperative → "will you?" tag! "Let's" ഉള്ള imperative → "shall we?" tag!
• Imperative sentence question tags — PSC must-know:
  - Please + command → will you? (Please sit down, will you?)
  - Direct command (no Please) → will you? / won't you? (Stop talking, will you?)
  - Negative command (Don't...) → will you? (Don't make noise, will you?)
  - Let's... → shall we? (Let's go for a walk, shall we?)
  - Let him/her/them... → will you? (Let him speak, will you?)
• Imperative sentences-ൽ subject ഇല്ല, "you" implied — അതുകൊണ്ട് tag = "will you?" / "shall we?"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative question tags PSC-ൽ regular pattern! "Please" = "will you?" — ഈ ഒരു rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ മതി!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Please open the window, will you? (Please + command → will you?)
  2. Don't interrupt me, will you? (Don't + command → will you?)
  3. Let's have dinner together, shall we? (Let's → shall we?)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) won't you?
• "Won't you?" = negative tag — "Please" ഉള്ള polite request-ൽ standard tag = "will you?" ആണ്, "won't you?" അല്ല. Direct commands-ൽ "won't you?" acceptable ആണ്, പക്ഷേ "Please" ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ "will you?" standard.
• "Please stop talking, won't you?" = grammatically possible but less standard than "will you?" — PSC answer = "will you?"
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Command = "won't you?" familiar feel — പക്ഷേ "Please" = polite request → standard tag = "will you?" not "won't you?"!

❌ (C) don't you?
• "Don't you?" = negative tag for positive statements — "She works hard, doesn't she?" ഇതുപോലെ declarative sentences-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. Imperative sentences-ൽ "don't you?" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Imperative = command/request, declarative statement അല്ല — "don't you?" = statement tag, imperative tag അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Don't" = familiar negative word → "don't you?" feel — പക്ഷേ imperative tag = always "will you?" or "shall we?", "don't you?" അല്ല!

❌ (D) do you?
• "Do you?" = positive tag for negative statements — "She doesn't work, does she?" type sentences-ൽ tag. Imperative context-ൽ "do you?" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "Do" auxiliary imperative sentence-ൽ tag ആയി relevant അല്ല — imperative tag = "will you?" or "shall we?" only.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Do" = common auxiliary → "do you?" natural feel — പക്ഷേ imperative tag = "will you?", "do you?" അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "PLEASE brings WILL! Please request = Will you please comply? PLEASE + WILL = always together in question tags!"
• Imperative tag shortcut: Please/command = will you? | Don't = will you? | Let's = shall we? — ഈ 3 rules ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ imperative tags miss ആകില്ല!
• PSC exam strategy: Imperative sentence tag blank-ൽ → "Please" ഉണ്ടോ? Yes → "will you?" instant answer. "Let's" ഉണ്ടോ? → "shall we?" These 2 rules = most PSC imperative tag questions cover ആകും, guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the word which is opposite in meaning to the given word:
Discrepancy

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) consistency

• "Discrepancy" = two things-ന് ഇടയിൽ ഉണ്ടാകുന്ന difference/inconsistency = "ചേർച്ചക്കുറവ്, വ്യത്യാസം" — ഉണ്ടാകരുതാത്ത ഒരു difference.
• "Consistency" = ഒരേ ഗുണനിലവാരം/pattern നിലനിർത്തുന്ന state = "ഒരുമ, ഒരേ രീതി" — ഇതാണ് "discrepancy"-യുടെ exact opposite.
• Antonym logic: "Discrepancy" = things don't match (negative) → antonym = things DO match, things are consistent (positive) = "consistency."
• "There is a discrepancy in the accounts" = accounts match ആകുന്നില്ല; antonym = "consistency in accounts" = accounts perfectly match ആകുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Discrepancy (dis = apart, things don't match) ↔ Consistency (con = together, things match)!
• "Discrepancy" prefix analysis:
  - "Dis-" = negative/apart prefix (disagree, disconnect, disorder)
  - "Discrepancy" = lack of agreement between things that should be same
• "Consistency" prefix analysis:
  - "Con-" = together/with prefix (connect, conform, consistent)
  - "Consistency" = state of always being the same, matching
• PSC vocabulary pairs — antonyms with prefixes:
  - Discrepancy ↔ Consistency
  - Agreement ↔ Disagreement
  - Conformity ↔ Nonconformity
  - Harmony ↔ Discord
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Inconsistency" = discrepancy-യുടെ synonym ആണ് — ഇതൊരു classic PSC trap! "Inconsistency" ≠ antonym!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. There was a discrepancy between the two reports. (discrepancy = difference)
  2. The teacher praised the student for consistency in studies. (consistency = same level)
  3. The discrepancy in the data was corrected after review.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) respectful
• "Respectful" = ബഹുമാനമുള്ള — "Discrepancy" = difference/inconsistency word ആണ്, ഇതിന്റെ antonym "respectful" ആകില്ല. Completely different semantic category — "respectful" = attitude/behaviour word.
• "Discrepancy" = comparison/matching concept, "respectful" = attitude concept — ഇവ unrelated ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Random positive word → antonym feel — പക്ഷേ "respectful" ≠ opposite of "discrepancy." Different categories, no connection!

❌ (C) disrespectful
• "Disrespectful" = ബഹുമാനമില്ലാത്ത — negative word ആണ്, പക്ഷേ "discrepancy"-യുടെ antonym അല്ല. Both "discrepancy" and "disrespectful" have "dis-" prefix — same prefix ≠ related antonym!
• Different categories: discrepancy = matching/difference concept, disrespectful = attitude concept.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both start with "dis-" → connection feel — പക്ഷേ "dis-" words can be completely unrelated in meaning!

❌ (D) inconsistency
• "Inconsistency" = "In-" (negative) + "consistency" = lack of consistency = discrepancy-യുടെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല!
• "Inconsistency" = same meaning as discrepancy — both mean "lack of agreement." Antonym = consistency (positive).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "In-" prefix = negative feel → antonym feel? ഇല്ല! Inconsistency ALSO = discrepancy = SYNONYM, not antonym! "Consistency" (no negative prefix) = antonym!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "DIS = apart (discrepancy = things apart/different). CON = together (consistency = things together/same). DIS ↔ CON = Discrepancy ↔ Consistency!"
• Prefix trick: "Dis-" = negative/apart | "In-" = negative (inconsistency = also negative = synonym!) | "Con-" = together/positive = antonym!
• PSC exam strategy: "Inconsistency" = most common wrong choice in this question — ഇത് SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല! "Consistency" = antonym. ഇത് ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഈ question-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText: Pick out the right synonym for the word 'awful' from the options given:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) terrible

• "Awful" = വളരെ മോശം, ഭയങ്കരമായ — "extremely bad, very unpleasant" എന്ന് meaning. "Terrible" = ഭയഭ്രാന്തമായ, വളരെ മോശം — exactly same meaning!
• "Awful" = "terrible" = synonyms — ഒരേ meaning, different words. "The weather was awful" = "The weather was terrible" — both sentences = same meaning.
• Synonym identify ചെയ്യാൻ: ആ word-ന്റെ emotion/category identify ചെയ്യൂ — "awful" = very bad/negative category, "terrible" = very bad/negative category — same category = synonym!
• "Terrible" = "terror" (ഭയം) root-ൽ നിന്ന് — ഭയം ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന/extremely bad = awful-ന്റെ perfect synonym.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Awful = Terrible = Horrible = Dreadful = Appalling — all mean "extremely bad"!
• "Awful" word history: originally "full of awe" = impressive, positive meaning. Modern English-ൽ = extremely bad (meaning shifted over centuries).
• Synonyms of "awful/terrible":
  - Terrible | Dreadful | Horrible | Appalling | Ghastly | Atrocious
• Antonyms of "awful":
  - Wonderful | Excellent | Magnificent | Splendid | Superb | Fantastic
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonym groups ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ — PSC-ൽ ഈ group-ൽ നിന്ന് questions വരും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The food at that restaurant was awful/terrible. (same meaning)
  2. She had a terrible/awful headache all morning. (interchangeable)
  3. The movie was dreadful — no one enjoyed it. (synonym of awful)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) nice
• "Nice" = pleasant, agreeable = നല്ലത്, സുഖകരം — "awful" = very bad-ന്റെ ANTONYM ആണ്, synonym അല്ല! Question asks synonym, "nice" = opposite direction.
• "Awful weather" ↔ "nice weather" — complete opposites. Synonym = same meaning, antonym = opposite meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Synonym question-ൽ antonym option-ൽ fall ആകുക — "nice" = awful-ന്റെ antonym, synonym അല്ല!

❌ (B) fight
• "Fight" = conflict/battle = ഒരു noun/verb. "Awful" = adjective describing something bad — completely different word class and meaning.
• "Fight" ≠ bad/terrible meaning. An "awful fight" = awful describes fight, they are not synonyms.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Awful fight" common phrase → "fight" seems connected — പക്ഷേ "awful" describes fight, they are not synonyms!

❌ (C) friendly
• "Friendly" = ദയയുള്ള, സൗഹൃദഭാവമുള്ള — positive adjective. "Awful" = negative adjective. Opposite emotions.
• "Friendly" = positive category, "awful" = negative category — antonym direction, synonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Friendly = opposite positive feel → seems related — പക്ഷേ awful ≠ unfriendly (they describe different things). Synonym = same meaning, "friendly" ≠ "awful"!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "AWFUL and TERRIBLE are twin brothers — both mean 'extremely BAD'! Awful weather = Terrible weather. See them as same-meaning twins!"
• Synonym group: awful = terrible = horrible = dreadful = appalling = ghastly. ഈ 6 words ഒരു group ആയി ഓർക്കൂ — all mean "very bad"!
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym question-ൽ emotion/category method use ചെയ്യൂ. "Awful" = very bad (negative). Options-ൽ also very bad (negative) meaning ഉള്ള word = synonym. "Terrible" = also very bad = synonym. "Nice/Friendly" = positive = antonym = wrong!

questionText: Find the meaning of idiom: 'beat around the bush'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) avoid saying something

• "Beat around the bush" = ഒരു കാര്യം നേരിട്ട് പറയാതെ ചുറ്റിപ്പറ്റി സംസാരിക്കുക = avoid coming to the main point carelessly or deliberately.
• "Stop beating around the bush and tell me what happened" = "ചുറ്റിപ്പറ്റി പറയാതെ, നടന്നത് നേരിട്ട് പറഞ്ഞോളൂ" — idiom-ന്റെ exact usage.
• "Avoid saying something" = talking around a subject without getting to the point = ഈ idiom-ന്റെ perfect meaning.
• Key concept: "around" = circular, indirect path — main topic-നെ directly address ചെയ്യാതെ around it talk ചെയ്യുക = indirect, avoiding.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "Beat AROUND the bush" = go AROUND the topic, not straight to it = indirect/avoiding!
• Idiom origin: Hunters would beat the surrounding bushes (around them) to drive animals out, instead of going directly to the main bush — indirect approach = beating AROUND!
• Similar idioms — direct/indirect communication (PSC must-know):
  - Beat around the bush = avoid talking directly (indirect)
  - Get to the point = talk about main matter directly
  - Come straight to the point = direct communication
  - Skirt around the issue = similar to beat around the bush
• PSC idioms — communication category:
  - Beat around the bush (indirect) | Spill the beans (reveal) | Hold your tongue (silent) | Break the ice (start conversation)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Beat around the bush" PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന idiom!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Stop beating around the bush and tell me the truth.
  2. He kept beating around the bush instead of asking for help directly.
  3. She finally stopped beating around the bush and admitted her mistake.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to agree completely
• "Agree completely" = പൂർണ്ണമായി സമ്മതിക്കുക — "beat around the bush" = avoiding/indirect ആണ്, agreement-ൽ opposite direction.
• Beating around bush = avoiding the topic. Agreeing completely = directly accepting — completely different meanings.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Agree" = positive outcome feel — പക്ഷേ idiom = indirect avoidance, agreement-ൽ connection ഇല്ല!

❌ (C) clear the forest
• "Clear the forest" = literal interpretation — "bush = forest" ആണ് എന്ന് ചിന്തിച്ചാൽ! Idioms = figurative meaning, literal meaning = always wrong!
• "Bush, beat, around" = literal words ഉപയോഗിച്ച് meaning guess ചെയ്യരുത് — idiom = figurative meaning always.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Bush = jungle/forest literal connection → "clear the forest" feel — idiom = figurative! Literal meanings = always wrong option in idiom questions!

❌ (D) pamper one's baby
• "Pamper one's baby" = no connection whatsoever to this idiom — "baby" word idiom-ൽ ഇല്ല, meaning-ൽ connection-ഉം ഇല്ല.
• Completely random option designed to confuse students who don't know the idiom.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar idiom → guess from words → no connection. ഇതുപോലുള്ള random options = idiom meaning clearly അറിയാതെ guess ചെയ്താൽ fall ആകും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Beat AROUND = go AROUND = circular path = indirect! Direct path = hit the bush straight. Going AROUND the bush = not saying directly! AROUND = avoiding the straight path!"
• Idiom pair: Beat around the bush (indirect, avoiding) ↔ Get to the point (direct). ഈ pair ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: "Beat around the bush" = ചുറ്റിവളഞ്ഞ്/avoiding — AROUND = circular = indirect = avoiding. Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning = always wrong. Figurative meaning = correct. ഇത് ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the sentence with the correct preposition:
We are very excited _____________ our trip to Dubai next week.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) about

• "Excited about" = standard English collocation — "excited" adjective-ൻ്റെ fixed preposition "about" ആണ്. ഇത് change ആകില്ല, fixed pair ആണ്.
• "We are very excited about our trip to Dubai" = "ഞങ്ങൾ ദുബായ് trip-നെക്കുറിച്ച് വളരെ ആകാംക്ഷയോടെ ഇരിക്കുകയാണ്."
• "About" = topic/subject refer ചെയ്യുന്ന preposition — excited "about" something = something-നെക്കുറിച്ച് excited. This is the natural English collocation.
• Feelings/emotions about topics = "about" — excited about, worried about, happy about, sad about, curious about — ഇവ all "about" use ചെയ്യുന്ന fixed collocations ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Feelings/emotions about topics → always "about"! Excited/worried/happy/sad/curious + ABOUT!
• Adjective + Preposition collocations — PSC must-know:
  - excited ABOUT | worried ABOUT | happy ABOUT | angry ABOUT
  - curious ABOUT | enthusiastic ABOUT | nervous ABOUT | passionate ABOUT
  - good AT | bad AT | clever AT | skilled AT (abilities/skills)
  - interested IN | involved IN | experienced IN (involvement)
  - proud OF | afraid OF | fond OF | tired OF | aware OF (feelings about things)
  - pleased WITH | satisfied WITH | bored WITH | familiar WITH (satisfaction)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective + preposition collocations PSC-ൽ top question type! "Excited about" = PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is excited about her new job. (excited + about ✓)
  2. He is worried about the exam results. (worried + about ✓)
  3. They are happy about the team's performance. (happy + about ✓)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) at
• "Excited at" = some older/informal usage-ൽ possible, പക്ഷേ modern standard English = "excited about." PSC = standard collocations.
• "At" = skills/abilities (good at, bad at) or location context — feelings about topics = "about," not "at."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "At" = common preposition → random selection — പക്ഷേ "excited" takes "about," not "at." Collocation fixed ആണ്!

❌ (B) with
• "Excited with" = non-standard collocation. "With" = satisfaction/dissatisfaction context (pleased with, satisfied with, bored with) — "excited with" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "Pleased with | satisfied with | bored with" = correct. "Excited with" = wrong combination.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "With" = common preposition → "excited with" natural feel — പക്ഷേ "excited" takes "about," "with" wrong!

❌ (C) over
• "Excited over" = informal/colloquial, not standard. "Over" = formal context: control over, authority over, victory over — "excited" collocation-ൽ "over" standard അല്ല.
• PSC exams = standard English collocations test ചെയ്യുന്നു — "excited about" = standard, "excited over" = non-standard.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Over the moon" (excited idiom) → "excited over"? ഇല്ല! "Over the moon" = idiom, "excited over" = non-standard collocation!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "EXCITED ABOUT = talking ABOUT something that excites you! You talk ABOUT the trip = you're EXCITED ABOUT the trip! ABOUT = the topic you're excited about!"
• Collocation family: excited ABOUT | worried ABOUT | happy ABOUT | curious ABOUT | sad ABOUT — feelings about topics = ALWAYS "about"!
• PSC exam strategy: "Excited" blank-ൽ → automatic "about." Collocation list memorize ചെയ്തൊരു second thought-ഉം വേണ്ട — excited about, interested in, good at, proud of, satisfied with. Each adjective has its ONE fixed preposition partner. ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Write the right form of the verb that agrees with the subject:
In his garden, the lights _____________ on automatically.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) turn

• "The lights" = plural noun — "lights" plural ആണ്, singular അല്ല. Plural subject-ന് Simple Present tense-ൽ verb-ൽ "-s" ഉണ്ടാകരുത്.
• Plural subject + Simple Present = base form of verb (no -s) — "The lights turn on automatically" = correct subject-verb agreement.
• "In his garden, the lights turn on automatically" = "അദ്ദേഹത്തിൻ്റെ തോട്ടത്തിൽ lights സ്വയം on ആകുന്നു" — plural subject "lights" → plural verb "turn."
• Subject-verb agreement rule: Singular subject → verb + s/es | Plural subject → verb (no s/es). "Lights" = plural → "turn" (no -s).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Plural subject → verb WITHOUT -s! Singular subject → verb WITH -s! (Opposite S rule!)
• Subject-Verb Agreement — Simple Present:
  - Singular (he/she/it/one thing): The light turns on. (turns = -s added)
  - Plural (they/we/multiple things): The lights turn on. (turn = no -s)
• Opposite S trick: "Lights" (noun ends in S = plural) → "turn" (verb has NO S). Noun-ൽ S ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ verb-ൽ S ഉണ്ടാകരുത്!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Subject-verb agreement PSC-ൽ regular question! Plural subject + "-s" verb = classic wrong option!
• Tricky cases PSC tests:
  - Collective nouns → usually singular: The team wins.
  - Each/Every + noun → singular: Every student needs a book.
  - Neither/Either + nor/or → nearest subject: Neither he nor they are wrong.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The children play in the park every evening. (plural → play, no -s)
  2. The flower blooms in spring. (singular → blooms, with -s)
  3. The machines work automatically in the factory. (plural → work, no -s)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) turns
• "Turns" = singular verb (-s added) — "The light turns on" = singular subject "light" → correct. പക്ഷേ "The lights turns on" = plural subject + singular verb = subject-verb disagreement!
• "Lights" = plural → singular verb "turns" = wrong combination. Plural subject needs plural verb "turn."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Turns on" phrasal verb = natural-sounding phrase feel — പക്ഷേ plural "lights" → "turns" wrong! "Turn on" = plural verb = correct!

❌ (B) turning
• "Turning" = present participle — auxiliary verb ഇല്ലാതെ (is/are) bare "turning" main verb ആകില്ല. "The lights turning on" = incomplete sentence.
• Present continuous passive = "are turning" — but "turning" alone = grammatically incomplete.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "-ing" form = modern/active feel → correct feel — പക്ഷേ "turning" alone (without "are") = incomplete, not a complete verb!

❌ (C) is turning
• "Is turning" = present continuous, singular auxiliary "is" — "lights" = plural → needs "are turning," not "is turning."
• "Is" = singular auxiliary (he/she/it is), "are" = plural auxiliary (they/lights are) — "lights" = plural → "is" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Is turning on" = natural-sounding — പക്ഷേ "lights" = plural → "are turning," not "is turning"!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LIGHTS (plural S) → TURN (no S)! Noun has S = verb loses S! Opposite S rule — noun-ൽ S ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ verb-ൽ S ഉണ്ടാകരുത്!"
• Quick check: Subject plural ആണോ? → Verb-ൽ -s ഇല്ല. Subject singular ആണോ? → Verb-ൽ -s ഉണ്ട്.
• PSC exam strategy: Subject-verb agreement blank-ൽ → ആദ്യം subject find ചെയ്ത് singular/plural decide ചെയ്യൂ. "Lights" = plural → -s ഇല്ലാത്ത verb = "turn." ഇത് ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Write the comparative form of the adjective in the sentence given:
Michelle is _____________ (tall) than her sister.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) taller

• "Michelle is _____ than her sister" — sentence-ൽ "than" ഉണ്ട്. "Than" = key signal word for comparative degree.
• "Than" കണ്ടാൽ → comparative form use ചെയ്യണം. "Tall" → comparative = "taller."
• "Taller" = comparative degree of "tall" — two people (Michelle + her sister) compare ചെയ്യുന്നു → comparative degree correct.
• "Michelle is taller than her sister" = "Michelle, sister-നെക്കാൾ ഉയരമുള്ളവളാണ്" — correct comparison.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "than" = comparative (-er/more)! "the...in/of" = superlative (-est/most)!
• Degrees of Comparison — three forms:
  - Positive: tall (simple quality — ഉയരമുണ്ട്)
  - Comparative: taller + than (two compare — ഇതിനെക്കാൾ ഉയരം)
  - Superlative: the tallest (highest among many — എല്ലാറ്റിലും ഉയരം)
• Formation:
  - Short adjectives (1-2 syllables): add -er/-est → tall/taller/tallest
  - Long adjectives (3+ syllables): more/most → beautiful/more beautiful/most beautiful
• Key signal words:
  - "than" in sentence → comparative (-er/more)
  - "the...in/of group" in sentence → superlative (-est/most)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Degrees of comparison "than" signal — PSC-ൽ guaranteed question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is smarter than her brother. (than → comparative)
  2. This is the most beautiful flower in the garden. (the...in → superlative)
  3. He runs faster than anyone in the class. (than → comparative)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) tallest
• "Tallest" = superlative — group-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഉയരമുള്ളത്. "Than her sister" = only TWO compared → comparative needed, superlative അല്ല.
• Superlative = 3 or more compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ. Two people = comparative. "Michelle and her sister" = 2 people = comparative.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Tallest" = most impressive-sounding — പക്ഷേ "than" signal = comparative! Superlative needs group/the, not "than"!

❌ (B) tall
• "Tall" = positive degree — plain description, comparison ഇല്ല. "Michelle is tall" = simple statement, comparison express ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• "Than her sister" = comparison happening → positive degree comparison convey ചെയ്യില്ല. Comparative form mandatory.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple form "tall" = clean look → correct feel — പക്ഷേ "than" = comparison signal = comparative "taller" needed!

❌ (D) the tallest
• "The tallest" = superlative with "the" — two people (Michelle + sister) only compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ superlative ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. Plus "than" signal = comparative, not superlative.
• "The tallest" = needs a group of 3 or more + "in/of" phrase — "than her sister" = comparative context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "The tallest" = formal/complete sound → correct feel — പക്ഷേ TWO people + "than" = taller (comparative), "the tallest" wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "THAN = comparaTIVE! Both have the letters T-A-N! THAN and comparaTIVE = partners! See THAN → think -ER!"
• Signal word shortcut: "than" → -er/more (comparative) | "the...in/of" → -est/most (superlative) | no comparison word → positive.
• PSC exam strategy: Degree blank-ൽ → ആദ്യം "than" ഉണ്ടോ check ചെയ്യൂ. Yes → comparative form (-er). "The...in/of" → superlative (-est). One keyword = instant answer, guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank with suitable article:
This is _____________ man I saw in Malaysia.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) the

• "The man I saw in Malaysia" = specific man — "I saw in Malaysia" clause ഈ man-നെ specifically identify ചെയ്യുന്നു. Specific/identified noun → "the" mandatory.
• "The" = definite article — already identified, specific, unique noun-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. "I saw in Malaysia" = identifying clause → "the" use ചെയ്യണം.
• "This is the man I saw in Malaysia" = "ഇതാണ് ഞാൻ Malaysia-ൽ കണ്ട ആ മനുഷ്യൻ" — "the" = ആ specific man-നെ refer ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Rule: noun + identifying clause (I saw / who / which / that / where) → always "the" — noun specific ആകുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Noun + identifying clause (I saw/who/which/that) → ALWAYS "the"!
• "The" use ചെയ്യുന്ന situations:
  - Specific/identified noun: "the man I saw" (I saw = identifying)
  - Superlatives: the highest, the best
  - Unique things: the sun, the moon, the earth
  - Second mention: "I saw a dog. The dog was friendly."
  - Ordinals: the first, the second, the last
• "A/An" use ചെയ്യുന്ന situations:
  - Non-specific, first mention: "I saw a man." (any man, no identification)
  - Any one of many: "She wants to buy a house."
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Noun + identifying clause = "the" — PSC top article rule! "The man who/that/I saw" = always "the"!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The girl who helped me was very kind. (who = identifying → the)
  2. This is the book I told you about. (I told you about = identifying → the)
  3. The man that called you is waiting outside. (that = identifying → the)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) an
• "An" = indefinite article before vowel sound. "Man" = /m/ sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു — consonant sound, "an" grammatically wrong.
• Plus "an" = non-specific (indefinite). "The man I saw" = specific → indefinite article wrong — double error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "An" = before vowel letter feel → "m" = consonant sound, "an" wrong. Plus specific man = "the"!

❌ (B) a
• "A" = indefinite article — non-specific, any one of many. "This is a man I saw" = any man (indefinite) — wrong meaning.
• "I saw in Malaysia" clause = specifically identifying this man → "a" (non-specific) wrong here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "A man" = very common phrase → familiar feel — പക്ഷേ identifying clause "I saw in Malaysia" = specific man = "the"!

❌ (D) none (no article)
• No article = generic/uncountable reference. "Man" = singular countable noun → article mandatory. "This is man I saw" = grammatically incorrect.
• Singular countable noun without article = grammar error in English.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "No article" = sophisticated feel — പക്ഷേ singular countable noun = article mandatory, identifying clause = "the" mandatory!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "THE = THAT specific one! 'THE man I saw' = THAT specific man, not just any man! Identifying clause = THE specific one!"
• Article quick rule: noun + "I saw / who / which / that / where" = identifying clause = always THE. ഈ pattern ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ article questions miss ആകില്ല!
• PSC exam strategy: Article blank-ൽ blank-ന് ശേഷം noun + clause ഉണ്ടോ check ചെയ്യൂ. "man I saw / book that / girl who" = identifying clause = THE = instant answer, guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the right one from the option to form the correct phrasal verb:
We couldn't take _____________ our jackets in the whole trip. It was very cold everywhere.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) off

• "Take off" = clothing remove ചെയ്യുക = ഊരി മാറ്റുക — ഇതൊരു standard English phrasal verb ആണ്.
• "We couldn't take off our jackets" = "ഞങ്ങൾക്ക് jackets ഊരി മാറ്റാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല" — cold weather-ൽ jackets remove ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല.
• Context clue: "It was very cold everywhere" = cold ആണ്, jackets remove ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല = "take off" meaning perfectly fits.
• "Take off" = clothing-ൻ്റെ context-ൽ remove/ഊരുക — ഇത് fixed phrasal verb meaning ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Clothes come OFF your body! Take OFF = clothes go OFF = remove! ON clothes → take OFF clothes!
• "Take off" — multiple meanings:
  - Remove clothing: Take off your shoes. Take off your jacket. (ഊരി മാറ്റുക)
  - Airplane departure: The plane took off at 9 AM. (പറക്കുക)
  - Sudden success: Her career really took off after that film. (വിജയിക്കുക)
  - Leave suddenly: He took off without saying goodbye. (പോകുക)
• Other "take" phrasal verbs PSC must-know:
  - take UP = start a hobby: She took up painting.
  - take DOWN = write down: Take down the notes.
  - take OVER = assume control: He took over the company.
  - take AFTER = resemble: She takes after her mother.
  - take IN = deceive/understand: I was taken in by his lies.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "Take off" = clothing context = remove — PSC-ൽ context clue-ൽ identify ചെയ്യേണ്ട phrasal verb!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Please take off your shoes before entering. (remove clothing)
  2. The flight took off on time despite the fog. (airplane departs)
  3. She took up painting after retirement. (start a hobby)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) down
• "Take down" = write something down / dismantle something — "Take down notes" = notes എഴുതുക. Clothing remove ചെയ്യുന്ന meaning-ൽ "take down" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Context: "couldn't take down our jackets" = no established meaning. Clothing removal = "take OFF."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Take down" = remove feel (dismantling) — പക്ഷേ clothing remove = "take OFF," not "take DOWN"!

❌ (B) up
• "Take up" = start a new activity/hobby — "She took up painting" = painting start ചെയ്തു. Jacket remove ചെയ്യുന്ന context-ൽ "take up" meaning ഇല്ല.
• "Take up space" = space occupy ചെയ്യുക — jackets remove ചെയ്യുന്ന context-ൽ fit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Take up jackets" = pick up feel — പക്ഷേ cold weather, couldn't remove = take OFF, not take UP!

❌ (D) in to
• "Take into" = consider/bring inside — "Take into account" = ഒരു കാര്യം consider ചെയ്യുക. Jacket removal context-ൽ meaning ഇല്ല.
• "In to" = not a standard particle for clothing removal in any context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Take into" = formal phrase feel — പക്ഷേ clothing removal = "take OFF" only!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Clothes are ON your body. Remove them = take them OFF! ON → OFF! Put ON jacket, take OFF jacket — ON and OFF are opposites!"
• Phrasal verb "take" family: take OFF (clothes/plane) | take UP (hobby) | take DOWN (notes) | take OVER (control) | take AFTER (resemble). ഓരോന്നിനും distinct meaning!
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb question-ൽ context clue ഉപയോഗിക്കൂ. "Jackets" + "cold everywhere, couldn't remove" = take OFF! Clothing + remove = OFF. Context = key to phrasal verb answers!

questionText:
Select the word with correct spelling:
Mrs. Naveen introduced the new _____________ to the PTA members.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) principal

• "Mrs. Naveen introduced the new _____ to the PTA members" — "PTA" = Parent Teacher Association = school context. School head = "principal."
• "Principal" = school/college head — "The principal of our school" = school-ൻ്റെ head person. PTA context-ൽ school head introduce ചെയ്യുന്നു = "principal."
• Correct spelling: p-r-i-n-c-i-p-a-l = "principal" (school head/main person). "Principle" = a rule/belief = different word!
• Context + spelling = two-level check: context (school = principal) + spelling (principal, not principle) — both confirm option C.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: principAL = A person (school head). principLE = a ruLE (belief/standard). AL = person, LE = rule!
• Principal vs Principle — PSC classic confusion pair:
  PRINCIPAL:
  - Noun = school/college head: "The principal of our school"
  - Adjective = main/chief: "The principal reason | the principal actor"
  - Finance = original sum: "principal amount"
  Memory: principAL = principAL person (AL = a person)
  PRINCIPLE:
  - Noun only = a rule, belief, moral standard
  - "the principle of equality | moral principles | scientific principle"
  Memory: principLE = ruLE (both end in -LE)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Principal vs Principle — PSC classic confusable words! Context check + spelling check = two-step method!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The principal welcomed the new students. (school head = principal)
  2. She always stands by her principles. (moral rules = principle)
  3. The principal reason for his success was hard work. (main/chief = principal)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) principle
• "Principle" = correct spelling, പക്ഷേ WRONG meaning in this context. "Principle" = a rule or belief, school head അല്ല.
• "She introduced the new principle" = wrong meaning — PTA-ൽ introduce ചെയ്യുന്നത് a person (school head), a rule/belief അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Principle" correctly spelled → looks right! പക്ഷേ wrong word. Context (PTA = school) = principal (person), principle (rule) അല്ല!

❌ (B) prinsiple
• "Prinsiple" = wrong spelling — "princi-" ആണ് correct, "prinsi-" wrong. Neither "principal" nor "principle" correct form ഇല്ല.
• "c" not "s" — p-r-i-n-c-i-p-l-e = correct. "Prinsiple" = misspelled.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Phonetic spelling attempt → "prinsiple" sounds like "principle" — പക്ഷേ correct = "princi-" not "prinsi-"!

❌ (D) prinsipal
• "Prinsipal" = wrong spelling — "princi-" ആണ് correct, "prinsi-" wrong. Correct = p-r-i-n-c-i-p-a-l.
• "c" not "s" — "princiPAL" not "prinsiPAL."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Phonetic attempt at "principal" — correct = "princi-" not "prinsi-"!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "principAL = your PAL who's the school head! My PAL is the principAL! principLE = a ruLE — both end in LE! PAL = person, LE = rule — never mix up!"
• Word distinction: Principal (AL) = person/chief. Principle (LE) = rule/belief. AL = a person. LE = like a rule.
• PSC exam strategy: Principal/Principle question-ൽ → context check ചെയ്യൂ. School/college/PTA = principal (AL = person). Moral/scientific/ethical = principle (LE = rule). Plus spelling: always "princi-" not "prinsi-"!

questionText:
Choose the suitable collective noun to complete the phrase given below:
A _______ of stairs.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) flight

• 'A flight of stairs' എന്നത് ഒരു നിലയ്ക്കും മറ്റൊരു നിലയ്ക്കും ഇടയിലുള്ള പടിക്കെട്ടുകളുടെ കൂട്ടത്തെ (a set of stairs) സൂചിപ്പിക്കുന്ന standard collective noun ആണ്.
• 'Flight' എന്ന വാക്ക് 'flying' (പറക്കൽ) എന്ന concept-മായി ബന്ധപ്പെട്ടിരിക്കുന്നു — stairs ഒരു level-ൽ നിന്ന് മറ്റൊരു level-ലേക്ക് 'rise' ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ ഈ പേര് ഉണ്ടായി.
• 'I climbed two flights of stairs to reach the office' — ഇതുപോലെ PSC exam-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കാം.
• Kerala PSC-ൽ Collective Nouns ഒരു favourite topic ആണ്. 'Flight of stairs' ഒരിക്കൽ പഠിച്ചാൽ ജീവിതകാലം മുഴുവൻ ഓർക്കും!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Collective noun = ഒരു കൂട്ടത്തെ ഒരൊറ്റ വാക്കിൽ describe ചെയ്യുന്നത്.
• Things-നുള്ള ചില important collective nouns:
  - A flight of stairs (പടിക്കെട്ടുകൾ)
  - A fleet of ships/cars (കപ്പലുകൾ / കാറുകൾ)
  - A bunch of keys/flowers (താക്കോലുകൾ / പൂക്കൾ)
  - A pack of cards (ചീട്ടുകൾ)
  - A suite of rooms/furniture (മുറികൾ / ഫർണിച്ചർ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns for things, animals, people — ഇവ PSC-ൽ വളരെ frequently വരുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She ran up a flight of stairs. (അവൾ ഒരു set പടിക്കെട്ടു കയറി ഓടി)
  2. A fleet of ships sailed into the harbour. (ഒരു fleet കപ്പലുകൾ തുറമുഖത്ത് വന്നടുത്തു)
  3. He pulled out a bunch of keys from his pocket. (അദ്ദേഹം ഒരു bunch താക്കോലുകൾ pocket-ൽ നിന്ന് എടുത്തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) suite — 'A suite' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് rooms-നും furniture-നും ആണ്: 'a suite of rooms', 'a suite of furniture'. Stairs-നു suite ചേരില്ല — ഇത് PSC-ൽ confuse ചെയ്യാൻ വച്ചതാണ്.

❌ (C) fleet — 'A fleet' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് ships, cars, planes, buses-നു ആണ്: 'a fleet of ships', 'a fleet of vehicles'. Stairs-ന് fleet ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.

❌ (D) pack — 'A pack' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് cards, wolves, lies-നു ആണ്: 'a pack of cards', 'a pack of wolves'. Stairs-നു pack തെറ്റ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Flight' = stairs fly up! — Stairs ഒരു level-ൽ നിന്ന് മറ്റൊരു level-ലേക്ക് 'fly' ചെയ്യുന്നതുപോലെ rise ചെയ്യുന്നു. ഇത് ഓർത്താൽ 'a flight of stairs' ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കില്ല!
• Word family to learn together: flight of stairs | fleet of ships | flock of birds | flock of sheep — 'fl' words are collective noun favourites!
• PSC exam strategy: Collective noun question വരുമ്പോൾ ആദ്യം wrong options eliminate ചെയ്യുക. Suite = rooms, fleet = vehicles, pack = cards/wolves — ഇവ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ flight of stairs ആണ് answer എന്ന് easily identify ചെയ്യാം. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Add the right question tag to the following statement to make it a question.
She seldom goes out, _______?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) does she?

• 'Seldom' എന്ന word-ന് negative meaning ആണ് — 'വളരെ അപൂർവ്വമായി' എന്നർഥം. അതായത് 'She seldom goes out' = 'She almost never goes out' — ഇത് ഒരു negative sentence ആണ്.
• Negative meaning ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ POSITIVE question tag ഉപയോഗിക്കണം — ഇതാണ് Question Tag-ന്റെ golden rule.
• Subject 'she' (third person singular) ആണ്, verb 'goes' Simple Present ആണ്. അതുകൊണ്ട് auxiliary verb 'does' + subject 'she' = 'does she?' ആണ് correct tag.
• 'She seldom goes out, does she?' — ഈ sentence-ൽ speaker ഒരു negative fact confirm ചെയ്യാൻ ശ്രമിക്കുകയാണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive statement → Negative tag | Negative statement → Positive tag
• Negative adverbs ഉള്ള sentences-ൽ POSITIVE tag വേണം:
  - seldom, rarely, hardly, never, scarcely, barely — ഇവ negative meaning ഉള്ള words ആണ്.
  - ഇവ sentence-ൽ ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ sentence negative ആണ് → positive tag വേണം.
• Simple Present tense-ൽ tag ഉണ്ടാക്കൽ:
  - He/She/It + goes = does he/she/it? (positive tag)
  - I/We/You/They + go = do I/we/you/they? (positive tag)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Seldom/never/rarely ഉള്ള question tags PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരുന്ന ഒരു tricky type ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She never lies, does she? (അവൾ ഒരിക്കലും കളവ് പറയാറില്ല, അല്ലേ?)
  2. He rarely comes on time, does he? (അദ്ദേഹം rarely time-ന് വരാറില്ല, അല്ലേ?)
  3. They hardly speak, do they? (അവർ ഏതാണ്ട് സംസാരിക്കാറേ ഇല്ല, അല്ലേ?)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) don't she? — 'don't' grammatically wrong ആണ്. 'She' (third person singular) ആയതുകൊണ്ട് 'don't' use ചെയ്യാൻ പറ്റില്ല — 'doesn't' വേണം. Plus 'seldom' ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് negative tag വേണ്ടേ ഇല്ല. Double error!

❌ (C) doesn't she? — 'seldom' ഒരു negative adverb ആണ്. Sentence already negative ആണ്. അതുകൊണ്ട് negative tag 'doesn't she?' ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത് — ഇത് double negative ആകും. Positive tag 'does she?' ആണ് correct.

❌ (D) did she? — 'did' Past tense auxiliary ആണ്. Sentence-ൽ 'goes' Simple Present ആണ്. Tag-ൽ main sentence-ന്റെ tense തന്നെ ഉണ്ടായിരിക്കണം. Present tense sentence → Present tense tag (does, doesn't). Past tense-ലെ 'did' ഇവിടെ തെറ്റ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SNHRS words = Negative meaning!' — Seldom, Never, Hardly, Rarely, Scarcely — ഈ 5 words ഉള്ള sentence negative ആണ്. Positive tag use ചെയ്യുക!
• Word family — Negative adverbs: seldom | rarely | hardly | never | scarcely | barely — ഇവ ഒരുമിച്ച് പഠിക്കുക.
• PSC exam strategy: Question tag ചോദ്യം വരുമ്പോൾ ആദ്യം sentence-ൽ negative word ഉണ്ടോ എന്ന് check ചെയ്യുക. Seldom / never / rarely / hardly — ഏതെങ്കിലും കണ്ടാൽ positive tag (does/do/is/are/has/have) select ചെയ്യുക!

questionText:
Fill up with the correct preposition:
Rohan is interested _______ learning new languages.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) in

• 'Interested in' ഒരു fixed collocation ആണ് — 'interested' എന്ന adjective-നു ശേഷം എപ്പോഴും 'in' ആണ് വരുന്നത്. ഇത് English grammar-ൽ ഒരു rigid rule ആണ്.
• 'Rohan is interested in learning new languages' = 'Rohan-ന് പുതിയ ഭാഷകൾ പഠിക്കുന്നതിൽ താൽപ്പര്യം ഉണ്ട്' — ഇതാണ് sentence-ന്റെ അർഥം.
• ഒരു adjective-നു ശേഷം ഏത് preposition വരണം എന്നത് fixed ആണ്. 'Interested + in' — ഇത് combination ആണ്, ഒരിക്കലും മറ്റ് preposition ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത്.
• PSC exams-ൽ adjective + preposition collocations ഒരു frequent topic ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Adjective + Preposition = Fixed Pair — ഇവ memorize ചെയ്യണം, rule derive ചെയ്യാൻ പറ്റില്ല!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective+Preposition collocations PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരുന്ന ഒരു topic ആണ്.
• Important Adjective + Preposition pairs:
  - interested IN (learning, music, sports)
  - good AT (mathematics, cooking)
  - afraid OF (dogs, darkness)
  - full OF (water, happiness)
  - proud OF (achievements)
  - responsible FOR (mistakes)
  - different FROM (others)
  - similar TO (something)
  - angry WITH/AT (person), angry ABOUT (thing)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is interested in painting. (അവൾക്ക് painting-ൽ താൽപ്പര്യം ഉണ്ട്)
  2. He is good at mathematics. (അദ്ദേഹം mathematics-ൽ നല്ല കഴിവ് ഉള്ള ആൾ ആണ്)
  3. They are proud of their achievement. (അവർ തങ്ങളുടെ നേട്ടത്തിൽ അഭിമാനിക്കുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to — 'interested to' grammatically wrong ആണ്. 'Interested' എന്ന adjective-നു ശേഷം 'to' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'To' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് 'similar to', 'compared to' ഇത്തരം collocations-ലാണ്. 'Interested' with 'to' — ഇത് ഒരിക്കലും correct ആകില്ല.

❌ (C) with — 'interested with' English-ൽ ഇല്ലാത്ത ഒരു combination ആണ്. 'With' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് 'angry with someone', 'familiar with something' ഇത്തരം contexts-ൽ ആണ്. 'Interested' with 'with' — incorrect.

❌ (D) for — 'interested for' ഉം English-ൽ incorrect ആണ്. 'For' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് 'responsible for', 'ready for', 'famous for' ഇത്തരം collocations-ൽ ആണ്. 'Interested' കൊണ്ട് 'for' use ചെയ്യില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IN = Inside your mind' — 'Interested IN' something = ആ topic മനസ്സിന്റെ INSIDE-ൽ കടക്കുന്നു! ഇൻ്ററസ്റ്റ് ഉള്ളത് 'IN' ആക്കി think ചെയ്യുക.
• Word family to learn together: interested IN | involved IN | succeed IN | result IN | believe IN — 'IN' family!
• PSC exam strategy: Preposition question വരുമ്പോൾ adjective-noun ഇവ identify ചെയ്ത് fixed collocation recall ചെയ്യുക. 'Interested in' ഒരു exam-ൽ ഒരിക്കലെങ്കിലും വരുന്ന combination ആണ്. ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Change the following sentence from active to passive form. Choose your answer from the given options:
My cousin has drawn this picture.
optionA: This picture is drawn by my cousin
optionB: This picture was drawn by my cousin
optionC: This picture has been drawn by my cousin
optionD: This picture had been drawn by my cousin

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) This picture has been drawn by my cousin

• Active sentence: 'My cousin has drawn this picture' — ഇത് Present Perfect tense ആണ്. 'Has drawn' = Present Perfect Active form.
• Active → Passive rule: Active tense = Passive tense. Present Perfect Active → Present Perfect Passive.
• Present Perfect Passive formula: has/have + been + V3 (past participle).
• 'Has drawn' → 'has been drawn' — ഇതാണ് correct passive form.
• Object 'this picture' subject ആകുന്നു, 'My cousin' → 'by my cousin' ആകുന്നു: 'This picture has been drawn by my cousin.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active tense → Same tense Passive (tense NEVER changes!)
• Passive voice-ന്റെ basic formula: Object + be verb (tense) + V3 + by + Subject
• Tense-wise Passive forms:
  - Simple Present: is/am/are + V3
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3
  - Present Continuous: is/am/are + being + V3
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3
  - Past Perfect: had + been + V3
  - Simple Future: will be + V3
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Active to Passive conversion — ഇത് ഏതാണ്ട് എല്ലാ PSC exam-ലും ഒരു question ഉണ്ടാകും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has written a letter. → A letter has been written by her.
  2. They have built a new house. → A new house has been built by them.
  3. He has completed the work. → The work has been completed by him.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) This picture is drawn by my cousin — 'is drawn' Simple Present Passive ആണ്. Original sentence Present Perfect ('has drawn') ആണ്. Tense change ചെയ്തത് wrong. Active tense = Passive tense rule break ചെയ്തിരിക്കുന്നു. 🪤 PSC Trap: ഏറ്റവും simple-ആക്കി 'is drawn' choose ചെയ്ത് students തെറ്റ് ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) This picture was drawn by my cousin — 'was drawn' Simple Past Passive ആണ്. Original sentence Present Perfect ആണ്, Past Simple അല്ല. Tense wrong ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has drawn' = past action ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും, but Present Perfect-ന് Passive 'has been drawn' ആണ്, 'was drawn' അല്ല.

❌ (D) This picture had been drawn by my cousin — 'had been drawn' Past Perfect Passive ആണ്. Original sentence Present Perfect ആണ്. 'Had + been + V3' = Past Perfect Passive — ഇത് Past Perfect Active-ൽ നിന്ന് ഉണ്ടാകുന്നതാണ്. Present Perfect active-ൽ നിന്ന് 'has been drawn' ആണ് വരേണ്ടത്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAS = HAS BEEN' — Present Perfect active-ൽ 'has/have' കണ്ടാൽ Passive-ൽ 'has/have BEEN' ഉണ്ടാകും. 'HAS' → 'HAS BEEN drawn'. Simple!
• Word family — Passive forms to memorize: is drawn | was drawn | has been drawn | had been drawn | will be drawn — ഈ 5 forms ഒരുമിച്ച് practise ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: Active to Passive question-ൽ ആദ്യം original sentence-ലെ tense identify ചെയ്യുക. 'Has/have + V3' = Present Perfect → answer-ൽ 'has/have + been + V3' ഉള്ള option select ചെയ്യുക. Tense match ആകുന്നത് always correct answer ആണ്!

questionText:
Fill up suitably:
'Wide' means the same as _______.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) broad

• 'Wide' = 'Broad' — ഇവ രണ്ടും ഒരേ അർഥം ഉള്ള synonyms ആണ്. 'Wide' = 'വിശാലമായ, വീതിയുള്ള' — 'broad' ഉം ഇതേ meaning ആണ്.
• 'A wide road' = 'A broad road' — 'വിശാലമായ ഒരു റോഡ്'. 'Wide shoulders' = 'Broad shoulders' — 'വീതിയേറിയ തോളുകൾ'.
• English-ൽ synonyms-ന്റെ ഉദ്ദേശം ഒരു concept-ൽ variety of expression ഉണ്ടാക്കാൻ ആണ്. 'Wide' ഉം 'broad' ഉം interchangeable ആണ്.
• Vocabulary-ൽ synonyms PSC-ൽ ഒരു must-know topic ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Synonym = Same meaning, different word. Antonym = Opposite meaning.
• 'Wide' / 'Broad' word family:
  - Wide: width (noun), widen (verb), widely (adverb), widespread (adjective)
  - Broad: breadth (noun), broaden (verb), broadly (adverb), broad-minded (adjective)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms and antonyms — ഇത് ഏതാണ്ട് എല്ലാ PSC paper-ലും ഒരു question ഉണ്ടാകും.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Narrow' = antonym of 'wide'. 'Long' = length (different dimension). 'High' = height (vertical). ഇവ confuse ചെയ്യിക്കാൻ ആണ് options-ൽ ഉൾക്കൊണ്ടത്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The river is very wide/broad at this point. (ഈ ഭാഗത്ത് നദി വളരെ വിശാലമാണ്)
  2. He has a wide/broad knowledge of history. (അദ്ദേഹത്തിന് ചരിത്രത്തെക്കുറിച്ച് വിശാലമായ അറിവ് ഉണ്ട്)
  3. This road is not wide/broad enough for two trucks. (ഈ റോഡ് രണ്ട് ട്രക്കുകൾക്ക് വേണ്ടത്ര വിശാലമല്ല)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) long — 'Long' = 'നീളം ഉള്ള' (length). 'Wide' = 'വീതിയുള്ള' (width). ഇവ different dimensions ആണ്. Long road = നീളമുള്ള റോഡ്. Wide road = വീതിയുള്ള റോഡ്. Synonym ആകില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: Both describe size, but different aspects of size.

❌ (B) narrow — 'Narrow' = 'ഇടുങ്ങിയ' — ഇത് 'wide'-ന്റെ ANTONYM (opposite) ആണ്, synonym അല്ല! 'Narrow' = opposite of 'wide/broad'. PSC-ൽ ഇത്തരം antonym-ഉം synonym-ഉം confuse ചെയ്യിക്കുന്ന options വച്ചിരിക്കും. ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക!

❌ (C) high — 'High' = 'ഉയരം ഉള്ള' (height, vertical dimension). 'Wide' = horizontal dimension. ഇവ completely different dimensions ആണ്. A high building = ഉയരമുള്ള building. A wide building = വിശാലമായ building. Synonym ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BROAD smile is WIDE!' — ഒരാൾ broad smile ചിരിക്കുമ്പോൾ face wide ആകുന്നു — BROAD = WIDE ഓർക്കൽ easy!
• Word family to learn: wide ↔ narrow (antonyms) | wide = broad (synonyms) | long ↔ short (antonyms) | high ↔ low (antonyms) — ഇവ pairs ആക്കി ഒരുമിച്ച് study ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym question-ൽ ആദ്യം antonym identify ചെയ്ത് eliminate ചെയ്യുക. 'Narrow' clearly opposite of 'wide' → ഉടനെ eliminate. 'Long' ഉം 'high' ഉം different dimensions → eliminate. ബാക്കി 'broad' — correct answer!

questionText:
Replace the underlined word in the following sentence with the suitable phrasal verb:
We will __cancel__ the trip if the weather is bad.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) call off

• 'Call off' = 'cancel' (ഒരു plan, event, meeting etc. നടത്തേണ്ടതില്ല എന്ന് decide ചെയ്യുക). 'We will call off the trip if the weather is bad' = 'കാലാവസ്ഥ മോശമാണെങ്കിൽ നമ്മൾ യാത്ര cancel ചെയ്യും'.
• Phrasal verbs-ൽ 'call off' PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും popular ആയ ഒന്നാണ്. ഇതിന്റെ meaning 'cancel' ആണ് — ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കില്ല.
• 'Cancel' = 'call off' — ഇവ perfect synonyms ആണ്. Event, match, meeting, trip, strike — ഇവ call off ചെയ്യാം.
• Sentence-ൽ underlined word 'cancel'-ന്റെ exact replacement ആണ് 'call off'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Phrasal verb = Verb + Preposition/Adverb → new idiomatic meaning.
• 'CALL' phrasal verb family:
  - Call off = cancel (match/meeting/event ഒഴിവാക്കുക)
  - Call on = visit someone formally (ആരെയെങ്കിലും formally സന്ദർശിക്കുക)
  - Call up = phone / summon (ഫോൺ ചെയ്യുക / വിളിപ്പിക്കുക)
  - Call for = require / demand (ആവശ്യപ്പെടുക)
  - Call out = shout / challenge (ഉറക്കെ വിളിക്കുക)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs — PSC exam-ൽ ഒരു guaranteed question ആണ്. 'Call off' ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന phrasal verb ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The match was called off due to rain. (മഴ കാരണം match cancel ചെയ്തു)
  2. The strike was called off after negotiations. (negotiations-ന് ശേഷം strike cancel ചെയ്തു)
  3. They called off the wedding at the last minute. (അവർ അവസാന നിമിഷം wedding cancel ചെയ്തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) call on — 'Call on' = ആരെയെങ്കിലും formally visit ചെയ്യുക, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ ആരോടെങ്കിലും ചെയ്യാൻ ആവശ്യപ്പെടുക. 'The teacher called on Ramu to answer.' 'Call on' = cancel ആകില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Call' base verb same ആണ്, preposition 'on' vs 'off' matter ചെയ്യുന്നു — ഇത് students confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) go off — 'Go off' = explode / ring / become bad. 'The bomb went off' (bomb പൊട്ടി), 'The alarm went off' (alarm അടിച്ചു), 'The milk went off' (പാൽ കേടായി). Cancel ആകില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Go off' trip-ന്റെ context-ൽ confusing ആകും.

❌ (D) go on — 'Go on' = continue (തുടരുക). 'Go on with your work' = ജോലി തുടർന്നു ചെയ്യൂ. Cancel-ന്റെ exact opposite meaning ആണ് 'go on' — cancel ≠ continue!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OFF = away, removed!' — 'Call OFF' = call away (remove) the plan. Trip OFF ആകുമ്പോൾ trip cancel ആകുന്നു! 'OFF' = away / cancelled.
• Word family: call off | put off | postpone | cancel — ഇവ ഒരേ context-ൽ use ആകുന്ന words. 'Call off' = immediate cancel, 'put off' = postpone (later date-ലേക്ക് മാറ്റുക).
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb question-ൽ base verb ഒരേ ആണ് എന്നതിനാൽ confuse ആകരുത്. Preposition/particle matter ചെയ്യുന്നു: 'call OFF' = cancel, 'call ON' = visit. 'Cancel' keyword കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ 'call off' select ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: Choose the correctly spelt word from the following:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) argument

• 'Argument' — ഇതാണ് correct spelling. 'Argue' (verb) + 'ment' (suffix) = 'argument'. പക്ഷേ 'argue'-ൽ നിന്ന് 'argument' ഉണ്ടാകുമ്പോൾ final 'e' drop ചെയ്യണം.
• 'Argue' → 'argument' (e drop ചെയ്യുന്നു). ഇത് English spelling-ൽ ഒരു common rule ആണ്: '-ment' suffix-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന consonant-ന് മുൻപ് silent 'e' drop ആകും.
• Malayalam-ൽ: 'argument' = 'വാദം, തർക്കം, ന്യായവാദം'.
• Spelling mistakes-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common ആയ ഒന്നാണ് 'arguement' — extra 'e' ചേർക്കുന്നത്. ഇത് ഒരിക്കലും correct അല്ല!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Verb ending in '-e' + suffix starting with consonant → DROP the 'e'.
• Silent 'e' dropping rule examples:
  - argue → argument (e drop)
  - judge → judgment (e drop)
  - acknowledge → acknowledgment (e drop)
  - manage → management (e stays? No — manageMENT: 'e' keeps here actually)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions — PSC-ൽ ഒരു regular feature ആണ്. 'Argument', 'occurrence', 'accommodation' ഇവ frequently test ചെയ്യുന്ന words ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They had a heated argument about politics. (അവർ politics-നെക്കുറിച്ച് ശക്തമായ തർക്കം നടത്തി)
  2. Her argument was very convincing. (അവളുടെ ന്യായവാദം വളരെ convincing ആയിരുന്നു)
  3. They settled the argument peacefully. (അവർ തർക്കം സമാധാനപരമായി അവസാനിപ്പിച്ചു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) arguement — 'argue' + 'e' + 'ment' = extra 'e' ചേർത്തത്. 'Argue'-ൽ നിന്ന് 'argument' ഉണ്ടാകുമ്പോൾ 'e' drop ചെയ്യണം. 'Arguement' incorrect. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Argue' + 'ment' ആണ് combination — naturally 'e' നിൽക്കും എന്ന് students think ചെയ്യും. But rule: e drops!

❌ (B) argeument — 'e' wrong position-ൽ insert ചെയ്തിരിക്കുന്നു — 'argeument'. ഇത് completely wrong spelling ആണ്. 'g' ഉം 'u' ഉം തമ്മിൽ extra 'e' ഉണ്ട്. ഒരു random error spelling ആണ്, completely incorrect.

❌ (D) argumant — 'argument'-ൽ 'e' → 'a' ആക്കിയിരിക്കുന്നു — 'argumant'. 'Argument' spelling-ൽ '-ment' ആണ്, '-mant' അല്ല. 'e' → 'a' substitution — incorrect.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ARGUMENT — no extra E!' — 'Argue' ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'E' (ego) drop ചെയ്യണം — argument-ൽ ego (e) ഇല്ല! ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ 'arguement' mistake ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ല.
• Word family: argue (verb) | argument (noun) | argumentative (adjective) | arguably (adverb). ഈ family ഒരുമിച്ച് practice ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ options carefully compare ചെയ്യുക — often ഒരു letter മാത്രമാണ് difference. 'arguement' vs 'argument' — extra 'e' ഉണ്ടോ ഇല്ലയോ? ഇല്ലാത്തത് correct. Eliminate ചെയ്ത് ഒരു option ബാക്കി ആകുമ്പോൾ ഉറപ്പ് verify ചെയ്യുക.

questionText:
Change the following sentence into reported speech:
"How do you feel this morning?", he asked me.
optionA: He asked me how I felt this morning
optionB: He asked me how I feel that morning
optionC: He asked me how I felt that morning
optionD: He asked me how I had felt that morning

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) He asked me how I felt that morning

• Direct speech-ൽ: 'How do you feel this morning?' — ഇത് ഒരു Wh-question ആണ്.
• Wh-question-ൽ reporting verb 'asked', conjunction 'how' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Tense backshift: 'do feel' (Simple Present) → 'felt' (Simple Past). ഇതാണ് reported speech-ലെ tense change rule.
• Time word change: 'this morning' → 'that morning'. Direct speech-ലെ 'this' reported speech-ൽ 'that' ആകുന്നു — speaker ആ moment-ൽ present ഇല്ലാതിരിക്കുന്നതിനാൽ.
• Final reported speech: 'He asked me how I felt that morning.' — ഇതാണ് correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Direct Speech → Reported Speech — Tense backshift + Time word change.
• Tense backshift table:
  - Simple Present → Simple Past
  - Simple Past → Past Perfect
  - Present Perfect → Past Perfect
  - Will → Would | Can → Could | May → Might
• Time word changes:
  - this → that
  - these → those
  - now → then
  - today → that day
  - tomorrow → the next day / the following day
  - yesterday → the previous day
  - this morning → that morning
• Wh-question reporting: ask + how/what/where/when/why/who + subject + verb (statement order).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported speech — PSC-ൽ frequently വരുന്ന ഒരു topic ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Where do you live?' he asked. → He asked where I lived.
  2. 'What are you doing?' she asked. → She asked what I was doing.
  3. 'How did you come here?' they asked. → They asked how I had come there.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He asked me how I felt this morning — Tense backshift correct ആണ് ('felt'), പക്ഷേ time word change ചെയ്തില്ല. 'This morning' → 'that morning' ആകണം. 'This' reported speech-ൽ 'that' ആകും. 🪤 PSC Trap: Tense change ഓർക്കും, time word change മറക്കും — ഇത് students commonly miss ചെയ്യുന്ന error ആണ്.

❌ (B) He asked me how I feel that morning — Tense backshift ചെയ്തില്ല. 'Do feel' → 'felt' ആകണം, 'feel' (present) ആകരുത്. Time word 'that morning' correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ tense wrong. 🪤 PSC Trap: Time word change ഓർക്കും, tense change മറക്കും.
