❌ (D) He asked me how I had felt that morning — 'had felt' = Past Perfect. Direct speech-ൽ Simple Present 'do feel' → Reported speech-ൽ Simple Past 'felt' ആണ് വേണ്ടത്. Past Perfect 'had felt' വരുന്നത് direct speech-ൽ Simple Past ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ ആണ്. ഇവിടെ overshift ആണ് — ഒരു step extra ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'STEP BACK one tense, STEP BACK time words!' — Reported speech-ൽ tense ഒരു step back (present → past), time words ഒരു step back (this → that, now → then, today → that day). ഇങ്ങനെ ഓർക്കൂ!
• Two changes to remember: (1) Tense backshift: Simple Present → Simple Past. (2) Time words: this/these → that/those | now → then | today → that day | this morning → that morning.
• PSC exam strategy: Reported speech question-ൽ ഒരേ സമയം tense AND time word ഒരുമിച്ച് check ചെയ്യുക. (A) tense correct, time wrong. (B) time correct, tense wrong. (C) ഇരണ്ടും correct — ഇതാണ് answer!

questionText:
Fill up with the correct tense form of the verb given in bracket:
When her mother arrived, Isha ______ (finish) the laundry.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) had finished

• 'When her mother arrived, Isha had finished the laundry' — ഇതാണ് correct sentence.
• 'When her mother arrived' = Past tense event (അമ്മ arrived ചെയ്ത time).
• 'Isha had finished the laundry' = Isha-ന്റെ action, അമ്മ arrive ചെയ്യുന്നതിനും BEFORE THAT complete ആയിരുന്നു.
• Past Perfect tense (had + V3) ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്: ഭൂതകാലത്ത് നടന്ന രണ്ട് events-ൽ ഒന്ന് മറ്റൊന്നിനേക്കാൾ EARLIER ആയി complete ആയിരുന്നു എന്ന് show ചെയ്യാൻ.
• 'Arrived' (earlier event visible) → 'had finished' (even earlier event). Laundry finished → THEN mother arrived.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Past Perfect = Had + V3 = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് മറ്റൊരു past action-ന് BEFORE complete ആയ action.
• When to use Past Perfect:
  - Two past actions → earlier one = Past Perfect, later one = Simple Past.
  - Signal words: when, before, after, by the time, already, just.
• Pattern: By the time / When + Simple Past → Past Perfect (earlier action).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Past Perfect tense — PSC exam-ൽ 'when' clause ഉള്ള questions-ൽ ഇത് ഒരു regular topic ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. When I reached the station, the train had left. (ഞാൻ station-ൽ എത്തിയപ്പോഴേക്കും train പോയിരുന്നു)
  2. She had eaten before he arrived. (അദ്ദേഹം വരുന്നതിന് മുൻപ് അവൾ ഭക്ഷണം കഴിച്ചിരുന്നു)
  3. By the time the movie started, they had found their seats. (movie തുടങ്ങുന്ന സമയത്തേക്കും അവർ seats കണ്ടെത്തിയിരുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) finished — 'Finished' Simple Past ആണ്. ഇവിടെ 'arrived' ഉം 'finished' ഉം രണ്ടും Simple Past ആകും, ഏത് action earlier ആണ് എന്ന് clear ആകില്ല. 'When her mother arrived, Isha finished the laundry' — ഇത് read ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ mother arrived ആയ SAME TIME-ൽ Isha finish ചെയ്തു എന്ന് തോന്നും. Past Perfect ഉള്ളതാണ് sequence clearly show ചെയ്യുന്നത്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'arrived' past ആണ്, so 'finished' past — ഇങ്ങനെ students think ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) has finished — 'Has finished' Present Perfect ആണ്. ഇതു past-ൽ ആരംഭിച്ച് present-ൽ relevant ആകുന്ന action-ന് ആണ്. 'When her mother arrived' = past time reference ഉള്ളതിനാൽ Present Perfect use ചെയ്യരുത്. Past time reference + Present Perfect = grammatically wrong combination.

❌ (C) will finish — 'Will finish' Simple Future ആണ്. 'Arrived' past event ആണ്. Past event-ന്റെ context-ൽ future tense ഒരിക്കലും use ചെയ്യില്ല. Sentence meaning complete opposite ആകും: 'When her mother arrived, Isha will finish' — nonsense!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'WHEN = Which came first? → HAD!' — 'When' clause-ൽ two past actions ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ, earlier action-ൽ 'HAD + V3' use ചെയ്യുക. 'When mother arrived (later), Isha HAD finished (earlier).' Earlier action → HAD.
• Word family to remember: Past Perfect signals: when, before, after, by the time, already, just, no sooner...than — ഇവ any of these words കണ്ടാൽ Past Perfect consider ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: 'When' clause ഉള്ള tense questions-ൽ main clause-ൽ earlier completed action → 'had + V3' (Past Perfect). Simple Past 'finished' vs Past Perfect 'had finished' — ഇവ sequence of events show ചെയ്യുക. Laundry already done BEFORE mother arrived = 'had finished'. Guaranteed!

questionText:
Complete the following sentence suitably:
Unless you study well, _______.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) you will not pass

• 'Unless' = 'if not' (ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ). 'Unless you study well' = 'If you do not study well' — ഇതിന്റെ meaning 'നന്നായി പഠിക്കുന്നില്ലെങ്കിൽ' എന്നാണ്.
• 'Unless' already negative meaning ഉള്ള word ആണ്. അതുകൊണ്ട് main clause-ൽ POSITIVE statement ആകണം? — ഇല്ല! 'Unless' = if not. 'If you do not study, you will not pass.' Unless-ൽ already negative ഉള്ളതിനാൽ logic ശരിക്ക് follow ചെയ്യണം.
• 'Unless you study well, you will not pass' = 'നന്നായി പഠിക്കുന്നില്ലെങ്കിൽ, നിങ്ങൾ pass ആകില്ല' — ഇത് logically correct statement ആണ്.
• ഇത് First Conditional (Real Conditional) ആണ്: Unless/If + Simple Present → will + V1 (future result).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Unless = If not. Unless + Simple Present → will + V1 (First Conditional pattern).
• 'Unless' usage:
  - Unless you hurry, you will miss the bus. (= If you don't hurry, you will miss the bus.)
  - Unless it rains, the match will go on. (= If it doesn't rain, the match will go on.)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Unless' conditional sentences PSC-ൽ ഒരു repeated favourite topic ആണ്.
• First Conditional structure review:
  - If + Simple Present, will + V1
  - Unless + Simple Present, will + V1
  - Both express real/possible future conditions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Unless you apologise, she will not forgive you. (നിങ്ങൾ ക്ഷമ ചോദിക്കുന്നില്ലെങ്കിൽ, അവൾ ക്ഷമിക്കില്ല)
  2. Unless we leave now, we will be late. (നമ്മൾ ഇപ്പോൾ പോകുന്നില്ലെങ്കിൽ, late ആകും)
  3. Unless you eat well, you will feel weak. (നന്നായി കഴിക്കുന്നില്ലെങ്കിൽ, ക്ഷീണം തോന്നും)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) you will pass — 'Unless you study well, you will pass' — ഇതിന്റെ meaning: 'നന്നായി പഠിക്കുന്നില്ലെങ്കിൽ, pass ആകും' — ഇത് logically wrong! പഠിക്കാതെ pass ആകും എന്ന് പറഞ്ഞാൽ ആർക്കും ബോധ്യം ആകില്ല. Unless = if not — so correct result should be negative. 🪤 PSC Trap: ഏറ്റവും easy-ആ തോന്നുന്ന option ആണ് ഇത്, students trap ആകും.

❌ (C) you could pass — 'Could' = past ability / possibility (ഒരുപക്ഷേ pass ആകാം). 'Unless' conditional-ൽ future real situation ആണ്. First Conditional-ൽ 'will' ആണ് standard. 'Could' uncertain possibility കാണിക്കുന്നു — ഇവിടെ certain consequence ആണ് expected. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Could' softer ആണ്, polite ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും — but grammar-ൽ wrong.

❌ (D) you could have passed — 'Could have passed' = Past possibility that didn't happen (Third Conditional idea). 'Unless you study well' = future/present condition ആണ്. Past unreal situation ആകില്ല. 'Could have passed' = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് pass ആകാമായിരുന്നു (but didn't) — ഇവിടെ ആ meaning relevant അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'UNLESS = U N LESS = You (do) NOT (the) LESS!' — 'Unless' = 'If not'. 'Unless you study' = 'If you don't study'. Result negative ആകണം — 'will NOT pass'!
• Word family: unless | if not | provided that | as long as | on condition that — ഇവ ഒരേ conditional meaning ഉള്ള words. ഒരുമിച്ച് learn ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Unless' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ result logically think ചെയ്യുക. 'Unless you study' = 'If you don't study' → result = 'you will NOT pass'. Logic ശരിക്ക് follow ചെയ്ത് answer select ചെയ്യുക. Logic + grammar = guaranteed mark!

questionText: Teacher could not help ______________ at the criticism.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) laughing

• "cannot help + V-ing" = English-ലെ ഒരു fixed expression ആണ് — "അടക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല" എന്നർഥം.
• "Teacher could not help laughing at the criticism" = വിമർശനം കേട്ട് teacher-ന് ചിരി അടക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല.
• "cannot/could not help" ന് ശേഷം എപ്പോഴും gerund (-ing form) വരും — infinitive (to laugh) അല്ല.
• ഇത് PSC-ൽ classic gerund question ആണ് — "cannot help" കണ്ടാൽ -ing form ഉടൻ choose ചെയ്യൂ!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: cannot/could not help + V-ing (gerund) — ഒരിക്കലും infinitive (to+V) അല്ല!
• "Cannot help" = "avoid ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല / അടക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല" — gerund-only expression.
• Gerund-only expressions (fixed phrases):
  - cannot help + V-ing: "I can't help smiling." (ചിരി അടക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല)
  - it's no use + V-ing: "It's no use crying." (കരഞ്ഞിട്ട് കാര്യമില്ല)
  - it's worth + V-ing: "It's worth trying." (try ചെയ്യേണ്ടതുണ്ട്)
  - have difficulty + V-ing: "I have difficulty sleeping." (ഉറക്കം വരാൻ ബുദ്ധിമുട്ടുണ്ട്)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "cannot help" + gerund PSC-ൽ repeatedly ചോദിക്കും — guaranteed mark!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She could not help laughing at the joke. (ചിരി അടക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല)
  2. I cannot help feeling sorry for him. (സഹതാപം തോന്നാതിരിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല)
  3. He could not help admiring her talent. (കഴിവ് കണ്ട് അത്ഭുതപ്പെടാതിരിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) laughs
• 'laughs' = Simple Present tense verb (third person singular) — ഇത് blank-ൽ fit ആകില്ല.
• "could not help laughs" = grammatically completely wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'laugh' verb ആണ് — 's' add ചെയ്‌താൽ correct ആകും എന്ന് feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (B) to laugh
• "cannot help to laugh" = wrong — 'help' verb-ന് ശേഷം infinitive വരുമ്പോൾ different meaning: "help someone to do something" (സഹായിക്കുക).
• "cannot help + to laugh" = not standard expression for "unable to stop."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Infinitive (to + verb) everywhere fit ആകും എന്ന് students assume ചെയ്യും — ഇത് classic trap!

❌ (D) laugh
• 'laugh' = bare infinitive — "cannot help laugh" = not correct English.
• Bare infinitive modal verbs-ന് (can, will, shall) ശേഷം വരും — "cannot help" ഒരു modal അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'laugh' simple ആണ്, correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel — പക്ഷേ -ing form വേണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Cannot HELP = cannot STOP = need -ING! Stop running, Stop laughing — STOP + ING pattern ഓർക്കൂ. Cannot help = cannot stop = -ing!" ഒരിക്കൽ ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ മറക്കില്ല!
• Gerund-only fixed expressions:
  - cannot help + ing | it's no use + ing | it's worth + ing | mind + ing | avoid + ing.
• PSC exam strategy: "cannot help" blank-ൽ കണ്ടാൽ options-ൽ -ing form ഉടൻ mark ചെയ്യൂ — "laughing" = C = done!

questionText: If I have the money, I ______________ a new dress for you.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) will buy

• "If I have the money" = If-clause-ൽ Simple Present tense → Type 1 Conditional (Real/Possible).
• Type 1 formula: If + Simple Present → will + V1.
• "If I have the money, I will buy a new dress for you" = പണം ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ (possible future) നിനക്ക് ഒരു dress വാങ്ങും.
• "have" = present tense → future possibility → "will buy" ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If-clause tense നോക്കൂ → main clause decide ചെയ്യൂ:
  - Type 1: If + Present → will + V1 (possible/real future)
  - Type 2: If + Past → would + V1 (unreal present/future)
  - Type 3: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3 (unreal past)
• "If I have" = present → Type 1 → "will buy."
• Type 1 = real possibility — പണം കിട്ടുമെന്ന് possible ആണ്, so will ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditionals PSC-ൽ very frequent — 3 types clearly ഓർക്കൂ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If it rains, we will cancel the match. (Type 1 - possible)
  2. If I were rich, I would travel the world. (Type 2 - unreal present)
  3. If she had studied, she would have passed. (Type 3 - unreal past)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) would buy
• "would buy" = Type 2 Conditional — If + Past → would.
• ഇവിടെ if-clause-ൽ "have" (present) ആണ്, past അല്ല → Type 2 wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "would buy" = more polite/formal feel → students prefer it — പക്ഷേ if-clause present ആണ്!

❌ (C) would have bought
• "would have bought" = Type 3 Conditional — if-clause-ൽ past perfect ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ.
• "If I have" = present → Type 3 ആകില്ല — completely wrong tense combination.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "would have bought" = sounds complex/correct — complexity = right ആണ് എന്ന് feel.

❌ (D) will have bought
• "will have bought" = Future Perfect — specific future time ആകുമ്പോൾ complete ആകുന്ന action-ന്.
• Conditional sentence structure-ൽ Future Perfect ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "will" = future + "have bought" = complete feel → both sounds logical but wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "If-clause = ROAD SIGN, Main clause = DESTINATION. Present road → will destination. Past road → would destination. Past perfect road → would have destination!"
• 3-type table:
  - Present → will | Past → would | Past Perfect → would have.
• PSC exam strategy: "If I have" = present tense in if-clause = Type 1 → ഉടൻ "will buy" choose ചെയ്യൂ. If-clause tense = answer key!

questionText: My mother ______________ in this house for ten years.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) has been living

• "for ten years" + present context (still living now) → Present Perfect Continuous tense.
• "My mother has been living in this house for ten years" = അമ്മ പത്ത് വർഷമായി ഈ വീട്ടിൽ താമസിക്കുന്നു — ഇപ്പോഴും തുടരുന്നു.
• Present Perfect Continuous = past-ൽ start ചെയ്‌ത് ഇപ്പോഴും ongoing ആയ action + "for/since" signal words.
• "for ten years" = duration → Present Perfect Continuous (has been + V-ing) ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: for/since + still ongoing action = Present Perfect Continuous (has/have been + V-ing).
• Signal words:
  - "for + duration" (for ten years, for two hours) → Present Perfect Continuous.
  - "since + starting point" (since 2020, since morning) → Present Perfect Continuous.
• Tense comparison:
  - has been living = still living NOW (ongoing) ✓
  - had been living = was living before, but stopped (past) ✗
  - was living = simple past continuous, no "for" connection ✗
  - had lived = past perfect, completed action ✗
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "for/since" signal words = PSC-ൽ very common — Present Perfect / Present Perfect Continuous ഓർക്കൂ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has been working here for five years. (still working)
  2. It has been raining since morning. (still raining)
  3. He has been studying for three hours. (still studying)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) was living
• "was living" = Past Continuous — past-ൽ ongoing action, ഇപ്പോൾ continue ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• "for ten years" present context ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് past tense wrong — ഇപ്പോഴും living ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "living" = continuous feel → "was living" ശരിയാണ് എന്ന് feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (B) had been living
• "had been living" = Past Perfect Continuous — past-ൽ start ചെയ്‌ത്, another past point-ൽ stop ചെയ്‌ത action.
• Mother ഇപ്പോഴും living ആണ്, stop ചെയ്‌തിട്ടില്ല → had been living wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "had been" = sounds more complete/accurate → choose ചെയ്യും, പക്ഷേ mother still lives there!

❌ (D) had lived
• "had lived" = Past Perfect — past-ൽ complete ആയ action.
• ഇവിടെ action ഇപ്പോഴും continuing ആണ് → past perfect wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "for ten years" = long time = past feeling → had lived choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "FOR + STILL DOING = HAS BEEN doing! For ten years + still living = HAS BEEN LIVING. FOR = duration clue → Present Perfect Continuous!"
• Signal word shortcut:
  - for/since + still ongoing → has/have been + V-ing
  - for/since + stopped → had been + V-ing (past)
  - yesterday/last year → Simple Past
• PSC exam strategy: "for ten years" കണ്ടാൽ ആദ്യം "is she still living?" check ചെയ്യൂ. Yes → "has been living." ഒരു question, ഒരു answer!

questionText: The poem tempts the readers to ponder ______________ the beauty of nature.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) over

• "ponder over" = fixed verb-preposition collocation — "ആഴത്തിൽ ചിന്തിക്കുക, ഗൗരവമായി ആലോചിക്കുക" എന്നർഥം.
• "The poem tempts the readers to ponder over the beauty of nature" = കവിത വായനക്കാരെ പ്രകൃതി സൗന്ദര്യത്തെക്കുറിച്ച് ആഴത്തിൽ ചിന്തിക്കാൻ പ്രേരിപ്പിക്കുന്നു.
• "ponder over" = "reflect on, think deeply about" — standard English collocation.
• PSC-ൽ verb + preposition fixed pairs ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — "ponder over" ഓർക്കൂ!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Verb + Preposition = fixed collocations — literal meaning ആകില്ല, memorize ചെയ്യണം.
• "ponder" collocations:
  - ponder over = think deeply about (ആഴത്തിൽ ആലോചിക്കുക) ✓
  - ponder on = also acceptable but less common
• PSC-ൽ important verb + preposition pairs:
  - ponder over | brood over | mull over (all = think deeply)
  - insist on | depend on | rely on
  - congratulate on | comment on
  - interfere in | participate in
  - search for | look for | wait for
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Prepositions PSC-ൽ regular — fixed collocations list ഓർക്കൂ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She pondered over the difficult decision for hours.
  2. He brooded over his failure for weeks.
  3. The students mulled over the question carefully.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) in
• "ponder in" = not a standard collocation — "in" = inside a space, not suitable here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "in nature" = common phrase → "ponder in the beauty" feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (B) after
• "ponder after" = not standard English collocation — "after" = time/sequence meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "look after" common phrase → "after" works with many verbs feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (C) into
• "ponder into" = not standard — "into" = movement/deeper entry.
• "look into" = investigate ആണ്, "ponder into" = not used.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "delve into, look into" = ആഴമുള്ള ചിന്ത → "ponder into" ഉം ആഴമുള്ളതാണ് feel ഉണ്ടാകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "PONDER OVER = think OVER and OVER — ആഴത്തിൽ repeatedly ചിന്തിക്കുന്നു! Over = again and again = deep thought!" OVER = deep repeated thinking.
• "Over" collocations for PSC: ponder over | brood over | mull over | preside over | gloss over.
• PSC exam strategy: "ponder" കണ്ടാൽ options-ൽ "over" ഉണ്ടോ നോക്കൂ — ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ guaranteed answer!

questionText: Give a one word substitute for 'a person who loves books':

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Bibliophile

• "a person who loves books" = Bibliophile — "പുസ്‌തകപ്രേമി" എന്നർഥം.
• Greek roots: "biblion" (book) + "philos" (loving) = bibliophile.
• Bibliophile = books-നോട് deep love ഉള്ള ആൾ — collect ചെയ്യുകയും read ചെയ്യുകയും ചെയ്യുന്ന book lover.
• PSC-ൽ "-phile" suffix ഉള്ള words ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — root ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guess ചെയ്യാം!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "-phile" suffix = lover of something (Greek "philos" = loving).
• "-phile" words:
  - bibliophile = lover of books (biblion = book)
  - cinephile = lover of cinema
  - audiophile = lover of sound/music
  - francophile = lover of France/French culture
  - technophile = lover of technology
• Other important roots for One Word Substitutes:
  - omni = all: omniscient (knows everything), omnipotent (all powerful), omnivore (eats everything)
  - poly = many: polyglot (speaks many languages), polygamy (many marriages)
  - biblio = book: bibliography, bibliophile, bibliophobia
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One Word Substitutes PSC-ൽ regular — person-related substitutes most common.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The bibliophile spent hours at the bookstore. (book lover)
  2. He is a polyglot who speaks five languages. (many languages)
  3. The omniscient narrator knows everything. (all knowing)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Omniscient
• 'omniscient' = "സർവ്വജ്ഞൻ — എല്ലാം അറിയുന്ന ആൾ" (omni = all + scire = know).
• "loves books" ≠ "knows everything" — ബന്ധം ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Omniscient = smart/knowledgeable feel → book lover = knowledgeable → omniscient ആണ് answer എന്ന് logic ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (C) Polyglot
• 'polyglot' = "പല ഭാഷകൾ സംസാരിക്കുന്ന ആൾ" (poly = many + glotta = tongue/language).
• "loves books" ≠ "speaks many languages."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Polyglot sounds educated/scholarly → book lover = scholarly → polyglot feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (D) Omnipotent
• 'omnipotent' = "സർവ്വശക്‌തൻ — unlimited power ഉള്ള ആൾ" (omni = all + potens = powerful).
• "loves books" ≠ "all powerful" — completely different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "omni" words sound impressive → any "omni" option = correct feel ഉണ്ടാകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "BIBLIO = BIBLE = BOOK! BIBLIOphile = BOOK lover. Bible is a book, biblio = book — ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കില്ല!"
• "-phile" family: bibliophile (books) | cinephile (cinema) | audiophile (audio) | technophile (tech).
• PSC exam strategy: "person who loves ___" = "___ + phile" pattern. Books = biblio + phile = bibliophile. Root words ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ One Word Substitutes = easy marks!

questionText: The correctly spelt word is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) vacuum

• "vacuum" = ശൂന്യാകാശം, വായു ഇല്ലാത്ത space — ഇതാണ് correct spelling.
• Latin "vacuus" = empty/void — ശൂന്യം എന്നർഥം.
• Common confusion: 'c' ഒന്ന് മാത്രം, 'u' ഒന്ന് — "vacuum" = v-a-c-u-u-m (double 'u', single 'c').
• PSC spelling questions-ൽ "vacuum" ഒരു classic tricky word ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: vacuum = 1 'c' + 2 'u' — "vac-u-um" = single c, double u.
• Why tricky: 'c' double ആണോ single ആണോ എന്ന confusion — single 'c' ആണ് correct.
• Latin root: "vacuus" (empty) → vacuum → vacancy → vacate → evacuate.
• Related word family:
  - vacuum (n) = ശൂന്യത, vacuum cleaner
  - vacuous (adj) = empty, meaningless
  - evacuate (v) = ഒഴിപ്പിക്കുക (e + vacuate)
  - vacancy (n) = ഒഴിവ്
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling Test topic-ൽ Latin-origin words with double letters frequent ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A vacuum cleaner removes dust effectively. (ശൂന്യാകാശ ക്ലീനർ)
  2. There is a complete vacuum of leadership. (നേതൃത്വ ശൂന്യത)
  3. The tube was sealed in a vacuum. (ശൂന്യാകാശത്തിൽ)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) vacum
• 'vacum' = 'u' ഒന്ന് മാത്രം — double 'u' missing. "vac-um" = incomplete spelling.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sound-ൽ "vac-yum" ആണ്, ഒരു 'u' മാത്രം കേൾക്കും — double 'u' ഓർക്കണം.

❌ (B) vaccum
• 'vaccum' = double 'c' + single 'u' — 'c' double ആണ് mistake, 'u' single ആണ് mistake.
• രണ്ടും wrong — double 'c' ഇല്ല, double 'u' ഉണ്ട്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "occasion, occasion" ഒക്കെ double 'c' ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് vacuum-ഉം double 'c' ആണ് feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (D) vaccuum
• 'vaccuum' = double 'c' + triple 'u' — doubly wrong!
• "vacc-uum" = 'c' double, 'u' triple — ഇത് exist ചെയ്യുന്ന word-ഇ അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: More letters = more correct feel — spelling questions-ൽ overthink ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "VACUUM = VAC + UU + M — 'UU' = two U's like a vacuum cleaner's two wheels! ഒരു vacuum cleaner-ന്റെ 2 wheels = 2 U's!" vac-UU-m — double U ഓർക്കൂ!
• Tricky double-letter words for PSC: vacuum (uu) | millennium (ll+nn) | occurrence (cc+rr) | necessary (one c, double s) | committee (mm+tt+ee).
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ options compare ചെയ്‌ത് different letters identify ചെയ്യൂ — ഇവിടെ 'c' count ഉം 'u' count ഉം different ആണ്. vacuum = 1c + 2u = (C) ✓

questionText: What does the foreign phrase 'vis a vis' mean?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) in relation to

• "vis-à-vis" = French phrase = "in relation to, compared with, face to face with" എന്നർഥം.
• French-ൽ literally "face to face" എന്നാണ് — "vis" = face (Latin "visus" = sight/face).
• English-ൽ "in relation to / compared with / regarding" sense-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Example: "Discuss the policy vis-à-vis its impact on students" = students-മായുള്ള ബന്ധത്തിൽ policy discuss ചെയ്യൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: vis-à-vis = "in relation to / face to face / compared with" — French origin.
• Foreign phrases PSC list:
  - vis-à-vis = in relation to / face to face (French)
  - vox populi = voice of the people (Latin)
  - status quo = existing state of affairs (Latin)
  - bona fide = genuine, in good faith (Latin)
  - alma mater = one's old school/college (Latin)
  - per capita = per head/person (Latin)
  - carte blanche = complete freedom/authority (French)
  - faux pas = social blunder/mistake (French)
  - déjà vu = feeling of having seen before (French)
  - c'est la vie = that's life (French)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Foreign phrases PSC-ൽ regular — French + Latin phrases most common.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The new law was discussed vis-à-vis its economic impact.
  2. His performance vis-à-vis last year has improved greatly.
  3. They sat vis-à-vis each other at the table. (face to face)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) in addition to
• "in addition to" = "കൂടാതെ, അതിനു പുറമേ" — vis-à-vis-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "vis-à-vis" sounds like "besides/additionally" feel — formal word = addition feel.

❌ (C) in contact with
• "in contact with" = "ബന്ധത്തിൽ ഇരിക്കുക" — physical/communication contact.
• vis-à-vis = face to face → contact ആണ് feeling, പക്ഷേ "in relation to" ആണ് exact meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "face to face" = contact feel → "in contact with" choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) in charge of
• "in charge of" = "ചുമതലപ്പെട്ട, ഉത്തരവാദിത്തമുള്ള" — completely different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "vis" sounds like "vision/supervisor" → in charge feel ഉണ്ടാകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "VIS-À-VIS = VISAGE to VISAGE = FACE to FACE! Visage = face (French). Face to face = in relation to each other!" VIS = face = see = vision — same root!
• French phrases cluster: vis-à-vis (face to face/relation) | faux pas (mistake) | carte blanche (free hand) | déjà vu (seen before) | c'est la vie (that's life).
• PSC exam strategy: "vis-à-vis" കണ്ടാൽ "in relation to" ഉടൻ choose ചെയ്യൂ — options-ൽ "relation" keyword ഉള്ളത് = answer!

questionText: Sheep : Flock, Fish : ?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) School

• Fish-ന്റെ collective noun = "a school of fish" — ഇതൊരു fixed expression ആണ്.
• "School" ഇവിടെ "school of learning" അല്ല — fish-ന്റെ group-നെ "school" എന്ന് വിളിക്കുന്നു.
• Analogy pattern: Sheep : Flock :: Fish : School — same structure, collective nouns.
• PSC-ൽ analogy + collective noun combination question — both ഒരുമിച്ച് test ചെയ്യുന്നു!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ഓരോ animal/group-നും specific collective noun ഉണ്ട് — memorize list!
• Important collective nouns for PSC:
  - Fish → school / shoal
  - Sheep → flock
  - Lions → pride
  - Wolves → pack
  - Birds → flock
  - Elephants → herd
  - Cattle → herd
  - Crows → murder
  - Bees → swarm
  - Ants → colony
  - Monkeys → troop
  - Soldiers → troop
  - Sailors → crew
  - Whales → pod
  - Dolphins → pod
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns PSC-ൽ regular — analogy format-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A school of fish swam past the boat.
  2. A flock of sheep grazed on the hill.
  3. A swarm of bees attacked the tree.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Cluster
• 'cluster' = grapes, stars, computers-ന്റെ group — fish-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "cluster" = group feel → any group = cluster ആകും എന്ന് students assume ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Colony
• 'colony' = ants, bats, penguins-ന്റെ group — fish-ന് "colony" standard അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Colony = organized group feel → fish ഒരുമിച്ച് swim ചെയ്യുന്നു = organized = colony feel.

❌ (C) Pod
• 'pod' = whales, dolphins, seals-ന്റെ group — fish-ന് "pod" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Pod = water animals group → fish ഉം water animal → pod choose ചെയ്യും. പക്ഷേ pod = marine mammals, not fish!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Fish go to SCHOOL! Fish-ന്റെ group swim together = like students in a school going together — a SCHOOL of fish!" Fish + School = school-ൽ കൂടി പോകുന്നത് ഓർക്കൂ!
• Water animal collective nouns:
  - Fish → school/shoal | Whales → pod | Dolphins → pod | Sharks → shiver.
• PSC exam strategy: Analogy question-ൽ first pair-ന്റെ relationship identify ചെയ്യൂ: Sheep:Flock = animal:collective noun. Fish-ന്റെ collective noun = school. Options-ൽ "School" = D = answer!

questionText:
Change into reported speech:
His father asked, "Do you think you know the lesson better than your teacher?"
optionA: His father asked whether he thought he knew the lesson better than his teacher
optionB: His father asked whether he thinks he knew the lesson better than his teacher
optionC: His father asked whether he thought he know the lesson better than his teacher
optionD: His father asked whether he think he did know the lesson better than his teacher

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) His father asked whether he thought he knew the lesson better than his teacher.

• Direct speech = "Do you think you know the lesson better than your teacher?" — Yes/No question.
• Indirect speech rules applied:
  1. Reporting verb "asked" + "whether" (Yes/No question → whether/if).
  2. Pronoun: "you" → "he" (father is asking son).
  3. Tense backshift: "Do you think" → "he thought" | "you know" → "he knew".
  4. "your teacher" → "his teacher".
• All 4 changes correctly applied in option A ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Yes/No question → whether/if + statement order (no inversion) + tense backshift.
• Tense backshift rules:
  - Simple Present → Simple Past (do/does → did | think → thought | know → knew)
  - Simple Past → Past Perfect (went → had gone)
  - will → would | can → could | may → might
• Pronoun changes:
  - you (person spoken to) → he/she/they
  - I (speaker) → he/she
  - your → his/her
• Question → Indirect: "Do you...?" → "whether he..." (remove question mark, normal sentence order)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Direct/Indirect Speech PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — Yes/No questions ഏറ്റവും tricky.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He asked, "Are you ready?" → He asked whether I was ready.
  2. She said, "I know the answer." → She said that she knew the answer.
  3. They asked, "Will you come?" → They asked whether I would come.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) ...whether he thinks he knew...
• "thinks" = present tense — backshift ചെയ്‌തില്ല. "think" → "thought" ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "thinks" = logical present feel (still true now) → students tense change ചെയ്യില്ല.

❌ (C) ...whether he thought he know...
• "know" = present tense — "know" → "knew" ആകണം (backshift).
• ഒന്ന് backshift ചെയ്‌തു (thought ✓), മറ്റൊന്ന് ചെയ്‌തില്ല (know ✗).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Half backshift — "thought" change ചെയ്‌തതുകൊണ്ട് correct feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (D) ...whether he think he did know...
• "think" = present (not backshifted) | "did know" = wrong form — multiple errors.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "did know" = emphasis feel → more correct sound ഉണ്ടാകും, പക്ഷേ indirect speech-ൽ "knew" ആണ് standard.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Indirect speech = TIME MACHINE — everything shifts ONE step to the PAST! Present → Past, Past → Past Perfect. ടൈം മെഷീനിൽ ഇട്ടാൽ ഒരു step back ആകും!"
• 3-step checklist for indirect speech:
  1. Reporting verb + whether/if (Yes/No) or that (statement).
  2. Pronoun change (you→he/she, I→he/she, your→his/her).
  3. Tense backshift (think→thought, know→knew, do→did).
• PSC exam strategy: All 3 steps correctly ചെയ്‌ത option = answer. Option A-ൽ thought ✓ + knew ✓ + his teacher ✓ = all correct = A!

questionText: Expand 'RDX':

explanation.correct:
✅ ശரിയുത്തരം: (C) Royal Demolition Explosive

• RDX = Royal Demolition Explosive — ഒരു powerful military explosive ആണ്.
• RDX = Research Department Explosive / Royal Demolition Explosive — ഇവ രണ്ടും acceptable expansions, പക്ഷേ PSC answer key-ൽ (C) ആണ്.
• RDX = cyclotrimethylenetrinitramine — ഒരു high-power explosive compound.
• Option (A) "Research Department for Explosives" = wrong expansion — 'for' extra ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: RDX = Royal Demolition Explosive (PSC standard answer).
• Important military/science abbreviations for PSC:
  - RDX = Royal Demolition Explosive
  - TNT = Trinitrotoluene
  - DNA = Deoxyribonucleic Acid
  - RNA = Ribonucleic Acid
  - LASER = Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation
  - RADAR = Radio Detection And Ranging
  - SONAR = Sound Navigation And Ranging
• Important organization abbreviations:
  - UNESCO = United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization
  - UNICEF = United Nations International Children's Emergency Fund
  - NATO = North Atlantic Treaty Organization
  - INTERPOL = International Criminal Police Organization
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Abbreviations PSC-ൽ regular — military, science, international organizations list ഓർക്കൂ.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. RDX is used in military operations and demolitions.
  2. The bomb squad discovered RDX at the site.
  3. RDX is more powerful than TNT.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Research Department for Explosives
• "Research Department for Explosives" = common misconception — "for" extra ആണ്, correct expansion അല്ല.
• "Research Department Explosive" (without 'for') = alternate version, പക്ഷേ PSC answer = "Royal Demolition Explosive."
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Research" = scientific/lab feel → correct ആണ് feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (B) Royal Defense Explosives
• "Defense" = wrong — "Demolition" ആണ് correct. Defense ≠ Demolition.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Military explosive = defense feel → "Royal Defense" logical feel ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (D) None of the above
• Option C = correct expansion — "None of the above" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: RDX-ന്റെ exact expansion confusing ആണ്, students "None of the above" safe option ആയി choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "RDX = Royal Demolition Explosive — ROYAL DEMOLITION = king-level destruction! RDX is the king of explosives!" Royal = powerful king = powerful explosive = RDX!
• Abbreviation cluster — explosives/military:
  - RDX (Royal Demolition Explosive) | TNT (Trinitrotoluene) | IED (Improvised Explosive Device).
• PSC exam strategy: RDX question-ൽ "Royal Demolition Explosive" ഉള്ള option ആണ് answer — "Demolition" keyword ഓർക്കൂ, "Defense" അല്ല!

questionText:
Change the following into Indirect Speech:
The teacher said, "The Earth revolves around the Sun."
optionA: The teacher said that the Earth revolved around the Sun.
optionB: The teacher said that the Earth had revolved around the Sun.
optionC: The teacher said that the Earth revolves around the Sun.
optionD: The teacher said that the Earth is revolving around the Sun.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The teacher said that the Earth revolves around the Sun.

• "The Earth revolves around the Sun" — ഇത് ഒരു scientific fact / universal truth ആണ്. ഭൂമി സൂര്യനെ ചുറ്റുന്നു — ഇത് എല്ലാ കാലത്തും ശരിയാണ്.
• Indirect Speech-ൽ reporting verb past tense ആണെങ്കിലും (said), universal truth-ന്റെ tense മാറില്ല — present tense-ൽ തന്നെ നിൽക്കും.
• "revolves" → "revolves" (മാറ്റമില്ല) — ഇതാണ് ഈ question-ന്റെ core rule.
• PSC-ൽ ഈ exception (universal truth) ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — ഈ rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Reporting verb = past, but Universal Truth/Scientific Fact = NO tense change!
• സാധാരണ Indirect Speech-ൽ: present → past ആകും (says → said, revolves → revolved).
• Exception: Universal Truths, Scientific Facts, Proverbs — tense മാറില്ല.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Direct/Indirect Speech PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും, especially exceptions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He said, "The sun rises in the east." → He said that the sun rises in the east. ✓
  2. She said, "Water boils at 100°C." → She said that water boils at 100°C. ✓
  3. He said, "Honesty is the best policy." → He said that honesty is the best policy. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) revolved
• "revolved" = past tense — സാധാരണ rule apply ചെയ്താൽ കിട്ടുന്ന answer ആണ്.
• പക്ഷേ ഇത് universal truth ആണ് — tense മാറ്റേണ്ടതില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Most students mechanically change present → past in indirect speech, ഈ exception അറിയാതെ A choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) had revolved
• "had revolved" = past perfect — ഇത് വളരെ പഴയ കാലത്തെ indicate ചെയ്യും.
• Universal truth-ന് past perfect ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "said" കണ്ടാൽ backshift ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് feel ആകും, had revolved choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) is revolving
• "is revolving" = present continuous — ongoing action indicate ചെയ്യും.
• Scientific fact-ന് continuous tense ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — simple present ആണ് ശരി.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "revolves around" = action ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, continuous choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SCIENCE NEVER CHANGES!" — Scientific fact Indirect Speech-ൽ tense മാറ്റില്ല. "Earth revolves" — billions of years ആയിട്ടും revolves; grammar-ലും മാറില്ല!
• Universal Truth family: Scientific facts, geographical facts, proverbs, mathematical truths — all stay in present tense.
• PSC exam strategy: Question-ൽ "The Earth...", "Water...", "The Sun..." കണ്ടാൽ — universal truth check ചെയ്യൂ. tense മാറ്റാതെ present tense-ൽ ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: Find the error and write the correct sentence:
optionB: I have been working here since 2019.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) I have been working here since 2019.

• "since 2019" = 2019 മുതൽ ഇപ്പോഴും തുടരുന്ന action — Present Perfect Continuous tense ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.
• "I have been working" = 2019 മുതൽ ഇപ്പോഴും work ചെയ്യുന്നു — ഇതാണ് correct.
• "since" = specific point in time (2019, Monday, January...) → Present Perfect Continuous signal word.
• PSC-ൽ "since/for" questions വളരെ common — ഈ rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ miss ആകില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: since/for + ongoing action = have/has been + V-ing (Present Perfect Continuous)
• "since" = specific start point (since 2019, since Monday, since childhood)
• "for" = duration (for 5 years, for two hours, for a long time)
• Both "since" and "for" with ongoing actions → Present Perfect Continuous.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: since/for tense questions PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ വരുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has been studying since morning. ✓
  2. They have been playing for two hours. ✓
  3. He has been working here since 2015. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) I am working here since 2019.
• "am working" = Present Continuous — ഇത് ഇപ്പോൾ നടക്കുന്ന action ആണ്, past-ൽ നിന്ന് continue ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല.
• Present Continuous-ൽ "since" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — ഇതാണ് question-ലെ error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "ഇപ്പോൾ working" = present continuous എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (C) I was working here since 2019.
• "was working" = Past Continuous — കഴിഞ്ഞ കാലത്ത് ഒരു specific time-ൽ നടന്ന action.
• "since 2019" ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ past continuous ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — action ഇപ്പോഴും continue ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 2019 = past ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, past tense choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) I had worked here since 2019.
• "had worked" = Past Perfect — ഇത് ഭൂതകാലത്തിൽ complete ആയ action ആണ്.
• ഇവിടെ action ഇപ്പോഴും continue ചെയ്യുന്നു, Past Perfect ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "had" + worked = formal/correct sound ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SINCE = START POINT → HAVE BEEN!" — since കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ "have/has been + V-ing" എഴുതൂ. 2019 = start point, ഇപ്പോഴും continue = have been working!
• Signal word family: since, for, how long, all day, all morning → Present Perfect Continuous.
• PSC exam strategy: "since" or "for" കണ്ടാൽ → have/has been + V-ing option ആണ് answer. ഒരിക്കലും simple present/past choose ചെയ്യരുത്.

questionText: Pick the correct comparative degree sentence:
optionA: My car is more fast than yours.
optionB: My car is fastest than yours.
optionC: My car is as faster as yours.
optionD: My car is faster than yours.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) My car is faster than yours.

• "faster" = "fast"-ന്റെ correct comparative form — one-syllable adjective ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് "-er" suffix ചേർക്കണം.
• "than" = comparative degree-ന്റെ signal word — രണ്ട് things compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ "than" ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• "My car is faster than yours" = ശരിയായ structure: adjective + -er + than.
• Short adjectives (1 syllable): fast→faster, tall→taller, big→bigger, hot→hotter.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 1 syllable adjective → add -er + than | 2+ syllables → more + adjective + than
• One-syllable: fast→faster, tall→taller, old→older, cold→colder
• Two+ syllables: beautiful→more beautiful, expensive→more expensive, difficult→more difficult
• Exceptions: good→better, bad→worse, far→farther/further, little→less
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Degrees of comparison PSC-ൽ regular ആയി വരും.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is taller than her sister. ✓
  2. This book is more interesting than that one. ✓
  3. He runs faster than me. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) more fast
• "more fast" = wrong — "fast" is one syllable, "more" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• One-syllable adjectives-ന് "-er" suffix മാത്രം — "more" + one-syllable = always wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "more" = comparative ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, "more fast" correct ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും.

❌ (B) fastest
• "fastest" = superlative degree — 3 or more things compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്.
• ഇവിടെ "than yours" ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് 2 things compare ആണ് → comparative degree വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "fastest" sounds strong and correct, but "than" = comparative signal.

❌ (C) as faster as
• "as...as" = positive degree structure — equal comparison-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് (as fast as).
• "as faster as" = grammatically impossible — "as...as"-ൽ comparative form ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "as...as" structure familiar ആണ്, "faster" correct ആണ് എന്ന് know ചെയ്യും, combine ചെയ്ത് choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SHORT = -ER, LONG = MORE!" — Short word (1 syllable) = add -er. Long word (2+ syllables) = add more. Fast is SHORT → faster ✓. Beautiful is LONG → more beautiful ✓.
• Degree family: fast→faster→fastest | good→better→best | bad→worse→worst.
• PSC exam strategy: "than" കണ്ടാൽ = comparative. "-er" or "more" check ചെയ്യൂ. "more + 1-syllable" = always wrong. "-est + than" = always wrong.

questionText:
Add suitable question tag to the following sentence:
Let's go for a walk, __________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) shall we?

• "Let's go for a walk" = "Let us go for a walk" — suggestion/invitation ആണ്.
• "Let's" ന്റെ question tag = always "shall we?" — ഇത് ഒരു fixed rule ആണ്.
• "Let's" = positive statement → positive tag "shall we?" — ഇത് exception rule ആണ്.
• PSC-ൽ "Let's" question tag ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — guaranteed mark ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Let's → shall we? (always! no exceptions!)
• Positive statement → Negative tag (He is tall, isn't he?)
• Negative statement → Positive tag (She isn't here, is she?)
• Special cases (must memorize for PSC):
  - Let's → shall we?
  - Imperative (Go!) → will you? / won't you?
  - I am → aren't I?
  - Nothing → is there?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ വരുന്ന grammar topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Let's play cricket, shall we? ✓
  2. Let's have dinner, shall we? ✓
  3. Let's go home, shall we? ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) will we?
• "will we?" = future tense tag — imperative sentences-ന് (Go!, Come!) ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• "Let's" = "shall we?" — "will we?" ഒരിക്കലും Let's-ന്റെ tag അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "will" = future ആണ്, "going for a walk" = future action ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (B) won't you?
• "won't you?" = negative imperative tag — "Don't do that, will you?" type sentences-ന്.
• "Let's" = "shall we?" — "you" tag ഒരിക്കലും Let's-ന് വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Question tag-ൽ "you" common ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (D) shall you?
• "shall you?" = grammatically incorrect — English-ൽ "shall you?" ഒരു standard tag അല്ല.
• "shall we?" ആണ് ശരി, "shall you?" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "shall we?" ഓർമ്മയിൽ നിന്ന് "we" മറന്ന് "you" write ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LET'S = SHALL WE? — ഒരുമിച്ച് പോകാം, SHALL WE?" — Let's = let us = we together → shall WE? Always!
• PSC Special Tag List (ഈ 4 ഓർക്കൂ): Let's → shall we? | Go! (imperative) → will you? | I am → aren't I? | Nobody → are they?
• PSC exam strategy: "Let's" കണ്ടാൽ — ഉടൻ "shall we?" — മറ്റ് options നോക്കേണ്ടതില്ല!

questionText:
Complete the sentence by adding a suitable word given below:
If she had studied harder, she __________ passed the exam.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) would have

• "If she had studied harder, she _____ passed the exam." — ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്.
• If clause-ൽ "had studied" (had + V3) ഉള്ളതിനാൽ main clause-ൽ "would have + V3" വേണം.
• "would have passed" = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് നടക്കാതിരുന്ന ഒരു കാര്യം — "അവൾ കൂടുതൽ പഠിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ pass ആയേനേ" — പക്ഷേ പഠിച്ചില്ല, pass ആയില്ല.
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്തിലെ unreal / imaginary situation.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (Third Conditional)
• Three types:
  - Type 1 (Real future): If + present → will + V1
  - Type 2 (Unreal present): If + past → would + V1
  - Type 3 (Unreal past): If + had + V3 → would have + V3
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditional sentences, especially Type 3, PSC-ൽ frequent ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had known, I would have helped. ✓
  2. If they had left early, they would have caught the train. ✓
  3. If he had tried harder, he would have succeeded. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) would
• "would" alone = Type 2 conditional (If + past → would + V1).
• ഇവിടെ "had studied" = past perfect ആണ്, Type 3 ആണ് → "would have" വേണം, "would" മാത്രം പോരാ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "would" = conditional ആണ് എന്ന് know ഉണ്ടാകും, "would" alone choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) will
• "will" = Type 1 conditional (future real) — if + present → will.
• ഇവിടെ "had studied" = past perfect → Type 3, "will" ഒരിക്കലും Type 3-ൽ വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "pass the exam" = future result ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, "will" choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) had
• "had" = past perfect — conditional main clause-ൽ "had" alone ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• conditional main clause requires "would have + V3", not "had".
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "had studied" കണ്ടതുകൊണ്ട് "had" pattern continue ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "HAD = WOULD HAVE!" — If clause-ൽ "had" കണ്ടാൽ main clause-ൽ "would have" — ഇത് ഒരു pair ആണ്. had → would have, always together!
• Conditional pair chart: will↔if+present | would↔if+past | would have↔if+had+V3
• PSC exam strategy: If clause-ൽ "had + V3" കണ്ടാൽ — ഉടൻ "would have" ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യൂ. 3 seconds-ൽ answer!

questionText: Choose the correct collective noun:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) A pride of lions

• "A pride of lions" — lions-ന്റെ ഒരു കൂട്ടത്തെ "pride" എന്നാണ് വിളിക്കുന്നത്. ഇത് ഇംഗ്ലീഷ് ഭാഷയിലെ ഒരു fixed collective noun ആണ്.
• "Pride" = അഭിമാനം എന്ന് general meaning, പക്ഷേ lions-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം = a pride — ഇത് specific collocation ആണ്.
• PSC-ൽ collective nouns of animals ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — "a pride of lions" ഏറ്റവും common ആയ question ആണ്.
• ഈ ഒരു answer ഓർത്തിരിക്കൂ: Lions = Pride, Sheep = Flock, Cattle = Herd, Wolves = Pack.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Collective Noun + of + Animal = Group name — ഓരോ animal-നും specific collective noun ഉണ്ട്.
• Animals-ന്റെ collective nouns (PSC most common):
  - Lions → a pride
  - Sheep/Birds → a flock
  - Cattle/Elephants → a herd
  - Wolves/Dogs → a pack
  - Fish → a school/shoal
  - Bees → a swarm
  - Crows → a murder
  - Ants → a colony
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Animal collective nouns PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും. Lions = pride ഏറ്റവും popular question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A pride of lions was resting under the tree. ✓
  2. A flock of birds flew over the lake. ✓
  3. A herd of cattle grazed in the field. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) A flock of lions
• "Flock" = sheep, birds, goats-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം — lions-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "A flock of sheep", "a flock of birds" — ഇതാണ് ശരി.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "flock" = animals-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം എന്ന് general feel ആകും, lions-ന് apply ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) A herd of lions
• "Herd" = cattle, elephants, horses-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം — lions-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "A herd of elephants", "a herd of cattle" — ഇതാണ് ശരി.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "herd" = large animals-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം, lions = large animal ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (D) A pack of lions
• "Pack" = wolves, dogs, foxes-ന്റെ കൂട്ടം — lions-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "A pack of wolves", "a pack of dogs" — ഇതാണ് ശരി.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: lions = powerful hunters ആണ്, wolves-ഉം powerful hunters ആണ്, അതിനാൽ same collective noun ഉണ്ടാകും എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LIONS HAVE PRIDE! — Lions = A Pride!" — Lion is the king of animals, they have pride (അഭിമാനം), just like the word! Pride of lions = king's group. ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കില്ല!
• PSC Collective Noun family (ഈ 4 ഓർക്കൂ): Lions=Pride | Sheep/Birds=Flock | Cattle=Herd | Wolves=Pack.
• PSC exam strategy: collective noun question-ൽ "lions" കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ "pride" ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യൂ. Flock/Herd/Pack = lions-ന് never!

questionText:
Choose the one-word substitute for the phrase given below:
One who does not believe in the existence of God

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Atheist

• "Atheist" = ദൈവത്തിന്റെ അസ്തിത്വത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കാത്ത ആൾ — "One who does not believe in the existence of God".
• Greek root: "a" = without/not + "theos" = God → Atheist = ദൈവം ഇല്ല എന്ന് believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• "Atheism" = atheist-ന്റെ belief system — ദൈവം ഇല്ല എന്ന philosophy.
• PSC-ൽ "theist"/"atheist"/"agnostic" confusion create ചെയ്യുന്ന question ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും — ഈ 3 words ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "a-" prefix = not/without — Atheist = not + God-believer.
• God-related one word substitutes (PSC important):
  - Atheist = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കാത്ത ആൾ (does NOT believe)
  - Theist = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ (believes in God)
  - Agnostic = ദൈവം ഉണ്ടോ ഇല്ലയോ എന്ന് അറിയില്ല എന്ന് പറയുന്ന ആൾ (uncertain)
  - Monotheist = ഒരു ദൈവം മാത്രം believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ (one God)
  - Polytheist = ഒന്നിലധികം ദൈവങ്ങളിൽ believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ (many Gods)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് വരും. Atheist/Agnostic confusion popular question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He is an atheist who believes religion is a myth. ✓
  2. Most scientists in the survey identified as agnostic. ✓
  3. A theist prays to God every day. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Agnostic
• "Agnostic" = ദൈവം ഉണ്ടോ ഇല്ലയോ എന്ന് അറിയില്ല എന്ന് പറയുന്ന ആൾ — "uncertain about God".
• Atheist = believes God does NOT exist. Agnostic = doesn't know if God exists. വ്യത്യാസം ഓർക്കൂ!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "agnostic" sounds like "not believing" — "a-" prefix = not, ആണ് ഇവിടെ ALSO ഉണ്ട് (Greek: agnostos = unknown). Atheist vs Agnostic confusion popular PSC trap.

❌ (B) Theist
• "Theist" = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ — question-ൽ ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കാത്ത ആൾ ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത്.
• Theist = believes in God (positive). Atheist = does NOT believe (negative). Opposite meanings!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "theist" = God-related word ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും, correct ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും.

❌ (D) Monotheist
• "Monotheist" = ഒരു ദൈവം മാത്രം believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — "mono" = one.
• Question-ൽ ദൈവം exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല എന്ന് believe ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത്, ഒരു ദൈവം believe ചെയ്യുന്നവൻ അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "mono" + "theist" = complex word ആണ്, formal/educated sound ആകും, correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "A for ABSENT — Atheist = God is ABSENT from their belief!" — 'A' = Absent, 'Atheist' = God is absent. Agnostic = 'A' for 'Ask me later, I don't know!'
• God belief family (ഈ 3 pair ഓർക്കൂ): Theist↔Atheist (believe↔not believe) | Agnostic (don't know) | Mono-↔Poly- (one↔many).
• PSC exam strategy: "does not believe in God" = ATHEIST. "uncertain about God" = AGNOSTIC. Question-ൽ "does NOT" കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ "A-" prefix ഉള്ള word choose ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: Choose the correct antonym of the word: ABUNDANT

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Scanty

• "Abundant" = വളരെ ധാരാളം ഉള്ള, plenty of something — "ഐശ്വര്യം ഉള്ള, സമൃദ്ധമായ".
• "Scanty" = വളരെ കുറഞ്ഞ, barely enough — "ദൗർലഭ്യം, കുറഞ്ഞ".
• Abundant ↔ Scanty = exact opposites — "ധാരാളം" ↔ "വളരെ കുറഞ്ഞ".
• PSC-ൽ antonym questions-ൽ synonyms-നെ options-ആയി ഇടും — ഈ trap ഓർക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Antonym = OPPOSITE meaning word — Abundant = plenty → Antonym = shortage/lack.
• Abundance word family:
  - Abundant (adj) → Abundantly (adv) → Abundance (noun)
  - Synonyms: plentiful, ample, sufficient, copious, profuse, lavish
  - Antonyms: scanty, scarce, meager, sparse, insufficient, deficient
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms/Antonyms PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common vocabulary questions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Rainfall was abundant this monsoon. ✓ (Antonym: The rainfall was scanty this year.)
  2. The region has abundant natural resources. ✓
  3. Scanty evidence led to the case being dismissed. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Plentiful
• "Plentiful" = ധാരാളം ഉള്ള — ഇത് "abundant"-ന്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല.
• Plentiful = abundant = same meaning. Question antonym ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Antonym question-ൽ synonyms ഇടും — ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common PSC trap! "Plentiful" sounds like correct answer.

❌ (B) Sufficient
• "Sufficient" = ആവശ്യത്തിന് ഉള്ള — ഇതും "abundant"-ന് close in meaning, opposite അല്ല.
• Sufficient = enough (neutral). Abundant = more than enough (positive). Scanty = not enough (negative).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "sufficient" = neutral, "abundant" = positive, feel-ൽ opposite ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നും.

❌ (D) Ample
• "Ample" = ധാരാളം, more than enough — ഇതും "abundant"-ന്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്.
• Ample = abundant = plentiful = all synonyms. Antonym question-ൽ ഇവ ഒന്നും correct ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Ample" = uncommon word ആണ്, students-ന് meaning അറിയില്ലെങ്കിൽ correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ABUNDANT = ABUNDANT food at a feast. SCANTY = Scanty food in a drought. Feast ↔ Drought = Abundant ↔ Scanty!"
• PSC Antonym Trap Alert: ഈ question-ൽ 3 options (A, B, D) = synonyms ആണ്! PSC ഇങ്ങനെ ചെയ്യും — ഒരു correct antonym + 3 synonyms. Always find the OPPOSITE, not the similar.
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ question word-ന്റെ meaning ആദ്യം മനസ്സിലാക്കൂ → ഏറ്റവും opposite meaning ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യൂ. Synonyms eliminate ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: Choose the correctly spelt word:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Miscellaneous

• "Miscellaneous" = വിവിധ തരം, mixed variety — "പലതരം കാര്യങ്ങൾ ഉൾക്കൊള്ളുന്ന".
• Correct spelling: M-I-S-C-E-L-L-A-N-E-O-U-S — 13 letters.
• Key points to remember: double "ll" (misceLLaneous), "eous" ending (not "ious" or "ious").
• PSC-ൽ long difficult words-ന്റെ spelling ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — "miscellaneous" popular choice ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: mis-cel-la-ne-ous — 5 syllables ആയി break ചെയ്ത് ഓർക്കൂ.
• Spelling breakdown:
  - mis + cell + an + eous
  - "misCELLaneous" — CELL ഓർക്കൂ (like a cell phone!)
  - Double L: miscel-L-L-aneous ✓ (ഒരു L മാത്രം = WRONG)
  - Ending: -eous (eous, not -ious, not -ious)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions PSC-ൽ regular ആണ്. Long words with double letters frequently tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He sold miscellaneous items at the market. ✓
  2. The box contained miscellaneous tools. ✓
  3. There were miscellaneous charges on the bill. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Miscelaneous
• ഇവിടെ "ll" ഇല്ല, ഒരു "l" മാത്രം — ഇതാണ് error.
• Correct: misceLLaneous (double L). Wrong: miscelaneous (single L).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Quick reading-ൽ correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും — double letter miss ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) Miscellanneous
• ഇവിടെ "nn" ഉണ്ട് — extra "n" ആണ് error.
• Correct: miscellanEous (one n). Wrong: miscellaNNeous (double n — wrong!).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double L ഓർത്ത്, double N-ഉം ചേർക്കും, over-correction ആണ് ഇത്.

❌ (D) Miscellanious
• Ending "ious" ആണ് — correct ending "eous" ആണ്.
• Correct: miscellane-OUS (eous). Wrong: miscellani-OUS (ious).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "-ious" suffix common ആണ് (various, serious, curious) — same pattern apply ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "mis-CELL-aneous — ഒരു CELL-ൽ miscellaneous items ഉണ്ട്!" — CELL (mobile phone cell) = double L. mis + CELL + aneous. CELL ഓർത്താൽ double L ഓർക്കും!
• Spelling check list: (1) Double L? YES — misceLLaneous ✓ (2) Ending -eous? YES — miscellaneOUS ✓ (3) Single N? YES — miscellanEous ✓
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ options carefully compare ചെയ്യൂ. Double letters, endings (-eous/-ious), silent letters — ഇവ ഒന്നൊന്നായി check ചെയ്യൂ. "CELL" trick ഉപയോഗിക്കൂ.

questionText:
Fill in the blank with a suitable preposition:
The minister presided __________ the meeting.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) over

• "Preside over" = ഒരു meeting/function-ന്റെ chairman/president ആയി ഇരിക്കുക — "അധ്യക്ഷം വഹിക്കുക".
• "The minister presided over the meeting" = മന്ത്രി meeting-ന് അധ്യക്ഷത വഹിച്ചു.
• "Preside over" = fixed collocation ആണ് — ഇത് മാറ്റാൻ കഴിയില്ല. Verb + Preposition pair ആണ്.
• PSC-ൽ "preside over" ഒരു common question ആണ് — ഈ collocation ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Preside + OVER (always!) — preside-ന്റെ only correct preposition is 'over'.
• Verb + Preposition fixed collocations (PSC most important):
  - Preside OVER (meeting/function)
  - Insist ON (something)
  - Consist OF (elements)
  - Depend ON (person/thing)
  - Congratulate ON (achievement)
  - Apologize FOR (mistake)
  - Arrive AT (place — small) / IN (city/country)
  - Interfere IN/WITH
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Verb+Preposition collocations PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ചോദിക്കുന്ന preposition type.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The chairman presided over the board meeting. ✓
  2. She presided over the ceremony with grace. ✓
  3. He presided over the committee for 5 years. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) at
• "Preside at" = incorrect collocation — "preside"-ന്റെ correct preposition "over" ആണ്.
• "At" = location/place-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന preposition — "at the meeting" (attend the meeting-ന്) ശരി, പക്ഷേ "preside at" wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "at the meeting" pattern familiar ആണ്, "presided at" correct ആണ് എന്ന് feel ആകും.

❌ (C) on
• "Preside on" = incorrect — "on" ഒരിക്കലും "preside"-ന്റെ preposition അല്ല.
• "On" = surface/day-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കും (on the table, on Monday).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "focused on", "concentrated on" patterns familiar ആണ്, same pattern apply ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) in
• "Preside in" = incorrect — "in" ഒരിക്കലും "preside"-ന്റെ preposition അല്ല.
• "In" = enclosed space/large place-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കും (in the room, in India).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "in the meeting" = ഒരു meeting-ൽ ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നു എന്ന് feel ആകും, "presided in" ആണ് correct എന്ന് തോന്നും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "Minister PRESIDES OVER all — OVER everyone in the meeting!" — Presiding = having authority OVER everyone. Authority = OVER. Preside OVER — authority OVER — ഒന്നിച്ച് ഓർക്കൂ!
• Preposition collocation family: Preside OVER | Chair OVER | Reign OVER — authority/control = OVER.
• PSC exam strategy: "preside" കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ "over" choose ചെയ്യൂ. at/on/in = never with preside. ഈ question 3 seconds-ൽ answer ചെയ്യാം!

questionText:
Fill in the blanks with suitable article.
________ university in which she is studying is one of the best universities in the world.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) the

• 'The university in which she is studying' — ഇത് ഒരു specific university-യെ refer ചെയ്യുന്നു, ഏതോ ഒരു specific university-ൽ അവൾ പഠിക്കുന്നു.
• 'The' = Definite article — already known/specific ആയ ഒരു noun-ന് മുൻപ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. 'Which she is studying in' = relative clause, specific university identify ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'University' = vowel letter ('u') കൊണ്ട് തുടങ്ങുന്നു, പക്ഷേ /j/ (consonant sound) ആണ് — 'a university' (not 'an'). ഇവിടെ specific ആണ് → 'the'.
• PSC-ൽ 'the' vs 'a/an' article choice ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — specific + relative clause = 'the' ആണ് signal.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Specific / already identified noun = THE | First mention / one of many = A/AN | General / uncountable = no article
• 'The' ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ട situations:
  - Relative clause identify ചെയ്യുന്ന noun: 'The book THAT I read was good.'
  - Unique things: the sun, the moon, the sky
  - Already mentioned: 'I saw a dog. The dog was friendly.'
  - Superlatives: the best, the highest
• 'A/An' ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ട situations:
  - First mention: 'I saw a dog.'
  - One of many (non-specific): 'She studies in a university.'
• 'University' = /juːnɪvɜːsɪti/ = /j/ sound (consonant) → 'a university' (not 'an') — sound rule!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Article questions — 'the' with relative clauses and specific nouns PSC-ൽ regular.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The college where he studied is famous. ✓ (relative clause → the)
  2. She is the best student in the class. ✓ (superlative → the)
  3. He works in a university in Delhi. ✓ (non-specific → a)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) a
• 'A university' = non-specific, one of many universities — ഇവിടെ specific university ആണ്, relative clause 'in which she is studying' specify ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'University' = vowel letter → 'an' wrong, 'a' ശരി feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ specific context = 'the' ആണ് correct.

❌ (C) an
• 'An' = vowel sound-ന് മുൻപ്. 'University' = /j/ consonant sound → 'an university' grammatically wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'University' = 'u' letter (vowel) → 'an' correct feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ sound (/j/) = consonant → 'a/the' ആണ്.

❌ (D) no article
• Specific noun ('the university in which she studies') + 'one of the best' = definitely needs 'the'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Proper nouns (university names) often no article ഇടും — ഇവിടെ name ഇല്ല, description ഉണ്ട്, article വേണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'WHICH/THAT/WHERE = THE signal!' — Relative clause (which/that/where) ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ noun specific ആകും = THE. 'The university WHICH she studies in' — WHICH കണ്ടാൽ THE ഉണ്ട്!
• University article trap: 'a university' (non-specific, consonant sound /j/) | 'an hour' (vowel sound /aʊ/) | 'the university near my house' (specific).
• PSC exam strategy: Blank fill article question-ൽ noun-ന് ശേഷം relative clause (which/that) ഉണ്ടോ? Yes → 'the'. Non-specific, first mention → 'a/an'. Sound check: /j/,/w/ = consonant → 'a'.

questionText: Find out the correct answer from the following sentences.
optionA: One of the student in the class scores full marks.
optionB: One of the students in the class scores full marks.
optionC: One of the students in the class score full marks.
optionD: One of the student in the class score full marks.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) One of the students in the class scores full marks.

• 'One of the students' — രണ്ട് rules ഒരുമിച്ച്: (1) 'one of the' + plural noun = 'students' (plural) ✓ (2) Subject = 'One' (singular) → verb = 'scores' (singular) ✓.
• 'One of the' pattern-ൽ: noun = plural ആകണം (students, not student) + verb = singular ആകണം (scores, not score).
• PSC-ൽ 'One of the...' pattern ഒരു popular trick question ആണ് — ഈ double rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: One of the + PLURAL NOUN + SINGULAR VERB
  One of the students SCORES ✓ (not 'score')
  One of the students SCORES ✓ (not 'student')
• ഈ pattern-ൽ രണ്ട് rules ഒരുമിച്ച്:
  Rule 1: 'one of the' ന് ശേഷം = plural noun (students, books, children)
  Rule 2: Real subject = 'One' (singular) → verb = singular form (scores, goes, works)
• Similar patterns:
  - Each of the students HAS a book. ✓ (Each = singular)
  - Neither of the boys WAS present. ✓ (Neither = singular)
  - Either of the answers IS correct. ✓ (Either = singular)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'One of the...' agreement PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന trick question.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. One of the books is missing from the shelf. ✓
  2. One of the teachers was absent today. ✓
  3. One of the children has won the prize. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) One of the STUDENT...SCORES
• 'Student' = singular — 'one of the' ന് ശേഷം plural noun വേണം ('students'). Rule 1 violate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'One' = singular → noun-ഉം singular ആകണം feel ആകും. 'One' singular ആണ്, പക്ഷേ noun plural ആകണം!

❌ (C) One of the STUDENTS...SCORE
• 'Students' = plural ✓ (Rule 1 correct), പക്ഷേ 'score' = plural verb — subject 'One' = singular → 'scores' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Students' = plural → verb-ഉം plural ('score') ആകണം feel ആകും. Subject = 'One' (singular) → singular verb!

❌ (D) One of the STUDENT...SCORE
• Both rules violate ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'student' (singular, should be plural) + 'score' (plural verb, should be singular).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'One student scores' pattern familiar → same apply ചെയ്യും, 'one of the' pattern different ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ONE of the MANY → ONE decides the verb!' — One of the MANY students (plural noun after 'of') → but ONE person → ONE scores (singular verb). MANY for the noun, ONE for the verb!
• Double rule shortcut: 'One of the ___(plural)___ ___(singular verb)___' — Fill plural first, then singular verb.
• PSC exam strategy: 'One of the' question-ൽ ആദ്യം noun check ചെയ്യൂ (plural ആണോ?) then verb check ചെയ്യൂ (singular ആണോ?). Both correct = correct answer!

questionText: Choose the correct question tag from the following.
optionA: No one has attended the meeting, hasn't they?
optionB: Let's go for a film, shouldn't we?
optionD: She seldom attended the meeting, didn't she?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Get out, will you?

• 'Get out' = Imperative sentence (command/request) — subject ഇല്ല, verb കൊണ്ട് തുടങ്ങുന്നു.
• Imperative sentence-ന്റെ question tag = 'will you?' (positive command) അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'won't you?' (polite request) — ഇത് fixed rule ആണ്.
• 'Get out, will you?' — command + 'will you?' tag = grammatically correct.
• Options A, B, D — ഓരോന്നിലും specific grammar errors ഉണ്ട്. Option C alone correct ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Positive statement → Negative tag
  Negative statement → Positive tag
  Imperative (command) → will you? / won't you?
  Let's → shall we?
• PSC Special Tag Rules (ഈ list ഓർക്കൂ):
  - Let's → shall we? ✓ (NOT shouldn't we / don't we)
  - Imperative (Go!/Come!/Do!) → will you? / won't you? ✓
  - No one/Nobody/Nothing → have they? / did they? (positive tag, plural)
  - Seldom/Rarely/Never = negative adverbs → positive tag (did she?, not didn't she?)
  - I am → aren't I?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags — especially special cases — PSC-ൽ regular ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Sit down, will you? ✓ (imperative → will you?)
  2. Let's have tea, shall we? ✓ (Let's → shall we?)
  3. Nobody came, did they? ✓ (nobody = negative → positive tag)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) No one has attended the meeting, hasn't they?
• Error 1: 'No one' = negative → positive tag വേണം (have they?, not hasn't they?)
• Error 2: 'hasn't they' = wrong — 'no one' = singular pronoun 'they' is not standard here, 'have they?' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'No one has' → 'hasn't' negative mirror feel ആകും. Negative → Positive rule: 'have they?' ആണ്.

❌ (B) Let's go for a film, shouldn't we?
• 'Let's' tag = always 'shall we?' — 'shouldn't we?' = wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Let's = suggestion' → 'shouldn't we?' polite feel ആകും. Fixed rule: Let's = shall we? only!

❌ (D) She seldom attended the meeting, didn't she?
• 'Seldom' = negative adverb ('hardly ever') — sentence already negative sense ഉണ്ട് → positive tag = 'did she?' ആണ് correct.
• 'Didn't she?' = negative tag — negative adverb sentence-ൽ negative tag again = wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'attended' = past tense, positive-looking → 'didn't she?' feel ആകും. 'Seldom' = negative signal!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'COMMAND = WILL YOU obey? — Imperative = will you?' — Police says 'Get out!' — 'Will you?' confirms. Command → Will you? Always!
• Negative adverbs PSC list (ഇവ കണ്ടാൽ positive tag): seldom, rarely, hardly, barely, never, scarcely, little, few.
• PSC exam strategy: 4 options-ൽ ഓരോന്നും individually verify ചെയ്യൂ. Errors: 'hasn't they' (should be 'have they'), 'shouldn't we' (should be 'shall we'), 'didn't she' after 'seldom' (should be 'did she'). Only C = error-free!

questionText:
Which is the correct passive voice of the sentence
Ram is eating a mango?
optionA: A mango is eaten by Ram
optionB: A mango was eaten by Ram
optionC: A mango is being eaten by Ram
optionD: A mango was being eaten by Ram

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) A mango is being eaten by Ram

• Active: 'Ram IS EATING a mango' — Present Continuous tense ആണ്.
• Active tense = Passive tense rule: Present Continuous → Passive Present Continuous.
• Present Continuous Passive formula: is/am/are + being + V3 (past participle)
• 'Is eating' → 'is being eaten' — 'is' (present) + 'being' + 'eaten' (V3).
• 'A mango' = passive subject. 'By Ram' = agent.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active tense = Passive tense — ALWAYS same tense!
• Present Continuous Passive: is/am/are + being + V3
  Active: Ram is eating a mango.
  Passive: A mango is being eaten by Ram.
• Tense-wise Passive formula table:
  - Simple Present: is/am/are + V3 ('A mango is eaten by Ram')
  - Present Continuous: is/am/are + being + V3 ('A mango is being eaten')
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3 ('A mango was eaten')
  - Past Continuous: was/were + being + V3 ('A mango was being eaten')
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3 ('A mango has been eaten')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Active-Passive — especially continuous tenses — PSC-ൽ frequent ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is writing a letter. → A letter is being written by her. ✓
  2. They are building a bridge. → A bridge is being built by them. ✓
  3. He is reading a book. → A book is being read by him. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) A mango is eaten by Ram
• 'Is eaten' = Simple Present Passive — 'is eating' = Present Continuous, tense mismatch.
• Simple Present Passive ≠ Present Continuous Passive. 'Is eating' → 'is being eaten', not 'is eaten'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Is eaten' = simple passive, commonly known → correct feel ആകും. 'Being' = continuous-ന്റെ signal, forget ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) A mango was eaten by Ram
• 'Was eaten' = Simple Past Passive — active sentence present tense ('is eating'), not past.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was eaten' = familiar passive structure → correct feel ആകും. Tense change = wrong!

❌ (D) A mango was being eaten by Ram
• 'Was being eaten' = Past Continuous Passive — active is Present Continuous ('is eating'), not past.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Being' = continuous signal ✓, പക്ഷേ 'was' = past tense, 'is' = present. 'Is being eaten' ആണ് correct.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IS EATING → IS BEING EATEN! — Both have IS. Both are present. -ING adds BEING in passive!' — Present Continuous: IS + EATING (active) → IS + BEING + EATEN (passive). 'Eating' ന്റെ passive = 'being eaten'!
• Continuous Passive pattern: is/are/was/were + BEING + V3 — 'being' = continuous-ന്റെ passive marker.
• PSC exam strategy: Active sentence-ൽ 'is eating' (present continuous) → Passive-ൽ 'is being eaten' (present continuous passive). Tense match check: 'is' = present → passive-ഉം 'is' = present. 'Was' options = automatically wrong.

questionText:
Fill in the blanks with correct phrasal verb of the word given in the bracket.
The meeting was ______ (cancelled) because of some personal issues of the manager.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Called off

• 'Called off' = cancel ചെയ്യുക — meeting/event cancel ആകുമ്പോൾ 'called off' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Question-ൽ bracket-ൽ 'cancelled' = synonym clue — 'called off' = 'cancelled' exact meaning ആണ്.
• 'The meeting was called off' = meeting cancel ചെയ്തു — manager-ന്റെ personal issues = reason.
• PSC-ൽ 'call' family phrasal verbs ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'called off = cancelled' ഓർത്തിരിക്കൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: CALL + preposition = completely different meaning!
• CALL family phrasal verbs (PSC must-know):
  - Call off = cancel ചെയ്യുക (the match was called off)
  - Call out = shout ചെയ്യുക / challenge ചെയ്യുക (call out for help)
  - Call up = telephone ചെയ്യുക / summon ചെയ്യുക (call up a friend)
  - Call on = visit ചെയ്യുക / request ചെയ്യുക (call on a friend)
  - Call for = demand ചെയ്യുക / require ചെയ്യുക (this calls for action)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — CALL family popular.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The match was called off due to rain. ✓
  2. She called up her friend to share the news. ✓
  3. The doctor called on his patient in the hospital. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Called out
• 'Called out' = shout/challenge — meeting cancel-ന്റെ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Call out' = known phrase, 'cancelled' context-ൽ feel ആകും.

❌ (C) Called up
• 'Called up' = phone ചെയ്യുക / military-ലേക്ക് summon ചെയ്യുക — cancel meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Called up' = common phrasal verb, familiar feel ആകും.

❌ (D) Called on
• 'Called on' = visit/request — cancel meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Call on' = professional context feel → meeting context-ൽ correct ആകും feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CALL OFF = Switch OFF the meeting! — OFF = stop/cancel. Call OFF = cancel. Meeting OFF ആക്കി = called off!' — OFF ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ = stop/cancel.
• CALL family cheat sheet: call OFF=cancel | call UP=phone | call ON=visit | call OUT=shout.
• PSC exam strategy: Bracket-ൽ synonym clue ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ (cancelled) → directly matching phrasal verb choose ചെയ്യൂ. 'Cancelled' = 'called off'. Cancel = OFF. Easy!

questionText: Choose the correct antonym of the word 'luminous'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) dark

• 'Luminous' = പ്രകാശമുള്ള, shining, bright — 'ദ്യുതിമാൻ, തേജസ്സുള്ള'.
• Antonym = opposite meaning: 'Luminous' (bright/shining) ↔ 'dark' (ഇരുട്ട്, പ്രകാശം ഇല്ലാത്ത).
• Latin root: 'lumen' = light → 'luminous' = full of light → antonym = dark (light ഇല്ലാത്ത).
• PSC-ൽ antonym questions-ൽ synonyms trap ആയി options-ൽ ഇടും — 'shiny' = synonym, 'dark' = antonym.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Antonym = OPPOSITE meaning — Luminous (bright) → dark (opposite)
• 'Luminous' word family:
  - Luminous (adj) → Luminosity (noun) → Luminously (adverb)
  - Synonyms: bright, shining, glowing, radiant, brilliant, incandescent
  - Antonyms: dark, dim, dull, gloomy, murky, opaque
• Etymology: Latin 'lumen' (light) → luminous = light-filled
  Same root: illuminate, luminescence, lux
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms/Antonyms PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common vocabulary questions.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The luminous stars lit up the night sky. (Antonym: The dark sky had no stars.) ✓
  2. She wore a luminous yellow dress. ✓
  3. The room was dark after the power cut. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Shiny
• 'Shiny' = പ്രകാശിക്കുന്ന, gleaming — ഇത് 'luminous'-ന്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Antonym question-ൽ ഏറ്റവും common trap = synonym option ഒന്നാമതായി ഇടും. 'Shiny' = same meaning, choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) diminish
• 'Diminish' = കുറയുക, reduce ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് light-ന്റെ opposite അല്ല, size/amount reduce ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Diminish light' = context-ൽ use ചെയ്യാം, antonym feel ആകും — പക്ഷേ exact antonym of 'luminous' = 'dark'.

❌ (D) Retract
• 'Retract' = പിൻവലിക്കുക, take back ചെയ്യുക — light/brightness-ന്റെ antonym ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar word = educated/correct sound ആകും feel — meaning check ചെയ്യാതെ choose ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LUMINOUS = LUMEN (light in Latin). LUMENous = full of LIGHT. Antonym = DARK (no light)!' — Lumen = light, luminous = full of light, dark = no light. LUMEN opposite = DARK!
• Light word family: luminous, illuminate, luminescence, lux — all from Latin 'lumen'.
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ ആദ്യം question word meaning: luminous = bright. Synonym options eliminate (shiny = bright = eliminate). 'Dark' = opposite. Clear choice!

questionText: Choose the word with correct spelling.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Collage

• 'Collage' = വിവിധ materials ഉപയോഗിച്ച് ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന ഒരു art form — French origin word, correctly spelled C-O-L-L-A-G-E.
• ഈ question-ൽ other 3 options-ൽ spelling errors ഉണ്ട് — only 'Collage' correctly spelled ആണ്.
• A) Seperate → Separate (e-a-r, not e-r-a), B) Tortioise → Tortoise (no extra 'i'), D) Plaigiarism → Plagiarism (no 'i' after 'la').
• PSC-ൽ commonly misspelled words-ന്റെ correct spelling choose ചെയ്യൽ regular question ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Spelling-ൽ common errors — extra letters, missing letters, wrong vowel order.
• ഈ question-ലെ spelling errors:
  - Seperate → Separate (memory trick: there is 'A RAT' in sepARAte)
  - Tortioise → Tortoise (T-O-R-T-O-I-S-E — extra 'i' between tort-oise ഇല്ല)
  - Collage → Collage ✓ (C-O-L-L-A-G-E — French art term, correctly spelled)
  - Plaigiarism → Plagiarism (P-L-A-G-I-A-R-I-S-M — 'lai' not 'lai', no extra 'i')
• PSC commonly misspelled words (must-know):
  Separate, Necessary, Occurrence, Miscellaneous, Plagiarism, Tortoise, Accommodation, Embarrass
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions PSC-ൽ regular — commonly confused spellings frequent.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The art teacher asked students to make a collage. ✓
  2. We must keep separate records for each department. ✓
  3. A tortoise can live for over 100 years. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Seperate
• Correct spelling: SEPARATE (S-E-P-A-R-A-T-E) — 'a-r-a-t-e' not 'e-r-a-t-e'.
• Memory trick: 'There is A RAT in sepARAte!' — sepARAte → A-R-A in the middle.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Seperate' = sounds correct when spoken, visually similar — most common English spelling mistake worldwide!

❌ (B) Tortioise
• Correct spelling: TORTOISE (T-O-R-T-O-I-S-E) — extra 'i' after 'tort' is wrong.
• 'Tortioise' has an extra 'i' — tort+i+oise = wrong. Correct: tort+oise = tortoise.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Malayalam-ൽ 'ടോർട്ടോയ്സ്' sound-ൽ extra syllable feel ആകും, 'i' ചേർക്കും.

❌ (D) Plaigiarism
• Correct spelling: PLAGIARISM (P-L-A-G-I-A-R-I-S-M) — 'plai' not 'plai+g' = 'plag'.
• 'Plaigiarism' = extra 'i' after 'la' — 'plagiarism' = plag+iar+ism.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Long word, complex spelling — extra letter add ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory tricks for all 3 wrong options:
  SEPARATE: 'There is A RAT in sepARAte!' — sepARAte ✓
  TORTOISE: 'TORT + OISE — like a French noise (oise)!' — tort-OISE ✓ (no extra i)
  PLAGIARISM: 'PLAG + IARISM — PLAG sounds like flag!' — PLAGiarism ✓
• Collage: French word (col + lage) — double L, ends in -age. Simple!
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ — ഓരോ option-ഉം mentally pronounce ചെയ്ത്, familiar wrong patterns check ചെയ്യൂ. Extra letters, missing letters identify ചെയ്യൂ. Correct option = error-free!

questionText:
Fill in the blanks with suitable modal verb.
Policemen ______ wear Khaki.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) must

• 'Policemen _____ wear Khaki.' — Police uniform wear ചെയ്യൽ = rule/duty/obligation ആണ്, choice അല്ല.
• 'Must' = strong obligation, rule, duty — ഒരു കാര്യം ചെയ്തിരിക്കണം, mandatory ആണ്.
• 'Policemen must wear Khaki' = police-ന്റെ official duty/rule — must = rule-based obligation.
• PSC-ൽ modal verbs-ന്റെ correct choice ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — obligation context = 'must' ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Obligation/Rule/Duty = MUST | Ability = CAN | Future intention = WILL | First person future = SHALL
• Modal verbs and their meanings (PSC must-know):
  - Must = obligation, rule, necessity ('You must wear a seatbelt')
  - Can = ability, possibility ('She can speak French')
  - Will = future intention, promise ('I will help you')
  - Shall = first person future, formal suggestion ('Shall we go?')
  - Should = advice, recommendation ('You should see a doctor')
  - May = permission, possibility ('You may leave now')
  - Might = weaker possibility ('It might rain today')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modal verb questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കും — 'must' for rules/obligations frequent.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Students must wear uniform to school. ✓ (rule)
  2. She can swim very well. ✓ (ability)
  3. Will you come to the party? ✓ (future)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) will
• 'Will' = future intention/promise — 'Policemen will wear Khaki' = future plan, not obligation/rule.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will' = common modal, general future use → police future-ൽ uniform ഇടും feel ആകും.

❌ (B) can
• 'Can' = ability/permission — 'Policemen can wear Khaki' = they have the ability/are allowed to, not they must.
