• ഇത് Words Often Confused category — (adopt / adept / adapt / accept) — PSC-ൽ frequent ആണ്!
• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  ADAPT = adjust to new situation (adapt to)
  ADOPT = to take as one's own (adopt a child, adopt a policy)
  ADEPT = highly skilled (adjective) (adept at)
  ACCEPT = to receive/agree to (accept an offer)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Confusable words — especially adapt/adopt — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദ്യം ആകുന്ന word confusion topics ആണ്!

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. She adapted to life in the city very quickly.
   (City-യിലെ ജീവിതത്തിൽ അവൾ വേഗം adapt ആകി)
2. They adopted a child from the orphanage.
   (Orphanage-ൽ നിന്ന് ഒരു കുട്ടിയെ adopt ചെയ്തു)
3. He is adept at playing the guitar.
   (Guitar വായിക്കുന്നതിൽ അദ്ദേഹം adept ആണ്)
4. She accepted the job offer.
   (Job offer accept ചെയ്തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) adopt — 'Adopt' = to take something/someone as your own (ഉദാ: adopt a child, adopt a new policy). 'Adopt to new situations' ഒരു valid expression ഇല്ല. 'Adopt' ഒരിക്കലും 'to' preposition-ൽ 'new situations' follow ചെയ്യില്ല — 🪤 Trap! 'Adapt' ↔ 'Adopt' most commonly confused pair!

❌ (B) adept — 'Adept' ഒരു ADJECTIVE ആണ്, verb ഇല്ല! 'Adept at something' = ഒരു skill-ൽ expert ആയ. Sentence-ൽ blank-ൽ verb ആണ് വേണ്ടത് ('take time to ___'), adjective ആകില്ല. 'Adept to' ഒരു valid construction ഇല്ല.

❌ (D) accept — 'Accept' = to receive or agree to something (ഒരു offer, gift, decision accept ചെയ്യുക). 'Accept to new situations' ഒരു recognized expression ഇല്ല. 'Accept' = receive/agree, 'adapt' = adjust — ഇവ different meanings.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ADaPT = AD + PAT the back! New situation-ൽ settle ആകുമ്പോൾ someone gives you a PAT — you've ADAPTED!' | 'ADoPT = AD + POT — you bring a POT (child/thing) home as your own!'
• Quick distinction card:
  ADAPT TO (adjust) — 'T' for Transform/Tune yourself
  ADOPT (take as own) — 'O' for Own/Ownership
  ADEPT AT (skilled) — adjective only, 'E' for Expert
  ACCEPT (agree) — 'C' for Consent/Confirm
• PSC exam strategy: 'new situations' ഉള്ള blank → 'adapt to' ആണ്. 'Adopt / adept / accept' — ഇവ confuse ആകരുത്: adjust = adapt, adopt = ownership, adept = skilled (adj).

questionText: Kiran's pen was _________ than that of Arun.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) worse

• 'Than' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ comparative degree ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ടത്.
• 'Bad'-ന്റെ degrees: bad (positive) → worse (comparative) → worst (superlative).
• 'Kiran's pen was worse than that of Arun' = 'Arun-ന്റേതിനേക്കാൾ Kiran-ന്റെ pen bad ആണ്'.
• 'Worse' = irregular comparative form of 'bad' — 'more bad' ആകില്ല, 'worse' ആണ്.
• 'Than' = always comparative degree-ന്റെ signal!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'than' keyword → Comparative Degree!
• Irregular Degrees — PSC most important:
  bad → worse → worst
  good → better → best
  many/much → more → most
  little → less → least
  far → farther/further → farthest/furthest
  old → older/elder → oldest/eldest
  late → later/latter → latest/last

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Irregular degrees of comparison PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദ്യം ആകും!

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. This road is worse than the other one.
   (ഈ road മറ്റേതിനേക്കാൾ bad ആണ്)
2. He is the worst player in the team.
   (Team-ൽ ഏറ്റവും bad player അദ്ദേഹമാണ്)
3. His health is getting worse every day.
   (അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ ആരോഗ്യം ദിവസം ദിവസം worse ആകുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) bad — 'Bad' positive degree ആണ്. 'Than' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ positive degree ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — comparative degree വേണം. 'Kiran's pen was bad than Arun's' = grammatically incorrect.

❌ (C) worst — 'Worst' superlative degree ആണ്. Superlative = 'the' + 'worst' — 'the worst in the group'. 'Than' keyword superlative-ന്റേതല്ല, comparative-ന്റേതാണ്. 'Worst than' = grammatically incorrect — 🪤 Trap! Students 'bad → worst' incorrectly jump ആകും.

❌ (D) worsest — 'Worsest' ഒരു real English word ഇല്ല! 'Worse' ഒരു irregular comparative ആണ് — 'worser' ഇല്ല, 'worsest' ഇല്ല. Regular comparison suffix '-est' ഇതിന് apply ആകില്ല. ഇത് nonsense option ആണ് — easily eliminate ചെയ്യാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'THAN = COMPARATIVE = WORSE!' — 'than' കണ്ടാൽ → comparative! bad-ന്റെ irregular comparative = 'WORSE' (not 'more bad'!). Worst = superlative = 'the worst'. 'THAN → Comparative → WORSE'.
• Irregular degrees card (PSC top 6):
  good-better-best | bad-worse-worst | many-more-most |
  little-less-least | far-farther-farthest | old-elder-eldest
• PSC exam strategy: 'than' keyword = comparative. 'The ___est/most' = superlative. 'Bad → worse (not worst!)' when 'than' ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ. 'Worst' ഉം 'worsest' ഉം — 'than' ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ eliminate!

questionText: The train _________ when he reached the railway station.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) had left

• ഈ sentence-ൽ രണ്ട് past actions ഉണ്ട്:
  1. Train leave ആകി (earlier action)
  2. He reached the station (later action)
• ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഒരു action-ന് മുൻപ് നടന്ന action = Past Perfect (had + V3)
• 'The train had left when he reached the station' = 'അദ്ദേഹം station-ൽ എത്തുന്നതിന് മുൻപേ train പോയിരുന്നു'.
• 'Reached' = Simple Past. Train-ന്റെ action (leave) 'reached'-ന് BEFORE ആയതിനാൽ → Past Perfect 'had left'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ഭൂതകാലത്ത് two actions → Earlier action = Past Perfect (had + V3) | Later action = Simple Past
• Signal words for Past Perfect: before, after, when, already, by the time, no sooner...than

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Past Perfect tense — 'had + V3' — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദ്യം ആകുന്ന tense!

Sentence structure:
[Earlier action — Past Perfect] + when/before/after + [Later action — Simple Past]
OR
[Later action — Simple Past] + when + [Earlier action — Past Perfect]

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. The students had left before the teacher arrived.
   (Teacher വരുന്നതിന് മുൻപ് students പോയിരുന്നു)
2. She had finished her work when I called her.
   (ഞാൻ call ചെയ്തപ്പോൾ അവൾ work finish ചെയ്തിരുന്നു)
3. By the time we arrived, the movie had started.
   (ഞങ്ങൾ എത്തിയപ്പോഴേക്കും movie start ആയിരുന്നു)
4. He had eaten his dinner before she came home.
   (അവൾ home-ൽ വരുന്നതിന് മുൻപ് അദ്ദേഹം dinner കഴിച്ചിരുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) is leaving — 'Is leaving' = Present Continuous — ഇത് NOW happening action-ന് ആണ്. Sentence-ൽ 'reached' = Simple Past ആണ്; ഒരേ sentence-ൽ Present Continuous + Simple Past = tense conflict. ഇവ past events ആണ്, present tense ഇല്ല.

❌ (B) has left — 'Has left' = Present Perfect — past action-ൽ connection to present ആണ് indicate ചെയ്യുന്നത്. 'Reached' Simple Past ആണ്; present perfect + simple past combination ഈ context-ൽ incorrect. Past Perfect 'had left' ആണ് sequence ഓഫ് past events-ന് ശരി.

❌ (D) will leave — 'Will leave' = Future tense. 'Reached' past ആണ്; future tense ഒരു completed past scenario-ൽ grammatically impossible. Train future-ൽ leave ആകും — 'reached' already complete ആകിയ ഒരു past event-ൽ future tense ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EARLIER HAD, LATER DID!' — Two past actions: EARLIER action → HAD + V3 (Past Perfect) | LATER action → V2 (Simple Past). 'Train LEFT first (earlier) → Train HAD LEFT. He REACHED later → reached (simple past).'
• Timeline image: ←Train left——He reached→ (Past Perfect comes before Simple Past on the timeline)
• PSC exam strategy: 'When he reached' (simple past) ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ — 'reached'-ന് BEFORE-ൽ ആണ് train action → Past Perfect 'had left'. Two past events → Earlier one = had + V3. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct answer:
Christians go to church every Sunday, ______ they?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) don't

• "Christians go to church every Sunday" — ഇത് positive sentence ആണ്, Simple Present tense-ൽ.
• Positive sentence → negative question tag — ഇതാണ് question tag-ന്റെ golden rule.
• "Go" = Simple Present tense → auxiliary verb "do" → negative form "don't"
• "Christians" = third person plural → tag-ൽ pronoun "they" വേണം.
• Final tag: "don't they?" — "Christians ഓരോ Sunday-ഉം church-ൽ പോകുന്നുണ്ടല്ലോ, അല്ലേ?" എന്ന് ചോദിക്കുന്ന form ആണ് ഇത്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive sentence → Negative tag | Negative sentence → Positive tag
• Simple Present tense + third person plural → auxiliary "do" → tag "don't they?"
• Tag formula: [auxiliary + n't] + [pronoun]?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags ഓരോ paper-ലും guaranteed topic!
• Subject → Pronoun mapping: Christians → they | He → he | She → she | I → I
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They play cricket every day, don't they? (positive → don't they?)
  2. She goes to school, doesn't she? (singular → doesn't she?)
  3. We work hard, don't we? (we → don't we?)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) didn't
• "Didn't" = Simple Past negative — sentence-ൽ "last Sunday" അല്ല, "every Sunday" ആണ് — present habit describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Simple Present sentence-ന് Past tense tag match ആകില്ല — tense mismatch!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "didn't" familiar ആണ്, students automatically select ചെയ്യും — tense match ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യുക!

❌ (C) won't
• "Won't" = will + not → future tense tag — sentence present tense ആണ്.
• Future tag future sentence-നാണ് — "She will come, won't she?" ഇതുപോലെ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "every Sunday" future-ൽ ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകും — "every Sunday" = present habit, not future!

❌ (D) shouldn't
• "Shouldn't" = should + not → modal verb tag — sentence-ൽ modal verb ഇല്ല.
• Tag-ൽ main sentence-ൻ്റെ auxiliary verb match ആകണം — "should" ഇവിടെ match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sunday church duty "should" ആണെന്ന് morally confuse ആകും — grammar-ൽ "should" ഇല്ല, "do" ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "POSITIVE → NEGATIVE" — see-saw ആണ് question tag! Positive sentence ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ tag negative, negative ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ tag positive. ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Simple Present tag family: go/goes/play/works കണ്ടാൽ → don't/doesn't they/he/she?
• PSC exam strategy: ആദ്യം auxiliary verb identify ചെയ്യുക → negative form → correct pronoun. 3 steps, 3 seconds, guaranteed mark!

questionText: My brother is interested _____ reading science fictions.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) in

• "Interested in" — ഇത് ഒരു fixed adjective-preposition collocation ആണ്, "ഒരു കാര്യത്തിൽ താൽപ്പര്യം ഉണ്ട്" എന്ന് express ചെയ്യുന്നതിന്.
• "My brother is interested in reading science fictions" — "എന്റെ ജ്യേഷ്ഠൻ science fiction വായിക്കുന്നതിൽ interest ഉണ്ട്" എന്നാണ് meaning.
• 'Interested' adjective-ന് ശേഷം 'in' മാത്രം — ഇത് English-ൽ fixed rule ആണ്, മാറ്റം ഇല്ല.
• PSC-ൽ "interested in", "good at", "afraid of" ഇത്തരം adjective+preposition collocations ആവർത്തിച്ച് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Interested + IN = always! ഒരിക്കലും other prepositions ഇടരുത്.
• Adjective + Preposition fixed collocations — ഇവ memorize ചെയ്യണം, rules derive ചെയ്യാനാകില്ല:
  - Interested IN | Good AT | Afraid OF | Fond OF | Proud OF
  - Angry WITH (person) / AT (thing) | Pleased WITH | Keen ON
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective+Preposition collocations ഓരോ paper-ലും frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is interested in painting. (painting-ൽ interest ഉണ്ട്)
  2. He is good at mathematics. (maths-ൽ talent ഉണ്ട്)
  3. They are proud of their achievements. (achievements-ൽ അഭിമാനം ഉണ്ട്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) on
• 'On' = surface/topic preposition — "interested on" English-ൽ exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• "On" commonly topic indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു: "a lecture on science" — പക്ഷേ "interested" adjective-നൊപ്പം "on" ഒരിക്കലും ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "On" most common preposition ആണ്, students familiar ആയതിനാൽ select ചെയ്യും!

❌ (B) at
• 'At' = location or specific point — "good at" collocation ഉണ്ട്, പക്ഷേ "interested at" ഇല്ല.
• "At" ഉള്ള collocations: good at, bad at, surprised at — ഇവ different adjectives ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "good at" familiar ആണ്, "interested at" ആണ് correct ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും — "at" ≠ "in" ഇവിടെ!

❌ (C) from
• 'From' = origin or source — "interested from" English-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും ഇല്ല.
• "From" ഉള്ള collocations: different from, absent from, free from — entirely different context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: interest-ന്റെ "source" from ആണെന്ന് students wrong ആയി think ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "I am INterested" — "IN" is already hiding inside the word "INterested"! 'INTER-ested' → 'IN'-terested → always uses 'IN'! ഈ trick ഓർത്താൽ ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Collocation family: interested IN, good AT, afraid OF, fond OF, proud OF — 5 most common PSC adjective+preposition pairs.
• PSC exam strategy: Adjective+Preposition questions-ൽ adjective identify ചെയ്യുക → memorized collocation recall ചെയ്യുക. "Interested" = always "in"!

questionText: I have been living in this house _____ the last ten years.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) for

• "For" = duration of time — "ഒരു കാലയളവ്" indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു ("the last ten years" = 10 years duration).
• "I have been living in this house for the last ten years" — "ഞാൻ ഈ വീട്ടിൽ കഴിഞ്ഞ പത്ത് വർഷമായി താമസിക്കുന്നു" — period of time = "for".
• "The last ten years" = a span/duration of time → "for" is the only correct preposition here.
• ⭐ PSC-ൽ "for vs since" combination most commonly asked Preposition topic!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: FOR = duration (period of time) | SINCE = point in time (specific moment)
• FOR ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്: number + time unit (for 10 years, for 3 hours, for a month)
• SINCE ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്: specific point in time (since 2015, since Monday, since morning)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: for/since distinction PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന preposition question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I have worked here for five years. (five years = duration → for)
  2. She has lived here since 2018. (2018 = point in time → since)
  3. He has been waiting for three hours. (three hours = duration → for)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) since
• "Since" = point in time — "since 2015", "since Monday", "since morning" ഇതുപോലെ specific moment indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• "The last ten years" = duration (period), specific point in time അല്ല → "since" match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "last ten years" കേൾക്കുമ്പോൾ "since" use ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് students confuse ആകും — "since ten years" wrong, "for ten years" correct!

❌ (B) from
• "From" = starting point of journey/time — "from 9 to 5", "from here to there" ഇതുപോലെ.
• Perfect tense-ൽ "from" ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — "have been living from" grammatically incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "from last ten years" spoken English-ൽ കേൾക്കാം, പക്ഷേ standard grammar-ൽ "for" ആണ് correct.

❌ (D) to
• "To" = direction or destination — "I am going to school", "from here to there" ഇതുപോലെ.
• Duration express ചെയ്യാൻ "to" ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "10 to 20 years" range sense-ൽ confuse ആകും — ഇവിടെ range അല്ല, single duration ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "FOR = FOUR walls = PERIOD of time" — FOR has 3 letters, period has length. SINCE = Single point in time! FOR/SINCE test: number + years/months/days → FOR | specific year/day/morning → SINCE.
• Formula: 'for two hours' ✓ | 'for 2015' ✗ | 'since morning' ✓ | 'since three hours' ✗
• PSC exam strategy: Time expression കണ്ടാൽ check ചെയ്യുക — number + unit ആണോ? → FOR. Specific moment ആണോ? → SINCE. 2 seconds-ൽ answer!

questionText: If you study well, you ______ pass the examination.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) will

• "If you study well, you will pass the examination" — ഇത് Type 1 Conditional (Real Future Conditional) ആണ്.
• "നിങ്ങൾ നന്നായി പഠിച്ചാൽ, പരീക്ഷ pass ആകും" — real, possible future situation describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Type 1 Conditional formula: If + Simple Present → will + base verb
• If clause-ൽ "study" (Simple Present) ഉള്ളതിനാൽ main clause-ൽ "will pass" ആണ് correct.
• PSC students-നു direct message: "If you study well, you WILL pass!" — ഈ sentence ഓർത്താൽ rule ഓർക്കും!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + Present Simple → will + V1 (Type 1 — Real Future Conditional)
• Type 1 Conditional = real possibility in the future — happen ആകാൻ possibility ഉണ്ട്.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditionals — especially Type 1 and Type 3 — PSC-ൽ frequent topic!
• Three conditional types:
  - Type 1 (Real): If + Present → will + V1 (possible future)
  - Type 2 (Imaginary): If + Past → would + V1 (imaginary present)
  - Type 3 (Past Unreal): If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (past regret)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If it rains, we will cancel the trip. (possible future → will)
  2. If she works hard, she will succeed. (real possibility → will)
  3. If you call him, he will answer. (real future → will)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) would
• "Would" = Type 2 Conditional main clause — "If I were rich, I would travel" ഇതുപോലെ imaginary/hypothetical situations-നാണ്.
• ഇവിടെ if clause-ൽ "study" (Simple Present) ആണ്, past tense അല്ല → Type 2 match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "would" polite/formal-ആയി തോന്നും — conditional type identify ചെയ്യണം ആദ്യം!

❌ (C) will have
• "Will have" = Future Perfect — "By tomorrow, I will have finished" ഇതുപോലെ future-ൽ complete ആകുന്ന action-നാണ്.
• Simple conditional result-നു Future Perfect വരില്ല — formula match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "will" ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് correct ആണോ എന്ന് students confuse ആകും — "will have" ≠ "will"!

❌ (D) would have
• "Would have" = Type 3 Conditional main clause — past unreal situations-നാണ്.
• ഇവിടെ if clause-ൽ past perfect ഇല്ല — "would have" ഒരിക്കലും match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "would have" complete ആയി തോന്നും — exam anxiety-ൽ students ഇത് select ചെയ്യും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "IF present → WILL future" — If clause-ൽ present tense കണ്ടാൽ main clause-ൽ "will" automatic! Present → Will | Past → Would | Had+V3 → Would Have. ഈ 3 pair ഓർക്കുക!
• Conditional family: Type 1 (will) | Type 2 (would) | Type 3 (would have) — easily distinguish by if-clause tense.
• PSC exam strategy: If clause-ൽ tense check ചെയ്യുക → conditional type identify → formula apply. "If + study (present)" → "will pass". 3 second rule!

questionText: One of the boys _____ absent today.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) is

• "One of the boys" — ഇതിൽ subject ആരാണ്? "One" ആണ്, "boys" അല്ല!
• "One" = singular → singular verb "is" വേണം.
• "Of the boys" = prepositional phrase, ഇത് subject-ൽ include ആകില്ല — ഇത് ഒരു PSC common trap!
• "One of the boys is absent today" — "ആൺകുട്ടികളിൽ ഒരാൾ ഇന്ന് absent ആണ്" — ഒരു person singular ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "One of the + plural noun" → SINGULAR verb (One controls the verb!)
• Subject-Verb Agreement rule: verb always agrees with the TRUE subject, not the nearest noun.
• "Of the boys" = prepositional phrase — ignore it when identifying subject!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "One of the..." pattern PSC-ൽ ഓരോ exam-ലും guaranteed!
• Similar patterns — always singular:
  - Each of the students IS present
  - Every one of them HAS a book
  - One of my friends IS a doctor
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. One of the teachers is absent. (One = singular → is)
  2. Each of the boys has a pen. (Each = singular → has)
  3. One of my friends is a doctor. (One = singular → is)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) are
• "Are" = plural verb — "boys" plural ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് "are" ആണ് correct ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും.
• പക്ഷേ subject "boys" അല്ല, "One" ആണ് — One = singular → "is" വേണം, "are" അല്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: nearest plural noun "boys" കണ്ടാൽ "are" select ചെയ്യും — TRUE subject find ചെയ്യണം!

❌ (B) were
• "Were" = plural past tense — subject "One" singular ആണ്, "today" present context ആണ്.
• Double mistake: subject mismatch (plural were vs singular one) + tense mismatch (past vs present).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "boys were" pattern familiar ആണ്, students "were" select ചെയ്യും — subject + tense check ചെയ്യുക!

❌ (C) have
• "Have" = plural present auxiliary — "boys have" ഉപയോഗിക്കാം, പക്ഷേ "One has" ആണ് singular form.
• "Absent today" = simple state, present perfect auxiliary ഇവിടെ fit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "have" commonly used verb ആണ്, students randomly select ചെയ്യും — subject number ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യുക!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ONE is always ONE!" — "One of the..." pattern കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ SINGULAR verb! "One = 1 person = singular = is/was/has." ഒരു person-നെ imagine ചെയ്യുക — അദ്ദേഹം "is" absent!
• Similar singular triggers: One of | Each of | Every one of | None of (+ plural noun) → always singular verb!
• PSC exam strategy: "One of the + plural noun + ___" pattern കണ്ടാൽ answer ഉടനെ singular form — is/was/has. 1 second answer!

questionText: I _____ Mumbai last year.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) visited

• "Last year" — ഇത് Simple Past tense-ന്റെ ഏറ്റവും clear signal word ആണ്. ഇത് കണ്ടാൽ Simple Past form ഉടനെ select ചെയ്യണം.
• "I visited Mumbai last year" — "ഞാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞ വർഷം Mumbai-ൽ പോയി" — completed past action.
• 'Visited' = 'visit'-ന്റെ Simple Past form (regular verb: visit → visited → visited).
• Completed action in the past + specific past time signal = Simple Past — ഇത് basic rule, ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കരുത്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: LAST (year/week/month/night) + AGO → Simple Past tense! Automatic!
• Simple Past tense: Subject + V2 (past form of verb)
• Signal words for Simple Past: yesterday, last year/week/month, ago, in 2023, then, once
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Tense questions ഓരോ PSC paper-ലും guaranteed — signal words identify ചെയ്യുക!
• Present Perfect vs Simple Past:
  - Simple Past: specific past time signal ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ (last year, yesterday, ago)
  - Present Perfect: no specific time / result matters now (I have visited = experience)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She visited Paris last year. (last year = Simple Past)
  2. He called me yesterday. (yesterday = Simple Past)
  3. They left two hours ago. (ago = Simple Past)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) have visited
• 'Have visited' = Present Perfect tense — specific past time signal ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ Present Perfect ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• "Last year" = specific past time → Simple Past only! Present Perfect-ൽ "last year" ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "have visited" complete experience-ആയി തോന്നും — "last year" signal word ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യുക!

❌ (C) have been visited
• 'Have been visited' = Present Perfect Passive — "I" passive subject ആകുന്നതിന് ഇവിടെ reason ഇല്ല.
• "I visited" = active (ഞാൻ visit ചെയ്തു). "I was visited" = passive (ഞാൻ visit ചെയ്യപ്പെട്ടു) — meaning completely different!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Complex-ആയ structure correct ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും — simple context = simple tense!

❌ (D) had visited
• 'Had visited' = Past Perfect tense — ഭൂതകാലത്ത് മറ്റൊരു past action-നു മുമ്പ് complete ആയ action-നാണ്.
• "By the time he arrived, I had visited the museum" — TWO past actions ഉള്ളപ്പോഴാണ് Past Perfect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "had" formal-ആയി തോന്നും — single past event + "last year" = Simple Past മതി!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LAST = PAST" — Last year, last week, last night കണ്ടാൽ Simple Past form ഉടനെ! ഈ rhyme ഓർക്കുക: "Last ആണോ? Past തന്നെ!" — exam hall-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Present Perfect vs Simple Past test: Specific time word ഉണ്ടോ? (last year/yesterday/ago) → Simple Past. Time word ഇല്ലേ? → Present Perfect possible.
• PSC exam strategy: Time signal word ആദ്യം identify ചെയ്യുക → tense decide ചെയ്യുക → answer choose ചെയ്യുക. "Last year" = Simple Past, 2 seconds!

questionText: I _____ my new dress on my birthday.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) put on

• "Put on" = "wear ചെയ്യുക, ധരിക്കുക" — clothes, shoes, glasses തുടങ്ങിയവ body-ൽ ഇടുന്നതിന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന phrasal verb.
• "I put on my new dress on my birthday" — "ഞാൻ എന്റെ birthday-ൽ എന്റെ പുതിയ dress ധരിച്ചു" — perfect meaning match!
• "Dress" = clothing → ധരിക്കുക concept → "put on" exact fit.
• ⭐ PSC favourite phrasal verb — "put" family PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequently asked!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: PUT ON = wear / dress yourself (clothes, shoes, glasses ധരിക്കുക)
• "Put" phrasal verb complete family:
  - Put on = wear / increase (dress ധരിക്കുക / weight കൂടുക)
  - Put off = postpone (delay ചെയ്യുക / നീട്ടിവെക്കുക)
  - Put up = erect / accommodate (നിർമ്മിക്കുക / താമസിക്കാൻ allow ചെയ്യുക)
  - Put up with = tolerate (സഹിക്കുക, tolerate ചെയ്യുക)
  - Put out = extinguish (കെടുത്തുക)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs ഓരോ paper-ലും 1-2 questions — "put" family most tested!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She put on her shoes before leaving. (shoes ധരിച്ചു)
  2. He put off the meeting to next week. (meeting postpone ചെയ്തു)
  3. I can't put up with this noise. (noise സഹിക്കാൻ കഴിയുന്നില്ല)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) put up
• 'Put up' = "erect ചെയ്യുക / accommodate ചെയ്യുക" — "put up a tent" (കൂടാരം കെട്ടുക) / "put up a guest" (ആതിഥ്യം നൽകുക).
• Dress ധരിക്കുക = "put up" ഒരിക്കലും ആകില്ല — context match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "put up" ഏറ്റവും commonly heard ആണ്, students familiar ആയതിനാൽ select ചെയ്യും!

❌ (B) put off
• 'Put off' = "postpone ചെയ്യുക, delay ചെയ്യുക" — "put off the meeting" (meeting നീട്ടിവെക്കുക).
• Dress ധരിക്കുന്നതിൽ postpone concept ഒട്ടും relevant ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "put off" common usage ആണ്, students meaning ഓർക്കാതെ select ചെയ്യും!

❌ (C) put up with
• 'Put up with' = "tolerate ചെയ്യുക, സഹിക്കുക" — "I can't put up with his rudeness" (അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ മോശം behaviour സഹിക്കാൻ കഴിയുന്നില്ല).
• Dress-ൽ tolerate concept ഇല്ല — completely wrong context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 3-word phrasal verb impressive-ആയി തോന്നും — meaning check ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "PUT ON = PUT clothes ON your body!" — Dress, shoes, glasses, coat — ഒക്കെ body-ൽ ON ആക്കുകയാണ്! "PUT + ON = wear" — ഈ image ഓർക്കുക: ദേഹത്ത് ON ചെയ്യുന്നത് = PUT ON!
• PUT family: ON (wear) | OFF (postpone) | UP (erect/host) | UP WITH (tolerate) | OUT (extinguish) — 5 meanings, 5 different contexts!
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb questions-ൽ sentence context/meaning ആദ്യം understand ചെയ്യുക. "New dress / birthday" → wear → put on. Context = key!

questionText: The antonym for 'amateur' is ______.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) professional

• 'Amateur' = "ഒരു കാര്യം hobby ആയി ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ, trained അല്ലാത്ത, unpaid" — professionally trained അല്ലാതെ passion-ൽ ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• 'Professional' = "professionally trained, paid, expert" — ഇത് 'amateur'-ന്റെ exact opposite!
• "Amateur photographer" = hobby-ആയി photo എടുക്കുന്ന ആൾ → "Professional photographer" = paid expert.
• Latin root: 'amator' = lover (one who loves/does for love) → antonym = one who does for pay/profession.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: AMATEUR (unpaid/untrained) ↔ PROFESSIONAL (paid/trained/expert)
• 'Amateur' synonyms: novice, beginner, layperson, hobbyist — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'professional' antonym ഉള്ളവ.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms topic ഓരോ PSC paper-ലും 1-2 questions guaranteed!
• Word formation:
  - amateur (noun/adj) → amateurish (adj) → amateurishly (adv)
  - professional (noun/adj) → professionally (adv) → professionalism (noun)
• Related antonym pairs:
  - Amateur ↔ Professional
  - Novice ↔ Expert
  - Beginner ↔ Veteran
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He is an amateur boxer. (hobby-ആയി boxing ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  2. She is a professional singer. (paid expert singer)
  3. The amateur team lost to the professionals. (trained experts)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) strange
• 'Strange' = "അപരിചിതമായ, unusual, weird" — unfamiliar or unexpected things describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• 'Amateur'-ന്റെ antonym ഇതല്ല — completely different meanings.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both are adjectives ആണ്, students "different-sounding word" ആണ് antonym ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും!

❌ (C) indifferent
• 'Indifferent' = "ഉദാസീനം, not caring, lacking interest" — neither good nor bad, unconcerned.
• 'Amateur' = passionate about something; 'indifferent' = unconcerned — ഇവ antonyms അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "amateur-ന്റെ opposite = indifferent (uncaring)" ആണെന്ന് logic confuse ആകും!

❌ (D) honest
• 'Honest' = "സത്യസന്ധൻ, truthful, not cheating" — character trait describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• Skill/expertise level-ഉം honesty-ഉം completely unrelated concepts — ഒരിക്കലും antonym ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Professional people are honest" → "honest is the opposite" — ഈ reasoning completely wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "AMATEUR = LOVE, PROFESSIONAL = LIVE" — Amateur does it for LOVE (hobby), Professional does it to LIVE (paid job)! Latin 'amator' = lover → does it for love, not money. Opposite = professional!
• Word family: amateur, novice, beginner → professional, expert, veteran — ഈ two groups ഒരുമിച്ച് learn ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym questions-ൽ ആദ്യം question word-ന്റെ exact meaning കണ്ടുപിടിക്കുക → opposite meaning choose ചെയ്യുക. Amateur = unpaid hobbyist → opposite = paid professional!

questionText: He has been elected to the ______.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) council

• 'Council' = "ഒരു governing body / committee" — municipal council, student council, UN Security Council ഇതുപോലെ ഒരു group of people who make decisions.
• "He has been elected to the council" — "അദ്ദേഹം council-ലേക്ക് elect ചെയ്യപ്പെട്ടു" — ഒരു body of representatives-ലേക്ക് elect ചെയ്യപ്പെടുന്നത് describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Elections → governing body → 'council' — ഇതാണ് correct word.
• ⭐ Council vs Counsel — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും commonly asked "words often confused" pair!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: COUNCIL = Governing body (group) | COUNSEL = Advice / Lawyer
• COUNCIL (noun only):
  - Municipal Council (നഗരസഭ)
  - Student Council (വിദ്യാർഥി സഭ)
  - UN Security Council
  - Panchayat Council
• COUNSEL (noun + verb):
  - Noun: advice, guidance / a lawyer ("King's Counsel")
  - Verb: to advise someone ("She counselled him to be patient")
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Words Often Confused pair PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The council decided to build a new park. (governing body → council)
  2. She sought counsel from her lawyer. (advice/lawyer → counsel)
  3. He was elected to the district council. (elected body → council)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) counsel
• 'Counsel' = "ഉപദേശം, advice" or "lawyer" — "legal counsel" (lawyer), "counsel of wisdom" (ഉപദേശം).
• "Elected to the counsel" — ഒരാളെ ഉപദേശത്തിലേക്ക് elect ചെയ്യുക? Meaning ഇല്ല! Council ആണ് elected body.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: council/counsel pronunciation ഒരേ പോലെ! Sound same, meaning different — classic PSC trap!

❌ (C) counsell
• 'Counsell' = misspelling — ഈ spelling English-ൽ exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• Correct spelling: counsel (ഒറ്റ 'l') — double 'l' wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar spelling options confuse ചെയ്യും — correct spelling ഓർക്കുക: coun-sel (ഒറ്റ l).

❌ (D) councill
• 'Councill' = misspelling — ഈ spelling English-ൽ exist ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• Correct spelling: council (ഒറ്റ 'l') — double 'l' wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Misspelled options ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തി confuse ചെയ്യുന്ന PSC strategy — always correct spelling memorize ചെയ്യുക!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "COUNCIL has CIL = Committee of Individuals for Legislation" — Council = committee/group! COUNSEL = COUNSEL-lor gives advice. ഇനി ഒരു shortcut: COUNCIL → elect ചെയ്യാം (members) | COUNSEL → seek ചെയ്യാം (advice)!
• Spelling tip: council / counsel — both end differently: council (ends in -cil) | counsel (ends in -sel). CIL = Committee of Important Leaders!
• PSC exam strategy: Context check ചെയ്യുക — "elected to" / "member of" → council. "legal" / "advice" / "lawyer" → counsel. Context = answer!

questionText: The synonym for 'amiable' is _____.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) friendly

• 'Amiable' = "സൗഹൃദമനോഭാവമുള്ള, pleasant and friendly" — others-നൊടൊക്കെ warm ആയി interact ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• 'Friendly' = "സൗഹൃദപൂർണ്ണം, warm and likeable" — 'amiable'-ന്റെ closest synonym!
• "She has an amiable personality" = "She has a friendly personality" — same meaning!
• French root: 'amiable' comes from Latin 'amicabilis' (amicus = friend) → friendly, lovable.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: AMIABLE = FRIENDLY = warm, pleasant, easy to like
• Synonyms of amiable: friendly, pleasant, likeable, affable, genial, warm, good-natured
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms topic ഓരോ PSC paper-ലും 1-2 questions!
• Etymology: Latin 'amicus' = friend → amiable (friendly) | amicable (peaceful/agreeable)
• Antonym: amiable ↔ unfriendly, hostile, disagreeable
• Word family:
  - amiable (adj) → amiably (adv) → amiability (noun)
  - Similar root words: amicable (friendly agreement), amity (friendship)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The amiable host greeted everyone warmly. (friendly host)
  2. She has a very amiable nature. (pleasant, likeable nature)
  3. His amiable smile made everyone comfortable. (warm, friendly smile)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) lively
• 'Lively' = "ഉത്സാഹമുള്ള, energetic, full of life" — active and enthusiastic describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• Amiable = friendly; Lively = energetic — related positive traits ആണ്, synonyms അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both are positive personality traits → students confuse ചെയ്യും. Friendly ≠ Energetic!

❌ (B) vigilant
• 'Vigilant' = "ജാഗ്രതയുള്ള, watchful, alert for danger" — caution describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• Amiable = friendly/pleasant; Vigilant = watchful/careful — completely different concepts.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Uncommon word → students meaning അറിയാതെ select ചെയ്യും. Vigilant = vigil (night watch) = watchful!

❌ (C) influential
• 'Influential' = "സ്വാധീനം ഉള്ള, having power to affect others" — power/impact describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• Amiable = pleasant/friendly; Influential = powerful — completely different qualities.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Popular people are often both amiable AND influential → students confuse the two!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "AMIABLE = AMI = FRIEND in French!" — 'Ami' means friend in French (Mon Ami = My Friend). So AMIABLE = FRIENDly! Root: Latin 'amicus' (friend) → amiable, amicable, amity — all FRIEND family!
• Word family: amiable (friendly person) | amicable (friendly agreement/settlement) | amity (friendship between nations) — ഒരു root, 3 important words!
• PSC exam strategy: Vocabulary questions-ൽ root/etymology hint use ചെയ്യുക. 'AMI' = friend → amiable = friendly. Root ഓർത്താൽ synonym automatic!

questionText:
Identify the error in the following sentence.
One of the girls are from mumbai.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) are

• ഈ sentence-ൽ error ആയ word 'are' ആണ് — 'is' ആകണം.
• "One of the girls" — ഇതിൽ subject 'One' ആണ്, 'girls' അല്ല. 'One' = singular → singular verb 'is' വേണം.
• "Of the girls" = prepositional phrase ആണ് — ഇത് subject-ൽ include ആകില്ല.
• Correct sentence: "One of the girls **is** from Mumbai." — "Girls-ൽ ഒരാൾ Mumbai-ൽ നിന്നാണ്" — ഒരു person, singular!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: "One of the + plural noun" → SINGULAR verb (is/was/has) — always!
• Subject-Verb Agreement: Verb always agrees with TRUE subject, not the nearest noun.
• "Of the girls" = prepositional phrase — ignore when finding subject. Subject = 'One' (singular).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: "One of the..." pattern PSC-ൽ ഓരോ exam-ലും guaranteed — highest frequency pattern!
• Similar singular patterns:
  - Each of the students IS present.
  - Every one of them HAS a book.
  - None of the water IS clean.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. One of the teachers **is** absent. (One = singular → is)
  2. Each of the boys **has** a pen. (Each = singular → has)
  3. One of my friends **is** a doctor. (One = singular → is)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) girls
• 'Girls' = correct plural noun — sentence-ൽ 'girls' ശരിയായ word ആണ്, error അല്ല.
• "One of the girls" — 'girls' plural ആണ്, ഇത് prepositional phrase-ൽ correct ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'girls' plural ആണ് → 'are' ആണ് correct ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും — subject 'One' ആണ്!

❌ (C) the
• 'The' = definite article — sentence-ൽ 'the' ശരിയായ article ആണ്, error ഇല്ല.
• "One of **the** girls" — specific girls refer ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് 'the' correct ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: article errors PSC-ൽ ചോദിക്കാറുണ്ട്, students 'the' suspect ചെയ്യും — ഇവിടെ 'the' correct!

❌ (D) from
• 'From' = correct preposition — "from Mumbai" correct usage ആണ്.
• 'From' origin/place indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്, ഇവിടെ perfect fit.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: preposition errors confuse ചെയ്യാൻ 'from' option ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിരിക്കുന്നു — 'from' correct, 'are' error!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ONE is always ONE!" — 'One of the...' കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ SINGULAR verb! ഒരു person = 'is'. Many girls, but ONE of them — that ONE is singular!
• Error identification strategy: Subject find ചെയ്യുക → verb agree ചെയ്യുന്നോ check ചെയ്യുക → mismatch = error.
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence correction/error questions-ൽ ആദ്യം subject identify ചെയ്യുക. "One of the..." pattern = automatic singular verb. 'are' കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ suspect ചെയ്യുക!

questionText:
Complete the sentence using the appropriate tag.
don't come in front of me, ______ ?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Will you

• "Don't come in front of me" — ഇത് negative imperative sentence ആണ് (command/order).
• Imperative sentences-ൽ (commands/requests) question tag ആയി "will you?" ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Negative imperative (Don't...) → tag = "will you?"
• "Don't come in front of me, will you?" — "എന്റെ മുന്നിൽ വരരുത്, ശരിയോ?" — polite insistence express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Imperative (command/request) → tag = "will you?"
• Imperative tag rules:
  - Positive imperative: "Come here, will you?" / "won't you?" (stronger)
  - Negative imperative: "Don't do that, will you?"
  - Let's (suggestion): "Let's go, shall we?"
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative question tags PSC-ൽ frequently asked — special case, separately memorize!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Open the door, will you? (positive imperative → will you?)
  2. Don't be late, will you? (negative imperative → will you?)
  3. Let's watch a movie, shall we? (let's → shall we?)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Can you
• 'Can you' = ability question — "Can you swim?" ഇതുപോലെ ability/permission ask ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാണ്.
• Imperative sentence-ൽ 'can you' tag ആകില്ല — formal request-ലല്ലാതെ ഇത് question tag ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "can you come?" natural-ആയി തോന്നും — imperative tag = will you, not can you!

❌ (B) Shall you
• 'Shall you' = incorrect English — "shall" second person-നൊപ്പം ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Shall' → "shall we" (Let's suggestions), "shall I" (offers) — ഇവ ഉണ്ട്, പക്ഷേ "shall you" ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "Let's go, shall we?" familiar ആണ്, students "shall" ആണ് tag ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും!

❌ (C) Would you
• 'Would you' = polite request form — "Would you like some tea?" ഇതുപോലെ.
• Imperative question tag ആയി 'would you' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — standard imperative tag = will you.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'would' polite-ആണ്, 'don't come' polite request-ആണ് → 'would you' ആണ് correct ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "COMMANDS = WILL YOU?" — Imperative (command/don't do) → always "will you?" tag! Will = willing to comply. "Don't do this, will you?" = "Please be willing not to do this!"
• Imperative tag family: Come here, will you? | Don't shout, will you? | Let's go, shall we? — 3 patterns ഓർക്കുക!
• PSC exam strategy: Command sentence (verb with/without 'don't') → "will you?" automatic. "Let's" → "shall we?" automatic. 2 patterns = covers 99% PSC questions!

questionText:
Identify the type of the verb in italics in the following sentence.
you let her __go__

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Bare infinitive

• "You let her **go**" — ഇവിടെ 'go' = Bare infinitive (to-ഇല്ലാത്ത infinitive).
• 'Let' = causative verb — causative verb-ന് ശേഷം object + bare infinitive (without 'to') വരുന്നു.
• "Let her go" = "അവളെ പോകാൻ allow ചെയ്യുക" — 'to go' ആകില്ല, 'go' (bare) ആണ്.
• Causative verbs: let, make, have + object + bare infinitive — ഇത് fixed rule ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: let/make/have + object + BARE infinitive (no 'to'!)
• Bare infinitive = verb-ന്റെ base form, 'to' ഇല്ലാതെ
• To infinitive = to + base form (to go, to eat)
• Causative verbs that take bare infinitive:
  - Let + object + V1: Let him speak.
  - Make + object + V1: She made him cry.
  - Have + object + V1: He had the mechanic fix it.
• Modal verbs ഉള്ളിടത്തും bare infinitive: can go, will come, must stay
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Bare infinitive vs To infinitive distinction PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Let me **go**. (let + object + bare infinitive)
  2. She made him **cry**. (make + object + bare infinitive)
  3. I want **to go**. (want + to infinitive — different!)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Gerund
• Gerund = verb + ing form used as a noun — "Swimming is fun", "I enjoy reading"
• 'Go' ഇവിടെ '-ing' form അല്ല, verb base form ആണ് — gerund ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'go' after object ആയതുകൊണ്ട് gerund ആണോ എന്ന് confuse ആകും — ing-form ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ gerund ഇല്ല!

❌ (C) To infinitive
• To infinitive = 'to' + base form — "to go", "to eat", "to speak"
• ഇവിടെ 'to' ഇല്ല — "let her **go**" (bare), "let her **to go**" (wrong!).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: go = infinitive ആണ് correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'to' ഉണ്ടോ ഇല്ലയോ — 'to' ഇല്ല = bare infinitive!

❌ (D) Present participle
• Present participle = verb + ing — going, eating, speaking
• 'Go' = base form, '-ing' form അല്ല → present participle ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: "going" vs "go" — '-ing' form ആണ് present participle, base form ആണ് bare infinitive!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LET GO!" — 'Let' കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ 'go' (bare infinitive)! 'Let him go' — 'to' ഇടരുത്! "Let BARE go — no TO needed!" Causative let/make/have = always bare infinitive.
• Comparison: BARE = naked (no 'to') | TO infinitive = dressed (with 'to') | GERUND = -ing party!
• PSC exam strategy: Causative verb (let/make/have) + object + V1 = bare infinitive. Modal + V1 = bare infinitive. 'To' ഉണ്ടോ ഇല്ലയോ check ചെയ്യുക → answer decide ചെയ്യുക!

questionText:
Correct the following sentence by choosing the right option.
Neither the Principal nor the teachers is going for the tour.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Are going

• "Neither the Principal **nor** the teachers" — ഇത് 'neither...nor' correlative conjunction ആണ്.
• 'Neither...nor' rule: verb agrees with the subject NEAREST to it (the last subject).
• Last subject = "the teachers" = plural → plural verb "are going" വേണം.
• Correct sentence: "Neither the Principal nor the teachers **are going** for the tour." ✓
• "Principal-ഉം teachers-ഉം tour-നു പോകുന്നില്ല" — plural subject + plural verb!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Neither...nor / Either...or → verb agrees with NEAREST (last) subject!
• This is called the PROXIMITY RULE — verb hugs the nearest subject.
• Pattern examples:
  - Neither Ram nor **his friends** ARE coming. (friends = plural → are)
  - Either the students or **the teacher** IS present. (teacher = singular → is)
  - Neither **she** nor I AM ready. (I = special → am)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Neither...nor / Either...or agreement PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു — Proximity Rule ഓർക്കുക!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Neither the manager nor the staff **are** happy. (staff = plural → are)
  2. Either she or **he** **is** responsible. (he = singular → is)
  3. Neither John nor **his parents** **are** here. (parents = plural → are)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Will going
• 'Will going' = grammatically incorrect form — 'will' modal-ന് ശേഷം base verb വേണം, '-ing' form അല്ല.
• Correct: 'will go' (not 'will going') — modal + base verb rule.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Progressive form confuse ആക്കി 'will going' ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിരിക്കുന്നു — modal verb-ന് ശേഷം -ing form ഇല്ല!

❌ (B) Goes
• 'Goes' = Simple Present singular — "She goes to school" ഇതുപോലെ.
• Nearest subject 'teachers' = plural → singular 'goes' match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Principal' singular ആണ്, students 'goes' select ചെയ്യും — nearest subject 'teachers' (plural) ആണ் verb control ചെയ്യുന്നത്!

❌ (D) Has gone
• 'Has gone' = Present Perfect singular — 'teachers' plural-ന് 'have gone' ആകണം, 'has gone' അല്ല.
• Perfect tense ഇവിടെ context-ൽ fit ആകില്ല — "going for tour" = ongoing plan, not completed action.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has gone' complete-ആയി തോന്നും — context + number both check ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NOR/OR = NEAREST NEIGHBOR!" — neither...nor / either...or-ൽ verb nearest subject-നോടൊപ്പം ചേരുന്നു. Verb = nearest subject-ന്റെ neighbour! Last subject = verb's best friend!
• Proximity Rule: Neither A nor **B** → B controls verb | Either A or **B** → B controls verb
• PSC exam strategy: Neither...nor / Either...or sentence കണ്ടാൽ last subject-ന്റെ number check ചെയ്യുക → verb agree ചെയ്യിക്കുക. Last subject plural → plural verb. 5 second rule!

questionText: Seeing a snake in the garden lalu screamed for help. This sentence may be transformed as
optionA: lalu saw a snake in the garden when he screamed for help
optionB: He screamed for help and saw a snake
optionC: lalu was screaming for help when seeing a snake in the garden
optionD: When he saw a snake in the garden lalu screamed for help

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) When he saw a snake in the garden lalu screamed for help

• "Seeing a snake in the garden" = participial phrase — ഇതിന്റെ meaning "when he saw a snake in the garden" ആണ്.
• Participial phrase (Seeing...) = time/cause relationship → transformation-ൽ 'when' + subject + past tense ആകണം.
• Original: Seeing (=when he saw) → Transformed: "When he saw a snake in the garden"
• Meaning, tense, logical sequence ഒക്കെ correctly preserved ആണ് option D-ൽ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Participial phrase (V+ing at start) → transform to 'When + subject + past tense'
• Participial phrase = shortened clause — full form-ൽ subject + verb expand ചെയ്യണം.
• Sequence: Seeing (cause) → screamed (effect) — cause came first, then effect.
• Transformation rules:
  - Present participle opening (Seeing/Walking/Hearing) → When + subject + Simple Past
  - Having + V3 opening → After + subject + had + V3
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Sentence transformation — participial phrase to clause — PSC-ൽ asked!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Hearing the news, she cried. → When she heard the news, she cried.
  2. Seeing the teacher, students stood up. → When they saw the teacher, students stood up.
  3. Walking fast, he missed the bus. → When he walked fast, he missed the bus.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) lalu saw a snake in the garden when he screamed for help
• ഈ option-ൽ 'when' clause reversed ആണ് — saw + when screamed. Original-ൽ seeing first, then screaming.
• Original sequence: saw snake → then screamed. Option A implies: screamed while saw — illogical!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Same words ഉണ്ട്, order reversed — meaning change ആകുന്നു. Sequence check ചെയ്യണം!

❌ (B) He screamed for help and saw a snake
• 'And' = coordinate conjunction — both actions equal/simultaneous describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Original-ൽ snake seeing = CAUSE, screaming = EFFECT — 'and' ഈ cause-effect relationship express ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple sentence ആണ്, easy-ആണ് → students select ചെയ്യും — logical relationship missing!

❌ (C) lalu was screaming for help when seeing a snake in the garden
• 'When seeing' = grammatically incorrect — 'when' + ing form standard English-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Correct: 'when he **saw**' (past tense), not 'when seeing' (participial after when = wrong).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ing' form original-ൽ ഉണ്ടതുകൊണ്ട് preserve ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് confuse ആകും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SEEING = WHEN HE SAW" — Participial phrase (V+ing) expand ചെയ്യാൻ: V+ing → When + subject + past tense. Seeing → when he saw | Hearing → when he heard | Walking → when he walked!
• Transformation checklist: (1) Meaning same ✓ (2) Sequence correct ✓ (3) Grammar correct ✓ — ഒക്കെ pass ചെയ്ത option = answer.
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence transformation questions-ൽ ആദ്യം original sentence meaning understand ചെയ്യുക. Cause-effect sequence identify ചെയ്യുക. 'Seeing/Hearing/Walking' at start → 'When + subject + V2' transformation!

questionText:
The italicized word in the following sentence means
As a teacher, she resented __invidious__ remarks made on her students by her colleagues.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Unpleasant

• 'Invidious' എന്ന word-ന്റെ പ്രധാന അർഥം — objectionable, obnoxious, അഥവാ unpleasant/disagreeable ആയത് — dictionary-യിൽ 'of an unpleasant or objectionable nature' എന്നാണ് ഒരു core sense.
• ഇവിടെ 'invidious remarks' = colleagues പറഞ്ഞ, കേൾക്കാൻ unpleasant ആയ, hurt ചെയ്യുന്ന remarks — 'Unpleasant remarks' ആണ് standard dictionary-aligned meaning.
• Latin root 'invidiosus' (ill-will ഉള്ളത്, envy-related) → പിന്നീട് പൊതുവായി 'unpleasant/objectionable' എന്ന sense-ലേക്ക് extend ചെയ്തു (ഉദാ: 'the invidious task of choosing' = unpleasant/thankless task).
• Sentence meaning: "ഒരു teacher എന്ന നിലയിൽ, colleagues students-നെ കുറിച്ച് പറഞ്ഞ unpleasant remarks-നോട് അവൾക്ക് അതൃപ്തി തോന്നി."
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Invidious = Objectionable/Unpleasant (resentment-causing ആയത്)
• Word etymology: Latin 'invidia' (envy, ill will) → 'invidious' = ആദ്യം 'envy-causing', പിന്നീട് പൊതുവായി 'unpleasant, objectionable, thankless' എന്ന broader sense-ലേക്ക് develop ചെയ്തു.
• Invidious-ന്റെ exact meanings (dictionary):
  - Likely to cause resentment or anger
  - Unfairly discriminating (invidious comparison = unfair comparison)
  - Unpleasant or objectionable (invidious task = unpleasant/thankless task)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Advanced vocabulary — 'invidious' PSC higher-grade papers-ൽ ആവർത്തിച്ച് വരാൻ സാധ്യതയുള്ള word ആണ്.
• Word family:
  - Invidious (adj) — unpleasant, objectionable
  - Invidiously (adv) — in an invidious manner
  - Invidiousness (noun) — the quality of being invidious
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. It would be invidious to single out one student for blame.
  2. He was given the invidious task of deciding who to fire.
  3. She avoided making invidious remarks about her colleagues.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Personal
• 'Personal' = വ്യക്തിപരമായ — someone's private life/character-നെ കുറിച്ചുള്ളത്.
• 'Invidious' = objectionable/unpleasant ആയത് — 'personal' എന്നത് exact meaning അല്ല, remarks personal ആകാമെങ്കിലും invidious-ന്റെ core sense അതല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Personal remarks-ഉം invidious remarks-ഉം overlap തോന്നാം, students confuse ആകും — പക്ഷേ invidious = specifically unpleasant/objectionable, personal = private/individual എന്ന വേറെ concept ആണ്!

❌ (B) Cruel
• 'Cruel' = ക്രൂരമായ — deliberately causing pain/suffering ഉള്ളത്.
• 'Invidious' ≠ cruel — invidious-ന് 'harsh, deliberate cruelty' എന്ന strong sense ഇല്ല; അത് കൂടുതൽ 'unpleasant, resentment-causing' എന്ന sense ആണ്. Cruel എന്നത് വളരെ strong/extreme word ആണ്, invidious-ന്റെ nuance-ന് ചേരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Resented' എന്ന വാക്ക് കണ്ടാൽ students സ്വാഭാവികമായി 'Cruel' select ചെയ്യും — പക്ഷേ ഇത് ഒരു classic PSC trap ആണ്, dictionary-യിൽ invidious-ന്റെ closest sense 'unpleasant' ആണ്, 'cruel' അല്ല!

❌ (C) Sarcastic
• 'Sarcastic' = irony/mockery ഉള്ള — bitter humour ഉപയോഗിച്ചുള്ളത്.
• 'Invidious' ≠ sarcastic — invidious-ന് mockery/irony എന്ന sense ഇല്ല, അത് പൊതുവേ unpleasant/objectionable ആയ ഒരു situation അല്ലെങ്കിൽ remark-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Sarcastic remarks' എന്നത് common expression ആണ്, students naturally select ചെയ്യും — പക്ഷേ invidious-ന് ഇതുമായി direct connection ഇല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "INVIDIOUS TASK = UNPLEASANT TASK!" — 'the invidious task of choosing' എന്ന common phrase ഓർക്കുക: invidious = unpleasant/thankless/objectionable. Envy-related root ആണെങ്കിലും, modern usage-ൽ മിക്കപ്പോഴും 'unpleasant' എന്നാണ് അർഥം.
• Word family to learn together: invidious (unpleasant) / odious (hateful) / obnoxious (objectionable) — ഇവയെല്ലാം 'negative/unpleasant quality' describe ചെയ്യുന്ന words ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'cruel' പോലെ strong/violent അല്ല.
• PSC exam strategy: Advanced vocabulary word-ന്റെ meaning identify ചെയ്യാൻ ഏറ്റവും strong/extreme option (ഉദാ: Cruel) തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കാതെ, dictionary-യിലെ nuanced/moderate meaning (ഉദാ: Unpleasant) ആണോ എന്ന് ചിന്തിക്കുക. Context clue 'resented' → negative feeling → എന്നാൽ അത് 'objectionable/unpleasant' എന്ന moderate sense-ലേക്കാണ് pointing ചെയ്യുന്നത്, 'cruel' എന്ന extreme sense-ലേക്കല്ല!

questionText:
The idiom in the following sentence means
Perfumes were __dime a dozen__ for him in those days.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Plenty and common

• 'Dime a dozen' = അമേരിക്കൻ idiom — 'dime' = 10-cent coin (very small value) → 'a dozen for a dime' = ഒരു dime കൊഡുത്താല്‍ 12 കിട്ടും = വളരെ cheap, common, abundant!
• Idiomatic meaning: Something so plentiful and common that it has little value — 'plenty and common.'
• "Perfumes were dime a dozen for him" = "ആ കാലത്ത് perfumes അവന് വളരെ സുലഭമായിരുന്നു, uncommon ആയിരുന്നില്‍."
• Origin: 1800s America-ല്‍ dime = smallest coin, dozen-ക്ക് ഒരു dime = cheap and plentiful expression.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Dime a dozen = Very common, plentiful, and of little value
• Idiom-ന്റെ literal vs idiomatic meaning:
  - Literal: A dozen items for one dime (10 cents) — very cheap!
  - Idiomatic: So common that it is not special or valuable
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — especially American idioms — PSC-ല്‍ frequently asked!
• Related idioms about abundance/commonness:
  - 'A dime a dozen' = very common
  - 'Ten a penny' (British equivalent) = same meaning
  - 'Two a penny' = very common and of little value
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Talented singers are a dime a dozen. (very common)
  2. These days, smartphones are dime a dozen. (easily available)
  3. Good opportunities are not a dime a dozen — grab them! (rare, not common)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Rarity
• 'Rarity' = അപൂർവ്വമായ, rare ആയ — exactly OPPOSITE of 'dime a dozen'!
• 'Dime a dozen' = very common, NOT rare — ഇത് complete opposite.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: Students idiom-ന്റെ literal meaning confuse ആകും → actually dime = tiny value = very cheap/common!

❌ (C) Precious and valuable
• 'Precious and valuable' = വിലപ്പെട്ടത്, rare ആണ് — 'dime a dozen' idiom-ന്റെ exact opposite meaning!
• Dime = smallest coin = cheap → dime a dozen = cheapest possible = NOT precious or valuable.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'Perfumes' = luxury item → students think 'precious' → പക്ഷേ idiom changes the meaning completely!

❌ (D) Expensive
• 'Expensive' = വിലകൂടിയ — 'dime' (10 cents) cheap ആണ്, expensive അല്ല.
• 'Dime a dozen' = cheapness/commonness express ചെയ്യുന്ന idiom, expensiveness അല്ല.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'Perfumes are expensive' real life-ല്‍ true ആണ്, students naturally select 'expensive' — idiom's meaning = complete opposite of literal context!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "DIME = TINY COIN = TINY VALUE = COMMON!" — Dime = America's smallest coin (10 cents). A dozen for ONE dime = super cheap = super common. 'Dime a dozen' = so many, so cheap = PLENTY AND COMMON!
• PSC idiom family to learn: dime a dozen (common) / once in a blue moon (rare) / a drop in the ocean (very small) — opposite idioms for PSC!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ല്‍ literal meaning ignore ചെയ്യുക. 'Dime a dozen' = perfumes abundant = plenty and common. Context: 'for HIM in THOSE DAYS' = past abundance → B) Plenty and common is confirmed!

questionText: I __bank on__ your word. The phrasal verb in italics means
optionC: Bases one's hope on something or someone

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Bases one's hope on something or someone

• 'Bank on' = rely on, depend on, put your trust/hope in something — 'ആശ്രയിക്കുക, വിശ്വസിക്കുക.'
• "I bank on your word" = "നിന്റെ വാക്കില്‍ ഞാന്‍ ആശ്രയിക്കുന്നു / വിശ്വാസമർപ്പിക്കുന്നു."
• 'Bank on' idiomatically means — to count on, to rely on, to base your confidence/hope on something.
• Etymology: 'Bank' (financial institution) → you deposit trust/hope in something like money in a bank → 'bank on' = trust/rely on.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Bank on = Rely on = Depend on = Trust in
• 'Bank' phrasal verb family:
  - Bank on = rely on, depend on (I bank on your help)
  - Bank up = accumulate (Snow banked up overnight)
• Similar 'rely/trust' phrasal verbs:
  - Depend on = rely on | Count on = rely on | Lean on = depend emotionally | Build on = base on
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs PSC-ല്‍ ഏറ്റവും frequent questions — 'bank on' high-value phrasal verb!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. You can bank on him to finish the work. (rely on him)
  2. Don't bank on the weather staying fine. (don't count on)
  3. She banked on her friend's support during the crisis. (relied on)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Spends on something or someone
• 'Spend on' = money/time ചെലവഴിക്കുക — "She spends money on books."
• 'Bank on' ≠ 'spend on' — 'bank' literal meaning (money) കൊണ്ട് students confuse ആകും.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'Bank' = financial institution → 'spends money' → confuse! But 'bank on' = trust/rely, not spend!

❌ (B) Collects money from someone
• 'Collect money' = bank-ന്റെ literal function ആണ്, phrasal verb 'bank on'-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല.
• 'Bank on' = idiomatic meaning (rely on) — bank-ന്റെ literal financial meaning ഇവിടെ applicable അല്ല.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'Bank' word കണ്ടാല്‍ financial meaning automatically assume ചെയ്യും — phrasal verb = idiomatic meaning, literal meaning അല്ല!

❌ (D) Spreads something
• 'Spreads something' = spread, distribute — ഇതിന് 'bank on'-മായി യാതൊരു connection ഇല്ല.
• 'Bank on' ≠ 'spread' — completely different meanings.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: Distractor option — wrong meanings list ചെയ്ത് students confuse ആക്കും. 'Bank on' = rely on, spread-നോട് ബന്ധമില്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "BANK = TRUST SAFE!" — Bank-ല്‍ money safe ആക്കുന്നു, trust ഉണ്ടായിട്ടാണ്. 'Bank on' = trust safe ആക്കുന്നു = RELY ON! 'I bank on you' = I deposit my trust in you!
• PSC phrasal verb family: Bank on / Count on / Depend on / Rely on / Build on — all mean 'to trust/depend' — same family!
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb questions-ല്‍ 'bank' literal meaning (financial) ignore ചെയ്യുക. 'Bank on' = idiomatic = rely/trust. Sentence context: 'bank on your word' = trust your word = C) Bases one's hope on!

questionText:
Correct the following sentence.
The affects of Covid 19 are far reaching.
optionA: The effects of covid 19 is far reaching.
optionB: The effects of covid 19 are far reaching.
optionC: The affect of covid 19 is far reaching.
optionD: The affects of covid 19 is far reaching.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The effects of covid 19 are far reaching.

• Original sentence-ല്‍ error: 'affects' (verb) ഉപയോഗിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നു — ഇവിടെ noun 'effects' ആകണം.
• 'Affect' vs 'Effect': Affect = verb (ബാധിക്കുക), Effect = noun (ഫലം/പ്രഭാവം).
• "The **effects** of Covid" = "Covid-19-ന്റെ **ഫലങ്ങൾ**" — noun ആണ്, verb ('affects') അല്ല.
• 'Effects' = plural noun → plural verb 'are' correct ആണ് — 'is' wrong.
• Correct sentence: "The effects of Covid 19 are far reaching." ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: AFFECT = verb (action) | EFFECT = noun (result)
• Quick memory: A = Action (verb) | E = End result (noun)
• Detailed breakdown:
  - Affect (verb): to influence, to have an impact on — 'ബാധിക്കുക'
    The rain affected the crops. (verb)
  - Effect (noun): the result, the outcome — 'ഫലം, പ്രഭാവം'
    The effect of rain was good crops. (noun)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Affect vs Effect = MOST commonly confused word pair in PSC — guaranteed question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Smoking **affects** your health. (verb — ബാധിക്കുന്നു)
  2. The **effects** of smoking are dangerous. (noun — ഫലങ്ങൾ)
  3. How did the accident **affect** him? (verb) / What were the **effects** of the accident? (noun)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The effects of covid 19 is far reaching.
• 'Effects' correct ആണ് (noun) — പക്ഷേ 'is' wrong! 'Effects' = plural noun → 'are' വേണം.
• Subject-verb agreement error: "effects (plural) **are** far reaching" — 'is' singular verb, 'effects' plural subject!
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'effects' ശരിയാക്കി, 'is' wrong ഇട്ടു — double error! Noun ശരിയാക്കിയ ശേഷം verb-ഉം check ചെയ്യണം!

❌ (C) The affect of covid 19 is far reaching.
• 'Affect' = verb — noun position-ല്‍ ('The ___') verb ഉപയോഗിക്കാന്‍ കഴിയാല്‍.
• 'Affect' singular ആക്കി, 'is' ഇട്ടു — noun 'effect' ആകണം, 'affect' (verb) ആകില്‍.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'affects' → 'affect' ആക്കി correct ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും — singular/plural change, but still wrong word!

❌ (D) The affects of covid 19 is far reaching.
• 'Affects' = verb, 'is' = singular — രണ്ടും wrong! Original sentence-ല്‍ ഉള്ളതേ — 'are' → 'is' മാത്രം changed, main error (affects→effects) fix ആയിട്ടില്‍.
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'are' → 'is' change ചെയ്ത് correct ആക്കി എന്ന് confuse ആകും — main error 'affects' still there!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "A for Action, E for End result!" — AFFECT = Action (verb) | EFFECT = End result (noun). Covid AFFECTS health → The EFFECT is illness! A→E: first action, then end result!
• Double check formula: 'The ___ of...' = always noun position = EFFECT (not affect). 'The' കണ്ടാല്‍ noun വേണം = effect!
• PSC exam strategy: (1) Noun position ('the ___') = effect, (2) Verb position ('it ___s') = affect. Subject-verb agreement-ഉം check ചെയ്യുക — 'effects' plural = 'are', 'effect' singular = 'is'!

questionText: His behavior at the party was __atrocious__. The synonym for the italicized word is

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Appalling

• 'Atrocious' = extremely bad, horrifying, cruel — 'ഭയാനകമായ, ക്രൂരമായ, അതിഘോരമായ.'
• 'Appalling' = causing horror or dismay, shocking — 'ഭയപ്പെടുത്തുന്ന, ഞെട്ടിക്കുന്ന' — exact synonym!
• Both 'atrocious' and 'appalling' = shocking/horrifying negative meaning — perfect synonyms.
• "അവന്റെ behavior atrocious/appalling ആയിരുന്നു" = "ഭയാനകമായിരുന്നു / ഞെട്ടിക്കുന്നതായിരുന്നു."
• Etymology: 'Atrocious' from Latin 'atrox' (fierce, cruel, savage) → 'atrocious' = extremely savage/cruel.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Atrocious = Appalling = Horrifying = Dreadful = Shocking
• Word family for 'atrocious':
  - Atrocious (adj) — extremely bad or cruel
  - Atrociously (adv) — in an atrocious manner
  - Atrocity (noun) — an act of extreme cruelty or evil (eg: war atrocities)
• Synonyms cluster (PSC high-value):
  Atrocious / Appalling / Horrifying / Dreadful / Awful / Heinous / Terrible
  All = extremely bad/shocking
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Vocabulary synonym questions-ല്‍ 'atrocious' group PSC-ല്‍ appeared!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The conditions in the prison were atrocious. (horrifyingly bad)
  2. It was an appalling display of rudeness. (shocking — synonym!)
  3. The atrocities committed during the war were unforgivable. (noun form)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Attractive
• 'Attractive' = ആകർഷണീയമായ, pleasing — OPPOSITE meaning of 'atrocious'!
• 'Atrocious' = horrifying/bad; 'attractive' = pleasing/beautiful — antonyms, not synonyms!
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'Atrocious' and 'attractive' both start with 'at' — sound similar! Students confuse due to similar starting letters. Sound-alike ≠ Same meaning!

❌ (C) Supportive
• 'Supportive' = സഹകരണം/support നൽകുന്ന — positive, helpful quality.
• 'Atrocious' = negative/horrifying — 'supportive' = positive — completely opposite qualities!
• 🪴 PSC Trap: Behavior described → students think 'supportive behavior' → പക്ഷേ 'atrocious' = bad behavior!

❌ (D) Compassionate
• 'Compassionate' = ദയയുള്ള, empathetic — deeply positive human quality.
• 'Atrocious' = cruel/horrifying — 'compassionate' = caring/kind — exact opposites!
• 🪴 PSC Trap: 'Compassionate' sounds like a positive personality trait → party behavior might be 'compassionate'? → പക്ഷേ 'atrocious behavior' = very bad behavior, not compassionate!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ATROCIOUS = ATROCITY!" — 'Atrocious' ഇസ് 'Atrocity' (war atrocity = terrible war crime)-ന്റെ adjective form. 'Atrocious behavior' = behavior like a war crime! = APPALLING! Both words = shocking, horrifying, terrible. Atrocious → atrocity → appalling — all in one family!
• 'A' trap: Atrocious (bad) vs Attractive (good) — both start with 'A' but opposite meanings! Be careful with similar-sounding words!
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym questions-ല്‍ answer word-ന്റെ strength/tone match ആകണം. 'Atrocious' = very strong negative word. Options-ല്‍ equally strong negative word = 'Appalling'. 'Attractive'/'Supportive'/'Compassionate' = positive words → eliminate instantly!

questionText:
Identify the segment of the sentence that contains a grammatical error.
It actually / does take place / and the water on this area / is forbidden.
optionC: and the water on this area

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) and the water on this area

• Error: 'on this area' — ഇവിടെ 'on' wrong preposition ആണ്. ശരിയായ preposition = 'in this area.'
• 'Area' = enclosed/defined space — enclosed space-ന് 'in' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം, 'on' അല്ല.
• Corrected: "...and the water **in** this area is forbidden."
• 'On' = surface ഉള്ളിടത്ത് (on the table, on the road) | 'In' = enclosed space-ൽ (in the room, in the area).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: IN = enclosed space / area / region | ON = surface
• Area/region/zone/district → always 'IN'
  - in this area, in the region, in the zone, in the district
• Surface/floor/road → always 'ON'
  - on the table, on the road, on the wall
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Preposition error identification — IN vs ON confusion PSC-ൽ frequently asked!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The water **in** this area is polluted. (area = enclosed space → in) ✓
  2. She lives **in** this locality. (locality = enclosed space → in) ✓
  3. The book is **on** the table. (table = surface → on) ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) is forbidden
• 'Is forbidden' = correct usage — 'forbidden' = prohibited, 'is forbidden' = passive construction. grammatically correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Forbidden' rare word ആണ് → students suspect ചെയ്യും — പക്ഷേ 'is forbidden' = perfectly correct passive!

❌ (B) does take place
• 'Does take place' = emphatic present tense — "it DOES happen" emphasize ചെയ്യുന്നതിനുള്ള correct form.
• 'Does + base verb' = emphasis → grammatically correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Does take' unusual-ആണ് → students confuse ആകും — emphatic 'does' correct usage!

❌ (D) It actually
• 'It actually' = subject + adverb — 'it' subject ആണ്, 'actually' = adverb for emphasis. Both correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'It' what refers to? ambiguous — പക്ഷേ grammatical error ഇല്ല, 'on this area' ആണ് error!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "AREA = INSIDE → IN!" — Area, region, zone = places you are INSIDE. Inside = IN. 'On area' = wrong — you can't stand ON an area like a surface!
• Error spotting strategy: Underlined/segmented sentence-ൽ prepositions ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യുക — IN/ON/AT/BY errors PSC favourite!
• PSC exam tip: 4 segments ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ prepositions, articles, verb forms quickly scan ചെയ്യുക → error identify ചെയ്യുക. 'On this area' → In this area = fix!

questionText: Select the most appropriate antonym of the word, __indefatigable__

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) indolent

• 'Indefatigable' = never getting tired, tireless — 'ക്ഷീണം അറിയാത്ത, അഥക്ഷമമായ, ഉദ്യമശീലമുള്ള.'
• 'Indolent' = lazy, habitually inactive — 'മടിയൻ, അലസൻ' — exact ANTONYM of indefatigable!
• Indefatigable person = never stops working | Indolent person = never wants to work — perfect opposites!
• Etymology: 'Indefatigable' = in (not) + defatigare (Latin: to tire out) = one who cannot be tired out. Antonym = easily tired = indolent!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Indefatigable (tireless) ↔ Indolent (lazy/idle)
• Word etymology:
  - Indefatigable: Latin 'in' (not) + 'defatigare' (exhaust) = inexhaustible
  - Indolent: Latin 'in' (not) + 'dolere' (to feel pain/effort) = avoiding effort = lazy
• Synonyms of indefatigable: tireless / untiring / persistent / energetic / assiduous
• Synonyms of indolent: lazy / slothful / idle / lethargic / sluggish
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Advanced vocabulary antonyms — indefatigable/indolent PSC higher-grade favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is an indefatigable worker — never rests! (tireless)
  2. His indolent nature cost him the promotion. (lazy)
  3. The indefatigable athlete trained 12 hours a day. (untiring)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) unabated
• 'Unabated' = without any reduction in intensity — 'കുറയാതെ, ശക്തി കുറയാതെ' — same meaning territory as 'indefatigable'!
• Unabated = not decreasing → actually CLOSER to a synonym of indefatigable, not antonym!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Un-' prefix → students think = negative → antonym! But unabated = not abated = still strong = similar meaning!

❌ (C) intense
• 'Intense' = very strong, extreme — 'തീവ്രമായ' — describes strength/degree, not energy/laziness.
• 'Intense' ≠ antonym of indefatigable — intensity and tirelessness are different qualities.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Intense work = hard work → seems related to tireless work → confuse ആകും!

❌ (D) feverish
• 'Feverish' = having fever OR extremely hurried/excited — 'ജ്വരത്തോടു കൂടിയ / ആവേശഭരിതമായ'
• 'Feverish activity' = frantic/hasty activity — still not antonym of tireless.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Feverish = high energy → opposite of tired? → No! Antonym of indefatigable = lazy/indolent, not energetic!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "INDEFATIGABLE = IRON MAN (never tires!) ↔ INDOLENT = IRON BED (never leaves bed!)" — Iron Man is indefatigable! A lazy person is indolent — stuck in bed!
• Root learning: 'in-' prefix often = not → indefatigable = NOT fatigued. Antonym = easily fatigued = indolent!
• PSC exam strategy: Advanced antonym questions-ൽ root word analyze ചെയ്യുക. 'Indefatigable' = tireless → antonym = tired/lazy → 'indolent' = lazy. Eliminate options that are neutral or same-side words!

questionText:
Rearrange the parts of the sentence in correct order.
Due to ____________
(P) the tsunami of 2004
(Q) the coastal regions of Tamil Nadu
(R) the lack of an adequate warning system,
(S) devastated a large portion of

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) RPSQ

• Correct order: "Due to **R**) the lack of an adequate warning system, **P**) the tsunami of 2004 **S**) devastated a large portion of **Q**) the coastal regions of Tamil Nadu."
• 'Due to' = cause introduce ചെയ്യുന്നു → first comes the cause: R (lack of warning system).
• After cause, the subject of the sentence: P (the tsunami of 2004) — subject ആണ്, verb-നു മുന്നേ.
• S (devastated a large portion of) = verb phrase — subject-ന്റെ action.
• Q (the coastal regions of Tamil Nadu) = object — devastated WHOM? The coastal regions.
• Complete: "Due to the lack of an adequate warning system, the tsunami of 2004 devastated a large portion of the coastal regions of Tamil Nadu."
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Due to [CAUSE] → [SUBJECT] [VERB] [OBJECT]
• Sentence rearrangement strategy:
  1. 'Due to' = cause phrase starter → next part = the cause (R)
  2. After cause clause = main subject (P = the tsunami)
  3. Verb/action follows subject (S = devastated)
  4. Object completes the verb (Q = the coastal regions)
• Connectors guide order: 'Due to', 'because of', 'owing to' = cause first, then effect.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Sentence rearrangement PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു — logical flow + grammar = key!
• ഉദാഹരണ:
  Due to [heavy rain] → [the match] → [was cancelled] → [by the officials]
  Cause → Subject → Verb → Object
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) PQRS
• "Due to the tsunami of 2004 the coastal regions of Tamil Nadu the lack of an adequate warning system devastated..." — meaningless! Subject before cause, no logical flow.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: P (tsunami) = most prominent idea → students start with P — but 'due to' needs cause (R) first!

❌ (B) PRSQ
• "Due to the tsunami of 2004 the lack of an adequate warning system, devastated a large portion of the coastal regions..." — subject (P) comes before cause (R), but 'due to' clause should contain the cause, not the subject!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: R has the comma → students think it goes second → but R = cause, must follow 'due to' directly!

❌ (C) RSPQ
• "Due to the lack of an adequate warning system, devastated a large portion of the tsunami of 2004 the coastal regions..." — verb 'devastated' (S) comes before subject (P). Verb before subject = wrong!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: R (with comma) looks like intro → S (verb) follows → but subject (P) must come before verb (S)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "DUE TO = BLAME GAME!" — 'Due to' always introduces who/what is to blame (cause). Find the CAUSE first after 'due to'. Then: Who? (subject) → Did what? (verb) → To whom/what? (object). R→P→S→Q = Cause→Subject→Verb→Object!
• Rearrangement 4-step: (1) Find the opening link word ('due to', 'because') → (2) cause follows → (3) subject next → (4) verb → object.
• PSC tip: Read all parts first, find Subject + Verb → build around that core. 'The tsunami' (P) = subject, 'devastated' (S) = verb → P must come before S!

questionText:
Select the correct indirect form of the given sentence.
John said to Teena, "Where were you going?"
optionA: John asked Teena where she was going.
optionB: John asked Teena where she had been going.
optionC: John asked Teena where were she going.
optionD: John asked Teena where was she going.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) John asked Teena where she had been going.

• Direct: "Where were you going?" — 'were going' = Past Continuous tense.
• Indirect speech-ൽ tense backshift rule: Past Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous.
• 'were going' → 'had been going' — backshift ചെയ്തു.
• WH question indirect: 'said to' → 'asked', question mark remove, normal word order (she had been going, not had she been going).
• Correct: "John asked Teena where she **had been going**." ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Tense Backshift Table (Direct → Indirect):
  Simple Present → Simple Past
  Simple Past → Past Perfect
  Past Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous ⭐
  Present Perfect → Past Perfect
  Future (will) → Conditional (would)
• WH Question Indirect rules:
  1. 'said to' → 'asked'
  2. WH word stays (where, when, why...)
  3. Inverted question order → normal S+V order
  4. Question mark (?) removed
  5. Tense backshifted
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Indirect speech — especially Past Continuous backshift — PSC-ൽ frequently tested!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. "Where were you?" → asked where she/he **had been**. (Past → Past Perfect)
  2. "What were you doing?" → asked what she **had been doing**. (Past Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous)
  3. "Where are you going?" → asked where she **was going**. (Present Continuous → Past Continuous)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) John asked Teena where she was going.
• 'Was going' = Past Continuous — ഇത് Present Continuous ('are going') backshift ആണ്, Past Continuous ('were going') backshift അല്ല.
• 'Were going' → 'had been going' (one step further back), 'was going' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Were going' → 'was going' (just pronoun+number change) — most common mistake! Tense backshift ആകണം!

❌ (C) John asked Teena where were she going.
• 'Were she going' = inverted/question word order — indirect speech-ൽ question word order allowed ആകില്ല!
• Indirect speech-ൽ normal word order: 'she had been going' (S+V), not 'were she going' (inverted).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Direct question inversion 'were you' → students keep inversion in indirect — wrong! Indirect = normal order!

❌ (D) John asked Teena where was she going.
• 'Was she going' = again inverted order, AND wrong tense ('was going' instead of 'had been going').
• Two errors: (1) inverted word order, (2) wrong tense backshift.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Direct question 'were you going' → students invert in indirect too — double error option to confuse!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "WERE GOING → HAD BEEN GOING!" — Past Continuous 'were going' → one step FURTHER back = Past Perfect Continuous 'had been going'. Think of time travel: each backshift = go ONE more step into the past!
• Indirect speech checklist: (1) said to → asked (2) WH word stays (3) normal S+V order (4) backshift tense (5) remove ?
• PSC exam strategy: WH question indirect — ആദ്യം tense identify ചെയ്യുക → backshift table apply ചെയ്യുക → word order normal ആക്കുക. Past Continuous 'were ___ing' → 'had been ___ing' always!

questionText: Choose the correct spelling from the options below.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) acquaintance

• 'Acquaintance' = ഒരു person whom you know slightly — 'പരിചയക്കാരൻ/ൾ' — correct spelling: a-c-q-u-a-i-n-t-a-n-c-e.
• Key letters: 'acqu' (not 'aqu') + 'ai' (not 'i') + 'ance' (not 'ence').
• Common errors: (1) missing 'c' after 'a' → 'aquaintance' wrong, (2) '-ence' instead of '-ance' → 'acquaintence' wrong.
• Pronunciation hint: ak-KWAYN-tance — the 'c' after 'a' is silent but present in spelling!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Correct: acquaintance = acqu + ain + t + ance
• Breaking it down: a-c-q-u-a-i-n-t-a-n-c-e (13 letters)
• Common spelling traps in 'acquaintance':
  Trap 1: 'acq' → missing 'c' → aquaintance ❌
  Trap 2: '-ence' instead of '-ance' → acquaintence ❌
  Trap 3: Missing 'a' after 'qu' → acquintance ❌
• Word family:
  - Acquaint (verb) = to make familiar with
  - Acquaintance (noun) = a person one knows
  - Acquainted (adj) = familiar with
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions-ൽ commonly confused words — acquaintance PSC-ൽ appeared!
• Similar '-ance' endings (not '-ence'): appearance, allowance, acceptance, performance, resistance
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) aquaintance
• Missing 'c' after 'a' — 'acqu' ആകണം, 'aqu' അല്ല. 'Acquire', 'acquaint' — 'acq' pattern ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Aqua' (water) spelling familiar ആണ് → students 'aqu' write ചെയ്യും — 'acqu' silent 'c' ഉൾപ്പെടുന്നു!

❌ (B) acquaintence
• '-ence' ending wrong — correct ending = '-ance'. 'Acquaintance' ends in '-ance' (like appearance, balance).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ence' vs '-ance' most common spelling confusion in English — many words have '-ence' (difference, silence) → students apply same to acquaintance!

❌ (D) acquintance
• Missing 'a' after 'u' — 'acqua-' ആകണം, 'acqui-' (only 'i') അല്ല. 'acqu**a**intance' → 'a' after 'u' essential.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'acqui-' sounds like 'acquire' → students confuse spelling patterns — acquaintance has 'a' not just 'i' after 'qu'!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ACQUAintance has AQUA!" — 'acqu**a**intance' = starts like 'aqua' (water) but with a 'c': a-c-q-u-a. Once you write 'acqua', add '-intance' → acqua+intance = acquaintance! And ending = '-ance' (like 'balance').
• Spelling mantra: A-C-Q-U-A-I-N-T-A-N-C-E — 'acqu-a-int-ance' → say it in parts!
• PSC spelling strategy: 4 options usually have 3 common errors — identify what changes in each option. Here: (A) missing 'c', (B) wrong ending, (D) missing 'a'. Correct = has ALL: c + a + -ance = (C)!

questionText: If she had taken the medicine, she ___________ better.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) would have felt

• "If she had taken the medicine" — 'had taken' = Past Perfect → ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്!
• Third Conditional formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (past participle).
• 'Feel' → past participle = 'felt' → 'would have **felt**' correct.
• Meaning: "Medicine കഴിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, അവൾക്ക് സുഖം തോന്നിയേനേ" — past unreal situation (medicine കഴിച്ചില്ല).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Third Conditional Formula:
  If + had + V3 (Past Perfect) → would have + V3 (Past Participle)
• Meaning: ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഉണ്ടാകാതിരുന്ന situation imagine ചെയ്യുന്നു — unreal past!
• All 3 Conditionals summary:
  Type 1 (Real Future): If + Present → will + V1
  Type 2 (Unreal Present): If + Past → would + V1
  Type 3 (Unreal Past): If + had + V3 → would have + V3 ⭐
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequently tested conditional type!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had studied, I would have passed. (didn't study, didn't pass)
  2. If they had left early, they would have caught the train. (didn't leave early)
  3. If she had called, I would have come. (she didn't call)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) could feel
