• 'Could feel' = Simple modal — Second Conditional structure ('If + Past → could/would + V1').
• ഇവിടെ 'had taken' = Third Conditional → main clause-ൽ 'would have + V3' വേണം, 'could feel' (V1) അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Could' = polite/possible → students feel it fits — but Third Conditional needs 'have + V3' after modal!

❌ (C) could have feel
• 'Could have feel' — modal + have + V1 wrong! 'Have' ന് ശേഷം Past Participle V3 വേണം, base form V1 അല്ല.
• Correct form would be 'could have **felt**' (V3), not 'could have feel'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have' + verb → students use base form 'feel' → must be past participle 'felt'!

❌ (D) would felt
• 'Would felt' — 'would' ന് ശേഷം base form (V1) അഥവാ 'have + V3' വേണം, directly 'felt' (V2/V3) ഇടരുത്.
• Correct: 'would have felt' (would + have + V3), not 'would felt'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' + 'felt' seems logical — would = past feeling — പക്ഷേ 'have' missing! Would **have** felt!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "HAD = WOULD HAVE!" — If clause-ൽ 'had' കണ്ടാൽ → main clause = 'would have + V3' automatic! 'Had taken' → 'would have felt'. H-A-D → W-H (would have)!
• Third Conditional song: "If I HAD done it, I WOULD HAVE been happy!" — Had → Would Have → always together!
• PSC exam strategy: If clause-ൽ 'had + V3' → Third Conditional confirmed → answer = 'would have + V3'. 'Would have felt' = (B) → mark it!

questionText: Every school and college ___________ a library. The students, along with their friends, ___________ the library.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) has, visit

• Blank 1: "Every school **and** college ___" — 'Every' + compound subject → SINGULAR verb 'has'!
  'Every' word-ൽ ഉള്ളതെല്ലാം singular ആക്കുന്നു — 'every A and B' = singular.
• Blank 2: "The students, along with their friends, ___" — Main subject = 'students' (plural).
  'Along with their friends' = parenthetical phrase — ignore it! Subject = students (plural) → 'visit'.
• Answer: has (singular for 'every') + visit (plural for 'students').
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Rule 1: Every/Each + [A and B] → SINGULAR verb
  Every man and woman IS invited. (not are)
  Every school and college HAS a library. ✓
• 🔑 Rule 2: Subject + along with/together with/as well as + [extra phrase] → verb agrees with MAIN subject only
  The students (plural) + along with their friends → PLURAL verb 'visit'
  The teacher (singular) + along with students → SINGULAR verb 'visits'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Double-blank SVA questions — 'every' rule + 'along with' rule combined — PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Every boy and girl **has** a uniform. (every → singular)
  2. The manager, along with his staff, **is** present. (manager = singular → is)
  3. The players, along with the coach, **are** ready. (players = plural → are)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) have, visit
• 'Have' for Blank 1 wrong — 'Every school and college' → 'every' = singular → 'has', not 'have'.
• 'Visit' for Blank 2 correct! But Blank 1 wrong, so whole option wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'school AND college' = two things → plural → 'have' → confuse ആകും! 'Every' overrides 'and' → singular!

❌ (B) has, visits
• 'Has' for Blank 1 correct! But 'visits' for Blank 2 wrong.
• 'The students' = plural main subject → plural verb 'visit', not singular 'visits'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Along with their friends' = many people → 'visits' looks wrong → but subject = students (plural) → visit!

❌ (D) have, visits
• Both wrong! 'Have' wrong (should be 'has'), 'visits' wrong (should be 'visit').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Completely reversed — designed to catch students who confuse both rules at once!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "EVERY = ONE! ALONG WITH = IGNORE!" — 'Every' = one at a time → singular always. 'Along with' = extra info, bracket it → find REAL subject before it!
• Two-blank strategy: Handle each blank independently! Don't let Blank 2 influence Blank 1.
• PSC exam strategy: (1) See 'every/each' → singular verb automatic. (2) See 'along with/as well as' → find subject BEFORE that phrase → match verb to that subject only. Apply one rule at a time!

questionText: His wife can't ___________ his terrible behaviour anymore.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) put up with

• 'Put up with' = tolerate, endure — 'സഹിക്കുക, ക്ഷമിക്കുക.'
• "His wife can't put up with his terrible behaviour" = "അവന്റെ ഭയങ്കര behavior അവളുടെ ഭാര്യക്ക് ഇനി സഹിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്‍."
• 'Put up with' = 3-word phrasal verb (put + up + with) — always together, never separated here.
• Context clue: 'terrible behaviour', 'can't anymore' → tolerance-related phrasal verb = 'put up with'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Put up with = Tolerate / Endure / Bear
• 'PUT' phrasal verb family (PSC high-frequency):
  - Put up with = tolerate (I can't put up with the noise)
  - Put off = postpone (The meeting was put off)
  - Put on = wear / pretend (Put on your coat / Put on an act)
  - Put out = extinguish / inconvenience (Put out the fire / Don't put yourself out)
  - Put in = insert / invest time (Put in effort)
  - Put forward = suggest (Put forward a proposal)
  - Put away = store (Put away your toys)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Put up with' = PSC-ൽ most frequently tested phrasal verb!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I can't put up with this noise anymore. (tolerate)
  2. She put up with his rudeness for years. (endured)
  3. How do you put up with such cold weather? (bear/tolerate)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) put in with
• 'Put in with' = not a standard English phrasal verb — ഈ combination exist ചെയ്യില്‍!
• 'Put in' exists (= invest/insert), but 'put in with' as 3-word phrasal verb = wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put up with' ഓർമ്മ ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'put in with' similar-ആണ് → confuse ആകും!

❌ (B) put on
• 'Put on' = wear (clothes) or pretend/fake — "Put on your shoes" / "She put on a brave face."
• Cannot tolerate behaviour — 'put on' = dress/pretend, 'put up with' = endure.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put on' = common phrasal verb → students select easily → wrong meaning for this context!

❌ (C) put out
• 'Put out' = extinguish (put out a fire) OR to inconvenience/bother someone.
• 'Put out' ≠ tolerate — completely different meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put out' sounds negative (like being fed up) → confuse ആകും — 'put out' = inconvenience, 'put up with' = tolerance!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "PUT UP WITH = STOMACH IT!" — 'Put up with' = ഉള്ളിലേക്ക് push ചെയ്ത് സഹിക്കുക! Something terrible → you 'put it up' inside and tolerate = put up with. Can't stomach it anymore = can't put up with it!
• PUT family flashcard: put OFF (postpone) | put ON (wear) | put OUT (extinguish) | put UP WITH (tolerate) — 4 meanings for PSC!
• PSC exam strategy: Context clue = 'terrible behaviour' + 'can't anymore' = tolerance being tested. Phrasal verb = 'put up with' = tolerate. Whenever 'can't stand/bear/tolerate' context → 'put up with' is the answer!

questionText: Find out the compound word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) all of these

• Compound word = രണ്ടോ അതിലധികമോ words ചേർത്ത് ഒരു new word ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്നത് — new meaning!
• (A) Whirlwind = whirl + wind — a spinning column of air — compound word ✓
• (B) Blackboard = black + board — a dark board for writing — compound word ✓
• (C) Blacksmith = black + smith — a metalworker — compound word ✓
• മൂന്നും compound words → answer = (D) all of these!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Compound word = Word 1 + Word 2 → New meaning
• Types of compound words:
  Closed (one word): blackboard, whirlwind, blacksmith, sunflower, football
  Hyphenated: mother-in-law, well-known, up-to-date
  Open (two words): ice cream, post office, full moon
• Key feature: Compound word-ന്റെ meaning = component parts-ന്റെ combined meaning-ൽ നിന്ന് DIFFERENT ആകാം!
  Blackboard = not a board that is black (in color) → specific school board!
  Blacksmith = not a black-colored smith → iron/metal worker!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Compound words identification PSC-ൽ frequently asked!
• ഉദാഹരണ compound words:
  1. Rainfall = rain + fall (rain that falls)
  2. Bookworm = book + worm (person who loves books)
  3. Butterfly = butter + fly (a beautiful insect — new meaning!)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) whirlwind only
• Whirlwind = compound word — ഇത് correct. പക്ഷേ blackboard, blacksmith-ഉം compound words ആണ് — 'only whirlwind' = incomplete answer.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students ഒരെണ്ണം identify ചെയ്ത് stop ആകും — 'all of these' option ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ check ചെയ്യണം!

❌ (B) blackboard only
• Blackboard = compound word — ഇതും correct. പക്ഷേ മൂന്നും compound words ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Blackboard' = most familiar compound word → students stop here — other options-ഉം check ചെയ്യണം!

❌ (C) blacksmith only
• Blacksmith = compound word — correct. 'Smith' = metal worker; 'black' = iron color when heated. Compound!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Blacksmith' = less familiar → students think it's NOT a compound → actually it is!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "TWO WORDS = ONE IDEA = COMPOUND!" — whirl+wind, black+board, black+smith — all two words making one thing. 'All of these' = all three = COMPOUND!
• 'All of these' strategy: PSC-ൽ 'all of these' option ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ — EVERY option check ചെയ്യുക. If all fit = 'all of these' is the answer!
• Compound word quick test: Can you split the word into 2 meaningful English words? whirl+wind ✓, black+board ✓, black+smith ✓ = all compound!

questionText: "Have the whip hand" means ___________
optionB: to be in a position to control

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) to be in a position to control

• 'Have the whip hand' = to be in a position of power or control over others — 'നിയന്ത്രണത്തിൽ ഇരിക്കുക, ആധിപത്യം ഉള്ളയാൾ ആയിരിക്കുക.'
• Origin: Horse-drawn carriage-ൽ whip-ഉള്ള hand = driver-ന്റെ dominant hand = control ചെയ്യുന്ന hand → 'have the whip hand' = controlling position.
• "She has the whip hand in negotiations" = "Negotiations-ൽ അവൾക്കാണ് control/advantage."
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Have the whip hand = Be in control / Have the upper hand
• Related 'power/control' idioms (PSC family):
  - Have the whip hand = be in control
  - Have the upper hand = have an advantage
  - Call the shots = make the decisions
  - Hold the reins = be in control (similar horse metaphor!)
  - Rule the roost = be dominant in a group
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom meanings — especially 'hand' idioms — PSC favourite!
• 'Hand' idioms PSC list:
  - Have the whip hand = control
  - Give a hand = applaud OR help
  - Lend a hand = help
  - Have a hand in = be involved in
  - Upper hand = advantage
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The manager has the whip hand in all decisions. (is in control)
  2. After the takeover, the new CEO had the whip hand. (held power)
  3. Don't let him have the whip hand — stand your ground! (control)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to swear falsely
• 'To swear falsely' = perjury — ഇതിനു 'have the whip hand'-മായി യാതൊരു connection ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Whip' = punishment → falsely swearing = punishment- യോഗ്യമായ act → students connect — pure distractor!

❌ (C) to smell unpleasant
• 'To smell unpleasant' — 'whip hand' idiom-ൽ smell-ന് relation ഇല്ല. Completely irrelevant meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Random wrong meaning — students unsure → choose 'smell unpleasant' thinking 'whip' = animal → animal smell? Pure confusion trap!

❌ (D) to a suspicion
• 'To a suspicion' — grammatically incomplete and unrelated to 'whip hand' meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Nonsensical option — if students don't know the idiom, they might guess between B and D. B is clearly a complete, meaningful phrase!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "WHIP = DRIVER'S CONTROL!" — Horse carriage-ൽ whip-ഉള്ള hand = control. Whip hand = controlling hand = CONTROL POSITION. 'Have the whip hand' = 'I hold the whip' = I'm in charge!
• 'Hand' idiom family: UPPER hand (advantage) | WHIP hand (control) | FREE hand (freedom to act) | HEAVY hand (strict control) — all about power!
• PSC idiom strategy: Idiom-ന്റെ origin/image think ചെയ്യുക. 'Whip' = control tool → 'have whip hand' = be in control position. Literal image → idiomatic meaning link ആക്കുക!

questionText: Identify the correct sentence :
optionA: The bouquet of flowers smell good
optionB: The bouquet of flowers smells good
optionC: The bouquet of flowers are smelling good
optionD: The bouquet of flowers were smelling good

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The bouquet of flowers smells good

• Subject = 'bouquet' (singular noun) — 'of flowers' = prepositional phrase, ignore ചെയ്യണം!
• 'Bouquet' = singular → singular verb 'smells' correct.
• "The bouquet of flowers **smells** good" — "ഫൂലഗുച്ഛം നന്നായി മണക്കുന്നു."
• 'Of flowers' = extra information — subject identify ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ prepositional phrase-ൽ ഉള്ള noun subject അല്ല!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'The [singular noun] of [plural noun]' → SINGULAR verb always!
• 'Of + plural noun' = prepositional phrase — verb is determined by the MAIN subject before 'of'.
• Similar patterns:
  - The bunch of keys **is** on the table. (bunch = singular → is)
  - A box of chocolates **was** given. (box = singular → was)
  - The team of players **is** ready. (team = singular → is)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Bouquet/bunch/box of...' SVA pattern PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The bouquet of roses **smells** beautiful. (bouquet = singular)
  2. A group of students **was** present. (group = singular)
  3. The bunch of grapes **is** sweet. (bunch = singular)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The bouquet of flowers smell good
• 'Smell' = plural verb form — 'bouquet' singular-ന് 'smells' (with 's') വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flowers' plural → 'smell' (no 's') plural → confuse ആകും. Subject = 'bouquet', not 'flowers'!

❌ (C) The bouquet of flowers are smelling good
• 'Are smelling' = plural continuous — 'bouquet' singular-ന് 'is smelling' ആകണം, 'are smelling' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flowers' plural + 'are' plural → students naturally combine → subject = 'bouquet' singular!

❌ (D) The bouquet of flowers were smelling good
• 'Were smelling' = plural past continuous — 'bouquet' singular-ന് 'was smelling' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Were' = past tense, 'flowers' plural → confuse ആകും. Both tense AND number wrong here!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "OF = OFF!" — 'of [plural noun]' = OFF from subject! Verb-ന് subject identify ചെയ്യാൻ 'of ...' ignore ചെയ്യുക. 'The bouquet (of flowers)' → bouquet = singular → smells!
• Prepositional phrase bracket trick: [The bouquet] (of flowers) smells good — bracket the subject, parenthesise the 'of' phrase!
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence-ൽ 'of' കണ്ടാൽ — 'of' phrase ignore ചെയ്ത് before subject find ചെയ്യുക → singular/plural check → verb agree ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: Europeans conquered many parts of the world before ___________ First World War.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) The

• 'First World War' = specific historical event — specific, unique event-ന് 'the' definite article ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'The First World War' = ലോകത്ത് ഒരേ ഒരു First World War — unique event → 'the' mandatory.
• "Europeans conquered many parts of the world before **the** First World War."
• Rule: Ordinal numbers (First, Second, Third) + War/Battle/Event → always 'the' precedes it.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Unique/specific historical events → THE
• 'The' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന situations:
  - Unique events: the First World War, the French Revolution
  - Ordinal numbers with nouns: the first prize, the second time
  - Superlatives: the best, the tallest
  - Previously mentioned: I saw a dog. The dog was cute.
• Historical events always take 'the':
  the First World War / the Second World War / the Great Depression / the French Revolution
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles with historical events — PSC-ൽ frequently asked!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. **The** Second World War ended in 1945. (unique historical event)
  2. **The** French Revolution changed Europe. (specific event)
  3. He won **the** first prize. (ordinal number)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) A
• 'A' = indefinite article — one among many, non-specific. 'First World War' = one specific, unique event — 'a' cannot be used.
• 'A First World War' = implies there could be many 'first world wars' — absurd!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'First' starts with consonant sound 'F' → 'a First' sounds natural → but specific events need 'the', not 'a'!

❌ (C) An
• 'An' = indefinite article before vowel sounds — 'First' starts with consonant 'F' → 'an' wrong pronunciation-wise too.
• Even ignoring sound rule, 'an' = non-specific → unique historical event cannot use 'an'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Confusion between 'a' and 'an' rule makes students pick 'an' — but both wrong; specific events = 'the'!

❌ (D) None of these
• 'First World War' without any article is incorrect in standard English — 'the' is mandatory.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students who are unsure between A/An/The may choose 'none' — but 'the' is required here!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "UNIQUE = THE!" — World Wars are UNIQUE — there is only ONE First World War. Unique things = 'the'. The sun, the moon, the First World War — all unique = all 'the'!
• Historical event 'the' family: the First World War / the Second World War / the Cold War / the Indian Independence Movement — always 'the'!
• PSC exam strategy: Historical event + ordinal number (First/Second) → 'the' automatic. Sun, moon, earth → 'the'. Superlative (best/tallest) → 'the'. These 3 patterns cover 90% of PSC article questions!

questionText: None of the boys have finished the homework, ___________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) have they?

• 'None of the boys have finished' = negative statement ('none' = not one = negative meaning).
• Negative statement → Positive question tag (have they? — positive form).
• Auxiliary verb used = 'have' → tag-ൽ 'have' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Subject pronoun: 'None of the boys' → 'they' (plural).
• Tag = have + they? = **have they?** ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Negative statement → Positive tag | Positive statement → Negative tag
• Negative words: no, none, never, neither, nobody, nothing, hardly, scarcely — these make statement negative!
• 'None' = not one = NEGATIVE → tag must be POSITIVE (have they?)
• Tag construction: same auxiliary + pronoun (positive form)
  - 'None ... have' (negative) → 'have they?' (positive tag) ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Negative word (none/never/neither) + question tag = PSC top pattern!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Nobody came to the party, **did they?** (nobody = negative → did they = positive)
  2. She never lies, **does she?** (never = negative → does she = positive)
  3. None of them agreed, **did they?** (none = negative → did they = positive)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) haven't they?
• 'Haven't they?' = negative tag — negative statement + negative tag = WRONG (double negative).
• 'None ... have' = already negative → tag must flip to positive 'have they?', not stay negative 'haven't they?'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students see 'have' in statement → copy 'haven't' for tag (making it negative) → but must flip!

❌ (C) did they?
• 'Did they?' uses past tense auxiliary 'did' — statement uses 'have' (present perfect auxiliary).
• Tag auxiliary must MATCH statement auxiliary: 'have' in statement → 'have' in tag, not 'did'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Finished' = past action → students think 'did' → auxiliary must match the stated tense 'have'!

❌ (D) didn't they?
• 'Didn't they?' = past tense + negative tag — wrong auxiliary AND it creates double confusion.
• Statement has 'have' (present perfect) — tag must use 'have', not 'did'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Finished' = past + negative statement → 'didn't they?' seems right — but auxiliary = 'have', not 'did'!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NONE = NEGATIVE → FLIP TO POSITIVE TAG!" — 'None' = no one = negative. Negative statement → positive tag. 'None...have' → 'have they?' (positive). NONE is secretly negative — it flips the tag!
• Negative word family: none / nobody / nothing / never / neither / hardly / scarcely → all make statement negative → all need POSITIVE tags!
• PSC exam strategy: (1) Find negative word in statement (2) statement = negative → tag = positive (3) find auxiliary in statement (4) copy auxiliary in tag (positive form) + pronoun. None...HAVE → HAVE they? ✓

questionText: If it rains tomorrow, we ___________ at home.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) will stay

• "If it rains tomorrow" — if clause-ൽ Simple Present ('rains') + 'tomorrow' = First Conditional!
• First Conditional formula: If + Simple Present → will + base verb (V1).
• "If it rains tomorrow, we **will stay** at home."
• Meaning: "നാളെ മഴ പെയ്താൽ, നമ്മൾ വീട്ടിൽ ഇരിക്കും" — real/possible future situation!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 All 3 Conditional Formulas:
  Type 1 (Real Future): If + Simple Present → WILL + V1
  Type 2 (Unreal Present): If + Simple Past → WOULD + V1
  Type 3 (Unreal Past): If + Past Perfect → WOULD HAVE + V3
• Key identifier here: 'rains' = Simple Present + 'tomorrow' = future plan → Type 1!
• First Conditional = realistic possibility: It might actually rain tomorrow.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditional sentence fill-in-blank PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequent — all 3 types ഓർക്കണം!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If she studies hard, she **will pass**. (Type 1 — realistic)
  2. If I were rich, I **would travel**. (Type 2 — unreal present)
  3. If he had come, we **would have met**. (Type 3 — unreal past)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) stayed
• 'Stayed' = Simple Past — conditional result-ൽ Simple Past ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• First Conditional result = 'will + V1', not Simple Past.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Rains' future → students think 'stayed' = past = wrong tense entirely!

❌ (B) would stay
• 'Would stay' = Second Conditional result — Second Conditional if-clause = Simple Past ('rained'), not Simple Present ('rains').
• If clause = 'rains' (Simple Present) → must be First Conditional → 'will stay', not 'would stay'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' sounds more polite/formal → students prefer 'would stay' — but 'rains' (present tense) = First Conditional = 'will'!

❌ (D) would have stayed
• 'Would have stayed' = Third Conditional result — needs 'had rained' (Past Perfect) in if-clause.
• If clause = 'rains' (simple present) — Third Conditional അല്ല, First Conditional ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would have' sounds complete/final → confuse ആകും — if clause tense check ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "RAINS → WILL!" — Simple Present in if clause → WILL in result. Easy memory: 'Present if → Will result'. If today's tense (present) → future will happen. RAINS (present) → WILL STAY!
• Conditional cheat sheet: Present → Will (Type 1) | Past → Would (Type 2) | Had+V3 → Would have (Type 3)
• PSC exam strategy: If clause-ൽ tense identify ചെയ്യുക → Type determine ചെയ്യുക → result formula apply ചെയ്യുക. 'Rains' = Simple Present → Type 1 → 'will + V1' = 'will stay' ✓

questionText: Alex said, "I have finished my task". Choose the correct conversation into Indirect Speech :
optionA: Alex said that he has finished his task
optionB: Alex said that I had finished my task
optionC: Alex said that I have finished my task
optionD: Alex said that he had finished his task

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Alex said that he had finished his task

• Direct: Alex said, "I have finished my task."
• Changes in indirect:
  (1) 'said,' + 'that' → 'said that'
  (2) 'I' → 'he' (third person, Alex is male)
  (3) 'my' → 'his' (possessive pronoun change)
  (4) 'have finished' (Present Perfect) → 'had finished' (Past Perfect) — tense backshift!
• Correct: "Alex said that **he had finished his** task." ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Indirect Speech Checklist:
  1. Reporting verb: 'said' stays 'said' (no asked — statement, not question)
  2. Add 'that' after reporting verb
  3. Pronoun change: I → he/she | my → his/her | we → they
  4. Tense backshift: Present Perfect (have/has + V3) → Past Perfect (had + V3)
• Full tense backshift table:
  Simple Present → Simple Past
  Present Perfect → Past Perfect ⭐
  Simple Past → Past Perfect
  will → would
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Indirect speech — statement conversion — PSC-ൽ guaranteed!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She said, 'I am tired.' → She said that **she was** tired.
  2. He said, 'I have eaten.' → He said that **he had eaten**.
  3. They said, 'We will come.' → They said that **they would come**.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Alex said that he has finished his task
• 'Has finished' = Present Perfect — tense backshift ആകാതിരുന്നു! 'Have finished' → 'had finished' (Past Perfect) ആകണം.
• Pronoun ('he') and possessive ('his') correct — only tense wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Pronoun correct ആണ് → students satisfied ആകും — tense backshift miss ചെയ്യും!

❌ (B) Alex said that I had finished my task
• 'I' and 'my' = first person — Direct speech-ൽ Alex-ന്റേത് 'I'/'my', indirect-ൽ 'he'/'his' ആകണം.
• Tense ('had finished') correct — but pronoun change missing!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Tense ശരിയാക്കി, pronoun change forget ചെയ്തു — half right = still wrong!

❌ (C) Alex said that I have finished my task
• Both wrong: (1) 'I'/'my' → should be 'he'/'his', (2) 'have finished' → should be 'had finished'.
• No changes made to either pronoun or tense — this is basically the direct speech without the quote marks!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students who don't know indirect rules may pick this — it looks like the original with 'that' added!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SAID + THAT + HE/SHE + HAD!" — Indirect statement formula: said + that + pronoun change + tense backshift. Alex 'I have' → Alex 'he had' — I→he, have→had, my→his!
• 4-point checklist for indirect statements: (1) said → said that (2) I→he/she (3) my→his/her (4) has/have→had
• PSC exam strategy: Indirect speech options-ൽ ആദ്യം pronoun check ചെയ്യുക (I/my = wrong), then tense check ചെയ്യുക (have = wrong, had = right). Options B,C wrong pronouns/tenses → D = correct!

questionText: Which of the following words is the closest in meaning to "delinquent"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) criminal

• 'Delinquent' = someone (especially a young person) who commits crimes or fails in duty — 'കുറ്റകൃത്യം ചെയ്യുന്ന, ചുമതല നിർവ്വഹിക്കാൻ വിസമ്മതിക്കുന്ന.'
• Closest meaning = 'criminal' — someone who commits offences.
• 'Juvenile delinquent' = young criminal — PSC-ൽ ഈ term familiar ആകണം!
• Etymology: Latin 'delinquere' (to fail in duty, offend) → English 'delinquent' = offender, criminal.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Delinquent ≈ Criminal / Offender / Wrongdoer
• Word family:
  - Delinquent (noun/adj) = a person who commits minor crimes; failing in duty
  - Delinquency (noun) = the state of being delinquent; minor criminal behavior
  - Juvenile delinquent = a young offender (young criminal)
• Synonyms: criminal / offender / wrongdoer / culprit / miscreant
• Antonyms: dutiful / obedient / law-abiding / innocent
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Delinquent' PSC vocabulary — especially in social/legal context!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The court dealt with juvenile delinquents separately. (young criminals)
  2. His delinquent behaviour got him expelled. (criminal/problematic)
  3. Delinquency rates rose during the crisis. (criminal activity)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) dutiful
• 'Dutiful' = ചുമതലാബോധമുള്ള, responsible — OPPOSITE of delinquent (who fails duty)!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Delinquent' → 'duty' connection (de-linquent = failing duty) → students pick 'dutiful' — opposite meaning!

❌ (C) innocent
• 'Innocent' = കുറ്റമില്ലാത്ത, harmless — opposite of 'criminal/delinquent'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Innocent' sounds like the opposite of criminal → students confuse question (asks for closest meaning, not antonym)!

❌ (D) obedient
• 'Obedient' = അനുസരണശീലമുള്ള — a delinquent is DISobedient, not obedient.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Options A, C, D are all ANTONYMS of delinquent — designed to confuse! Question asks for SYNONYM = B criminal!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "DELINQUENT = DELINQUENCY = CRIME!" — News-ൽ 'juvenile delinquency' = young people's crime. Delinquent = young criminal. 'De-linquent' = de (away from) + linquent (duty) = running away from duty = criminal behavior!
• Trap awareness: Options A (dutiful), C (innocent), D (obedient) = all ANTONYMS. PSC deliberately puts antonyms in options — question ആദ്യം clearly read ചെയ്യുക: 'closest in MEANING' = synonym, not opposite!
• PSC vocabulary tip: -ent/-ency words: delinquent/delinquency, negligent/negligence, diligent/diligence — noun-adjective pairs learn ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: Select the antonym of "derelict" :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) attentive

• 'Derelict' = abandoned, neglected, failing in duty — 'ഉപേക്ഷിക്കപ്പെട്ട, ശ്രദ്ധ കൊടുക്കാതിരിക്കുന്ന.'
• 'Attentive' = paying close attention, careful, dutiful — 'ശ്രദ്ധാലുവായ, ജാഗ്രതയുള്ള.'
• Derelict = negligent/abandoned ↔ Attentive = careful/diligent — perfect antonyms!
• "A derelict building" = abandoned | "An attentive student" = careful — opposite qualities!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Derelict (neglected/abandoned) ↔ Attentive (careful/watchful)
• 'Derelict' meanings:
  - Abandoned/deserted: a derelict building (ഉപേക്ഷിക്കപ്പെട്ട കെട്ടിടം)
  - Negligent in duty: derelict in his duties (ചുമതല ഒഴിവാക്കി)
• Synonyms of derelict: negligent / careless / abandoned / deserted / forsaken
• Antonyms of derelict: attentive / careful / diligent / dutiful / conscientious
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Advanced vocabulary antonyms — derelict/attentive PSC-ൽ appeared!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The derelict factory was demolished. (abandoned)
  2. He was derelict in performing his duties. (negligent)
  3. The attentive nurse noticed the change immediately. (antonym — careful)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) careless
• 'Careless' = ശ്രദ്ധയില്ലാത്ത — SYNONYM of derelict (both mean negligent/not careful).
• Question asks for ANTONYM — careless = same side as derelict, not opposite!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Most common error — 'careless' seems correct because it's a familiar negative word, same as 'derelict'!

❌ (B) deserted
• 'Deserted' = ഉപേക്ഷിക്കപ്പെട്ട, empty — SYNONYM of 'derelict' (one meaning = abandoned).
• Both derelict and deserted = abandoned — same meaning, not antonym!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Derelict building' = 'deserted building' — students know both mean abandoned → pick 'deserted' thinking it's different!

❌ (D) negligent
• 'Negligent' = അശ്രദ്ധമായ — SYNONYM of derelict (both mean failing in care/duty).
• Negligent = careless in duty = exactly what derelict means → same meaning, not antonym!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Options A, B, D = all SYNONYMS of derelict! PSC put 3 synonyms as traps — only C (attentive) is the antonym!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "DERELICT BUILDING = NOBODY CARES!" — Derelict = nobody pays attention. Antonym = somebody pays careful attention = ATTENTIVE! 'Derelict' = abandoned, no attention → 'Attentive' = full attention = exact opposite!
• Trap alert: A (careless), B (deserted), D (negligent) = ALL synonyms. PSC loves putting 3 synonyms as traps! When question asks 'antonym' and 3 options seem similar/related to the word — the ODD one out = antonym!
• PSC antonym strategy: Find the word's core meaning → find the OPPOSITE feeling/quality → that's the antonym. Derelict = neglected → opposite = attentive (cared for)!

questionText: The teacher asked the students to ___________ their assignment before submitting it.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) look over

• 'Look over' = to examine or review carefully — 'ശ്രദ്ധാപൂർവ്വം പരിശോധിക്കുക, review ചെയ്യുക.'
• "Teacher asked students to look over their assignment" = "submit ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് മുന്നേ assignment ഒരു തവണ review ചെയ്യാൻ."
• Context: before submitting = checking/reviewing = 'look over' perfect fit!
• 'Look over' = inspect/review — teacher wanting students to review = 'look over' ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയמം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Look over = Review / Examine / Check through
• 'LOOK' phrasal verb family:
  - Look over = review/examine carefully
  - Look after = take care of (Look after the baby)
  - Look up = search for information (Look up a word)
  - Look into = investigate (Look into the matter)
  - Look down on = consider inferior (Don't look down on others)
  - Look forward to = anticipate with pleasure (Looking forward to the trip)
  - Look out = be careful (Look out for cars!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Look' phrasal verbs PSC-ൽ frequently tested!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Please look over my report before sending. (review)
  2. The doctor looked over the patient carefully. (examined)
  3. Look over your answers before submitting. (check/review)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) turn down
• 'Turn down' = refuse / reject OR reduce volume — "She turned down the job offer."
• Context: teacher wants students to CHECK the assignment, not REJECT it!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Turn down' = common phrasal verb → students know it → but meaning = reject, not review!

❌ (C) set off
• 'Set off' = to start a journey OR to trigger/cause — "We set off at 6 AM."
• Nothing to do with reviewing an assignment — completely wrong context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Set off' = familiar phrasal verb → students pick familiar over correct!

❌ (D) look down
• 'Look down' alone = physically look downward OR (with 'on') = consider someone inferior.
• 'Look down their assignment' = nonsensical — 'look over' = review, not 'look down'!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Look down' vs 'look over' — same 'look' base → confuse ആകും — but 'over' = going through = review, 'down' = inferior!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LOOK OVER = OVERLOOK ERRORS!" — 'Look over' an assignment = look for errors to fix before submitting. You 'look OVER' the whole thing from top to bottom = review/inspect!
• LOOK family flashcard: OVER (review) | AFTER (care) | UP (search) | INTO (investigate) | DOWN ON (despise) | FORWARD TO (anticipate) — 6 meanings for PSC!
• PSC exam strategy: Context = 'before submitting assignment' = review/check action needed. 'Look over' = review = A. Turn down (reject), set off (journey), look down (inferior) — all wrong context!

questionText: A handwriting that cannot be read:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) illegible

• 'Illegible' = handwriting/text that cannot be read — 'വായിക്കാൻ കഴിയാത്ത കൈയ്യക്ഷരം.'
• 'A handwriting that cannot be read' = illegible — exact one-word substitute!
• Etymology: 'il-' (not) + 'legible' (readable, from Latin 'legere' = to read) = not readable.
• "His handwriting is illegible" = "അവന്റെ കൈയ്യക്ഷരം വായിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല."
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Illegible = Cannot be read (handwriting)
• 'il-' prefix = not (used before words starting with 'l'):
  - il + legible = illegible (cannot be read)
  - il + logical = illogical (not logical)
  - il + legal = illegal (not legal)
  - il + literate = illiterate (cannot read/write)
• All 4 options compared:
  - Indelible = cannot be erased (indelible ink = permanent ink)
  - Illegible = cannot be read ✓ (answer)
  - Illogical = not logical/reasonable
  - Inaudible = cannot be heard
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One-word substitute + negative prefix questions PSC-ൽ popular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The doctor's handwriting is illegible. (cannot be read)
  2. Indelible ink cannot be washed off. (cannot be erased)
  3. The whisper was inaudible in the crowd. (cannot be heard)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) indelible
• 'Indelible' = cannot be erased or removed — 'മായ്ക്കാൻ കഴിയാത്ത' (indelible ink = permanent).
• Indelible ≠ unreadable — erasing and reading are different things!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both 'indelible' and 'illegible' start with 'i' and relate to writing — students confuse indelible (erase) with illegible (read)!

❌ (C) illogical
• 'Illogical' = not logical, without reason — 'യുക്തിരഹിതമായ.'
• Handwriting that cannot be read is not 'illogical' — illogical = about reasoning, not readability.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'il-' prefix same as 'illegible' → confuse ആകും — but illogical = reasoning, illegible = reading!

❌ (D) inaudible
• 'Inaudible' = cannot be heard — 'കേൾക്കാൻ കഴിയാത്ത' (inaudible sound).
• Handwriting → cannot be READ, not heard — 'inaudible' = hearing, 'illegible' = reading!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'In-' prefix + 'not+ability' pattern → confuse ആകും — each word relates to different sense!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "LEGIBLE = LEG + IBLE = CAN WALK THROUGH (READ)!" More seriously: 'legi-' = read (Latin). il+legible = not readable = ILLEGIBLE. il+legal = not legal = illegal. Same 'il-' trick!
• Sense family: indelible (erase/remove) | illegible (read) | inaudible (hear) | invisible (see) — one word = one sense!
• PSC one-word substitute strategy: Definition keyword = 'cannot be **read**' → find word with 'leg/legib' root (= reading) → illegible. Match the keyword to the root of the word!

questionText: The idiom "on cloud nine" means:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) extremely happy or elated

• 'On cloud nine' = extremely happy, overjoyed — 'ആനന്ദത്തിന്റെ കൊടുമുടിയിൽ, അതിസന്തോഷത്തിൽ.'
• "She was on cloud nine after hearing the good news" = "സന്തോഷ വാർത്ത കേട്ടപ്പോൾ അവൾ അതിസന്തോഷത്തിലായിരുന്നു."
• Origin: Cloud nine = highest cloud level = top of happiness. 9th cloud = highest = maximum happiness!
• 'Elated' = extremely happy — perfect synonym for 'on cloud nine'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: On cloud nine = Extremely happy / Overjoyed / Elated
• Related happiness idioms (PSC family):
  - On cloud nine = extremely happy ⭐
  - Over the moon = very happy/excited
  - On top of the world = feeling wonderful
  - Walking on air = feeling extremely happy
  - In seventh heaven = in a state of extreme happiness
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Happiness idioms PSC-ൽ frequently asked — 'cloud nine' highest frequency!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She was on cloud nine after her promotion. (extremely happy)
  2. The couple was on cloud nine on their wedding day. (overjoyed)
  3. When he got the job, he was on cloud nine! (elated)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) completely indifferent
• 'Indifferent' = no feeling, uncaring — opposite of 'on cloud nine' (maximum happiness)!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Cloud nine' = high up = far away → students think 'indifferent/detached' = far from earth → wrong!

❌ (B) slightly joyful and content
• 'Slightly joyful' = mild happiness — 'on cloud nine' = EXTREME happiness, not mild!
• 'Cloud nine' = highest level of happiness — 'slightly joyful' is too mild an interpretation.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Joy is present in both → students pick 'slightly joyful' thinking it's about joy → 'cloud nine' = EXTREME joy!

❌ (C) feeling confused or lost
• 'Confused/lost' = negative feelings — 'on cloud nine' = positive extreme happiness.
• 'In the clouds' = confused/lost (a different idiom!). 'On cloud nine' ≠ 'in the clouds'!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Cloud' = up in the air = confused/dreamy → confuse ആകും — 'in the clouds' (confused) vs 'on cloud nine' (happy)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NINE = HIGHEST!" — Cloud 9 = the topmost cloud level = TOP of happiness scale! When you're at the PEAK of joy = you're on cloud nine. 9 = maximum score = maximum happiness!
• Confusion trap: 'In the clouds' (confused/dreamy) ≠ 'On cloud nine' (extremely happy) — two different idioms! 'IN the clouds' vs 'ON cloud NINE' — number 9 = NINE = extreme happiness signal!
• PSC happiness idiom family: cloud nine / over the moon / on top of the world / seventh heaven / walking on air — all = extreme happiness. Learn the whole family!

questionText: The quality of the oranges __________ not satisfactory.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) was

• Subject = 'quality' (singular noun) — 'of the oranges' = prepositional phrase, ignore ചെയ്യണം!
• 'Quality' = singular → singular past tense verb = 'was'.
• "The quality of the oranges **was** not satisfactory." = "ഓറഞ്ചുകളുടെ ഗുണനിലവാരം തൃപ്തികരമായിരുന്നില്ല."
• 'Of the oranges' = extra info about which quality — verb always agrees with 'quality', not 'oranges'!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'The [singular noun] of [plural noun]' → SINGULAR verb always!
• 'Of + plural noun' = prepositional phrase — subject is the word BEFORE 'of'.
• Pattern examples:
  - The quality of the goods **was** poor. (quality = singular → was)
  - The price of vegetables **has** risen. (price = singular → has)
  - The color of the flowers **is** beautiful. (color = singular → is)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'The [noun] of [plural]' SVA pattern PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The quality of mangoes **was** excellent. (quality → was)
  2. The smell of the roses **is** sweet. (smell → is)
  3. The taste of those fruits **was** sour. (taste → was)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) were
• 'Were' = plural past tense — 'quality' singular-ന് 'was' ആകണം, 'were' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Oranges' plural → 'were' plural → confuse ആകും. Subject = 'quality' (singular)!

❌ (C) have been
• 'Have been' = plural present perfect — singular subject-ന് 'has been' ആകണം, 'have been' അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Present Perfect tense seem ചെയ്യും — പക്ഷേ singular → 'has been', AND context = simple past 'not satisfactory' → 'was'!

❌ (D) are
• 'Are' = plural present tense — 'quality' singular-ന് 'is' ആകണം (present-ൽ), 'was' (past-ൽ).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Oranges' plural + 'are' plural → students naturally match — subject = 'quality', not 'oranges'!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "OF = BRACKET IT!" — 'The quality (of the oranges)' — bracket the 'of' phrase out of the picture. What's left? 'The quality was' — singular! 'Of' phrase is decoration, not the subject!
• Quick test: Remove 'of the oranges' → 'The quality ___ not satisfactory' → clearly singular → 'was'!
• PSC exam strategy: Subject identification — ആദ്യം 'of' phrase bracket ചെയ്യുക → remaining noun check ചെയ്യുക → singular/plural decide ചെയ്യുക → verb agree ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: He _____________ 10 years of service by next March. (~~deleted by psc~~)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) will complete

• "By next March" = future time marker — future-ൽ ഒരു milestone complete ആകും.
• "He will complete 10 years of service by next March" = "അടുത്ത മാർച്ചോടെ അദ്ദേഹം 10 വർഷം സർവീസ് complete ചെയ്യും."
• 'Will complete' = Simple Future — future-ൽ ഒരു action/milestone happen ആകും എന്ന് indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• PSC context-ൽ 'by + future time' + completion milestone → Simple Future ('will complete') correct answer ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Key tenses with 'by + future time':
  Simple Future: will + V1 → action to happen before/at a future point
  Future Perfect: will have + V3 → action completed BY a future point (strict grammar)
• Options analysis for this sentence:
  - will complete (B) = Simple Future — milestone happen ആകും ✓ (PSC accepted)
  - will be completing (A) = Future Continuous — ongoing action at a specific future time
  - will have been completing (C) = Future Perfect Continuous — duration until a future point
  - shall complete (D) = formal/archaic first person future — 'he' (third person) with 'shall' = incorrect!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Future tense + 'by next [time]' questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She will complete her degree by next year. (milestone)
  2. He will finish the project by Friday. (future completion)
  3. They will reach the station by 6 PM. (future event)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) will be completing
• 'Will be completing' = Future Continuous — ഒരു specific future moment-ൽ action in progress ആകും.
• '10 years of service' = a milestone, not an ongoing activity at next March.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Completing' word കണ്ടാൽ 'will be completing' select ചെയ്യും — but milestone completion = 'will complete'!

❌ (C) will have been completing
• Future Perfect Continuous — duration of ongoing action up to a future point.
• 'Completing 10 years' = not a prolonged activity — it's a milestone point. Duration format (-ing) fits badly.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'By next March' → future perfect structure → confuse ആകും — 'will complete' cleanest answer!

❌ (D) shall complete
• 'Shall' = first person future (I/we) — 'he' = third person → 'shall' incorrect for third person!
• Standard modern English: 'will' for all persons; 'shall' only for I/we (and even that is archaic).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Shall' sounds formal/official → students pick it — but 'shall' with 'he' = grammatically wrong!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "SHALL = I/WE only (old English)!" — 'Shall' is old-fashioned and only for I/We. 'He shall' = wrong! For 'he/she/they/it' = always WILL. 'He WILL complete' not 'He SHALL complete'!
• Milestone + future = 'will + V1': finish, complete, reach, achieve + by [future time] → will + base verb.
• PSC exam strategy: 'By next [time]' + milestone (complete/finish/reach) → 'will + V1'. Check subject (he = 3rd person → will, not shall). Eliminate 'shall' for 3rd person always!

🚫 Deleted Question: PSC ഔദ്യോഗിക Final Answer Key-ൽ ഈ ചോദ്യം ഒഴിവാക്കി (deleted/cancelled), സ്കോറിംഗിൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിട്ടില്ല. സാധ്യതയുള്ള കാരണം: 'By next March' എന്ന strict future time-marker സാങ്കേതികമായി Future Perfect Tense ('will have completed') ആണ് ആവശ്യപ്പെടുന്നത് — ഒരു future point-ന് മുൻപ് പൂർത്തിയാകുന്ന ഒരു milestone സൂചിപ്പിക്കാൻ. എന്നാൽ options-ൽ ('will complete', 'will be completing', 'will have been completing', 'shall complete') ഈ കൃത്യമായ 'will have completed' രൂപം ഇല്ല — ഏറ്റവും അടുത്ത option 'will complete' (Simple Future) ആണെങ്കിലും, കൃത്യമായ grammar rule പ്രകാരം അത് 100% ശരിയല്ല. ഈ ambiguity കാരണം candidates-ന്റെ complaint-ന് ശേഷം PSC ഈ ചോദ്യം cancel ചെയ്തതാകാം.

questionText: None of us played the game well,

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) did we?

• 'None of us played' — 'none' = not one = negative meaning → POSITIVE tag.
• Auxiliary: 'played' = Simple Past → auxiliary = 'did'.
• Subject pronoun: 'None of us' → 'we' (us = object form; tag-ൽ subject pronoun 'we' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം).
• Tag = did (positive, past) + we (subject pronoun) = **did we?** ✓
• "None of us played the game well, **did we?**" — none = negative → tag = positive 'did we?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Negative statement → Positive tag
• 'None' = no one = NEGATIVE word → statement is negative → tag must flip to POSITIVE.
• Tag construction for Simple Past:
  Positive Past → Negative tag: didn't + pronoun
  Negative Past (none/never) → Positive tag: did + pronoun ⭐
• Pronoun rule: 'None of us' → tag pronoun = 'we' (NOT 'us' — 'us' is object, 'we' is subject).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'None' question tag pattern PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. None of them came, **did they?** (none = negative → did they = positive)
  2. None of us knew the answer, **did we?** (negative → positive)
  3. Nobody spoke, **did they?** (nobody = negative → did they = positive)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) didn't we?
• 'Didn't we?' = negative tag — 'none...played' = negative statement → tag must be POSITIVE.
• Negative statement + negative tag = WRONG (double negative error).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Played' = past → 'didn't we?' past negative → confuse ആകും — but 'none' flips to positive!

❌ (B) did us?
• 'Did us?' = wrong pronoun — 'us' = object pronoun. Question tag-ൽ SUBJECT pronoun വേണം = 'we'.
• 'Did we?' correct, 'did us?' grammatically impossible.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'None of **us**' → students copy 'us' into tag → but tag needs subject form 'we', not object 'us'!

❌ (C) don't they?
• 'Don't they?' = present tense + wrong pronoun — statement is past tense ('played') and subject is 'we' not 'they'.
• Two errors: (1) 'don't' = present, should be past 'did'; (2) 'they' ≠ 'we'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'None of them' type questions use 'they' — but here 'none of US' → 'we'!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NONE = NEGATIVE → FLIP! US → WE!" — Two things to remember: (1) 'None' is secretly negative → flip tag to positive → 'did we?' (2) 'us' in sentence → 'we' in tag (object → subject swap).
• Object → Subject pronoun in tags: us → we | them → they | him → he | her → she
• PSC exam strategy: (1) Find negative word (none) → positive tag (2) Tense = past (played) → 'did' in tag (3) Subject = 'us' → tag pronoun = 'we'. Three checks = answer = 'did we?'!

questionText: If you had reached the venue at the right time, you ______ the Chief guest.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) could have met

• 'If you had reached' = Past Perfect in if-clause → Third Conditional!
• Third Conditional formula: If + had + V3 → could/would + have + V3.
• 'Met' = past participle of 'meet' → 'could have met' ✓
• "You could have met the Chief guest" = "Chief guest-നെ കണ്ടുമുട്ടാമായിരുന്നു" — but you didn't reach in time, so you didn't meet them.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Third Conditional Formula:
  If + had + V3 → would/could/might + have + V3
• All three modals work in Third Conditional result:
  would have met (certainty — definitely would have)
  could have met (possibility — might have been able to) ✓
  might have met (lesser possibility)
• 'Meet' irregular verb: meet → met → met (base → past → past participle)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional result clause — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും tested conditional!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If she had studied, she could have passed. (Third Conditional)
  2. If he had tried, he would have succeeded. (would have)
  3. If they had left early, they could have caught the bus. (could have)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) could met
• 'Could met' = missing 'have' — Third Conditional needs 'could **have** met', not 'could met'.
• Modal + V2 (past form directly) = grammatically wrong. Modal → must be followed by base form or 'have + V3'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students write 'could met' thinking 'met' (past tense) after modal = correct — must be 'could have met'!

❌ (C) could have meet
• 'Could have meet' — 'meet' = base form, but 'have' ന്  ശേഷം Past Participle 'met' ആകണം.
• 'Could have + V3' → 'met' (past participle), not 'meet' (base form).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have + base form' confuse ആകും — 'have' ന് ശേഷം V3 (past participle) വേണം, V1 (base form) അല്ല!

❌ (D) will meet
• 'Will meet' = Simple Future — Third Conditional result needs 'could/would have + V3', not Simple Future.
• If-clause = past perfect → result cannot be simple future!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Chief guest meet ആകണം' → future action → 'will meet' — but conditional tense structure follow ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "HAD → COULD HAVE!" — If clause-ൽ 'had' കണ്ടാൽ → result = 'could/would have + V3'. HAD = third conditional signal → HAVE in result too! 'Had reached' → 'could have met'!
• 'Meet' V3 check: meet-met-met (irregular!) → 'could have **met**' (not 'meet', not 'meeted')!
• PSC exam strategy: If clause tense = Past Perfect ('had + V3') → Third Conditional → result = 'could/would/might + have + V3'. Check past participle (not base form, not past form) after 'have'!

questionText: Teacher could not _____________ beating the student for his misbehaviour.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) keep from

• 'Keep from' = to prevent oneself from doing something, to refrain from — 'സ്വയം തടയുക, ഒഴിഞ്ഞു നിൽക്കുക.'
• "Teacher could not keep from beating" = "അദ്ധ്യാപകന് beating-ൽ നിന്ന് സ്വയം തടഞ്ഞുനിർത്താൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല."
• 'Keep from + V-ing' = cannot stop oneself from doing something — standard English idiom.
• Example: "I couldn't keep from laughing" = "ചിരി തടഞ്ഞുനിർത്താൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല."
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Keep from + V-ing = Cannot stop/refrain from doing
• 'KEEP' phrasal verb family:
  - Keep from = refrain from / prevent (She kept from crying)
  - Keep down = suppress / control (Keep your voice down)
  - Keep back = withhold / hold in place (Keep back the crowd)
  - Keep away = stay at a distance (Keep away from danger)
  - Keep up = maintain / continue (Keep up the good work)
  - Keep on = continue doing (Keep on trying)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Keep' phrasal verbs PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He couldn't keep from smiling. (refrain from)
  2. I kept from eating sweets. (prevented myself)
  3. She kept from crying during the speech. (stopped herself)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) keep down
• 'Keep down' = suppress / reduce / control (keep costs down, keep voice down).
• 'Teacher could not keep down beating' = doesn't make idiomatic sense here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Keep down' = control → teacher controlling anger = related? → but 'keep from' is the specific idiom for self-restraint!

❌ (C) keep back
• 'Keep back' = hold back / withhold information OR stay behind.
• 'Could not keep back beating' = unnatural phrasing, not standard usage.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Keep back' = hold back = some connection to restraining → but 'keep from + V-ing' is the correct idiom!

❌ (D) keep away
• 'Keep away' = maintain distance from something/someone (Keep away from fire!).
• 'Teacher kept away beating' = wrong — 'keep away from' = avoid a place/person, not self-restraint from an action.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Keep away from' = avoid → self-restraint → similar concept → but 'keep from + V-ing' is correct structure!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "KEEP FROM = KEEP YOURSELF FROM DOING!" — 'Keep from' = keep yourself AWAY FROM an action. 'Keep from beating' = keep yourself from beating. FROM separates you from the action!
• KEEP family: KEEP FROM (self-restraint) | KEEP DOWN (suppress) | KEEP BACK (hold back) | KEEP AWAY (stay distant) | KEEP UP (maintain)
• PSC exam strategy: Context = teacher couldn't stop herself from beating = self-restraint. 'Keep from + V-ing' = cannot stop oneself = B! Whenever 'couldn't stop doing' type context → 'keep from'!

questionText: The correctly spelt word is

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Memento

• 'Memento' = a souvenir or keepsake — 'ഓർമ്മ വസ്തു, ഓർമ്മസ്മാരകം.'
• Correct spelling: M-E-M-E-N-T-O (7 letters)
• Key: 'mem' + 'ento' — mem (like memory) + ento.
• Common errors: (1) 'momento' — wrong 'o' instead of 'e' after 'm'; (2) 'memanto' — 'a' instead of 'e' in middle; (3) 'momanto' — 'o' instead of 'e' twice!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Correct: M-E-M-E-N-T-O
• Etymology: Latin 'memento' = 'remember!' (imperative form of 'meminisse' — to remember)
• Memory-meaning connection: Memento = remember → 'mem' = related to 'memory'!
• Common spelling traps:
  - Momento ❌ — 'moment' confusion: moment (mo-ment) ≠ memento (me-ment-o)
  - Memanto ❌ — 'a' instead of second 'e'
  - Momanto ❌ — 'o' and 'a' both wrong
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Commonly misspelled words — 'memento' vs 'momento' confusion PSC-ൽ appeared!
• Word family:
  - Memento (noun) = keepsake, souvenir
  - Memory (same Latin root — 'mem')
  - Memorabilia = memorable items (same root)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Momento
• 'Momento' — students confuse with 'moment' → add 'o' → 'momento'. But the word has no relation to 'moment'!
• Correct: M**e**mento, not M**o**mento. First vowel = 'e', not 'o'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Momento' = most common misspelling in the world! 'Moment' feeling → 'momento'. Root is 'mem' (memory), not 'mom/moment'!

❌ (B) Memanto
• 'Memanto' — middle 'e' changed to 'a': mem**a**nto. Correct = mem**e**nto.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: First 'mem' correct → students relax → make error in middle: 'a' instead of 'e'!

❌ (D) Momanto
• 'Momanto' — both first and second vowels wrong: m**o**m**a**nto instead of m**e**m**e**nto.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Random combination of wrong vowels — designed to confuse students who aren't sure of exact spelling!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "MEMENTO = MEMORY OBJECT!" — Memento comes from 'memory' — same 'MEM' start! M-E-M-E-N-T-O. Both vowels after 'M' are 'E' not 'O' or 'A'! MEM-E-NTO: mem (memory) + e + nto!
• Spelling mantra: MEM + ENTO = MEMENTO (not momento, not momanto).
• PSC spelling tip: 'Memento' = ഓർക്കാൻ ഉദ്ദേശിക്കുന്ന item → 'MEM' = memory! 'E' after mem, not 'O'. Movie 'Memento' (2000) — about memory loss! Same word!

questionText: The court observed the agreement null and void. Here 'null and void' means

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Invalid

• 'Null and void' = having no legal force or effect; invalid — 'നിയമപ്രകാരം ഫലരഹിതം, ബലരഹിതം, അസാധുവായ.'
• Legal term: 'null' = having no value; 'void' = empty of legal effect → together = completely without legal validity.
• "The court declared the agreement null and void" = "Agreement അസാധുവും ഫലശൂന്യവും ആണ് എന്ന് കോടതി പ്രഖ്യാപിച്ചു."
• 'Invalid' = not legally valid — exact meaning of 'null and void'!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Null and void = Invalid = Having no legal effect
• Legal idioms family (PSC high-value):
  - Null and void = invalid, having no legal effect
  - Bona fide = genuine, done in good faith
  - Pro bono = professional work done without charge
  - Sub judice = under judicial consideration
  - Prima facie = at first appearance/sight
• Synonyms of 'null and void': invalid / ineffective / cancelled / unenforceable
• Antonyms: valid / operative / legitimate / enforceable
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Legal idioms and terms PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ asked!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The contract was declared null and void by the judge. (invalid)
  2. Without a signature, the document is null and void. (has no legal value)
  3. The new law rendered the old agreement null and void. (cancelled)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Operative
• 'Operative' = in force, effective, working — OPPOSITE of 'null and void'!
• 'Operative' = valid and in effect; 'null and void' = no longer in effect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Operative' sounds legal/official → students may pick it for a legal context — it's the exact antonym!

❌ (C) Authentic
• 'Authentic' = genuine, real — not related to legal validity.
• Something can be 'authentic' (genuine document) but still 'null and void' (legally ineffective).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Court + agreement → authentic sounds right → but 'null and void' = legally invalid, not about authenticity!

❌ (D) Legitimate
• 'Legitimate' = lawful, valid — again OPPOSITE of 'null and void' (which means NOT legitimate).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Legal context → 'legitimate' feels right → but 'null and void' = the opposite of legitimate!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "NULL = ZERO, VOID = EMPTY → ZERO LEGAL VALUE!" — Null = zero (like null set in maths = empty set). Void = vacuum/empty. Null and void = zero + empty = NOTHING legally! = INVALID!
• Legal idiom trio: null and void (invalid) | operative (valid) | sub judice (under court) — 3 legal terms for PSC!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Null and void' = court/legal = no effect. Options A (operative), C (authentic), D (legitimate) = positive/valid meanings. INVALID = B = opposite = correct! Legal negative = INVALID!

questionText: Pick out the word for a person who does not believe in and is strongly against accepted ideas, traditions, images etc.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Iconoclast

• 'Iconoclast' = a person who attacks or criticizes cherished beliefs, traditional institutions, or sacred images — 'പരമ്പരാഗത ആചാരങ്ങളെ/വിശ്വാസങ്ങളെ ശക്തമായി എതിർക്കുന്ന വ്യക്തി.'
• Etymology: Greek 'eikon' (image/icon) + 'klastes' (breaker) = image-breaker → extended meaning: one who breaks traditions/beliefs.
• "She is an iconoclast who challenges every convention" = "ഓരോ ആചാരത്തെയും ചോദ്യം ചെയ്യുന്ന iconoclast ആണ് അവൾ."
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Iconoclast = One who attacks traditions/sacred beliefs/images
• Word etymology: Greek 'ikon' (image) + 'klao' (break) = breaks images → metaphorically = breaks traditions
• 'Crat' words (leaders/rulers):
  - Autocrat = ruler with absolute power (auto = self, crat = power)
  - Democrat = believer in democracy
  - Aristocrat = member of nobility
• Other '-clast' words:
  - Iconoclast = tradition-breaker
  - Osteoclast = cell that breaks down bone (medical)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One-word substitutes — especially from Greek/Latin roots — PSC-ൽ popular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The artist was an iconoclast who rejected all classical forms. (tradition-breaker)
  2. His iconoclastic views shocked the conservatives. (attacking traditions)
  3. Iconoclasts often face resistance from traditionalists. (challengers of norms)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Autocrat
• 'Autocrat' = a ruler who has complete, unchecked power — 'സ്വേഛാധിപതി'.
• Power/ruling-ആണ് autocrat-ന്റെ context — beliefs/traditions attack ചെയ്യൽ അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Auto' = self, 'crat' = power → absolute self-rule. Nothing to do with attacking traditions!

❌ (C) Diplomat
• 'Diplomat' = a skilled person representing their country in foreign affairs — 'നയതന്ത്രജ്ഞൻ.'
• Diplomats are tactful/careful — opposite personality to an iconoclast!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Diplomat' = a known word → students pick familiar words → but meaning is entirely different!

❌ (D) Agnostic
• 'Agnostic' = a person who believes that the existence of God cannot be known — 'ദൈവാസ്തിത്വം അജ്ഞേയം എന്ന് കരുതുന്ന വ്യക്തി.'
• 'Not believe' in God context → students pick 'agnostic'. But definition says 'strongly against traditions/images' = iconoclast!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Does not believe' phrase in definition → 'agnostic' (who doesn't believe in God) → but iconoclast = against ALL traditions, not just religion!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "ICONOCLAST = ICON SMASHER!" — 'Icon' = image/idol. 'Clast' = breaker/smasher. Iconoclast = smashes icons and traditions! Think of a rebel who smashes all the old statues and says 'these traditions are wrong!' = ICONOCLAST!
• '-clast' root: anything with '-clast' = breaker. Iconoclast = icon-breaker = tradition-smasher!
• PSC one-word strategy: Read definition carefully — 'strongly AGAINST accepted ideas/traditions' → radical/rebel person → ICONOCLAST. 'Agnostic' = about GOD only. 'Autocrat' = about POWER. 'Iconoclast' = about TRADITIONS!

questionText:
Change into reported speech.
He said, "will you listen to such an advice ?"
optionA: He asked whether they would have listened to such an advice
optionB: He asked whether they would listened to such an advice
optionC: He asked whether they had listened to such an advice
optionD: He asked whether they would listen to such an advice

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) He asked whether they would listen to such an advice

• Direct: He said, "will you listen to such an advice?" = Yes/No question.
• Changes:
  (1) 'said' + question → 'asked'
  (2) Yes/No question → 'whether' / 'if'
  (3) 'you' → 'they' (pronoun change)
  (4) 'will' → 'would' (tense backshift)
  (5) 'listen' remains base form after 'would'
  (6) Word order: normal S+V (they would listen), not inverted
• Correct: "He asked whether **they would listen** to such an advice." ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Yes/No Question Indirect Speech Formula:
  asked + whether/if + subject + backshifted verb (normal order)
• Yes/No question changes:
  said → asked
  will → would (backshift)
  you → they/he/she (pronoun change)
  'whether' or 'if' added before subject
  Question mark (?) removed
  Inverted order → Normal S+V order
• 'Will + V1' → 'would + V1' (base form stays the same after modal)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Yes/No question indirect speech — 'whether/if' pattern — PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Will you come?' → asked whether she **would come**. (will→would)
  2. 'Can you help?' → asked whether they **could help**. (can→could)
  3. 'Do you know?' → asked whether they **knew**. (do know→knew)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He asked whether they would have listened to such an advice
• 'Would have listened' = conditional perfect — 'will listen' → backshift = 'would listen', NOT 'would have listened'.
• 'Would have' = Third Conditional or Perfect Modal — here backshift of 'will' = simply 'would'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would have' looks like proper backshift → actually 'would + base form' = correct backshift of 'will'!

❌ (B) He asked whether they would listened to such an advice
• 'Would listened' — after modal 'would', base form needed, not past form!
• 'Would + V1 (base)' correct: 'would listen'. 'Would listened' = grammatically wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students backshift 'listen' to 'listened' thinking it needs past form — modal + base verb (not past form)!

❌ (C) He asked whether they had listened to such an advice
• 'Had listened' = Past Perfect — 'will listen' backshift = 'would listen', not 'had listened'.
• 'Had listened' = backshift of 'have listened' (Present Perfect), not 'will listen' (Simple Future).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had' = past = backshift → confuse ആകും — 'will' backshift = 'would', not 'had'!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "WILL → WOULD, LISTEN STAYS!" — 'Will listen' in indirect = 'would listen'. 'Would' = backshift of 'will'. 'Listen' = base form, stays base form after modal! Would + LISTEN (base), not listened, not have listened!
• Yes/No indirect speech: (1) asked + whether (2) you→they (3) will→would (4) listen stays base form (5) remove ?
• PSC exam strategy: Yes/No question indirect = 'asked whether + normal order'. 'Will you' → 'whether they would'. Base form after modal = LISTEN (not listened, not have listened). D = correct!

questionText: Expand "UPI".

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Unified Payments Interface

• UPI = **U**nified **P**ayments **I**nterface — ഇന്ത്യയുടെ real-time digital payment system.
• NPCI (National Payments Corporation of India) 2016-ൽ launch ചെയ്തു.
• UPI = phone-ൽ നിന്ന് directly bank account-to-account transfer ചെയ്യാൻ ഉതകുന്ന interface.
• Google Pay, PhonePe, Paytm, BHIM — ഒക്കെ UPI platform-ൽ പ്രവർത്തിക്കുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 UPI = Unified Payments Interface
• Technology/Finance abbreviations (PSC current affairs):
  - UPI = Unified Payments Interface (digital payments)
  - NPCI = National Payments Corporation of India
  - BHIM = Bharat Interface for Money (UPI app)
  - NEFT = National Electronic Funds Transfer
  - RTGS = Real Time Gross Settlement
  - IMPS = Immediate Payment Service
• UPI key facts:
  Launched: 2016 by NPCI
  Regulated by: RBI
  Works on: mobile phones via Virtual Payment Address (VPA)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Banking/Technology abbreviations PSC-ൽ current affairs section-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Pay using UPI — scan the QR code. (Unified Payments Interface)
  2. India's UPI transactions crossed 10 billion per month. (digital payment system)
  3. UPI has revolutionized cashless payments in India. (instant bank transfer)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) United Powered Institution
• 'United Powered Institution' = fabricated expansion — ഇങ്ങനെ ഒരു institution ഇല്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: U=United, P=Power..., I=Institution → students guess based on letters → random wrong expansion!

❌ (C) Unified Programme Implementation
• 'Unified Programme Implementation' = sounds government-y but is incorrect.
• 'P' in UPI = Payments, not 'Programme'. 'I' = Interface, not 'Implementation'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Unified' (U) correct start → students think 'Programme Implementation' = government scheme → wrong!

❌ (D) United Payments Investment
• 'United Payments Investment' — 'U' should be 'Unified' not 'United', and 'I' = Interface not 'Investment'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Payments' correct in middle → students feel this is right → but U=Unified (not United), I=Interface (not Investment)!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: "UPI = UNIFIED PAYMENT INSTANTLY!" — UPI = Unified Payments Interface. Think of UPI as a UNIFIED (combined) system that INTERFACES (connects) all PAYMENT systems. One interface to pay everywhere!
• Digital payment abbreviations to know: UPI (Unified Payments Interface) | NEFT (National Electronic Funds Transfer) | RTGS (Real Time Gross Settlement) | IMPS (Immediate Payment Service)
• PSC current affairs tip: Technology/banking abbreviations ഓർക്കാൻ: Use the service daily (GPay/PhonePe) = UPI = Unified Payments Interface. Daily life connection = never forget!

questionText:
Find the option that is closest in meaning to the underlined word.
Modern technology makes it easier than ever to __eavesdrop__ on electronic communication.
optionA: To communicate with diverse groups simultaneously
optionB: To stay away from a confidential discussion
optionC: To give some space for private conversation
optionD: To listen secretly to a private conversation

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) To listen secretly to a private conversation

• 'Eavesdrop' എന്ന വാക്കിന്റെ അർത്ഥം — മറ്റുള്ളവരുടെ സ്വകാര്യ സംഭാഷണം ഒളിഞ്ഞുനോക്കി/ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേൾക്കുക എന്നതാണ്.
• ഈ sentence-ൽ 'modern technology makes it easier to eavesdrop on electronic communication' — ഇലക്ട്രോണിക് ആശയവിനിമയം ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേൾക്കാൻ technology സഹായിക്കുന്നു എന്നാണ് ഉദ്ദേശം.
• Option D — 'To listen secretly to a private conversation' — ഇത് eavesdrop-ന്റെ exact meaning ആണ്.
• 'Secret listening' — ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേൾക്കൽ — ഇതാണ് eavesdrop എന്ന വാക്കിന്റെ core meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Eavesdrop' = Listen secretly to a private conversation without permission
• 'Eavesdrop' എന്ന വാക്ക് Old English-ൽ നിന്നു വന്നതാണ് — 'eaves' (മേൽക്കൂരയുടെ അറ്റം) + 'drop' — ആരോ വീടിന്റെ eaves-ന്റെ അടിയിൽ നിന്ന് അകത്തെ സംഭാഷണം കേൾക്കുന്ന image-ൽ നിന്നാണ് ഈ വാക്ക് ഉണ്ടായത്.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Vocabulary synonyms — PSC-യിൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ഉണ്ടാകുന്ന topic ആണ്.
• Etymology hint: Eaves (വീടിന്റെ overhanging roof) + Drop (fall) → secretly listen near the eaves
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She eavesdropped on her neighbors' conversation through the wall. (അവൾ ചുമരിലൂടെ അയൽക്കാരുടെ സംഭാഷണം ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേട്ടു.)
  2. It is wrong to eavesdrop on someone's phone calls. (ആരുടെയെങ്കിലും phone calls ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേൾക്കുന്നത് തെറ്റാണ്.)
  3. The spy eavesdropped on the secret meeting. (ഗൂഢാലോചനകൾ ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേൾക്കാൻ spy ശ്രമിച്ചു.)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) To communicate with diverse groups simultaneously — ഇത് 'eavesdrop'-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല. 'Communicate' (ആശയവിനിമയം നടത്തുക) — active action ആണ്, eavesdrop passive/secretive action ആണ്. PSC trap: 'electronic communication' കണ്ടിട്ട് communication-related option തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കുന്നത് ഒരു classic mistake ആണ്.

❌ (B) To stay away from a confidential discussion — 'Stay away' (അകലം പാലിക്കുക) — ഇത് opposite direction ആണ്. Eavesdrop ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ആൾ close ആകുന്നു, away ആകുന്നില്ല. ഈ option confuse ചെയ്യാൻ 'confidential' (രഹസ്യ) എന്ന word ഉപയോഗിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നു.

❌ (C) To give some space for private conversation — ഇതും opposite ആണ്. 'Give space for privacy' — privacy കൊടുക്കുക — ഇതല്ല eavesdrop. Eavesdrop ചെയ്യുന്നത് privacy invade ചെയ്യലാണ്, protect ചെയ്യലല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EAVES-DROP' — EAVES (മേൽക്കൂര) + DROP — കഴിഞ്ഞ കാലത്ത് ആൾക്കാർ വീടിന്റെ eaves-ന്റെ ചുവടെ നിന്ന് roof-ൽ നിന്നു വീഴുന്ന water drops-ൽ ഒളിഞ്ഞ് അകത്തെ സംഭാഷണം കേൾക്കുമായിരുന്നു — ഈ image ഓർത്തിരിക്കൂ!
• Word family: eavesdrop (verb) → eavesdropper (noun, ഒളിഞ്ഞുകേൾക്കുന്ന ആൾ) → eavesdropping (gerund)
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym question-ൽ underlined word-ന്റെ Malayalam meaning ആദ്യം മനസ്സിൽ ഉറപ്പിക്കുക. പിന്നെ options-ൽ നിന്ന് ആ meaning-ൽ ഏറ്റവും അടുത്ത് നിൽക്കുന്ന ഒന്ന് തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കുക.

questionText:
Choose the suitable relative pronoun from the options given.
Do you remember the passenger ________ travelled yesterday without a ticket?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) who

• 'The passenger who travelled yesterday without a ticket' — ഇവിടെ 'the passenger' ആണ് relative clause-ലെ subject. Passenger 'travelled' — ഈ action-ന്റെ doer ആണ് passenger.
• Subject position-ൽ ഉള്ള relative pronoun: Person → 'who'; Thing → 'which'
• 'Who' — person-നെ refer ചെയ്യാൻ, subject position-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന relative pronoun ആണ്.
• 'Do you remember the passenger who travelled yesterday without a ticket?' — 'ഇന്നലെ ticket ഇല്ലാതെ യാത്ര ചെയ്ത യാത്രക്കാരനെ നിനക്ക് ഓർമ്മയുണ്ടോ?' എന്നാണ് meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Person + Subject position = WHO | Person + Object position = WHOM | Possession = WHOSE | Thing/Animal = WHICH
• Relative pronouns ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന വിധം:
  - WHO: person, subject of relative clause ആകുമ്പോൾ
  - WHOM: person, object of relative clause ആകുമ്പോൾ
  - WHOSE: possession കാണിക്കാൻ (person/thing)
  - WHICH: thing or animal, subject/object ആകുമ്പോൾ
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Relative pronouns — Kerala PSC-യിൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The teacher who teaches us Maths is very kind. (Subject: teacher = who)
  2. The boy whom I met yesterday is my cousin. (Object: boy = whom)
  3. The book which is on the table is mine. (Thing: book = which)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) whom — 'Whom' person-നെ refer ചെയ്യുന്ന relative pronoun ആണ്, പക്ഷേ ഇത് object position-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ടതാണ്. ഈ sentence-ൽ passenger ആണ് 'travelled'-ന്റെ subject — object അല്ല. 'Whom' ഇവിടെ grammatically incorrect ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'whom' sophisticated ആണെന്ന് തോന്നി students ഇത് തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കും.

❌ (C) whose — 'Whose' possession കാണിക്കാൻ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'The passenger whose ticket was lost' — ഇതുപോലെ possession ഉള്ളിടത്ത് 'whose' വരും. ഇവിടെ possession ഒന്നും ഇല്ല, action (travelled) ആണ് ഉള്ളത്.

❌ (D) which — 'Which' things/animals-നെ refer ചെയ്യാൻ ഉള്ളതാണ്. 'Passenger' ഒരു person ആണ്, thing അല്ല. Person-നെ refer ചെയ്യാൻ 'who' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'whom' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം, 'which' ഒരിക്കലും persons-നു ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: WHO = Subject (S), WHOM = Object (O) — 'WHO does the action, WHOM receives the action' — ഇത് ഓർത്തിരിക്കൂ!
• Quick test: blank-ന്റെ ശേഷം verb directly വന്നാൽ → WHO (subject). Blank-ന്റെ ശേഷം subject + verb ആണെങ്കിൽ → WHOM (object).
• PSC exam strategy: First decide — is the antecedent a person or thing? Person → who/whom. Then decide — is it subject or object? Subject → who. Object → whom. ഈ രണ്ടു ചോദ്യം ചോദിച്ചാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the superlative form of the adjective from the options.
Martin ate the ________ amount of shawarma at the party, preferring to snack on vegetables.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) least

• 'Little → Less → Least' — ഇതാണ് ഈ adjective-ന്റെ three degrees of comparison.
• Sentence-ൽ 'the ________ amount' — definite article 'the' ഉള്ളതും, party-യിൽ ആരെയെക്കാൾ കുറവ് കഴിച്ചു എന്ന comparison ഉള്ളതും superlative ആണ് ആവശ്യം എന്ന് കാണിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Least' = ഏറ്റവും കുറഞ്ഞ — superlative degree of 'little'
• Martin party-യിൽ ഏറ്റവും കുറച്ചു shawarma കഴിച്ചു, vegetables ആണ് prefer ചെയ്തത് എന്നാണ് sentence-ന്റെ meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Little → Less → Least (Positive → Comparative → Superlative)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Degrees of comparison — PSC-യിൽ frequently ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• Important irregular degrees:
  - Good → Better → Best
  - Bad → Worse → Worst
  - Little → Less → Least
  - Much/Many → More → Most
  - Far → Farther/Further → Farthest/Furthest
• Superlative ഉപയോഗിക്കുമ്പോൾ 'the' ഉണ്ടാകും — 'the best', 'the least', 'the most'
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She ate the least amount of food at the dinner. (ഏറ്റവും കുറഞ്ഞ ഭക്ഷണം കഴിച്ചത് അവളാണ്.)
  2. He has the least experience in the team. (team-ൽ experience ഏറ്റവും കുറഞ്ഞത് അവനാണ്.)
  3. This is the least expensive option available. (ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും കുറഞ്ഞ ചെലവുള്ള option.)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) little — ഇത് Positive degree ആണ്. Superlative ആവശ്യമുള്ള place-ൽ positive degree ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല. 'The little amount' grammatically unnatural ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'little' familiar word ആയതിനാൽ students ഇത് select ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) less — ഇത് Comparative degree ആണ് — രണ്ടു കാര്യങ്ങൾ compare ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. Party-യിൽ (multiple people among whom Martin ate least) superlative വേണം. 'Less' = two things compare ചെയ്യൽ; 'Least' = more than two compare ചെയ്യൽ.

❌ (C) lest — 'Lest' ഒരു conjunction ആണ്, adjective-ന്റെ degree അല്ല. 'Study hard lest you should fail' (fail ആകാതിരിക്കാൻ കഠിനമായി പഠിക്കൂ) — ഇതുപോലെ purpose clause-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'Lest' never 'least'-ന്റെ variant അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Little-Less-Least' — L-L-L pattern ഓർക്കൂ! Positive-Comparative-Superlative — Little (ഒരു അളവ്), Less (കൂടുതൽ ആളുകളിൽ 2 compare ചെയ്യൽ), Least (3+ ആളുകളിൽ ഏറ്റവും കുറഞ്ഞത്).
• 'The' article കണ്ടാൽ → superlative degree ആണ് blank-ൽ വേണ്ടത് — ഇത് ഒരു guaranteed shortcut!
• PSC exam strategy: Irregular comparative/superlative forms (good-better-best, bad-worse-worst, little-less-least, many-more-most) ഒരു list ഉണ്ടാക്കി heart-ൽ ഇടുക — ഇവ PSC-യിൽ guaranteed questions ആണ്.

questionText:
Identify the meaning of the idiom underlined.
Pursuing a graduate degree from a reputable institution these days can cost __an arm and a leg__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരிയുത്തരം: (C) Expensive

• 'Cost an arm and a leg' — ഈ idiom-ന്റെ അർത്ഥം 'വളരെ വില കൂടിയതാണ്' / 'extremely expensive' എന്നാണ്.
• 'Pursuing a graduate degree from a reputable institution these days can cost an arm and a leg' — ഒരു നല്ല institution-ൽ graduate degree ചെയ്യുന്നത് ഇക്കാലത്ത് വളരെ ചെലവേറിയ ഒന്നായി മാറിയിരിക്കുന്നു.
• Idiom-ന്റെ origin: 'An arm and a leg' — ശരീരത്തിലെ ഏറ്റവും വിലപ്പെട്ട ഭാഗങ്ങൾ — arm ഉം leg ഉം കൊടുക്കേണ്ടി വരുന്ന വില അർത്ഥത്തിൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Answer: Expensive — ഇതാണ് 'cost an arm and a leg' idiom-ന്റെ exact synonym.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Cost an arm and a leg' = Very expensive / Costs a lot of money
• Body part idioms — PSC-യിൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ചോദിക്കുന്ന idiom category:
  - Cost an arm and a leg → Very expensive
  - Keep an eye on → Watch carefully (ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക)
  - Turn a blind eye → Ignore deliberately (ബോധപൂർവം അവഗണിക്കുക)
  - Bite the bullet → Endure pain bravely (ധൈര്യത്തോടെ സഹിക്കുക)
  - Have a heart of gold → Very kind person (വളരെ ദയാലുവായ ആൾ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Body part idioms — almost every PSC paper-ൽ ഉണ്ടാകും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A good smartphone these days can cost an arm and a leg. (ഒരു നല്ല smartphone ഇന്ന് വളരെ ചെലവേറിയതാണ്.)
  2. The car repairs cost an arm and a leg. (Car repair-ന് വളരെ പണം ചെലവായി.)
  3. Traveling abroad can cost an arm and a leg. (വിദേശ യാത്ര വളരെ ചെലവേറിയ ഒന്നാണ്.)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Affordable — 'Affordable' = വിലക്ക് ഒത്ത, ചെലവുകൊള്ളാവുന്ന. 'Cost an arm and a leg'-ന്റെ exactly opposite meaning ആണിത്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'cost' word കണ്ടിട്ട് positively affordable ആണെന്ന് think ചെയ്ത് ഈ option select ചെയ്യുന്ന students ഉണ്ടാകും.

❌ (B) Economical — 'Economical' = ചെലവ് കുറഞ്ഞ, money save ചെയ്യുന്ന. ഇതും opposite meaning ആണ്. 'An arm and a leg' — ഏറ്റവും valuable items sacrifice ചെയ്യേണ്ട situation describe ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്, saving അല്ല.

❌ (D) Inexpensive — 'Inexpensive' = cheap, not expensive. ഇതും directly opposite ആണ്. 'Cost an arm and a leg' always negative — high cost indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന idiom ആണ്. 'In-' prefix 'not' meaning കൊടുക്കുന്നതിനാൽ inexpensive = not expensive = wrong answer.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ARM + LEG' — ഒരാൾ ഏറ്റവും price ആയ body parts — arm ഉം leg ഉം — കൊടുക്കേണ്ടി വരുന്ന വില = EXTREMELY EXPENSIVE. ഈ visual image ഓർത്തിരിക്കൂ!
• Body part idioms list (PSC favourites): cost an arm and a leg (very expensive), keep an eye on (watch), pull someone's leg (joke), turn a blind eye (ignore), have cold feet (feel nervous)
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom question-ൽ options-ൽ two opposite meanings ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ (expensive vs affordable) idiom positive ആണോ negative ആണോ എന്ന് ആദ്യം decide ചെയ്യൂ — ഇത് 50% chance guarantee ചെയ്യും!

questionText: Which one of the following words is an example of a compound word consisting of a verb + adverb pattern?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Breakthrough

• 'Breakthrough' = 'Break' (verb) + 'through' (adverb) — ഇതാണ് verb + adverb pattern-ൽ ഉള്ള compound word.
• 'Break' — ഒരു verb ആണ്. 'Through' — ഒരു adverb ആണ് (direction/movement indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന).
