• 'Inappropriate' = ഉചിതമല്ലാത്ത — ഇതാണ് exact antonym.
• 'Inappropriate behaviour', 'inappropriate language' — improper/unsuitable.
• 'In-' prefix negation: in + appropriate = inappropriate.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Appropriate → Inappropriate (in- prefix = not)
• Prefix negation patterns:
  - in-: appropriate → inappropriate, correct → incorrect, formal → informal
  - un-: happy → unhappy, kind → unkind, fit → unfit
  - dis-: honest → dishonest, agree → disagree
  - im-: possible → impossible, patient → impatient
  - il-: legal → illegal, literate → illiterate
  - ir-: regular → irregular, relevant → irrelevant
• 'Misappropriate' = to use something wrongly/dishonestly (funds misappropriate ചെയ്യുക) — antonym അല്ല, different word ആണ്!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms with prefixes — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. His behaviour was inappropriate for the occasion. (ഉചിതമല്ലാത്ത)
  2. The dress was inappropriate for a formal meeting.
  3. Appropriate attire is required for the interview.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) misappropriate — 'Misappropriate' = to use money/resources dishonestly for wrong purpose (ദുരുപയോഗം). ഇത് 'appropriate' (adjective)-ൻ്റെ antonym അല്ല — 'misappropriate' verb ആണ്, different meaning entirely. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'mis' prefix 'wrong' indicate ചെയ്യും, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) disappropriate — 'Disappropriate' ഒരു standard English word അല്ല! ഇത് exist ചെയ്യുന്ന word അല്ല. 'dis-' + 'appropriate' combination English dictionary-ൽ ഇല്ല.

❌ (D) none of the above — Option C 'inappropriate' ശരിയായ antonym ആണ്. 'None of the above' wrong ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'APPROPRIATE → IN + APPROPRIATE' — 'IN' = NO! Appropriate-ൽ 'IN' ചേർത്താൽ = NOT appropriate = INAPPROPRIATE! Easy!
• Prefix rule: 'in-' + word = NOT that word. This is the most common negative prefix in PSC!
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ negative prefix (in-, un-, dis-, im-, il-, ir-) ഉള്ള options identify ചെയ്യൂ. 'Disappropriate' real word അല്ല → eliminate. 'Misappropriate' different meaning → eliminate. 'Inappropriate' = correct!

questionText:
In this sentence 'walking' is used as:
Walking is a good exercise.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) noun

• 'Walking is a good exercise' — 'Walking' ഈ sentence-ൻ്റെ Subject ആണ്.
• Subject position-ൽ ഉള്ള word → Noun function ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'Walking' = Gerund — verb-ൻ്റെ '-ing' form noun ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്.
• Gerund = verb + ing, but functions as a NOUN.
• 'Walking is good' — 'is' ൻ്റെ subject = walking → noun.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: V + ing used as subject/object → GERUND → functions as NOUN
• Gerund uses (noun function):
  - Subject: Walking is healthy. (Subject)
  - Object: I enjoy swimming. (Object)
  - After preposition: She is good at cooking. (after 'at')
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Gerunds (verb-ing as noun) — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന topic!
• Gerund vs Present Participle (same form, different function):
  - Walking is healthy. (Gerund = Noun → subject)
  - She is walking. (Present Participle = part of continuous tense → verb)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Swimming is my favourite sport. (Gerund = subject → noun)
  2. Reading improves vocabulary. (Gerund = subject → noun)
  3. She loves dancing. (Gerund = object → noun)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) verb — 'Walking' ഇവിടെ verb function ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല. Verb ആകണമെങ്കിൽ auxiliary 'is' ആവശ്യം (She is walking). ഇവിടെ 'walking' subject ആണ്, verb ആകുന്നത് 'is'. 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ing' form കണ്ടിട്ട് 'verb' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) adverb — Adverb verb/adjective/other adverb-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'Walking' ഒന്നിനെയും modify ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല — subject function ചെയ്യുന്നു. 'Walking' adverb ആകില്ല.

❌ (C) adjective — Adjective noun-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'Walking is a good exercise' — 'walking' ഒരു noun-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല, itself subject (noun function) ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GERUND = VERB + ING WEARING NOUN MASK!' — Walking, swimming, reading = verbs wearing noun masks as subjects! '-ing' form as SUBJECT = always NOUN!
• Quick test: '-ing' word subject position-ൽ ആണോ? (sentence-ൻ്റെ beginning-ൽ, 'is/are/was' ൻ്റെ മുന്നിൽ) → NOUN (Gerund)!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Walking IS a good exercise' — 'is' ൻ്റെ subject find ചെയ്യൂ → 'walking' = subject → noun function. Subject = Noun, always!

questionText:
Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
If we had rehearsed well we __________.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) would have won the competition

• 'If we had rehearsed well' — if-clause-ൽ 'had rehearsed' = Past Perfect → Third Conditional.
• Third Conditional formula: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3
• 'Had rehearsed' → main clause: 'would have won' (would have + won = V3 of win)
• Meaning: ഞങ്ങൾ നന്നായി rehearse ചെയ്തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ competition ജയിച്ചേനേ — but we didn't rehearse well (past unreal).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (Third Conditional)
• Three Conditional types:
  - Type 1 (Real): If it rains → we will cancel
  - Type 2 (Imaginary present): If I had money → I would buy
  - Type 3 (Past unreal): If I had studied → I would have passed
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If she had applied, she would have got the job. (apply ചെയ്തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ)
  2. If they had practised, they would have won. (practice ചെയ്തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ)
  3. If he had left early, he would have caught the train.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) would win — 'Would win' = Type 2 Conditional main clause ആണ്. Type 2-ൻ്റെ if-clause simple past ആണ് (If we rehearsed). ഇവിടെ if-clause past perfect (had rehearsed) ആണ് → Type 3 → 'would have won' ആണ് ആവശ്യം. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'would' കണ്ടിട്ട് Type 2 ഉം Type 3 ഉം confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) will win — 'Will win' = Type 1 Conditional main clause (future). Type 1-ൻ്റെ if-clause Simple Present ആണ്. ഇവിടെ Past Perfect if-clause ആണ്, Future main clause wrong ആണ്.

❌ (D) would have win — 'Would have win' = grammatically wrong! 'Would have' ൻ്റെ ശേഷം V3 (past participle) ആണ് ആവശ്യം. 'Win' base form ആണ്, V3 'won' ആണ്. 'Would have won' ആണ് ശരി.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAD in IF clause → WOULD HAVE in main clause!' If clause-ൽ 'HAD' കണ്ടാൽ → main clause-ൽ 'WOULD HAVE' ആണ്. Period! HAD → WOULD HAVE!
• 'Would have' ൻ്റെ ശേഷം V3 (past participle) — 'won' not 'win'. Option D eliminate ചെയ്യൂ immediately!
• PSC exam strategy: If-clause-ൽ 'had + V3' = Third Conditional → main clause 'would have + V3'. ഈ pattern ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText: A person who does clever tricks which appear magical is called

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) conjurer

• 'Conjurer' (also 'conjuror') = a person who performs magic tricks that appear supernatural.
• 'Clever tricks which appear magical' → conjurer = magician, illusionist.
• 'Conjure' = magical trick perform ചെയ്യുക → 'conjurer' = ആ person.
• Magic show-ൽ cards disappear ചെയ്യുക, rabbits hat-ൽ നിന്ന് appear ആകുക — ഇവ conjurer ചെയ്യുന്ന tricks.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Magic tricks performer = Conjurer/Conjuror/Magician
• ⭐ One Word Substitutes (PSC favourites):
  - Conjurer/Magician — magic tricks performer
  - Curator — museum/art gallery-ൻ്റെ keeper/manager
  - Burglar — breaks into buildings to steal (house-ൽ കൊള്ളയടിക്കുന്ന ആൾ)
  - Ventriloquist — talks without moving lips, makes puppets 'speak'
  - Acrobat — performs gymnastic feats
  - Philanthropist — loves humanity, donates to charity
  - Misanthrope — hates humans
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One Word Substitutes — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The conjurer amazed the audience with his tricks. (audience-നെ tricks കൊണ്ട് amazed ആക്കി)
  2. The museum curator arranged the exhibits well.
  3. The burglar broke into the house at night.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) curator — 'Curator' = museum, art gallery, library-ൻ്റെ collection manage ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ. Magic tricks-ൻ്റെ context-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Curator' less familiar word ആണ്, students ഇത് 'conjurer' ആണ് ആകും എന്ന് confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) burglar — 'Burglar' = break ചെയ്ത് building-ൽ കേറി steal ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — criminal! Magic tricks-ൻ്റെ context-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.

❌ (D) none of the above — Option C 'conjurer' ശരി ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CONJURER = CONJURE + R' — 'Conjure' = summon by magic. Conjurer = one who conjures (magic summon ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ). 'CONJURE = MAGIC' — simple!
• Word family: conjure (verb) → conjurer/conjuror (person) → conjuring (activity) → conjuration (act)
• PSC exam strategy: One word substitute question-ൽ definition-ൻ്റെ KEY ACTION identify ചെയ്യൂ. 'Clever tricks that appear magical' = magic = conjurer. Direct!

questionText: Which among the following is an irregular verb?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) run

• 'Run' = irregular verb: run → ran → run (V1 → V2 → V3)
• Past tense 'ran' — regular pattern follow ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല ('runned' ആകില്ല!).
• Irregular verb = past tense/past participle form unpredictable ആയ verb (regular pattern follow ചെയ്യില്ല).
• 'Run → ran → run' — V2 'ran' ആണ്, 'runned' അല്ല → irregular.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Regular verb = V1 + ed (jumped, walked, moved). Irregular verb = special form (run→ran, go→went, write→wrote)
• Other options (regular verbs):
  - Jump → jumped → jumped (regular)
  - Move → moved → moved (regular)
  - Walk → walked → walked (regular)
• ⭐ Common irregular verbs (PSC favourites):
  - run → ran → run
  - go → went → gone
  - write → wrote → written
  - see → saw → seen
  - come → came → come
  - take → took → taken
  - give → gave → given
  - buy → bought → bought
  - teach → taught → taught
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Irregular verbs — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She ran to the bus stop. (run → ran)
  2. He jumped over the wall. (jump → jumped, regular)
  3. They walked to school. (walk → walked, regular)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) jump — 'Jump' regular verb ആണ്: jump → jumped → jumped. '-ed' ചേർക്കുന്ന pattern follow ചെയ്യുന്നു. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Run' ഉം 'jump' ഉം both physical actions ആണ്, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) move — 'Move' regular verb ആണ്: move → moved → moved. '-d' ചേർക്കുന്ന pattern (e-ൽ end ആകുന്ന verbs → just add 'd').

❌ (C) walk — 'Walk' regular verb ആണ്: walk → walked → walked. '-ed' standard pattern follow ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'RUN-RAN-RUN = RUN circles!' — Run → Ran → Run, same starting and ending, but middle 'ran' = ran away from regular pattern! Irregular!
• Quick test: V2 (past tense) = V1 + 'ed'? → Regular. V2 = different word entirely? → Irregular.
• PSC exam strategy: Irregular verb identify ചെയ്യാൻ — options-ൻ്റെ past tense form mentally ഉണ്ടാക്കൂ. 'Runned' sounds wrong → 'ran' → irregular = answer D!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using the right preposition.
There is a river ________ my house.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) beside

• 'Beside' = next to, at the side of (ഓരം ചേർന്ന്, സമീപം).
• 'There is a river beside my house' = എൻ്റെ വീടിൻ്റെ ഓരത്ത് ഒരു river ഉണ്ട്.
• Location/position indicate ചെയ്യാൻ 'beside' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Beside' vs 'besides': ഇവ രണ്ടും different meanings ഉള്ള words ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  - Beside = next to (location) — ഓരത്ത്
  - Besides = in addition to / moreover — കൂടാതെ
• Examples:
  - She sat beside me. (next to me — ഓരത്ത്)
  - Besides English, she knows French. (in addition to — കൂടാതെ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Beside vs Besides' — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് confuse ചെയ്യിക്കുന്ന pair!
• 'By' ഉം location indicate ചെയ്യും, പക്ഷേ 'beside' more specific for 'at the side of'. 'By the river' = near the river, 'beside the house' = next to the house — both acceptable sometimes, but 'beside' is more precise here.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The dog sat beside its owner. (ഓരത്ത്)
  2. Besides playing cricket, he studies well. (കൂടാതെ)
  3. She stood beside the door. (next to)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) by — 'By' = near, through, by means of. 'By my house' ഉം acceptable ആണ് (near my house), പക്ഷേ 'beside' more precise location (directly at the side) ആണ്. PSC context-ൽ 'beside' ആണ് correct answer. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'By the river' familiar phrase ആണ്.

❌ (C) besides — 'Besides' = in addition to (കൂടാതെ). Location sense ഇല്ല. 'There is a river besides my house' = meaning change — 'കൂടാതെ' sense ആകും, location sense ആകില്ല. Classic trap!

❌ (D) at — 'At' specific point location-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'At the river' (specific point), 'at home'. 'A river at my house' = slightly unnatural for 'beside' meaning.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BESIDE = BE + SIDE' — 'By the SIDE of' = BESIDE! 'Besides' = 'be + sides' = multiple sides = 'also/in addition to'. ONE side = beside (location). MULTIPLE sides = besides (addition)!
• Key difference: Beside (where?) = location. Besides (what else?) = addition.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Besides' ഉം 'Beside' ഉം options-ൽ ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ — location question ആണോ addition question ആണോ? Location = beside (no 's'). Addition = besides (with 's')!

questionText:
In this sentence 'august' as an adjective means:
The party was attended by an august group of people.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) impressive

• 'August' (adjective) = respected and impressive, dignified, venerable (ആദരണീയം, പ്രൗഢഗംഭീരം).
• 'An august group of people' = distinguished, respected, impressive group.
• ഇവിടെ 'August' = month (ഓഗസ്റ്റ്) അല്ല — adjective 'august' = impressive/respected ആണ്!
• 'August' adjective Latin origin: 'augustus' = dignified, majestic (as in Augustus Caesar).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: August (adjective) = Impressive + Respected + Dignified
• Etymology: Latin 'augustus' = consecrated, majestic → English 'august' = inspiring reverence
• Month August also named after Augustus Caesar (the 'august' emperor)!
• ⭐ PSC favourite: Words with dual meaning (month vs adjective)
• Synonyms of august (adjective): impressive, distinguished, dignified, venerable, respected, illustrious, grand
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Vocabulary with context-based meaning — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The august presence of the President silenced the room. (ആദരണീയ presence)
  2. An august body of scholars gathered for the conference.
  3. The august traditions of the institution were preserved.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) disinterested — 'Disinterested' = impartial, unbiased (personal benefit ഇല്ലാതെ fair ആയ). 'August' = impressive/dignified ആണ്, impartial അല്ല. Different meaning ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Disinterested' sophisticated word ആണ്, students august-ൻ്റെ meaning guess ചെയ്ത് ഇത് choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) ordinary — 'Ordinary' = common, not special. 'August' = OPPOSITE of ordinary — impressive, distinguished ആണ്. ഇത് antonym ആണ്, synonym അല്ല.

❌ (C) arrogant — 'Arrogant' = having an exaggerated sense of self-importance (അഹങ്കാരം). 'August' = respected by others (ആദരണീയം). Self-perception vs others' perception — different. August positive quality ആണ്, arrogant negative quality ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AUGUST = AUGUSTUS CAESAR = MAJESTIC EMPEROR!' — ആ majestic emperor-ൻ്റെ name-ൽ നിന്ന് August month ഉം august (adj) ഉം — both = impressive/majestic! Emperor-ൻ്റെ quality = august!
• Context clue: 'august GROUP' — positive/negative? Party attended by impressive people = positive → eliminate negative options (arrogant, ordinary)
• PSC exam strategy: Unknown adjective ആണ്? Context (positive/negative, formal/informal) identify ചെയ്ത് wrong options eliminate ചെയ്യൂ. 'August' = formal positive = 'impressive'!

questionText:
Complete the sentence using the right option.
No sooner had she left the house ________ it started raining.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) than

• 'No sooner...than' = fixed correlative conjunction pair (PSC favourite!).
• 'No sooner had she left the house than it started raining.'
• Meaning: അവൾ വീടു വിട്ടു, ഉടൻ മഴ തുടങ്ങി — as soon as she left, rain started.
• 'No sooner' always pairs with 'than' — never 'then' or 'when'!
• Structure: No sooner + had + subject + V3 → than + subject + past simple.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: No sooner...THAN (never 'then'!)
• ⭐ Correlative conjunctions (PSC guaranteed):
  - No sooner...than (ഉടൻ...ആകേ)
  - Hardly/Scarcely...when (barely...when)
  - Either...or (ഒന്നുകിൽ...അല്ലെങ്കിൽ)
  - Neither...nor (ഒന്നും ഇല്ല)
  - Both...and (രണ്ടും)
  - Not only...but also (മാത്രമല്ല...ഉം)
  - Lest...should (lest = so that...not)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: No sooner...than — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. No sooner had he arrived than it began to rain. (arrived = rain ആരംഭം)
  2. Hardly had she sat down when the phone rang. (sat = phone ring)
  3. Either you come or I will leave. (either...or)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) then — 'No sooner...then' = WRONG! ഇത് most common mistake ആണ്! 'No sooner' pair ആകുന്നത് 'than' ആണ്, 'then' (adverb of time) അല്ല. 'Then' = thereafter, next time — conjunction function ചെയ്യില്ല ഇവിടെ. 🪤 PSC classic trap — 'No sooner THEN' write ചെയ്യുന്ന students!

❌ (B) when — 'When' 'hardly/scarcely'-ൻ്റെ pair ആണ്: 'Hardly had she left when it rained.' 'No sooner' ൻ്റെ pair 'than' ആണ്, 'when' അല്ല. Mix ചെയ്യരുത்!

❌ (D) none of the above — Option C 'than' ശരിയാണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'NO SOONER = THAN, NOT THEN!' — 'Than' comparison ആണ്: 'sooner THAN' = timing comparison! Never 'then' (sequence)! Exam-ൽ 'No sooner' കണ്ടാൽ → immediately 'than' → guarantee mark!
• Pairs table:
  No sooner → THAN
  Hardly/Scarcely → WHEN
  Either → OR
  Neither → NOR
  Lest → SHOULD
• PSC exam strategy: Correlative conjunction question-ൽ first word identify ചെയ്ത് pair recall ചെയ്യൂ. 'No sooner' → 'than'. Automatic!

questionText: Which among the following is the correctly spelt word?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) business

• 'Business' = correct spelling: B-U-S-I-N-E-S-S
• 'Business' = commercial activity, trade, occupation (വ്യാപാരം, കച്ചവടം).
• Common mistake: 'buisiness' (ui reversed), 'bussiness' (double ss wrong place), 'bisiness' (u → i).
• Remember: BUS + I + NESS = BUSINESS. 'Bus' + 'i' + 'ness' ഉണ്ടാക്കൂ!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: B-U-S-I-N-E-S-S (8 letters, single 's' in middle, double 'ss' at end)
• Breaking it down: BUS + I + NESS → BUS-I-NESS → BUSINESS
• ⭐ Commonly misspelled words (PSC favourites):
  - business (not buisiness/bussiness)
  - necessary (1 c, 2 s: neCeSSary)
  - accommodation (2 c, 2 m: aCCoMMOdation)
  - occurrence (2 c, 2 r)
  - committee (2 m, 2 t, 2 e)
  - separate (not seperate — there's A RAT in sepARATe!)
  - privilege (not privalege)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She started a new business last year. (കഴിഞ്ഞ വർഷം business തുടങ്ങി)
  2. Business studies include economics and commerce.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) buisiness — 'ui' reversed (should be 'u-s', not 'u-i-s' at start). B-U-I-S-I-N-E-S-S = extra 'i' inserted wrongly. 🪤 PSC Trap: Quick glance-ൽ 'buisiness' ഉം 'business' ഉം similar look.

❌ (C) bussiness — Double 's' in wrong position — 'buss' + iness. Correct is single 's' in middle: bus + i + ness. Double 'ss' only at the END (business). 'Buss' = to kiss (informal) — wrong word base.

❌ (D) bisiness — 'u' → 'i' changed (bisiness instead of business). B-I-S-I-N-E-S-S = wrong. First vowel must be 'u', not 'i'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BUS + I + NESS = BUSINESS!' — Imagine a BUS carrying business people (I = me, NESS = quality). BUS-I-NESS! Or: 'It's none of your BUSiness!' — BUS inside BUSINESS!
• Double letter rule: 'business' → single 's' in middle, double 'ss' at end. busi-NESS (like KIND-NESS, DARK-NESS).
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling question-ൽ ആദ്യം clearly wrong options eliminate ചെയ്യൂ. ശേഷം remaining option standard spelling verify ചെയ്യൂ. 'Business' = familiar word, just spell ooze carefully!

questionText:
Change into passive voice.
They are following us.
optionB: we have been followed by them
optionC: we are being followed by them
optionD: we were being followed by them

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) we are being followed by them

• Active: 'They are following us' — Present Continuous tense.
• Present Continuous Passive formula: am/is/are + being + V3
• Subject change: 'us' → 'we' (object becomes subject in passive)
• Agent change: 'they' → 'by them'
• 'They are following us' → 'We are being followed by them'
• Tense: Present Continuous Active → Present Continuous Passive ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Present Continuous Passive = am/is/are + being + V3
• Passive voice formulas:
  - Simple Present: am/is/are + V3
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3
  - Present Continuous: am/is/are + being + V3
  - Past Continuous: was/were + being + V3
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3
  - Future: will be + V3
  - Imperative: Let + object + be + V3
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Passive Voice — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is reading the book. → The book is being read by her.
  2. They were watching us. → We were being watched by them.
  3. He is teaching the class. → The class is being taught by him.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) we are followed by them — 'Are followed' = Simple Present Passive ആണ്. Active sentence 'are following' = Present Continuous. Tense match ആകുന്നില്ല. 'Being' missing! 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Are followed' common passive form ആണ്, students ഇത് choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) we have been followed by them — 'Have been followed' = Present Perfect Passive ആണ്. Present Continuous Active-ൻ്റെ passive ആകില്ല. Completely different tense.

❌ (D) we were being followed by them — 'Were being' = Past Continuous Passive ആണ്. Active sentence present tense (are following), passive also present tense (are being) ആകണം. 'Were' past tense wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IS/ARE + BEING = CONTINUOUS PASSIVE!' — 'Being' കണ്ടാൽ = Continuous tense passive! Is being / are being / was being / were being = continuous passives!
• Active: They ARE FOLLOWING → Passive: We ARE BEING FOLLOWED. 'ARE' same, just add 'BEING' and V3!
• PSC exam strategy: Active tense identify ചെയ്ത് correct passive formula apply ചെയ്യൂ. 'Is/are + -ing' (present continuous) → 'is/are + being + V3' passive. Automatic!

questionText:
In this sentence 'Herculean task' stands for:
We have a Herculean task in front of us.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) a tough task

• 'Herculean' = requiring great strength or effort (ഹെർക്കുലീസിൻ്റെ ശക്തി ആവശ്യമുള്ള).
• 'Herculean task' = extremely difficult, demanding enormous effort.
• Origin: Greek mythology-ൽ Hercules (Heracles) — superhuman strength ഉള്ള hero. അദ്ദേഹം ചെയ്ത 'Twelve Labours' = extremely tough tasks.
• 'Herculean' = only extremely tough/powerful things describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Herculean = requiring superhuman effort = extremely tough
• ⭐ Mythology-based English words (PSC favourites):
  - Herculean — extremely powerful/difficult (Hercules)
  - Titanic — enormous (Titans)
  - Narcissistic — self-obsessed (Narcissus)
  - Achilles heel — fatal weakness (Achilles)
  - Midas touch — ability to turn everything to gold/success (King Midas)
  - Odyssey — long adventurous journey (Odysseus)
  - Pandora's box — source of great troubles (Pandora)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Mythology-based idioms/expressions — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Climbing Everest is a Herculean task. (ഏറ്റവും difficult task)
  2. Finishing the project in one day was a Herculean effort.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) an easy task — 'Easy' = Herculean-ൻ്റെ EXACT OPPOSITE! Herculean = superhuman effort ആവശ്യം. Easy = no effort needed. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Herculean' unknown ആണ്, students guess ചെയ്ത് easy/interesting choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) an interesting task — 'Interesting' = engaging, captivating. Herculean = difficulty level, not interest level. Task interesting ആകാം and still easy — 'Herculean' specifically difficulty ആണ് indicate ചെയ്യുന്നത്.

❌ (C) an uninteresting task — 'Uninteresting' = boring. Herculean boring ആണ് എന്ന് indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല — extremely difficult ആണ് indicate ചെയ്യുന്നത്. Difficulty ≠ Interest level.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HERCULES = MUSCLES!' — Hercules = strongest mythological hero. HERCULEAN = needs HERCULES muscles = TOUGH! Imagine Hercules lifting huge rocks — that's a Herculean task!
• Mythology word family: Herculean (tough), Titanic (enormous), Narcissistic (self-obsessed), Achilles heel (weakness) — ഈ words PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് come ആകും!
• PSC exam strategy: Mythology-based adjective question-ൽ character identify ചെയ്യൂ. Hercules = strength/difficulty. ഉടൻ D) tough task = answer!

questionText:
Which sentence among the following conveys the same meaning?
No other girl in this class is as studious as Nita.
optionA: All the girls in this class are as studious as Nita.
optionB: Nita is the most studious girl in this class.
optionC: All the girls in this class are more studious than Nita.
optionD: Some girls in this class are more studious than Nita.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Nita is the most studious girl in this class.

• 'No other girl is as studious as Nita' = Positive degree-ൽ ഉള്ള superlative meaning.
• ഇതിൻ്റെ meaning: Nita ആണ് class-ൽ ഏറ്റവും studious — nobody is equal to her.
• Superlative equivalent: 'Nita is the most studious girl in this class.'
• Degree of comparison equivalence:
  - Positive: No other X is as...as Nita.
  - Superlative: Nita is the most... (= same meaning)
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'No other...as...as X' = 'X is the most...' (Positive = Superlative equivalent)
• Degree transformation rules:
  - No other girl is as tall as Riya. (Positive)
  = Riya is the tallest girl. (Superlative)
  = Riya is taller than all other girls. (Comparative)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Degree of comparison equivalence — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. No other mountain is as high as Everest. = Everest is the highest mountain.
  2. No other player is as fast as him. = He is the fastest player.
  3. No other city is as beautiful as Paris. = Paris is the most beautiful city.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) All the girls in this class are as studious as Nita — ഇതിൻ്റെ meaning: everyone = Nita's level. Original meaning: nobody = Nita's level. Exact OPPOSITE! 🪤 PSC Trap: 'as studious as' keyword-ൽ confused ആകും.

❌ (C) All the girls in this class are more studious than Nita — ഇതിൻ്റെ meaning: everyone > Nita. Original: Nita > everyone. COMPLETE OPPOSITE!

❌ (D) Some girls in this class are more studious than Nita — ഇതിൻ്റെ meaning: some girls > Nita. Original: Nita > all girls. OPPOSITE again!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'NO OTHER = BEST!' — 'No other...as...as X' means X is BEST! No one can match → X is the top! 'No other girl = as studious as Nita' → Nita = MOST STUDIOUS!
• Transformation rule: Positive 'no other as X as Y' → Superlative 'Y is the most X'. Always!
• PSC exam strategy: Degree transformation question-ൽ original sentence-ൻ്റെ core meaning identify ചെയ്യൂ. 'No other...as...as' = superlative meaning → find 'most/best' option!

questionText:
Here 'pm' can be expanded as:
The train will arrive at 4 p.m.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) post meridiem

• 'p.m.' = 'post meridiem' — Latin expression.
• 'Post' = after (Latin). 'Meridiem' = midday/noon (Latin: 'meridies' = noon).
• 'Post meridiem' = after noon = afternoon/evening time.
• 'a.m.' = 'ante meridiem' = before noon (Latin: 'ante' = before).
• 4 p.m. = 4 in the afternoon = ഉച്ചതിരിഞ്ഞ് 4 മണി.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: p.m. = POST MERIDIEM (Latin: after noon)
• Important Latin abbreviations (PSC guaranteed):
  - a.m. = ante meridiem (before noon)
  - p.m. = post meridiem (after noon)
  - e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)
  - i.e. = id est (that is)
  - etc. = et cetera (and so on)
  - viz. = videlicet (namely)
  - P.S. = post scriptum (written after)
  - N.B. = nota bene (note well)
  - R.I.P. = requiescat in pace (rest in peace)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin abbreviations — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The meeting is at 10 a.m. (ante meridiem = before noon)
  2. School ends at 4 p.m. (post meridiem = after noon)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) past meridiem — 'Past' English word ആണ്, 'post' Latin word ആണ്. p.m. = Latin abbreviation → Latin expansion ആണ് ആവശ്യം. 'Past' → 'post' ആകില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'past' ഉം 'post' ഉം similar sound, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) post midday — 'Post' correct ആണ്, 'midday' wrong ആണ്. Latin expansion-ൽ 'midday' (English) ആകില്ല — 'meridiem' (Latin) ആണ് ആവശ്യം. 'Midday' English translation ആണ്, abbreviation expansion ആകില്ല.

❌ (D) past midday — Two errors: (1) 'past' instead of 'post', (2) 'midday' instead of 'meridiem'. Full expansion Latin ആകണം — both words wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'P.M. = POST MERIDIEM = PM after Midday!' — P = POST (after), M = MERIDIEM (noon). 'POST = AFTER' in Latin — Postgraduate (after graduation), Postmortem (after death), Post meridiem (after noon)!
• am/pm pair: AM = ANTE (before) MERIDIEM. PM = POST (after) MERIDIEM. Both Latin! Ante = before, Post = after.
• PSC exam strategy: Latin abbreviations — focus on 'post = after' and 'ante = before'. p.m. = post meridiem. ഇത് ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct verb form.
Mr. Gopal, accompanied by his two managers, __________ leaving for London.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) is

• Subject: 'Mr. Gopal' — singular noun ആണ്.
• 'Accompanied by his two managers' — ഇത് ഒരു parenthetical phrase ആണ്, subject-നെ affect ചെയ്യില്ല.
• Singular subject → singular verb: 'is'
• 'Mr. Gopal is leaving for London' — managers ഒപ്പം ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും subject Mr. Gopal ഒരാൾ ആണ്.
• 'Accompanied by' phrase commas-ൽ enclosed ആണ് — ignore ചെയ്ത് main subject verb match ചെയ്യൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Accompanied by / along with / together with / as well as' phrases subject change ചെയ്യില്ല — main subject-ൻ്റെ number-ൽ verb depend ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Key phrases that do NOT change subject number:
  - accompanied by
  - along with
  - together with
  - as well as
  - in addition to
  - including
  - except
• Compare: 'Mr. Gopal AND his managers ARE leaving' — 'and' plural ആക്കും! 'And' = plural, but 'accompanied by/along with' = singular stays!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Subject-Verb Agreement with intervening phrases — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The teacher, along with her students, is going on a trip. (teacher = singular → is)
  2. Rajan, accompanied by his friends, has arrived. (Rajan = singular → has)
  3. The CEO, as well as the directors, was present. (CEO = singular → was)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) are — 'Are' plural verb ആണ്. 'Mr. Gopal' singular ആണ്. 'His two managers' (plural) കണ്ടിട്ട് plural verb choose ചെയ്യുന്ന classic PSC trap. 'Accompanied by' phrase subject plural ആക്കില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'managers' plural word കണ്ടിട്ട് 'are' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) has — 'Has' Present Perfect auxiliary ആണ്. 'Leaving' ൻ്റെ കൂടെ 'is' ആണ് correct (is leaving = present continuous). 'Has leaving' grammatically wrong ആണ്.

❌ (D) have — 'Have' plural Present Perfect auxiliary ആണ്. Singular subject + wrong tense combo — double error.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ACCOMPANIED BY = ACCOMPANIED BUT ALONE!' — Mr. Gopal is the STAR — managers just accompany him. Star is ONE → singular verb! 'Accompanied by' = still one subject!
• Quick test: Commas-ൽ enclosed phrase bracket ചെയ്ത് ignore ചെയ്യൂ. 'Mr. Gopal [, accompanied by his two managers,] IS leaving' — bracket ignore → Mr. Gopal is → IS!
• PSC exam strategy: Subject identify ചെയ്ത് commas-ൽ ഉള്ള middle phrase ignore ചെയ്യൂ. Main subject singular? → singular verb. ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!

questionText:
Complete the sentence with the correct option.
If you work with confidence, you __________.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) will succeed

• 'If you work with confidence' — if-clause-ൽ Simple Present ('work') ആണ്.
• Type 1 Conditional (Real/Possible): If + Simple Present → will + V1
• 'If you work' → main clause: 'you will succeed'
• Meaning: confidence-ൽ ജോലി ചെയ്‌താൽ (real possibility) → success guaranteed ആകും.
• Future possibility ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശം → 'will succeed' ആണ് correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + Simple Present → WILL + V1 (Type 1 Conditional)
• Three Conditional types:
  - Type 1 (Real/Possible): If it rains → we will cancel (future possibility)
  - Type 2 (Unreal Present): If I had money → I would buy (imaginary)
  - Type 3 (Past Unreal): If I had studied → I would have passed (past regret)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Type 1 Conditional — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If you study hard, you will pass. (simple present → will + V1)
  2. If it rains, we will stay indoors.
  3. If she practises daily, she will improve.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) succeed — 'Succeed' base form alone wrong ആണ്. Main clause-ൽ Type 1 Conditional-ൽ 'will succeed' ആണ് ആവശ്യം, bare 'succeed' alone grammatically incomplete ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'succeed' correct form ആണ് (base), പക്ഷേ 'will' missing.

❌ (B) succeeded — 'Succeeded' Simple Past ആണ്. If-clause Simple Present ആണ്, past tense main clause Type 1-ൽ wrong ആണ്. Type 3-ൽ if-clause Past Perfect + 'would have + V3' ആണ്.

❌ (D) would have succeeded — 'Would have succeeded' Type 3 Conditional main clause ആണ്. Type 3-ൽ if-clause Past Perfect (had + V3) ആണ് ആവശ്യം. ഇവിടെ if-clause Simple Present ആണ് → Type 1 → 'will succeed' ആണ് correct.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IF PRESENT → WILL FUTURE!' — If-clause Simple Present (work) → main clause WILL + V1 (will succeed). Present → Will. Simple!
• Conditional shortcut: if-clause tense → main clause:
  Simple Present → WILL
  Simple Past → WOULD
  Past Perfect (had) → WOULD HAVE
• PSC exam strategy: If-clause-ൽ tense identify ചെയ്യൂ → main clause select ചെയ്യൂ. 'If you WORK' = present → 'will succeed'!

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct article.
Richard refused to accept the free ride because he was __________ honourable man.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) an

• 'Honourable' — letter 'h' ആണ് ആദ്യം, പക്ഷേ 'h' SILENT ആണ്! Pronunciation: /ɒnərəbl/ — vowel sound /ɒ/ ആദ്യം.
• Vowel sound-ന് മുന്നിൽ → 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'An honourable man' — Richard free ride accept ചെയ്യില്ല, കാരണം അദ്ദേഹം noble/honourable ആയ ഒരു man ആണ്.
• Sound rule: Letter 'h' silent ആണ് → vowel sound start → 'an' ആണ് correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Vowel SOUND → 'an' (letter നോക്കരുത്!)
• Silent 'h' → 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന words:
  - An honourable man (h silent)
  - An hour (h silent)
  - An honest person (h silent)
  - An heir (h silent)
  - An honour (h silent)
• 'Honourable' = worthy of respect, noble. 'Honour' + able = honourable.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles 'a' vs 'an' — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He is an honourable judge. (h silent → an)
  2. She served the country with honour. (h silent)
  3. It is an honest mistake. (h silent → an)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) the — 'The' definite article ആണ് — specific honourable man indicate ചെയ്യും. ഇവിടെ indefinite 'a/an' ആണ് ആവശ്യം (general reference: he was an honourable man). 🪤 PSC Trap: Context 'specific person Richard' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'the' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) any — 'Any' determiner ആണ്, article അല്ല. 'He was any honourable man' = grammatically unnatural ആണ്. 'Any' questions/negatives-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്.

❌ (D) a — 'A' consonant sound-ൻ്റെ മുന്നിൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'Honourable' = vowel sound (h silent) → 'a' wrong, 'an' correct. 'A honourable man' = wrong pronunciation.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HONOURABLE sounds like ONOURABLE' — h silent ആണ്! 'AN ONOURABLE' = 'AN HONOURABLE'! Pronounce ചെയ്ത് കേൾക്കൂ — vowel sound! → AN!
• Silent h family: hour, honest, honour, honourable, heir, heiress — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'AN' ആണ്! H Hiding (silent) → AN!
• PSC exam strategy: 'h' letter ഉള്ള word article question-ൽ — pronounce ചെയ്ത് check ചെയ്യൂ. h sound ഉണ്ടോ? 'hotel' (/h/ sound) → a hotel. h silent ആണോ? 'honest' (/ɒ/ sound) → an honest. !

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct modal verb.
The Registrar of the University said that the students __________ earn 85 per cent attendance to be eligible to appear for the examination.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) should

• Context: Attendance requirement — eligibility rule ആണ്. 'Should' = obligation/requirement (ആവശ്യം/നിർബന്ധം).
• 'The students should earn 85% attendance' = attendance earn ചെയ്യണം (obligation).
• 'Said that' = reported speech-ൽ 'must' → 'should' ആകും (backshift option).
• Eligibility condition indicate ചെയ്യാൻ 'should' (strong recommendation/requirement) ആണ് most appropriate.
• Registrar-ൻ്റെ official requirement: 'should' = strong obligation.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Obligation/Requirement = SHOULD (strong advice/necessity)
• Modal verbs and their meanings:
  - Should = obligation, advice, requirement (ആവശ്യം/ഉചിതം)
  - Can = ability/possibility (കഴിവ്)
  - Will = future/determination (ഭാവി)
  - Could = past ability / polite possibility (ഭൂതകാല കഴിവ്)
  - Must = strong obligation/certainty
  - May = permission/possibility
• Reported speech modal backshift: must → should (in some contexts)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Students should submit assignments on time. (obligation)
  2. You should consult a doctor. (strong advice)
  3. Candidates should carry their hall tickets. (requirement)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) can — 'Can' = ability/possibility. 'Students can earn 85% attendance' = ability-ൽ ഉണ്ട് (കഴിയും) — ഇത് requirement indicate ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല, ability indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു. Registrar ഒരു eligibility RULE state ചെയ്യുകയാണ്, ability confirm ചെയ്യുകയല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'can' familiar modal ആണ്.

❌ (C) will — 'Will' future tense / determination indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'Said that... will' reported speech-ൽ tense backshift wrong ആണ് (will → would). Plus, rule/requirement context-ൽ 'will' ഉചിതം അല്ല.

❌ (D) could — 'Could' past ability / polite suggestion indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. Attendance requirement-ൻ്റെ official statement-ൽ 'could' too weak ആണ്. 'Could earn' = optional suggestion ആകും, mandatory requirement ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SHOULD = RULE! CAN = ABLE! WILL = FUTURE! COULD = POLITE!' — Registrar rule ആണ് state ചെയ്യുന്നത് → SHOULD. Official requirement = SHOULD.
• Context clue: 'eligible to appear for examination' = eligibility REQUIREMENT → obligation → SHOULD.
• PSC exam strategy: Modal verb question-ൽ context identify ചെയ്യൂ. Rule/requirement → should. Ability → can. Future → will. Past ability/politeness → could. Context = key!

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag.
They hardly remember anything, __________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) do they

• 'They hardly remember anything' — 'hardly' = negative meaning adverb (almost never).
• Negative meaning statement → Positive question tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• Subject: 'they' (plural). Tense: Simple Present.
• Positive tag: 'do they?' (plural present positive auxiliary)
• 'They hardly remember anything, do they?' = അവർ ഒന്നും ഓർക്കില്ല, അല്ലേ?
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Negative adverb in statement → POSITIVE question tag
• Negative adverbs → positive tag:
  - hardly → do/does/did + subject?
  - never → do/does/did + subject?
  - seldom → do/does/did + subject?
  - barely → do/does/did + subject?
  - scarcely → do/does/did + subject?
• Tag structure: auxiliary + subject pronoun (no main verb in tag!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags with negative adverbs — PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She hardly speaks, does she? (hardly → does she)
  2. He never arrives on time, does he? (never → does he)
  3. They barely managed, did they? (barely → did they)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) can they — 'Can' modal ആണ്. Statement-ൽ 'remember' simple present ആണ്, modal 'can' ഉപയോഗിച്ചിട്ടില്ല. Tag-ൽ statement-ൻ്റെ auxiliary match ആകണം. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'can they' question-like feel ഉണ്ട്, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) won't they — 'Won't' = will not — negative future modal. Statement simple present ആണ്, future tag wrong ആണ്. Plus 'won't' negative tag ആണ്, 'hardly' negative meaning-ൻ്റെ statement → positive tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം.

❌ (C) don't they — 'Don't they' negative tag ആണ്. 'Hardly' = negative meaning → positive tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം. Negative + Negative = wrong. 'Do they?' (positive) ആണ് correct.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HARDLY = ALMOST NEVER = NEGATIVE → POSITIVE TAG!' — 'Hardly' already negative sense ആണ്, so give POSITIVE tag: 'do they?' Negative word = Positive tag! Always!
• Negative adverbs (all → positive tag): HARDLY, NEVER, SELDOM, BARELY, SCARCELY, RARELY — ഈ words ഒരു list ഉണ്ടാക്കി ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Statement-ൽ 'hardly/never/seldom/barely/scarcely' കണ്ടാൽ → DO/DOES/DID + subject (positive tag). Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct phrasal verb.
As little Albert could not __________ his nagging grandmother, he decided to leave home.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) put up with

• 'Put up with' = tolerate, endure, bear patiently (സഹിക്കുക, ക്ഷമിക്കുക).
• 'Could not put up with his nagging grandmother' = nagging grandmother-നെ സഹിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ല.
• Context: 'decided to leave home' = cannot tolerate → has to leave. Perfect fit for 'put up with'.
• 'Nagging' = constantly complaining/criticizing → hard to tolerate → 'put up with'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Put up with = Tolerate/Endure (സഹിക്കുക)
• 'Put' phrasal verbs — PSC favourites:
  - Put up with = tolerate (സഹിക്കുക)
  - Put off = postpone, delay (മാറ്റിവയ്ക്കുക)
  - Put away = store in correct place, save (വച്ചിടുക)
  - Put in = insert, invest effort (ഇടുക, effort give ചെയ്യുക)
  - Put out = extinguish, publish (കെടുത്തുക)
  - Put on = wear, increase weight (ഇടുക, weight gain)
  - Put across = communicate clearly (വ്യക്തമാക്കുക)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Put up with' — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന phrasal verb!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I cannot put up with this noise. (ഈ ശബ്ദം സഹിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല)
  2. Put away your toys after playing. (കളിച്ചതിന് ശേഷം toys വച്ചിടൂ)
  3. Don't put off till tomorrow what you can do today. (നാളേക്ക് മാറ്റിവയ്ക്കരുത്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) put away — 'Put away' = store in correct place / save money. Grandmother-നെ 'put away' (store ചെയ്യുക) = literal/weird meaning. Tolerate meaning ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'put away' familiar phrase ആണ്.

❌ (C) put off — 'Put off' = postpone/delay. Cannot 'postpone' grandmother = doesn't make sense in context. Tolerate meaning ഇല്ല. 'The meeting was put off' ഇതുപോലെ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്.

❌ (D) put in — 'Put in' = insert, invest effort, apply for. 'Could not put in his grandmother' = no sensible meaning. 'She put in a lot of effort' ഇതുപോലെ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PUT UP WITH = PATIENCE PUT UP (raised) to the LIMIT!' — Patience UP ആക്കി hold ചെയ്ത് WITH someone = tolerate! 'Put up' = raise, 'with' = together → raise patience with someone!
• Context clue: 'nagging grandmother + left home' = couldn't TOLERATE → 'put up with' = tolerate. Context = clue to phrasal verb meaning!
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb question-ൽ context (result/consequence) ആദ്യം read ചെയ്യൂ. 'Left home because couldn't ___' → cannot tolerate → 'put up with'. !

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct form of the verb.
If your team __________ again, you will be out of the competition.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) lose

• 'If your team _______, you will be out' — if-clause + main clause 'will be' → Type 1 Conditional.
• Type 1: If + Simple Present → will + V1
• If-clause: Simple Present verb ആണ് ആവശ്യം → 'lose' (base form = simple present).
• 'If your team loses again' — actually 'loses' ആകണം (he/she/it = team → loses). But 'lose' ആണ് options-ൽ correct, as 'loses' ഇല്ല.
• 'Lose' = to fail to win (തോൽക്കുക) — verb.
• Options: loss (noun), lose (verb), loose (adjective), lost (past tense) — 'lose' alone verb ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Lose (verb: to fail to win) ≠ Loss (noun) ≠ Loose (adjective: not tight)
• Confusing set:
  - Lose (verb): She will lose the match. (തോൽക്കുക)
  - Loss (noun): The team suffered a loss. (തോൽവി)
  - Loose (adjective): These shoes are too loose. (അയഞ്ഞ)
  - Lost (past tense of lose): They lost the game. (തോറ്റു)
• Type 1 Conditional: If + Simple Present, Subject + will + V1
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Lose/Loss/Loose confusion — PSC favourite trap word!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If they lose this match, they are out. (verb: lose)
  2. The team suffered a heavy loss. (noun: loss)
  3. The bolt is loose. (adjective: loose)
  4. They lost yesterday. (past tense: lost)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) loss — 'Loss' ഒരു noun ആണ് (the loss, a loss). Verb position-ൽ noun ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല. 'If your team loss again' = grammatically wrong. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'loss' ഉം 'lose' ഉം confuse ആകും — one extra 's'!

❌ (C) loose — 'Loose' ഒരു adjective ആണ് (not tight, not firm). 'Loose' verb position-ൽ wrong ആണ്. 'Loose' = adjective (loose shirt), 'lose' = verb (lose the match) — completely different words!

❌ (D) lost — 'Lost' Simple Past ആണ്. Type 1 Conditional if-clause Simple Present ആണ് ആവശ്യം, past tense wrong ആണ്. 'If your team lost' → Type 2 Conditional ആകും (main clause 'would be'), 'will be' ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LOSE = 1 O (one o), LOOSE = 2 O's (two o's)!' — LOSE (verb, one 'o') vs LOOSE (adjective, two 'oo'). ഒരേ ഒരു 'o' = lose (verb). Double 'oo' = loose (not tight). LOSS = no 'e' = noun!
• Sentence test: Can you add 'the' before it? 'The loss' = yes → noun. Can you say 'very ___'? 'Very loose' = yes → adjective. Action? 'Lose the match' → verb!
• PSC exam strategy: Lose/Loss/Loose — spelling matter! One 'o' = lose (verb). Two 'oo' = loose (adjective). No 'e' = loss (noun). Type 1 Conditional needs verb → 'lose'!

questionText: Pick out the antonym of the word 'clear' from the following.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) murky

• 'Clear' = transparent, easy to see/understand, not cloudy (വ്യക്തം, സ്വഛം).
• 'Murky' = dark, dirty, cloudy, not clear (ഇരുണ്ടത്, അവ്യക്തം) — exact ANTONYM.
• 'Murky water' = cloudy, unclear water. 'Clear water' = transparent water — opposites!
• Options B, C, D (lucid, obvious, distinct) — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'clear'-ൻ്റെ synonyms ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Clear (transparent/obvious) ↔ Murky (dark/unclear)
• Synonyms of 'clear': lucid, obvious, distinct, transparent, evident, apparent, plain
• Antonyms of 'clear': murky, obscure, vague, cloudy, opaque, unclear, ambiguous
• 'Murky' uses:
  - Murky water (അവ്യക്ത/ഇരുണ്ട വെള്ളം)
  - Murky intentions (തെളിച്ചമില്ലാത്ത ഉദ്ദേശ്യങ്ങൾ)
  - Murky past (ഇരുണ്ട ഭൂതകാലം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The river water was murky after the flood. (flood-ന് ശേഷം water murky ആയി)
  2. The instructions were clear and easy to follow. (clear = obvious)
  3. His explanation was lucid. (lucid = synonym of clear)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) lucid — 'Lucid' = clearly expressed, easy to understand. ഇത് 'clear'-ൻ്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല. 'A lucid explanation' = 'A clear explanation'. 🪤 PSC Trap: Antonym question-ൽ synonyms put ചെയ്ത് students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) obvious — 'Obvious' = easy to see/understand = 'clear'-ൻ്റെ synonym. 'It is obvious' = 'It is clear' — same meaning.

❌ (D) distinct — 'Distinct' = clearly different, easy to distinguish = also synonym of 'clear' in some contexts. 'A distinct smell' = 'A clear smell'. Antonym അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MURKY = MUDDY + DARK!' — MURky = MURky MUD! Muddy water = unclear = MURKY. Clear water ↔ Murky (muddy) water — perfect opposites!
• Antonym question strategy: Options-ൽ clear-ൻ്റെ synonyms (lucid, obvious, distinct) identify ചെയ്ത് eliminate ചെയ്യൂ. Remaining option (murky) = antonym!
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern common ആണ്. Synonyms eliminate ചെയ്ത് antonym find ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct idiom.
The students found the project to be __________ because it was very easy.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) a piece of cake

• 'A piece of cake' = very easy task, something done without effort.
• Context: 'because it was very easy' — DIRECT CLUE! 'Very easy' = 'a piece of cake'
• 'The students found the project to be a piece of cake' = project വളരെ easy ആണ് എന്ന് students-ന് തോന്നി.
• 'Piece of cake' — eating cake ഒരു easy enjoyable thing ആണ് → easy task-ൻ്റെ idiom!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: A piece of cake = Very easy (ഒരു easy task)
• ⭐ PSC favourite idioms (food related):
  - A piece of cake = very easy
  - Spill the beans = reveal a secret (secret പറഞ്ഞുകളയുക)
  - Cost an arm and a leg = very expensive
  - A bite to eat = a small meal (ചെറിയ ഭക്ഷണം)
• Other options explained:
  - Hand in glove = very closely associated, working together (ഒരുമിച്ച് ജോലി)
  - Apple of their eye = someone very precious/beloved (ഏറ്റവും പ്രിയപ്പെട്ടത്)
  - Cut corners = do something in the easiest/cheapest way, often poorly (quality compromise ചെയ്യുക)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — PSC-ൽ guaranteed topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The maths test was a piece of cake for her. (easy ആയിരുന്നു)
  2. They work hand in glove with each other. (closely together)
  3. She is the apple of her father's eye. (most beloved)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) hand in glove — 'Hand in glove' = closely associated, in collaboration. 'Easy' meaning ഇല്ല. 'They work hand in glove' = closely together work ചെയ്യുന്നു. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'easy' context-ൽ 'hand' related idiom confuse ആകും.

❌ (B) apple of their eye — 'Apple of one's eye' = most precious/beloved person or thing. 'Easy' meaning ഇല്ല. 'The daughter is the apple of her father's eye' = ഏറ്റവും പ്രിയപ്പെട്ടവൾ.

❌ (C) cut corners — 'Cut corners' = do something hastily/cheaply, sacrifice quality. 'Very easy' meaning അല്ല — shortcut/quality compromise meaning ആണ്. 'Easy' ≠ 'cutting corners'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PIECE OF CAKE = SWEET + EASY!' — Cake eat ചെയ്യൽ easy ഉം sweet ഉം ആണ്! Easy task = piece of cake! Imagine eating a delicious slice of cake — effortless! That's 'a piece of cake'!
• Context clue: 'because it was very easy' = direct hint! 'Very easy' → 'piece of cake'! Context ആണ് idiom answer ഒരിക്കൽ reveal ചെയ്യുന്നത്!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom blank question-ൽ sentence-ൻ്റെ 'because/since/as' clause ആദ്യം read ചെയ്യൂ — reason = clue to idiom. 'Because very easy' → piece of cake. Guaranteed!

questionText:
Substitute the underlined phrase with the correct word.
Hitler believed himself to be __all powerful__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Omnipotent

• 'All powerful' = having unlimited/supreme power (സർവ്വശക്തൻ).
• 'Omnipotent' = omni (all) + potent (powerful) = all powerful.
• 'Hitler believed himself to be omnipotent' = Hitler himself-നെ all-powerful ആണ് എന്ന് believe ചെയ്തു.
• 'Omni-' prefix = all/every (Latin) — ഇത് ഓർക്കൂ: omni = all!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Omnipotent = Omni (all) + Potent (powerful) = ALL POWERFUL
• ⭐ 'Omni-' prefix words (PSC guaranteed):
  - Omnipotent = all powerful (സർവ്വശക്തൻ)
  - Omniscient = all knowing (സർവ്വജ്ഞൻ)
  - Omnipresent = present everywhere (സർവ്വവ്യാപി)
  - Omnivore = eats everything/all food types (സസ്യഭക്ഷണവും മാംസഭക്ഷണവും)
• Latin roots:
  - omni = all
  - potent = powerful (potential, potency)
  - scient = knowing (science, conscience)
  - present = present/being there
  - vore = eater (herbivore, carnivore)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Omni- prefix words — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. God is omnipotent. (all powerful — സർവ്വശക്തൻ)
  2. God is omniscient. (all knowing — സർവ്വജ്ഞൻ)
  3. God is omnipresent. (everywhere — സർവ്വവ്യാപി)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Omniscient — 'Omni + scient' = ALL KNOWING (സർവ്വജ്ഞൻ). 'All powerful' ≠ 'all knowing' — different qualities. Hitler 'knew everything' ആണ് claim ഇവിടെ ഉദ്ദേശം അല്ല, 'all powerful' ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: All 'omni-' words similar look ഉണ്ട്, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Omnipresent — 'Omni + present' = PRESENT EVERYWHERE (സർവ്വവ്യാപി). 'All powerful' = omnipotent ആണ്, 'everywhere present' = omnipresent ആണ്. Different divine qualities.

❌ (D) Omnivore — 'Omni + vore' = EATS EVERYTHING. Bears, pigs are omnivores. 'All powerful' meaning ഇല്ല. Omnivore = biological eating habit indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്, power indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OMNI- = ALL. POTENT = POWER (potential/potency). OMNI + POTENT = ALL POWER = OMNIPOTENT!' — 'Potential' → 'potent' → 'omnipotent'. Power words ഒക്കെ 'pot-' root ഉള്ളവ!
• Omni- family quick memory:
  POTENT (power) → omnipOTENT
  SCIENCE (know) → omniSCIENT
  PRESENT (here) → omniPRESENT
  VORE (eat) → omniVORE
• PSC exam strategy: 'All powerful' → omni + power = omnipOTENT. Root 'potent' = power (remember: impotent = powerless). !

questionText:
Use an appropriate article.
After a long enquiry, they bought ________ ewe from Chennai.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) a

• 'Ewe' = female sheep (പെൺ ആട്). Pronunciation: /juː/ — ആദ്യ sound 'y' (consonant sound) ആണ്, vowel sound അല്ല!
• 'Ewe' letter 'e' ൽ start ആകുന്നു, പക്ഷേ pronunciation /juː/ = 'you' പോലെ — consonant sound 'j/y' ആണ്.
• Consonant sound-ൻ്റെ മുന്നിൽ → 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'A ewe' — ഒരു പെൺ ആട് — 'a' ആണ് correct article.
• Classic PSC trap: 'ewe' vowel letter-ൽ start ആകുന്നു, പക്ഷേ sound consonant ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Letter നോക്കരുത് — SOUND നോക്കൂ! Vowel SOUND → 'an' | Consonant SOUND → 'a'
• Vowel letter but CONSONANT sound → 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം:
  - A ewe (/juː/ = y sound → a)
  - A university (/juː/ = y sound → a)
  - A European (/jʊər/ = y sound → a)
  - A one-eyed man (/w/ = w sound → a)
  - A useful tool (/juː/ = y sound → a)
• Consonant letter but VOWEL sound → 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം:
  - An hour (h silent → an)
  - An honest man (h silent → an)
  - An heir (h silent → an)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles 'a' vs 'an' — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A ewe was grazing in the field. (/juː/ → a)
  2. A university degree helps in career. (/juː/ → a)
  3. An hour has sixty minutes. (h silent → an)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) an — 'An' vowel sound-ൻ്റെ മുന്നിൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'Ewe' letter 'e' ആണ്, പക്ഷേ sound /juː/ (y sound) ആണ്. Vowel letter ≠ vowel sound! 'An ewe' = wrong pronunciation. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: 'e' letter vowel ആണ് → 'an' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) the — 'The' definite article specific thing indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'The ewe' = previously mentioned specific ewe. ഇവിടെ 'a' (indefinite, first time mention) ആണ് ആവശ്യം.

❌ (D) No article — 'Ewe' countable singular noun ആണ്. Singular countable noun-ന് indefinite article (a/an) ആവശ്യം ഉണ്ട്. Article ഇല്ലാതെ singular countable noun ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EWE = YOU!' — 'Ewe' /juː/ sounds exactly like 'you'! 'A you' (a ewe) — 'you' consonant sound 'y' → 'a'! Sheep = 'you' sounds like! A ewe!
• Tricky 'a' words (vowel letter, consonant sound): ewe, university, European, union, uniform, unit, unique, eulogy, euphoria — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'a' ആണ്!
• PSC exam strategy: Word ഉറക്കെ pronounce ചെയ്യൂ. 'Ewe' = 'yoo' = y sound = consonant = 'a'. Letter trap-ൽ വീഴരുത്!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable preposition.
The proposal had significant drawbacks so she was angry ________ my decision to accept it.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) at

• 'Angry at' = fixed adjective + preposition collocation.
• 'She was angry at my decision' = എൻ്റെ decision-നോട് അവൾ ദേഷ്യം ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നു.
• 'Angry at' — a decision/situation/thing-നോടുള്ള anger indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് 'at' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Angry with' = person-നോടുള്ള anger. 'Angry at' = situation/thing/action-നോടുള്ള anger.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Angry AT (thing/situation/action) | Angry WITH (person)
• ⭐ PSC favourite adjective + preposition collocations:
  - Angry at (thing) / angry with (person)
  - Pleased with (person/thing)
  - Satisfied with
  - Disappointed with/at
  - Surprised at/by
  - Annoyed at/with
  - Excited about
  - Worried about
  - Jealous of
  - Proud of
  - Capable of
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective + Preposition — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She was angry at the delay. (delay = thing → at)
  2. He was angry with his friend. (friend = person → with)
  3. They were angry at the decision. (decision = thing → at)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) on — 'Angry on' = not a standard English collocation. 'On' time/surface/topic indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'Angry on my decision' = wrong preposition. 🪤 PSC Trap: Malayalam-ൽ 'decision-ൽ ദേഷ്യം' → 'on' translate ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) in — 'Angry in' = not a collocation for 'angry'. 'In' place/time/state indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'Angry in my decision' = unnatural.

❌ (C) by — 'Angry by' = also not standard. 'By' agent/means indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ് (passive voice). 'Angry by' fixed phrase ഇല്ല English-ൽ.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ANGRY AT = AIM AT the target!' — Anger AT a specific target (thing/decision). 'At' = pointing towards something specific. Decision = specific target → angry AT!
• Angry AT/WITH distinction: 'AT' thing, 'WITH' person. 'I am angry AT the rule / angry WITH the rulemaker.'
• PSC exam strategy: Adjective + preposition question-ൽ fixed collocation ഓർക്കൂ. 'Angry at' ഒരു standard pair ആണ്. options-ൽ 'at' ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ anger context-ൽ = first check ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText: Which of the following is correctly spelt?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Lapidary

• 'Lapidary' = correct spelling: L-A-P-I-D-A-R-Y.
• 'Lapidary' = relating to the cutting, polishing, or engraving of gems and precious stones; also a person who cuts gems.
• ശരിയായ spelling: Lap + i + dary = Lapidary.
• Etymology: Latin 'lapidarius' (lapis = stone) → English 'lapidary'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: L-A-P-I-D-A-R-Y (8 letters — LAP + I + DARY)
• 'Lapidary' meaning:
  - Adjective: relating to precious stones or their engraving
  - Noun: a person who cuts, polishes, or engraves gems
  - Also: precise and elegant (lapidary prose = beautifully concise writing)
• Etymology: Latin 'lapis' = stone → lapidary (stone cutter/polisher)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Advanced vocabulary spelling — Main examination-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The lapidary carefully cut the diamond. (gem cutter)
  2. He wrote in a lapidary style. (precise and elegant)
  3. Lapidary work requires great skill and patience.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Laepidary — 'ae' combination wrong ആണ്. 'Lapidary' = 'a' ഒന്നേ ഉള്ളൂ ആദ്യ syllable-ൽ. 'Lae-' prefix wrong. 🪤 PSC Trap: Similar looking spelling confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Lapeadary — 'ea' ഉം extra syllable ഉം wrong ആണ്. 'Lapidary' = 3 syllables (Lap-i-da-ry), 'Lapeadary' = extra 'ea' inserted, incorrect.

❌ (D) None of these — Option C 'Lapidary' ശരിയായ spelling ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LAPIDARY = LAP + I + DARY' — Lap-ൽ (lap) ഇരുന്ന് precious stones-ൻ്റെ work ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ! LAP + I + DARY. Or: LAPIS (stone in Latin) → LAPIDARY!
• Word root: LAPIS = stone (Latin). Lapidary = stone person. Remember: LAPIS LAZULI (blue precious stone) → lapis = stone!
• PSC exam strategy: Advanced spelling-ൽ Latin root ഓർക്കൂ. Lapis (stone) → lapidary. Root ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ spelling automatically correct ആകും!

questionText: Find one word for 'A public place for walking'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Promenade

• 'Promenade' = a public place/path designed for leisurely walking, especially along the seafront or in a park.
• 'A public place for walking' = promenade — ഇതാണ് exact definition.
• 'Promenade' = ജനങ്ങൾ വിനോദ സഞ്ചാരം/morning walk ചെയ്യുന്ന public pathway.
• Famous promenades: Marine Drive (Mumbai), Marina Beach walkway (Chennai) — ഇവ promenades ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Public walking place = Promenade
• ⭐ One Word Substitutes — Places (PSC favourites):
  - Promenade = public place for walking
  - Alley = narrow passage between buildings
  - Byway = small road/path away from main road
  - Maze = complex network of paths (labyrinth)
  - Arena = place for sports/events
  - Amphitheatre = open-air theatre
  - Sanctuary = place of refuge/safety
  - Mortuary = place where dead bodies are kept
  - Aviary = place for keeping birds
  - Apiary = place where bees are kept
• Etymology: French 'promener' = to take for a walk → promenade
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes (places) — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They strolled along the promenade by the sea. (seafront walking path)
  2. The dark alley was dangerous at night. (narrow passage)
  3. They got lost in the maze. (complex paths)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Byway — 'Byway' = a minor road or path away from the main roads, often in rural areas. PUBLIC walking-ൻ്റെ specific meaning ഇല്ല, general small path ആണ്. 'Public place' specifically indicate ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Byway' = walking related, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) Alley — 'Alley' = a narrow passage/lane between buildings. Public walking-നായി designed ആയ place അല്ല — buildings-ൻ്റെ ഇടയ്ക്കുള്ള narrow path ആണ്. Often dark/private.

❌ (D) Maze — 'Maze' = a complex network of paths where it's easy to get lost (labyrinth). Public walking place അല്ല — puzzle/entertainment purpose ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PROMENADE = PRO + MENADE (MADE for walking)!' — PRO = forward, MENADE = made/walking path. 'PROM' ഓർക്കൂ — school prom-ൽ ആളുകൾ dress up ചെയ്ത് walk ചെയ്യുന്നു! Promenade = formal walk!
• Places one-word substitutes: Promenade (public walk), Alley (narrow lane), Maze (labyrinth), Arena (sports), Aviary (birds), Apiary (bees), Mortuary (dead bodies) — ഒരു list ഉണ്ടാക്കി ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Public place for walking' = promenade. Public + walk = promenade. Definition directly map ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText:
Report the given sentence.
She said to him, "Stay here until your turn comes."
optionA: She requested him to stay there until her turn came.
optionB: She requested him that he should stay there until his turn came.
optionC: She requested him to stay there until his turn came.
optionD: She told him to stay there until his turn comes.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) She requested him to stay there until his turn came.

• Direct speech: 'Stay here until your turn comes' — imperative sentence (polite request/instruction).
• Reported speech changes:
  - 'Stay' (imperative) → 'to stay' (infinitive in reported)
  - 'here' → 'there' (place change)
  - 'your turn' → 'his turn' (pronoun change: 'you' → 'he')
  - 'comes' → 'came' (tense backshift: present → past)
  - 'said to him' + imperative → 'requested/told him to'
• Option C: all changes correct — 'to stay there until his turn came'. ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Imperative reported speech: said to + [person] → told/requested + [person] + to + V1
• Imperative Reported Speech rules:
  - Positive imperative: 'Do X' → told/requested/asked + person + to + X
  - Negative imperative: 'Don't do X' → told/asked + person + not to + X
• Changes in reported speech:
  - here → there
  - this → that
  - come → go
  - your → his/her (context based)
  - Verb tense backshift: comes → came
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported speech — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She said, 'Come here.' → She told him to go there.
  2. He said, 'Wait outside.' → He told her to wait outside.
  3. Teacher said, 'Be quiet.' → The teacher told students to be quiet.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) until her turn came — 'Her turn' = wrong pronoun. 'She' said to 'him' → 'him' (male) = 'his turn', not 'her turn'. Pronoun error. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'She' subject ആണ്, students 'her' pronoun use ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) that he should stay there — 'That he should stay' = awkward reported imperative. Standard form: 'requested him TO STAY' (infinitive), not 'that he should'. This structure is grammatically awkward for imperative reporting.

❌ (D) until his turn comes — 'Comes' = tense backshift ചെയ്തില്ല! Reported speech-ൽ 'said' (past) ആണ് reporting verb → 'comes' → 'came' ആകണം. Present tense → past tense backshift missed.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: '4 CHANGES in reported speech: (1) Verb form (to+V1), (2) HERE→THERE, (3) YOUR→HIS/HER, (4) Tense backshift (comes→came)' — ഈ 4 changes ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ options verify ചെയ്യൂ!
• Option elimination: A = wrong pronoun (her). B = awkward structure. D = tense not backshifted. C = all correct!
• PSC exam strategy: Reported speech options-ൽ ഓരോ change ഒന്നൊന്നായി check ചെയ്യൂ: pronoun ✓, place word ✓, tense ✓, structure ✓ → correct answer!

questionText: Which of the following words is the antonym of the word 'defiance'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Submission

• 'Defiance' = bold resistance, open disobedience, refusal to obey (അനുസരണക്കേട്, ധിക്കാരം).
• 'Submission' = the act of accepting/yielding to authority, surrender (കീഴടങ്ങൽ, അനുസരണം).
• Defiance (resist/defy) ↔ Submission (accept/yield) — exact opposites!
• 'In defiance of orders' vs 'in submission to orders' — complete contrast.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Defiance (resist) ↔ Submission (yield/accept)
• Word family:
  - Defy (verb) → Defiance (noun) → Defiant (adjective) → Defiantly (adverb)
  - Submit (verb) → Submission (noun) → Submissive (adjective)
• Synonyms of 'defiance': contumacy, insolence, resistance, rebellion, disobedience
• Antonyms of 'defiance': submission, compliance, obedience, surrender, yielding
• ⭐ Options A, C, D are all SYNONYMS of defiance!
  - Contumacy = stubborn resistance to authority (defiance synonym)
  - Insolence = disrespectful boldness (related to defiance)
  - Resistance = opposing/withstanding (defiance synonym)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. His defiance of the rules surprised everyone. (rules ധിക്കരിച്ചു)
  2. The soldiers' submission ended the war. (surrender)
  3. She stood in defiance against the injustice.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Contumacy — 'Contumacy' = stubborn refusal to obey authority — ഇത് 'defiance'-ൻ്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്! Antonym അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: Options-ൽ 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern — students synonym-ൽ confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) Insolence — 'Insolence' = rude/disrespectful behaviour = another SYNONYM of defiance. Antonym അല്ല. 'Insolent behaviour' = defiant/disrespectful.

❌ (D) Resistance — 'Resistance' = opposing/withstanding = synonym of defiance. Antonym അല്ല. 'Resistance to authority' = 'defiance of authority' — same meaning.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DEFIANCE = DEFY + ANCE = REFUSE! SUBMISSION = SUBMIT + ION = ACCEPT!' — Defy = refuse → Defiance. Submit = accept → Submission. REFUSE ↔ ACCEPT = exact opposites!
• Antonym question strategy: 3 options (contumacy, insolence, resistance) — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'resistance/disobedience' meaning ആണ്. ഒരേ meaning ഉള്ള 3 options → ഒന്ന് remove, remaining (submission) = antonym!
• PSC exam strategy: When 3 options have similar meaning → they're all synonyms → answer is the odd one out = antonym!

questionText:
Identify the meaning of the idiomatic expression.
'To take the wind out of one's sails' (~~deleted by psc~~)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Dishearten

• 'To take the wind out of someone's sails' = to make someone feel less confident or enthusiastic, to deflate their excitement (ആവേശം/ആത്മവിശ്വാസം കുറയ്ക്കുക).
• 'Dishearten' = to make someone lose hope or enthusiasm = exact meaning.
• Origin: Sailing-ൽ wind (wind) sail-ൽ നിന്ന് take away ചെയ്‌താൽ ship-ന് speed ഇല്ലാതാകുന്നു — person-ൻ്റെ momentum/enthusiasm take away ചെയ്യുന്ന image.
• 'His criticism took the wind out of my sails' = his criticism-ൽ enthusiasm ഇല്ലാതായി.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Take the wind out of sails = Dishearten / Deflate enthusiasm
• ⭐ Sailing/nautical idioms:
  - Take the wind out of one's sails = dishearten, deflate
  - Plain sailing = easy progress (smooth sailing)
  - In the same boat = in the same difficult situation
  - Rock the boat = disturb a stable situation
  - Miss the boat = miss an opportunity
  - Sail through = complete easily without problems
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — PSC-ൽ guaranteed topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Her negative comments took the wind out of his sails. (enthusiasm കുറഞ്ഞു)
  2. The failure took the wind out of their sails. (ആത്മവിശ്വാസം ഇല്ലാതായി)
  3. Don't let criticism take the wind out of your sails. (dishearten ആകരുത്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Excite — 'Excite' = enthusiasm increase ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് OPPOSITE meaning ആണ്! Wind take away ചെയ്‌താൽ ship slow ആകുന്നു (excitement decreases). 'Excite' = wind add ചെയ്‌താൽ ആണ്, take away ചെയ്‌താൽ അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: Wind/sails = exciting image → 'excite' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Thrive — 'Thrive' = grow/prosper/flourish (ഉയർച്ചയടയുക). ഇതും positive meaning ആണ്. Taking wind out = negative effect → 'thrive' wrong.

❌ (C) Invigorate — 'Invigorate' = give energy/vitality to (ഊർജ്ജം കൊടുക്കുക). ഇതും positive/energizing meaning ആണ്. Wind take away = energy take away → invigorate opposite direction.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'WIND = ENERGY. TAKE AWAY WIND = TAKE AWAY ENERGY = DISHEARTEN!' — Imagine a sailing ship: no wind = stopped, deflated, sad ship! That's how someone feels when wind is taken from their sails = disheartened!
• Nautical idioms all options-ൽ: 'take the wind OUT' = remove energy = dishearten. 'Give wind' = inspire. 'Take away' = always negative → dishearten/deflate!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom meaning-ൽ positive/negative identify ചെയ്യൂ. 'TAKE/REMOVE something' = negative → positive options (excite, thrive, invigorate) eliminate ചെയ്യൂ → 'Dishearten' = answer!

🚫 Deleted Question: PSC ഔദ്യോഗിക Final Answer Key-ൽ ഈ ചോദ്യം ഒഴിവാക്കി (deleted/cancelled), സ്കോറിംഗിൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിട്ടില്ല. കൃത്യമായ കാരണം സ്ഥിരീകരിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞിട്ടില്ല — 'Dishearten' എന്നത് ഈ idiom-ന്റെ standard, widely-accepted meaning ആണ് (മറ്റ് options പോലെ ambiguity ഉള്ള ഒരു genuine alternative ഇവിടെ കാണുന്നില്ല). അതിനാൽ ഈ deletion, ചോദ്യത്തിന്റെ meaning-ലെ ambiguity കൊണ്ടല്ല, മറിച്ച് Malayalam-medium ചോദ്യപേപ്പറിലെ ഒരു printing/translation പിശക് കൊണ്ടാകാം എന്ന് അനുമാനിക്കുന്നു.

questionText:
Identify the underlined word with correct parts of speech.
A long night of travelling left me with __bloodshot__ eyes.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Adjective

• 'Bloodshot' = having the white part of the eye streaked with red (blood vessels visible due to tiredness).
• 'Bloodshot eyes' — 'bloodshot' 'eyes' (noun)-നെ modify/describe ചെയ്യുന്നു → Adjective.
• Adjective = noun-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word. 'Bloodshot' eyes describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'A long night of travelling left me with bloodshot [eyes]' — bloodshot = tired, red-streaked eyes describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Word that describes/modifies a noun → ADJECTIVE
• Adjective identification test: Can you say 'very ___' or '___ noun'? If yes → adjective.
  - 'Very bloodshot' = possible → adjective
  - 'Bloodshot eyes' (adj + noun) → adjective
• Compound adjectives (noun + past participle): bloodshot, heartbroken, sun-dried, hand-made, well-known, short-sighted
• 'Bloodshot' = blood + shot (blood-streaked) = compound adjective
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of speech identification — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She had bloodshot eyes after crying. (bloodshot = adjective modifying eyes)
  2. He is a well-known actor. (well-known = adjective)
  3. She wore a hand-made dress. (hand-made = adjective)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Adverb — Adverb verb/adjective/other adverb-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. 'Bloodshot' ഒരു noun (eyes)-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നു. Noun modify ചെയ്‌താൽ = adjective, verb modify ചെയ്‌താൽ = adverb. 🪤 PSC Trap: '-shot' ending verb-related feel ഉണ്ട്.

❌ (C) Noun — 'Bloodshot' ഒരു person/place/thing/idea indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല. 'Eyes' (noun)-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന quality word ആണ് → adjective, noun അല്ല.

❌ (D) Verb — 'Bloodshot' action indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല. 'Left' ആണ് ഈ sentence-ൽ main verb. 'Bloodshot' eyes describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BLOODSHOT DESCRIBES EYES = ADJECTIVE!' — Bloodshot = describing word for eyes. Any word describing a noun = adjective. Simple test: 'What kind of eyes?' → bloodshot eyes → bloodshot = adjective!
• Compound adjective pattern: Noun + past participle = adjective. Blood + shot = bloodshot (describing condition of eyes). Similar: heart + broken = heartbroken, sun + dried = sun-dried.
• PSC exam strategy: Underlined word + noun after it? → check if it describes the noun. Describes noun = adjective. 'Bloodshot EYES' → bloodshot describes eyes → adjective!

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag.
Few people seem to understand these new regulations, __________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) do they

• 'Few people seem to understand' — 'few' = negative meaning (almost nobody).
• Negative meaning statement → Positive question tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• Subject: 'Few people' → pronoun 'they' (third person plural).
• Tense: Simple Present ('seem') → auxiliary 'do'.
• Positive tag: 'do they?' (plural present positive)
• 'Few people seem to understand these regulations, do they?' = ഈ regulations ഒന്നും ആർക്കും മനസ്സിലാകുന്നില്ല, അല്ലേ?
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Few' = negative meaning → Positive tag
• 'Few' vs 'A few':
  - Few = negative (almost none, not enough) → positive tag
  - A few = positive (some, enough) → negative tag
• Negative quantifiers → positive tag:
  - Few people → do they?
  - Little money → does it?
  - Hardly anyone → do they?
  - Never/seldom/rarely → do/does/did + subject?
• Subject-pronoun matching:
  - Few people → they (third person plural)
  - NOT 'we' (first person)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags with 'few' and negative quantifiers — PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Few students passed, did they? (few → positive tag → did)
  2. A few students passed, didn't they? (a few → negative tag → didn't)
  3. Few people know this, do they? (few → do they?)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) do we — 'We' = first person plural. Subject 'few people' = third person plural → 'they'. 'We' wrong pronoun ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'These new regulations' general context-ൽ 'we' feel ആകും.

❌ (B) don't we — Two errors: (1) 'we' wrong pronoun (should be 'they'). (2) 'don't' negative tag ആണ്, 'few' negative meaning → positive tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം.

❌ (D) don't they — Pronoun correct (they) ✓, പക്ഷേ 'don't' negative tag ആണ്. 'Few' = negative meaning → positive tag 'do they?' ആണ് ആവശ്യം. Negative + Negative = double negative = wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FEW = NOT MANY = NEGATIVE → POSITIVE TAG!' — FEW = negative idea (not many people understand). Negative idea = positive tag (do they?). Remember: FEW already said 'NO', so tag says 'YES (do they)?'
• Few vs A few trick: 'A FEW = positive (some). FEW alone = negative (almost none). FEW → do/does. A FEW → don't/doesn't.'
• PSC exam strategy: 'Few people' → they (pronoun, not we). 'Few' negative → positive tag (do). 'do they?' = C = correct!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable phrasal verb.
Everyone expected him to ________ within a week and start succeeding.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) come around

• 'Come around' = (1) recover from illness or a setback, regain consciousness; (2) change one's opinion; (3) occur again (cyclically).
• Context: 'within a week and start succeeding' = setback-ൽ നിന്ന് recover ചെയ്ത് succeed ആകുന്നു.
• 'Everyone expected him to come around within a week' = ഒരാഴ്ചക്കുള്ളിൽ recover ചെയ്ത് succeed ആകും എന്ന് എല്ലാരും expect ചെയ്തു.
• 'Come around' = recover/bounce back = this context-ൽ perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Come around = recover from setback / change mind / regain consciousness
• 'Come' phrasal verbs — PSC favourites:
  - Come around/round = recover, change opinion (recover ചെയ്യുക)
  - Come by = obtain/acquire (something rare) (കിട്ടുക)
  - Come about = happen, occur (സംഭവിക്കുക)
  - Come up = arise, be mentioned (ഉദിക്കുക, വരുക)
  - Come across = find/meet unexpectedly (ആകസ്മികമായി കണ്ടെത്തുക)
  - Come forward = present oneself voluntarily (മുന്നോട്ടു വരുക)
  - Come through = survive, succeed despite difficulties (survive ചെയ്യുക)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She came around after the surgery. (recover from anaesthesia)
  2. Good jobs are hard to come by. (rare to obtain)
  3. How did this situation come about? (happen/occur)
  4. A problem came up at work. (arose)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) come by — 'Come by' = obtain or acquire something (especially something rare). 'Come by success' = obtain success — possible meaning, പക്ഷേ 'within a week and start succeeding' = recovery context ആണ്, acquisition context അല്ല. 'Come around' more naturally fits 'bounce back/recover'. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'come by success' feel ആകും.

❌ (C) come about — 'Come about' = happen/occur. 'He came about within a week' = he happened/occurred within a week — doesn't make sense with a person subject in this recovery context.

❌ (D) come up — 'Come up' = arise, be mentioned, (sun) rise. 'He came up within a week' = slightly different — 'come up' more for issues/sun rising, not personal recovery/bounce-back context.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'COME AROUND = CIRCLE BACK TO NORMAL!' — Imagine a path going around in a circle — 'come around' = complete the circle = return to normal state = RECOVER! Setback → come around → back to normal!
• Context clue: 'within a week and START SUCCEEDING' = recovery/bounce-back context → 'come around' (recover) is the natural fit!
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb question-ൽ context (what happens before/after blank) read ചെയ്യൂ. 'Start succeeding after setback' = recovery = come around. Context ആണ് key!

questionText: John, as well as his brother ________ in honesty and hard work.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Believes

• 'John, as well as his brother' — ഇവിടെ main subject 'John' ആണ് (singular).
• 'As well as', 'along with', 'together with', 'in addition to' — ഇവ two subjects-നെ join ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ verb ആദ്യത്തെ subject-നെ follow ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'John' = singular → verb-ൽ 's' ചേർക്കണം → 'Believes'.
