• 'John believes in honesty' — ഇതാണ് core sentence. 'as well as his brother' extra information ആണ്.
• 'ജോൺ, അവൻ്റെ സഹോദരനും, ആത്മാർഥതയിലും കഠിനാധ്വാനത്തിലും വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നു' — Believes ആണ് correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Subject1 + [as well as / along with / together with] + Subject2 → verb agrees with Subject1 ONLY!
• ഈ phrases verb agreement change ചെയ്യില്ല:
  - As well as
  - Along with
  - Together with
  - In addition to
  - Besides
  - Accompanied by
  - Rather than
• ഇവ bracketed addition ആണ് — remove ചെയ്‌തും sentence correct ആകണം.
• 'And' = different! John AND his brother = plural → believe (no 's').
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Subject-verb agreement — PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The teacher, as well as students, was present. (teacher = singular → was)
  2. Raju, along with his friends, plays cricket. (Raju = singular → plays)
  3. The manager, together with staff, attends meetings. (manager = singular → attends)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Believe — 'Believe' = plural verb form (no 's'). 'As well as his brother' കണ്ട് students 'brother' ഉം ഉള്ളതിനാൽ plural choose ചെയ്യും. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: 'as well as' = 'and' like feel → plural verb. But 'as well as' ≠ 'and'!

❌ (C) Believed — 'Believed' = past tense. Question ഒരു general truth/habit state ചെയ്യുന്നു (present tense ആണ് ആവശ്യം). Past tense context ഒന്നും sentence-ൽ ഇല്ല.

❌ (D) Believing — 'Believing' = present participle. Main verb ആയി use ചെയ്യുന്നത് wrong. Auxiliary verb ('is believing') ഇല്ലാതെ participle alone verb ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AS WELL AS = BRACKET ഇടൂ!' — 'as well as his brother' ഒരു bracket-ൽ ഇടൂ: 'John [as well as his brother] believes.' Bracket remove ചെയ്‌താൽ 'John believes' = correct! Bracket inside ഉള്ളത് verb-നെ affect ചെയ്യില്ല!
• AND vs AS WELL AS: 'John AND his brother believe' (plural). 'John AS WELL AS his brother believes' (singular). This difference is a guaranteed PSC question!
• PSC exam strategy: Subject ഇടത്തു നോക്കൂ. 'As well as / along with' കണ്ടാൽ → first subject-ൻ്റേത് ആണ് verb → bracket trick use ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText: Which of the following sentences contains a gerund?
optionB: Kiran is standing outside the waiting room.
optionD: Screaming aloud, he ran out of the room.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Swimming is a good exercise.

• 'Swimming' — ഇവിടെ sentence-ൻ്റെ SUBJECT ആണ്. 'Swimming is good' = 'നീന്തൽ നല്ലതാണ്'.
• Verb-ൻ്റെ -ing form ഒരു NOUN ആയി act ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ = GERUND.
• 'Swimming' ഇവിടെ 'what is good?' എന്ന ചോദ്യത്തിൻ്റെ answer = noun function = gerund.
• Test: Subject/Object ആണോ? → Gerund. Verb-ൻ്റെ part ആണോ? → Participle.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: -ing form = NOUN function → GERUND | -ing form = VERB part → PARTICIPLE
• Gerund ആകുന്ന situations:
  - Subject ആകുമ്പോൾ: Swimming is fun. (gerund)
  - Object ആകുമ്പോൾ: I enjoy swimming. (gerund)
  - Preposition-ൻ്റെ ശേഷം: He is good at swimming. (gerund)
• Participle ആകുന്ന situations:
  - Continuous tense-ൽ: He is swimming. (participle — auxiliary 'is' ഉണ്ട്)
  - Adjective ആകുമ്പോൾ: a swimming pool (adjective)
  - Participial phrase: Swimming fast, he won. (participle phrase)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Gerund vs Participle — PSC Main exam-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Reading is my hobby. (Reading = gerund, subject)
  2. She is reading a book. (reading = participle, part of continuous tense)
  3. He is tired of waiting. (waiting = gerund after preposition 'of')
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He is smoking a cigarette — 'Smoking' ഇവിടെ Present Continuous tense-ൻ്റെ part ആണ് (is + smoking). Auxiliary verb 'is' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ ഇത് PARTICIPLE ആണ്, gerund അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'smoking' -ing form ആണ്, students gerund choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Kiran is standing outside the waiting room — 'Standing' = present continuous participle. 'Waiting' = 'waiting room' = adjective (participle acting as adjective). രണ്ടും gerund അല്ല.

❌ (D) Screaming aloud, he ran out of the room — 'Screaming' = participial phrase (Screaming aloud = while screaming). ഇത് 'he' (subject)-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്ന participle ആണ്, gerund അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GERUND = GERMS (noun) — ഗൂഢമായി noun ആകുന്ന verb!' — Gerund = verb disguised as noun. 'Swimming is fun' = 'Noun is fun' = gerund! Is ഉണ്ടോ? (Continuous) → Participle. Is ഇല്ലാതെ subject/object ആണോ? → Gerund!
• Quick test: '-ing word' substitute 'activity' ആക്കി നോക്കൂ — 'Activity is a good exercise' ✓ = gerund! 'He is activity a cigarette' ✗ = participle!
• PSC exam strategy: -ing word-ൻ്റേ function നോക്കൂ. Sentence-ൽ subject/object ആണോ? = gerund. Verb-ൻ്റേ part ആണോ (is/was/were + -ing)? = participle.

questionText: Choose the correct sentence from the choices given below.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) He entered the room.

• 'Enter' = transitive verb — directly object എടുക്കുന്ന verb. Preposition ആവശ്യം ഇല്ല.
• 'He entered the room' = 'അദ്ദേഹം മുറിയിൽ പ്രവേശിച്ചു' — ഇതാണ് correct.
• 'Enter into' = wrong for physical space entry. 'Enter into' ഒരു fixed phrase ആണ് abstract concepts-ൻ്റേ കൂടെ: 'enter into an agreement/discussion' = ശരി. Physical room-ൻ്റേ കൂടെ 'enter into' = wrong.
• Rule: Physical space = 'enter + place' (no preposition). Abstract = 'enter into + noun'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Enter + physical place (NO preposition) | Enter into + abstract concept (agreement/discussion/negotiation)
• 'Enter' without preposition:
  - Enter the room ✓
  - Enter the building ✓
  - Enter the country ✓
• 'Enter into' (abstract only):
  - Enter into an agreement ✓
  - Enter into a discussion ✓
  - Enter into a contract ✓
• Other verbs that DON'T take prepositions (common PSC errors):
  - Discuss something (NOT discuss about)
  - Reach somewhere (NOT reach to/at)
  - Request something (NOT request for)
  - Order something (NOT order for)
  - Accompany someone (NOT accompany with)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Redundant prepositions — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They entered the hall silently. (no preposition)
  2. The two countries entered into a peace treaty. (abstract → into)
  3. Please discuss the matter calmly. (NOT discuss about)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) He entered into the room — 'Into' physical room-ൻ്റേ കൂടെ use ചെയ്യില്ല. 'Enter into' = abstract nouns (agreement, contract). 'Into the room' = wrong. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: 'into' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ correct feel ആകും — 'into the room' Malayalam-ൽ natural, English-ൽ redundant.

❌ (C) He entered by the room — 'By' = means/agent/proximity indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന preposition. 'By the room' = room-ൻ്റേ അടുത്ത്/പ്രകാരം. 'Entered by the room' = unnatural, no standard meaning.

❌ (D) He entered in the room — 'In' ഇവിടെ wrong. 'He is in the room' (location, correct) vs 'He entered in the room' (entry action, wrong). Enter = action → no preposition.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ENTER = ENTER! No extra baggage!' — 'Enter' is complete on its own for physical places. Adding 'into/in/by' = extra unnecessary baggage! 'He entered the room' = clean and correct!
• Golden list — verbs that need NO preposition: Enter (a place), Discuss (a topic), Reach (a place), Request (something), Marry (someone), Resemble (someone). Preposition ഇട്ടാൽ = error!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Enter' + physical place question-ൽ preposition ഇല്ലാത്ത option = answer. Simply 'entered the room' = always correct!

questionText: If he wore a lifebuoy, he ____________

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Would not drown

• 'If he wore a lifebuoy' — if-clause-ൽ past simple ('wore') ആണ്.
• Past simple in if-clause = Second Conditional (present/future imaginary situation).
• Second Conditional formula: If + past simple → would + V1.
• 'Wore' = past simple → main clause = 'would not drown'.
• 'അദ്ദേഹം lifebuoy ധരിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, മുങ്ങില്ലായിരുന്നേനേ' — present imaginary situation (he is probably not wearing one now).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  - Type 1: If + Present → will + V1 (real future)
  - Type 2: If + Past Simple → would + V1 (imaginary present/future)
  - Type 3: If + Past Perfect (had+V3) → would have + V3 (past unreal)
• Type 2 Signal: If-clause = past tense (wore, had, came, went)
• 'Wore' = past simple of 'wear' → Type 2 → would + V1
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditionals — PSC-ൽ guaranteed topic! Type 2 and Type 3 most frequently tested.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had money, I would buy a car. (Type 2 — imaginary)
  2. If she studied harder, she would pass. (Type 2 — imaginary)
  3. If it rains, I will stay home. (Type 1 — real future)
  4. If he had studied, he would have passed. (Type 3 — past unreal)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Will not drown — 'Will' = Type 1 Conditional. Type 1 if-clause-ൽ present tense ആണ്: 'If he wears a lifebuoy, he will not drown.' ഇവിടെ 'wore' (past) ആണ് → Type 2 → 'will' wrong. 🪤 PSC Trap: Students 'future' meaning → 'will' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) may not drown — 'May' = possibility (maybe). Conditional sentences-ൽ 'would' ആണ് standard. 'May' uncertainty indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്, conditional result indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല.

❌ (D) Would not have drowned — 'Would not have drowned' = Type 3 Conditional result. Type 3-ൽ if-clause = past perfect (had + V3): 'If he had worn a lifebuoy, he would not have drowned.' ഇവിടെ 'wore' (past simple) ആണ് — past perfect അല്ല → Type 3 result wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: '1-1-1, 2-2-2, 3-3-3 pattern!' — Type 1: Present+Will. Type 2: Past+Would. Type 3: Had+Would Have. If-clause tense = type number × 1 past step!
• Wore = past simple = Type 2 = Would. Had worn = past perfect = Type 3 = Would have. Wears = present = Type 1 = Will!
• PSC exam strategy: If-clause-ലെ verb tense identify ചെയ്യൂ → wore (past) = Type 2 → would + V1 = answer!

questionText:
Convert the following sentence into reported speech.
Sheela asked me: "do you believe in the essential goodness of humanity?"
optionA: Sheela asked me whether I believed in the essential goodness of humanity.
optionB: Sheela asked me whether he believed in the essential goodness of humanity.
optionC: Sheela asked me that I believed in the essential goodness of humanity.
optionD: Sheela asked me whether I believe in the essential goodness of humanity.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Sheela asked me whether I believed in the essential goodness of humanity.

• Direct speech: 'do you believe...?' — Yes/No question (do/does/did/is/are/was/were ഉള്ള question).
• Yes/No question reported speech → 'whether' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'if' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'you' (Sheela was asking 'me') → 'I' (reporter = me, first person).
• Tense backshift: 'believe' (present) → 'believed' (past) — reporting verb 'asked' (past) ആണ്.
• Option A: whether ✓ + I (correct pronoun) ✓ + believed (tense backshift) ✓ = Correct!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  - Yes/No question → whether / if
  - Wh- question (what/where/when/who/why/how) → same wh- word
• Yes/No question reported changes:
  - Question mark removed
  - 'whether/if' added
  - Auxiliary verb (do/does/did) removed
  - Tense backshift
  - Pronouns adjusted
• Tense backshift table:
  - Present simple → Past simple (believe → believed)
  - Present continuous → Past continuous
  - Past simple → Past perfect
  - Will → Would
  - Can → Could
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported speech (questions) — PSC Main exam-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He asked: 'Are you happy?' → He asked whether I was happy.
  2. She asked: 'What do you want?' → She asked what I wanted.
  3. He asked: 'Can you help?' → He asked whether I could help.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) whether he believed — 'He' wrong pronoun ആണ്. Sheela 'me'-നോട് ചോദിക്കുന്നതാണ് — 'you' = 'me' (reporter) = 'I'. 'He' = third person, ഇവിടെ ആ context ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Sheela' = she, 'me' = he? Confused pronoun.

❌ (C) asked me that I believed — 'That' = statements-ൻ്റേ reported speech-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'She said that...' Yes/No question-ൽ 'whether/if' ആണ് ആവശ്യം. 'That' ഇവിടെ grammatically incorrect.

❌ (D) whether I believe — 'Believe' = tense backshift ചെയ്തില്ല! Reporting verb 'asked' = past tense. Past reporting verb → present tense 'believe' → past 'believed' ആകണം. Present tense retain ചെയ്‌താൽ = error.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'YES/NO = WHETHER! WH = WH!' — Yes/No question reported = Whether/If. Wh- question reported = same wh- word. Simple!
• 3 check points in reported speech: (1) Whether/if (Yes/No) ✓, (2) Pronoun (you → I/he/she) ✓, (3) Tense backshift (believe → believed) ✓ — check all 3 → A is the only option with all 3 correct!
• PSC exam strategy: Options-ൽ quickly scan: 'that' കണ്ടാൽ (C) = wrong for question. Tense not shifted (D) = wrong. Wrong pronoun (B) = wrong. Remaining = answer!

questionText:
Dora is a scientist who is interested in __the fossil history of life__.
Choose the suitable one-word expression for the parts given in italics.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Palaeontologist

• 'The fossil history of life' = fossils, prehistoric organisms, ancient life-ൻ്റേ study.
• 'Palaeontologist' = a scientist who studies fossils and prehistoric life (പ്രാചീന ജീവജാലങ്ങളെ fossils വഴി പഠിക്കുന്ന ശാസ്ത്രജ്ഞൻ).
• 'Palaeontology' = paleo (ancient) + ontos (being) + logos (study) = ancient life study.
• Dinosaur bones, fossils, prehistoric plants — ഇവ study ചെയ്യുന്നത് = Palaeontologist.
• Dora = fossil history-ൽ interested = Palaeontologist.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Fossils + Prehistoric life = Palaeontologist
• ⭐ PSC favourite '-ologist' scientists:
  - Palaeontologist = fossil/prehistoric life (paleo = ancient)
  - Anthropologist = human societies and cultures (anthropo = human)
  - Archaeologist = human past through artifacts and excavation (archaio = ancient + human)
  - Gynaecologist = women's reproductive health (gyne = woman)
  - Ornithologist = birds (ornitho = bird)
  - Entomologist = insects (entomo = insect)
  - Geologist = earth and rocks (geo = earth)
  - Meteorologist = weather (meteoro = atmosphere)
  - Cardiologist = heart (cardio = heart)
  - Oncologist = cancer (onco = tumor)
• Etymology: PALEO = ancient (Greek) → Palaeontology, Palaeolithic (Stone Age)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes (scientist types) — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The palaeontologist discovered a dinosaur fossil. (fossils)
  2. The anthropologist studied tribal customs. (human society)
  3. The archaeologist excavated ancient ruins. (artifacts/ruins)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Anthropologist — 'Anthropologist' = studies human societies, cultures, and evolution (മനുഷ്യ സമൂഹം). Fossil history ഇതിൻ്റേ primary focus അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ancient' → 'anthropologist' ആകും students.

❌ (B) Archaeologist — 'Archaeologist' = studies human past through physical artifacts, excavations (utensils, buildings, tools). 'Fossil history of life' ≠ archaeology. Archaeology = human-made objects. Paleontology = natural fossils/prehistoric organisms.

❌ (C) Gynaecologist — 'Gynaecologist' = doctor specializing in women's reproductive health. Fossils/prehistoric life-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല. This option is clearly wrong but tests if students confuse '-ologist' words.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PALEO = OLD/ANCIENT = FOSSILS!' — 'Paleo' diet = ancient diet. Paleolithic = ancient stone age. PALEOntologist = ancient + life = FOSSILS! 'Paleo' = old → ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ fossil = palaeontologist guaranteed!
• Key distinction: Archaeologist vs Palaeontologist — Archaeologist = human artifacts (pots, tools). Palaeontologist = natural fossils (dinosaurs, ancient plants). Both study old things but different types!
• PSC exam strategy: 'fossil history of life' = paleo (ancient life). PALEO root ഓർക്കൂ → palaeontologist = guaranteed answer!

questionText: What is the meaning of the idiom, 'kick the bucket'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) To die

• 'Kick the bucket' = to die (informal/humorous expression for death).
• 'അദ്ദേഹം bucket kick ചെയ്‌തു' = informal way of saying 'he died'.
• Origin: ഒരു theory പ്രകാരം — execution/suicide-ൽ bucket kick ചെയ്ത് നിന്നിരുന്ന bucket remove ചെയ്യുന്നത് → death. Another theory: 'bucket' = beam in French ('buquet') used for slaughtering — animals' legs kick when hung.
• PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന idiom.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Kick the bucket = Die
• ⭐ PSC favourite idioms about life and death:
  - Kick the bucket = die
  - Bite the dust = die/fail
  - Meet one's maker = die
  - Pop one's clogs = die (British)
  - Breathe one's last = die
  - Burn the midnight oil = work very late
  - Bite the bullet = endure a difficult situation bravely
  - Beat around the bush = avoid the main point
  - Break the ice = start a conversation in awkward situation
  - Spill the beans = reveal a secret
  - Let the cat out of the bag = accidentally reveal a secret
  - Hit the nail on the head = say exactly the right thing
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — PSC-ൽ guaranteed topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The old professor finally kicked the bucket last year. (died)
  2. He burned the midnight oil for his exams. (worked late)
  3. She spilled the beans about the surprise party. (revealed secret)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) To lose a game — 'Kick' = game, 'bucket' = score കിട്ടാത്ത feeling — ഈ literal interpretation wrong ആണ്. Idioms literal ആയി interpret ചെയ്യില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'kick' + 'game' = lose a game feel ആകും.

❌ (C) To hit the target — 'Kick' + 'bucket' = aim at something feel ആകും. 'Hit the nail on the head' = hit the target (correct idiom). 'Kick the bucket' ≠ hit target.

❌ (D) To curse — 'Kick' = anger feel ആണ്, students 'curse' choose ചെയ്യും. 'Kick the bucket' ൻ്റേ meaning anger/curse-ആയി ബന്ധപ്പെടുന്നതല്ല — informal death expression ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BUCKET LIST = things to do BEFORE dying! KICK the bucket = DONE with bucket list = DIED!' — 'Bucket list' (before-death list) ഓർക്കൂ. Kick = finish/done. Kick the bucket = done with life = die!
• PSC idiom golden list: kick the bucket (die), bite the dust (fail/die), burn midnight oil (work late), spill the beans (reveal secret), break the ice (start conversation) — ഈ 5 ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed marks!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Kick the bucket' = classic PSC idiom, direct meaning: TO DIE. No second thought needed — B is the answer!

questionText:
There was a rumour about an __advancing__ army.
Choose the most appropriate word opposite in meaning of the word given in italics.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Retreating

• 'Advancing army' = forward movement-ൽ ഉള്ള (enemy-നോട് approach ചെയ്യുന്ന) army.
• 'Retreating army' = backward movement-ൽ ഉള്ള (enemy-ൽ നിന്ന് withdraw ചെയ്യുന്ന) army.
• Military context-ൽ 'advance' (മുന്നോട്ട്) ↔ 'retreat' (പിന്നോട്ട്) = exact opposites.
• 'Retreating' = exact military antonym of 'advancing' — army context-ൽ perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Advancing (forward) ↔ Retreating (backward/withdrawing) — military context
• Word family of 'advance':
  - Advance (verb/noun) = move forward
  - Advanced (adjective) = ahead, progressive
  - Advancement (noun) = progress
  - Advancing (adjective/participle) = moving forward
• Word family of 'retreat':
  - Retreat (verb/noun) = move back, withdraw
  - Retreating (participle) = withdrawing
• Receding vs Retreating:
  - Receding = gradually moving back (receding tide, receding hairline) — natural/gradual
  - Retreating = military withdrawal or backing away from danger
  - In army context → Retreating is the specific antonym
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The advancing army crossed the border. (moving forward)
  2. The retreating army left the battlefield. (withdrawing)
  3. The tide is receding. (gradual natural backward movement)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) disadvancing — 'Disadvancing' = not a real English word! 'dis-' prefix 'advance'-ൻ്റേ കൂടെ use ചെയ്യില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'dis-' = opposite prefix → students 'disadvancing' choose ചെയ്യും. Real word: retreat/recede.

❌ (B) Receding — 'Receding' = gradually moving away (natural process). 'Receding hairline', 'receding tide' — gradual, natural movement. Army context-ൽ specific military withdrawal term 'retreating' ആണ് more appropriate antonym.

❌ (D) Proceeding — 'Proceeding' = continuing forward, moving ahead. ഇത് 'advancing'-ൻ്റേ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല! 'Proceeding' = going forward = advancing. Wrong direction.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ARMY ADVANCE = ATTACK! ARMY RETREAT = RUN BACK!' — Advancing army = ആക്രമിക്കുന്ന army. Retreating army = ഓടിപ്പോകുന്ന army. ADVANCE ↔ RETREAT = military opposites!
• Receding vs Retreating: Receding = tide/hairline (nature). Retreating = army/people (intentional withdrawal). Army context → Retreating!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Disadvancing' = fake word → eliminate. 'Proceeding' = synonym not antonym → eliminate. 'Receding' vs 'Retreating' — army context → Retreating wins!

questionText:
"Any way, we have to ________ this problem."
Choose the appropriate phrasal verb from the choices given below.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Sort out

• 'Sort out' = to deal with/resolve/solve a problem (ഒരു problem resolve ചെയ്യുക).
• 'We have to sort out this problem' = 'ഈ problem-ൽ നിന്ന് ഒരു resolution ഉണ്ടാക്കണം'.
• 'Sort out' = organize confusion into order, resolve a difficulty.
• Context: 'we have to _____ this problem' = problem solve ചെയ്യണം = sort out.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Sort out = Resolve/Solve (a problem, situation, confusion)
• ⭐ PSC favourite 'out' phrasal verbs:
  - Sort out = resolve, organize (problem/situation solve ചെയ്യുക)
  - Find out = discover information (information കണ്ടെത്തുക)
  - Seek out = search for deliberately (specifically search ചെയ്‌തു find ചെയ്യുക)
  - Put out = extinguish (fire/light/cigarette), also: inconvenience someone
  - Work out = solve (a problem/calculation), also: exercise
  - Figure out = understand/solve
  - Rule out = eliminate as a possibility
  - Carry out = perform/execute a task
  - Point out = indicate/draw attention to
  - Stand out = be noticeable
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Let's sort out this misunderstanding. (resolve)
  2. Can you find out when the train arrives? (discover)
  3. They sought out the best doctor. (deliberately searched)
  4. Firefighters put out the fire. (extinguish)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Find out — 'Find out' = discover information ('find out the answer', 'find out who did it'). Problem-നെ solve/resolve ചെയ്യുക അല്ല — information discover ചെയ്യുകയാണ്. 'Find out this problem' = awkward/wrong meaning. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'find' + 'problem' = solution find feel.

❌ (C) Seek out — 'Seek out' = deliberately search for something/someone specific. 'Seek out this problem' = strange meaning. Problem ഇതിനകം identified ആണ്, ഇനി search ചെയ്യേണ്ടതില്ല. Solve ചെയ്യണം = sort out.

❌ (D) Put out — 'Put out' primarily = extinguish (fire, candle, cigarette). Problem extinguish ചെയ്യുക idiomatic ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'sort out' ആണ് standard. 'Put out a problem' = unusual. Standard usage: 'put out a fire/light'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SORT = ORGANIZE = SOLVE! Sort out = ORGANIZE the problem OUT of your life!' — Sorting = putting things in order. Sort out a problem = organize/remove the problem = SOLVE IT!
• Problem-solving phrasal verbs: Sort out, Work out, Figure out, Resolve — ഇവ ഒക്കെ problem solve ചെയ്യുന്നതിന്. Find out = discover. Seek out = search. Put out = extinguish.
• PSC exam strategy: 'problem solve' context = 'sort out'. Problem + solve feeling = sort out, work out, figure out. Options-ൽ sort out ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ = first choice!

questionText: What is the expansion of "e.g." meaning "for example"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Exempli gratia

• 'e.g.' = Latin 'exempli gratia' = 'for the sake of example' = 'for example'.
• 'Exempli' = of an example (genitive form of 'exemplum' = example).
• 'Gratia' = for the sake of, out of (Latin).
• 'e.g.' = 'exempli gratia' — ഇത് exactly 'for example' ആണ്.
• Usage: 'I like fruits, e.g., apples and mangoes.' = for example, apples and mangoes.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: e.g. = exempli gratia = for example
• ⭐ PSC favourite Latin abbreviations:
  - e.g. = exempli gratia = for example (examples കൊടുക്കുന്നതിന്)
  - i.e. = id est = that is (clarification ചെയ്യുന്നതിന്)
  - etc. = et cetera = and so on (list continue ആകുന്നു)
  - vs. = versus = against
  - cf. = confer = compare
  - NB = nota bene = note well (important point)
  - P.S. = post scriptum = after writing (letter-ൻ്റേ ശേഷം add ചെയ്യുന്ന note)
  - A.M. = ante meridiem = before noon
  - P.M. = post meridiem = after noon
  - viz. = videlicet = namely (specific list)
• e.g. vs i.e.: e.g. = examples (list-ൻ്റേ part). i.e. = exact meaning (complete explanation).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Abbreviations/Latin phrases — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I enjoy water sports, e.g., swimming and surfing. (for example)
  2. The CEO, i.e., the chief executive, will attend. (that is)
  3. We need food, water, shelter, etc. (and so on)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Exempli gratuise — 'Gratuise' = not a Latin word. Correct: 'gratia'. 'Gratuise' = fake/misspelled option. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Exempli' correct part ഉള്ളതിനാൽ students confirm ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം, 'gratuise' wrong spelling miss ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) Example gratui — 'Example' = English word, Latin abbreviation-ൽ English word ഇല്ല. 'Exempli' (Latin) ആണ് correct, 'Example' (English) അല്ല. 'Gratui' = also incomplete/wrong Latin.

❌ (C) Exaea gratia — 'Exaea' = not a Latin word at all! 'Exempli' ആണ് correct first word. 'Exaea' = completely wrong. 'Gratia' part correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ first word totally wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'e.g. = EXAMPLE GIVEN! — e for example, g for given!' — Simple English: e.g. = Example Given. Real Latin: Exempli Gratia. Remember either way!
• e.g. vs i.e. trick: 'e.g. = EGG (example, guess) — I give examples'. 'i.e. = I EXPLAIN' — complete clarification. 'I like citrus fruits, e.g., lemons.' (examples follow). 'He is vegetarian, i.e., he doesn't eat meat.' (exact meaning follows).
• PSC exam strategy: Latin abbreviations ഒരു list ആക്കി ഓർക്കൂ: e.g.=exempli gratia, i.e.=id est, etc.=et cetera, P.S.=post scriptum — ഈ 4 = guaranteed PSC marks!

questionText: A group of eagles is called:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Convocation

• Eagles-ൻ്റെ group-നെ 'Convocation' എന്ന് വിളിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Convocation' = a formal gathering/assembly — eagles-ൻ്റേ ceremonial gathering-ൻ്റേ image.
• Eagles soaring together in the sky = convocation of eagles.
• Collective nouns for animals PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ചോദിക്കുന്ന topic ആണ്.
• 'ഒരു കൂട്ടം കഴുകൻ = A convocation of eagles' — ഇത് ഓർത്തിരിക്കണം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Collective noun = group-നെ ഒറ്റ word-ൽ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന noun
• ⭐ PSC favourite collective nouns for animals:
  - Eagles = Convocation
  - Owls = Parliament
  - Crows = Murder
  - Lions = Pride
  - Wolves = Pack
  - Fish = School / Shoal
  - Bees = Swarm
  - Ants = Colony
  - Geese = Gaggle (on ground) / Skein (in flight)
  - Elephants = Herd
  - Monkeys = Troop
  - Cats = Clowder
  - Dogs = Pack
  - Whales = Pod
  - Butterflies = Flutter
  - Kangaroos = Mob
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns for animals — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A convocation of eagles circled the mountain. (eagles)
  2. A pride of lions rested under the tree. (lions)
  3. A swarm of bees attacked the hive. (bees)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Parliament — 'Parliament' = a group of OWLS-ൻ്റേ collective noun ആണ്! Eagles-ൻ്റേ അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: 'Parliament' = formal gathering feel, eagles formal birds → students confuse ചെയ്യും. Parliament = Owls, Convocation = Eagles!

❌ (C) Council — 'Council' = ഒരു committee/advisory group (human). Animal collective noun ആയി standard ആയി use ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല. Eagles-ൻ്റേ group = convocation, council അല്ല.

❌ (D) Headquarters — 'Headquarters' = main office/base of an organization. Collective noun അല്ല. Eagles-ൻ്റേ group-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EAGLE = CONVOCATION (graduation ceremony)!' — Eagles = majestic, proud birds. Convocation = graduation = proud achievement! Eagles-ൻ്റേ group = Convocation. Parliament = Owls (parliament = wise decisions, owls = wise birds)!
• Eagles vs Owls confusion: Eagles = CONVOCATION (majestic flight together). Owls = PARLIAMENT (wise, sitting in judgment). ഈ pair ഓർക്കൂ!
• PSC exam strategy: Animal collective nouns list — Eagles/Convocation, Owls/Parliament, Lions/Pride, Bees/Swarm, Fish/School — ഈ 5 pairs guaranteed PSC material!

questionText: Find out the compound noun:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Blockhead

• 'Blockhead' = block + head = a stupid person (മണ്ടൻ) — compound NOUN ആണ്.
• 'Blockhead' ഒരു person-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന NOUN ആണ് — 'He is a blockhead.'
• Compound noun = two words combine ചെയ്ത് ഒരു new NOUN ഉണ്ടാകുന്നു.
• Test: 'Blockhead' sentence-ൽ noun position-ൽ (subject/object) use ചെയ്യാം → compound noun ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Compound NOUN = two words → new NOUN (person/place/thing/idea)
• Types of compound words:
  - Compound Noun: two words = noun (blockhead, football, notebook, sunlight)
  - Compound Verb: two words = verb (brainwash, proofread, babysit, daydream)
  - Compound Adjective: hyphenated usually, = adjective (ice-cold, grass-fed, well-known, kind-hearted)
• Identifying compound nouns:
  - Can it be subject/object in a sentence? → noun
  - 'He is a ___' → if fits = noun
  - 'Blockhead' = 'He is a blockhead.' ✓ → noun
  - 'Brainwash' = 'They brainwash people.' = verb, not noun in primary use
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Compound words — Parts of Speech identification topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ compound nouns:
  1. Blockhead = stupid person (noun)
  2. Football = sport (noun)
  3. Notebook = writing book (noun)
  4. Sunflower = plant (noun)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Brainwash — 'Brainwash' = primarily a compound VERB (to brainwash someone = to manipulate their thinking). 'They brainwash prisoners.' = verb. Noun form: 'brainwashing'. Compound noun question-ൽ answer wrong. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'brain' + 'wash' = noun feel, students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) Ice-cold — 'Ice-cold' = compound ADJECTIVE (hyphenated). 'An ice-cold drink' = adjective modifying noun. Noun അല്ല — person/place/thing indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല.

❌ (D) Grass-fed — 'Grass-fed' = compound ADJECTIVE (hyphenated past participle). 'Grass-fed cattle' = adjective. Noun position-ൽ use ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'COMPOUND NOUN = it IS something (a person/thing)!' — 'Blockhead IS a person (stupid one).' = NOUN! 'Brainwash = an ACTION' = VERB! 'Ice-cold = a QUALITY' = ADJECTIVE!
• Quick test for compound noun: Put it after 'He is a ___' or 'I have a ___.' If it fits naturally = compound noun! 'He is a blockhead ✓' 'He is a brainwash ✗' 'He is an ice-cold ✗'
• PSC exam strategy: Hyphenated words (-) = almost always adjective. Verb-feel word = verb. Clear person/thing = noun. 'Blockhead' = clearly a person → compound noun!

questionText: Antonym of the word 'lasting' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Momentary

• 'Lasting' = continuing for a long time, permanent, enduring (ദീർഘകാലം നിലനിൽക്കുന്ന).
• 'Momentary' = lasting for only a moment, very brief, transient (ഒരു നിമിഷം മാത്രം ഉള്ള).
• Lasting (permanent/long) ↔ Momentary (brief/instant) = exact opposites.
• 'Momentary pain' = just for a second. 'Lasting peace' = long-lasting. Perfect antonyms!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Lasting (long duration) ↔ Momentary (very brief)
• Synonyms of 'lasting': perpetual, eternal, infinite, permanent, enduring, everlasting
• Antonyms of 'lasting': momentary, fleeting, transient, temporary, brief, short-lived
• ⭐ Options A, C, D are all SYNONYMS of 'lasting'!
  - Perpetual = continuing forever (synonym)
  - Infinite = without end (synonym)
  - Eternal = lasting forever (synonym)
• Word family of 'moment':
  - Moment (noun) = a very short time
  - Momentary (adjective) = very brief
  - Momentarily (adverb) = for a moment
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms — PSC-ൽ guaranteed topic! 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The pain was only momentary. (just for a second)
  2. They built a lasting friendship. (long-term)
  3. The peace was momentary — soon war resumed. (brief)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Perpetual — 'Perpetual' = never ending, continuous = SYNONYM of 'lasting'. Antonym അല്ല. 'Perpetual motion', 'perpetual peace' = forever continuing. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: 3 synonyms + 1 antonym pattern. Students ഒരു synonym choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം.

❌ (C) Infinite — 'Infinite' = without limit or end = SYNONYM of 'lasting'. 'Infinite patience', 'infinite universe' = everlasting. Antonym അല്ല.

❌ (D) Eternal — 'Eternal' = lasting forever = SYNONYM of 'lasting'. 'Eternal life', 'eternal love' = everlasting. Antonym അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MOMENT = BLINK! Momentary = BLINK and it's gone!' — A moment = blink of an eye. Momentary = blink-fast duration = opposite of lasting!
• Antonym pattern trick: Options A, C, D (perpetual, infinite, eternal) — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'forever' meaning. Same meaning = synonyms = eliminate all 3! Remaining B (momentary) = antonym!
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ options-ൽ 3 same-direction words കണ്ടാൽ → ആ 3 = synonyms → 4th option = antonym answer!

questionText: Which is the correct spelling?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Lieutenant

• 'Lieutenant' = correct spelling: L-I-E-U-T-E-N-A-N-T (10 letters).
• 'Lieutenant' = a military/police officer rank (ലഫ്റ്റനൻ്റ്).
• Breakdown: Lieu + ten + ant = Lieutenant.
• 'Lieu' = place (French) — 'in lieu of' = in place of. Lieutenant = one who holds the place (of a superior).
• British pronunciation: 'Leftenant' (ഇങ്ങനെ pronounce ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം, പക്ഷേ spelling 'Lieutenant' ആണ്!).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: L-I-E-U-T-E-N-A-N-T (Lieu + ten + ant)
• Etymology: French 'lieu' (place) + 'tenant' (holding) = one holding the place of a superior
• 'Lieu' word family:
  - Lieutenant (military rank)
  - In lieu of = instead of (ഇതിൻ്റേ പകരം)
• British vs American pronunciation:
  - British: 'Leftenant' (pronounced like 'left')
  - American: 'Lootenent'
  - But SPELLING is always: Lieutenant (not Leftenant!)
• ⭐ PSC favourite tricky spellings:
  - Lieutenant = L-I-E-U-T-E-N-A-N-T
  - Necessary = N-E-C-E-S-S-A-R-Y (1C + 2S)
  - Accommodation = A-C-C-O-M-M-O-D-A-T-I-O-N (2C + 2M)
  - Occurrence = O-C-C-U-R-R-E-N-C-E (2C + 2R)
  - Separate = S-E-P-A-R-A-T-E (not 'seperate')
  - Privilege = P-R-I-V-I-L-E-G-E
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The lieutenant led the platoon bravely. (military rank)
  2. I accepted cash in lieu of a cheque. (in place of)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Leiutenant — 'ei' ആദ്യം, പിന്നെ 'u' — wrong order. Correct: 'lieu' (L-I-E-U). 'Lei-u' = wrong vowel order. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ei' vs 'ie' confusion — students common mistake.

❌ (B) Leuitenant — 'Leu' ≠ 'Lieu'. Vowel order wrong: L-E-U vs correct L-I-E-U. 'i' missing ആണ്. Similar sound, wrong spelling.

❌ (C) Leftenant — ഇത് British PRONUNCIATION ആണ്! British people 'Lieutenant'-നെ 'Leftenant' എന്ന് pronounce ചെയ്യുന്നു. പക്ഷേ ഇത് correct SPELLING അല്ല. Pronunciation = leftenant, Spelling = Lieutenant. 🪤 PSC Trap: British accent-ൽ 'leftenant' കേൾക്കുന്നതിനാൽ ആ spelling correct feel ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LIEU = PLACE (French)! Lieutenant = PLACE HOLDER!' — 'In lieu of' = in place of. Lieutenant = place holder (holds place of higher officer). LIEU spelling: L-I-E-U! Like 'LIED' but with U at end: LIE+U = LIEU!
• Don't confuse pronunciation with spelling: British say 'leftenant' but write 'lieutenant'. PSC asks spelling, not pronunciation!
• Break it: LIE + U + TENANT = LIEUTENANT. 'A tenant LIED to his lieu-landlord' — silly story, ഓർക്കൂ!

questionText:
When does the class __commence__?
The meaning of the word 'Commence' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) start

• 'Commence' = to begin, to start (ആരംഭിക്കുക).
• 'When does the class commence?' = 'ക്ലാസ്സ് എപ്പോൾ ആരംഭിക്കും?'
• 'Commence' = formal/official word for 'start/begin'.
• 'Commencement ceremony' = graduation ceremony (beginning of a new chapter).
• Start = Commence = Begin — same meaning, different register (commence = more formal).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നிയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Commence = Start = Begin (formal register)
• Word family of 'commence':
  - Commence (verb) = to start
  - Commencement (noun) = the beginning; also: graduation ceremony
  - Commencing (participle) = starting
• Usage: 'Commence' is more formal than 'start':
  - Formal: 'The ceremony will commence at 9 AM.'
  - Informal: 'The party starts at 9 PM.'
• ⭐ PSC favourite formal vocabulary synonyms:
  - Commence = start/begin
  - Conclude = end/finish
  - Procure = obtain/get
  - Reside = live/stay
  - Inquire = ask
  - Terminate = end/stop
  - Enumerate = list/count
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms of formal vocabulary — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The meeting will commence at 10 AM. (= start)
  2. The commencement ceremony was held outdoors. (= graduation/beginning)
  3. Work commenced on the new building. (= started)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) end — 'End' = opposite of 'commence'. Commence = start. End = finish. Complete opposite meaning! 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Conclude' and 'end' both appear — students might pick wrongly.

❌ (B) continue — 'Continue' = to keep going (already started). Commence = to start for the first time. 'Continue' implies already in progress; 'commence' = fresh start.

❌ (C) conclude — 'Conclude' = to end, to finish (='end' synonym). Commence ↔ Conclude = antonyms! 'The speech commenced at 9 and concluded at 10.' Start → End. Conclude = opposite of commence.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'COMMENCE = COME + MENCE = COME to START!' — 'Come' + start feeling = commence! Or: 'Commencement speech' = GRADUATION = a NEW BEGINNING = START!
• Commence ↔ Conclude = antonyms pair! PSC ഈ pair ഒരുമിച്ച് test ചെയ്യുന്നു. 'The meeting commenced at 9 and concluded at 11.' = Start → End!
• PSC exam strategy: Formal vocab synonym question-ൽ simple English equivalent search ചെയ്യൂ. Commence = start (simple) = D = answer!

questionText: Add prefix and suffix to the base form 'believe':

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) unbelievable

• 'un' (prefix) + 'believe' (base) + 'able' (suffix) = 'unbelievable'.
• Prefix 'un-' = not (negative). Suffix '-able' = capable of being.
• 'Unbelievable' = not capable of being believed = അവിശ്വസനീയം.
• 'That's unbelievable!' = 'ഇത് വിശ്വസിക്കാൻ പറ്റില്ല!' — common everyday word.
• Both prefix AND suffix correctly added to 'believe' → unbelievable ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: un + believe + able = unbelievable
• Common prefixes with negative meaning:
  - un- = not (unhappy, unclear, unbelievable)
  - in- = not (incomplete, incorrect, invisible)
  - im- = not (impossible, impatient, impolite)
  - ir- = not (irregular, irresponsible)
  - il- = not (illegal, illegible, illogical)
  - dis- = not/opposite (disagree, dishonest, disbelieve)
  - mis- = wrong (mistake, misunderstand, misplace)
• Common suffixes:
  - -able/-ible = capable of being (believable, visible, comfortable)
  - -ness = state of (happiness, kindness, sadness)
  - -ful = full of (beautiful, helpful, wonderful)
  - -less = without (homeless, careless, speechless)
  - -ment = result of action (movement, achievement, development)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Prefixes and Suffixes — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. His performance was unbelievable! (un + believe + able)
  2. It is impossible to fly without wings. (im + possible)
  3. She is irresponsible with money. (ir + responsible)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) enbelievable — 'en-' prefix 'believe'-ൻ്റേ കൂടെ use ചെയ്യില്ല. 'En-' prefix = cause to be (enable, encourage, enlarge). 'Enbelievable' = not a real English word. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'en-' = a real prefix → students use ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം.

❌ (B) forbelievable — 'for-' = not a standard English prefix for adjective formation here. 'Forbelievable' = not a real word at all. No English words use 'for-' in this pattern.

❌ (D) onbelievable — 'on-' = not a negative prefix in English. 'On' = preposition (on the table). 'Onbelievable' = not a real word. The correct negative prefix for 'believe' = 'un-'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'UN + BELIEVE + ABLE = UNBELIEVABLE! UN = NOT, ABLE = CAN. So: NOT CAN BELIEVE = UNBELIEVABLE!' — Simple logic chain: un (not) + believe + able (can) = 'cannot believe' = unbelievable!
• Prefix elimination: en/for/on = not real negative prefixes for 'believe'. Only UN = negative for believe family. 'Unbelievable' is a word you use every day!
• PSC exam strategy: Prefix + base + suffix question-ൽ real word recognize ചെയ്യൂ. 'Unbelievable' = common everyday word. Fake words (enbelievable, forbelievable, onbelievable) = eliminate. Real word = C = answer!

questionText: What does the term __id est__ mean? (~~deleted by psc~~)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) that is

• 'Id est' = Latin for 'that is' — abbreviated as 'i.e.' in writing.
• 'Id' = that (Latin demonstrative). 'Est' = is (Latin verb 'esse' = to be).
• 'i.e.' = 'id est' = 'that is' — clarification or exact explanation provide ചെയ്യുന്നതിന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'She is a polyglot, i.e., she speaks many languages.' = 'polyglot' exactly explain ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• i.e. = restate/clarify. e.g. = give examples.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: id est = i.e. = 'that is' (clarification/exact meaning)
• ⭐ PSC favourite Latin abbreviations:
  - id est (i.e.) = that is (clarification)
  - exempli gratia (e.g.) = for example
  - et cetera (etc.) = and so on
  - nota bene (N.B.) = note well
  - post scriptum (P.S.) = after writing
  - versus (vs.) = against
  - vice versa = the other way around
  - ante meridiem (A.M.) = before noon
  - post meridiem (P.M.) = after noon
  - per annum (p.a.) = per year
  - ad hoc = for this specific purpose
  - status quo = the existing state of affairs
• i.e. vs e.g. distinction:
  - i.e. = exact/complete explanation ('that is')
  - e.g. = partial examples ('for example' — more examples possible)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin abbreviations — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I need a break, i.e., a holiday. (exact clarification = 'that is')
  2. I like sports, e.g., cricket and football. (examples = 'for example')
  3. P.S. Don't forget to call me. (after writing)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) for example — 'For example' = 'exempli gratia' = 'e.g.' — ഇത് 'e.g.'-ൻ്റേ meaning ആണ്, 'i.e.' (id est)-ൻ്റേ meaning അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: 'i.e.' vs 'e.g.' confusion — both Latin abbreviations, students mix up.

❌ (C) for reference — 'For reference' = not a standard Latin abbreviation meaning. 'cf.' (confer) = compare/refer to. 'Id est' ≠ 'for reference' — completely wrong meaning.

❌ (D) for sure — 'For sure' = colloquial English expression, not a Latin abbreviation meaning at all. 'Id est' has a precise academic meaning = 'that is'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'I.E. = I EXPLAIN! E.G. = EXAMPLE GIVEN!' — i.e. (id est) = I Explain exactly. e.g. (exempli gratia) = Example Given. Never mix them up!
• i.e. vs e.g. usage: 'I love one sport, i.e., cricket.' (only one = exact). 'I love sports, e.g., cricket.' (one of many = example). i.e. = complete. e.g. = partial!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Id est' question-ൽ — 'est' = 'is' (Latin, like Spanish 'es' = is). 'That IS' = 'that is' = id est! Latin 'est' = English 'is' → 'that is' = answer!

🚫 Deleted Question: PSC ഔദ്യോഗിക Final Answer Key-ൽ ഈ ചോദ്യം ഒഴിവാക്കി (deleted/cancelled), സ്കോറിംഗിൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിട്ടില്ല. കൃത്യമായ കാരണം സ്ഥിരീകരിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞിട്ടില്ല — 'id est' = 'that is' എന്നത് വ്യക്തവും widely-accepted ആയ meaning ആണ് (മറ്റ് options-ൽ ambiguity സൃഷ്ടിക്കുന്ന ഒരു genuine alternative ഇവിടെ കാണുന്നില്ല). അതിനാൽ ഈ deletion, ചോദ്യത്തിന്റെ meaning-ലെ ambiguity കൊണ്ടല്ല, മറിച്ച് Malayalam-medium ചോദ്യപേപ്പറിലെ ഒരു printing/translation പിശക് കൊണ്ടാകാം എന്ന് അനുമാനിക്കുന്നു.

questionText: Give the synonym of the word 'abundance':

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) plenty

• 'Abundance' = a very large quantity, more than enough (ആധിക്യം, സമൃദ്ധി).
• 'Plenty' = a large amount, enough and more (ധാരാളം) = synonym of abundance.
• 'Abundance of food' = 'plenty of food' = ധാരാളം ഭക്ഷണം.
• 'Land of plenty' = 'land of abundance' = same meaning.
• PSC context: Both words indicate 'more than enough' → synonyms.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Abundance = Plenty = Plentiful = Ample = Copious
• Synonyms of 'abundance':
  - Plenty = ample amount
  - Profusion = large quantity
  - Plethora = excessive amount
  - Copious = large in quantity
  - Ample = more than enough
  - Affluence = wealth/plenty
• Antonyms of 'abundance':
  - Scarce = rare, not enough
  - Meagre = very small amount
  - Little = small quantity
  - Lack = absence
  - Scarcity = shortage
  - Dearth = shortage
• ⭐ Options A, B, D are all ANTONYMS of 'abundance'!
  - Little = small amount (antonym)
  - Meagre = very small/inadequate (antonym)
  - Scarce = barely available (antonym)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The river valley had an abundance of crops. (= plenty)
  2. There is plenty of time remaining. (= abundance)
  3. Food was scarce during the drought. (= opposite of abundance)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) little — 'Little' = small amount = ANTONYM of abundance. 'Little food' ↔ 'abundance of food'. 🪤 PSC Trap: Synonym ചോദ്യം, students antonym choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം.

❌ (B) meagre — 'Meagre' = very small, inadequate amount = ANTONYM of abundance. 'Meagre rations', 'meagre salary' = very little. Abundance = opposite.

❌ (D) scarce — 'Scarce' = barely available, rare = ANTONYM of abundance. 'Water is scarce in the desert' = very little water. Abundance = opposite of scarce.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ABUNDANCE = ABUN-DANCE = Abundant DANCE moves! LOTS of moves = PLENTY!' — Abundance = lots and lots. Plenty = lots and lots. Same! Little/meagre/scarce = very few = opposite!
• Antonym trick: Options A (little), B (meagre), D (scarce) — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'very few/not enough' meaning. Same direction = eliminate all 3! Remaining C (plenty) = synonym answer!
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym question-ൽ 3 antonyms + 1 synonym pattern common ആണ്. 'Abundance' = lots → 3 options meaning 'less/few' = all antonyms → eliminate → C (plenty) = answer!

questionText:
We are eager to __get on__ in life.
The meaning of the phrasal verb 'get on' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) progress

• 'Get on in life' = to be successful, to make progress in life (ജീവിതത്തിൽ മുന്നേറുക, വിജയിക്കുക).
• 'We are eager to get on in life' = 'ജീവിതത്തിൽ പ്രഗതി ഉണ്ടാകാൻ ഞങ്ങൾ ആഗ്രഹിക്കുന്നു'.
• 'Get on' in this context = advance, make progress, succeed.
• 'How are you getting on?' = 'How are you progressing/doing?' = common usage.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Get on (in life) = progress / succeed / advance
• 'Get on' — multiple meanings (context-dependent):
  - Get on in life = make progress, succeed
  - Get on with someone = have a good relationship ('They get on well.')
  - Get on a bus/train = board
  - Get on with work = continue working
• ⭐ PSC favourite 'get' phrasal verbs:
  - Get on = progress/succeed (in life); board (bus/train)
  - Get over = recover from (illness/shock)
  - Get through = complete/pass (exam); contact (by phone)
  - Get up = rise from bed
  - Get away = escape
  - Get along = have a good relationship
  - Get by = manage with little money/resources
  - Get across = communicate/convey
  - Get down to = start working seriously
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന topic.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She worked hard to get on in her career. (progress)
  2. He got on the train just in time. (boarded)
  3. They get on very well with each other. (good relationship)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) fail — 'Fail' = complete opposite of 'get on in life'. 'We are eager to fail' makes no sense. 'Get on' = positive forward movement. Fail = negative downward. 🪤 PSC Trap: Options include positive + negative, students second-guess.

❌ (C) ruin — 'Ruin' = to destroy or be destroyed. 'Get on in life' = forward movement, not destruction. 'Ruin' is also a strong negative — completely wrong meaning.

❌ (D) fall — 'Fall' = go down, decline. 'Get on' = go up, advance. Opposite directions! 'Fall in life' = failure/decline. 'Get on in life' = success/progress. Complete antonyms.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GET ON = GET ON TOP! Progress = moving up, getting on top of life!' — GET ON = climb up = progress. Like getting on a bus = moving forward! GET ON IN LIFE = moving forward in life = progress!
• Context clue: 'We are EAGER to get on' — eager = positive desire. Positive desire = positive action = progress (not fail/ruin/fall)!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Get on in life' = idiom/phrasal verb for success/progress. When 'eager' + 'get on in life' = clearly positive context → progress = answer. Negative options (fail/ruin/fall) = eliminate!

questionText: Mary is a good student. I like her ________ nature.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Childlike

• 'Mary is a good student' — positive description. 'I like her nature' — positive quality.
• 'Childlike' = having the positive qualities of a child — innocent, simple, pure, trusting (കുട്ടിയെ പോലുള്ള നിഷ്കളങ്കത).
• 'Childlike' = POSITIVE connotation — simplicity, innocence, wonder.
• 'Childlike nature' = pure, innocent, genuine nature = fits 'good student' context perfectly.
• Mary is a good student → positive nature → childlike (innocent, eager to learn).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Childlike = POSITIVE (innocent/pure) | Childish = NEGATIVE (immature/silly)
• '-like' vs '-ish' distinction:
  - -like = resembling in a positive/neutral way (childlike, godlike, lifelike, businesslike)
  - -ish = somewhat like, often negative/dismissive (childish, foolish, snobbish, selfish)
• Connotation pairs:
  - Childlike = innocent, pure, wonder-filled ✓ (positive)
  - Childish = immature, silly, irresponsible ✗ (negative)
  - Womanly = dignified, mature (positive)
  - Womanish = negative/dismissive
  - Manly = strong, dignified (positive)
  - Mannish = negative/masculine in a bad way
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Word formation (-ish vs -like suffix distinction) — PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Her childlike curiosity made everyone smile. (positive = innocent)
  2. Don't be childish — act your age! (negative = immature)
  3. He had a childlike wonder about nature. (positive = pure)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Childish — 'Childish' = NEGATIVE connotation = immature, silly. 'Mary is a good student' → good/positive context. 'I like her childish nature' = 'I like that she is immature' — doesn't fit 'good student'. 🪤 PSC Classic Trap: Childish vs Childlike. Both related to 'child' but opposite connotations!

❌ (B) Childed — 'Childed' = not a real English word at all! No standard suffix '-ed' for 'child' in adjective form. This is a fake option to test vocabulary.

❌ (D) None of these — Option C 'Childlike' is correct and fits perfectly. 'None of these' = wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CHILDLIKE = GOOD child (pure, innocent). CHILDISH = BAD child (naughty, immature)!' — '-LIKE' = likeable = positive. '-ISH' = dismiss-ish = negative/dismissive! LIKE = positive, ISH = often negative!
• Context clue: 'Mary is a GOOD student. I LIKE her nature.' — good + like = positive context. Childlike = positive. Childish = negative. Context automatically tells you the answer!
• PSC exam strategy: '-ish' vs '-like' suffix — remember positive/negative. 'Good student' = positive context = childlike. Any time context is positive → choose '-like' over '-ish'!

questionText: A group of whales is called:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) pod

• തിമിംഗലങ്ങളുടെ ഒരു കൂട്ടത്തെ 'pod' എന്നാണ് വിളിക്കുന്നത്.
• Collective noun-കൾ ഓരോ ജന്തുവിനും വ്യത്യസ്തമാണ് — ഇത് PSC-യുടെ favourite topic ആണ്.
• 'Pod' എന്ന വാക്ക് whales, dolphins, seals തുടങ്ങിയ marine animals-നു ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• ഈ ചോദ്യം straight factual knowledge test ആണ് — ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തുവെച്ചാൽ guarantee mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ഓരോ animal-നും ഒരു specific collective noun ഉണ്ട് — ഇത് memorize ചെയ്യണം.

PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും കൂടുതൽ വരുന്ന Collective Nouns:
• A pod of whales / dolphins
• A pride of lions
• A flock of birds / sheep
• A pack of wolves
• A herd of cattle / elephants
• A school of fish
• A swarm of bees
• A colony of ants / bats
• A troop of monkeys
• A gaggle of geese

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns for animals വർഷംതോറും PSC-ൽ വരുന്നു.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) troops — 'Troop' monkeys-നോ soldiers-നോ ഉള്ള collective noun ആണ്, whales-നല്ല.

❌ (C) colony — 'Colony' ants, bats, penguins തുടങ്ങിയവർക്ക് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. Whales-നു ഇത് ശരിയല്ല.

❌ (D) swarm — 'Swarm' bees, locusts, flies തുടങ്ങിയ insects-നു മാത്രം ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Whales travel in pods like peas in a pod' — pod-ൽ peas ഇരിക്കുന്നതുപോലെ whales ഒരുമിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നു!
• PSC-ൽ marine animals (whales, dolphins, seals) collective noun ചോദിക്കുമ്പോൾ ഉത്തരം മിക്കവാറും 'pod' ആണ്.
• ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കുക: Pod=whales, Pride=lions, Pack=wolves, Flock=birds — ഈ four guaranteed PSC questions-ആണ്!

questionText: The antonym of 'contaminate' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) purify

• 'Contaminate' എന്നാൽ 'മലിനപ്പെടുത്തുക' — ദോഷകരമായ substances കലർത്തി pure ആയ ഒന്നിനെ dirty ആക്കുക.
• Antonym (വിപരീതം) = 'purify' — ശുദ്ധീകരിക്കുക, clean ആക്കുക.
• 'Purify water' (ജലം ശുദ്ധീകരിക്കുക) — ഇത് daily life-ൽ പരിചിതമായ ഉദാഹരണം.
• PSC antonym ചോദ്യങ്ങളിൽ opposite meaning clearly മനസ്സിലാക്കിയാൽ answer obvious ആകും.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Antonym = exact opposite meaning.
• 'Contaminate' (മലിനമാക്കുക) → Antonym → 'Purify' (ശുദ്ധമാക്കുക)

Word family:
• Contaminate (verb) → Contamination (noun) → Contaminated (adjective)
• Purify (verb) → Purification (noun) → Pure (adjective)

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• The factory contaminated the river. (ഫാക്ടറി നദി മലിനമാക്കി)
• We need to purify drinking water. (കുടിവെള്ളം ശുദ്ധീകരിക്കണം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഉറപ്പായും ഒരു ചോദ്യം!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) defile — 'Defile' = മലിനപ്പെടുത്തുക, അശുദ്ധമാക്കുക. ഇത് 'contaminate'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്, antonym അല്ല!

❌ (B) invigorate — 'Invigorate' = ഉന്മേഷം നൽകുക, ഊർജ്ജം നൽകുക. ഇത് 'contaminate'-മായി യാതൊരു connection-ഉം ഇല്ല.

❌ (C) taint — 'Taint' = കലർപ്പ് ചേർക്കുക, dirty ആക്കുക. ഇതും 'contaminate'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: defile, taint — ഇവ synonyms-ആണ്, antonyms-അല്ല!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Contaminate = dirty, Purify = clean' — C for Contaminate/Corrupt, P for Pure/Purify.
• PSC antonym ചോദ്യങ്ങളിൽ options-ൽ synonyms-ഉം antonyms-ഉം ഒരുമിച്ചു കൊടുക്കും — carefully meaning check ചെയ്യുക.
• 'Purify' word family: purify, pure, purity, purification — ഇവ ഒക്കെ ഒരുമിച്ചു പഠിക്കുക.

questionText: Either the parents or the girl ________ to blame in this affair. (~~deleted by psc~~)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) is

• 'Either...or' ഉള്ള sentences-ൽ verb, 'or'-നു ശേഷം വരുന്ന subject-നു അനുസരിച്ചിരിക്കും.
• ഇവിടെ 'or'-നു ശേഷം 'the girl' (singular) ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് singular verb 'is' വേണം.
• 'Either the parents or the girl IS to blame' — girl singular ആണ്, so 'is' correct.
• ഈ rule PSC-ൽ ഒരു guaranteed question pattern ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Either...or / Neither...nor — verb follows the NEAREST subject (or-നു / nor-നു ശേഷം ഉള്ള subject)

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• Either the students or the teacher IS responsible. (teacher = singular → is)
• Either the teacher or the students ARE responsible. (students = plural → are)
• Neither Ramu nor his friends HAVE come. (friends = plural → have)
• Neither the boys nor the girl HAS arrived. (girl = singular → has)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Either/or, Neither/nor agreement — ഒരു classic PSC question!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) are — 'Parents' plural ആണ്, പക്ഷേ verb decide ചെയ്യുന്നത് 'or'-നു ശേഷം വരുന്ന 'the girl' (singular) ആണ്. അതുകൊണ്ട് 'are' തെറ്റ്.

❌ (C) has — 'Has' third person singular-ന്റെ perfect form ആണ്. ഇവിടെ 'to blame' (infinitive) ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'is' ആണ് ചേരുക, 'has' അല്ല.

❌ (D) was — Past tense ആണ്. ഈ sentence present situation describe ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ present tense 'is' വേണം. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'parents' plural കണ്ടപ്പോൾ 'are' select ചെയ്യും — nearest subject rule മറക്കരുത്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Either/or, Neither/nor — look at the word BEFORE the blank (nearest subject)!'
• Exam strategy: Either/or ചോദ്യം കണ്ടാൽ 'or'-നു ശേഷം ഉള്ള subject singular ആണോ plural ആണോ എന്ന് first check ചെയ്യുക.
• ഈ same rule: Neither Ramu nor his brothers HAVE come (brothers plural → have).

🚫 Deleted Question: PSC ഔദ്യോഗിക Final Answer Key-ൽ ഈ ചോദ്യം ഒഴിവാക്കി (deleted/cancelled), സ്കോറിംഗിൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയിട്ടില്ല. കൃത്യമായ കാരണം സ്ഥിരീകരിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞിട്ടില്ല — 'nearest subject' rule ഈ either/or sentence-ന് വ്യക്തവും standard ആയ grammar rule ആണ് (മറ്റ് options-ൽ ambiguity സൃഷ്ടിക്കുന്ന ഒരു genuine alternative ഇവിടെ കാണുന്നില്ല). അതിനാൽ ഈ deletion, ചോദ്യത്തിന്റെ meaning-ലെ ambiguity കൊണ്ടല്ല, മറിച്ച് Malayalam-medium ചോദ്യപേപ്പറിലെ ഒരു printing/translation പിശക് കൊണ്ടാകാം എന്ന് അനുമാനിക്കുന്നു.

questionText: Pazhassi Raja ________ a brave fight against British army.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) put up

• 'Put up a fight' = ധൈര്യത്തോടെ ചെറുത്തുനിൽക്കുക, resist ചെയ്യുക.
• Pazhassi Raja ബ്രിട്ടീഷ് സൈന്യത്തിനെതിരെ ധീരമായ പോരാട്ടം നടത്തി — 'put up a brave fight' perfect ആണ്.
• 'Put up' എന്ന phrasal verb ഇവിടെ 'offer resistance' അർഥത്തിൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Historical context-ൽ ഈ phrasal verb use ചെയ്യുന്നത് PSC-ൽ common ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 'PUT' phrasal verbs — ഒരേ verb, വ്യത്യസ്ത meanings:
• Put up = resist / ചെറുത്തുനിൽക്കുക; tolerate / സഹിക്കുക; accommodate / താമസിപ്പിക്കുക
• Put off = postpone / നീട്ടിവെക്കുക
• Put in = insert / കയറ്റുക; apply / അപേക്ഷ നൽകുക
• Put down = suppress / അടിച്ചമർത്തുക; write down / എഴുതുക
• Put out = extinguish / കെടുത്തുക
• Put forward = propose / നിർദ്ദേശിക്കുക

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs with PUT — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരെണ്ണം guaranteed!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) put off — 'Put off' = postpone ചെയ്യുക. 'Put off a fight' = fight നീട്ടിവെക്കുക — ഇത് sentence-ന്റെ heroic meaning-ൽ ചേരുന്നില്ല.

❌ (C) put in — 'Put in effort' = effort ഇടുക. 'Put in a fight' grammatically awkward ആണ്, ഈ context-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.

❌ (D) put down — 'Put down' = suppress ചെയ്യുക, crush ചെയ്യുക. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The British put down the revolt' — ഇങ്ങനെ British-നെ subject ആക്കി put down use ചെയ്യും, Pazhassi Raja-യ്ക്ക് ഈ context ശരിയല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Put UP a fight = Stand UP and fight!' — UP = resist, fight back.
• 'Put off = postpone' (off = away, later-ലേക്ക് മാറ്റി), 'Put down = suppress' (down = crush ചെയ്യുക) — ഈ contrast ഓർക്കുക.
• PSC-ൽ phrasal verb context ഉറ്റുനോക്കുക — sentence meaning ഏതു option-നോടു ചേരുന്നു എന്ന് identify ചെയ്യുക.

questionText:
Mimi said, "I have been writing this letter."
Change it into indirect speech. Mimi said that
optionA: she had been writing that letter.
optionB: she has been writing this letter.
optionC: she had been writing this letter.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) she had been writing that letter

• Direct: 'I have been writing this letter' → Indirect: 'she had been writing that letter'
• 'I' → 'she' (pronoun change)
• 'have been writing' → 'had been writing' (tense backshift: present perfect continuous → past perfect continuous)
• 'this' → 'that' (demonstrative change)
• Mimi said 'that' — reporting verb past tense ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് tense backshift ഉണ്ടാകണം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Tense Backshift in Indirect Speech:
• Simple Present → Simple Past
• Present Continuous → Past Continuous
• Present Perfect → Past Perfect
• Present Perfect Continuous → Past Perfect Continuous ✅ (ഇവിടെ ഇത്!)

Demonstratives:
• this → that
• these → those
• here → there
• now → then

ഉദാഹരണം:
• Direct: 'I am reading.' → Indirect: She said that she was reading.
• Direct: 'I have been writing.' → Indirect: She said that she had been writing.

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Direct to Indirect Speech — ഓരോ paper-ലും 1-2 questions!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) she has been writing this letter — Tense backshift ഇല്ല ('has been' → 'had been' ആകണം). 'This' → 'that' ആകണം. രണ്ടു mistakes!

❌ (C) she had been writing this letter — Tense correct ആണ് ('had been writing') പക്ഷേ 'this' → 'that' ആകണം. Demonstrative change miss ചെയ്തു. 🪤 PSC Trap: ഈ option-ൽ tense ശരിയാണ്, ഒരു student 'this/that' notice ചെയ്യില്ലെങ്കിൽ ഇത് select ചെയ്യും!

❌ (D) she is writing that letter — Tense backshift ഇല്ല. Present continuous-ൽ നിന്ന് past-ലേക്ക് shift ആയില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Said that → Tense goes BACK one step!' — Reporting verb past tense ആണെങ്കിൽ tense ഒരു step past-ലേക്ക് പോകും.
• 'This/these/here/now' → 'that/those/there/then' — ഈ four pairs indirect speech-ൽ always change ആകും.
• PSC Exam strategy: Indirect speech question കണ്ടാൽ — 1) tense check, 2) pronoun check, 3) demonstrative check — ഈ three steps follow ചെയ്യുക.

questionText: Choose the correct noun form for a male swan.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) cob

• Male swan-നെ 'cob' എന്നാണ് വിളിക്കുന്നത്.
• Female swan = pen, Baby swan = cygnet — ഇവ മൂന്നും ഒരുമിച്ചു ഓർക്കണം.
• ഇത് pure factual/vocabulary knowledge ചോദ്യം ആണ്.
• 'Cob' — ഒരിക്കൽ ഓർത്തുവെച്ചാൽ PSC-ൽ guaranteed mark!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Male/Female/Young — Animal gender names PSC favourite topic:

Swan: Male = cob | Female = pen | Young = cygnet
Horse: Male = stallion | Female = mare | Young = foal
Cow: Male = bull | Female = cow | Young = calf
Deer: Male = stag/buck | Female = doe/hind | Young = fawn
Duck: Male = drake | Female = duck | Young = duckling
Dog: Male = dog | Female = bitch | Young = puppy
Lion: Male = lion | Female = lioness | Young = cub
Sheep: Male = ram | Female = ewe | Young = lamb

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Animal gender names — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരെണ്ണം!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) pen — 'Pen' = female swan. Male-ന്റെ ഉത്തരം ചോദിച്ചപ്പോൾ female-ന്റെ name കൊടുത്തു! 🪤 PSC Trap: pen-ഉം cob-ഉം swans-ന്റെ gender names ആണ് — ഏത് male, ഏത് female എന്ന് confuse ആകാൻ chance ഉണ്ട്.

❌ (B) doe — 'Doe' = female deer. Swan-ന് ഇത് relevant അല്ല.

❌ (C) drake — 'Drake' = male duck. Swan-ന്റെ male gender name ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത്, duck-ന്റേത് അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Cob = male swan' — Cob ഉരുളക്കിഴങ്ങ് (corn cob) പോലെ sturdy ആണ്, male swan sturdy ആണ്!
• Swan family: Cob (male) + Pen (female) + Cygnet (baby) — ഈ three ഒരുമിച്ചു PSC-ൽ ഏതു form-ലും വരാം.
• Drake = male DUCK (D for Drake, D for Duck) — ഈ trick ഓർത്തിരിക്കുക.

questionText: Our new employee is good at ________.
optionD: to be in the same boat

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) thinking out of the box

• 'Think out of the box' = conventionally ചിന്തിക്കാതെ, creative ആയ new ideas കൊണ്ടുവരുക.
• 'Our new employee is good at thinking out of the box' = പുതിയ ജീവനക്കാരൻ creative thinking-ൽ മിടുക്കൻ.
• Context ഇവിടെ key: 'good at' + gerund (thinking) ആണ് grammatically correct.
• Employee-ന്റെ skill describe ചെയ്യാൻ ഈ idiom perfectly fits.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Common idioms — ഓരോ idiom-ന്റെയും exact Malayalam meaning ഓർക്കണം:
• Think out of the box = creative ആയി ചിന്തിക്കുക
• Big picture = overall view, വലിയ ചിത്രം
• In the same boat = ഒരേ പ്രശ്നത്തിൽ ഉള്ളവർ
• Break a leg = Good luck!
• Burn the midnight oil = രാത്രി ഉറക്കമൊഴിച്ചു പഠിക്കുക
• Hit the nail on the head = exactly ശരിയായ answer

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഉറപ്പ്!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) change of place — ഇത് ഒരു idiom അല്ല, literal phrase ആണ്. 'Good at change of place' grammatically incorrect.

❌ (C) big picture — 'Big picture' = overall perspective. 'Good at big picture' = overall thinking-ൽ നല്ലത്. പക്ഷേ creativity/innovative thinking-ന്റെ context ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല. Moreover grammatically 'good at big picture' awkward.

❌ (D) to be in the same boat — 'In the same boat' = sharing same difficulty. 'Good at to be in the same boat' grammatically wrong — 'good at' + gerund വേണം, 'to be' direct infinitive ഇവിടെ ചേരില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: familiar idioms തെറ്റായ context-ൽ കൊടുക്കും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Out of the BOX = outside normal thinking, like escaping a box!'
• 'Good at' + gerund (-ing form): good at thinking, good at swimming, good at cooking — always -ing!
• PSC strategy: Idiom meaning ചോദിക്കുമ്പോൾ options-ലെ closest synonym തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കുക — literal meaning ഒരിക്കലും ആകില്ല answer.

questionText: By the time we reached the station, the guard ________ the whistle.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) had blown

• 'By the time we reached the station' = ഞങ്ങൾ station-ൽ എത്തുന്നതിനു മുമ്പ് guard whistle blown ആയിക്കഴിഞ്ഞിരുന്നു.
• രണ്ടു past actions — ഒന്ന് മറ്റൊന്നിനു മുമ്പ് — ആദ്യം സംഭവിച്ചത്: Past Perfect (had + V3)
• 'Blow' → Past Participle = 'blown' (irregular verb)
• So: had blown ✅
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: By the time + Simple Past → Past Perfect in main clause

'By the time' = ഒരു action-ന് മുമ്പ് മറ്റൊന്ന് complete ആയി.

Irregular verb: Blow → Blew → Blown

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• By the time she arrived, the class had started.
• By the time I reached home, it had started raining.
• By the time they came, we had finished dinner.

Time markers for Past Perfect:
• By the time..., Before..., After..., When (earlier action)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'By the time' + Past Perfect — frequently tested!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) blew — Simple past ആണ്. രണ്ടു past actions ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ, ആദ്യം നടന്നതിന് Past Perfect വേണം. 'Blew' = simple past, ഇത് sequence ഓർഡർ ക്ലിയർ ആക്കുന്നില്ല.

❌ (B) had blew — 'Had' + past participle വേണം, past tense (blew) അല്ല. 'Had blew' grammatically wrong — 'had blown' ആണ് correct. 🪤 PSC Trap: ഈ option students-നെ confuse ആക്കാൻ ഉദ്ദേശിച്ചതാണ്!

❌ (D) has blown — Present Perfect ആണ്. ഇവിടെ both actions past-ൽ ആണ്. Present Perfect ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'By the time = BEFORE that time, so use HAD (past perfect)!'
• Irregular verbs ഓർക്കുക: Blow-Blew-BLOWN (not 'blowed'!)
• PSC strategy: 'By the time' കണ്ടാൽ → other clause Past Perfect → had + V3. ഇത് guaranteed pattern!

questionText: The synonym of the word 'eloquent' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) fluent

• 'Eloquent' = articulate ആയി, convincingly ആയി, beautifully ആയി സംസാരിക്കുന്ന — വാക്കുകൾ persuasive ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന.
• 'Fluent' = clearly and easily speak ചെയ്യുന്ന, smooth ആയ ഭാഷ.
• Eloquent ഒരു powerful speaker-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു — fluent ഇതിന്റെ closest synonym.
• 'An eloquent speaker' = 'a fluent speaker' — PSC answer: C.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Synonym = same/similar meaning.
• Eloquent (adj) → അർഥം: well-spoken, expressive, articulate
• Closest synonyms: fluent, articulate, expressive

Word family:
• Eloquent (adj) → Eloquence (noun) → Eloquently (adverb)

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• Martin Luther King was an eloquent speaker.
• She spoke fluently about the topic.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരു question guaranteed!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) taciturn — 'Taciturn' = habitually silent, കുറഞ്ഞ വാക്ക് ഉള്ള — eloquent-ന്റെ exact ANTONYM ആണ്! 🪤 PSC Trap: antonym-ഉം synonym-ഉം ഒരുമിച്ചു options-ൽ ഇടും.

❌ (B) garrulous — 'Garrulous' = excessively talkative, വളരെ കൂടുതൽ സംസാരിക്കുന്ന. ഇത് talkative ആണ്, eloquent ആണ് (quality of speech, not quantity). Similar field ആണ്, synonym അല്ല.

❌ (D) talkative — 'Talkative' = വളരെ കൂടുതൽ സംസാരിക്കുന്ന. ഇതും speech quantity ആണ്, eloquent = speech quality. Synonym ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Eloquent = Elegant speech' — elegant speaking = fluent speaking.
• 'Taciturn' = opposite (silent) — PSC-ൽ ഈ antonym options-ൽ ഇടും, careful!
• Synonym ചോദ്യങ്ങളിൽ: meaning-ൽ ഏറ്റവും close ആയ word select ചെയ്യുക. 'Garrulous/talkative' quantity ആണ്, 'fluent/eloquent' quality ആണ്.

questionText: She gave me ________ book that I wanted.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) the

• 'The book that I wanted' — 'that I wanted' ഒരു specific book identify ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Specific ആയ, already known ആയ, identify ചെയ്യപ്പെട്ട nouns-നു 'the' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'She gave me THE book that I wanted' = 'ഞാൻ വേണ്ടെന്ന് പറഞ്ഞ ആ specific book' — definite article.
• Relative clause (that I wanted) specific book indicate ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ 'the' compulsory.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Use 'THE' when:
• Specific/unique object: the sun, the moon
• Already mentioned: I have a dog. The dog is brown.
• Qualified by relative clause: the book THAT I wanted ✅
• Superlatives: the best, the biggest

Use 'A/AN' when:
• First mention, non-specific: I want a book.

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• Give me the pen on the table. (specific pen)
• The movie that we watched was amazing.
• She is the girl who won first prize.

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Definite article 'the' — most tested article in PSC!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a — 'A' non-specific book-നു ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു. ഇവിടെ 'that I wanted' clause ഒരു specific book indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു, so 'a' ഇവിടെ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.

❌ (C) an — 'An' = 'a'-ന്റെ vowel sound version. 'Book' consonant sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നതിനാൽ 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. Plus, specificity issue same as above.

❌ (D) those — 'Those' = plural demonstrative. 'Book' singular ആണ്, 'those books' plural ആകും. Grammatically wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Relative clause (that/which/who) after noun = THE before that noun!'
• 'The + noun + that/which/who' — ഈ pattern always works.
• PSC strategy: Blank-നു ശേഷം 'that/which/who' clause ഉണ്ടോ? ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ answer 'the' ആണ് guaranteed!

questionText: Something bitter and ________ tasting was coming up in my mouth.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) vile

• 'Vile' = disgusting, extremely unpleasant — 'വളരെ മോശം, അറപ്പ് തോന്നിക്കുന്ന' എന്നർഥം.
• 'Something bitter and vile tasting' = കയ്പ്പും വെറുപ്പുളവാക്കുന്നതുമായ — context-ൽ perfectly ചേരുന്നു.
• 'Vile' taste/smell describe ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന adjective.
• ഈ ചോദ്യം words often confused category — vile/veil/vale/vail ഒക്കെ similar sound ഉള്ള words.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Words Often Confused — similar sound, different meaning:
• Vile = disgusting, wicked (adj) — 'vile smell'
• Veil = a covering (noun) — 'bridal veil' (മൂടുപടം)
• Vale = a valley (noun) — 'vale of tears' (കണ്ണീർ താഴ്‌വര)
• Vail = a tip/gratuity (archaic noun) — rarely used

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• The garbage had a vile smell. (garbage-ന് വെറുപ്പുളവാക്കുന്ന smell)
• The bride wore a white veil. (മൂടുപടം)
• She cried in the vale. (താഴ്‌വരയിൽ)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Words often confused — homophones/near-homophones PSC favourite!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) vale — 'Vale' = a valley (ഭൂമിശാസ്ത്ര term). Taste describe ചെയ്യുന്ന context-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.

❌ (C) vail — Archaic/rare word, modern English-ൽ almost ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Bitter and vail tasting' meaningless.

❌ (D) veil — 'Veil' = covering, a cloth (noun). 'Veil tasting' = meaning തന്നെ ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'veil' familiar word ആണ്, students ഇത് choose ചെയ്തേക്കും — meaning check ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'VILE = VILE like VILLAIN' — villain = wicked/disgusting, vile = disgusting!
• 'Veil = V + eil = cover' — bride wears a veil to COVER her face.
• PSC strategy: Similar sounding words കണ്ടാൽ context-ൽ meaning check ചെയ്യുക — sound ആധാരമാക്കി answer ചെയ്യരുത്.

questionText: Extremely thin and bony is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) gaunt

• 'Gaunt' = extremely thin and bony, lean from illness or hunger — 'ക്ഷീണിച്ച്, അസ്ഥിമാത്രമായ' എന്നർഥം.
• 'Extremely thin and bony is gaunt' — one word substitute perfectly.
• ദീർഘനാൾ നിരാഹാരം, illness, exhaustion — ഇവ cause ആകുന്ന thinness-നെ 'gaunt' describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Classic one word substitute question — meaning exactly match ചെയ്യണം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 One Word Substitutes — body types:
• Gaunt = extremely thin and bony ✅
• Obese = extremely overweight
• Emaciated = abnormally thin due to illness
• Stout = rather fat or heavily built
• Lean = thin, without much flesh
• Lanky = tall, thin and awkward
• Corpulent = fat (formal)
• Slender = gracefully thin

ഉദാഹരണം:
• After the long illness, she looked gaunt and pale.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One Word Substitutes — ഓരോ paper-ലും 1-2 questions!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) graffiti — 'Graffiti' = walls/surfaces-ൽ paint ചെയ്ത unauthorized writing/drawings. Thinness-മായി ഒരു connection-ഉം ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: unfamiliar word കണ്ടാൽ students guess ചെയ്തേക്കും!

❌ (C) crouch — 'Crouch' = to bend low, to squat (ഇരുന്നു കുനിയുക). ഇത് body size describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word അല്ല, action describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ്.

❌ (D) clod — 'Clod' = a lump of earth/clay, or a stupid person (informal). Thinness-ന് ഒരു relation-ഉം ഇല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GAUNT = HAUNTED look' — gaunt people look like they've been haunted, hollow-cheeked!
• Related words to learn: gaunt, emaciated, haggard — ഈ three body thinness-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• PSC one word substitutes: definition ശ്രദ്ധിച്ചു read ചെയ്യുക, closest meaning match ചെയ്യുക — never guess!

questionText:
He reads the news.
Change the sentence into passive voice.
optionA: The news were read by him.
optionB: The news is read by him.
optionC: The news was read by him.
optionD: The news is being read by him.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The news is read by him

• 'He reads the news' → Active, Simple Present Tense.
• Passive: Object (the news) + is/are + V3 + by + Subject
• 'News' = uncountable noun, singular — 'is' ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• 'Read' past participle = 'read' (same spelling, different pronunciation: 'red').
• So: The news IS read by him. ✅
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Active to Passive — Simple Present:
• Active: Subject + V1(s/es) + Object
• Passive: Object + is/am/are + V3 + by + Subject

Simple Present Passive:
• He reads (singular) → is read
• They read (plural) → are read

Note: 'News' is always singular — always 'is', never 'are'!

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• He writes a letter → A letter is written by him.
• She sings a song → A song is sung by her.
• He reads the news → The news is read by him. ✅

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Active/Passive — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരു question!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The news were read by him — 'News' uncountable/singular ആണ്, 'were' plural past tense ആണ്. Double mistake: number wrong + tense wrong.

❌ (C) The news was read by him — Tense wrong. Original sentence Simple Present ആണ് ('reads'), passive-ൽ Simple Present passive ('is read') വേണം, Simple Past passive ('was read') അല്ല.

❌ (D) The news is being read by him — 'Is being read' = Present Continuous Passive. Original sentence Simple Present ആണ് ('reads'), continuous ('is reading') അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'is being' = continuous passive — tense correctly identify ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Active tense = Passive tense — ALWAYS!' Simple present active → Simple present passive.
• 'News' always singular: The news IS good. The news WAS shocking. Never 'were'.
• Passive formula: is/am/are + V3 (present), was/were + V3 (past), is being + V3 (continuous) — ഈ three patterns ഓർക്കുക.

questionText: Where ________ we going to find a tiger?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) none of these

• 'Where ________ we going to find a tiger?' — ഇവിടെ ശരിയായ auxiliary verb 'are' ആണ്.
• 'We are going to...' = First person plural present continuous.
• Options-ൽ 'are' ഇല്ല — is (singular), were (past plural), was (past singular) — ഇവ ഒന്നും ശരിയല്ല.
• അതുകൊണ്ട് correct answer = (D) none of these.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 'Be going to' structure — auxiliary verb 'be' subject-നനുസരിച്ച്:
• I am going to...
• He/She/It is going to...
• We/You/They are going to... ✅ (ഇവിടെ 'we')

Present tense 'be' forms:
• am — I am
• is — he, she, it, singular nouns
• are — we, you, they, plural nouns

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• Where are we going? (we = are)
• Where is he going? (he = is)
• Where were they going? (past plural = were)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Auxiliary verbs — 'none of these' ഒരു common PSC trick option!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) is — 'Is' = third person singular (he/she/it). Subject 'we' plural ആണ്, singular 'is' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.

❌ (B) were — 'Were' = past tense plural. ഈ sentence present tense ആണ് ('going to find'), past tense ആകില്ല.

❌ (C) was — 'Was' = past tense singular. Subject 'we' plural ആണ്, plus past tense wrong. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'none of these' option കണ്ടാൽ students avoid ചെയ്യും — പക്ഷേ ഇവിടെ ഇതാണ് correct!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'WE = ARE, always!' — We are, We were (past). Never 'we is' or 'we was'.
• PSC strategy: 'None of these' option കണ്ടാൽ first other options eliminate ചെയ്ത് confirm ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് ഒരു valid answer ആകാം!
• 'Going to' structure: am/is/are + going to — subject ആദ്യം identify ചെയ്ത് correct form select ചെയ്യുക.

questionText: Which of the following sentences is wrong?
optionA: Both Arya and Amritha like swimming.
optionB: Neither Arya nor Amritha likes cycling.
optionC: I would rather to have a cup of coffee.
optionD: I would prefer to have a cup of coffee.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) I would rather to have a cup of coffee

• 'Would rather' ശേഷം bare infinitive (to ഇല്ലാത്ത) വേണം — 'would rather HAVE' (not 'would rather TO HAVE').
• 'I would rather to have' = WRONG — 'to' വരരുത്!
• ഈ ചോദ്യം 'which sentence is WRONG?' ചോദിക്കുന്നു — C wrong sentence ആണ്.
• Rule: would rather + V1 (bare infinitive), never 'would rather + to + V1'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 'Would rather' = bare infinitive (no 'to'):
• ✅ I would rather have tea.
• ❌ I would rather to have tea. (WRONG)

• 🔑 'Would prefer' = to + infinitive:
• ✅ I would prefer to have tea.

Comparison:
• would rather + V1 (no to)
• would prefer + to + V1
• had better + V1 (no to)
• used to + V1 (with to)

Correlatives check:
• Both...and → plural verb ✅
• Neither...nor → nearest subject → singular verb ✅

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Would rather vs would prefer' — classic correction question!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Both Arya and Amritha like swimming — 'Both...and' = plural → 'like' ✅ Correct sentence.

❌ (B) Neither Arya nor Amritha likes cycling — 'nor'-നു ശേഷം 'Amritha' singular → 'likes' ✅ Correct sentence.
