❌ (D) I would prefer to have a cup of coffee — 'Would prefer + to + V1' ✅ Grammatically correct. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'would rather' and 'would prefer' ഒരുമിച്ചു കൊടുക്കും — ഇവ തമ്മിലുള്ള difference ഓർക്കുക!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Would RATHER → BARE (no to)' — Rather ആണെങ്കിൽ bare infinitive; Prefer ആണെങ്കിൽ 'to' വേണം.
• ഒരുമിച്ചു ഓർക്കുക: had BETTER + bare, would RATHER + bare, would PREFER + to.
• PSC 'wrong sentence' questions-ൽ grammar rules carefully apply ചെയ്ത് ഓരോ option-ഉം verify ചെയ്യുക.

questionText:
Get lost. Beat it.
Identify the type of sentence.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Imperative sentence

• 'Get lost. Beat it.' — ഇവ commands/orders ആണ്.
• Imperative sentences = commands, orders, requests, advice നൽകുന്ന sentences.
• Subject (you) implied ആണ്, explicitly പറയില്ല.
• 'Get lost' = go away!, 'Beat it' = leave! — ഇവ clearly commands.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Types of Sentences:
• Declarative = statement (The sky is blue.)
• Interrogative = question (Is the sky blue?)
• Exclamatory = strong emotion (What a beautiful sky!)
• Imperative = command/request/advice (Sit down. Please help me.) ✅

Imperative sentence-ന്റെ features:
• Subject 'you' hidden (implied)
• Verb-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു
• Command, order, request, warning, advice

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• Open the door. (command)
• Please be quiet. (request)
• Don't touch that. (warning)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Types of sentences — ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരുന്ന topic!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Declarative sentence — Declaration/statement ആണ്. 'The train is late' — ഇതുപോലെ fact പറയുന്ന sentence. 'Get lost' ഒരു fact അല്ല, command ആണ്.

❌ (B) Interrogative sentence — Question ആണ്. 'Where are you going?' — question mark ഉള്ള sentences. 'Get lost' question അല്ല.

❌ (C) Exclamatory sentence — Strong emotion express ചെയ്യുന്നു. 'What a day!' — exclamation mark ഉള്ളത്. 'Get lost' exclamation mark ഇല്ല, command ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: emotion ഉള്ള commands exclamatory ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും — sentence TYPE ആണ് നോക്കേണ്ടത്, emotion level അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Imperative = command, like an EMPEROR giving orders!'
• Clue: Subject 'you' ഇല്ലാതെ verb-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന sentence → always Imperative.
• 'Get, Beat, Come, Go, Sit, Stand' — ഇങ്ങനെ verb-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന sentences PSC-ൽ Imperative ആണ്!

questionText: Synonym for 'immobilize' is:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) paralyze

• 'Immobilize' = to make unable to move, to stop movement — 'movement ഇല്ലാതാക്കുക'.
• 'Paralyze' = to make someone unable to move or function — 'തളർത്തുക, movement stop ചെയ്യുക'.
• ഇവ രണ്ടും 'unable to move' എന്ന core meaning share ചെയ്യുന്നു — perfect synonyms.
• 'The accident paralyzed him' = 'The accident immobilized him' — same meaning.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Synonym = closest meaning word.
• Immobilize (verb) → make unable to move
• Synonym: paralyze, incapacitate, disable

Word family:
• Immobilize → Immobile (adj) → Immobility (noun)
• Paralyze → Paralyzed (adj) → Paralysis (noun)

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• The heavy snowfall immobilized the city. (city-യെ ഇളക്കാനാവാതെ ആക്കി)
• Fear paralyzed her. (ഭയം അവളെ തളർത്തി)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonym/Antonym questions — every paper!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) mobilize — 'Mobilize' = to organize and prepare for action, to SET IN MOTION. ഇത് 'immobilize'-ന്റെ exact ANTONYM ആണ്! 🪤 PSC Trap: 'mobilize' 'immobilize'-ൽ നിന്ന് 'im' prefix നീക്കിയ word — students confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) actuate — 'Actuate' = to cause to act/function, to activate. ഇതും 'to set in motion' meaning ആണ് — immobilize-ന്റെ opposite direction.

❌ (D) convoke — 'Convoke' = to call together for a meeting (ഒരു meeting-ലേക്ക് ക്ഷണിക്കുക). ഇത് immobilize-മായി no relation.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Paralyze = Para (beside/abnormal) + lyze (loosen) = abnormal loosening of muscles = unable to move!'
• 'im-' prefix = not: immobile = not mobile, immobilize = make not mobile.
• Antonyms in options: mobilize, actuate (both = make move) — opposite of immobilize. Correct answer = paralyze (same meaning as immobilize).

questionText:
If I did not fall, I ________
(i. will run  ii. would won  iii. would win  iv. will win)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) iii only (would win)

• 'If I did not fall, I ________' — 'did not fall' = past tense in if-clause → Second Conditional.
• Second Conditional formula: If + Past Simple → would + V1
• 'Would win' (option iii) = would + V1 ✅
• 'Would won' (option ii) = wrong — would + V1 വേണം, past tense (won) അല്ല.
• Correct: 'If I did not fall, I would win.' — iii only = C.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Three Conditional Types:

Type 1 (Real): If + Present Simple → will + V1
• If it rains, I will stay home.

Type 2 (Unreal Present): If + Past Simple → would + V1 ✅
• If I did not fall, I would win.
• If I had money, I would buy a car.

Type 3 (Unreal Past): If + Past Perfect → would have + V3
• If I had studied, I would have passed.

ഉദാഹരണം:
• If she tried, she would succeed. (Type 2)
• If he ran faster, he would win. (Type 2)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditionals — most tested grammar topic in PSC!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (i) will run — 'Will' = Type 1 Conditional. If-clause-ൽ 'did not' (past tense) ഉള്ളതിനാൽ Type 2 ആണ്. 'Will' Type 1-ൽ മാത്രം.

❌ (ii) would won — 'Would' correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'won' past tense ആണ്. 'Would + V1 (base form)' = 'would win', 'would + past tense' = WRONG. 🪤 PSC Trap: would + won confuse ചെയ്യിക്കും!

❌ (iv) will win — Type 1 Conditional-ൽ use ചെയ്യും. If-clause past tense ആയതിനാൽ 'will' ഇവിടെ wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'If + DID → WOULD win. Did = past? Then WOULD (not will)!'
• 'Would + V1 (base form)' — never 'would + past tense'. Would win ✅, Would won ❌.
• Quick check: If-clause verb tense → Past Simple = Type 2 → main clause = would + V1. Guaranteed!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using the correct phrasal verb.
I ________ a condition.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) put forward

• 'Put forward a condition' = ഒരു condition propose/suggest ചെയ്യുക, അവതരിപ്പിക്കുക.
• 'I put forward a condition' = ഞാൻ ഒരു നിബന്ധന മുന്നോട്ട് വെച്ചു.
• Context: condition/idea/proposal 'put forward' ചെയ്യുക = a natural English collocation.
• This is a fixed phrase: 'put forward a proposal/condition/argument'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 'PUT' phrasal verbs — full list:
• Put forward = propose, suggest (ഒരു idea/condition present ചെയ്യുക) ✅
• Put on = wear (ഇടുക); gain weight
• Put out = extinguish (കെടുത്തുക)
• Put off = postpone (നീട്ടിവെക്കുക)
• Put up = tolerate; resist; accommodate
• Put down = suppress; write down
• Put away = store (വെക്കുക)

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• She put forward a new idea in the meeting.
• He put on his coat and left.
• Please put out the fire.

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs with PUT — multiple questions per year!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) put on — 'Put on' = wear something, or gain weight. 'I put on a condition' meaningless — clothes/weight 'put on' ചെയ്യാം, condition 'put on' ചെയ്യില്ല.

❌ (B) pass away — 'Pass away' = to die (മരണപ്പെടുക). Condition-മായി ഒരു relation-ഉം ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: completely different meaning — clearly wrong.

❌ (C) put out — 'Put out' = extinguish (fire കെടുത്തുക), or to inconvenience someone. 'I put out a condition' — ഈ context-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Put FORWARD = put your idea FORWARD (in front of others) = propose!'
• 'Put forward' always goes with: idea, proposal, suggestion, condition, argument — ഈ nouns ഓർക്കുക.
• PSC phrasal verb strategy: context നോക്കി meaning eliminate ചെയ്യുക — 'pass away' clearly death meaning ആണ്, first eliminate!

questionText:
Choose the correct idiomatic phrase.
________, the entire procedure was painful.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) In a nutshell

• 'In a nutshell' = briefly, in summary, in a few words — 'ചുരുക്കി പറഞ്ഞാൽ'.
• '________, the entire procedure was painful' = ചുരുക്കി പറഞ്ഞാൽ, ആ procedure മുഴുവൻ painful ആയിരുന്നു.
• 'In a nutshell, the entire procedure was painful' — perfectly meaningful.
• Idiomatic phrase that introduces a summary.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Common Idioms — exact meanings:
• In a nutshell = briefly, in summary (ചുരുക്കി പറഞ്ഞാൽ) ✅
• When pigs fly = never (ഒരിക്കലും സംഭവിക്കാത്ത കാര്യം)
• Break a leg = Good luck! (ഭാഗ്യം)
• Eat like a horse = eat a lot (ധാരാളം ഭക്ഷിക്കുക)
• In the same boat = in the same difficult situation
• Bite the bullet = endure pain/difficulty

ഉദാഹരണം:
• In a nutshell, the project failed due to poor planning.
• When pigs fly, he will return the money!

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — every paper, guaranteed!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) When pigs fly — 'When pigs fly' = something that will never happen. '________, the entire procedure was painful' — 'when pigs fly, it was painful' meaningless sentence.

❌ (C) Break a leg — 'Break a leg' = Good luck. Sentence-നു ശേഷം 'the procedure was painful' — ഇതിനു 'good luck' context ചേരില്ല.

❌ (D) Eat like a horse — 'Eat like a horse' = eat very heavily. Procedure painful ആയതിനു ഇതു ഉചിതം അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: familiar idioms unfamiliar context-ൽ കൊടുക്കും — meaning AND context check ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'NUTshell = NUT = compact/small — In a nutshell = compact summary!'
• PSC idiom questions-ൽ blank-ൽ fill ചെയ്യുന്ന type ആണെങ്കിൽ sentence meaning ഒന്ന് complete ആകുന്ന option choose ചെയ്യുക.
• Top 5 PSC idioms: in a nutshell, break a leg, when pigs fly, bite the bullet, in the same boat — ഇവ meaning-സഹിതം ഓർക്കുക!

questionText: Which of the following is correctly spelt?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Pseudonym

• 'Pseudonym' = ഒരു person ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന false/pen name — 'തൂലികാനാമം'.
• ശരിയായ spelling: P-S-E-U-D-O-N-Y-M
• 'Pseudo' (Greek: false) + 'nym' (Greek: name) = false name.
• Authors, artists, activists ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന alternate name. E.g. Mark Twain = pseudonym of Samuel Clemens.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Spelling rule: Greek origin words-ൽ 'ps' silent 'p' ആണ് — 'ps' = 's' sound:
• Pseudonym (false name) — 'p' silent, 'syoo-do-nim'
• Psychology — 'p' silent
• Psalm — 'p' silent
• Psychiatry — 'p' silent

Common spelling traps for 'Pseudonym':
• Psuedonym ❌ (eu reversed to ue)
• Psuedonim ❌ (both eu reversed + nym to nim)
• Suedonym ❌ (silent 'p' dropped)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരെണ്ണം!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Psuedonym — 'eu' reversed ആണ്, 'ue' ആക്കി. PSEUdonym — E first, U second. ഇത് most common mistake.

❌ (C) Psuedonim — 'eu' reversed + 'nym' → 'nim' ആക്കി. രണ്ടു mistakes ഒരുമിച്ചു! 🪤 PSC Trap: familiar word പക്ഷേ subtle spelling change — careful reading വേണം.

❌ (D) Suedonym — Silent 'p' drop ചെയ്തു. 'Pseudo' spelling-ൽ 'p' ഉണ്ട്, sound-ൽ ഇല്ലെങ്കിലും writing-ൽ 'p' വേണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PSEUDO = FALSE — PSEUdo, not PSUEdo! E comes before U!'
• Greek 'ps' words: Psychology, Psalm, Psychiatry, Pseudonym — ഇവ ഒരുമിച്ചു ഓർക്കുക.
• PSC spelling questions: options carefully compare ചെയ്ത് subtle differences identify ചെയ്യുക — often one letter position change മാത്രം.

questionText:
Use an appropriate article.
Yesterday I bought ________ yellow shirt.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) a

• 'Yesterday I bought ________ yellow shirt.'
• 'Yellow' = 'y' consonant sound-ൽ (yeh) തുടങ്ങുന്നു.
• Consonant sound → 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'A yellow shirt' ✅ — first mention, non-specific shirt.
• 'An' = vowel sound-ൽ (a, e, i, o, u sound) തുടങ്ങുന്ന words-നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 a / an — SOUND rule, not spelling rule:
• 'a' = before consonant SOUND
• 'an' = before vowel SOUND (a, e, i, o, u sounds)

Tricky examples (sound ≠ spelling):
• a university (u = 'yoo' sound, consonant)
• a European (E = 'yoo' sound, consonant)
• a one-eyed man (o = 'w' sound, consonant)
• an hour (h = silent, 'ow' vowel sound)
• an honest man (h = silent)
• an MBA (M = 'em' vowel sound)

• 'yellow' starts with 'y' = consonant sound → 'a yellow'

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരെണ്ണം!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) an — 'An' vowel sound-നു ഉള്ളതാണ്. 'Yellow' 'y' (consonant sound)-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു. 'An yellow' = grammatically wrong.

❌ (C) the — 'The' specific/known shirt-നു ഉള്ളതാണ്. ഇവിടെ 'yesterday I bought' = first mention, non-specific. 'The' specific context ഇല്ല.

❌ (D) no article — Countable singular nouns-നു article ഉണ്ടായിരിക്കണം. 'Shirt' countable singular ആണ്, 'no article' option incorrect. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'y' letter vowel പോലെ തോന്നും, പക്ഷേ sound consonant 'yeh' ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'An HOUR, an HONEST man — silent H vowel sound ആണ്. But a HOTEL, a HOUSE — H sounds!'
• 'Y' = always consonant sound (yes, yellow, year) → always 'a'
• PSC article trick: letter നോക്കാതെ SOUND നോക്കുക — 'an MBA' (em-bee-ay, starts with 'em' vowel sound).

questionText: Find one word for 'Emergency procedure to revive someone to consciousness':

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Resuscitation

• 'Resuscitation' = Emergency procedure to revive someone to consciousness — 'ബോധം നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ട ഒരാളെ തിരിച്ചുകൊണ്ടുവരാനുള്ള procedure'.
• CPR (Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation) = the classic example.
• 'Re' (again) + 'suscit' (rouse) + 'ation' = to rouse again.
• Medical term — PSC frequently asks medical/scientific one-word substitutes.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 One Word Substitutes — Medical/Scientific:
• Resuscitation = reviving from unconsciousness (CPR) ✅
• Obsolescence = process of becoming outdated
• Cessation = stopping/coming to an end
• Exploration = investigating an unfamiliar area

Important One Word Substitutes for PSC:
• Autobiography = life story written by oneself
• Plagiarism = copying someone's work
• Amnesia = loss of memory
• Insomnia = inability to sleep
• Philanthropy = love of humanity, charity
• Epicure = one who enjoys good food
• Insolvent = unable to pay debts

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One Word Substitutes — guaranteed in every paper!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Obsolescence — 'Obsolescence' = process of becoming obsolete/outdated. Technology, products outdated ആകുന്ന process. Emergency revival-മായി no connection.

❌ (B) Cessation — 'Cessation' = stopping, coming to an end ('ceasefire' = cessation of fire). Revival-ന്റെ opposite meaning-ൽ ആണ്!

❌ (C) Exploration — 'Exploration' = exploring unknown areas or ideas. Emergency procedure-മായി ഒരു relation-ഉം ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: long/technical-sounding words confuse ചെയ്യും — ഓരോ word-ന്റെയും meaning precisely ഓർക്കണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'RE-suscitation = RE-vive = bring BACK to life! Re = again!'
• CPR = Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation — ഈ expansion ഓർത്താൽ meaning forget ആകില്ല.
• PSC one-word substitute: definition-ൽ key words underline ചെയ്ത് exact match option find ചെയ്യുക.

questionText:
Complete the sentence using a suitable preposition.
The officer managed to remain calm ________ the chaotic situation.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) despite

• 'The officer managed to remain calm ________ the chaotic situation.'
• 'Despite' = in spite of, notwithstanding — 'ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നിട്ടും, ആണെങ്കിലും'.
• 'Remain calm despite the chaos' = chaos ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നിട്ടും calm ആയി.
• 'Despite' + noun phrase — no verb after 'despite'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 'Despite' vs 'Although':
• Despite + noun/noun phrase (no verb)
• Although + subject + verb (full clause)

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• Despite the rain, we went out. ✅
• Despite it was raining, we went out. ❌
• Although it was raining, we went out. ✅

Prepositions in context:
• Remain calm DESPITE chaos ✅ (contrast/concession)
• Among = surrounded by (people/things)
• In = inside
• Throughout = all through a time/space

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Prepositions/conjunctions of contrast — frequently tested!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) among — 'Among' = surrounded by, in the middle of (a group). 'Remain calm among the situation' — situational contrast express ചെയ്യില്ല, wrong context.

❌ (B) in — 'In the chaotic situation' grammatically possible but meaning-ൽ contrast ഇല്ല. 'Remain calm IN chaos' = simply being in that situation, contrast express ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.

❌ (D) throughout — 'Throughout' = all through a period of time. 'Calm throughout the situation' = ആ situation മുഴുവൻ സമയവും — time duration focus, contrast focus ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'throughout' partial meaning ചേരും, പക്ഷേ 'despite' better captures the contrast.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Despite = DE-spite = face it anyway = overcome contrast!'
• 'Despite + noun, Although + clause' — ഈ pair ഒരുമിച്ചു ഓർക്കുക.
• Contrast prepositions: despite, in spite of, notwithstanding — ഇവ ഒരേ meaning, different forms.

questionText:
Report the following sentence.
Selin said, "I drink a cup of coffee every morning."
optionA: Selin said that I drank a cup of coffee every morning.
optionB: Selin said that she drank a cup of coffee every morning.
optionC: Selin said that she drank a cup of coffee that morning.
optionD: Selin said that she drinks a cup of coffee every morning.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Selin said that she drank a cup of coffee every morning

• Direct: 'I drink a cup of coffee every morning.'
• 'I' → 'she' (pronoun change)
• 'drink' (present simple) → 'drank' (past simple — backshift)
• 'every morning' → 'every morning' (habitual expression — stays same)
• Selin said that she drank... every morning ✅
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Indirect Speech — Key changes:
1. Pronoun: I → she/he
2. Tense backshift: Present Simple → Past Simple
3. Time expressions: this morning → that morning, now → then

BUT: 'every morning/day/year' = habitual truth — verb backshift ആകും, but time expression stays!

• said that + Past Tense (backshift)
• says that + No change

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• 'I eat rice' → She said that she ate rice.
• 'I go to school every day' → She said that she went to school every day.

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported Speech — ഓരോ paper-ലും ഒരു question!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Selin said that I drank... — 'I' pronoun change ആയില്ല. Direct speech-ൽ 'I' = Selin, indirect-ൽ 'she' ആകണം.

❌ (C) Selin said that she drank a cup of coffee that morning — 'every morning' → 'that morning' wrong. 'Every morning' habitual time expression ആണ്, 'that morning' = one specific morning. Meaning change ആകും!

❌ (D) Selin said that she drinks... — Tense backshift ഇല്ല. 'Said' (past) reporting verb ആണ്, so 'drinks' → 'drank' backshift ആകണം. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'every morning' stay ആകണോ change ആകണോ — habitual expression stay ആകും, specific time expressions change ആകും!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SAID → backshift one step back. Every morning STAYS as every morning!'
• Time expression rule: 'this/now/here/today' → change. 'Every day/every morning/always' → STAY.
• Three-step check: (1) Pronoun changed? (2) Tense backshifted? (3) Time expression correct? — ഈ three check ചെയ്താൽ indirect speech errors ഉണ്ടാകില്ല!

questionText:
Identify the underlined words with correct parts of speech.
We had an __expensive and delicious__ dinner at Italian restaurant.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Adjective

• 'We had an __expensive and delicious__ dinner at Italian restaurant.'
• '__expensive and delicious__' — ഈ words 'dinner' (noun)-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Noun-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന/qualify ചെയ്യുന്ന word = Adjective.
• 'Expensive' (how much it costs) + 'delicious' (how it tastes) — both describe 'dinner'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Parts of Speech — Quick Guide:
• Noun = person, place, thing, idea (dinner, Ramu, city)
• Verb = action/state (eat, is, run)
• Adjective = describes a noun (expensive, delicious, tall) ✅
• Adverb = describes verb/adjective/adverb (quickly, very, well)
• Pronoun = replaces noun (he, she, they)
• Preposition = shows relationship (in, on, at)
• Conjunction = joins (and, but, or)
• Interjection = exclamation (Oh! Wow!)

Test: 'expensive and delicious' ________ dinner → What kind of dinner? → Adjective!

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of Speech identification — regular PSC question!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Noun — Noun = name of person/place/thing. 'Expensive' ഒരു thing-ന്റെ name അല്ല, quality describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ്.

❌ (C) Adverb — Adverb verb/adjective-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു. 'Expensive dinner' — 'expensive' 'dinner' (noun)-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു, verb-നെ അല്ല. So adjective ആണ്, adverb അല്ല.

❌ (D) Verb — Verb = action/state word. 'Expensive' ഒരു action അല്ല, quality ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: underlined words identify ചെയ്യാൻ ആദ്യം 'what does it describe?' ചോദിക്കുക.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ADJective = ADDs description to noun!' Expensive dinner, beautiful flower, tall building.
• Quick test: 'What kind of ___?' or 'How many ___?' ചോദ്യത്തിന് answer ആകുന്ന word = Adjective.
• Adverb vs Adjective: 'She sings beautifully' (beautifully = adverb, describes verb sings). 'She has a beautiful voice' (beautiful = adjective, describes noun voice).

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag.
None of the family members have come to the party, ________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) have they

• 'None of the family members have come to the party, ________?'
• 'None of the family members have come' = negative statement (none = not any)
• Negative statement → Positive question tag
• Auxiliary verb used: 'have' → tag: 'have they'
• Subject: 'family members' = they → tag subject: 'they'
• So: have they ✅
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Question Tag Rules:
1. Positive statement → Negative tag
2. Negative statement → Positive tag ✅ (ഇവിടെ ഇത്!)
3. Tag-ൽ same auxiliary verb use ചെയ്യുക
4. Tag-ൽ subject pronoun use ചെയ്യുക

'None' = negative word (not any)
'None of the members HAVE come' = negative → tag: HAVE THEY ✅

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• She is coming, isn't she? (positive → negative tag)
• They haven't eaten, have they? (negative → positive tag)
• None of them have tried, have they? ✅

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question Tags — every paper has one!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) haven't they — 'Haven't they' = negative tag. Statement already negative ആണ് (none = no one). Negative statement + negative tag = WRONG. Negative → positive tag വേണം.

❌ (B) don't they — 'Don't' wrong auxiliary. Statement-ൽ 'have' auxiliary ഉപയോഗിച്ചു. Tag-ൽ same auxiliary 'have' വേണം, 'do' അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'do/don't' most common default — auxiliary carefully check ചെയ്യണം!

❌ (D) do they — 'Do' wrong auxiliary (same reason as B). Tag-ൽ 'have/haven't' only correct.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Negative words: NONE, NO, NEVER, NOBODY, NOTHING, NEITHER = negative statement → positive tag.
• Quick test: statement-ൽ negative word ഉണ്ടോ? → positive tag. Auxiliary match ആകുന്നുണ്ടോ?
• None/no one/nobody/nothing — ഇവ കണ്ടാൽ → positive tag (have they, is it, do they — auxiliary match check ചെയ്തു).

questionText:
Complete the sentence using a phrasal verb meaning 'Postpone'.
The committee voted to ________ the proposal for further consideration.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) stand over

• 'Stand over' = to postpone, to defer for later consideration.
• 'The committee voted to stand over the proposal' = proposal later consideration-നായി postpone ചെയ്തു.
• 'Stand over' = defer, adjourn — formal/official context-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന phrasal verb.
• Context clue: 'for further consideration' = postpone meaning confirm ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Phrasal verbs meaning POSTPONE:
• Stand over = postpone (formal, committee/official use) ✅
• Put off = postpone (general use)
• Call off = cancel (not postpone!)
• Defer = postpone (formal verb)

Other 'STAND' phrasal verbs:
• Stand out = be noticeable, excel
• Stand up for = defend, support
• Stand by = support; wait nearby
• Stand down = resign; back off
• Stand in for = substitute for

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs — every paper!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) stand out — 'Stand out' = to be prominent, to be noticeable. 'Stand out a proposal' meaningless — 'stand out' = excel/be different, postpone അല്ല.

❌ (B) stand at — 'Stand at' = to be at a certain level/position. 'Prices stand at 100 rupees' — proposal postpone ചെയ്യുന്ന meaning ഇല്ല.

❌ (C) stand up for — 'Stand up for' = to defend or support a cause/person. 'The committee stood up for the proposal' = support ചെയ്തു. Sentence meaning postpone ആണ്, support അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'stand up for' familiar phrase — meaning carefully check ചെയ്യണം!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Stand OVER = leave it OVER for later = postpone!'
• 'Put off' and 'Stand over' — ഈ two = postpone meaning. PSC-ൽ ഏതെങ്കിലും ഒന്ന് ഉത്തരം ആകും.
• Context clue strategy: 'for further consideration' = postpone/defer = stand over/put off. Context words ഉപയോഗിക്കുക!

questionText:
Find the meaning of the underlined idiom.
She refused to be __led by the nose__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) to be submissive

• 'Led by the nose' = to be controlled completely by someone else, to follow blindly — 'ആരുടെയോ control-ൽ ആയിരിക്കുക, submissive ആയി follow ചെയ്യുക'.
• 'She refused to be led by the nose' = ആരുടെയെങ്കിലും control-ൽ ആകാൻ അവൾ വിസമ്മതിച്ചു.
• 'Submissive' = meekly obedient, easily controlled — exact meaning match.
• Animal analogy: a bull led by a ring through its nose = complete control.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Idioms — body parts:
• Led by the nose = completely controlled ✅
• Turn a blind eye = ignore deliberately
• Cost an arm and a leg = very expensive
• Keep an eye on = watch carefully
• Put your foot in it = make an embarrassing mistake
• Pull someone's leg = to tease/joke
• By heart = from memory

ഉദാഹരണം:
• He let his wife lead him by the nose. (completely controlled by her)
• She turned a blind eye to the corruption.

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms — guaranteed in every paper!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to be assertive — 'Assertive' = confident, standing up for oneself — 'led by the nose'-ന്റെ exact OPPOSITE! Assertive person ആരുടെ control-ലും ആകില്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'refused to be led by the nose' context കണ്ടു 'assertive' choose ചെയ്യും — 'refused to' action ആണ്, idiom meaning 'led by the nose' ആണ്!

❌ (C) to be defiant — 'Defiant' = openly resistant, rebellious. 'Led by the nose' rebellious meaning അല്ല, passive/submissive meaning ആണ്.

❌ (D) to be obstinate — 'Obstinate' = stubborn, refusing to change. 'Led by the nose' stubbornness-ന്റെ opposite — completely follow ചെയ്യുക ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Nose ring-ൽ കൊണ്ടുനടക്കുക = complete control = submissive!' — bull nose ring image ഓർക്കുക.
• Idiom meaning question: 'refused to be led by the nose' — 'refused' action ആണ്, idiom meaning (submissive) ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത്.
• Confused pairs: assertive (strong) vs submissive (weak/controlled) — 'led by the nose' = weak/controlled = submissive.

questionText: Which of the following words is opposite to the word 'Petrified'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) brave

• 'Petrified' = extremely frightened/terrified — 'ഭയന്ന് കല്ലായി, വളരെ ഭയന്ന' എന്നർഥം.
• Antonym = opposite of extremely frightened = brave/courageous.
• 'Brave' = not afraid, facing danger without fear — perfect antonym.
• 'Petrified' literally = turned to stone (Greek: petra = stone) — fear-ൽ freeze ആകുന്ന feeling.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Antonym = exact opposite meaning:
• Petrified (extremely afraid) → Brave (unafraid) ✅

Word family:
• Petrified (adj) → Petrify (verb) → Petrification (noun)
• Brave (adj) → Bravery (noun) → Bravely (adverb)

Synonyms of 'Petrified': terrified, horrified, aghast, apprehensive, scared
Antonyms: brave, courageous, fearless, bold, valiant

ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ:
• She was petrified by the loud noise. (ഭയന്ന് കല്ലായി)
• He was brave enough to enter the forest. (ഭയമില്ലാതെ)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonyms — every paper!
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) apprehensive — 'Apprehensive' = anxious, worried about future — ഭയം/ഉദ്വേഗം. ഇത് 'petrified'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്, antonym അല്ല! 🪤 PSC Trap: same meaning group-ൽ നിന്ന് options കൊടുക്കും.

❌ (B) aghast — 'Aghast' = filled with horror/shock. ഇതും 'petrified'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ് — extremely frightened meaning.

❌ (D) nervous — 'Nervous' = anxious, uneasy. 'Petrified'-നോട് similar meaning ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും exact synonym ആണ്, antonym അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: Options A, B, D — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'fear' family-ൽ ആണ്. Antonym = opposite family = brave!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Petrified = PETRA (stone/rock) — turned to stone with fear! Antonym = brave (not stone, moves forward!)'
• PSC antonym strategy: Options-ൽ 3 similar words + 1 opposite — opposite = answer. ഇവിടെ apprehensive/aghast/nervous (fear words) vs brave (opposite) — brave = answer!
• Synonym trap: PSC-ൽ synonyms antonym options-ൽ ഇടും — 'fear' = petrified, aghast, apprehensive, nervous. ഇവ 4-ൽ 3 options-ൽ ഇടും, confuse ആകരുത்!

questionText: Neither Mr. Gupta's sons nor his daughter ________ to the gym regularly.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) goes

• 'Neither...nor' ഉപയോഗിക്കുമ്പോൾ verb, 'nor'-ന് ശേഷം വരുന്ന subject-നെ (his daughter) അനുസരിക്കണം — ഇതാണ് Proximity Rule.
• 'His daughter' singular ആയതിനാൽ singular verb 'goes' വേണം.
• 'Neither Mr. Gupta's sons nor his daughter goes to the gym' — അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ മക്കളോ മകളോ gym-ൽ പോകാറില്ല.
• ഈ question-ൽ PSC ഒരു classic trap ഉണ്ട് — 'sons' (plural) കണ്ടാൽ 'go' എഴുതും, പക്ഷേ 'nor'-ന് ശേഷമുള്ള 'daughter' ആണ് decide ചെയ്യുന്നത്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Neither...nor / Either...or → verb matches the NEAREST subject (Proximity Rule)
• 'nor'-ന്റെ ഏറ്റവും അടുത്ത് നിൽക്കുന്ന subject singular ആണെങ്കിൽ → singular verb
• 'nor'-ന്റെ ഏറ്റവും അടുത്ത് നിൽക്കുന്ന subject plural ആണെങ്കിൽ → plural verb

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• Neither the teacher nor the students were present. (students → plural → were)
• Neither the students nor the teacher was present. (teacher → singular → was)
• Either you or he is responsible. (he → singular → is)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Subject-Verb Agreement questions വളരെ frequently exam-ൽ വരും — specially neither/nor and either/or patterns.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) gone — 'Gone' Past Participle form ആണ്. 'has/have gone' ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കണം, standalone verb ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല. ഇത് ഒരു incomplete form ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'gone to the gym' familiar ആയതുകൊണ്ട് ആകർഷകമായി തോന്നും, പക്ഷേ auxiliary verb ഇല്ലാതെ 'gone' വരില്ല.

❌ (B) go — 'Go' plural verb form ആണ്. 'Sons' കണ്ടിട്ട് plural verb വേണം എന്ന് തോന്നും — ഇതാണ് PSC trap! പക്ഷേ Proximity Rule അനുസരിച്ച് 'nor his daughter' (singular) ആണ് last subject, അതിനാൽ 'go' തെറ്റ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് ഈ question-ന്റെ main trap — most students 'go' choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) going — 'Going' present participle / gerund form ആണ്. Blank-ൽ main verb വേണം, gerund വേണ്ടാ. 'is going' ആയിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ correct ആകുമായിരുന്നു, but 'going' alone grammatically incorrect ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Neither...Nor' — NOR-ന് ശേഷം ഉള്ളത് NEAR → NEAR ആയ subject നോക്കൂ! (Nor = Near)
• Always look at the subject CLOSEST to the verb in neither/nor and either/or constructions.
• PSC exam strategy: Question-ൽ neither/nor കണ്ടാൽ — ഉടനെ 'nor'-ന്റെ ശേഷം ഉള്ള subject singular/plural check ചെയ്യൂ — ഉത്തരം immediate!

questionText: If she called me, I ________ the party.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) would attend

• 'If she called me' — 'called' Past Simple ആണ്, ഇത് Second Conditional sentence ആണ്.
• Second Conditional: If + Past Simple → would + V1 — ഇതാണ് formula.
• 'അവൾ എന്നെ വിളിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, ഞാൻ party-ൽ പങ്കെടുക്കുമായിരുന്നു' — ഇപ്പോൾ സംഭവിക്കാൻ സാധ്യത കുറഞ്ഞ അല്ലെങ്കിൽ ഇനി ഒരിക്കലും സംഭവിക്കാത്ത situation imagine ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'would attend' = present/future-ലെ unreal situation-ന്റെ result.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Type 1: If + Present Simple → will + V1 (real future possibility)
  Type 2: If + Past Simple → would + V1 (unreal present/future)
  Type 3: If + Past Perfect → would have + V3 (unreal past)

• ഈ question-ൽ 'called' (Past Simple) → 'would attend' (would + V1) — Type 2 ആണ്.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditional sentences PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• If I had money, I would buy a car. (Type 2 — present unreal)
• If it rained, we would cancel the match. (Type 2)
• If she were the principal, things would be different. (Type 2 — 'were' for all persons)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) attended — Simple Past ആണ്. Main clause-ൽ past tense alone ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ conditional structure allow ചെയ്യില്ല. Conditional-ൽ main clause-ൽ modal verb (would/could/might) വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: if-clause-ൽ 'called' (past) കണ്ടിട്ട് 'attended' (past) ആകും main clause-ൽ വേണ്ടതെന്ന് തോന്നിയേക്കാം — ഇത് trap ആണ്.

❌ (C) will attend — First Conditional-ൽ ഉള്ളതാണ്. 'If she calls me, I will attend' ആണ് correct First Conditional. ഇവിടെ if-clause-ൽ Past Simple ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'will' ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Future-ൽ ആണ് party, അതുകൊണ്ട് 'will' ശരിയെന്ന് തോന്നും — പക്ഷേ if-clause tense decide ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) would have attended — Third Conditional (past unreal) ആണ്. 'If she had called me, I would have attended' ആകും past unreal. ഇവിടെ 'had called' ഇല്ല, 'called' മാത്രമാണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് Third Conditional തെറ്റ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: If-clause tense → Main clause formula:
  Simple Present → will (First: real)
  Simple Past → would (Second: imagine)
  Past Perfect (had) → would have (Third: already gone!)
• PSC exam strategy: if-clause-ൽ ഉള്ള verb form ആണ് answer decide ചെയ്യുന്നത് — ആദ്യം if-clause നോക്കൂ, then main clause choose ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: ________ boy who talked to me yesterday, turned out to be my old neighbour's son.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) the

• 'The boy who talked to me yesterday' — 'who talked to me yesterday' എന്ന phrase ആ boy-നെ specifically identify ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• ഒരു specific, already-identified person-നെ refer ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ definite article 'the' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'ഇന്നലെ എന്നോട് സംസാരിച്ച ആ ആൺകുട്ടി' — 'ആ' = 'the' ആണ്.
• Relative clause (who talked to me yesterday) ആ boy-നെ unique ആക്കുന്നു, അതുകൊണ്ട് 'the' obligatory ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  'a/an' = ഒരു random noun, first mention, unspecified
  'the' = specific noun, already identified/known

• Relative clause (who/which/that) ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ — noun specific ആകുന്നു → 'the' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles-ൽ 'the' vs 'a/an' questions PSC-ൽ frequent ആണ്.

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• The man who broke the window was arrested. (specific man — relative clause identifies him)
• The book that I gave you is very useful. (specific book)
• A boy called yesterday. (random, unspecified) → The boy who called yesterday is my cousin. (now specific)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) an — 'An' indefinite article ആണ്, consonant sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന nouns-ന് 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം. 'Boy' /b/ sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് 'an boy' grammatically impossible. കൂടാതെ, 'the' ആണ് ഇവിടെ meaning-ൽ correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'an' vs 'a' confusion trick — 'b' vowel അല്ലാത്തതുകൊണ്ട് 'an' completely wrong.

❌ (B) a — 'A' indefinite article ആണ്, 'a boy' = any random boy. പക്ഷേ sentence-ൽ relative clause 'who talked to me yesterday' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ ഒരു specific boy-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ് പറയുന്നത്. Specific noun-ന് 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'boy' consonant sound-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നതുകൊണ്ട് 'a boy' grammatically correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ context-ൽ wrong.

❌ (C) those — 'Those' demonstrative pronoun/adjective ആണ്, plural nouns-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'Boy' singular ആണ്, 'those boy' grammatically incorrect. 'Those boys' ആകുമായിരുന്നു, but meaning-ൽ ഉം wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Relative clause (who/which/that) = THE club! — Relative clause കണ്ടാൽ ഉടനെ 'the' ഇടൂ, because that clause makes the noun SPECIFIC.
• 'ആ/ഈ' = the, 'ഒരു' = a/an — Malayalam translation ഉപയോഗിച്ച് article decide ചെയ്യൂ.
• PSC exam strategy: 'who/which/that' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ blank fill ആണെങ്കിൽ — guaranteed 'the' ആണ് answer!

questionText: I would rather have tea ________ coffee.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) than

• 'Would rather...than' — ഇത് ഒരു fixed correlative expression ആണ്.
• 'I would rather have tea than coffee' = 'coffee-യേക്കാൾ tea ആണ് എനിക്ക് ഇഷ്ടം' എന്ന് preference express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'Would rather' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ comparison word ആയി 'than' മാത്രമേ ഉപയോഗിക്കൂ — മറ്റൊന്നും accept ആകില്ല.
• ഇത് ഒരു memorize ചെയ്യേണ്ട fixed phrase ആണ്: 'would rather [choice 1] than [choice 2]'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: would rather + V1 + than + V1/noun
• 'Would rather' = prefer to. Comparison-ൽ 'than' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'would rather...than' PSC English section-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• I would rather walk than take the bus. (നടക്കുന്നതാണ് bus-ൽ കയറുന്നതിനേക്കാൾ ഇഷ്ടം)
• She would rather stay home than go to the party.
• They would rather study now than fail later.

Related expressions:
• 'prefer...to' (prefer tea to coffee)
• 'would sooner...than' (same meaning as would rather...than)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) to — 'Prefer...to' pattern-ൽ 'to' ഉപയോഗിക്കും: 'I prefer tea to coffee'. പക്ഷേ 'would rather...to' ഒരു pattern ഇല്ല — 'would rather' എന്ന expression-ൽ 'than' മാത്രമേ ഉള്ളൂ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'prefer tea to coffee' familiar pattern ആയതുകൊണ്ട് 'to' choose ചെയ്യും — ഇതാണ് trap! 'Prefer' → 'to', 'would rather' → 'than'.

❌ (C) for — 'For' ഒരു preposition ആണ്, ഇവിടെ comparison word ആയി 'for' ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'I would rather have tea for coffee' meaningless ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'for' preposition ആണ്, ചിലർ instinctively 'for' തിരഞ്ഞെടുക്കും — completely wrong choice.

❌ (D) but — 'But' conjunction ആണ്, contrast express ചെയ്യാൻ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ്. 'Would rather...but' ഒരു fixed expression ഇല്ല. Preference comparison-ൽ 'but' grammatically fit ആകില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: RATHER goes with THAN — both have 'R'! (Rathe**R** → **R**ather...than)
• ഒരു simple rule: 'would rather' → always 'than', 'prefer' → always 'to'
• PSC exam strategy: 'would rather' കണ്ടാൽ — directly 'than' choose ചെയ്യൂ, no second thought needed! Guaranteed mark.

questionText: I am older than you, ________?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) aren't I?

• 'I am' sentence-ൽ question tag ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് 'aren't I?' ആണ് — ഇത് ഒരു special exception ആണ്.
• Grammatically 'am not I?' ആകണം, പക്ഷേ spoken English-ൽ 'amn't I?' awkward ആയതിനാൽ 'aren't I?' ആണ് accepted form.
• 'I am' → positive statement → negative tag → 'aren't I?' (not 'am not I?')
• PSC-ൽ 'I am' sentence-ന്റെ question tag ഒരു favourite question ആണ് — ഇത് ഒരിക്കൽ പഠിച്ചാൽ guaranteed mark.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive statement → Negative tag, Negative statement → Positive tag
• 'I am' → 'aren't I?' (special exception — 'amn't' or 'am not I' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല)

• General rule:
  Subject + auxiliary verb → tag uses same auxiliary, reversed polarity + pronoun

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• I am right, aren't I? (special case)
• She is happy, isn't she?
• They were late, weren't they?
• He can swim, can't he?
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'I am → aren't I?' PSC exams-ൽ repeatedly ആവർത്തിക്കുന്ന question ആണ്.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) isn't I? — 'Isn't' = is + not. 'I am' sentence-ൽ 'is' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — 'I' pronoun-ന് 'am' ആണ് auxiliary. 'Isn't I' grammatically impossible.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'isn't it' familiar ആണ്, 'isn't I' ഉടനടി ഉത്തരമെന്ന് തോന്നിയേക്കാം — പക്ഷേ subject 'I' ആണ്, 'it' അല്ല.

❌ (B) am I? — 'Am I?' positive tag ആണ്. Positive statement-ൽ negative tag ആണ് വേണ്ടത്. 'I am older, am I?' — ഇത് sentence-ന്റെ polarity match ആകുന്നില്ല, question tag confirm ചെയ്യാൻ negative form ആണ് ഇവിടെ appropriate.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'am' ആണ് 'I am'-ന്റെ auxiliary, അതുകൊണ്ട് 'am I?' ഉടനെ correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — പക്ഷേ polarity wrong!

❌ (C) are I? — 'Are' plural/second person ആണ് (you are, they are, we are). 'I'-ന് ഒരിക്കലും 'are' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Are I' completely wrong — grammatically impossible combination.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'I am' + tag = 'aren't I?' — ഇത് English grammar-ൽ ഉള്ള ഒരേ ഒരു irregular question tag. ഇത് ഒരു exception ആണ് — memorize it!
• Rhyme: 'I AM → AREN'T I — remember this, it's the only one'
• PSC exam strategy: 'I am' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ question tag blank കണ്ടാൽ — directly 'aren't I?' choose ചെയ്യൂ. This comes up in almost every PSC batch!

questionText: Little Ann always ________ to her older brother.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) looks up to

• 'Look up to' = ആദരിക്കുക, മാതൃകയായി കാണുക (to admire and respect someone).
• 'Little Ann always looks up to her older brother' — Ann എപ്പോഴും ചേട്ടനെ ആദരിക്കുന്നു, മാതൃകയായി കാണുന്നു.
• ഒരു ചെറിയ കുട്ടി older brother-നെ respect ചെയ്യുന്നത് വളരെ natural context — 'look up to' perfect fit ആണ്.
• 'Up to' — physically 'look up' ചെയ്യുന്ന image — ഒരു ചെറിയ കുട്ടി above-ൽ നോക്കുന്നതുപോലെ, respect ചെയ്യുന്ന ആളെ 'higher' ആയി കാണുന്നതിന്റെ metaphor ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: LOOK + particle = completely different meaning!
• 'Look' phrasal verbs — PSC favourites:
  look up to = admire/respect (ആദരിക്കുക)
  look into = investigate (അന്വേഷിക്കുക)
  look out = be careful (സൂക്ഷിക്കുക)
  look after = take care of (ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക)
  look forward to = anticipate eagerly (ആകാംക്ഷയോടെ കാത്തിരിക്കുക)
  look down on = disrespect (അവഹേളിക്കുക)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• Students look up to their teachers. (ആദരിക്കുക)
• The police looked into the matter. (അന്വേഷിച്ചു)
• Look out! There's a car coming. (സൂക്ഷിക്കൂ!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verbs — especially LOOK and GET — PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) looks into — 'Look into' = investigate/examine. 'The manager looked into the complaint' — ഒരു complaint അല്ലെങ്കിൽ matter investigate ചെയ്യുക. ഇവിടെ 'ആദരിക്കുക' എന്ന meaning ആണ് വേണ്ടത്, investigation അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'looks into' professional-sounding ആയതുകൊണ്ട് confuse ആകും — meaning ശ്രദ്ധിക്കൂ.

❌ (C) looks out — 'Look out' = be careful/watch out. 'Look out for danger' — warning/caution express ചെയ്യാൻ. Ann ജാഗ്രതൈ ആകുന്നതിനെ കുറിച്ചല്ല sentence, ആദരവിനെ കുറിച്ചാണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Looks out for' (takes care of) ആണോ എന്ന് confuse ആകും — 'looks out' alone ≠ 'looks out for'.

❌ (D) looks around — 'Look around' = explore/survey surroundings. 'We looked around the museum' — ഒരു place visit ചെയ്ത് നോക്കുക. Ann ഒരിടം explore ചെയ്യുകയല്ല, ചേട്ടനെ respect ചെയ്യുകയാണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Look UP TO' = You look UP at people you respect (physically tall = metaphorically great!). Opposite: 'look DOWN ON' = disrespect!
• LOOK family complete list: up to ✅ into ✅ out ✅ after ✅ forward to ✅ down on ✅ — ഇവ 6-ഉം ഒരുമിച്ച് പഠിക്കൂ.
• PSC exam strategy: phrasal verb question-ൽ context word 'admire/respect' meaning ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ → 'look up to' guaranteed!

questionText: Diana's shoes were the perfect ________ to her entire outfit.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) complement

• 'Complement' = something that completes or enhances something else (പൂർണ്ണമാക്കുന്നത്, ഒരുമിച്ച് perfect ആകുന്നത്).
• 'Diana's shoes were the perfect complement to her outfit' — ആ shoes outfit-നെ perfectly complete ചെയ്തു, ഒരു perfect match ആയിരുന്നു.
• 'Complement' — 'complete' എന്ന word-ൽ നിന്ന് വരുന്നു — completion, perfect match എന്ന idea ആണ്.
• Fashion context-ൽ shoes outfit-നെ 'complement' ചെയ്യുന്നു = perfectly match ആകുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  complement (noun/verb) = complete ചെയ്യുക, perfect pair ആകുക
  compliment (noun/verb) = praise ചെയ്യുക, complain-ന്റെ opposite

• Etymology:
  complement ← Latin 'complementum' (that which fills up/completes)
  compliment ← Latin 'complimentum' (expression of courtesy/praise)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• The wine complemented the meal perfectly. (ഭക്ഷണത്തിന് perfect match)
• He complimented her on her cooking. (അദ്ദേഹം cooking-നെ praise ചെയ്തു)
• The red curtains complement the white walls. (perfect combination)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Words Often Confused' section-ൽ complement/compliment ഒരു favourite pair ആണ്.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) compliment — 'Compliment' = praise/appreciation. 'He paid her a compliment' = അദ്ദേഹം അവളെ praise ചെയ്തു. Shoes ഒരു person-നെ praise ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല, outfit-നെ complete ചെയ്യുന്നതാണ്. ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common mistake!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'compliment' and 'complement' pronunciation almost identical — spelling difference ആണ് key! compliMENT vs compliMENT... wait — compliMENT (praise) vs complEMENT (complete).

❌ (C) implement — 'Implement' = carry out/execute (നടപ്പിലാക്കുക). 'Implement a plan' = ഒരു plan execute ചെയ്യുക. Shoes ഒരു plan implement ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല, outfit-ന്റെ completion ആണ്. Completely different context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ment' ending-ൽ ഉള്ള word ആയതുകൊണ്ട് confuse ആകും.

❌ (D) instrument — 'Instrument' = a tool or musical instrument (ഉപകരണം). Fashion context-ൽ 'instrument' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Musical instrument', 'surgical instrument' — ഇതാണ് normal usage.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick:
  compLEment → has 'LE' → think 'compLEte' → completion!
  compLIment → has 'LI' → think 'fLIrty praise' → flattery!
• Quick test: 'She complemented/complimented him on his dress' — here 'praised him' → complimented ✅
• PSC exam strategy: 'perfect ___ to the outfit' pattern → always 'complement' (completion context). 'He paid her a ___' pattern → always 'compliment' (praise context).

questionText: The company gives bonus to its employees only ________ because the management is not very generous.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) once in a blue moon

• 'Once in a blue moon' = വളരെ അപൂർവ്വമായി (very rarely, almost never).
• 'Management is not very generous' — management ഉദാരമല്ല, bonus വളരെ കുറച്ചേ കൊടുക്കൂ.
• 'Once in a blue moon' = 'blue moon' (ഒരു month-ൽ രണ്ടാമത്തെ full moon) വളരെ rare ആയ event — ഈ idiom 'extremely rarely' express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Context: generous അല്ലാത്ത management → bonus very rarely → 'once in a blue moon' perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Idioms — literal meaning ignore ചെയ്യൂ, figurative meaning ഓർക്കൂ!

• ഈ question-ലെ 4 idioms/phrases:
  once in a blue moon = very rarely (വളരെ അപൂർവ്വം)
  every now and then = occasionally (ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ)
  to miss the boat = lose an opportunity (opportunity നഷ്ടമാകുക)
  to sit on the fence = avoid taking sides (neutral ആയി നിൽക്കുക)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• He visits his parents once in a blue moon. (വളരെ rarely)
• I exercise every now and then. (ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ)
• Don't miss the boat — apply today! (opportunity നഷ്ടമാക്കരുത്)
• Stop sitting on the fence and make a decision! (ഒരു side choose ചെയ്യൂ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idioms section PSC-ൽ regular — 'once in a blue moon' ഒരു favourite ആണ്.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) every now and then — 'Every now and then' = occasionally, from time to time (ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ). ഇത് 'sometimes but not always' meaning ആണ്. Context-ൽ management 'not very generous' ആണ്, 'ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ' bonus കൊടുക്കുന്നത് reasonable ആണ് — പക്ഷേ 'once in a blue moon' (extreme rarity) context-ൽ better fit ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇത് ഏറ്റവും close answer ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'not very generous' suggest ചെയ്യുന്നത് 'very rarely' — 'occasionally' അല്ല.

❌ (B) to miss the boat — 'Miss the boat' = opportunity നഷ്ടമാകുക. 'She missed the boat by not applying early' — ഒരു opportunity late ആയതുകൊണ്ട് lose ചെയ്യുക. Bonus frequency-യുമായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'boat' = ചിലർ literally think ചെയ്യും — idiom meaning remember ചെയ്യൂ.

❌ (D) to sit on the fence — 'Sit on the fence' = two sides-ൽ ഒന്നും choose ചെയ്യാതിരിക്കുക, neutral ആയി നിൽക്കുക. Political or controversial decisions-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന idiom ആണ്. Bonus payment frequency-യുമായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Blue Moon (blue-coloured moon) = impossible/rare sight in sky → 'once in a blue moon' = very rarely!
• Key contrast to remember: 'once in a blue moon' (very rarely) vs 'every now and then' (occasionally) — PSC ഈ two options ഒരുമിച്ച് trap ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• PSC exam strategy: 'only ___' + context of 'not generous/not frequent' → 'once in a blue moon' is the answer. 'Every now and then' = regular occasional → wrong in extreme scarcity context.

questionText:
Substitute the underlined phrase with suitable word from the following:
Susan doesn't go to church as she calls herself as __someone who doesn't believe in the existence of God__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) atheist

• 'Atheist' = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വാസമില്ലാത്ത ആൾ (someone who doesn't believe in the existence of God).
• 'Susan doesn't go to church' — church-ൽ പോകാത്തതിന്റെ reason: ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വാസമില്ലാത്തതുകൊണ്ട്.
• Etymology: Greek 'a-' (without) + 'theos' (God) = God-ഇല്ലാത്ത = atheist.
• 'Doesn't believe in the existence of God' → ഈ phrase-ന്റെ perfect one-word substitute = atheist.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Greek root 'theos' = God — ഈ root ഉള്ള words:
  theist = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ (believes in ONE God)
  atheist = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കാത്ത ആൾ (a- = without)
  pantheist = എല്ലാം ദൈവം ആണെന്ന് വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ (pan- = all)
  polytheist = പല ദൈവങ്ങളിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ (poly- = many)
  monotheist = ഒരു ദൈവം മാത്രം (mono- = one)
  agnostic = ദൈവം ഉണ്ടോ ഇല്ലോ എന്ന് അറിയില്ല (knows not)

• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One Word Substitutes — religion-related words PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) theist — 'Theist' = ദൈവത്തിൽ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ. Susan church-ൽ പോകാത്തത് ദൈവത്തിൽ BELIEVE ചെയ്യുന്നതുകൊണ്ടല്ല, BELIEVE ചെയ്യാത്തതുകൊണ്ടാണ്. Exact opposite ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'theist' familiar word ആയതുകൊണ്ട് automatically choose ചെയ്യും — 'a-' prefix ഓർക്കൂ!

❌ (B) pantheist — 'Pantheist' = nature/universe everything is God (pan = all). ഇത് ദൈവം ഇല്ല എന്ന് വിശ്വസിക്കുന്നതല്ല — actually ഒരു തരം belief ആണ്, just different. Susan's description 'doesn't believe in existence of God' — pantheism match ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-theist' ending കണ്ട് confuse ആകരുത്.

❌ (C) polytheist — 'Polytheist' = multiple Gods-ൽ വിശ്വസിക്കുന്ന ആൾ (poly = many). Hinduism traditional sense-ൽ polytheistic. Susan God-ൽ തന്നെ believe ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല — ഒരു God-ഉം multiple Gods-ഉം question ഇല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Prefix 'A-' = without/not in Greek:
  a-theist = WITHOUT God belief
  a-moral = WITHOUT morals
  a-typical = WITHOUT being typical (unusual)
• God belief family — ഒരിക്കൽ memorize ചെയ്തോളൂ:
  NO God → atheist | ONE God → theist/monotheist | MANY Gods → polytheist | ALL is God → pantheist
• PSC exam strategy: 'doesn't believe in God' phrase കണ്ടാൽ → immediately 'atheist'. 'a-' prefix = NEGATIVE meaning!

questionText: Which of the following has the same meaning as "Complete"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) whole

• 'Whole' = complete/entire (മുഴുവൻ, പൂർണ്ണം) — 'complete'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്.
• 'The whole truth' = the complete truth. 'A whole day' = an entire day.
• 'Whole' and 'complete' — both convey totality, entirety.
• ഇത് ഒരു homophones trap question ആണ് — hole/whole/hall/haul ഒക്കെ similar sound ഉള്ളതാണ്, പക്ഷേ spelling-ഉം meaning-ഉം different.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Spelling = Meaning (sound same ആണ് — sense different!)
• Homophones — ഒരേ sound, different meaning:
  whole (/hoʊl/) = complete, entire (പൂർണ്ണം)
  hole (/hoʊl/) = a hollow space (ദ്വാരം, കുഴി)
  hall (/hɔːl/) = a large room or corridor (ഹാൾ, ഇടനാഴി)
  haul (/hɔːl/) = to pull/drag; a large amount (വലിക്കുക; ഒരു കൂമ്പാരം)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• I read the whole book. (= complete book)
• There's a hole in my shoe. (ദ്വാരം)
• The event was held in the main hall. (ഹാൾ)
• The fishermen brought in a huge haul. (വലിയ catch)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Homophones/spelling questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) hole — 'Hole' = ദ്വാരം, കുഴി (a hollow space). 'There is a hole in the road' — ഇത് 'complete'-ന്റെ synonym അല്ല, opposite sense ആണ് (something missing/empty).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'hole' and 'whole' pronunciation similar (/hoʊl/) — ഒരേ sound, different spelling, completely different meaning.

❌ (B) hall — 'Hall' = ഒരു വലിയ room അല്ലെങ്കിൽ corridor. 'Assembly hall', 'dining hall' — 'complete'-ന്റെ synonym ആകില്ല. Completely different category.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'hall' sound 'whole'-ന് similar ആണ് (/hɔːl/ vs /hoʊl/) — pronunciation carefully distinguish ചെയ്യൂ.

❌ (D) haul — 'Haul' = drag ചെയ്യുക (to pull with effort) അല്ലെങ്കിൽ a large amount obtained. 'The truck hauled heavy cargo' / 'a haul of fish'. 'Complete' meaning ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'haul' and 'hall' similar (/hɔːl/) — ഇവ രണ്ടും confuse ആകും, both wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'W-HOLE' → 'W' + 'HOLE' = the WHOLE thing without any HOLE in it = COMPLETE!
• Spelling anchor: whole → w-h-o-l-e (just add 'w' to 'hole') — but meanings are opposites!
• PSC exam strategy: Sound-alike words question-ൽ — spelling ആണ് key! 'Complete' synonym → 'whole' (w-h-o-l-e). Sound-ൽ confuse ആകരുത്, spelling-ൽ focus ചെയ്യൂ.

questionText: Identify the sentence with correct subject - verb agreement :
optionA: The group of students is going on a field trip tomorrow.
optionB: She and her brother goes to the gym every evening.
optionC: The car parked in the driveway are my parents.
optionD: My collection of books is increasing every month.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) The group of students is going on a field trip tomorrow.

• 'Group' ഒരു collective noun ആണ് — ഒരു unit ആയി refer ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ singular verb ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'The group of students **is** going' — 'group' singular → 'is' ✅ correct subject-verb agreement.
• 'Students' plural ആണ്, പക്ഷേ subject 'the group' ആണ്, 'students' അല്ല — ഇതാണ് key insight.
• 'The group of students' = ഒരു കൂട്ടം students = ഒരു unit → singular verb 'is' perfect fit.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Collective nouns (group, team, class, committee, family) → singular verb when acting as ONE unit.
• 'Of + noun' phrase ignore ചെയ്ത് subject identify ചെയ്യൂ:
  The group **of students** → subject = 'group' (singular) → is/was
  A bunch **of keys** → subject = 'bunch' (singular) → is/was
  The team **of players** → subject = 'team' (singular) → is/was

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• The committee has decided on the new policy. (committee → singular → has)
• A flock of birds is flying over the lake. (flock → singular → is)
• The class was very attentive today. (class → singular → was)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Group/team/class of + plural noun' SVA questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) She and her brother goes — 'She and her brother' compound subject (AND ഉപയോഗിച്ചത്) → plural → 'go' വേണം, 'goes' തെറ്റ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'brother' singular ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് 'goes' correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — പക്ഷേ AND compound → always plural verb.

❌ (C) The car parked in the driveway are my parents — Subject = 'The car' (singular) → 'is' വേണം, 'are' wrong. 'Parked in the driveway' ഒരു modifier phrase ആണ്, subject change ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'parents' plural കണ്ടിട്ട് 'are' choose ചെയ്യും — subject = 'car' (singular) ആണ്, 'parents' predicate ആണ്.

❌ (D) My collection of books is increasing — ഇതും grammatically correct ആണ്. പക്ഷേ PSC answer key (A)-നെ classic collective noun SVA example ആയി mark ചെയ്യുന്നു, കാരണം 'group of students' ആണ് most commonly tested pattern.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OF + plural noun' കണ്ടാൽ — confuse ആകരുത്! OF-ന് മുൻപ് ഉള്ള word ആണ് subject.
  'The group **of** students' → group = subject → singular!
• Quick test: OF phrase cover ചെയ്ത് subject നോക്കൂ: 'The group ___ is going' — group is going ✅
• PSC exam strategy: Collective noun (group/team/class/committee) + of + plural → singular verb. ഇത് ഒരു guaranteed question type ആണ്!

questionText:
Choose the correct punctuation for the following sentence.
She opened the door and exclaimed "What a surprise"
optionA: She opened the door and exclaimed, "What a surprise!"
optionB: She opened the door and exclaimed : "What a surprise."
optionC: She opened the door and exclaimed "What a surprise."
optionD: She opened the door and exclaimed- "What a surprise!"

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) She opened the door and exclaimed, "What a surprise!"

• Exclamation sentence introduce ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ reporting verb-ന് ശേഷം **comma (,)** ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'What a surprise!' — ഇത് ഒരു exclamation ആണ്, അതിനാൽ **exclamation mark (!)** closing quote-ന് ഉള്ളിൽ വേണം.
• 'exclaimed, "What a surprise!"' — comma + double quotes + exclamation inside = perfect punctuation.
• 'അവൾ വാതിൽ തുറന്നു, ആശ്ചര്യത്തോടെ പറഞ്ഞു — "എന്തൊരു അദ്ഭുതം!"'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula for punctuating speech:
  said/told/asked/exclaimed + **,** + **"** + speech + **!/?/."**

• Reporting verb-ന്റെ ശേഷം:
  Statement → comma (,) then quote: She said, "I am fine."
  Exclamation → comma then quote with !: He exclaimed, "What a mess!"
  Question → comma then quote with ?: She asked, "Are you okay?"

• Exclamation mark (!) ആണോ period (.) ആണോ? — Speech exclamation ആണ് → ! ഉള്ളിൽ
• Colon (:) speech introduce ചെയ്യാൻ formal writing-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കാം, but comma ആണ് standard
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Punctuation-based correct sentence questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) exclaimed : "What a surprise." — Colon (:) ഇവിടെ formal/technical context-ൽ ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത്. Speech reporting-ൽ comma ആണ് standard. കൂടാതെ, 'surprise.' — period (.) wrong, exclamation → '!' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Colon professional ആയി തോന്നും — speech reporting-ൽ comma ആണ് correct.

❌ (C) exclaimed "What a surprise." — Reporting verb-ന് ശേഷം comma ഇല്ല — grammatically wrong. കൂടാതെ period (.) wrong, '!' വേണം. രണ്ടു errors ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: comma ഇല്ലാതെ direct quote ഇടുന്ന pattern informal writing-ൽ കാണും — formal grammar-ൽ comma mandatory.

❌ (D) exclaimed- "What a surprise!" — Dash (-) speech introduce ചെയ്യാൻ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. Dash = pause/interruption context-ൽ. Exclamation mark (!) correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ dash wrong punctuation choice.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: COMMA introduces the QUOTE — 'said comma quote' pattern! said, "..."
• Exclamation sentence → exclamation mark INSIDE quotes: "Wow!" (not "Wow"!)
• PSC exam strategy: Speech punctuation question-ൽ — (1) reporting verb + comma ✓, (2) exclamation → ! inside quotes ✓. ഈ 2 points check ചെയ്താൽ answer clear!

questionText: Select the sentence that uses the correct form of the verb 'to be' in the past tense.
optionA: Yesterday, She run to the store to buy some milk.
optionB: They has been at the party since 6 PM.
optionC: He were playing basketball with his friends when it started raining.
optionD: We were all very tired after the long hike yesterday.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) We were all very tired after the long hike yesterday.

• 'We were' — 'we' plural subject → 'were' ✅ correct past tense of 'to be'.
• 'Yesterday' past tense signal word ആണ് — 'were' perfect fit.
• 'We were all very tired' — 'ഞങ്ങൾ നീണ്ട trek-ന് ശേഷം വളരെ ക്ഷീണിതരായിരുന്നു' — grammatically perfect sentence.
• 'To be' past tense: was (I/he/she/it) | were (you/we/they) — ഈ rule perfectly follow ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — 'To be' past tense:
  WAS → I, He, She, It (singular)
  WERE → You, We, They (plural)

• Common errors:
  'He were' ❌ → 'He was' ✅
  'They was' ❌ → 'They were' ✅
  'They has been' ❌ → 'They have been' ✅

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• I was happy yesterday. (I → was)
• She was at home. (she → was)
• We were late for the meeting. (we → were)
• They were excited about the trip. (they → were)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Was/were usage PSC Tenses questions-ൽ ഒരു favourite topic ആണ്.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Yesterday, She run to the store — 'Run' present/base form ആണ്. Past tense-ൽ 'ran' വേണം. 'She ran to the store' ആകണം. 'To be' verb-ഉം ഇല്ല ഇവിടെ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'yesterday' past context ആയതിനാൽ 'run' correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — 'run' past tense അല്ല, 'ran' ആണ്.

❌ (B) They has been at the party — 'They' plural subject → 'have been' ആണ് correct. 'Has been' = he/she/it (singular)-ന് ഉള്ളതാണ്. 'They has' subject-verb agreement error ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'has been' familiar phrase ആണ്, ഒരുപക്ഷേ correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — 'they' plural → 'have been'.

❌ (C) He were playing basketball — 'He' singular subject → 'was' ആണ് correct, 'were' wrong. 'He was playing basketball' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'were playing' Progressive tense pattern familiar ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'he' singular-ന് 'were' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — 'was' വേണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: WAS = 1 person (I, he, she, it) | WERE = more than 1 or 'you' (we, they, you)
• Quick rhyme: 'I was, he was, she was — WE WERE, THEY WERE!'
• PSC exam strategy: 'To be' past tense question-ൽ — subject identify ചെയ്ത് singular/plural check ചെയ്യൂ: singular → was, plural → were. Simple rule, guaranteed mark!

questionText: Identify the sentence with the correct use of a modal verb.
optionA: I can went to the store yesterday.
optionB: You must to finish your homework before the deadline.
optionC: She might can help you with the math problem.
optionD: We should bring an umbrella in case of rain.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) We should bring an umbrella in case of rain.

• 'Should' ഒരു modal verb ആണ്, 'bring' base form (V1) ആണ് — correct structure: modal + V1.
• 'We should bring an umbrella' — advice/recommendation express ചെയ്യുന്നു: 'മഴ വരുന്ന case-ൽ ഒരു കുട കൊണ്ടുവരണം'.
• Modal verbs-ന് ശേഷം ALWAYS base form (V1) — no 'to', no past tense, no -ing.
• 'Should bring' = correct modal + V1 structure ✅
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Modal verb + V1 (base form) — always!
  Modal verbs: can, could, will, would, shall, should, may, might, must, ought to

• Common modal verb errors:
  ❌ can went (past tense after modal)
  ❌ must to finish ('to' after modal — except 'ought to')
  ❌ might can (two modals together)
  ✅ should bring (modal + V1)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• You can do this. (can + V1 ✅)
• She must leave now. (must + V1 ✅, no 'to')
• They might come tomorrow. (might + V1 ✅)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modal verb correct usage questions PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) I can went to the store yesterday — 'Can' modal-ന് ശേഷം past tense 'went' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Can + go' (V1) ആണ് correct. Past ability express ചെയ്യണമെങ്കിൽ 'could go' ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'yesterday' past context ആയതിനാൽ 'went' correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — modal-ന് ശേഷം past tense ഒരിക്കലും വരില്ല.

❌ (B) You must to finish your homework — 'Must'-ന് ശേഷം 'to' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'Must finish' ആണ് correct. Exception: 'ought to' = ഒരേ ഒരു modal + to combination.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'have to finish' (=must) familiar ആണ്, 'must to' ആകും confusion — 'must' + V1 directly, no 'to'.

❌ (C) She might can help you — രണ്ടു modal verbs ഒരുമിച്ച് (might + can) ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല. 'She might be able to help' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'She can help' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'might can' emphatic ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — English grammar-ൽ double modals (standard British/American) allowed അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Modal + BARE infinitive (V1) — no TO, no -ED, no -ING after modals (except 'ought TO')!
• Three deadly errors:
  Modal + past tense ❌ ('can went')
  Modal + to + V1 ❌ ('must to go') — except ought to
  Modal + Modal ❌ ('might can')
• PSC exam strategy: Modal verb question-ൽ — modal-ന് ശേഷം base form (V1) ഉള്ള option select ചെയ്യൂ!

questionText:
Identify the correct transformation of the following sentence from direct to indirect speech.
Direct speech : The teacher said, "The exam will be held the next week."
optionA: The teacher told that the exam will be held the following week.
optionB: The teacher said that the exam would be held the next week.
optionC: The teacher mentioned that the exam will be held the following week.
optionD: The teacher said that the exam will be held in the next week.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The teacher said that the exam would be held the next week.

• Direct: 'The teacher said, "The exam **will** be held..."'
• Indirect Rule: Reporting verb past tense (said) → backshift: will → **would** ✅
• 'Said that' — 'said' indirect speech-ൽ 'said that' ആകും (object ഇല്ലാതെ). ✅
• 'Would be held' = will-ന്റെ backshifted form — correct transformation.
• ഇതിൽ 'next week' → 'following week' change ഇല്ല, പക്ഷേ crucial 'will → would' backshift ഉള്ളതിനാൽ ഇതാണ് best answer.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula — Backshift Table (reporting verb = past tense):
  will → would
  can → could
  may → might
  is/am/are → was/were
  has/have → had
  Present Simple → Past Simple

• Time expression changes:
  'next week' → 'the following week'
  'yesterday' → 'the day before'
  'now' → 'then'
  'today' → 'that day'

• 'Said' vs 'Told': said (no object needed) | told (object needed: told HIM/HER)
  ✅ He said that... | ❌ He told that...
  ✅ He told me that... | ❌ He said me that...
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Direct-Indirect Speech PSC-ൽ regularly ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The teacher told that — 'Told' requires an indirect object: 'told US/HIM that'. 'Told that' alone grammatically wrong. കൂടാതെ 'will' not backshifted (will → would ആകണം).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'told' = 'said'-ന്റെ synonym ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — grammar-ൽ 'told' needs an object!

❌ (C) The teacher mentioned that the exam will be held — 'Will' backshift ആകണം → 'would'. 'Mentioned' reporting verb ആയി ഉപയോഗിക്കാം, പക്ഷേ 'will → would' change ഇല്ലാത്തത് main error ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'following week' correct ആണ്, 'mentioned' reasonable ആണ് — പക്ഷേ 'will' backshift ആകാത്തത് fatal error.

❌ (D) the teacher said that the exam will be held in the next week — 'Will' backshift ആകണം → 'would'. 'In the next week' grammatically odd (redundant 'in'). 'Will' → 'would' backshift ഇല്ലാത്തത് main error.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Reported speech = PAST tense → push everything ONE step back!
  will → would (one step back in time)
  can → could (one step back)
  is → was (one step back)
• 'Said vs Told' rule — ഒരിക്കൽ memorize:
  said → no object needed ('said that')
  told → object needed ('told me/him/her that')
• PSC exam strategy: will → would ആണോ check ചെയ്യൂ — ഇതാണ് most common test point in reported speech!

questionText: Which of the following words is a synonym for 'abundant'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Plentiful

• 'Abundant' = ധാരാളം ഉള്ളത്, സമൃദ്ധമായ (existing in large quantities, more than enough).
• 'Plentiful' = ധാരാളം, സമൃദ്ധം — 'abundant'-ന്റെ exact synonym ആണ്.
• 'There was an abundant/plentiful supply of food' — ഈ രണ്ടും interchangeable ആണ്.
• Etymology: 'Abundant' ← Latin 'abundare' (to overflow). 'Plentiful' ← 'plenty' (fullness).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Abundant = Plentiful = Ample = Copious = Bountiful (all mean 'in large supply')

• Word family of 'abundant':
  abundant (adj) → abundance (noun) → abundantly (adverb)
  'There is an abundance of opportunities' = opportunities ധാരാളം

• Opposite words (antonyms):
  scarce, meager, sparse, rare, insufficient

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• Kerala has abundant rainfall during monsoon. (ധാരാളം മഴ)
• The market had a plentiful supply of vegetables. (ധാരാളം vegetables)
• Her abundant energy impressed everyone. (ഊർജ്ജം ധാരാളം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonyms questions — abundant/plentiful pair PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Meager — 'Meager' = വളരെ കുറച്ചു മാത്രം (very little, insufficient). 'A meager salary' = വളരെ കുറഞ്ഞ ശമ്പളം. ഇത് 'abundant'-ന്റെ antonym ആണ്, synonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: PSC വിപരീതാർത്ഥം synonym ആയി offer ചെയ്യും — meager = opposite of abundant!

❌ (C) Scarce — 'Scarce' = അപൂർവ്വം, ലഭ്യതക്കുറവ് (not easily available). 'Water is scarce in summer' — ഇതും 'abundant'-ന്റെ antonym ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Scarce' → 'scarcely' familiar — meaning opposite direction ആണ്.

❌ (D) Sparse — 'Sparse' = ചിതറിക്കിടക്കുന്ന, കുറഞ്ഞ (thinly distributed, not dense). 'Sparse vegetation' = ചെടികൾ കുറവ്. ഇതും antonym ആണ്, synonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Sparse' sound unfamiliar ആയതിനാൽ 'correct' ആണെന്ന് തോന്നിയേക്കാം — meaning check ചെയ്യൂ.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: ABUNDANT = ABUN-DANT = 'A BOX DONE AND MORE' — box full and still overflowing!
• Synonym chain remember ചെയ്യൂ: abundant = plentiful = ample = copious
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym question-ൽ — ആദ്യം given word-ന്റെ Malayalam meaning ഓർക്കൂ: abundant = ധാരാളം → 'plentiful' = ധാരാളം → match ✅

questionText: What is the best antonym for 'miserly'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Generous

• 'Miserly' = പിശുക്കൻ, ഒട്ടും കൊടുക്കാത്ത (extremely unwilling to spend money, stingy).
• 'Generous' = ഉദാരൻ, ധാരാളമായി കൊടുക്കുന്ന (willing to give freely) — exact opposite ആണ്.
• 'A miserly person never shares' ↔ 'A generous person always shares' — perfect antonyms.
• Etymology: 'Miserly' ← 'miser' (Latin: wretched person who hoards money). 'Generous' ← Latin 'generosus' (of noble birth, hence giving freely).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Miserly = Stingy = Mean = Tight-fisted (not willing to give)
  Antonyms: Generous = Liberal = Bountiful = Munificent (willing to give)

• Confusable words:
  Miserly = negative (never gives) — extreme
  Thrifty = careful with money (positive trait — wise spending)
  Frugal = simple living, avoiding waste (positive trait)
  Greedy = wants more for oneself (negative but different)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• The miserly landlord refused to repair the house. (പിശുക്കൻ)
• She was generous with her time and money. (ഉദാരൻ)
• Being thrifty helped him save for his future. (thrifty = wise, not bad)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonym questions — miserly/generous pair PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Thrifty — 'Thrifty' = ചെലവ് കുറച്ച് ജ്ഞാനത്തോടെ manage ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ (careful and wise with money). ഇത് ഒരു positive quality ആണ്! Miserly (negative) ≠ thrifty (positive). Both involve money carefulness, but miserly = selfish extreme.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Thrifty' = 'money careful' ആണ്, miserly-ന്റെ near-synonym ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — but thrifty is POSITIVE, miserly is NEGATIVE.

❌ (C) Greedy — 'Greedy' = അത്യാഗ്രഹം (wanting more than one's share). Miserly = doesn't give others, Greedy = wants more for oneself. Both negative qualities, but greedy ≠ antonym of miserly — they're different flaws.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Greedy sounds like the 'bad' version — but antonym means OPPOSITE, not similar bad quality.

❌ (D) Frugal — 'Frugal' = ലളിതജീവിതം (simple lifestyle, avoiding waste — a virtue). Similar trap to 'thrifty' — frugal is positive. A frugal person is wise; a miserly person is selfish. Not antonyms.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: MISER = MISERABLE person who counts every coin → opposite = GENEROUS (जेनरस = give freely)
• Key distinction to remember:
  Miserly ❌ (bad — won't give) | Thrifty ✅ (good — spends wisely) | Frugal ✅ (good — lives simply)
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym question-ൽ — given word negative ആണ്? → positive option choose ചെയ്യൂ! Miserly (negative) → Generous (positive) = antonym ✅

questionText: Choose the word that means the opposite of 'harmonious'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Discordant

• 'Harmonious' = ഐകരൂപ്യമുള്ള, സ്വരമേളനമുള്ള (pleasant, agreeable, free from conflict).
• 'Discordant' = അസ്വരം, ഐകരൂപ്യമില്ലാത്ത (harsh, clashing, lacking harmony) — exact opposite.
• Music context: harmonious = beautiful sounds together ↔ discordant = clashing, unpleasant sounds.
• Etymology: 'Discordant' ← Latin 'dis' (apart) + 'cor' (heart) = hearts apart = disagreement.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  harmony → harmonious (adj) → harmoniously (adv)
  discord → discordant (adj) → discordantly (adv)
  'dis-' prefix = opposite/apart (disagree, disconnect, discordant)

• Word relationships:
  Harmonious ↔ Discordant (antonyms)
  Melodious = pleasant-sounding (similar to harmonious — not antonym)
  Concordant = in agreement (similar to harmonious — not antonym)

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• The family had a harmonious relationship. (സ്നേഹമുള്ള, ഐകരൂപ്യമുള്ള)
• The discordant notes ruined the performance. (അസ്വരം)
• Their views were discordant with each other. (വിരുദ്ധം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonym questions — music/sound related words PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Melodious — 'Melodious' = ഇമ്പമുള്ള, ശ്രുതിമധുരമായ (pleasant to hear). ഇത് harmonious-ന്റെ SYNONYM ആണ്, antonym അല്ല. 'A melodious song' = 'a harmonious song' — same positive meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'melodious' sounds opposite because of unfamiliar word — but it's actually similar in meaning!

❌ (C) Congrous — 'Congruous' (correct spelling) = fitting, appropriate (യോജിച്ച). ഇതും harmonious-ന് similar meaning ആണ്, antonym അല്ല. Note: 'Congrous' as written in the paper is a spelling error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar word → seems like opposite — always check meaning before selecting.

❌ (D) Concordant — 'Concordant' = in agreement, harmonious (യോജിപ്പുള്ള). 'Con-' prefix = together (same as in 'harmonious'). ഇതും synonym ആണ്, antonym അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Dis-' vs 'Con-' prefix — 'dis' = apart (discordant = antonym), 'con' = together (concordant = synonym).
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DIS-cordant' — 'DIS' always means OPPOSITE! Disconnect, disagree, discordant — all negative/opposite!
• Prefix clue: dis- = opposite | con- = together | un- = not
• PSC exam strategy: Antonym for harmonious → look for 'dis-' prefix option → discordant! Prefix knowledge = instant answer in vocabulary questions.

questionText: Select the word that is most similar in meaning to 'ephemeral'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Temporary

• 'Ephemeral' = വളരെ ചെറിയ കാലം മാത്രം നിലനിൽക്കുന്ന (lasting for a very short time, transient).
• 'Temporary' = താൽക്കാലിക (not permanent, lasting for a limited time) — closest synonym.
• 'Ephemeral fame' = 'temporary fame' = fame that doesn't last long.
• Etymology: Greek 'ephemeros' = 'epi' (on) + 'hemera' (day) = lasting only one day! ഒരു ദിവസം മാത്രം — that's how brief 'ephemeral' is.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Ephemeral = Temporary = Transient = Fleeting = Momentary (all = short-lived)

• Antonyms of ephemeral:
  Lasting, Permanent, Eternal, Enduring, Everlasting

• Word usage:
  Ephemeral is more literary/formal than 'temporary'
  Often used for: beauty, fame, life, trends, feelings

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• Social media fame is often ephemeral. (ക്ഷണനേരത്തേക്ക് മാത്രം)
• The rainbow is ephemeral — it disappears quickly. (ക്ഷണഭംഗുരം)
• His happiness was temporary, not lasting. (temporary = ephemeral)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Ephemeral' PSC vocabulary section-ൽ ഒരു favourite word ആണ്.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Lasting — 'Lasting' = ദീർഘകാലം നിലനിൽക്കുന്ന (enduring, permanent). ഇത് 'ephemeral'-ന്റെ exact ANTONYM ആണ്. 'A lasting impression' = impression that stays forever.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: PSC-ൽ antonym synonym ആയി offer ചെയ്യും — 'lasting' = exactly opposite of ephemeral.

❌ (C) Eternal — 'Eternal' = നിത്യം, എന്നെന്നും (everlasting, without end). ഇതും 'ephemeral'-ന്റെ antonym ആണ്. 'Eternal life' = life that never ends — opposite of ephemeral (which ends very quickly).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Eternal' unfamiliar ആയതിനാൽ correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നിയേക്കാം — meaning check ചെയ്യൂ.

❌ (D) Brittle — 'Brittle' = എളുപ്പത്തിൽ ഒടിയുന്ന (fragile, easily broken — about physical strength). ഇത് time duration-നെ കുറിച്ചല്ല, fragility-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ്. 'Ephemeral' time-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ്, physical strength-നെ കുറിച്ചല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: EPHEMERAL ← Greek 'hemera' = DAY. ഒരു ദിവസം മാത്രം ജീവിക്കുന്ന — like a mayfly! = temporary, fleeting.
• Synonym chain: ephemeral = temporary = transient = fleeting = short-lived
• PSC exam strategy: 'Ephemeral' കണ്ടാൽ — 'short-lived/temporary' meaning ഓർക്കൂ → 'Temporary' ✅. Lasting/Eternal = opposites → immediately eliminate!

questionText:
Find the one word for the expression.
Craze for flowers.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Florimania

• 'Florimania' = പൂക്കളോടുള്ള ഭ്രാന്തൻ ആഗ്രഹം (craze/obsession for flowers).
• Etymology: Latin 'flori' (flower) + Greek 'mania' (obsession/craze) = flower craze.
• 'Craze for flowers' = obsessive love for flowers = florimania.
• '-mania' suffix = excessive craze/obsession — ഒരു word part ആണ്, ഇത് recognize ചെയ്തോളൂ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ROOT + mania = craze for that thing
  flori (flower) + mania = florimania (craze for flowers)

• Common '-mania' words PSC-ൽ ആവർത്തിക്കുന്നവ:
  Kleptomania = craze for stealing (മോഷ്ടിക്കാനുള്ള ഭ്രാന്ത്)
  Pyromania = craze for setting fire (തീ കൊളുത്തുന്നതിനുള്ള ഭ്രാന്ത്)
  Bibliomania = craze for books (പുസ്തകങ്ങളോടുള്ള ഭ്രാന്ത്)
  Megalomania = craze for power/greatness (അധികാര ഭ്രാന്ത്)
  Monomania = obsession with one idea (ഒരേ ഒരു idea-യോടുള്ള ഭ്രാന്ത്)
  Florimania = craze for flowers (പൂക്കളോടുള്ള ഭ്രാന്ത്)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: '-mania' words One Word Substitutes section-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Hypermania — 'Hypermania' = excessive energy/excitement (അതിയായ ഊർജ്ജം). Medical term — bipolar disorder-ൽ extreme high energy state. Flowers-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല. 'Hyper-' = excessive, 'mania' = craze — but not about flowers.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-mania' suffix ഉള്ളതുകൊണ്ട് correct ആണെന്ന് തോന്നും — root word 'hyper' = energy, 'flori' = flower.

❌ (C) Idolomania — 'Idolomania' = craze for idols/images (വിഗ്രഹങ്ങളോടുള്ള ഭ്രാന്ത്). 'Idol' (image/statue) + mania. Flowers-നെ കുറിച്ചല്ല, idols-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar word ആണ്, correct ആണോ എന്ന് confuse ആകും — 'idolo' = idol, 'flori' = flower remember ചെയ്യൂ.

❌ (D) Kleptomania — 'Kleptomania' = craze for stealing (മോഷ്ടിക്കാനുള്ള obsession). Greek 'kleptein' (to steal) + mania. ഇത് flowers-നെ കുറിച്ചല്ല, stealing-നെ കുറിച്ചാണ്. PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് kleptomania ചോദിക്കും, ഇവിടെ answer അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: FLORI = FLORA = flowers! 'Floral design' = flower design — FLORI always = flowers.
  flori + mania = flower craze = FLORIMANIA
• '-mania' family PSC shortlist ഒരു page-ൽ write ചെയ്ത് memorize ചെയ്യൂ:
  Klepto (steal) | Pyro (fire) | Biblio (books) | Flori (flowers) | Mega (power)
• PSC exam strategy: Root word identify ചെയ്യൂ — 'craze for flowers' → flowers = flora/flori → florimania. Root knowledge = guaranteed one-word substitute answers!

questionText:
He came early, _________
Use the correct question tag.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) didn't he?

• 'He came early' — positive statement, past tense (came).
• Rule: Positive statement → Negative question tag.
• 'Came' = Simple Past → auxiliary in tag = 'did' → negative = 'didn't'.
• Subject = 'he' → pronoun in tag = 'he'.
• അതിനാൽ: didn't he? ✅ — 'അവൻ നേരത്തേ വന്നു, അല്ലേ?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula:
  Positive statement + negative tag?
  Negative statement + positive tag?

• Simple Past sentence-ൽ tag: did/didn't + subject pronoun
  He came → didn't he?
  She left → didn't she?
  They went → didn't they?

• Simple Present-ൽ tag: do/don't OR does/doesn't
  He goes → doesn't he?
  They play → don't they?

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
• She finished early, didn't she? (past positive → negative tag)
• He doesn't smoke, does he? (present negative → positive tag)
• They came on time, didn't they? (past positive → negative tag)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ആവർത്തിക്കും.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ
