3. They will build a house. → A house will be built by them. (Simple Future)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Write a letter by me — ഇതിന് passive voice structure ഇല്ല. Auxiliary verb 'is/was/will be' ഇല്ല. ഇത് ഒരു imperative (command) sentence-ൽ ഒരു fragment ആണ്, passive voice transformation അല്ല.

❌ (C) I a letter write — Word order completely wrong. English sentence structure: Subject + Verb + Object. 'I write a letter' ആണ് active form — 'I a letter write' nonsensical word order ആണ്. Grammar rule-ഉകൾ ഒന്നും follow ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.

❌ (D) I a letter written — Auxiliary verb ഇല്ല, word order wrong. 'Written' = past participle, ഇതിന് 'is/was' auxiliary ഒക്കെ വേണം. 'I a letter written' = incomplete, non-grammatical sentence. 🪤 PSC Trap: B, C, D ഒക്കെ clearly wrong structure — (A) മാത്രം complete passive voice structure ഉള്ളത്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: PASSIVE = OBJECT FIRST! Object + is/am/are + V3 + by + me/him/her
• Tense stays same: Active Simple Present → Passive Simple Present (is/am/are + V3)
• PSC exam strategy: Object identify ചെയ്യുക → sentence-ന്റെ beginning-ൽ വയ്ക്കുക → tense-ഉ അനുസരിച്ച് is/am/are/was/were + V3 → 'by' + object pronoun. Formula follow ചെയ്ത് answer select ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: Which of the following words is a synonym for 'vivid'?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Vivacious

• 'Vivid' = തിളക്കമുള്ള, ജീവസ്സുറ്റ, വ്യക്തമായ (bright, clear, lively, striking).
• 'Vivacious' = ഉൽസാഹഭരിതമായ, ആനന്ദകരമായ, ഊർജ്ജസ്വലമായ (lively, animated, full of life).
• രണ്ട് words-ഉം Latin root 'vivere' (to live = ജീവിക്കുക) എന്നതിൽ നിന്ന് വരുന്നതാണ്.
• 'VIV' root = life/living → vivid, vivacious, revive, survive — ഒരേ family!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: VIVID (bright/lively) → Synonym = VIVACIOUS (animated/lively)
• Etymology: Latin 'vivere' (to live) → vivid, vivacious, vivify, revive, survive
• Word Family of VIVID:
  - vivid (adj) = bright, striking (ഉജ്ജ്വലമായ)
  - vividly (adv) = in a striking/clear manner
  - vividness (n) = sharpness, clarity
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonym questions — especially root-based word pairs — PSC-ൽ ഏതു paper-ലും 2-3 ഉണ്ടാകും

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. She gave a vivid description of the accident. (അവൾ അപകടത്തിന്റെ ജീവസ്സുറ്റ വിവരണം നൽകി)
2. He has a vivid memory of his childhood. (അദ്ദേഹത്തിന് ബാല്യകാലത്തിന്റെ തിളക്കമുള്ള ഓർമ്മ ഉണ്ട്)
3. She is a vivacious and energetic speaker. (അവൾ ഊർജ്ജസ്വലയും ഉൽസാഹഭരിതയുമായ speaker ആണ്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Dull — 'Dull' = boring, without brightness (ഉത്സാഹമില്ലാത്ത, മങ്ങിയ). ഇത് 'vivid'-ന്റെ exact antonym ആണ്, synonym അല്ല. 'A dull colour' vs 'A vivid colour' — ഇവ opposites. 🪤 PSC Trap: Antonym-ഉം option-ൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തി confuse ചെയ്യുന്ന common PSC technique.

❌ (C) Dreary — 'Dreary' = dull and bleak (ഏകതാനവും വിരസവുമായ). ഇതും 'vivid'-ന്റെ antonym ആണ്. 'A dreary day' = മൂഢവും ഉൽസാഹമില്ലാത്തതുമായ ദിവസം. Options A, C, D ഒക്കെ antonyms — vivid-ന്റെ opposite meaning ഉള്ളവ.

❌ (D) Lethargic — 'Lethargic' = sluggish, without energy (മടിയനായ, ഉൽസാഹമില്ലാത്ത). 'He felt lethargic after the meal' = ഭക്ഷണശേഷം ഉൽസാഹക്കുറവ് അനുഭവപ്പെട്ടു. ഇതും vivid-ന്റെ opposite idea ഉള്ള word ആണ്. 🪤 PSC Trap: A, C, D ഒക്കെ antonyms — ഇവ synonym question-ൽ trap ആയി include ചെയ്തിരിക്കുന്നു.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: VIV = LIFE! VIVID = life-filled (ജീവൻ നിറഞ്ഞ), VIVACIOUS = life-giving (ഊർജ്ജം നൽകുന്ന) — ഒരേ root, ഒരേ meaning family!
• Word family: vivid → vivacious → revive → survive → vital — ഇവ ഒക്കെ 'life' idea share ചെയ്യുന്നു
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym question-ൽ options-ൽ antonyms ഒഴിവാക്കുക (Dull, Dreary, Lethargic). Root word match ഉള്ള option select ചെയ്യുക — 'VIVACIOUS'-ൽ 'VIV' root കണ്ടാൽ confirm!

questionText: Choose the antonym of the word 'urbane':

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Rural

• 'Urbane' = sophisticated, refined, city-like in manner (സ്നേഹസമ്പന്നം, city-educated style). Latin 'urbanus' = of the city (നഗരം) → Urban → Urbane.
• 'Rural' = village/countryside related (ഗ്രാമീണം, നഗരേതര). 'Urban' (city) vs 'Rural' (village) — opposites.
• Urbane-ൽ 'urban' (city) root ഉള്ളതിനാൽ, 'rural' (non-city) ആണ് antonym.
• PSC exam-ൽ word origin-based antonyms ഇത്തരം question-ൽ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: URBANE (urban = city → sophisticated) ↔ RURAL (country/village = opposite)
• Etymology: Latin 'urbs' = city (നഗരം) → urban (adj) → urbane (refined, like city person)
• Word Pairs:
  - Urbane ↔ Rural (city concept vs village concept)
  - Urbane ↔ Rustic (sophisticated vs simple/village-like)
  - Refined ↔ Crude (similar concept pair)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Etymology-based antonym questions PSC-ൽ frequently appear ചെയ്യുന്നു

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. He has very urbane manners. (അദ്ദേഹത്തിന് refined ആയ പെരുമാറ്റം ഉണ്ട്)
2. She leads a rural life away from the city. (അവൾ നഗരത്തിൽ നിന്ന് അകലെ ഗ്രാമീണ ജീവിതം നയിക്കുന്നു)
3. The urbane diplomat charmed everyone with his grace. (Sophisticated diplomat-ൽ elegance ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Refined — 'Refined' = elegant, cultured (ഭദ്രമായ, sophisticated). ഇത് 'urbane'-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്, antonym അല്ല! 'Refined taste' = educated/cultivated ആശ്വസ്തം. 🪤 PSC Trap: Antonym question-ൽ synonyms trap ആയി include ചെയ്യുന്നു.

❌ (B) Sophisticated — 'Sophisticated' = knowledgeable, refined (educated, culture-ഉള്ള). ഇതും urbane-ന്റെ synonym ആണ്. 'Sophisticated person' = refined education ഉള്ള ആൾ. 🪤 PSC Trap: A, B, D ഒക്കെ 'urbane'-ന്റെ synonyms! Antonym question-ൽ ഇവ select ചെയ്ത് students fall ആകുന്നു.

❌ (D) Polished — 'Polished' = refined, smooth in manner (elegant). 'A polished speaker' = eloquent, refined. ഇതും urbane-ന് similar meaning ഉള്ള word ആണ്. Options A, B, D ഒക്കെ synonyms — (C) Rural മാത്രം opposite concept.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: URBAN = CITY. URBANE = city-like. RURAL = VILLAGE. City-ന്റെ opposite Village — URBANE-ന്റെ opposite RURAL!
• Pattern recognition: Antonym question-ൽ 3 options synonyms ആയിരിക്കും, 1 option antonym. 'Odd one out' = answer!
• PSC exam strategy: Options-ൽ 3 similar-meaning words കണ്ടാൽ → ബാക്കി 1 = antonym = correct answer. ഇത് quick elimination technique!

questionText: Identify the word that means the opposite of excessive:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Moderate

• 'Excessive' = beyond what is necessary, extreme (ആവശ്യത്തിൽ കൂടുതൽ, അനാവശ്യം, extreme ആയ).
• 'Moderate' = within reasonable limits, not extreme (മിതമായ, ന്യായമായ, balanced).
• 'Excessive spending' = ആവശ്യത്തിൽ കൂടുതൽ ചെലവ്; 'Moderate spending' = മിതമായ ചെലവ് — ഇവ exact opposites.
• PSC-ൽ ഇത്തരം clearly opposite meanings-ഉള്ള antonym pairs ഒരു common question type ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: EXCESSIVE (too much/extreme) ↔ MODERATE (just right/balanced)
• Word Family of EXCESSIVE:
  - excess (n) = too much (അധികം)
  - excessive (adj) = more than needed
  - excessively (adv) = in an extreme way
• Word Family of MODERATE:
  - moderate (adj) = balanced, not extreme
  - moderately (adv) = to a moderate degree
  - moderation (n) = the quality of being moderate (മിതത്വം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonym questions — especially extreme vs balanced opposites — PSC-ൽ frequently appear

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. He has an excessive habit of buying new clothes. (ആവശ്യത്തിൽ കൂടുതൽ clothes വാങ്ങുന്ന ശീലം)
2. She maintains a moderate diet. (മിതമായ ഭക്ഷണക്രമം)
3. Excessive use of phones is harmful. (Phones-ന്റെ അമിത ഉപയോഗം ദോഷകരം)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Abundant — 'Abundant' = plentiful, in large quantity (ധാരാളമുള്ള, സമ്പുഷ്ടം). 'Abundant resources' = ധാരാളം resources. ഇത് excessive-ഓ similar 'large amount' idea share ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ്, opposite അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Abundant'-ഉം 'excessive'-ഉം 'a lot' idea share ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ confuse ആകുന്നു.

❌ (B) Lavish — 'Lavish' = extravagant, very generous (ധൂർത്ത്, ആഡംബരപ്രിയം). 'Lavish spending' = extravagant spending. ഇത് excessive-ഓ similar meaning ഉള്ള word ആണ്, antonym അല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Lavish' = excessive-ന്റെ near-synonym — antonym ആണെന്ന് confuse ആകുന്നു.

❌ (D) Copious — 'Copious' = large in quantity, plentiful (ധാരാളം). 'Copious notes' = ധാരാളം notes. ഇതും 'large amount' idea ഉള്ള word ആണ്. Options A, B, D ഒക്കെ 'more than enough' idea convey ചെയ്യുന്നു; (C) Moderate മാത്രം 'just right' idea convey ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: EX-CESSIVE = EXtra, EXtra MORE! MODERATE = 'NORMAL amount'. 'കൂടുതൽ'-ന്റെ opposite 'ശരിക്ക്' — EXCESSIVE opposite = MODERATE!
• Pattern: A (Abundant), B (Lavish), D (Copious) ഒക്കെ 'too much' meaning — eliminate! (C) Moderate = 'just right' — that's the antonym!
• PSC exam strategy: Options-ൽ 'too much' meaning ഉള്ള words eliminate ചെയ്യുക → 'balanced/just right' word = antonym of excessive = correct answer!

questionText: Which word is most similar to resilient?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Flexible

• 'Resilient' = ബുദ്ധിമുട്ടുകളിൽ നിന്ന് വേഗം recover ആകാൻ കഴിവുള്ള, elastic (able to spring back).
• 'Flexible' = easily change/adapt ആകാൻ കഴിവുള്ള, not rigid (adapting without breaking).
• രണ്ടും 'ability to adapt' idea share ചെയ്യുന്നു — resilient person = difficulties-ൽ നിന്ന് back വരുന്ന ആൾ; flexible person = change-ഉ adapt ആകുന്ന ആൾ.
• Latin 'resilire' (to spring back) = resilient-ന്റെ root. Both resilient & flexible = not breaking under pressure.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: RESILIENT = bounce back easily = FLEXIBLE = adapt easily (same core idea)
• Etymology: Latin 'resilire' = to spring back (spring back ചെയ്യുക)
• Word Family of RESILIENT:
  - resilient (adj) = quick to recover
  - resilience (n) = ability to recover (ദൃഢത, recovery power)
  - resiliently (adv) = in a resilient manner
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modern vocabulary synonyms — especially personality/character adjectives — PSC-ൽ ഇടക്കിടെ ചോദിക്കുന്നു

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. Children are very resilient and adapt to change easily. (കുട്ടികൾ resilient ആണ്, മാറ്റങ്ങളോട് adapt ആകും)
2. Rubber is a flexible material. (Rubber = flexible material)
3. She has a flexible approach to problem-solving. (Flexible approach to problems)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Fragile — 'Fragile' = easily broken (പൊടിഞ്ഞുപോകുന്ന, easily damaged). 'Fragile glass' = easily breaks. ഇത് 'resilient'-ന്റെ exact opposite ആണ്! Resilient = strong, bounces back; Fragile = breaks easily. 🪤 PSC Trap: Exact antonym synonym question-ൽ option ആകുന്നതാണ് — ഇത് synonym question ആണ്!

❌ (C) Brittle — 'Brittle' = hard but easily broken (easily crack ആകുന്ന). 'Brittle bones' = easily breaking. ഇതും resilient-ന്റെ opposite ആണ്. Brittle material = breaks under pressure; Resilient material = bends but comes back. 🪤 PSC Trap: Fragile, Brittle, Weak — ഈ മൂന്ന് options-ഉം resilient-ന്റെ opposites.

❌ (D) Weak — 'Weak' = lacking strength (ദുർബലം). 'A weak foundation' = easily falls. Resilient = strong, recovers; Weak = not strong. Options A, C, D ഒക്കെ antonyms — (B) Flexible മാത്രം synonym!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: RESILIENT = 'Rubber band person' — stretch ചെയ്ത് back വരും! FLEXIBLE = 'yoga pose' — bend ആകും, break ആകില്ല! Both = adapt without breaking!
• PSC pattern: ഈ question-ൽ A, C, D ഒക്കെ antonyms — 3 antonyms + 1 synonym pattern = quickly identify (B) as the answer!
• Word family: resilient/resilience, flexible/flexibility, adaptable/adaptability — ഒരുമിച്ച് learn ചെയ്യുക!

questionText:
Find the one word for the statement:
To choose not to use a vote either in favour of or against something.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Abstain

• 'Abstain' = vote ചെയ്യാൻ ഒഴിവാക്കുക, neither for nor against (to choose not to vote).
• Question definition: 'To choose not to use a vote either in favour of or against' = vote-ൽ participate ചെയ്യാതിരിക്കൽ = Abstain.
• 'Three members abstained from the vote' = 3 members vote ചെയ്തില്ല, neither yes nor no.
• Latin 'abs' (away from) + 'tenere' (to hold) = abstain = 'hold away from' the vote.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ABSTAIN = 'NOT voting YES or NO — standing aside from the vote'
• Etymology: Latin 'abs' (away) + 'tenere' (to hold) = to hold away = abstain
• Word Family of ABSTAIN:
  - abstain (v) = to choose not to vote
  - abstention (n) = the act of not voting (വോട്ട് ചെയ്യാതിരിക്കൽ)
  - abstinent (adj) = choosing not to participate
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One Word Substitutes — civic/voting/government terms PSC-ൽ ഇടക്കിടെ ചോദ്യ subject

ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
1. Three members voted yes, two voted no, and five abstained. (5 members vote ചെയ്തില്ല)
2. He decided to abstain from the election. (Election-ൽ participate ചെയ്യാതിരുന്നു)
3. India abstained from the UN vote. (UN vote-ൽ India abstain ചെയ്തു — news-ൽ ഈ word കേൾക്കാം)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Absolute — 'Absolute' = complete, total, without limits (സമ്പൂർണ്ണം). 'Absolute power' = unlimited power. ഇത് voting-ഉമായി ഒരു connection-ഉം ഇല്ല. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Absolute', 'Abstain' — രണ്ടും 'ABS' ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു, sound-alike trap!

❌ (B) Abolish — 'Abolish' = to formally end/stop something (ഔദ്യോഗികമായി നിർത്തലാക്കുക). 'Abolish a law' = law officially remove ചെയ്യുക. ഇത് vote ചെയ്യാതിരിക്കുന്നതുമായി ബന്ധമില്ല. Abolish = end something; Abstain = not participate in voting. 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ABO-' prefix — Abolish vs Abstain sound confusingly similar.

❌ (C) Absurd — 'Absurd' = ridiculous, illogical (അർത്ഥമില്ലാത്ത, ridiculous). 'That idea is absurd' = ആ idea ridiculous ആണ്. ഇതിന് voting-ഉമായി ഒരു ബന്ധവും ഇല്ല. Options A, B, C ഒക്കെ 'ABS-' prefix ഉള്ള words — PSC-ൽ same-prefix traps ഒരു common technique!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: ABSTAIN = 'ABS-TAIN = ABSENT from vote'! Vote-ൽ absent ആകുക = abstain. 'India was ABSENT in the vote' = India ABSTAINED!
• Word family: abstain (v) → abstention (n) → abstinent (adj). UN news-ൽ 'abstention' word frequently ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു
• PSC exam strategy: One Word Substitute-ൽ definition carefully read ചെയ്യുക. 'choose not to use a vote' → vote-ൽ absent → ABSTAIN. 'ABS-' prefix words (Absolute/Abolish/Absurd) definition match ആകുന്നോ check ചെയ്ത് eliminate ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: Find out the correct spelling:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) handkerchiefs

• 'Handkerchief' എന്ന word-ന്റെ plural form ആണ് 'handkerchiefs' — ഇത് 'f' ൽ അവസാനിക്കുന്ന words-ന്റെ ഒരു special category ആണ്.
• ഇംഗ്ലീഷിൽ 'f' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'fe' ൽ അവസാനിക്കുന്ന ചില words-ന്റെ plural-ൽ 'f' → 'ves' ആകും (knife → knives, leaf → leaves). പക്ഷേ 'handkerchief' അതിൽ ഉൾപ്പെടില്ല — ഇതിന്റെ plural simply 's' add ചെയ്‌താൽ മതി.
• 'Handkerchief' എന്ന word ഒരു compound word ആണ്: 'hand' + 'kerchief' — കൈ തൂവാല. ഇതിന്റെ plural 'handkerchiefs' ✓
• PSC exams-ൽ ഇതുപോലെ irregular-ആണെന്ന് തോന്നിക്കുന്ന but actually regular plural ഉള്ള words പലപ്പോഴും ചോദിക്കാറുണ്ട്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'f/fe' → 'ves' rule EXCEPTIONS ഉണ്ട് — ചില words simply 's' മാത്രം ചേർക്കും!
• 'f' → 'ves' ആകുന്ന words: knife→knives, leaf→leaves, loaf→loaves, wolf→wolves, half→halves, life→lives, wife→wives, self→selves, shelf→shelves
• 'f' → simply 's' ആകുന്ന words (exceptions): roof→roofs, chief→chiefs, handkerchief→handkerchiefs, belief→beliefs, cliff→cliffs, proof→proofs
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Spelling questions-ൽ ഈ 'f/fe → ves' vs 'fs' confusion വളരെ frequent ആണ്
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She always carries two handkerchiefs in her bag.
  2. The roofs of the old buildings were damaged in the storm.
  3. The thieves stole all the knives from the kitchen.
  4. The chiefs of the tribes gathered for a meeting.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) handkerchiefes
• 'handkerchief' + 'es' എന്ന് ചേർത്തത് — ഇങ്ങനെ ഒരു rule ഇല്ല.
• 'es' ചേർക്കുന്നത് 's', 'x', 'z', 'ch', 'sh' ൽ അവസാനിക്കുന്ന words-ന് (box→boxes, church→churches) — 'f' ൽ അവസാനിക്കുന്ന words-ന് അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'f' after a vowel sound-ൽ plural ആകുമ്പോൾ 'es' ചേർക്കണം എന്ന് തോന്നും — but ഇത് തെറ്റ്.

❌ (C) handkerchieves
• 'f' → 'ves' rule incorrectly apply ചെയ്‌തത്.
• 'Handkerchief' ആ category-ൽ ഉൾപ്പെടില്ല — ഇത് exception list-ൽ ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'knife→knives', 'leaf→leaves' pattern ഓർത്ത് ഇതും അങ്ങനെ തന്നെ ആകും എന്ന് students തെറ്റിദ്ധരിക്കും.

❌ (D) handkerchives
• 'kerchief' → 'kerchives' എന്ന് മാറ്റിയത് — ഇത് spelling തന്നെ തെറ്റ്.
• 'chief' → 'chives' ആകില്ല; 'chief' → 'chiefs' ആണ് correct plural.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ves' ending ഓർത്ത് but wrong position-ൽ apply ചെയ്‌ത് confusion ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്നു.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Chief uses handkerchief → Chiefs use handkerchiefs' — 'chief' ന്റെ plural 'chiefs' ആണെന്ന് ഓർത്താൽ, 'handkerchief' ന്റെ ending 'chief' ആണ്, അതിനാൽ plural 'handkerchiefs' ✓
• ഈ 'fs' group ഒരുമിച്ച് പഠിക്കുക: roof→roofs, proof→proofs, chief→chiefs, belief→beliefs, handkerchief→handkerchiefs
• PSC exam strategy: 'ves' option-ഉം 'fs' option-ഉം ഒരേ question-ൽ ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ — word-ന്റെ ending ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക. 'chief' ending → always 'fs'.

questionText: What is the meaning of 'a dog in the manger policy'?
optionA: a person who considers others very low in status
optionC: a person who is very loyal to his master
optionD: a man who prevents others from enjoying something

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) a man who prevents others from enjoying something

• 'A dog in the manger' — ഒരു പഴഞ്ചൊൽ കഥയിൽ നിന്നാണ് ഈ idiom വന്നത്: ഒരു നായ manger-ൽ (കാലിത്തൊഴുത്തിലെ പുൽക്കൂട്) കിടക്കുന്നു — അതിന് ആ പുല്ല് കഴിക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല, പക്ഷേ മറ്റ് മൃഗങ്ങളെ കഴിക്കാൻ അനുവദിക്കുന്നുമില്ല!
• ഇതിന്റെ അർത്ഥം: തനിക്ക് ഉപകാരം ഇല്ലെങ്കിലും മറ്റുള്ളവർ enjoy ചെയ്യുന്നത് തടയുന്ന ആൾ — ഒരു selfish, dog-in-the-manger attitude.
• 'Policy' ചേർക്കുമ്പോൾ: ഇത്തരം selfishness ഒരു ശീലമോ നയമോ ആയി കാണുന്ന ആളെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Real life example: ഒരാൾ ഒരു job offer reject ചെയ്‌ത് — മറ്റൊരാൾ accept ചെയ്യുന്നത് prevent ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — that's a dog in the manger!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Dog in manger = 'Self-less harm' — തനിക്ക് benefit ഇല്ല, മറ്റുള്ളവർക്ക് harm ആണ്!
• ഈ idiom Aesop's Fables-ൽ നിന്നാണ് — 'The Dog in the Manger' story.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Animal-based idioms PSC-ൽ very frequent ആണ്.
• Related animal idioms:
  1. 'Let the cat out of the bag' — രഹസ്യം വെളിപ്പെടുത്തുക
  2. 'Kill two birds with one stone' — ഒരു കല്ലിൽ രണ്ടു പക്ഷി
  3. 'Barking up the wrong tree' — തെറ്റായ ആളിനെ blame ചെയ്യുക
  4. 'Every dog has its day' — ഓരോ ആൾക്കും ഒരു good time വരും
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He is following a dog in the manger policy by refusing to rent his house but not selling it either.
  2. Stop being a dog in the manger — if you don't want the tickets, let someone else have them.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a person who considers others very low in status
• ഇത് 'snobbish' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'condescending' person-നെ describe ചെയ്യുന്നു — dog in the manger-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Dog' എന്ന word ഉള്ളതിനാൽ someone inferior consider ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ എന്ന് തോന്നാം — but story context വ്യത്യസ്‌തം.

❌ (B) a hypocratic person
• 'Hypocrite' (note: 'hypocratic' is a spelling error — should be 'hypocritical') — ഒരു കാര്യം പറഞ്ഞ് മറ്റൊന്ന് ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• Dog in the manger hypocrisy അല്ല — selfishness ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Option-ൽ ഒരു spelling mistake ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ (hypocratic) — PSC students confuse ആകാറുണ്ട്.

❌ (C) a person who is very loyal to his master
• 'Loyal like a dog' — 'dog' word ഉള്ളതിനാൽ loyalty ആണ് meaning എന്ന് തോന്നാം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Dogs are known for loyalty — students ഈ common knowledge-ൽ confuse ആകും. But ഇത് 'manger' context — loyalty അല്ല, selfishness ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: Picture a DOG sitting in a HAY TROUGH (manger) — hay ഒന്നും കഴിക്കുന്നില്ല, cows-നെ near വിടുന്നുമില്ല — 'I won't use it, you can't have it either!' — ഇതാണ് dog in the manger!
• Related selfish idioms: 'sour grapes' (കഴിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ലെങ്കിൽ grapes sour ആണ് എന്ന് പറയുക — denial), 'dog in the manger' (കഴിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞില്ലെങ്കിൽ others-നെ prevent ചെയ്യുക).
• PSC exam strategy: Animal idioms ഉള്ള questions-ൽ story background ആലോചിക്കുക — meaning literal അല്ല, figurative ആണ്.

questionText:
Spot the error:
Although he left home early / but he / reached school late / No error

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) but he

• ഈ sentence-ൽ 'Although' ഉം 'but' ഉം ഒരുമിച്ച് ഉപയോഗിച്ചതാണ് error — ഇത് double conjunction ആണ്, ഇംഗ്ലീഷ് grammar-ൽ ഇത് അനുവദനീയമല്ല.
• 'Although' ഒരു subordinating conjunction ആണ്. ഇത് ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് contrast express ചെയ്യാൻ ശേഷമുണ്ട് — 'but' ഒരിക്കലും കൂടെ വേണ്ട.
• Correct form: 'Although he left home early, he reached school late.' (but ഇല്ലാതെ)
• OR: 'He left home early but reached school late.' (Although ഇല്ലാതെ)
• ഇത് ഒരു common mistake ആണ് — Malayalam-ൽ 'ആണെങ്കിലും... പക്ഷേ...' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന pattern English-ലേക്ക് directly translate ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ഈ error വരുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: ALTHOUGH + [clause] → NO 'BUT'! One conjunction = one contrast.
• Subordinating conjunctions (ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് work ചെയ്യുന്നവ): although, though, even though, because, since, as, when, while, if, unless
• ഇവ ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ 'but', 'yet', 'still' main clause-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കരുത്.
• Exception: 'Though... yet' is acceptable in formal English — 'Though he was tired, yet he continued.'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Although...but' error ഒരു PSC favourite error type ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. ❌ Although she studied hard, but she failed. → ✅ Although she studied hard, she failed.
  2. ❌ Because he was sick, but he came to work. → ✅ Because he was sick, he came to work.
  3. ✅ He was sick, but he came to work. (no 'although' here — so 'but' is fine)
  4. ✅ Though he tried hard, yet he couldn't succeed. (though...yet — acceptable)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Although he left home early
• ഈ clause grammatically correct ആണ് — 'although' + subject + verb — perfect.
• Subordinating conjunction correctly used ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Although' alone ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് wrong ആണ് എന്ന് students confused ആകും — but 'although' alone ഉപയോഗിക്കാം!

❌ (C) reached school late
• 'reached school late' — grammatically correct ആണ്, 'reached' + place + time adverb — perfect.
• Main clause-ൽ ഒരു error ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'School-ൽ late ആയി' arrive ചെയ്‌തത് wrong ആണ് (logically) — but grammatically part (C) correct ആണ്.

❌ (D) No error
• Error clearly ഉണ്ട് — 'although' ഉം 'but' ഉം ഒരുമിച്ച് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന double conjunction error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sentence-ന്റെ overall meaning clear ആണ്, അതുകൊണ്ട് 'No error' ആണ് ശരി എന്ന് students തെറ്റിദ്ധരിക്കും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ALTHOUGH kicks out BUT' — ഒരു room-ൽ although ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ but-ന് entry ഇല്ല! ✋
• ഇതേ pattern ഉള്ള pairs ഓർക്കുക:
  - Although / Though / Even though → no 'but'
  - Because / Since / As → no 'so'
  - If → no 'then' (in formal English)
• PSC exam strategy: Error spotting questions-ൽ conjunction pairs ആദ്യം check ചെയ്യുക — 'although...but', 'because...so', 'if...then' ഇവ common error patterns ആണ്.

questionText: 'Facsimile' means:

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) exact copy

• 'Facsimile' (fak-SIM-i-lee) — ഒരു document, image, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ object-ന്റെ exact reproduction ആണ്.
• ഇന്ന് നാം 'fax machine' എന്ന് പറയുന്നത് 'facsimile machine'-ന്റെ short form ആണ്!
• FAX = FAcsimile Transmission — ഒരു document-ന്റെ exact copy wire-ലൂടെ send ചെയ്യുന്ന machine.
• Etymology: Latin 'fac simile' = 'make similar/alike' — 'fac' (make) + 'simile' (similar). ഇതുകൊണ്ടാണ് 'exact copy' meaning ആകുന്നത്.
• Historical context: Manuscripts-ന്റെ exact reproductions-നെ 'facsimile editions' എന്ന് വിളിക്കുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: FAX = FAcsimile — ഇത് ഓർത്താൽ meaning ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Etymology: Latin 'fac' (make/do) + 'simile' (similar/like)
• 'Simile' root ഉള്ള words: similar (alike), simile (comparison in literature), simulate (pretend/copy), facsimile (exact copy)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One-word meaning questions-ൽ Latin-origin words frequent ആണ്.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The museum displayed a facsimile of the ancient manuscript.
  2. Please send a facsimile of your certificate to the office.
  3. This painting is not the original — it is a facsimile made by a skilled artist.
  4. He sent the documents via facsimile (fax) before the deadline.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) fake
• 'Fake' = കള്ളം, genuine അല്ലാത്തത്.
• 'Facsimile' ഒരു fake copy അല്ല — ഇത് ഒരു EXACT, accurate copy ആണ്. ഗുണനിലവാരത്തിൽ difference ഉണ്ടാകാം, but ഉദ്ദേശം accurate reproduction ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Fac' → 'fake' ആണ് root എന്ന് sound-ൽ confuse ആകും — but 'fac' Latin-ൽ 'make' ആണ്.

❌ (C) at its zenith
• 'Zenith' = ഏറ്റവും ഉയർന്ന point, peak, pinnacle.
• Facsimile-ന് ഇതുമായി ഒരു connection ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Uncommon words ഒരുമിച്ച് option-ൽ ഇടുമ്പോൾ 'at its zenith' familiar-ആയ phrase ആണ് — students guess ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) wide view
• 'Wide view' = panorama അല്ലെങ്കിൽ vista.
• Facsimile-ന് view-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Wide' + 'view' combination ഒരു confident-ആയ distractor ആണ് — many students randomly ഇത് select ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: FAX machine ഉപയോഗിച്ചിട്ടുണ്ടോ? FAX = FAcsimile! — document-ന്റെ exact copy send ചെയ്യുന്ന machine. ഇത് ഓർത്താൽ facsimile = exact copy ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• 'Simile' root family: similar, simulate, facsimile — ഇവ മൂന്നും 'alike/copy' idea ഉള്ളവ.
• PSC exam strategy: 'means' ഉള്ള vocabulary questions-ൽ word-ന്റെ prefix/suffix/root ആദ്യം analyse ചെയ്യുക. 'fac simile' = make similar → exact copy.

questionText: Naveen said, "Need I go at once?" (Change into reported speech)
optionA: Naveen said if he had to go just then
optionB: Naveen asked if I had to go just then
optionC: Naveen requested if he had to go just then
optionD: Naveen asked if he had to go just then

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Naveen asked if he had to go just then

• 'Need I go at once?' — ഇത് ഒരു Yes/No question ആണ്. Indirect speech-ൽ Yes/No questions → 'if' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'whether' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Reporting verb change: 'said' → 'asked' (question-ന് 'asked' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം, 'said' അല്ല).
• Modal verb change: 'Need' (modal auxiliary expressing necessity) → 'had to' (past form of need/necessity) — backshift rule.
• Pronoun change: 'I' → 'he' (Naveen ആണ് speaker, third person).
• Time expression change: 'at once' → 'just then' (immediacy in past context).
• Complete transformation: 'Need I go at once?' → 'if he had to go just then' ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Direct Question → Indirect: said→asked + if/whether + subject + verb (backshift)
• Yes/No questions indirect speech rule:
  - Add 'if' or 'whether' after reporting verb
  - Remove question mark and question word order
  - Apply backshift (present→past, will→would, can→could, need→had to)
• Modal backshift table:
  - will → would
  - can → could  
  - may → might
  - need (auxiliary) → had to
  - shall → should
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported speech questions PSC-ൽ guaranteed ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Can you help me?' → She asked if I could help her.
  2. 'Will you come?' → He asked if she would come.
  3. 'Need I wait?' → She asked if she had to wait.
  4. 'Do you know him?' → He asked me if I knew him.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Naveen said if he had to go just then
• 'said if' — Yes/No questions indirect speech-ൽ reporting verb 'said' → 'asked' ആകണം.
• 'said' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് statements-ന് മാത്രം — questions-ന് 'asked' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Options (A) ഉം (D) ഉം majors differences ഒറ്റ word മാത്രം — 'said' vs 'asked'. Careful reading ആവശ്യം!

❌ (B) Naveen asked if I had to go just then
• 'I' — pronoun change ചെയ്‌തില്ല! Direct speech-ൽ Naveen 'I' (ഞാൻ) പറഞ്ഞത്, indirect speech-ൽ ആ 'I' → 'he' ആകണം.
• Naveen-ന്റെ perspective third person-ൽ report ചെയ്യേണ്ടതിനാൽ 'he' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Pronoun change forget ആകുന്നത് very common mistake — option (B) exactly ഈ mistake capture ചെയ്‌തിരിക്കുന്നു!

❌ (C) Naveen requested if he had to go just then
• 'requested' — 'request' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് polite commands/suggestions-ന് — 'Please do this' type sentences-ന്.
• 'Need I go?' ഒരു question ആണ്, request അല്ല — reporting verb 'asked' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Need' = ആവശ്യം → 'request' ചെയ്യുന്നതുപോലെ തോന്നും — but grammatically 'asked' ആണ് correct.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SAID for Statements, ASKED for Questions' — S-S, A-Q! Statement→Said, Question→Asked. ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ (A) option automatically eliminate ആകും.
• Pronoun change cheatsheet: I→he/she, my→his/her, me→him/her, we→they, our→their, you→they/he/she (context അനുസരിച്ച്)
• PSC exam strategy: Reported speech questions-ൽ 4 things check ചെയ്യുക: (1) Reporting verb correct? (2) 'if/whether' ഉണ്ടോ? (3) Pronouns changed? (4) Tense backshift ആയോ?

questionText: None of the workmen arrived, ————?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) did they?

• 'None of the workmen arrived' — ഈ sentence-ൽ 'None' subject ആണ്, ഇതിന് negative meaning ഉണ്ട് ('not any' = ആരും ഇല്ല).
• Negative statement → Positive question tag — ഇത് question tag-ന്റെ golden rule ആണ്.
• Main verb: 'arrived' — Simple Past tense. Question tag-ൽ auxiliary verb ഉപയോഗിക്കണം — Simple Past-ന് 'did/didn't'.
• 'None of the workmen' — 'workmen' plural ആണ്, pronoun → 'they'.
• Negative statement ('None...') + Positive tag ('did they?') = (C) ✓
• Summary: None (negative) + arrived (past) + workmen (plural=they) → 'did they?' ✅
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive statement → Negative tag | Negative statement → Positive tag
• 'None', 'no', 'never', 'neither', 'hardly', 'scarcely', 'rarely', 'seldom' — ഇവ negative words ആണ്. ഇവ ഉള്ള statements positive tag ആവശ്യമാണ്.
• Auxiliary verb selection (Simple Past sentences):
  - Main verb past tense (arrived, went, came) → 'did/didn't'
  - was/were → was/wasn't / were/weren't
  - had → had/hadn't
• Pronoun selection: workmen/workers/people → they | everyone/someone/nobody → they (informal)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'None', 'nobody', 'neither' ഉള്ള question tags PSC-ൽ regular ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Nobody came to the party, did they?
  2. None of the students passed, did they?
  3. She never lies, does she?
  4. He hardly works, does he?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) do they?
• Present tense tag — but main verb 'arrived' past tense ആണ്. Tense match ചെയ്‌തില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'None of the workmen' present context-ൽ ആണ് (workmen ഇപ്പോഴും workmen ആണ്) — present tense tag correct ആണ് എന്ന് തോന്നാം. But verb tense ആണ് tag decide ചെയ്യുന്നത്.

❌ (B) don't they?
• Present negative tag — verb tense wrong (arrived = past, not present).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'None' negative ആണ്, so negative tag 'don't they?' ആണ് ശരി എന്ന് students confuse ആകും — but negative statement-ന് POSITIVE tag വേണം!

❌ (D) didn't they?
• Past tense — correct! പക്ഷേ negative tag ആണ് (didn't). 'None' already negative ആയതിനാൽ positive tag 'did they?' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common mistake — tense correct ആണ്, but positive/negative confusion. 'None' = negative → tag = positive ('did', not 'didn't').
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'NONE is NEGATIVE — so tag must be POSITIVE!' — None/Nobody/Never → always positive tag (did/does/is/was etc.)
• Negative words to remember: none, no, never, neither, nobody, nothing, hardly, scarcely, barely, rarely, seldom
• PSC exam strategy: Question tag questions-ൽ 2 steps: Step 1 — Statement positive or negative? Step 2 — Main verb tense? ഈ 2 steps ഉപയോഗിച്ചാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: 'Leptospirosis' is related with ————.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) rat

• 'Leptospirosis' — ഒരു bacterial disease ആണ്, primarily rats-ൽ നിന്ന് പടരുന്നത്.
• Rats-ന്റെ urine-ൽ ഉള്ള Leptospira bacteria മലിനജലത്തിലൂടെ മനുഷ്യർക്ക് പകരുന്നു — ഇതാണ് 'rat fever' (leptospirosis) എന്ന് Kerala-ൽ അറിയപ്പെടുന്ന രോഗം.
• Kerala floods-ന്റെ time-ൽ leptospirosis outbreak ഒരു major health concern ആണ് — ഇത് PSC students-ന് familiar ആയ topic ആണ്.
• Etymology: Greek 'leptos' (thin/fine) + 'speira' (coil) + 'osis' (disease condition) — bacteria-യുടെ coiled shape describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• ഓർക്കുക: Leptospirosis = Rat Fever = Rats ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Disease → Animal carrier connections PSC-ൽ vocabulary questions ആയി ചോദിക്കും!
• Medical/disease vocabulary — animal connections:
  - Leptospirosis (Rat Fever) → Rat
  - Rabies → Dog/bat
  - Malaria → Mosquito (Anopheles)
  - Dengue → Mosquito (Aedes)
  - Plague → Rat (via fleas)
  - Bird Flu (Avian Influenza) → Birds
  - Swine Flu → Pig
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Disease-animal, disease-doctor name vocabulary frequent ആണ്
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Leptospirosis spreads through water contaminated with rat urine.
  2. The flood victims were warned about the risk of leptospirosis.
  3. Rat fever cases rise during monsoon season in Kerala.
  4. Wear rubber boots to prevent leptospirosis when wading through floodwater.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) cat
• Cats can occasionally carry leptospirosis but are NOT the primary carrier.
• Leptospirosis-മായി ഏറ്റവും closely associated ആയ animal rat ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Cats ഉം rats ഉം common household animals — students confuse ചെയ്യാൻ chance ഉണ്ട്.

❌ (C) tiger
• Tigers wild animals ആണ്, leptospirosis-ന്റെ primary carrier അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Wild/dangerous animal option — easy to eliminate, but some students panic ചെയ്‌ത് guess ചെയ്യും.

❌ (D) cow
• Cows leptospirosis carry ചെയ്യാൻ possibility ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും, primary/most common carrier rat ആണ്.
• PSC context-ൽ 'rat fever' = leptospirosis — rat ആണ് correct answer.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Cows common domestic animals ആണ്, disease spread through them possible — students confuse ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'RAT FEVER = LEPTOSPIROSIS' — Kerala-ൽ flood time-ൽ doctors rat fever warn ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ leptospirosis ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശിക്കുന്നത്. ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!
• Disease-carrier pairs: Rat→Leptospirosis/Plague | Mosquito→Malaria/Dengue | Dog→Rabies | Pig→Swine Flu | Birds→Bird Flu
• PSC exam strategy: Disease-related vocabulary questions-ൽ Kerala context ഓർക്കുക — flood→rat fever→leptospirosis→rat. ഈ chain ഓർത്തിരിക്കുക.

questionText: ————— bird in hand is worth two in ————— bush.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) a, the

• ഈ sentence ഒരു famous English proverb ആണ്: 'A bird in hand is worth two in the bush.'
• First blank — 'a bird': ഇവിടെ 'bird' ആദ്യമായി mention ചെയ്യുന്നു, specific bird അല്ല, general ഒരു bird — indefinite article 'a' ✓
• Second blank — 'the bush': ഇവിടെ 'bush' specific ആണ് — proverb-ൽ imagine ചെയ്യുന്ന ആ particular bush (already implied context). Definite article 'the' ✓
• Proverb meaning: കൈയ്യിലുള്ള ഒരു bird, bush-ൽ ഉള്ള രണ്ടിനേക്കാൾ valuable — ഉറപ്പുള്ളത് uncertain-ആയതിനേക്കാൾ നല്ലത്.
• Article rule: First mention / non-specific → 'a/an' | Specific / already known → 'the'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: First mention = 'a/an' | Second mention / specific = 'the'
• 'A' vs 'The' distinction:
  - 'a/an' — indefinite, non-specific, first introduction: 'I saw a dog.' (ഏതെങ്കിലും ഒരു dog)
  - 'the' — definite, specific, already known: 'The dog barked.' (ആ dog, നമുക്കറിയാം)
• Proverbs-ൽ articles fixed ആണ് — memorise the exact proverb form!
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Articles questions PSC-ൽ guaranteed! Proverb-based articles extra tricky.
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I bought a book. The book was interesting. (a → the)
  2. A stitch in time saves nine. (general proverb = 'a')
  3. The sun rises in the east. (unique = 'the')
  4. A bird in hand is worth two in the bush. (proverb — fixed!)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a, a
• Second blank-ൽ 'a bush' — wrong. Proverb-ൽ 'the bush' ആണ് correct. 'Bush' specific context ആണ് ഇവിടെ — definite article 'the' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both blanks ഒരുപോലെ 'a' ആകും എന്ന് symmetry തോന്നിക്കും — but second blank specific.

❌ (B) the, a
• 'The bird' — wrong. 'Bird' ഇവിടെ non-specific, first mention — indefinite 'a' വേണം.
• 'a bush' — second blank-ൽ also wrong — 'the bush' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The bird in hand' sounds familiar/specific — but proverb-ൽ 'a bird' ആണ്.

❌ (C) the, the
• 'The bird' — first blank wrong (non-specific bird, 'a' വേണം).
• 'The bush' — second blank correct! But first blank wrong ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both 'the' use ചെയ്‌ത് specific context create ചെയ്യാം എന്ന് തോന്നും — but proverb exact form 'a bird...the bush' ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'a BIRD first time flies in, the BUSH is the one specific bush we all imagine!' — Proverb picture: ഒരു bird (any bird) കൈയ്യിൽ — ആ specific bush-ൽ (the one we're talking about) രണ്ടു birds.
• Article quick rules: a/an = new/unknown | the = known/specific | no article = plural general (Birds fly.)
• PSC exam strategy: Proverb/idiom-based article questions-ൽ exact proverb form memorise ചെയ്‌ത് വയ്ക്കുക. Common PSC proverbs with articles: 'A rolling stone gathers no moss', 'The pen is mightier than the sword', 'A bird in hand is worth two in the bush'.

questionText: He is being accused ————— theft.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) of

• 'Accused of' — ഇത് ഒരു fixed collocation (verb + preposition combination) ആണ്. 'Accuse' verb-ന് ശേഷം always 'of' ആണ് വരുന്നത്.
• Meaning: 'He is being accused of theft' = 'അദ്ദേഹം മോഷണം ആരോപിക്കപ്പെടുകയാണ്.'
• Pattern: accuse + someone + of + crime/wrongdoing
• 'Accused of' legal/formal context-ൽ widely use ചെയ്യുന്നു — court cases, news reports, etc.
• Related: 'guilty of', 'convicted of', 'charged with' — legal context prepositions!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: accuse/accused → always 'OF' — 'accused OF a crime'
• Legal/crime context preposition collocations:
  - Accused OF (crime)
  - Guilty OF (crime)
  - Convicted OF (crime)
  - Acquitted OF (charge)
  - Charged WITH (offence) — note: 'charged' uses 'with', not 'of'!
  - Suspected OF (crime)
  - Suspected OF (doing something)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Preposition collocations PSC-ൽ every paper-ൽ ഉണ്ടാകും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She was accused of stealing the money.
  2. He was found guilty of murder.
  3. They were convicted of fraud.
  4. The suspect was acquitted of all charges.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) with
• 'Accused with' — incorrect collocation. 'With' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് 'charged with' ആണ് (not 'accused with').
• 'Accused' + 'with' ഇംഗ്ലീഷ് grammar-ൽ standard collocation അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Charged with theft' familiar ആണ് — students ഈ pattern 'accused'-ലേക്ക് transfer ചെയ്യും. But 'accused' → 'of' ആണ്, 'with' അല്ല!

❌ (C) in
• 'Accused in theft' — ഇത് grammatically wrong, no standard usage.
• 'In' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് place/time context-ൽ — crime accusation-ൽ 'in' വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Involved in' pattern ഓർത്ത് 'accused in' ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകാം.

❌ (D) to
• 'Accused to theft' — grammatically incorrect, no standard usage.
• 'To' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് direction/recipient context-ൽ (speak to, give to) — accusation-ൽ 'to' വരില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Admit to' pattern ഓർത്ത് 'accused to' ആകും എന്ന് tease ചെയ്യുന്ന option.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ACCUSE someone OF something' — A-O: Accuse-Of! A ഉം O ഉം pair ആണ് — ഒരിക്കലും മറക്കരുത്!
• Legal preposition pairs ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കുക:
  - Accused OF / Guilty OF / Convicted OF / Acquitted OF
  - Charged WITH / Confronted WITH
• PSC exam strategy: Preposition questions-ൽ verb/adjective + preposition fixed collocations ആണ് ഉത്തരം — grammar rule കൊണ്ട് derive ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല, memorise ചെയ്‌ത് വയ്ക്കണം. Top 20 collocations list ഉണ്ടാക്കി practice ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: They have contacted everyone concerned. (Change the voice)

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Everyone concerned has been contacted

• Active sentence: 'They have contacted everyone concerned.' — Present Perfect Active.
• Passive transformation step by step:
  1. Object → Subject: 'everyone concerned' → subject ആകുന്നു
  2. Verb change: 'have contacted' (Present Perfect Active) → 'has been contacted' (Present Perfect Passive)
  3. Subject 'They' → 'by them' (optional, omitted here as agent is unclear/unimportant)
• Why 'has' not 'have'? — 'Everyone' singular pronoun ആണ്! 'Everyone' always takes singular verb — 'Everyone HAS, not HAVE'.
• Passive formula for Present Perfect: has/have + been + past participle
• 'Everyone' singular → 'has been contacted' ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Active tense = Passive tense ALWAYS | 'Everyone' = singular = 'has'
• Passive voice tense chart:
  - Simple Present: is/am/are + V3
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3
  - Past Perfect: had + been + V3
  - Present Continuous: is/am/are + being + V3
  - Simple Future: will + be + V3
  - Modal: modal + be + V3
• 'Everyone/someone/anyone/nobody/each' — always SINGULAR (has, is, was)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Voice change + singular-plural agreement combination — PSC favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. They have finished the work. → The work has been finished.
  2. She has written the letter. → The letter has been written by her.
  3. Everyone has submitted the form. (singular 'has') ✓
  4. Everyone concerned has been informed. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Everyone have been contacted
• 'Everyone...have' — double error! 'Everyone' singular ആണ്, 'have' plural ആണ്. 'Has been contacted' ആകണം.
• Plus 'concerned' omit ചെയ്‌തു — original sentence-ൽ 'everyone concerned' ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Everyone' sounds like many people (plural) → 'have' ആകും എന്ന് students confuse ആകും.

❌ (B) Everyone concerned have been contacted
• 'Everyone concerned...have' — 'everyone' singular ആണ്, 'have' plural ആണ്. Error in subject-verb agreement.
• 'concerned' ഉണ്ട്, അത് correct — but 'have' → 'has' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Option (B)-ഉം (C)-ഉം ഒരു word മാത്രം different (have vs has) — careless reading-ൽ miss ആകും!

❌ (D) Everyone concerned is being contacted
• 'is being contacted' — Present Continuous Passive ആണ്. Original sentence Present Perfect ('have contacted') ആണ്.
• Tense match ചെയ്‌തില്ല — Active tense = Passive tense ALWAYS.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'is being' formal ആയി sound ആകും — but tense wrong ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EVERYONE is ONE person — so ONE verb (has/is/was)!' — Everyone = Every ONE = singular. 'Has been', not 'have been'.
• Singular pronouns list: everyone, someone, anyone, no one, nobody, somebody, anybody, each, either, neither — ഇവ all singular!
• PSC exam strategy: Voice change questions-ൽ 2 things check: (1) Tense match ആയോ? (2) Subject singular/plural ആണോ? Both correct ആയ option select ചെയ്യുക.

questionText:
The publisher told me that my mistakes wouldn't be a problem in the future. I thought he was very kind, but later, I realized that he was giving me a left-handed compliment.

What does the idiom "a left-handed compliment" mean?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Insulting remark

• 'A left-handed compliment' = compliment-ന്റെ form-ൽ പറയുന്ന ഒരു insult — praise-ൽ ഒളിഞ്ഞിരിക്കുന്ന criticism.
• Story context: Publisher 'mistakes won't be a problem' എന്ന് പറഞ്ഞത് kindness ആണ് എന്ന് thought ചെയ്‌തു — but later realize ചെയ്‌തു it was actually an insult (mistakes ഉണ്ട് as a fact, just 'won't matter' is a backhanded remark).
• 'Left-handed' = indirect, insincere, awkward — ചരിത്രപരമായി 'left' = sinister/unlucky association.
• Another name: 'backhanded compliment' — ഇതേ meaning. 'Backhanded' = indirect, not straightforward.
• Real life: 'You look nice today' (implying you don't usually look nice) — ഇതൊരു left-handed compliment ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Left-handed compliment = Praise ആയി sound ആകുന്ന Insult!
• Related 'praise-but-insult' idioms:
  - 'Damn with faint praise' — barely praise ചെയ്‌ത് actually criticize ചെയ്യുക
  - 'Two-edged compliment' — same meaning
  - 'Backhanded compliment' — most common synonym
• Body part + hand idioms PSC favourites:
  - 'Old hand' — experienced person
  - 'Upper hand' — advantage
  - 'Hand in glove' — in close cooperation
  - 'Hand to mouth' — barely surviving
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom meaning questions passage-context-ൽ ചോദിക്കുന്നത് common ആണ്
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Saying 'Your English is good for a non-native speaker' is a left-handed compliment.
  2. She gave him a left-handed compliment by saying he ran fast for his age.
  3. His 'appreciation' sounded like a backhanded remark.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Positive feedback
• Positive feedback = genuine, sincere appreciation — left-handed compliment-ന്റെ exact opposite ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Compliment' word ഉള്ളതിനാൽ positive ആണ് meaning എന്ന് students confuse ആകും — but 'left-handed' qualifier ഉള്ളതിനാൽ negative ആകുന്നു.

❌ (C) Approval
• Approval = agreement, endorsement — ഒരാളുടെ action/idea accept ചെയ്യുക.
• Left-handed compliment approval അല്ല — hidden criticism ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Publisher 'won't be a problem' എന്ന് പറഞ്ഞത് approval ആണ് എന്ന് initial reading-ൽ തോന്നാം.

❌ (D) Encouragement
• Encouragement = genuine support to continue/improve.
• Left-handed compliment demoralizing ആണ്, not encouraging.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Publisher's words kind ആണ് എന്ന് narrator initially thought — so 'encouragement' seem right — but context 'later I realized' clue ആണ്, meaning it was NOT genuine.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LEFT hand = WRONG hand (traditionally)' — so left-handed compliment = WRONG compliment = insult in disguise! Left ഉം wrong ഉം ഒരേ feel!
• Synonym pair ഓർക്കുക: left-handed compliment = backhanded compliment — PSC ഇവ രണ്ടും ചോദിക്കാറുണ്ട്.
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ passage context carefully read ചെയ്യുക. 'Later I realized' = clue that initial impression was WRONG — so the opposite of the first impression is the answer.

questionText: Give a one word substitute for "a sudden complete failure".

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Debacle

• 'Debacle' (deh-BAH-kl) = ഒരു sudden, complete, chaotic failure — ഒന്ന് പെട്ടെന്ന് ആകെ collapse ആകുന്നത്.
• Etymology: French 'débâcle' → 'débâcler' (to unbar) → originally ice breaking up and causing chaos in a river. പിന്നീട് 'sudden catastrophic failure' meaning ആകി.
• Examples: A business debacle, a military debacle, an election debacle — sudden, complete, embarrassing failures.
• Context clue: 'Sudden + complete + failure' = three keywords → Debacle perfectly matches all three.
• Kerala PSC context: Match-fixing scandal, financial collapse, project failure — all can be called a 'debacle'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: DEBACLE = Sudden + Complete + Failure (3-in-1 word!)
• One word substitutes for failure-related concepts:
  - Debacle = sudden complete failure/disaster
  - Fiasco = complete failure (especially a planned event)
  - Catastrophe = great sudden disaster
  - Calamity = serious disaster with lasting effects
  - Collapse = sudden fall/failure of a system
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: One word substitutes PSC-ൽ every paper-ൽ certain ആണ്!
• Etymology hints (remember these roots):
  - 'mal-' = bad (malady, malicious, malpractice)
  - 'holo-' = whole, 'caust' = burning (holocaust)
  - 'nem-' = justice/retribution (nemesis)
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The launch of the new product was a complete debacle.
  2. The company's overseas expansion ended in a debacle.
  3. The team's performance in the finals was an utter debacle.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Holocaust
• Holocaust = mass destruction, especially of people — genocide.
• 'Holocaust' specifically refers to large-scale destruction of human life (World War II Jewish genocide most famously).
• ഇത് 'sudden complete failure' അല്ല — destruction of lives ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Complete destruction' feel ഉള്ളതിനാൽ confuse ആകും — but human lives involved ആകുമ്പോൾ 'holocaust', general failure-ന് 'debacle'.

❌ (C) Malady
• Malady = illness, disease, disorder.
• 'Mal' (bad) root — 'malady' = bad physical condition.
• Failure-ന്റെ meaning ഇല്ല — sickness ആണ് meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Mal' prefix = something bad → failure ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകാം — but 'malady' specifically = disease.

❌ (D) Nemesis
• Nemesis = (1) a long-standing rival/enemy, or (2) inescapable punishment/downfall.
• Greek mythology: Nemesis = goddess of retribution/revenge.
• 'Sudden complete failure' ഒരു nemesis ആകണമെന്നില്ല — nemesis-ൽ 'deserved punishment' feel ഉണ്ട്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Nemesis sounds dramatic and serious like a 'complete failure' — but meaning specific: fate/rival, not sudden collapse.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DE-BACLE = DE-structed BADLY' — everything came apart suddenly and badly! 'De' = down/apart, 'bâcle' = chaos. Picture a dam breaking suddenly — that's a debacle!
• The 4 'D-words' for disaster: Debacle (sudden failure), Disaster (great harm), Devastation (widespread destruction), Downfall (ruin of a person/system)
• PSC exam strategy: One word substitute questions-ൽ definition-ൽ ഉള്ള KEY words underline ചെയ്യുക. Here: 'sudden' + 'complete' + 'failure' = 3 keys → Debacle is the only option matching all 3.

questionText: Find the word which is a synonym of "Cliche".

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Stereotype

• 'Cliché' = ഒരു phrase, idea, or element that has been overused to the point of losing its original meaning or impact — ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ കേൾക്കുന്ന, worn-out expression.
• 'Stereotype' = a fixed, oversimplified, overused idea or image about a particular group — ഒരു group/idea-നെക്കുറിച്ചുള്ള oversimplified, oft-repeated belief.
• Both 'cliché' and 'stereotype' share the core idea of being OVERUSED and LACKING ORIGINALITY.
• Etymology: 'Cliché' is French — originally a printing term (stereotype printing plate). 'Stereotype' is also from printing — both words literally came from the same printing process!
• Common: Both mean something repeated so many times it becomes meaningless/predictable.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Cliché = Stereotype = Overused/Predictable idea
• Etymology connection: Both 'cliché' and 'stereotype' originated from PRINTING press terms! Stereotype plate = a fixed metal plate used repeatedly in printing.
• Word family for 'cliché':
  - Clichéd (adjective) = overused
  - 'A cliché' (noun) = an overused expression
  - Common clichés: 'Time heals all wounds', 'Actions speak louder than words'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Synonym questions with French/Latin-origin words PSC-ൽ frequent
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Better late than never' is a classic cliché.
  2. The film relied on stereotypes instead of original characters.
  3. His speech was full of clichés — nothing new or inspiring.
  4. Saying 'follow your dreams' has become such a cliché.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Novelty
• Novelty = newness, something new and unusual — cliché-ന്റെ ANTONYM ആണ്!
• Cliché = old/overused; Novelty = new/fresh — exact opposites.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: PSC-ൽ antonym options synonyms-ആയി disguise ചെയ്‌ത് confuse ചെയ്യും — 'novelty' exactly ഇങ്ങനെ ആണ്!

❌ (C) Creative
• Creative = original, imaginative, innovative — cliché-ന്റെ opposite feel.
• Cliché = lack of creativity; Creative = full of originality.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Creative sounds positive — students think 'cliché has creative energy'. But cliché is the DEATH of creativity!

❌ (D) Original
• Original = first of its kind, not copied — cliché-ന്റെ opposite ആണ്.
• Cliché = not original at all; Original = completely new.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Original' sounds like it could be a type of expression — students confuse with 'source' meaning of original. But here original = new/unique.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Cliché & Stereotype = Twins from the PRINTING PRESS' — ഒരേ printing machine-ൽ നിന്ന് ജനിച്ച രണ്ട് words! Both = overused, fixed, repeated. ഈ printing story ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ synonym ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Cliché antonyms (PSC trap options): novelty, original, creative, innovative, fresh, unique
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym questions-ൽ 'overused/repeated' concept ഉള്ള word ആണ് cliché — option-ൽ 'new/fresh' words antonyms ആണ് — automatically eliminate ചെയ്യുക.

questionText:
Fill in the blank with a suitable preposition:
Negligence from the parents causes the children to look ————— affection elsewhere.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) for

• 'Look for' = search for something, seek — ഒന്ന് അന്വേഷിക്കുക, തിരക്കുക.
• Sentence meaning: 'Parents-ന്റെ negligence മൂലം children other places-ൽ affection അന്വേഷിക്കാൻ നിർബന്ധിതരാകുന്നു.'
• 'Look for affection' = affection-ന് വേണ്ടി search ചെയ്യുക — 'for' ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ 'seeking/searching' meaning ആകുന്നു.
• This is a fixed phrasal verb collocation: 'look for' = to seek/search.
• Context clue: 'elsewhere' (മറ്റൊരിടത്ത്) = children are SEARCHING for affection in other places — 'look for' perfectly fits.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: look FOR = search/seek | look AT = observe | look INTO = investigate | look AFTER = take care of
• 'Look' + different prepositions = completely different meanings:
  - look for = search (ഞാൻ keys-ന് വേണ്ടി look for ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  - look at = observe/watch (picture-ൽ look at ചെയ്യുക)
  - look after = take care (baby-നെ look after ചെയ്യുക)
  - look into = investigate (matter look into ചെയ്യുക)
  - look up = search in dictionary / respect (word look up ചെയ്യുക)
  - look forward to = anticipate with excitement
  - look down on = disrespect
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Look' phrasal verbs PSC-ൽ regularly ചോദിക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is looking for a new job.
  2. The police are looking into the matter.
  3. Please look after the children while I'm away.
  4. Children look for love and attention from their parents.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) at
• 'Look at' = observe, watch — ഒന്ന് നോക്കുക (physically).
• 'Look at affection' — makes no logical sense. Affection-ൽ നോക്കുക (observe) — you can't physically look AT an emotion.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Look at' most common 'look' phrase ആണ് — students automatically choose it.

❌ (B) in
• 'Look in' — not a standard phrasal verb for seeking/searching.
• 'Look in' = briefly visit or check (informal British English).
• 'Look in affection' makes no grammatical sense here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'In' sounds like 'looking inside for something' — but standard English-ൽ this meaning-ന് 'look for' ആണ്.

❌ (C) with
• 'Look with' — not a standard phrasal verb.
• 'Look with affection' = affection-ൽ നോക്കുക (looking at someone lovingly) — but that changes the sentence meaning entirely.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'With' = accompaniment — 'seek affection with someone' meaning confuse ചെയ്യും. But sentence meaning 'search for affection' ആണ்.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Look FOR = FOR-age/FOR-age = going FORWARD to find' — FOR means going out to find something. When you're SEARCHING, use FOR! ഒരു child affection-ന് elsewhere 'go forward and find' ചെയ്യുന്നു — look FOR ✓
• 'Look' family cheatsheet: for=seek | at=watch | after=care | into=investigate | up=search/admire | forward to=anticipate | down on=disrespect
• PSC exam strategy: 'Look ___' blank questions-ൽ sentence meaning decode ചെയ്യുക — 'search/seek' meaning ഉണ്ടോ? → FOR. 'Observe' meaning ഉണ്ടോ? → AT. 'Care' meaning ഉണ്ടോ? → AFTER.

questionText:
Find the figure of speech of the following sentence:
"My blood turned cold like the moon."

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Simile

• 'My blood turned cold LIKE the moon' — ഇവിടെ 'like' എന്ന comparison word ഉപയോഗിച്ച് blood-നെ moon-ഉം compare ചെയ്‌തിരിക്കുന്നു.
• Simile = 'like' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'as' ഉപയോഗിച്ച് രണ്ടു വ്യത്യസ്‌ത things-നെ directly compare ചെയ്യുന്ന figure of speech.
• Simile-ന്റെ signature words: 'like', 'as...as', 'as if', 'as though'
• ഈ sentence-ൽ: blood (human, warm) → moon (cold, distant) comparison — 'like' keyword ✓
• Meaning: 'എന്റെ രക്തം moon-ിന്റേതു പോലെ തണുത്തുറഞ്ഞു' — extreme fear/shock express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: LIKE/AS = SIMILE | IS/ARE (without like/as) = METAPHOR
• Figures of speech — quick ID guide:
  - Simile: comparison using 'like' or 'as' → 'brave as a lion', 'cold like ice'
  - Metaphor: direct comparison WITHOUT like/as → 'He IS a lion', 'Life is a journey'
  - Personification: giving human qualities to non-human things → 'The wind whispered', 'Sun smiled'
  - Oxymoron: two contradictory words together → 'deafening silence', 'living dead', 'bitter sweet'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Figure of speech questions PSC-ൽ 100% ഉണ്ടാകും — guaranteed!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is as fast as a cheetah. (Simile — 'as')
  2. He is a cheetah on the track. (Metaphor — direct)
  3. The trees danced in the wind. (Personification)
  4. It was an open secret. (Oxymoron)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Metaphor
• Metaphor = 'like' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'as' ഇല്ലാതെ direct comparison.
• Example: 'My blood IS ice' (not 'like ice') — that would be metaphor.
• ഇവിടെ 'like' clearly ഉണ്ട് → Simile, not Metaphor.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simile ഉം Metaphor ഉം ഒരേ comparison idea — students confuse ആകും. Key difference: 'like/as' ഉണ്ടോ? Yes=Simile, No=Metaphor.

❌ (C) Personification
• Personification = non-human things-ന് human qualities കൊടുക്കുക.
• 'Blood turned cold like the moon' — ഇവിടെ moon-ന് human quality കൊടുക്കുന്നില്ല, blood-നെ moon-ഉം compare ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Moon' ഒരു non-human object ആണ് — personification ആണ് meaning എന്ന് confuse ആകാം.

❌ (D) Oxymoron
• Oxymoron = contradictory words ഒരുമിച്ച് ഉപയോഗിക്കുക.
• 'Cold like the moon' — ഇവിടെ contradiction ഒന്നും ഇല്ല; cold ഉം moon ഉം contradict ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Oxymoron unfamiliar word ആണ് — students random guess ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SIMILE = SIMilar with LIKE' — Simile-ൽ 'SIM' = similar, comparison 'LIKE' ഉപയോഗിക്കും! Metaphor-ൽ directly 'IS' ആകും — 'He IS a lion' (no like).
• Quick ID table:
  - See 'like' or 'as' → Simile
  - See human action by non-human → Personification
  - See opposites together → Oxymoron
  - See direct equation without like/as → Metaphor
• PSC exam strategy: Figure of speech questions-ൽ ആദ്യം 'like'/'as' keywords search ചെയ്യുക — ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ Simile. ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ other options consider ചെയ്യുക.

questionText: Pick out the sentence which is grammatically correct:
optionA: Between you and me, everything is a mystery
optionB: Between you and I, everything is a mystery
optionC: Between I and you everything is a mystery
optionD: Between you and he, everything is a mystery

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Between you and me, everything is a mystery

• 'Between' ഒരു preposition ആണ്. Prepositions-ന് ശേഷം ALWAYS object pronouns (me, him, her, us, them) ഉപയോഗിക്കണം — subject pronouns (I, he, she, we, they) അല്ല.
• 'Between you and me' — 'you' (object form same as subject) ✓, 'me' (object pronoun) ✓ — both correct!
• Rule: Preposition + Object Pronoun. 'Between' is a preposition → 'me' (not 'I'), 'him' (not 'he'), 'her' (not 'she').
• Common mistake in spoken English: 'Between you and I' sounds formal/educated but is actually GRAMMATICALLY WRONG!
• ഓർക്കുക: 'Between you and I' = hypercorrection — people try to sound educated but end up making an error.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Preposition + me/him/her/us/them (NEVER I/he/she/we/they)
• Object pronouns (prepositions-ന് ശേഷം ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നവ):
  - I → me | he → him | she → her | we → us | they → them | who → whom
• Common prepositions that trigger this rule: between, with, for, to, from, of, by, about, after, before
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Between you and ___' pronoun choice PSC-ൽ classic question ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. This is between you and me. (NOT 'you and I') ✓
  2. She came with him and me. (NOT 'he and I') ✓
  3. The prize was divided between us. ✓
  4. To whom did you speak? (NOT 'who') ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Between you and I
• 'I' = subject pronoun. 'Between' preposition ആണ് → 'me' (object pronoun) വേണം.
• 'Between you and I' ഒരു very common hypercorrection ആണ് — educated ആയി sound ആകാൻ 'I' use ചെയ്യുന്നു, but grammatically wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇത് ഏറ്റവും common wrong choice! 'I' formal ആണ് → correct ആകും എന്ന് students believe ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) Between I and you
• 'I' = subject pronoun, preposition-ന് ശേഷം wrong. Plus word order unusual ('you' always first in 'you and I/me' convention).
• Double error: wrong pronoun + unusual order.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easily eliminatable — but some students choose this thinking 'I' before 'you' is more polite.

❌ (D) Between you and he
• 'He' = subject pronoun. 'Between' preposition ആണ് → 'him' (object pronoun) വേണം.
• 'Between you and him' ആണ് correct form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'He' and 'him' confusion — students forget preposition requires object form.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Between you and ME — ME is the one BETWEEN us!' — Picture yourself standing between two people: 'ME' is in the middle. ME = object pronoun. After prepositions, always use the one that can stand in the MIDDLE (object form)!
• Quick test: Remove 'you and' — does it sound right alone? 'Between I' ❌ | 'Between me' ✓ — this trick works every time!
• PSC exam strategy: Preposition + pronoun questions-ൽ 'between/with/for/to + I/he/she/we/they' ഉള്ള options immediately eliminate ചെയ്യുക — they are ALWAYS wrong.

questionText:
Fill in the blank with an apt phrasal verb:
Looking past in life, I still can't ————— what went wrong!

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Figure out

• 'Figure out' = understand, determine, solve — ഒരു problem/situation-ന്റെ answer കണ്ടുപിടിക്കുക, മനസ്സിലാക്കുക.
• Sentence meaning: 'ജീവിതത്തിൽ പിന്നോട്ട് നോക്കുമ്പോഴും, എന്ത് തെറ്റ് സംഭവിച്ചെന്ന് എനിക്ക് ഇന്നും മനസ്സിലാകുന്നില്ല.'
• 'I can't figure out what went wrong' — perfect fit. 'Figure out' = mentally work out/understand.
• Context clue: 'What went wrong' = a mystery/problem needing understanding → 'figure out' (understand) ✓
• Informal but very common in everyday English and PSC papers.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: FIGURE OUT = Mentally FIND OUT the answer
• 'Figure' family phrasal verbs:
  - Figure out = understand / solve
  - Figure in = include in a calculation
• Other 'OUT' phrasal verbs (PSC favourites):
  - Find out = discover information
  - Work out = solve / exercise
  - Point out = indicate / draw attention to
  - Carry out = execute/perform
  - Run out = exhaust supply
  - Turn out = result in / produce
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Phrasal verb blank-fill questions PSC-ൽ very common!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. I can't figure out why she is upset.
  2. The police are trying to figure out what happened.
  3. We need to figure out a solution to this problem.
  4. Can you figure out this puzzle?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Drag out
• 'Drag out' = prolong unnecessarily, make something last longer than needed — ഒരു കാര്യം അനാവശ്യമായി നീട്ടുക.
• 'I can't drag out what went wrong' — makes no sense in this context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Looking past in life... drag out' — past events drag out ആകും എന്ന് feel ആകാം. But 'drag out' = prolong, not understand.

❌ (C) Stand out
• 'Stand out' = be noticeable, be prominent — ശ്രദ്ധേയനാകുക, വ്യത്യസ്‌തനാകുക.
• 'I can't stand out what went wrong' — grammatically awkward and meaningless here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Stand out' common phrasal verb — students choose familiar options without checking context.

❌ (D) Drop out
• 'Drop out' = quit, withdraw from school/race — ഉപേക്ഷിക്കുക, പിൻവലിയുക.
• 'I can't drop out what went wrong' — grammatically wrong and meaningless.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Drop out' well-known term (college dropout etc.) — familiarity confuses students.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FIGURE = draw a picture in your head to UNDERSTAND' — figure out = mentally draw/map the answer. 'I can't figure out' = I can't draw a mental picture of what happened!
• 'OUT' phrasal verbs = completion/discovery theme: find OUT, figure OUT, work OUT, sort OUT — all mean 'completely understand/complete something'.
• PSC exam strategy: Phrasal verb questions-ൽ context meaning decode ചെയ്യുക. 'Can't ___ what went wrong' = can't UNDERSTAND. Which option = understand? Figure out ✓

questionText:
Choose the correct spelling:
They performed the last ————— with all due respect, before the body was cremated.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) rites

• 'Rites' (plural of 'rite') = religious or solemn ceremony/ritual — ആചാരം, ചടങ്ങ്, മതപരമായ കർമ്മം.
• 'Last rites' = funeral ceremony, the final religious rituals performed before burial/cremation — അന്ത്യ കർമ്മങ്ങൾ.
• Sentence context: 'before the body was cremated' — clearly a funeral ceremony → 'last rites' ✓
• Etymology: Latin 'ritus' = religious ceremony/custom.
• Common phrases with 'rite': last rites, rite of passage, initiation rite, burial rites.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: RITES = Religious Rituals | RIGHTS = Entitlements | WRITES = verb (to write) | WRIGHTS = craftsmen
• Homophones/near-homophones confusion group:
  - rite/rites = ceremony (last rites, rite of passage)
  - right/rights = correct; entitlement (human rights, right answer)
  - write/writes = to form letters/words (she writes daily)
  - wright/wrights = maker/craftsman (playwright = play+wright; wheelwright)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Homophones — words that sound same but different meaning/spelling — PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The priest performed the last rites at the funeral. (ceremony)
  2. Everyone has the right to education. (entitlement)
  3. She writes a diary every night. (verb)
  4. Shakespeare was a famous playwright. (wright = maker)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) rights
• 'Rights' = entitlements, freedoms — 'human rights', 'voting rights', 'right to education'.
• 'Performed the last rights' — grammatically possible to read but WRONG meaning. You don't 'perform rights' (entitlements).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Rights' most familiar spelling — students auto-choose familiar word without checking context.

❌ (C) writes
• 'Writes' = third person singular present tense of 'write' — She writes, He writes.
• 'Performed the last writes' — completely wrong; 'writes' is a verb, not a noun.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Same sound as 'rites' — phonetic confusion.

❌ (D) wrights
• 'Wrights' = craftsmen/makers (plural) — playwright, wheelwright, shipwright.
• 'Performed the last wrights' — completely meaningless.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Unfamiliar word — students think 'it's the uncommon one, so maybe correct?'. Always go with context meaning, not unfamiliarity.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'RITES = RITUALS — both start with RI!' — Rite/Rites = Ritual/Rituals. Last RITES = last RITUALS before cremation. RI → RI connection!
• Homophone alert group for PSC: rite/right/write/wright — all sound similar! Context is the only way to choose.
• PSC exam strategy: Homophone questions-ൽ sentence context meaning ആദ്യം understand ചെയ്യുക. 'Cremated, funeral, respect' = religious ceremony context → 'rites' (ceremony). Never choose by sound alone.

questionText: Identify the sentence which is in the passive voice:
optionA: He has reserved his seat on the bus
optionB: They had finished only half the work
optionC: Let him be invited to the party
optionD: I shall inform them of this

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Let him be invited to the party

• 'Let him be invited' — ഇവിടെ 'be invited' = passive construction (be + past participle).
• Passive voice-ന്റെ structure: be/been/being + past participle (V3).
• 'invited' = past participle of 'invite'. 'be invited' = passive form.
• Imperative passive: 'Let + object + be + V3' — ഇത് passive imperative sentence ആണ്.
• Active equivalent would be: 'Let someone invite him to the party' — ഇതിൽ 'him' ആണ് action receive ചെയ്യുന്നത്, passive ആണ്.
• Meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹത്തെ party-ലേക്ക് ക്ഷണിക്കട്ടെ' — action receive ചെയ്യുന്നത് 'him' (passive subject) ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Passive = be/been/being + V3 (past participle). ഈ pattern കണ്ടാൽ → Passive!
• How to identify passive voice quickly:
  - Look for: am/is/are/was/were/be/been/being + V3
  - The subject RECEIVES the action (action done TO the subject)
• Passive structures by tense:
  - Simple Present: is/am/are + V3
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3
  - Imperative: Let + object + be + V3
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Identify passive voice' questions PSC-ൽ regular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The letter was written by her. (was + written = passive) ✓
  2. Let the work be done on time. (be + done = passive imperative) ✓
  3. The bridge is being constructed. (is being + constructed = passive) ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He has reserved his seat on the bus
• 'Has reserved' = Present Perfect ACTIVE. 'He' is the doer — he reserved the seat himself.
• 'Reserved' ഒരു V3 (past participle) ആണ്, but ഇവിടെ 'has + V3' = Present Perfect Active, not passive.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Reserved' past participle ആണ് → passive ആണ് എന്ന് students confuse ആകും. But passive needs 'be/been/being' before V3.

❌ (B) They had finished only half the work
• 'Had finished' = Past Perfect ACTIVE. 'They' are the doers.
• 'Had + V3' = Past Perfect Active — not passive.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Finished' past participle — but no 'been' before it, so not passive.

❌ (D) I shall inform them of this
• 'Shall inform' = Simple Future ACTIVE. 'I' am the doer.
• No passive structure here at all — straightforward active sentence.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Shall' sounds formal/different → might feel like a special/passive form to students.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BE + V3 = PASSIVE — BE the receiver!' — When you 'BE' something done to you, that's passive. 'be invited' = you are being invited (action done TO you).
• Passive spotter: Find 'be/been/being' in the sentence → then check if V3 follows → PASSIVE confirmed!
• PSC exam strategy: Passive identification questions-ൽ each option-ൽ 'be/been/being + V3' pattern search ചെയ്യുക. ഉള്ള option = passive. ഇല്ലാത്ത options = active → eliminate.

questionText:
Choose the appropriate question tag:
He chose a book, ————?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Didn't he?

• 'He chose a book' — ഈ statement positive ആണ് (no negative word). So tag NEGATIVE ആകണം.
• Main verb: 'chose' = past tense of 'choose'. Simple Past tense → auxiliary 'did/didn't' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Subject: 'He' → tag-ൽ 'he' ✓
• Positive statement + Past tense + He → Negative past tag = 'didn't he?' ✓
• Step-by-step: (1) Positive → negative tag ✓ (2) Past tense → did/didn't ✓ (3) Subject = he ✓ → 'Didn't he?' ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive Statement → Negative Tag | Past Verb (chose/went/came) → did/didn't
• Question tag formula: Statement auxiliary (inverted) + pronoun?
• Tense → auxiliary matching:
  - Simple Present (goes) → does/doesn't
  - Simple Past (chose/went) → did/didn't
  - Present Perfect (has gone) → has/hasn't
  - will → will/won't
  - is/am/are → is/isn't, am/aren't, are/aren't
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Question tags PSC-ൽ every paper-ൽ ഉണ്ടാകും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She sings well, doesn't she? (positive → negative, present)
  2. They went home, didn't they? (positive → negative, past)
  3. He chose the blue shirt, didn't he? (positive → negative, past) ✓
  4. You will come, won't you? (positive → negative, future)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Did he?
• 'Did he?' = positive tag. But statement 'He chose' is positive → tag must be NEGATIVE ('didn't he?').
• Positive statement → positive tag is WRONG rule.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Past tense 'chose' → 'did' is correct auxiliary — but students forget the positive/negative flip rule.

❌ (B) Isn't he?
• 'Isn't he?' uses present tense 'is' — but main verb 'chose' is past tense.
• Tense mismatch: chose (past) → isn't (present) ✗
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'He' subject → 'is/isn't he?' pattern familiar — but tense must match main verb.

❌ (D) Won't he?
• 'Won't he?' = future negative tag — but 'chose' is past tense.
• Tense mismatch: chose (past) → won't (future) ✗
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Won't he?' sounds polite and questioning — students choose it for 'formal' feel.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CHOSE = PAST → tag uses DID/DIDN'T' — irregular verb 'choose → chose' is Simple Past. Any Simple Past verb (went, came, saw, chose) → did/didn't in tag.
• 2-step rule for question tags:
  - Step 1: Positive or Negative statement? → Flip it for tag
  - Step 2: What tense is the verb? → Use same tense auxiliary
• PSC exam strategy: Question tag questions-ൽ ആദ്യം statement positive/negative identify ചെയ്യുക, then verb tense identify ചെയ്യുക — ഈ 2 steps follow ചെയ്‌താൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: Would you mind ————— your car from the way?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) moving

• 'Would you mind ___?' — 'mind' verb-ന് ശേഷം ALWAYS gerund (V+ing) വേണം — infinitive (to + V) അല്ല.
• 'Would you mind moving your car?' = 'നിങ്ങളുടെ car മാറ്റിയാൽ ആകുമോ?' — ഒരു polite request.
• Rule: 'Mind' + gerund (V+ing). Mind-ന് ശേഷം infinitive ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Other examples: 'Do you mind waiting?' / 'I don't mind helping.' — എല്ലാം V+ing.
• Meaning of 'mind' here: object to / have a problem with — 'ആക്ഷേപം ഉണ്ടോ?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: MIND + V+ing (ALWAYS! Never 'to + V')
• Verbs that take ONLY gerund (V+ing) after them — PSC most tested list:
  - mind, enjoy, avoid, consider, suggest, recommend, finish, keep, practice, admit, deny, risk, miss, postpone, delay
• Verbs that take ONLY infinitive (to + V):
  - want, wish, hope, decide, plan, agree, refuse, manage, promise, tend
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Mind + gerund' rule PSC-ൽ almost every paper-ൽ ഉണ്ടാകും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Would you mind opening the window? (moving ✓)
  2. I don't mind waiting for you.
  3. Do you mind helping me with this?
  4. She doesn't mind working late.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) move
• 'Move' = base form / infinitive without 'to'. 'Mind move' — grammatically wrong.
• 'Mind' gerund ആണ് ആവശ്യം, base form അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would you mind move' — direct/simple ആയി sound ആകും, students choose ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) moves
• 'Moves' = third person singular present tense. 'Mind moves' — wrong form after 'mind'.
• 'Mind' + finite verb (moves) = grammatically incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Subject 'you' → third person connection → 'moves' wrong association.

❌ (C) moved
• 'Moved' = past tense or past participle. 'Mind moved' — grammatically wrong after 'mind'.
• Past participle ശേഷം 'be' വേണം (passive) — ഇവിടെ 'be' ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Moved' sounds polite (moved from the way) — context misleads students.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MIND + ING = MINDING your own business!' — when you MIND something, you are actively -ING (doing) it mentally. Mind + V+ing — the ING shows ongoing mental objection!
• Gerund-only verbs acronym: PERMS — Practice, Enjoy, Risk, Mind, Suggest (and many more!)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Would you mind ___?' questions-ൽ automatically V+ing choose ചെയ്യുക. ഇത് 100% rule — no exceptions for 'mind'!

questionText:
In the following sentence, supply a verb in agreement with its subject:
Either the casts or the director ————— responsible.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) was

• 'Either...or' rule: verb-ന്റെ number, OR-ന് ശേഷം വരുന്ന (NEARER) subject-നെ അനുസരിക്കണം.
• Structure: 'Either the casts OR the director ___ responsible'
• Nearer subject (OR-ന് ശേഷം): 'the director' = singular
• Singular subject + past context → 'was' ✓
• Sentence past tense context ആണ് (responsible = statement about a past situation) → 'was' (not 'is')
• Rule name: 'Proximity Rule' — verb agrees with the subject NEAREST to it.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Either A or B → verb agrees with B (nearer subject)
• Correlative conjunction agreement rules:
  - Either...or → verb = nearer subject
  - Neither...nor → verb = nearer subject
  - Not only...but also → verb = nearer subject
• Examples of proximity rule:
  - Either the boys or the teacher IS here. (teacher = singular → is)
  - Either the teacher or the boys ARE here. (boys = plural → are)
  - Neither she nor they WERE present. (they = plural → were)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Either/Neither subject-verb agreement PSC-ൽ guaranteed!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Either the manager or the employees are responsible.
  2. Either the employees or the manager is responsible.
  3. Neither the boys nor the girl was present.
  4. Neither the girl nor the boys were present.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) were
• 'Were' = plural past. 'The director' singular ആണ് — 'were' plural subject-ന് ആണ്.
• 'Casts' (plural) ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'were' choose ചെയ്യും — but nearer subject 'director' (singular) ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Either the casts...' — 'casts' plural → 'were' select ചെയ്യും. But nearer subject rule forget ആകുന്നു!

❌ (C) are
• 'Are' = plural present. Sentence past context + singular nearer subject — both wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Casts' plural + present feel → 'are' choose ചെയ്യും. But proximity rule + past tense both override this.

❌ (D) am
• 'Am' = first person singular (I am). Subject 'director' third person ആണ് — 'am' ഒരിക്കലും correct അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy to eliminate — but some students choose it thinking 'singular = am'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OR looks RIGHT → Right side wins!' — Either A OR B: look to the RIGHT of OR, that's your verb controller. 'Either casts OR director' → look right → 'director' (singular) → 'was'!
• Proximity rule applies to: either...or | neither...nor | not only...but also
• PSC exam strategy: Either/Neither questions-ൽ OR/NOR-ന്റെ right side subject identify ചെയ്‌ത് singular/plural check ചെയ്യുക → verb decide ചെയ്യുക. ഇത് guaranteed mark formula!

questionText: Mr. Bean saw a ————— of keys lying on the floor.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) bunch

• 'A bunch of keys' = ഒരു കൂട്ടം താക്കോൽ — ഇത് English-ൽ fixed collective noun expression ആണ്.
• 'Bunch' = a group of things fastened or growing together — keys, grapes, flowers, bananas ഇവയ്ക്ക് 'bunch' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'A bunch of keys' ദൈനംദിന ജീവിതത്തിൽ widely used expression ആണ് — keychain-ൽ keys ഒരുമിച്ചിരിക്കുന്നത് 'bunch' ആണ്.
• Mr. Bean comedy-ൽ keys bunch ആയി carry ചെയ്യുന്ന scene imagine ചെയ്‌ത് ഓർക്കുക!
• Fixed collocations: a bunch of keys/flowers/grapes/bananas.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: BUNCH = things clustered/grouped together (keys, grapes, flowers)
• Collective nouns for common objects — PSC must-know list:
  - A bunch of keys / grapes / flowers / bananas
  - A flock of birds / sheep / geese
  - A herd of cattle / elephants / deer
  - A pack of wolves / cards / lies
  - A bundle of sticks / notes / clothes
  - A group of people / islands
  - A fleet of ships / cars
  - A gaggle of geese
  - A pride of lions
  - A school of fish
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective nouns PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She gave me a bunch of flowers on my birthday.
  2. A bunch of keys was found on the table.
  3. A flock of birds flew over the lake.
  4. A bundle of sticks cannot be broken easily.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) flock
• 'A flock of' = birds, sheep, or geese-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന collective noun.
• 'A flock of keys' — wrong; flock living creatures-ന് ആണ്, objects-ന് അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flock' general 'group' feel ഉണ്ട് — students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) group
• 'A group of' = general collective — people, things, ideas-ന് broadly use ചെയ്യാം.
• 'A group of keys' technically possible but NOT the standard/fixed expression — 'bunch' is the specific correct term.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Group' most generic option — students choose it as 'safe' option. But PSC wants SPECIFIC collective noun.

❌ (D) bundle
• 'A bundle of' = sticks, clothes, papers, hay — things tied together loosely.
• Keys ഒരുമിച്ച് tied/fastened ആണ് — but 'bundle' keys-ന് standard English-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല; 'bunch' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Bundle' and 'bunch' similar feel — both = grouped things. But 'bunch of keys' is the fixed expression.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip
