• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BUNCH of BANANAS = BUNCH of KEYS' — ഒരു banana bunch-ൽ bananas ഒരുമിച്ച് cluster ആയി ഇരിക്കുന്നതുപോലെ, keys ഒരുമിച്ച് cluster ആകും — BUNCH! Banana bunch = Key bunch!
• Bunch uses: bunch of keys, bunch of flowers, bunch of grapes, bunch of bananas — 'small clustered things'
• PSC exam strategy: Collective noun questions-ൽ the MOST SPECIFIC/FIXED expression choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Group' always too general — PSC wants exact collective noun like 'bunch', 'flock', 'herd'.

questionText: You must get some more questions, ————— you can't complete your project.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) otherwise

• 'Otherwise' = or else, if not — ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ, അല്ലാത്തപക്ഷം.
• Sentence meaning: 'നിങ്ങൾ കൂടുതൽ questions നേടണം — ഇല്ലെങ്കിൽ project complete ആകില്ല.'
• 'Otherwise' ഒരു condition + consequence connector ആണ്: [do this] otherwise [bad result happens].
• Structure: Positive action + otherwise + negative consequence.
• 'Otherwise' = 'or else' — ഇത് conditional warning/advice-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: OTHERWISE = OR ELSE = if you don't do X, Y will happen
• Connectors and their meanings:
  - otherwise = or else / if not (condition-consequence)
  - whereas = while / in contrast (comparison/contrast)
  - however = nevertheless / but (contrast/concession)
  - moreover = furthermore / in addition (addition)
• How to use 'otherwise': [Warning/Instruction], otherwise [Bad Result]
  - Study hard, otherwise you will fail.
  - Hurry up, otherwise you'll miss the bus.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Connector/linking word fill-in-blank questions PSC-ൽ regular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Take an umbrella, otherwise you will get wet.
  2. Pay attention, otherwise you will miss important points.
  3. Save your work, otherwise you might lose it.
  4. You must rest, otherwise your health will worsen.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) whereas
• 'Whereas' = while, on the other hand — contrast/comparison connector.
• 'You must get questions, whereas you can't complete' — contrast meaning ഇവിടെ fit ആകുന്നില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Whereas' formal ആണ് → educated-sounding → students choose it.

❌ (C) however
• 'However' = nevertheless, but — concession/contrast connector.
• 'You must get questions, however you can't complete' — 'you can't complete' is not a contrasting idea here; it's a consequence.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'However' common connector — students default to it when unsure.

❌ (D) moreover
• 'Moreover' = furthermore, in addition — used to ADD another point.
• 'You must get questions, moreover you can't complete' — adding information doesn't make sense here; a consequence is needed.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Moreover' sounds formal and connecting — students confuse it with cause-effect connector.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OTHERWISE = OTHER + WISE = choose the other wise path!' — If you don't take the wise path (do the action), the other (bad) result happens. Do it OR ELSE!
• Connector quick guide:
  - Adding? → moreover, furthermore, besides
  - Contrasting? → however, whereas, nevertheless, yet
  - Consequence/warning? → otherwise, or else, therefore, thus
• PSC exam strategy: Connector questions-ൽ two clauses-ന്റെ relationship identify ചെയ്യുക. Here: clause 1 = instruction, clause 2 = bad consequence → 'otherwise' (or else) perfect fit!

questionText: If I had known that you were coming,
optionA: I would have prepared your favourite dishes
optionB: I would receive you at the railway station
optionC: I will take you to some important destination
optionD: I would ask you to bring some sweets

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) I would have prepared your favourite dishes

• 'If I had known' — ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്: If + had + V3 (past perfect in if-clause).
• Third Conditional rule: If + had + V3 → would have + V3
• 'If I had known' → main clause must be: 'would have + V3'
• (A) 'I would have prepared' = would have + V3 ✓ — perfect match!
• Meaning: 'നീ വരുന്നെന്ന് എനിക്ക് അറിഞ്ഞിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, ഞാൻ നിന്റെ favourite dishes prepare ചെയ്‌തേനേ' — but I didn't know, so I didn't prepare. Past unreal situation.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (Third Conditional — past unreal)
• Three Conditional types:
  - Type 1 (Real Future): If + Simple Present → will + V1
    e.g., If it rains, I will stay home.
  - Type 2 (Imaginary Present): If + Simple Past → would + V1
    e.g., If I were rich, I would travel the world.
  - Type 3 (Past Unreal): If + Past Perfect → would have + V3
    e.g., If I had studied, I would have passed.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Conditional sentences PSC-ൽ every paper-ൽ ഉണ്ടാകും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If she had called, I would have come.
  2. If they had left early, they would have caught the train.
  3. If I had known, I would have helped you.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) I would receive you at the railway station
• 'Would receive' = would + V1 — ഇത് Type 2 Conditional (present imaginary) result clause ആണ്.
• 'If I had known' (Type 3 if-clause) + 'would receive' (Type 2 result) = tense mismatch.
• Correct Type 3 result: 'would have received' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' correct word — but 'would + V1' vs 'would have + V3' difference miss ആകുന്നു.

❌ (C) I will take you to some important destination
• 'Will take' = Type 1 Conditional result (future real).
• 'If I had known' (past perfect) + 'will take' (future) = completely wrong tense combination.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will' sounds confident and natural — students choose it without checking tense match.

❌ (D) I would ask you to bring some sweets
• 'Would ask' = would + V1 — again Type 2 result clause, not Type 3.
• Type 3 if-clause needs 'would have + V3' result — not 'would + V1'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' is present — close to correct but missing 'have'. PSC specifically tests this distinction!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAD in if-clause → HAVE in result!' — If you see 'had known/done/gone' in the if-clause, the result MUST have 'have' in it: 'would HAVE prepared/done/gone'. HAD ↔ HAVE!
• Third Conditional = 'time machine regret' — ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ചെയ്യാതിരുന്ന/അറിയാതിരുന്ന കാര്യങ്ങളെക്കുറിച്ച് regret express ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• PSC exam strategy: If-clause-ൽ 'had + V3' (Past Perfect) കണ്ടാൽ → result clause-ൽ automatically 'would have + V3' search ചെയ്യുക — that is the ONLY correct option.

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the correct reported speech:
She asked me
optionC: does she visit this place often

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) if I needed lunch

• 'She asked me ___' — ഇത് reported speech (indirect speech) ആണ്. Reporting verb 'asked' ആണ്.
• Yes/No question indirect speech → 'if' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'whether' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Original direct question was probably: 'Do you need lunch?' → Indirect: 'if I needed lunch'
• Changes made:
  (1) 'Do you need?' → 'if I needed' (question structure removed, 'if' added)
  (2) Tense backshift: need (present) → needed (past)
  (3) Pronoun change: 'you' → 'I' (she asked me — the speaker is now 'I')
  (4) Question mark removed ✓
• All four changes correctly applied in option (A) ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Reported Yes/No Question = asked + if/whether + subject + verb (backshift)
• Indirect speech — 4 rules always:
  1. Reporting verb: 'said' → 'asked' (for questions)
  2. Add 'if/whether' (for Yes/No questions)
  3. Tense backshift: present→past, will→would, can→could, need→needed
  4. Pronoun change: I→he/she, you→I/they (context-based), we→they
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Reported speech questions PSC-ൽ guaranteed!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Do you like tea?' → She asked if I liked tea.
  2. 'Will you come?' → He asked if I would come.
  3. 'Can you help?' → She asked if I could help.
  4. 'Do you need lunch?' → She asked if I needed lunch. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) do you come with me
• Direct speech form — question word order (do you) ഇവിടെ ഉണ്ട്, 'if' ഇല്ല.
• Reported speech-ൽ question structure (do/does/did + subject) remove ആകണം.
• Pronoun 'you' change ആയിട്ടില്ല, tense backshift ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Direct speech-ന്റെ feel ഉള്ളതിനാൽ natural ആയി sound ആകുന്നു — students confuse ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) does she visit this place often
• 'She' pronoun — 'she asked me' context-ൽ 'she' → 'I' ആകണം, not retain 'she'.
• 'Does' present tense — backshift ആയില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Third person 'she' → 'she visit' natural feel — but pronoun should change to 'I'.

❌ (D) do you like pizza?
• Direct speech — 'do you', question mark ഇരിക്കുന്നു. ഇവ reported speech-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും ഉണ്ടാകില്ല.
• Reported speech-ൽ question mark ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല, question word order ഒഴിവാക്കണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Question mark ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'complete question' ആണ് → correct ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകുന്നു.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IF I = Reported Speech Signature!' — Reported Yes/No question-ൽ always 'if I / if he / if she' pattern ഉണ്ടാകും. Question mark ഇല്ല, 'do/does/did' ഇല്ല — 'if' മാത്രം!
• Reported speech red flags (wrong options identify ചെയ്യാൻ):
  ❌ Question mark ഉണ്ടോ? → Wrong (reported speech-ൽ ഇല്ല)
  ❌ Do/Does/Did + subject order ഉണ്ടോ? → Wrong
  ❌ 'If/whether' ഇല്ലേ (Yes/No questions)? → Wrong
• PSC exam strategy: 'She/He asked me ___' questions-ൽ 'if + subject + verb (past)' pattern ഉള്ള option automatically correct!

questionText: By the time we reached home, the thief ————— the spot.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) had left

• 'By the time we reached home' — 'reached' past tense ആണ്. 'We reaching home' = past reference point.
• Thief left BEFORE we reached — ഒരു past action, another past action-ന് before ആണ്. ഇതിന് Past Perfect (had + V3) ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Rule: ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഒരു action, another past action-ന് earlier ആയി happen ആയിട്ടുണ്ടെങ്കിൽ → Past Perfect (had + V3).
• 'By the time' = signal phrase for Past Perfect — 'By the time X happened, Y had already happened.'
• 'The thief had left the spot' (before we arrived) → had left ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: By the time (past) → Past Perfect (had + V3) for the earlier action
• Past Perfect signal words/phrases:
  - By the time... (by the time I arrived, she had left)
  - Before... (Before I came, he had eaten)
  - After... (After she had finished, she left)
  - Already... (He had already gone)
  - When... (When I reached, the film had started)
  - No sooner...than (No sooner had he left than it rained)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Past Perfect tense 'by the time' pattern PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. By the time we arrived, the match had ended.
  2. She had already left when I called.
  3. By the time the ambulance came, the patient had died.
  4. He had finished his homework before she came.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) leave
• 'Leave' = base form / present tense. Sentence past context ആണ് — 'reached' past tense ആണ്.
• Past context-ൽ base form ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy elimination — clearly wrong tense.

❌ (B) left
• 'Left' = Simple Past. Simple Past ഉം sentence-ൽ possible ആണ് — but meaning change ആകും.
• 'We reached + left' = two simultaneous past actions — but thief left BEFORE we reached, so Past Perfect needed.
• 'By the time' signals that one action preceded another → had left, not just 'left'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Left' past tense ആണ് → correct ആണ് എന്ന് students think. But 'by the time' Past Perfect demand ചെയ്യുന്നു!

❌ (C) leaves
• 'Leaves' = Simple Present (third person singular). Sentence past context ആണ് — present tense wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Easy elimination — clearly wrong tense in past context.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BY THE TIME = Time Machine — go FURTHER BACK!' — By the time something happened (past), something else had ALREADY happened even before that. Two steps back in time = Past Perfect (had + V3)!
• Past Perfect triggers: by the time, before, after, already, no sooner...than, hardly...when, scarcely...when
• PSC exam strategy: 'By the time' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ automatic Past Perfect (had + V3) choose ചെയ്യുക — guaranteed mark! ✓

questionText:
Fill in the blank using an article:
Ramesh is ————— MBA graduate.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) an

• 'MBA' — M-ന്റെ pronunciation 'em' (/ɛm/) ആണ് — ഇത് ഒരു vowel SOUND ആണ്.
• Article rule: 'a' vs 'an' letter-നെ അടിസ്ഥാനമാക്കിയല്ല — SOUND-നെ അടിസ്ഥാനമാക്കിയാണ്!
• Vowel sound (/a/, /e/, /i/, /o/, /u/ sounds) → 'an'
• Consonant sound → 'a'
• 'M' letter consonant ആണ്, but 'em' SOUND vowel (/ɛ/) ആണ് → 'an MBA' ✓
• ഓർക്കുക: 'an MBA', 'an MLA', 'an MP', 'an HR manager' — M, N, L, R, H, S, X ഇവ abbreviation-ൽ vowel sound ആണ്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: a/an = SOUND, not SPELLING! Vowel SOUND → an | Consonant SOUND → a
• Tricky cases — consonant letter but vowel sound → 'an':
  - an MBA (em-bee-ay)
  - an MLA (em-el-ay)
  - an MP (em-pee)
  - an hour (the 'h' is silent — 'our' sound starts with vowel)
  - an honest man ('h' silent → 'onest' vowel sound)
  - an X-ray (ex-ray — 'ex' vowel sound)
• Tricky cases — vowel letter but consonant sound → 'a':
  - a university ('yu-ni' — starts with 'y' consonant sound)
  - a European ('yu-ro' — 'y' consonant sound)
  - a one-way street ('wan' — 'w' consonant sound)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Abbreviation + article PSC-ൽ favourite trick question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is an MBA graduate from Delhi University.
  2. He is an honest person.
  3. It took an hour to reach.
  4. It is a university, not a college.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a
• 'A MBA' — 'M' consonant letter → 'a' ആകും എന്ന് students think. But 'M' pronunciation 'em' (vowel sound) → 'an' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇതാണ് ഏറ്റവും common mistake! 'MBA' letter 'M' = consonant → 'a MBA'. But sound rule forget ആകുന്നു!

❌ (C) the
• 'The' = definite article, specific reference-ന്. 'Ramesh is the MBA graduate' = ഒരു particular, known MBA graduate-നെ indicate ചെയ്യും.
• ഇവിടെ first mention, non-specific → 'a/an' ആണ് correct.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Ramesh specific person ആണ് → 'the' use ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് confuse ആകും. But 'graduate' non-specific ആണ് ഇവിടെ.

❌ (D) of
• 'Of' = preposition, not an article. 'Ramesh is of MBA graduate' = completely wrong grammar.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Distractor option — easily eliminatable.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SAY IT ALOUD — what sound do you hear FIRST?' — MBA → say it: 'Em-Bee-Ay' → first sound 'Em' = vowel → 'AN'. Hour → say it: 'Our' → vowel → 'AN'. University → say it: 'Yu-ni' → 'Y' consonant → 'A'.
• Abbreviation article rule: Spell out each letter — if first letter SOUNDS like vowel → 'an'
  M(em), N(en), L(el), R(ar), H(aitch), S(es), X(ex), F(ef) → ALL vowel sounds → 'AN'
• PSC exam strategy: Abbreviation article questions-ൽ letter-നെ silent ആയി read ചെയ്‌ത് first SOUND identify ചെയ്യുക. Sound = vowel → 'an'. Sound = consonant → 'a'.

questionText: His explanation was so complicated that only ————— students responded to his queries in the interactive session.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) a few

• 'Students' = countable noun (plural). Countable noun-ന് 'a few' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'A few' = some (not many) — countable nouns-ന്: a few students, a few books, a few chairs.
• Sentence meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ explanation ഇത്ര complicated ആയിരുന്നു — കുറച്ച് students മാത്രം respond ചെയ്‌തു.'
• 'A few' = positive sense — some students DID respond (small number but some).
• 'Only a few' = very few — 'only' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ negativity added, but 'a few' itself is countable ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: COUNTABLE → few/a few | UNCOUNTABLE → little/a little
• Quantifier rules:
  - a few = some (positive), countable: a few books, a few students, a few people
  - few = very few (negative/pessimistic), countable: Few people understand this.
  - a little = some (positive), uncountable: a little water, a little time, a little money
  - little = very little (negative), uncountable: Little time is left.
• Remember: 'A' makes it positive! 'a few' (some) vs 'few' (barely any)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: few/a few/little/a little confusion PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A few students passed the exam. (some students — countable)
  2. A little milk is left in the bottle. (some milk — uncountable)
  3. Few people know this secret. (almost none — countable, negative)
  4. Little hope remains. (almost none — uncountable, negative)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) little
• 'Little' = uncountable nouns-ന് (little water, little time, little money).
• 'Students' countable ആണ് → 'little students' wrong.
• 'Little' also has negative/pessimistic connotation — 'only little students' grammatically and contextually wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Little' and 'a few' both mean 'not much/many' — students confuse countable/uncountable rule.

❌ (C) the little
• 'The little students' = specific, identified small students — not the intended meaning.
• 'The little' ഒരു standard quantifier phrase അല്ല ഈ context-ൽ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The' adds specificity — sounds formal/correct to some students.

❌ (D) a little
• 'A little' = uncountable nouns-ന് (a little water, a little time).
• 'Students' countable ആണ് → 'a little students' grammatically wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'A little' ഉം 'a few' ഉം similar meaning (some) — but countable/uncountable difference miss ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FEW = can COUNT on fingers, LITTLE = can't count (liquid/abstract)!' — Few STUDENTS (count: 1, 2, 3 students) → FEW. Little WATER (can't count: how many water?) → LITTLE.
• Quick test: Can you count it? → few/a few. Can't count it? → little/a little.
• Common countable + few: students, people, books, chairs, days, questions
• Common uncountable + little: water, milk, time, money, information, advice, knowledge
• PSC exam strategy: '___ students/books/people' ഉള്ള options-ൽ automatically 'a few' → correct. '___ water/time/money' → 'a little' → correct.

questionText:
Put the phrases in the correct order to unscramble the sentence:
(a) the most interesting movies  (b) I have ever seen  (c) This is one of

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) (c)-(a)-(b)

• Correct sentence: 'This is one of the most interesting movies I have ever seen.'
• (c) 'This is one of' → (a) 'the most interesting movies' → (b) 'I have ever seen'
• Logic of the structure:
  - 'This is one of...' = main clause (subject + verb)
  - '...the most interesting movies...' = superlative noun phrase (what kind of movies)
  - '...I have ever seen' = relative clause (defining which movies)
• 'One of + superlative + plural noun + relative clause' = standard English pattern.
• ഓർക്കുക: 'One of the most ___' — superlative-ന് ശേഷം plural noun വരണം: 'one of the most interesting movieS' (movies, not movie) ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Sentence order = Main idea first → Describing phrase → Relative clause
• 'One of the most ___' structure:
  - One of + the most + adjective + plural noun + relative clause
  - 'This is one of the tallest buildings I have ever seen.'
  - 'She is one of the best teachers I have ever had.'
  - 'It is one of the most beautiful places I have visited.'
• Sentence unscrambling strategy:
  1. Find the SUBJECT + VERB phrase first (usually starts the sentence)
  2. Connect the describing/qualifying phrases
  3. Relative clauses (I have ever seen/done/visited) come LAST
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Sentence ordering questions PSC-ൽ regular ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. This is one of the best films I have ever watched.
  2. He is one of the most talented players I have seen.
  3. That was one of the worst decisions she had ever made.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) (b)-(a)-(c) → 'I have ever seen the most interesting movies This is one of'
• Relative clause 'I have ever seen' ആദ്യം വരുന്നു — sentence structure completely wrong.
• Meaning ഇല്ലാത്ത arrangement ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Random ordering — easy to eliminate by reading aloud.

❌ (B) (a)-(b)-(c) → 'the most interesting movies I have ever seen This is one of'
• Main clause 'This is one of' അവസാനം വരുന്നു — wrong. Sentence always starts with main clause.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: (a) adjective phrase ആദ്യം sound interesting ആണ് — but English sentence subject+verb ആദ്യം വേണം.

❌ (D) (b)-(c)-(a) → 'I have ever seen This is one of the most interesting movies'
• 'I have ever seen' ആദ്യം — relative clause sentence-ന്റെ beginning-ൽ alone നിൽക്കുന്നത് wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Fragments alone at start confuse students — always check main clause (This is...) first.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BUILDING SENTENCE = BUILD LIKE A HOUSE — Foundation first!' — Main clause (This is one of) = Foundation. Describing phrase (the most interesting movies) = Walls. Relative clause (I have ever seen) = Roof. Foundation → Walls → Roof = c → a → b!
• Sentence ordering golden rule: Subject + Verb ഉള്ള phrase ആദ്യം (main clause). Relative clause (who/which/that/I have...) always LAST.
• PSC exam strategy: Unscramble questions-ൽ ആദ്യം 'This is / He is / It was' type main clause phrase identify ചെയ്‌ത് ആദ്യം place ചെയ്യുക. Relative clause ('I have ever seen/done') always last place ചെയ്യുക.

questionText: The serang used a ____________ rope to secure the boat to the dock.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) thick, old, nylon

• ഇംഗ്ലീഷിൽ multiple adjectives ഒരു noun-ന് മുൻപ് ഒരു FIXED ORDER-ൽ വരണം — random ആയി arrange ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയില്ല.
• 'thick, old, nylon rope' — ഇത് correct order ആണ്:
  - thick = Physical quality/Size (ഘടന)
  - old = Age (പ്രായം)
  - nylon = Material (material)
• Standard adjective order: Opinion → Size → Physical quality → Shape → Age → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose
• ഇവിടെ: thick (size/quality) → old (age) → nylon (material) = correct!
• Meaning: 'The serang ബോട്ട് dock-ൽ കെട്ടിയിടാൻ ഒരു thick, old, nylon rope ഉപയോഗിച്ചു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: OSASCOMP — Opinion, Size, Age, Shape, Colour, Origin, Material, Purpose
• Adjective order full chart:
  1. Opinion/Observation: lovely, strange, ugly, interesting
  2. Size: big, small, tall, thick, long
  3. Physical quality: heavy, rough, smooth, clean
  4. Shape: round, square, flat
  5. Age: old, young, new, ancient
  6. Colour: red, blue, black
  7. Origin: Indian, British, Chinese
  8. Material: nylon, wooden, metal, silk, gold
  9. Purpose/qualifier: cleaning, cooking, racing
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective order questions English medium PSC papers-ൽ regular ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A beautiful small old French silver ring. (opinion-size-age-origin-material)
  2. A thick old nylon rope. (size-age-material) ✓
  3. A lovely little antique silver box. (opinion-size-age-material)
  4. Two enormous black leather boots. (number-size-colour-material)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) nylon, old, thick
• Material (nylon) ആദ്യം, age (old), size (thick) അവസാനം — OSASCOMP order-ന്റെ reverse ആണ്.
• Material always SIZE-ന്റെ ശേഷവും AGE-ന്റെ ശേഷവും വരണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Nylon' specific identifier ആണ് → ആദ്യം place ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് feel ആകാം — but material LAST ആണ്.

❌ (B) thick, nylon, old
• Size (thick) correct position. പക്ഷേ material (nylon) age (old)-ന്റെ മുൻപ് വന്നു — wrong.
• Age (old) material (nylon)-ന്റെ മുൻപ് വരണം: thick → old → nylon.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: thick ആദ്യം correct ആണ് → students ആദ്യ word correct → option correct end-ൽ confuse ആകും.

❌ (D) nylon, thick, old
• Material (nylon) ആദ്യം — wrong. Size-Age-Material order-ൽ material always last.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Nylon' product name/brand feel → ആദ്യം place ചെയ്‌ത് define ചെയ്യണം എന്ന് students think.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: OSASCOMP — 'Our School Always Surprises Children On Mondays Perfectly!' — Opinion, Size, Age, Shape, Colour, Origin, Material, Purpose. ഈ sentence ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ adjective order ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Quick PSC shortcut: Size/Quality → Age → Material (the last 3 most tested in PSC)
• PSC exam strategy: Multiple adjective order questions-ൽ OSASCOMP mentally apply ചെയ്‌ത് each option's adjectives classify ചെയ്യുക. Wrong order ഉള്ള options eliminate ചെയ്‌ത് correct order find ചെയ്യുക.

questionText: The idiom 'To haul over the coals' means

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) To speak angrily

• 'To haul over the coals' = someone-നെ severely scold/reprimand ചെയ്യുക — ശക്തമായി ശകാരിക്കുക, കുറ്റം പറയുക.
• Etymology: Medieval punishment — heretics were dragged over burning coals! ഈ brutal punishment-ൽ നിന്ന് 'severe scolding' meaning ആകി.
• 'Haul' = to pull with force, 'coals' = burning embers — someone-നെ coals-ന് മുകളിൽ കൂടി drag ചെയ്യുന്ന vivid image!
• Synonym idioms: 'tear someone a strip', 'give someone a dressing-down', 'read the riot act'
• Usage: A boss hauling an employee over the coals for a major mistake.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Haul over the coals = SCOLD severely (imagine painful coals = painful scolding!)
• Fire/heat-related idioms PSC favourites:
  - Haul over the coals = scold severely
  - Add fuel to the fire = make a bad situation worse
  - Fire on all cylinders = working at full capacity
  - Out of the frying pan into the fire = from bad to worse
  - Baptism by fire = a difficult first experience
  - Fan the flames = encourage conflict
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Idiom meaning questions English medium papers-ൽ regular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The manager hauled the accountant over the coals for the financial error.
  2. She was hauled over the coals by her teacher for submitting the assignment late.
  3. The CEO hauled the project team over the coals for missing the deadline.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) To make a confusion
• 'Make a confusion' = chaos ഉണ്ടാക്കുക — haul over the coals-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Coals = fire = chaos/confusion feel — students literal image-ൽ confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) To exaggerate dramatically
• 'Exaggerate' = കൂടുതലാക്കി പറയുക — haul over the coals-മായി ഒരു connection ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Dramatically pulling someone over coals' sounds exaggerated — students literal meaning-ൽ confuse ആകും.

❌ (D) To destroy one's reputation
• 'Destroy reputation' = character assassination — haul over the coals private scolding ആണ്, public reputation destroy ചെയ്യുന്നതല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Severe action = reputation damage feel — but meaning specifically 'scold/reprimand' ആണ്, destroy reputation അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'COALS are HOT = SCOLDING is HOT-tempered!' — Imagine your boss pulling you over burning coals while ANGRILY scolding you. Hot coals = hot (angry) words! Haul over coals = scold angrily.
• Similar 'scolding' idioms group: haul over the coals, tear a strip off, give a dressing-down, read the riot act, blow someone up
• PSC exam strategy: Fire/punishment imagery ഉള്ള idioms-ൽ 'scold/punish/reprimand' meaning search ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് most common theme ആണ്.

questionText:
Choose the correct question tag to fill up the blank.
Don't be late, ————?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) will you

• 'Don't be late' = negative imperative sentence (ഒരു command/request).
• Imperative sentences-ന്റെ question tag rules:
  - Positive imperative ('Do this') → 'will you?' (positive tag — requesting compliance)
  - Negative imperative ('Don't do this') → 'will you?' (ALSO 'will you?' — NOT 'won't you?')
• Special rule: Negative imperative-ന്റെ tag ALSO 'will you?' ആണ് — 'won't you?' അല്ല!
• 'Don't be late, will you?' = ദയവായി late ആകരുത് — soft request.
• ഓർക്കുക: Imperatives-ൽ positive/negative flip rule apply ചെയ്യില്ല — always 'will you?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Imperative (positive OR negative) → 'will you?' tag ALWAYS
• Imperative question tag special cases:
  - Come here, will you? (positive imperative → will you)
  - Don't be late, will you? (negative imperative → will you)
  - Let's go, shall we? ('Let's' → 'shall we')
  - Let him do it, will you? / shall we? (Let him/her/them → 'will you' or 'shall we')
• Note: 'won't you?' is also sometimes used for positive imperative (more polite/warm tone)
  - 'Have some tea, won't you?' = very polite invitation
  - But for NEGATIVE imperative → ONLY 'will you?'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Imperative question tags PSC-ൽ tricky question ആയി ഇടുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Sit down, will you?
  2. Don't make noise, will you?
  3. Let's start the meeting, shall we?
  4. Be careful, will you?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) would you
• 'Would you' = polite request auxiliary — but question tags-ൽ 'would' imperatives-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Would you' separate question ആണ് ('Would you do this?') — tag form അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' 'will'-ന്റെ polite form → imperative-ന് more polite ആകും എന്ന് students think.

❌ (C) do you
• 'Do you' = present tense question auxiliary. Imperatives-ന് 'do you' tag ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Do you' tag Simple Present statements-ന് (He goes → does he?).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Don't' = 'do not' → 'do' related → 'do you' tag ആകും എന്ന് students confuse ആകും.

❌ (D) doesn't you
• Grammatically wrong — 'doesn't you' ഒരിക്കലും correct അല്ല. 'Doesn't' third person singular-ന് (She doesn't → does she?).
• 'You' + 'doesn't' = subject-verb mismatch.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Don't' negative → 'doesn't' similar negative form → students confuse ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'COMMANDS always ask WILL YOU comply?' — Every imperative (do this / don't do this) is a command. Commands ask 'will you follow?' → always 'will you?'. Let's = 'shall we?' (we're included).
• Imperative tag summary: Do/Don't → will you? | Let's → shall we?
• PSC exam strategy: Imperative sentence (starts with verb or 'Don't') → automatically 'will you?' choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Let's' → 'shall we?'. ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: Find the correctly spelt word.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Manoeuvre

• 'Manoeuvre' = ഒരു skilled movement or action — തന്ത്രപരമായ നീക്കം, maneuver ചെയ്യുക.
• British English spelling: M-A-N-O-E-U-V-R-E → 'Manoeuvre'
• American English spelling: 'Maneuver' (simpler form)
• PSC (Kerala) British English follow ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ 'Manoeuvre' ആണ് correct.
• Etymology: French 'manœuvre' → Latin 'manuoperare' (manu=hand + operare=work) — hand-work/manual operation.
• Military context: 'Military manoeuvres' = tactical movements of troops.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Man-OE-uvre = the 'OE' digraph is the key! M-A-N-O-E-U-V-R-E
• British vs American spelling differences (PSC follows British):
  - Manoeuvre (British) / Maneuver (American)
  - Colour (British) / Color (American)
  - Honour (British) / Honor (American)
  - Realise (British) / Realize (American)
  - Travelling (British) / Traveling (American)
• 'OE' digraph words in English (from French/Latin):
  - Manoeuvre, Foe, Poem, Canoe, Shoe
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Tricky French-origin word spelling PSC-ൽ regular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The driver performed a difficult manoeuvre to park in the tight space.
  2. The army carried out extensive manoeuvres near the border.
  3. Political manoeuvres delayed the decision.
  4. She manoeuvred the boat through the narrow channel.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Manouvere
• 'ou' wrong position + extra 'e' at wrong place — 'Mano-ou-vere' incorrect arrangement.
• 'OE' digraph missing — the key feature of correct spelling.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Sounds like correct pronunciation but spelling arrangement wrong.

❌ (B) Manevour
• 'Mane-vour' — 'o' dropped from 'mano', vowels rearranged incorrectly.
• Looks like a made-up word — no standard English form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students who guess phonetically arrive at this wrong form.

❌ (C) Manouvre
• 'Mano-uvre' — 'e' missing after 'o' (should be 'oe' not just 'o'), and 'u' position wrong.
• Very close to correct but missing the 'OE' combination.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Closest wrong option — 'Manouvre' vs 'Manoeuvre' differ only in 'OE' vs 'O'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MAN + OE + UVRE — the MAN does a beautiful OE dance move!' — Picture a man doing a graceful OE-shaped dance move: M-A-N-OE-U-V-R-E. The 'OE' in the middle is the dance step!
• French-origin words often have unusual spellings — 'oe', 'eau', 'ue' combinations: manoeuvre, queue, fiance, naive
• PSC exam strategy: Tricky spelling questions-ൽ 'OE' combination ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക — 'Manoeuvre' ആണ് British standard. Memory trick: Mano + Oeuvre (French for 'work') = manual work = manoeuvre!

questionText: Choose the correct one word for a person or thing that is the centre of attention.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Cynosure

• 'Cynosure' (SY-nuh-shoor) = a person or thing that is the centre of attention and admiration — ശ്രദ്ധ, ആകർഷണം, admiration-ന്റെ കേന്ദ്രം.
• Etymology: Greek 'Kynosoura' = 'dog's tail' → the constellation Ursa Minor (Little Bear), whose tail-tip is the North Star (Polaris) — sailors used it for navigation, everyone's eyes were on it! → 'centre of attention' meaning.
• Cynosure of all eyes = everyone is watching/admiring → ഏവരുടെയും ശ്രദ്ധ ആകർഷിക്കുന്ന ആൾ/വസ്‌തു.
• Usage: 'She was the cynosure of all eyes at the party' — party-ൽ എല്ലാവരുടെ attention-ഉം അവളിൽ ആയിരുന്നു.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: CYNOSURE = CY-no-sure — the one everyone is SURE to look at!
• One word substitutes — attention/admiration related:
  - Cynosure = centre of attention/admiration
  - Celebrity = famous person
  - Luminary = a person who inspires others
  - Paragon = a perfect example of something
  - Idol = a person greatly admired
• Other words in the options:
  - Flotilla = a small fleet of ships/boats (കപ്പൽക്കൂട്ടം)
  - Banter = light-hearted teasing or joking (നേരമ്പോക്ക്)
  - Parlance = a particular way of using words in a specific context (ഭാഷാ ശൈലി)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Uncommon one-word substitutes English medium PSC-ൽ favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The young actress became the cynosure of all eyes at the awards ceremony.
  2. Her diamond necklace was the cynosure at the exhibition.
  3. The new player became the cynosure of the tournament.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Flotilla
• Flotilla = ചെറിയ കപ്പലുകളുടെ ഒരു fleet — a group of small naval vessels.
• Persons/things-ന്റെ attention-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Uncommon word → students don't know meaning → random guess ചെയ്യും.

❌ (C) Banter
• Banter = playful, teasing conversation — നേരമ്പോക്ക് സംഭാഷണം.
• Centre of attention-ന്റെ meaning ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Banter' known word → familiar → choose ചെയ്യും. But meaning completely different.

❌ (D) Parlance
• Parlance = a way of speaking used by a particular group — 'in legal parlance', 'in common parlance'.
• Attention/admiration-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Parlance' sounds sophisticated → students think it means something important/central.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CYNOSURE = CY (see!) + NO (no one can) + SURE (surely look away!)' — When someone is a cynosure, you SEE them and NO one can SURELY look away! Everyone's eyes are fixed on them!
• Alternative memory: North Star (Polaris/Cynosure) — sailors always looked at it for direction. Cynosure = the star everyone looks at!
• PSC exam strategy: One word substitute questions-ൽ definition keywords underline ചെയ്യുക: 'centre of attention' → Cynosure. Unknown words-ൽ etymology hint ആലോചിക്കുക — 'Cyno' = dog (Cynology = study of dogs), 'sure' = certain → not directly helpful here, but North Star connection ഓർക്കുക!

questionText:
Convert into passive voice:
Let him try this.
optionA: This will be tried by him.
optionB: This may be tried by him.
optionC: May this be tried by him.
optionD: Let this be tried by him.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Let this be tried by him.

• Active: 'Let him try this.' — Imperative sentence with 'Let'.
• Passive transformation step by step:
  1. 'Let' structure preserved — imperative passive always keeps 'Let'
  2. Object of active ('this') → Subject of passive ('this')
  3. Subject of active ('him') → 'by him' at the end
  4. Verb 'try' → 'be tried' (be + V3 = passive)
• Pattern: Let + object + V1 → Let + object + be + V3
• 'Let him try this' → 'Let this be tried by him' ✓
• Meaning preserved: ഇത് അദ്ദേഹത്തെക്കൊണ്ട് try ചെയ്യിക്കട്ടെ.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Let + O + V1 (active) → Let + O + be + V3 (passive)
• 'Let' imperative passive rule:
  - Active: Let + person + V1 + object
  - Passive: Let + object + be + V3 + by + person
• Other imperative passive patterns:
  - Do this → Let this be done
  - Open the door → Let the door be opened
  - Sing a song → Let a song be sung
  - Let him write it → Let it be written by him
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Let' sentence passive voice PSC-ൽ classic tricky question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Let her solve the problem. → Let the problem be solved by her.
  2. Let them complete the task. → Let the task be completed by them.
  3. Let him try this. → Let this be tried by him. ✓
  4. Let me do it. → Let it be done by me.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) This will be tried by him.
• 'Will be tried' = Simple Future Passive — but original sentence 'Let him try' imperative ആണ്, future tense അല്ല.
• 'Let' structure completely lost ആയി — imperative feel ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will be tried' standard passive ആണ് → students choose ചെയ്യും. But 'Let' imperative structure preserve ചെയ്യണം.

❌ (B) This may be tried by him.
• 'May be tried' = Modal passive — original-ൽ 'may' ഇല്ല.
• 'May' permission/possibility indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'Let' imperative-ന് 'may' ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'May be tried' = polite passive → 'Let' = permission → same feel. But grammar rule 'Let' → 'Let' preserved.

❌ (C) May this be tried by him.
• Inverted question form — passive imperative-ൽ inversion ഒരിക്കലും ഉണ്ടാകില്ല.
• 'May this be tried' interrogative feel ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്നു — original statement ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'May' formal/permission feel → Let-ന്റെ passive feel match ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LET stays as LET in passive — it never leaves!' — 'Let him try this' → 'Let this be tried by him'. LET is loyal — it stays in passive form too! Only the object and verb form change.
• Let passive formula visual:
  Let [person] [V1] [object] → Let [object] be [V3] by [person]
  Let him try this → Let this be tried by him ✓
• PSC exam strategy: 'Let' sentence passive questions-ൽ 'Let this/it/them be + V3' pattern ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് 100% correct formula!

questionText:
Adolescents easily ————— peer pressure.
Choose the appropriate phrasal verb from below to complete the sentence.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) succumb to

• 'Succumb to' = yield to, give in to — ഒരു pressure/temptation-ന് വഴങ്ങുക, overcome ആകുക.
• 'Succumb' + 'to' = fixed collocation. 'To' ഇല്ലാതെ 'succumb' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല (preposition fixed).
• Sentence meaning: 'Adolescents easily peer pressure-ന് വഴങ്ങുന്നു.'
• 'Succumb to' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന contexts: succumb to pressure, succumb to temptation, succumb to illness, succumb to fear.
• Etymology: Latin 'succumbere' = to lie down under → surrender/yield — 'to' ആണ് the direction of yielding.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: succumb TO = always 'TO' — you yield TO something
• Fixed verb + preposition collocations (PSC must-know):
  - succumb TO (pressure/temptation/disease)
  - yield TO (pressure/demands)
  - submit TO (authority/rules)
  - conform TO (standards/rules)
  - adhere TO (principles/rules)
  - resort TO (methods/means)
  - object TO (a proposal/idea)
  - agree TO (a plan/proposal)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Verb + preposition collocations English medium PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Many people succumb to peer pressure during their teenage years.
  2. He finally succumbed to the temptation of eating sweets.
  3. The patient succumbed to the disease despite treatment.
  4. Don't succumb to negative influences.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) succumb with
• 'Succumb with' — not a standard collocation. 'With' = accompaniment/instrument — yielding 'with' pressure makes no sense.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Deal with pressure', 'cope with pressure' pattern ഓർത്ത് 'succumb with' ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) succumb by
• 'Succumb by' — not standard. 'By' = agent/means — 'yield by pressure' grammatically awkward.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Passive voice-ൽ 'by' agent ആണ് → 'succumbed by pressure' passive feel — but 'succumb' active sentence ആണ് ഇവിടെ.

❌ (D) succumb for
• 'Succumb for' — not a standard collocation. 'For' = purpose/benefit — 'yield for pressure' makes no logical sense.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Fall for' (be deceived) pattern → 'succumb for' ആകും എന്ന് students confuse ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'You FALL TO the ground when you SUCCUMB TO pressure!' — When you succumb, you fall DOWN TO something. TO shows the direction of falling/yielding. Succumb TO = fall TO!
• 'TO' collocation group — yield/surrender verbs: succumb TO, yield TO, surrender TO, submit TO, give in TO — all use 'TO'!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Succumb ___' question-ൽ automatically 'to' choose ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് fixed collocation ആണ്, exception ഇല്ല. Preposition collocation questions-ൽ FIXED combinations memorise ചെയ്‌ത് വയ്ക്കുക.

questionText: Which of the following sentence is correct?
optionA: The jury is divided in their opinion regarding the film selected for the award.
optionB: The jury was divided in their opinion regarding the film selected for the award.
optionC: The jury were divided in their opinion regarding the film selected for the award.
optionD: The jury are divided in their opinion regarding the film selected for the award.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The jury were divided in their opinion regarding the film selected for the award.

• 'Jury' = collective noun. Collective nouns രണ്ട് ways-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കാം:
  - As a UNIT (all thinking/acting together) → singular verb (is/was)
  - As INDIVIDUALS (each member separately) → plural verb (are/were)
• 'Divided in THEIR opinion' — 'their' = plural pronoun. Each jury member has a DIFFERENT opinion = acting as individuals.
• Individual action → plural verb: 'were' ✓
• Consistency rule: 'their' (plural pronoun) + 'were' (plural verb) = grammatically consistent ✓
• 'The jury were divided' (British English) = each member voted differently — perfectly correct!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Collective noun + 'their/they' → plural verb | Collective noun + 'its/it' → singular verb
• Collective noun agreement rules:
  - Acting as ONE unit → singular: 'The team IS playing well.' / 'The jury HAS reached a verdict.'
  - Acting as INDIVIDUALS → plural: 'The team ARE arguing among themselves.' / 'The jury WERE divided.'
• Pronoun consistency test:
  - If sentence uses 'their/they' → verb must be plural (were/are)
  - If sentence uses 'its/it' → verb must be singular (was/is)
• Common collective nouns: jury, team, committee, staff, family, class, crew, government, public
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Collective noun verb agreement PSC-ൽ regular tricky question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The committee was unanimous in its decision. (unit → singular + its)
  2. The committee were divided in their opinions. (individuals → plural + their)
  3. The team is performing well. (unit)
  4. The team were arguing about their positions. (individuals)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The jury is divided in their opinion
• 'Is' (singular) + 'their' (plural) = inconsistency! Singular verb + plural pronoun cannot coexist.
• Either: 'The jury IS divided in ITS opinion' (unit) OR 'The jury WERE divided in THEIR opinion' (individuals).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Jury' singular noun → 'is' → correct feel. But 'their' pronoun contradiction miss ആകുന്നു!

❌ (B) The jury was divided in their opinion
• 'Was' (singular past) + 'their' (plural) = same inconsistency as (A).
• Past tense version of the same error.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was' sounds formal and past tense → students choose it. But pronoun 'their' mismatch persist.

❌ (D) The jury are divided in their opinion
• 'Are' (plural present) + 'their' (plural) — pronoun consistent! But tense issue: sentence describes a specific past situation (a film was already selected for award) → past tense 'were' more appropriate than present 'are'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Are' + 'their' both plural → consistent → correct feel. But 'were' is more appropriate for this completed-action context.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'THEIR needs WERE, ITS needs WAS!' — If you see 'their' with a collective noun → use 'were/are'. If you see 'its' → use 'was/is'. Pronoun decides the verb!
• Quick consistency check: Circle the pronoun in the sentence (their/its/they/it) → match verb accordingly.
• PSC exam strategy: Collective noun questions-ൽ pronoun ('their' or 'its') first identify ചെയ്യുക → 'their' = plural verb (were/are), 'its' = singular verb (was/is). This 2-second check = guaranteed correct answer!

questionText: The Director as well as the Staff ————— eagerly waiting for the new project.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) was

• 'As well as' = together with — ഇത് coordinating conjunction (and) അല്ല. ഇത് ഒരു parenthetical addition ആണ്.
• Rule: 'As well as' ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ verb, FIRST subject-നെ (primary subject) അനുസരിക്കണം.
• First subject: 'The Director' = singular → 'was' ✓
• 'As well as the Staff' = additional information (bracket ആയി treat ചെയ്‌ത് remove ചെയ്‌ത് നോക്കാം: 'The Director ___ eagerly waiting' → singular → 'was')
• Compare: 'The Director AND the Staff were waiting' (and = both → plural). But 'as well as' ≠ 'and'!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയமം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'As well as' = ignore second part → verb agrees with FIRST subject only
• Expressions that follow FIRST subject rule (not 'and'):
  - as well as: The teacher, as well as students, WAS present.
  - along with: The captain, along with his team, WAS ready.
  - together with: She, together with her friends, WAS invited.
  - in addition to: The manager, in addition to staff, WAS informed.
  - with: The boy, with his parents, WAS travelling.
  - besides: Ram, besides his brothers, WAS selected.
  - accompanied by: The Prime Minister, accompanied by officials, WAS there.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'As well as / along with' subject-verb agreement PSC-ൽ guaranteed!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The Principal as well as the teachers was present. (Principal = singular → was)
  2. The students as well as the teacher were ready. (students = plural → were)
  3. John, along with his friends, was invited. (John = singular → was)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) were
• 'Were' = plural. 'Staff' plural ആണ് → 'were' choose ചെയ്യുന്നു — but first subject 'Director' singular ആണ്.
• 'As well as' second subject-നെ primary ആക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Director + Staff' = two people → plural → 'were' feel. This is the most common mistake!

❌ (C) have been
• 'Have been' = Present Perfect plural. 'Director' singular + present perfect plural = wrong.
• 'Has been' ആകണം singular-ന് — but even that wrong here ('was' more natural with 'waiting').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Staff' plural → 'have been' plural feel → students choose it.

❌ (D) are
• 'Are' = plural present. Same error as (A) — treating compound subject as plural.
• Plus 'waiting' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'are waiting' = Present Continuous — contextually possible but subject agreement wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The Director and Staff are' natural phrasing → students confuse 'as well as' with 'and'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AS WELL AS = carry bag — the FIRST person carries it!' — 'The Director [carries] AS WELL AS the Staff [rides along]' — Director is the main person, Staff just comes along. Main person = verb controller. Director (singular) → WAS!
• Bracket trick: Put 'as well as....' in brackets and ignore: 'The Director (as well as the Staff) ___ waiting' → 'The Director ___ waiting' → singular → 'was'. Works every time!
• PSC exam strategy: 'As well as / along with / together with' questions-ൽ second part mentally bracket ചെയ്‌ത് ignore ചെയ്യുക. First subject alone verb decide ചെയ്യുന്നു — guaranteed mark!

questionText: What is the meaning of Sine qua non?
optionA: a thing that is absolutely essential

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) a thing that is absolutely essential

• 'Sine qua non' (SIN-ay kwah NON) = Latin phrase literally meaning 'without which not' — ഇല്ലാതിരിക്കാൻ കഴിയാത്തത്, അനിവാര്യമായ കാര്യം.
• 'Sine' = without | 'qua' = which | 'non' = not → 'without which, not possible' = absolutely essential condition.
• Usage: 'Discipline is the sine qua non of success' = success-ന് discipline അനിവാര്യം.
• Context: Legal, academic, formal writing-ൽ ഒരു essential condition/prerequisite describe ചെയ്യാൻ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Synonyms: indispensable, prerequisite, essential condition, necessary requirement.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Sine qua non = SINE (without) + cannot exist = ABSOLUTELY ESSENTIAL
• Latin phrases PSC must-know list:
  - Sine qua non = absolutely essential condition
  - Ad hoc = for this specific purpose only
  - Status quo = the existing state of affairs
  - Bona fide = genuine, in good faith
  - Per se = by itself, in itself
  - Vice versa = with the main terms reversed
  - Alma mater = school/college one attended
  - De facto = in fact, in reality
  - Prima facie = at first sight/appearance
  - Post mortem = examination after death
  - Viva voce = oral examination
  - Curriculum vitae (CV) = life course/resume
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Latin/foreign phrases English medium PSC-ൽ VERY frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Hard work is the sine qua non of success.
  2. Trust is the sine qua non of any good relationship.
  3. Good health is the sine qua non of a happy life.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) for the meantime
• 'For the meantime' / 'in the meantime' = temporarily, for now — ഇത് 'ad interim' or 'pro tempore' (Latin)-ന്റെ meaning ആണ്.
• Sine qua non-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Non' = no/not → 'not for now' = 'meantime' feel — students confuse ആകും.

❌ (C) whether right or wrong
• 'Whether right or wrong' = 'per fas et nefas' (Latin) or 'rightly or wrongly' (English).
• Sine qua non-ന്റെ meaning ഇതല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Sine qua non' sounds like a moral judgment phrase → 'right or wrong' feel ആകാം.

❌ (D) to be done after sometime
• 'To be done after sometime' = postponed action — 'post' related Latin terms (postpone, post-date) ആണ് ഈ meaning.
• Sine qua non future/delayed action-നെ indicate ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Non' = not now = later feel → 'after sometime' confuse ആകാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SINE QUA NON = SINe (sin) QUA (quality) NON (none) — Without this quality, NONE of it works!' — Sine qua non = the one quality/thing without which NOTHING works = ESSENTIAL!
• Latin phrase family for PSC:
  - ESSENTIAL: sine qua non
  - TEMPORARY: ad interim, pro tempore
  - GENUINE: bona fide
  - AS IS: status quo
  - IN REALITY: de facto
• PSC exam strategy: Latin phrase questions-ൽ literal translation try ചെയ്യുക. 'Sine' = without, 'non' = not → 'without which, not possible' → absolutely essential. ഈ literal meaning approach many Latin phrases-ന് work ചെയ്യും!

questionText: I saw ten ————— flower vases in the shop :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Small antique Chinese porcelain

• Adjective order-ൽ OSASCOMP rule follow ചെയ്യണം: Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose.
• ഈ sentence-ൽ ഉള്ള adjectives: Small (Size) + Antique (Age) + Chinese (Origin) + Porcelain (Material).
• OSASCOMP order പ്രകാരം: Size (Small) → Age (Antique) → Origin (Chinese) → Material (Porcelain) = 'Small antique Chinese porcelain' ✓
• 'Ten' = number ആണ്, adjective order-ൽ number ഏറ്റവും ആദ്യം വരും: Ten + Small antique Chinese porcelain flower vases.
• Meaning: 'ഞാൻ shop-ൽ പത്ത് ചെറിയ, പഴക്കമുള്ള, Chinese, porcelain flower vases കണ്ടു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: OSASCOMP = Opinion → Size → Age → Shape → Colour → Origin → Material → Purpose
• Adjective order chart:
  1. Determiner/Number: a, the, ten, some
  2. Opinion/Quality: beautiful, lovely, ugly
  3. Size: small, large, tiny
  4. Age: old, antique, new
  5. Shape: round, square, flat
  6. Colour: red, blue, golden
  7. Origin: Chinese, Indian, French
  8. Material: porcelain, wooden, silk
  9. Purpose/Qualifier: flower (vases), sleeping (bag)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Adjective order questions English medium PSC-ൽ guaranteed question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A beautiful large old round black Chinese silk dress.
     (Opinion→Size→Age→Shape→Colour→Origin→Material)
  2. Three small antique Chinese porcelain vases.
     (Number→Size→Age→Origin→Material)
  3. A lovely little old Italian car.
     (Opinion→Size→Age→Origin)
  4. A big square blue French painting.
     (Size→Shape→Colour→Origin)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Chinese small antique porcelain
• Origin (Chinese) ആദ്യം വരുന്നത് തെറ്റ്. OSASCOMP-ൽ Origin 7th position ആണ്, Size (small) 3rd position ആണ്.
• 'Chinese' = Origin → Size-ന് ശേഷം വരണം, അതിന് മുൻപ് ഒരിക്കലും ഇടാൻ പാടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Chinese porcelain' common phrase ആണ് → students ആദ്യം Chinese ഇടാൻ tempt ആകും.

❌ (C) Small porcelain Chinese antique
• 'Porcelain' (Material) 'Chinese' (Origin)-ന് മുൻപ് വരുന്നത് തെറ്റ്. Origin → Material order ആണ് ശരി, Material → Origin അല്ല.
• 'Antique' (Age) ഏറ്റവും ഒടുക്കം വരുന്നത് തെറ്റ് — Age, Material-ന് മുൻപ് വരണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Random arrangement confuse ആക്കും — OSASCOMP ഓർത്തില്ലെങ്കിൽ ഏത് option-ഉം correct feel ആകും.

❌ (D) Antique Chinese small porcelain
• 'Antique' (Age) ആദ്യം വരുന്നത് തെറ്റ്. Age = 4th position, Size (small) = 3rd position — Size ആദ്യം വരണം.
• 'Small' (Size) 3rd position-ൽ ഉള്ളത് 'Chinese' (Origin, 7th) ശേഷം വരുന്നത് തെറ്റ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Antique Chinese' collocation naturally sound ആകും → ആദ്യം ഇടാൻ students select ചെയ്യും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OS-AS-COMP' — O(pinion) S(ize) A(ge) S(hape) C(olour) O(rigin) M(aterial) P(urpose). ഒരു beautiful (O) large (S) old (A) round (S) red (C) Chinese (O) silk (M) dress (P) ഇങ്ങനെ memorize ചെയ്യുക!
• Quick test: Size (small/large/tiny) ALWAYS comes before Age (old/new/antique) — Size before Age is S before A in OSASCOMP. S-A order ഓർക്കുക!
• PSC exam strategy: Options-ൽ Size, Age, Origin, Material ഉള്ള adjectives കണ്ടാൽ: Size → Age → Origin → Material order ആണ് ശരി. ഈ 4-word pattern ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ adjective order questions-ൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: Looks like it ————— rain :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) might

• 'Might' = Modal verb expressing possibility — 'ആകാം / ആകുമായിരിക്കും' എന്ന് ഉദ്ദേശം.
• 'Looks like it might rain' = 'മഴ പെയ്‌തേക്കാം' — ഒരു possibility indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'Looks like' = visual evidence based prediction → 'might' (weak possibility) perfectly fits.
• 'Might rain' = may rain-ന്റെ more tentative version — sky looks cloudy, ഒരുപക്ഷേ മഴ വരും.
• Modal verb 'might' + V1 (bare infinitive) — 'might rain' ✓ (might + to rain ✗).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Modal + V1 (bare infinitive, 'to' ഇല്ലാതെ) — might rain, can swim, must go
• Modal verbs expressing possibility (possibility modals):
  - may = 50% possibility: 'It may rain.'
  - might = less than 50% possibility: 'It might rain.' (weak possibility)
  - could = theoretical possibility: 'It could rain.'
  - must = near certainty (deduction): 'It must be raining.'
• 'Looks like' + might = visual evidence + weak prediction
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Modal verb fill-in-the-blank questions PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. It might rain this evening. (maybe/maybe not)
  2. She might come to the party. (uncertain)
  3. You might be right about that. (possibility)
  4. It looks like it might snow tonight. (visual prediction)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) be
• 'Looks like it be rain' — 'be' bare form ഇവിടെ grammatically impossible. 'Be' auxiliary ആണ്, modal verb അല്ല.
• 'It be rain' = no modal, no proper verb structure — completely ungrammatical.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'will be raining' pattern ഓർത്ത് 'be' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം — but 'will' ഇല്ലാതെ 'be' alone ഒരിക്കലും work ആകില്ല.

❌ (B) naught
• 'Naught' = nothing (zero) — ഇത് ഒരു noun/pronoun ആണ്, modal verb അല്ല.
• 'Looks like it naught rain' = completely meaningless sentence.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'naught' sounds like 'ought' → students confuse ആകും. 'Ought to' ഉണ്ടല്ലോ options-ൽ, 'naught' ≠ 'ought'.

❌ (C) ought to
• 'Ought to' = moral obligation/duty — 'ചെയ്യണം' എന്ന് obligation ആണ് meaning.
• 'Looks like it ought to rain' = grammatically possible but wrong meaning — sky-ന് rain ആകണം എന്ന obligation ഇല്ല! Rain ആകും/ആകാം (possibility) ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ought to' sounds formal and correct → students choose it. But meaning = obligation, not possibility.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'MIGHT = Maybe... I'm not sure!' — 'might' speak ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ uncertain face imagine ചെയ്യുക: 'Might rain... maybe... not sure.' Possibility + uncertainty = might!
• Modal certainty ladder (low to high): might → may → could → should → must
• PSC exam strategy: 'Looks like / seems like / probably' ഉള്ള sentences-ൽ possibility modal (might/may/could) choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Ought to' obligation questions-ൽ (what you should do) ഉപയോഗിക്കും — visual prediction-ൽ 'might' ആണ് ശരി!

questionText: Is Kochi ————— than Trivandrum?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) more busy

• 'Than' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ comparative degree വേണം (superlative അല്ല).
• 'Busy' 2-syllable adjective ആണ് → comparative: 'busier' OR 'more busy' (both acceptable).
• Options-ൽ ഉള്ള ഏക correct comparative form: 'more busy' (C) ✓
• 'More busier' (A) = double comparative (WRONG: more + -er ഒരിക്കലും ഒരുമിക്കില്ല).
• 'Most busy'/'most busier' (D, B) = superlative forms → 'than'-ന് ഒപ്പം ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'than' കണ്ടാൽ → Comparative degree | 'the most / -est' കണ്ടാൽ → Superlative
• Double comparative = ALWAYS WRONG: more + -er ഒരിക്കലും കൂടി ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല
  - more bigger ✗ (bigger / more big = ✓)
  - more taller ✗ (taller / more tall = ✓)
  - more busier ✗ (busier / more busy = ✓)
• Comparative formation rules:
  - 1-syllable: add -er (tall → taller, fast → faster)
  - 2-syllable ending in -y: change y→i, add -er (busy → busier) OR more + adj
  - 2+ syllable: more + adjective (beautiful → more beautiful)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Double comparative trap PSC-ൽ classic question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is more intelligent than her brother. ✓
  2. This road is wider than that one. ✓
  3. He is more busier (✗) / busier (✓) than before.
  4. Kochi is more busy than Trivandrum. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) more busier
• Classic double comparative error! 'More' + '-er' ഒരു adjective-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും ഒരുമിക്കില്ല.
• Either 'more busy' OR 'busier' — രണ്ടും ഒരേ adjective-ൽ ഒരുമിപ്പിക്കാൻ പാടില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഇത് ഏറ്റവും common wrong answer — students emphasis ആകണം എന്ന് കരുതി 'more busier' write ചെയ്യും.

❌ (B) most busier
• Double error! 'Most' (superlative marker) + 'busier' (comparative form) — ഒരേ adjective-ൽ രണ്ട് comparison markers ഒരുമിച്ച് ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Plus 'than' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ superlative 'most' ഇവിടെ wrong ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Confusing superlative 'most' with comparative context.

❌ (D) most busy
• 'Most' = superlative marker. Superlative + 'than' ഒരിക്കലും ഒരുമിക്കില്ല.
• 'Than' comparison/contrast indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു → comparative 'more busy' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Most busy' natural phrasing feel ആകും — but 'than' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ superlative wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'THAN = TWO things compared = comparative (not superlative)!' — 'Than' കണ്ടാൽ ഉടൻ: comparative degree choose ചെയ്യുക. More busy OR busier — not most!
• Double comparison red flag: Options-ൽ 'more + -er' (more busier, more taller, more faster) ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ ആ option ALWAYS WRONG — eliminate immediately without thinking!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Than' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ: (1) superlative options eliminate (most), (2) double comparative eliminate (more + -er), (3) remaining correct comparative choose. Usually takes 5 seconds!

questionText: He enjoys swimming and —————.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) running

• 'Enjoys' verb = gerund (V+ing) form ആണ് ആവശ്യം — 'enjoy + V-ing' fixed pattern.
• 'Swimming' = gerund (V+ing) ആണ്. Parallel structure rule: 'swimming AND running' — ഒരേ form (V+ing) ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'Enjoys swimming and running' = ✓ (both gerunds, parallel)
• Meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹം നീന്തലും ഓടലും enjoy ചെയ്യുന്നു.'
• Parallel structure: joined items MUST be in the same grammatical form.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: enjoy / mind / avoid / keep + V-ing (gerund) — NEVER 'enjoy to V'
• Verbs that ALWAYS take gerund (V-ing):
  enjoy, mind, avoid, keep, consider, suggest, finish, practice, miss, deny, quit, imagine, risk, admit
• Parallel structure rule: 'A and B' — A and B must be in same form:
  - swimming and running ✓ (both gerunds)
  - swimming and to run ✗ (gerund + infinitive = mismatch)
  - to swim and to run ✓ (both infinitives)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'enjoy' gerund rule + parallel structure PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She enjoys reading and writing. ✓
  2. He avoids smoking and drinking. ✓
  3. They finished eating and sleeping. ✓
  4. I enjoy to swim (✗) / swimming (✓).
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to run
• 'Enjoy + to run' = wrong! 'Enjoy' never takes infinitive ('to + V').
• Plus parallel structure broken: 'swimming' (gerund) + 'to run' (infinitive) = mismatch.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'to run' natural infinitive form → students automatically choose it, forgetting 'enjoy' takes gerund.

❌ (C) to ran
• 'Ran' = Simple Past tense. 'To ran' = infinitive + past = grammatically impossible combination.
• No such form exists in English grammar.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Ran' is familiar past tense → combined with 'to' sounds official to some students.

❌ (D) to running
• 'To running' = neither proper infinitive nor gerund. 'To' + V-ing is not a standard English form.
• Gerund (running) ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് stand ചെയ്യും — 'to' ആവശ്യമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'To + running' feels like it combines both forms → students try to 'be safe' by adding 'to'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ENJOY = ENJOYing something — always -ING!' — You ENJOY the activity itself (the -ing), not the 'to do' part. Enjoy swimming, enjoy reading, enjoy running — always -ing family!
• GEMS verbs = Gerund-taking verbs: G(o) E(njoy) M(ind) S(uggest) — if you GEMS, you take -ing!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Enjoy' കണ്ടാൽ → automatic gerund (V-ing) — no thinking needed! Plus check parallel structure: first verb form = second verb form. Both gerunds or both infinitives — never mix!

questionText: Had I known, I ————— it differently :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) would have done

• 'Had I known' = Third Conditional (inverted form). Normal form: 'If I had known'.
• Inversion: 'If' remove ചെയ്‌ത് 'Had' ആദ്യം ഇടുന്നത് formal/literary style ആണ്.
• Third Conditional pattern: If + had + V3 → would have + V3
• 'Had I known' (If I had known) → main clause: 'would have done' ✓
• Meaning: 'എനിക്ക് അറിയാമായിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, ഞാൻ ഇത് വ്യത്യസ്‌തമായി ചെയ്‌തേനേ.' (past unreal — actually did NOT know).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Had + subject + V3 (inverted) = If + subject + had + V3 → result: would have + V3
• Third Conditional (Inverted form) patterns:
  - Normal: If I had studied, I would have passed.
  - Inverted: Had I studied, I would have passed. ✓
  - Normal: If she had come, we would have met.
  - Inverted: Had she come, we would have met. ✓
• Other inverted conditional forms:
  - Were I + adj... → Second Conditional: Were I rich, I would travel.
  - Should you + V... → First Conditional: Should you need help, call me.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Inverted conditional form PSC-ൽ tricky question — always appears!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Had I known earlier, I would have helped. ✓
  2. Had they arrived on time, they would have caught the flight. ✓
  3. Had she called, I would have answered. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) will do
• 'Will do' = Simple Future — but 'Had I known' = past unreal situation. Future tense cannot describe past unreal events.
• Third Conditional main clause = 'would have + V3', never 'will + V1'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will do' sounds decisive/confident → students think it fits. But this is PAST unreal — future tense wrong.

❌ (B) had done
• 'Had done' = Past Perfect — this is the IF clause form, not the main clause form.
• Pattern confusion: 'Had I known' (if clause - Past Perfect) → main clause must be 'would have done' (not Past Perfect again).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had known... had done' — both Past Perfect → feels parallel → students choose it. But rule says: if-clause = Past Perfect, main clause = would have + V3.

❌ (D) did
• 'Did' = Simple Past — this is real past action, not conditional unreal situation.
• Third Conditional = unreal/hypothetical past. 'Did' = actual fact happened.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had I known, I did it differently' — sounds like a story narration. But conditional unreal needs 'would have'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAD at the START = Third Conditional INVERT!' — When a sentence starts with 'Had I / Had she / Had they' (no 'if'), it is inverted Third Conditional → automatic 'would have + V3' in main clause!
• Inversion signal words: Had I... / Were I... / Should you... — no 'If', but conditional meaning!
• PSC exam strategy: Sentence starts with 'Had + subject + V3' → immediately choose 'would have + V3' option. This is a 100% reliable formula. 'Would have done' is always the answer when question starts with 'Had I known / Had she gone / Had they arrived' pattern!

questionText: She is so rich, she stayed in a ————— of rooms in the hotel :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) suite

• 'Suite of rooms' = hotel-ൽ connected ആയ ഒരു set of rooms — ഇത് ഒരു fixed phrase ആണ്.
• Suite (pronounced 'sweet') = ഒരു high-class hotel-ൽ living room, bedroom, bathroom connected ആയ luxury accommodation.
• 'She is so rich' = luxury lifestyle indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു → 'suite of rooms' perfectly fits.
• Meaning: 'അവൾ വളരെ സമ്പന്നയാണ്, hotel-ൽ ഒരു suite of rooms-ൽ ആണ് താമസിച്ചത്.'
• Common collocations: 'bridal suite', 'presidential suite', 'honeymoon suite' — all hotel luxury rooms.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Suite = 'sweet' ആണ് sound, hotel-ൽ rooms-ന്റെ group = suite of rooms
• 'Suite' vs 'Sweet' — homophones (same sound, different meaning):
  - Suite (noun): a set of connected rooms / a set of furniture / a musical composition in parts
  - Sweet (adjective/noun): sugary taste / a candy
• Suite collocations:
  - Suite of rooms (hotel)
  - Suite of furniture (sofa set + chairs)
  - Honeymoon suite / Presidential suite
  - Office suite (connected offices)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Homophone confusion (suite/sweet) PSC-ൽ common trap!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The VIP stayed in the presidential suite.
  2. They booked a honeymoon suite for their stay.
  3. The living room suite came with three sofas.
  4. A 'sweet' taste ≠ A hotel 'suite'.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) floor
• 'A floor of rooms' = not a standard phrase. 'Floor' = building-ൽ ഒരു level ആണ് (ground floor, first floor) — rooms-ന്റെ set അല്ല.
• 'She stayed on the third floor' ✓ — but 'a floor of rooms' = unnatural collocation.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Hotel-ൽ floors ഉണ്ട് → 'floor of rooms' possible feel ആകും. But 'suite' = standard hotel terminology.

❌ (C) flat
• 'Flat' = a self-contained apartment (British English) — usually a permanent residence, not hotel accommodation.
• 'She stayed in a flat' = she rented an apartment — this suggests long-term stay, not hotel luxury.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flat' sounds upscale in some contexts → rich person might stay in a 'flat'. But 'suite of rooms' is the hotel-specific term.

❌ (D) apartment
• 'Apartment' = permanent/semi-permanent residential unit — hotels have 'suites', residential buildings have 'apartments'.
• 'A apartment of rooms' = ungrammatical anyway (should be 'an apartment').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Luxury apartments are expensive → rich person + apartment sounds right. But hotel context + 'suite' = correct choice.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'A SWEET hotel SUITE!' — Suite sounds like 'sweet' and luxury hotel rooms are indeed 'sweet'! Sweet = taste, Suite = hotel rooms. Both sound identical but spelled differently!
• Fixed phrase to memorize: 'a suite of rooms' — this is the ONLY correct phrase for a connected set of hotel rooms.
• PSC exam strategy: Hotel + luxury + 'of rooms' → automatically choose 'suite'. If question involves hotel stay and multiple rooms, 'suite' is always the answer. Eliminate floor/flat/apartment immediately in hotel context questions!

questionText: The most suitable meaning of the idiom "Break a leg" is :
optionA: Break a bone in the leg
optionC: Wishing the leg will heal soon

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Wishing good luck

• 'Break a leg!' = theatre/performance world-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന famous idiom — 'ഭാഗ്യം / All the best!' എന്നർഥം.
• Origin: Performers വിശ്വസിച്ചിരുന്നു: stage-ൽ 'Good luck!' പറഞ്ഞാൽ bad luck വരും (superstition). So they said the opposite: 'Break a leg!' = bad thing wish = actually means good luck!
• Theatre slang ആണ്, ഇപ്പോൾ general usage-ൽ കൂടി ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു — exam, interview, performance — ഏത് context-ലും.
• Example: 'Break a leg at your interview tomorrow!' = 'Interview-ൽ ഭാഗ്യം!'
• Idiom = literal meaning അല്ല, figurative meaning ആണ് — leg break ആകണം എന്ന് ഉദ്ദേശമില്ല!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Idiom = literal meaning ≠ actual meaning. 'Break a leg' = literal: leg break ചെയ്യുക ≠ actual: Good luck!
• Common 'good luck' idioms in English:
  - Break a leg = Best of luck (especially performance/exam)
  - Knock on wood = Hope for good luck (superstition)
  - Keep your fingers crossed = Hope it goes well
  - Best of luck = direct well-wishing
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Break a leg' PSC-ൽ classic idiom question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Break a leg at your audition!' (stage performance)
  2. 'Break a leg in your exams!' (PSC exam context)
  3. 'You've been practising so hard — break a leg tonight!'
  4. Origin: Theatre superstition — wishing the opposite of good luck actually brings good luck.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Break a bone in the leg
• ഇത് 'break a leg'-ന്റെ literal (word-for-word) meaning ആണ്. Idioms never mean their literal meaning.
• 'Break a leg' = good luck wish ആണ്, actual leg break ആകണം എന്ന് ആരും ഉദ്ദേശിക്കില്ല!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഏറ്റവും obvious trap — 'break' + 'leg' = bone break ആകും എന്ന് students confuse ആകും. Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning ALWAYS wrong.

❌ (C) Wishing the leg will heal soon
• ഇത്  injury-recover wish ആണ് — 'Get well soon' ആണ് recovery wish. 'Break a leg' recovery wish അല്ല.
• Leg injury-ക്ക് ശേഷം heal ആകണം എന്ന context ഇവിടെ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Break a leg' → broken leg → healing → 'heal soon' feel ആകും. But idiom = good luck, not recovery wish.

❌ (D) Break a tree branch
• 'Branch' ഒരിക്കലും 'leg' idiom-ൽ ഇല്ല — ഈ option completely unrelated ആണ്.
• Distractor option — students confused ആകാൻ ഉൾപ്പെടുത്തിയത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Break a branch/leg' — both 'breaking something' feel → some students select this.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'In theatre, saying GOOD LUCK is BAD LUCK — so actors say BREAK A LEG instead!' — ഈ theatre superstition story ഒരിക്കൽ കേട്ടാൽ ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല. Break a leg = the OPPOSITE of bad luck = GOOD LUCK!
• Idiom golden rule: Always choose the figurative (non-literal) meaning. If literal body injury meaning appears as option → eliminate it immediately!
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning eliminate ചെയ്യുക. 'Break a leg' = good luck — this is one of the most PSC-tested idioms. Story-based memory (theatre superstition) = never forget!

questionText: What is a one-word that can be substituted for "someone who hates marriage"?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Misogamist

• 'Misogamist' = someone who hates or has a strong aversion to marriage.
• Etymology: Greek 'misos' (hatred) + 'gamos' (marriage) = marriage-ഇഷ്ടമില്ലാത്ത ആൾ.
• 'Misogamy' = the hatred of marriage (noun). 'Misogamist' = the person.
• ഓർക്കാൻ: 'gamos' = marriage → polygamy (many marriages), monogamy (one marriage), misogamy (hatred of marriage).
• Meaning: 'She is a confirmed misogamist — she never wants to get married.' = 'അവൾക്ക് വിവാഹം ഒരിക്കലും വേണ്ട.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Miso-' = hatred prefix. Root word tells you WHAT is hated.
• 'Miso-' family (Greek: hatred):
  - Misogamist = hates marriage (gamos = marriage)
  - Misanthropist = hates mankind (anthropos = human)
  - Misogynist = hates women (gyne = woman)
  - Misologist = hates reason/argument (logos = reason)
  - Misophonia = hates specific sounds (phone = sound)
• 'Gamos' (marriage) family:
  - Monogamy = marriage to one person
  - Polygamy = marriage to many
  - Bigamy = marriage to two (illegal)
  - Misogamy = hatred of marriage
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Miso-' prefix one word substitutes PSC-ൽ regular question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A misogamist refuses to consider marriage.
  2. A misanthrope avoids human company.
  3. A misogynist discriminates against women.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Misanthropist
• Misanthropist = hates MANKIND/HUMANS (anthropos = human being) — marriage അല്ല.
• 'A misanthropist avoids all human contact' — people-ഇഷ്ടമില്ലാത്ത ആൾ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Miso-' prefix same ആണ് → all options sound similar → students confuse ആകും. Root word 'anthropos' (human) ≠ 'gamos' (marriage).

❌ (B) Misogynist
• Misogynist = hates WOMEN specifically (gyne = woman) — marriage generally അല്ല.
• 'A misogynist treats women unfairly' — women-ഇഷ്ടമില്ലാത്ത ആൾ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Misogynist is very commonly known → students choose it as default. But it is women-hatred, not marriage-hatred.

❌ (C) Misologist
• Misologist = hates REASON/ARGUMENT/LOGIC (logos = reason/word) — marriage അല്ല.
• 'A misologist refuses to engage in rational debate' — reasoning-ഇഷ്ടമില്ലാത്ത ആൾ.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Least known option → students avoid it or confuse with 'misogynist'. Logos = reason, not marriage.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GAMOS = MARRIAGE — G for Getting Married!' — GAMOs sounds like 'game' + 'os' → Marriage is a LIFE GAME for two people. MisoGAMist = hates the marriage game!
• 'Miso-' family quick chart:
  - Miso + GAMOS (marriage) = Misogamist
  - Miso + ANTHROPOS (human) = Misanthropist
  - Miso + GYNE (woman) = Misogynist
  - Miso + LOGOS (reason) = Misologist
• PSC exam strategy: 'Miso-' options-ൽ ROOT WORD identify ചെയ്യുക. 'Marriage hatred' question → 'gamos' root ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക. GAMOS = marriage (same root as monogamy, polygamy) — ഇത് ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: She is busy this weekend, her ————— are coming for a visit :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Sisters-in-law

• 'Are coming' = plural verb ആണ് → plural noun ആവശ്യം.
• 'Sister-in-law' = compound noun. Plural ഉണ്ടാക്കാൻ: MAIN NOUN plural ആക്കണം, 'in' / 'law' അല്ല.
• Main noun = 'Sister' → Sisters. So: Sister-in-law → Sisters-in-law ✓
• 'Sister-in-laws' (C) = wrong plural (main noun 'sister' അല്ല 'law' plural ആക്കിയത് — WRONG).
• Meaning: 'അവൾ ഈ weekend busy ആണ്, അവളുടെ sisters-in-law (ഭർത്താവിന്റെ/ഭർത്താവ്‌/ഭാര്യയുടെ sisters) visit-ന് വരുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Compound noun plural = MAIN NOUN gets -s (not the last word!)
• Compound noun plural rules:
  - Sister-in-law → Sisters-in-law (sister = main noun)
  - Brother-in-law → Brothers-in-law
  - Mother-in-law → Mothers-in-law
  - Father-in-law → Fathers-in-law
  - Son-in-law → Sons-in-law
  - Daughter-in-law → Daughters-in-law
  - Passer-by → Passers-by (passer = main)
  - Commander-in-chief → Commanders-in-chief
  - Man-of-war → Men-of-war (man → men, irregular)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Compound noun plural (sister-in-law / passer-by) PSC guaranteed question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. My sisters-in-law are visiting this week.
  2. Two passers-by helped the accident victim.
  3. All the commanders-in-chief attended the meeting.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Sister-in-law
• Singular form ആണ്. 'Are coming' = plural verb → singular noun use ചെയ്യാൻ പ്പറ്റില്ല.
• 'Her sister-in-law is coming' (singular) ✓ — but sentence-ൽ 'are' plural ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Sister-in-law' familiar form → students select it without checking the verb 'are'.

❌ (C) Sister-in-laws
• Most common error! 'Law'-ന് '-s' ചേർക്കുന്നത് = completely wrong.
• Main noun 'sister' ആണ് plural ആകേണ്ടത്, last word 'law' അല്ല.
• 'Sister-in-laws' = wrong in standard English — the main noun rules the plural.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഏറ്റവും common student mistake! 'Law-s' end-ൽ വരുന്നതിനാൽ naturally add ചെയ്യും — but WRONG.

❌ (D) Cousin sister
• 'Cousin sister' = informal Indian English expression — not standard British/American English.
• Standard English: Just 'cousin' (no need for 'sister' or 'brother' — cousin is gender-neutral).
• Context-ൽ 'in-law' relationship describe ചെയ്യുന്നു — cousin ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശമല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Common in Indian spoken English → students feel it is correct. But PSC tests standard English.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'In compound nouns, the SISTER is the STAR — she gets the S!' — Sisters-in-law: Sister is the main person (star of the family!), so Sister gets the -s. Law doesn't change, in doesn't change — only SISTER changes to SISTERS!
• Quick family list: Sisters-in-law / Brothers-in-law / Mothers-in-law / Fathers-in-law / Sons-in-law / Daughters-in-law — ALL pluralize the FIRST word.
• PSC exam strategy: Compound noun plural question → identify MAIN NOUN (usually first word) → add -s to THAT word only. 'Sister-in-laws' option ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ immediately eliminate. 'Sisters-in-law' = always correct!

questionText: The Superstar's visit was a ————— moment for the institution :

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Historic

• 'Historic' = famous, important, or significant in history — 'ചരിത്രപ്രസിദ്ധമായ, memorable' moment.
• 'The Superstar's visit was a historic moment' = ആ visit ഒരു important, memorable event ആയിരുന്നു — institution-ന്റെ history-ൽ remembered ആകും.
• 'Historic' vs 'Historical' confusion: Historic = something famous/important IN history. Historical = related to history (as a subject/period).
• Examples: 'historic victory', 'historic speech', 'historic meeting' — all = memorable, significant events.
• Meaning: 'ആ Superstar-ന്റെ visit institution-ന് ഒരു ചരിത്രനിമിഷമായിരുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Historic = FAMOUS/IMPORTANT event | Historical = RELATED TO history (subject/period)
• Historic vs Historical:
  - Historic: a historic moment / a historic win / a historic speech / a historic building (famous/important)
  - Historical: a historical document / historical evidence / a historical novel / historical period (about the past/history as subject)
• Other confusing pairs in this word family:
  - Histrionic = overly dramatic, theatrical behavior (NOT related to history!)
  - History = noun (subject/events of the past)
• Memory test:
  - 'The 1969 moon landing was a _____ moment.' → historic (famous, important event)
  - 'We studied _____ events of the 18th century.' → historical (history as a subject)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Historic/Historical confusion PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. India's independence was a historic moment. (famous event)
  2. The museum has historical artifacts. (related to history as subject)
  3. This is a historic agreement between the two nations. (important event)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Historical
• 'Historical' = related to history as a subject or the study of past events.
