• 'A historical moment' is technically used sometimes, but 'historic moment' is the standard fixed phrase for memorable occasions.
• 'Historical' more accurately describes documents, evidence, research, novels about the past.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Most common confusion! 'Historical' sounds more formal → students choose it. But 'historic moment' is the correct fixed phrase.

❌ (B) History
• 'History' = noun (the subject or the record of past events). Cannot be used as adjective before 'moment'.
• 'A history moment' = grammatically wrong — noun modifying noun without hyphen or proper compound form.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'History' is the most familiar word → students choose what they know. But adjective needed here, not noun.

❌ (D) Histrionic
• 'Histrionic' = overly dramatic, exaggerated emotional behavior — theatre/drama related, NOT history related.
• 'A histrionic moment' = a moment of over-dramatic behavior — negative connotation.
• This has NOTHING to do with history, despite sounding like 'historic'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Histrionic' sounds like 'historic' → students who don't know the word might choose it. But meaning is completely different.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HISTORIC = I-C (I Can remember it!) — a famous moment you CAN remember forever. HISTORICAL = related to old history you READ about in textbooks.' — Historic things happen TO you (you feel it). Historical things you READ about in books.
• Quick test: Can you say 'I was there for this famous moment'? → HISTORIC. Is it just 'from the past/in history books'? → HISTORICAL.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Moment / victory / speech / occasion / achievement' + blank → always 'historic'. 'Documents / research / evidence / novel / period' + blank → always 'historical'. And 'histrionic' = drama/over-acting — eliminate if question is about importance, not drama!

questionText:
Fill in the blank suitably using the appropriate clause :
She ————— the examination, if she had studied well.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) would have passed

• If clause: 'if she had studied well' = Past Perfect (had + V3) → ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്.
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഉണ്ടാകാതെ പോയ unreal situation describe ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Pattern: If + had + V3 → would have + V3
• 'If she had studied well' → main clause: 'would have passed' ✓
• Meaning: 'അവൾ നന്നായി പഠിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, exam pass ആയേനേ.' — but actually she did NOT study well, so she did NOT pass.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + had + V3 → would have + V3 (Third Conditional)
• Three Conditional types:
  1. First (Real future): If + present simple → will + V1
     'If she studies, she will pass.'
  2. Second (Imaginary present): If + past simple → would + V1
     'If she studied, she would pass.'
  3. Third (Past unreal): If + had + V3 → would have + V3
     'If she had studied, she would have passed.' ✓
• Key signal: 'had studied' in if-clause = Third Conditional → main clause must have 'would have + V3'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional PSC-ൽ guaranteed question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I had left early, I would have caught the train.
  2. If they had tried harder, they would have won.
  3. If he had taken medicine, he would have recovered.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) had passed
• 'Had passed' = Past Perfect — if-clause-ന്റെ form ആണ് ഇത്, main clause-ന്റേത് അല്ല.
• If clause: had studied (Past Perfect) → main clause: would have passed (NOT Past Perfect again).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had studied... had passed' both Past Perfect → parallel feel ആകും. But rule says main clause = 'would have + V3', not Past Perfect.

❌ (C) would pass
• 'Would pass' = Second Conditional main clause form (If + past simple → would + V1).
• But if-clause ഇവിടെ 'had studied' (Past Perfect) ആണ് — Second Conditional 'studied' (simple past) ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• Third Conditional-ൽ 'would pass' wrong — 'would have passed' വേണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' കണ്ടാൽ correct feel ആകും — but 'would pass' ≠ 'would have passed'. The 'have' is critical!

❌ (D) will pass
• 'Will pass' = First Conditional (future real) — but sentence describes PAST unreal situation.
• 'Had studied' = clear past signal → future 'will' completely wrong here.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will pass' = positive/hopeful → students choose optimistic options. But grammar = past unreal, not future.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAD in if-clause = HAVE in main clause!' — If she HAD studied → she would HAVE passed. 'Had' and 'have' are a pair — they always appear together in Third Conditional!
• Conditional formula chart:
  - If + Present → Will (First)
  - If + Past → Would (Second)
  - If + Had V3 → Would Have V3 (Third)
• PSC exam strategy: Identify the if-clause verb first. 'Had + V3' seen in if-clause → automatically select 'would have + V3' in main clause. This formula never fails — guaranteed mark!

questionText: He has hardly any money —————?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) has he?

• 'Hardly' = negative adverb. 'Hardly any money' = 'almost no money' = negative meaning.
• Negative meaning sentence → Positive question tag (positive tag ഉപയോഗിക്കണം).
• Auxiliary verb in main sentence: 'has' (present tense of 'have').
• Positive tag with 'has': 'has he?' ✓
• Rule: Negative meaning + positive auxiliary → 'has he?' ✓
• Meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹത്തിന് ഏതാണ്ട് പണമില്ല, അല്ലേ?' (confirmation seeking)
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Positive sentence → Negative tag | Negative/negative-meaning sentence → Positive tag
• Negative adverbs that make a sentence negative in meaning (→ positive tag needed):
  - hardly (ഏതാണ്ട് ഇല്ല) → has he? / is he? / can he?
  - scarcely (നന്നേ കുറച്ചു മാത്രം) → same rule
  - barely (വളരെ കഷ്ടിച്ച്) → same rule
  - seldom/rarely (അപൂർവ്വമായി) → same rule
  - never (ഒരിക്കലും ഇല്ല) → same rule
• Tag formation: Use the SAME auxiliary as in the sentence + SUBJECT pronoun
  'He has hardly...' → auxiliary = has → tag = 'has he?' ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Negative adverb (hardly/never/seldom) + positive tag = classic PSC question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She can hardly walk, can she? (hardly → positive tag)
  2. He never lies, does he? (never → positive tag)
  3. They seldom visit, do they? (seldom → positive tag)
  4. She barely speaks, does she? (barely → positive tag)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) does he?
• 'Does' = auxiliary for simple present (main verbs: She goes, He speaks). But sentence uses 'has' (have = auxiliary verb here, not main verb).
• When 'have/has/had' is the main auxiliary → tag must use 'have/has/had', not 'do/does'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Does he?' feels like natural question tag → students default to 'does/do' without checking the auxiliary.

❌ (C) hasn't he?
• 'Hasn't he?' = negative tag. But 'hardly' makes the sentence negative in meaning → POSITIVE tag needed.
• Negative sentence + negative tag = double negative = wrong pattern.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Hasn't he?' mirrors 'has' in the sentence → students think opposite of 'has' is 'hasn't'. But sentence is already negative (hardly) → tag must be positive.

❌ (D) had he?
• 'Had' = past tense. Main sentence uses 'has' (present) → tag must also be present tense.
• Tense mismatch: present 'has' + past 'had' = wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has/had' sound similar → students confuse present 'has' with past 'had' in the tag.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HARDLY = NEGATIVE in disguise!' — 'Hardly any money' = 'no money almost'. When you see 'hardly/never/seldom/barely/scarcely' in a sentence, treat that sentence as NEGATIVE → positive tag needed!
• Negative adverb squad: HARDLY, NEVER, SELDOM, RARELY, BARELY, SCARCELY — all make sentences negative → all need POSITIVE tags.
• PSC exam strategy: Step 1 — spot negative adverbs (hardly/never/seldom). Step 2 — choose POSITIVE tag (not 'n't' form). Step 3 — match same auxiliary as in sentence. Here: 'has' in sentence → 'has he?' positive tag. Three steps = guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Report the sentence properly :
"Have you met the Principal?" the teacher said to me.
optionA: The teacher asked me, have I met the Principal
optionB: The teacher asked me to meet the Principal
optionC: The teacher asked me, if I have met the Principal
optionD: The teacher asked me, whether I had met the Principal

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) The teacher asked me, whether I had met the Principal

• Direct speech: 'Have you met the Principal?' = Yes/No question (interrogative).
• Yes/No questions report ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ: 'if' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'whether' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• Tense backshift: 'Have you met' (Present Perfect) → 'had met' (Past Perfect) ✓
• Pronoun change: 'you' (talking to me) → 'I' (now I'm the subject reporting).
• Word order change: Question word order → Statement word order ('had I met' ✗ → 'I had met' ✓).
• 'Whether I had met the Principal' = correct reported form ✓
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Yes/No question → 'if/whether' + subject + verb (statement order) + tense backshift
• Tense backshift table:
  - Present Simple → Past Simple
  - Present Continuous → Past Continuous
  - Present Perfect → Past Perfect ✓ (have met → had met)
  - Past Simple → Past Perfect
  - Will → Would
  - Can → Could
  - May → Might
• Yes/No question reporting:
  - 'Are you ready?' → asked if/whether I was ready.
  - 'Have you eaten?' → asked if/whether I had eaten.
  - 'Did you go?' → asked if/whether I had gone.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Direct/Indirect speech (questions) PSC-ൽ frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Do you know him?' → She asked me if I knew him.
  2. 'Have you finished?' → He asked whether I had finished.
  3. 'Will you come?' → She asked if I would come.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) The teacher asked me, have I met the Principal
• Question word order ('have I met') retained — indirect speech-ൽ statement order ആണ് ആവശ്യം ('I had met').
• No 'if/whether' used — Yes/No question reporting-ൽ 'if/whether' mandatory.
• No tense backshift: 'have met' → should be 'had met'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Direct speech-ന്റെ words directly copy ചെയ്‌ത് feel ആകും — three errors in one option.

❌ (B) The teacher asked me to meet the Principal
• 'Asked me to meet' = reporting a command/request (imperative), not a question.
• 'Have you met?' = question about past experience — 'to meet' = future action request. Meaning completely changed.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Asked me to...' sounds natural reported speech — but this reports a REQUEST, not a QUESTION about past.

❌ (C) The teacher asked me, if I have met the Principal
• 'If' is correct ✓ — but 'have met' = Present Perfect, no tense backshift!
• Reporting verb 'asked' is past → reported clause must also shift to past: 'had met' not 'have met'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'If' used correctly → students think option C is correct. But the tense error 'have met' makes it wrong.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'YES/NO question = IF/WHETHER in reported speech!' — 'Have you...?' 'Did you...?' 'Are you...?' — all Yes/No questions → reported with 'if' or 'whether'. WH-questions (What, Where, Who) use the WH-word itself.
• Backshift reminder: 'have met' → 'had met' (Present Perfect goes back one step to Past Perfect).
• PSC exam strategy: Indirect speech question — check THREE things: (1) if/whether for Yes/No questions ✓, (2) statement word order (not question order) ✓, (3) tense backshift ✓. Option with all three correct = answer. Here only (D) has all three!

questionText:
Pick out the correct article and fill the sentence aptly :
After ————— year or two, the question can be reconsidered.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) a

• 'Year' = 'y' letter-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു, പക്ഷേ sound /j/ (consonant sound) ആണ്.
• Article rule: SOUND-based, spelling-based അല്ല. Consonant sound → 'a'. Vowel sound → 'an'.
• 'Year' pronunciation: /jɪər/ — /j/ consonant sound → 'a year' ✓ (NOT 'an year' ✗)
• 'A year or two' = fixed phrase meaning 'ഒന്നോ രണ്ടോ വർഷം' — indefinite period.
• 'An year' = completely wrong, 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ vowel sound (/a/, /e/, /i/, /o/, /u/) ആവശ്യം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'a/an' = SOUND rule, not SPELLING rule!
  - Vowel SOUND (/a,e,i,o,u/ sound) → 'an'
  - Consonant SOUND → 'a'
• Tricky words — letter vs sound mismatch:
  Consonant sound despite vowel letter (use 'a'):
  - a year /j/ (y sounds like consonant)
  - a university /j/ (u sounds like 'you')
  - a European /j/ (Eu sounds like 'you')
  - a one-time /w/ (o sounds like 'wun')
  Vowel sound despite consonant letter (use 'an'):
  - an hour /aʊər/ (h is silent → vowel sound)
  - an honest man (h silent)
  - an MBA (M = 'em' → vowel sound)
  - an X-ray (X = 'ex' → vowel sound)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'a year / a university / an hour' article traps PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She will return after a year. ✓
  2. He is a university student. ✓ (not 'an university')
  3. Wait for an hour. ✓ (h silent)
  4. This is a one-time offer. ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) an
• 'An year' = classic error. 'Year' starts with letter 'y' but sound /j/ (consonant) → 'a year' correct, 'an year' wrong.
• Sound rule: /j/ sound = consonant → 'a', not 'an'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Year' starts with vowel letter 'y' → students think 'an year'. But SOUND, not letter decides! /j/ is a consonant sound.

❌ (C) one
• 'One year or two' = grammatically odd — 'one or two' is correct, but 'a year or two' is the idiomatic fixed phrase.
• 'One' = specific number emphasis. 'A year or two' = indefinite, approximate period — which is the intended meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'One year' sounds natural numerically → students choose it. But 'a year or two' is the standard English idiom here.

❌ (D) the
• 'The year or two' = definite article suggesting a specific known year — sentence ഇവിടെ indefinite approximate period ഉദ്ദേശിക്കുന്നു.
• 'The' = specific/known. 'A' = non-specific/any → 'a year or two' = approximately one or two years (non-specific).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The' sounds formal/correct → students pick it for formal sentences. But 'the' needs specific reference.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'A YEAR starts with CONSONANT SOUND /j/ — like YES!' — 'Year' sounds like it starts with 'Y' in 'YES' → /j/ consonant sound → 'a'. Compare: 'a year' (like 'a yellow') vs 'an apple' (vowel sound).
• Sound family to memorize for PSC:
  Use 'a': year, university, European, one-eyed, useful, unique
  Use 'an': hour, honest, MBA, X-ray, NCC, MLA
• PSC exam strategy: Never decide a/an by the LETTER — always check the SOUND. Say the word aloud: 'year' → /j/ sound → 'a'. 'Hour' → /aʊ/ sound → 'an'. Pronunciation test = guaranteed correct answer!

questionText:
Complete the sentence using correct tense :
The bell ————— (ring), before I reached the school.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) had rung

• 'Before I reached the school' = Past Simple (reached) — ഒരു past event.
• Bell ringing happened BEFORE reaching the school — so bell ringing is EARLIER past action.
• Earlier past (ആദ്യം സംഭവിച്ചത്) = Past Perfect (had + V3) ✓
• 'Had rung' = Past Perfect of 'ring' (ring → rang → rung)
• Sequence: Bell rang FIRST → I reached SECOND. First action = Past Perfect, Second action = Past Simple.
• Meaning: 'ഞാൻ school-ൽ എത്തുന്നതിന് മുൻപ് bell അടിച്ചിരുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Two past actions → Earlier action = Past Perfect (had+V3) | Later action = Past Simple
• Signal words for Past Perfect:
  - before: 'The bell had rung before I arrived.'
  - after: 'After he had eaten, he slept.'
  - by the time: 'By the time I came, she had left.'
  - already: 'He had already gone when I called.'
  - when: 'When I reached, the movie had started.'
• Irregular verb: ring → rang → rung
  - Past Simple: rang (He rang the bell.)
  - Past Perfect: had rung (The bell had rung.) ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Past Perfect + 'before/when/by the time' PSC-ൽ regular question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The train had left before we arrived.
  2. She had finished cooking when he came home.
  3. By the time I reached, the match had ended.
  4. I had already read the book when the movie released.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) was ringing
• 'Was ringing' = Past Continuous — an ongoing action at a specific past time.
• Past Continuous describes background action happening WHILE another action occurred: 'The bell was ringing when I arrived' (bell ringing and arriving = same time).
• But 'before I reached' = bell action COMPLETED before arriving → not ongoing → Past Perfect needed.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was ringing' = past, progressive feel → sounds descriptive and correct. But 'before' signal needs Past Perfect, not continuous.

❌ (B) rang
• 'Rang' = Simple Past. When two past events occur, Simple Past alone cannot show which happened EARLIER.
• 'The bell rang before I reached' — both Simple Past — ambiguous, no clear sequence shown.
• Past Perfect 'had rung' specifically shows the bell ringing was EARLIER than reaching.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Rang' is the most natural past form of 'ring' → students choose familiar form. But two-action sequences need Past Perfect for the earlier action.

❌ (D) rings
• 'Rings' = Simple Present. The entire sentence is in the past ('reached' = past) → present tense is wrong.
• Tense consistency: past context + present tense = mismatch.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Rings' is the base form of the verb → students who confuse Present Simple with the verb form choose it.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BEFORE = the first action gets HAD!' — When you see 'before' connecting two past events, the action mentioned BEFORE 'before' happened EARLIER → give it 'had+V3'. 'Bell RANG → before I reached' — bell happened first → HAD RUNG!
• Two-past-action formula: [Earlier action: HAD + V3] + before/when/after + [Later action: Simple Past]
• PSC exam strategy: 'Before / by the time / when' ഉള്ള past sentences-ൽ — two actions identify ചെയ്യുക, earlier one = had+V3. Also remember irregular: ring → rang → RUNG (not 'ringed'!). Both grammar + irregular verb knowledge needed here!

questionText:
Give the one word substitute for :
A person who studies about the Sun, the Moon, Stars and the Universe.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Astronomer

• Astronomer = Sun, Moon, Stars, Universe-നെ scientifically study ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — ഇതാണ് astronomy.
• Etymology: Greek 'astron' (star) + 'nomos' (law/arrangement) = stars-ന്റെ laws study ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• Astronomy = a science — telescopes, mathematics, physics ഉപയോഗിച്ച് celestial bodies study ചെയ്യുന്ന ശാസ്ത്രം.
• Famous astronomers: Galileo, Copernicus, Stephen Hawking — all studied the physical universe.
• Meaning: 'ഒരു astronomer Sun-ഉം Moon-ഉം Stars-ഉം Universe-ഉം scientific ആയി study ചെയ്യുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Astro = stars/celestial bodies. Nomer = study/arrangement → Astro+nomer = star scientist
• Astronomer vs Astrologer — classic confusion pair:
  - Astronomer = SCIENTIST who studies celestial bodies (science ✓)
  - Astrologer = person who PREDICTS future/fate from star positions (belief, not science)
• Other '-omer / -ologist' professions:
  - Astronomer — studies stars/universe (science)
  - Astrologer — reads horoscopes (not science)
  - Meteorologist — studies weather
  - Geologist — studies earth/rocks
  - Archaeologist — studies ancient civilizations
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Astronomer vs Astrologer confusion PSC-ൽ classic one word substitute!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. An astronomer discovered a new planet last year.
  2. The astronomer used a telescope to study distant stars.
  3. An astrologer predicted his future from his horoscope. (different!)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Astrologer
• Astrologer = horoscope / future prediction based on star positions — ഇത് science അല്ല, belief ആണ്.
• Question-ൽ 'studies about the Sun, Moon, Stars' = scientific study → Astronomer, not Astrologer.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Astro-' prefix same → Astrologer ആണ് familiar word (horoscope familiar!) → students confuse. But '-logy' = study/prediction, '-nomy' = scientific law/arrangement.

❌ (B) Cobbler
• Cobbler = shoe repair ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ. Stars, Moon, Universe-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• This is a deliberate easy eliminator option.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Some students may not know 'cobbler' → guess and choose. Easy eliminate: cobbler = shoes, not stars!

❌ (D) Astrologist
• 'Astrologist' = non-standard form of 'astrologer'. Standard terms: astronomer / astrologer.
• Even if accepted, it means astrologer (horoscope predictor), not scientific star student.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ologist' suffix sounds very scientific → students think it means scientist. But 'astrology' is not a science — 'astronomy' is.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AstroNOMer uses NUMBERS (math/science) — AstroLOGer uses LOGIC of stars for fate!' — Astronomer = numbers and science. Astrologer = logic of fate. NOM = numbers/science → Astronomer = scientist!
• Key difference: Astronomy = SCIENCE (Newton, Galileo, telescopes, physics). Astrology = BELIEF (horoscope, zodiac signs, fate prediction).
• PSC exam strategy: 'studies the Sun, Moon, Stars scientifically' → Astronomer (not Astrologer). Keyword 'scientifically / studies' = Astronomer. 'Predicts future / horoscope / zodiac' = Astrologer. One word, two meanings — never confuse!

questionText:
Fill in the blank with the opposite of the underlined word :
Swalih looked __cheerful__ whereas Sumin looked —————.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) gloomy

• 'Cheerful' = happy, bright, full of joy — 'സന്തോഷഭരിതമായ, പ്രസന്നമായ'.
• Antonym of 'cheerful' = 'gloomy' = sad, dark, depressed — 'ദുഃഖഭരിതമായ, ഇരുണ്ട'.
• 'Whereas' = contrast conjunction → opposite meaning required in second part.
• 'Swalih looked cheerful WHEREAS Sumin looked gloomy' = contrast between two people's expressions.
• Meaning: 'Swalih പ്രസന്നനായി കാണപ്പെട്ടു, Sumin ദുഃഖഭരിതനായും.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'whereas / but / while / on the other hand' = contrast signal → antonym needed in second part
• Cheerful antonym family:
  - cheerful ↔ gloomy (most direct opposite)
  - cheerful ↔ sad
  - cheerful ↔ melancholy (formal)
  - cheerful ↔ depressed
  - cheerful ↔ sorrowful
• 'Gloomy' word family:
  - Gloomy (adj) = sad and dark
  - Gloom (noun) = darkness/sadness
  - Gloomily (adverb) = in a gloomy way
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Antonym + 'whereas/but' contrast questions PSC-ൽ regular!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The morning was cheerful but the evening turned gloomy.
  2. She looked cheerful whereas her sister appeared gloomy.
  3. His cheerful voice contrasted with her gloomy silence.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) happy
• 'Happy' = synonym of 'cheerful', not antonym. Both mean positive emotional state.
• 'Swalih looked cheerful whereas Sumin looked happy' = NO contrast — both positive!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Question asks for 'opposite' — 'happy' and 'cheerful' both positive feelings → students who don't read carefully might choose it.

❌ (C) content
• 'Content' = satisfied, at peace — also a positive/neutral emotional state.
• Not an antonym of 'cheerful' — in fact similar in positive tone.
• 'Whereas' needs an OPPOSITE — content is not opposite of cheerful.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Content' sounds different from 'cheerful' → students may think it is opposite. But both are positive states.

❌ (D) joyous
• 'Joyous' = full of joy — strong synonym of 'cheerful'. Complete opposite of what 'whereas' signals.
• 'Cheerful whereas joyous' = no contrast at all — both very positive.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Joyous' sounds formal/different → students confuse with antonym. But joyous = more cheerful, not less!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GLOOMY = GLOOM = Dark room with no light!' — Gloomy = dark, sad, no happiness. Cheerful = bright, sunny, full of happiness. Gloomy room ↔ cheerful sunshine — perfect opposites!
• 'Whereas' = automatic antonym signal. When you see 'whereas / but / while / however', second blank = OPPOSITE of the underlined word.
• PSC exam strategy: (1) Identify the underlined word 'cheerful' = positive/happy. (2) 'Whereas' = need opposite. (3) Eliminate all positive options (happy, content, joyous). (4) Only negative option = 'gloomy' ✓. Three-step elimination = guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Which is the apt synonym for the __underlined__ word?
The woman looked very __weak__.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) feeble

• 'Weak' = lacking strength, not powerful — 'ദുർബലമായ, ശക്തിയില്ലാത്ത'.
• 'Feeble' = very weak, lacking physical/mental strength — exact synonym of 'weak'.
• Etymology: Old French 'fleible' → lacking strength. Very close in meaning to 'weak'.
• 'The woman looked very weak/feeble' — both describe physical lack of strength.
• Meaning: 'ആ സ്ത്രീ വളരെ ദുർബലയായി കാണപ്പെട്ടു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകรണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Feeble' = physically/mentally weak — strongest synonym of 'weak'
• 'Weak' synonym family (strength to degree):
  - feeble = very weak (physical weakness, often illness-related)
  - frail = delicate and weak (often elderly)
  - fragile = easily broken/hurt
  - powerless = without power
  - infirm = weak due to age/illness
• Other options in this question:
  - smart = intelligent/clever (unrelated to weak)
  - wicked = evil/bad (unrelated to weak)
  - amiable = friendly/pleasant (unrelated to weak)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Weak' synonyms (feeble/frail/fragile) PSC-ൽ regular vocabulary question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The old man was too feeble to walk without support.
  2. Her feeble voice could barely be heard.
  3. After the illness, he felt feeble and tired.
  4. A feeble excuse = a weak/unconvincing excuse.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) smart
• 'Smart' = intelligent, clever, or well-dressed — positive quality, opposite direction from 'weak'.
• No relationship with physical weakness whatsoever.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Smart' is a common English word → students might choose familiar words. But meaning is completely different from 'weak'.

❌ (C) wicked
• 'Wicked' = evil, morally bad — character/moral quality, not physical strength.
• 'Weak' = physical/mental strength. 'Wicked' = moral character. No connection.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Wicked' sounds negative like 'weak' → both negative feel → students confuse. But type of negativity is different: weak = no strength, wicked = no goodness.

❌ (D) amiable
• 'Amiable' = friendly, pleasant, likeable — positive personality trait.
• Complete antonym zone of 'weak' — strong positive social quality.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Amiable' is a vocabulary word students may not know → guess incorrectly. Amiable = friendly (amigo = friend in Spanish → amiable = friendly).
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FEEBLE = FEEL WEAK!' — When you feel feeble, you FEEL WEAK! 'Feeble' sounds like 'feel-ble' → feeling of weakness. Feeble = feel weak = perfect synonym!
• Weak synonym trio for PSC: FEEBLE (very weak) / FRAIL (delicate weak) / FRAGILE (breakable weak) — all mean weak but with slight nuances.
• PSC exam strategy: Synonym questions — eliminate antonyms and unrelated words first. 'Smart' (intelligent), 'wicked' (evil), 'amiable' (friendly) — all unrelated to 'weak'. Only 'feeble' = weak. Process of elimination takes 5 seconds!

questionText:
Use the correctly spelt word to complete the sentence :
If you ————— money, you will quit the business.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) lose

• 'Lose' = verb — to not have something anymore, to fail to win — 'നഷ്ടപ്പെടുക'.
• 'If you lose money' = First Conditional (If + present simple → will + V1).
• Sentence structure: 'If you lose (present simple) → you will quit (future)' ✓
• 'Lose' pronunciation: /luːz/ — rhymes with 'choose', 'news'.
• Meaning: 'നിങ്ങൾക്ക് പണം നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടാൽ, business ഉപേക്ഷിക്കും.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: lose (verb/present) ≠ lost (verb/past) ≠ loose (adjective) ≠ loss (noun)
• The 'lose' confusion family — 4 different words:
  - lose /luːz/ = verb, present tense: 'I lose', 'you lose' (നഷ്ടപ്പെടുക — present)
  - lost /lɒst/ = verb, past tense: 'I lost', 'she lost' (നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടു — past)
  - loose /luːs/ = adjective: not tight, not fixed (അയഞ്ഞ, ഇളകുന്ന) — 'a loose button'
  - loss /lɒs/ = noun: the act of losing (നഷ്ടം) — 'a financial loss'
• Pronunciation tip:
  - lose = /luːz/ (z sound at end)
  - loose = /luːs/ (s sound at end)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: lose/loose/loss/lost confusion PSC-ൽ guaranteed spelling question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Don't lose your keys. (verb — present)
  2. She lost her wallet yesterday. (verb — past)
  3. The screw is loose. (adjective — not tight)
  4. The company suffered a huge loss. (noun)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) loss
• 'Loss' = noun (നഷ്ടം). Sentence needs a VERB ('if you ___ money'). Noun cannot fill verb position.
• 'If you loss money' = ungrammatical — noun in verb slot.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Loss' sounds like 'lose' → most common spelling confusion. But loss = noun, lose = verb.

❌ (B) lost
• 'Lost' = past tense of 'lose'. Conditional sentence 'If you ___ money' uses present simple (First Conditional).
• 'If you lost money' = Second Conditional (imaginary present) — changes the meaning entirely.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Lost' is the most familiar form of 'lose' → students default to it. But present tense needed here for First Conditional.

❌ (C) loose
• 'Loose' = adjective meaning 'not tight' (അയഞ്ഞ). Cannot describe losing money.
• 'If you loose money' = ungrammatical and meaningless — adjective in verb position.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Loose' and 'lose' look very similar → one extra 'o' difference → students mix them up. 'Loose' = two o's = adjective. 'Lose' = one o = verb.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LOOSE has TWO O's — it's TOO LOOSE! LOSE has ONE O — it LOST one O!' — Loose (adjective, not tight) = double 'oo' like it's stretched/loose. Lose (verb, to misplace) = single 'o' because it already LOST the extra 'o'!
• Quick identification:
  - 'loss' ends in 'ss' → noun (নষ্টম)
  - 'lost' ends in 't' → past verb
  - 'loose' has double 'oo' → adjective (not tight)
  - 'lose' single 'o' → present verb (to misplace)
• PSC exam strategy: 'lose/loose/loss/lost' question-ൽ sentence slot identify ചെയ്യുക. Verb needed? → lose (present) or lost (past). Adjective? → loose. Noun? → loss. Here 'if you ___ money' = verb slot + present tense → 'lose'!

questionText:
Pick out the correct sentence with the right phrasal verb for the __underlined__ word :
The doctor advised me to __abandon__ smoking.
optionA: The Doctor advised me to give away smoking
optionB: The Doctor advised me to call off smoking
optionC: The Doctor advised me to give up smoking
optionD: The Doctor advised me to put out smoking

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) The Doctor advised me to give up smoking

• 'Abandon' = to completely stop/quit something forever — 'ഉപേക്ഷിക്കുക, പൂർണ്ണമായും നിർത്തുക'.
• 'Give up' = to stop doing something permanently — perfect synonym of 'abandon' in this context.
• 'Give up smoking' = smoking ഉപേക്ഷിക്കുക — standard, fixed English phrase.
• Famous usage: 'Give up smoking' is the most common medical advice collocation in English.
• Meaning: 'Doctor എന്നോട് smoking ഉപേക്ഷിക്കാൻ ഉപദേശിച്ചു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'give up' = stop permanently (= abandon, quit, discontinue)
• 'Give' phrasal verb family (PSC must-know):
  - give up = stop/abandon permanently ('give up smoking/drinking/hope')
  - give away = distribute for free / reveal a secret ('give away prizes / give away the surprise')
  - give in = surrender/yield ('give in to pressure')
  - give out = distribute / stop working ('give out pamphlets / the engine gave out')
  - give off = emit/release ('give off smell/heat/light')
• Other 'abandon' synonyms as phrasal verbs:
  - give up ✓ (most common)
  - quit (not phrasal but synonym)
  - call it off (informal)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'give up' phrasal verb PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He gave up alcohol for health reasons.
  2. She gave up her job to raise her children.
  3. Don't give up hope — keep trying!
  4. The doctor asked him to give up junk food.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) give away smoking
• 'Give away' = distribute free / reveal a secret — 'smoking give away ചെയ്യുക' = smoking free-ആയി distribute ചെയ്യുക?
• Makes no logical sense in this context. 'Give away' ≠ abandon.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Give' same base verb → 'give away' sounds similar to 'give up' → students confuse. But 'away' ≠ 'up' in meaning.

❌ (B) call off smoking
• 'Call off' = to cancel a planned event — 'call off a meeting / call off a strike'.
• Smoking is a habit, not a planned event — habits are 'given up', events are 'called off'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Call off' = stop → stop smoking → seems like abandon. But 'call off' = cancel events, not quit habits.

❌ (D) put out smoking
• 'Put out' = extinguish (fire/cigarette) OR inconvenience someone — 'put out a fire / put out a candle'.
• 'Put out smoking' = extinguish the act of smoking? Grammatically awkward and wrong meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Put out a cigarette' is correct (extinguish one cigarette) — but 'put out smoking' (abandon the habit) = wrong. Nuance difference!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'GIVE UP = Give UP on something — you lift your hands UP and surrender!' — When you give up, you raise your hands UP in surrender, meaning you stop/abandon. Give up smoking = surrender to the doctor's advice!
• 'Give' phrasal family shortcut:
  - Give UP = stop permanently (habits: smoking, drinking, hope)
  - Give AWAY = distribute free (prizes, secrets)
  - Give IN = surrender to pressure
  - Give OFF = emit (smell, light, heat)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Abandon' = 'give up' — this is a 100% fixed equation for PSC. Whenever 'abandon a habit' appears → 'give up' is the phrasal verb. Memorise: abandon smoking = give up smoking!

questionText: The tourist guide along with the tourists ____________ arrived.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) has

• 'Along with' = 'as well as' പോലെ ഒരു parenthetical addition ആണ് — coordinating conjunction 'and' അല്ല.
• Rule: 'Along with' ഉള്ളപ്പോൾ verb, FIRST subject-നെ (primary subject) അനുസരിക്കണം.
• First subject: 'The tourist guide' = singular → singular verb 'has' ✓
• 'Along with the tourists' = additional information — bracket ആയി treat ചെയ്‌ത് remove ചെയ്‌ത് നോക്കാം: 'The tourist guide ___ arrived' → singular → 'has'.
• Meaning: 'Tourist guide, tourists-ഉമായി കൂടി, arrived.' — guide ആണ് primary subject.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: 'Along with / as well as / together with / in addition to' → verb agrees with FIRST subject only
• Expressions that follow FIRST subject rule:
  - along with: The teacher, along with students, HAS arrived.
  - as well as: The manager, as well as the staff, WAS informed.
  - together with: She, together with her friends, WAS invited.
  - in addition to: The principal, in addition to teachers, IS present.
  - with: The boy, with his parents, WAS travelling.
  - besides: Ram, besides his brothers, WAS selected.
• Compare with 'AND' — 'and' joins subjects = plural:
  - The guide AND the tourists HAVE arrived. (plural)
  - The guide along with the tourists HAS arrived. (singular — first subject)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Along with / as well as' subject-verb agreement PSC-ൽ very frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The captain, along with his team, has won the trophy.
  2. The Prime Minister, along with ministers, has arrived.
  3. She, along with her sisters, is coming tomorrow.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) have
• 'Have' = plural auxiliary. 'Tourists' plural ആണ് → 'have' choose ചെയ്യുന്നു — but first subject 'tourist guide' singular ആണ്.
• 'Along with' second subject-നെ primary ആക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Guide + tourists' = two entities → plural → 'have' feel. Most common error!

❌ (C) are
• 'Are' = plural present tense. Same error as (B) — treating compound as plural.
• Plus 'arrived' = past tense → 'are arrived' = grammatically inconsistent tense.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Tourists are' common plural usage → students apply it here automatically.

❌ (D) will be
• 'Will be' = future tense. 'Arrived' = past tense. Future + past = complete tense mismatch.
• The action is already completed ('arrived') — future auxiliary 'will be' cannot precede past verb.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students unfamiliar with 'has arrived' (Present Perfect) pattern may choose 'will be' guessing.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ALONG WITH = carry bag — the GUIDE carries it, tourists just ride along!' — Tourist guide is the main person; tourists just come along. Main person = verb controller. Guide (singular) → HAS!
• Bracket trick: Remove 'along with....' mentally: 'The tourist guide (along with the tourists) ___ arrived' → 'The tourist guide ___ arrived' → singular → 'has'. Works 100% of the time!
• PSC exam strategy: 'Along with / as well as / together with' questions-ൽ second part bracket ചെയ്‌ത് ignore ചെയ്യുക. First subject alone = verb controller. Singular first subject → has/was/is. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Let's go for a walk, __________ ?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) shall we?

• 'Let's' = 'Let us' — ഇത് a suggestion/invitation ആണ് (ഒരുമിച്ച് ചെയ്യാം എന്ന proposal).
• 'Let's' sentences-ന്റെ question tag = ALWAYS 'shall we?' — ഇത് fixed rule ആണ്.
• 'Shall we?' = 'നമ്മൾ ഇത് ചെയ്യട്ടെ, അല്ലേ?' — invitation confirm ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'Let's go for a walk, shall we?' = 'ഒരു walk-ന് പോകാം, ശരിയാണോ?'
• ഈ rule exception ഇല്ല — 'Let's' → 'shall we?' always.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Let's + action → shall we? (ALWAYS — no exceptions!)
• Special question tag rules (PSC must-know):
  - Let's go → shall we? ✓ (suggestion/invitation)
  - I am right → aren't I? (not 'amn't I')
  - Let him do it → will he? (command about a third person)
  - Let me try → will you? (request for permission)
  - Nothing will help → will it?
  - Everyone is happy → aren't they?
• Normal tag rule (for reference):
  - Positive sentence → Negative tag
  - Negative sentence → Positive tag
  - Use same auxiliary as in sentence
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Let's → shall we?' PSC-ൽ classic special tag question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Let's have a party, shall we?
  2. Let's start the meeting, shall we?
  3. Let's take a break, shall we?
  4. Let's try again, shall we?
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) shall you
• 'Shall you' = wrong — question tag subject must match the subject of 'Let's' which is 'us/we' — not 'you'.
• 'Let's' = Let us → 'we' is the implied subject → tag subject = 'we', not 'you'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Shall' correct feel → students pick 'shall you' thinking they got the auxiliary right. But subject 'you' is wrong — 'we' needed.

❌ (B) will you
• 'Will you?' = tag for imperative commands directed AT the listener ('Open the door, will you?').
• 'Let's' = invitation including speaker + listener (we). Commands use 'will you'. Suggestions use 'shall we'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will you?' = very common tag → students default to it. But 'will you' = command tag, 'shall we' = suggestion tag.

❌ (D) may we
• 'May we?' = asking permission — 'May we go?' = requesting permission to go.
• Question tags are not permission requests — they seek CONFIRMATION of a statement.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'May' sounds polite/formal → invitation context → students choose it. But 'may we' = permission request, not confirmation tag.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LET'S = WE + SHALL = shall we!' — 'Let's' means 'let us' → US = WE → tag uses WE. And 'Let's' is a suggestion → SHALL (future intention/suggestion). Put together: LET'S → SHALL WE? Simple formula, zero exceptions!
• Special tags to memorize for PSC:
  - Let's → SHALL WE?
  - I am → AREN'T I?
  - Imperatives → WILL YOU?
• PSC exam strategy: 'Let's' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ → immediately 'shall we?' choose ചെയ്യുക. Don't look at other options — this is a FIXED rule with NO exceptions. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Change to indirect speech :
He says, "I am attending a training".
optionA: He said that he was attending a training.
optionB: He says that he was attending a training.
optionC: He said that he is attending a training.
optionD: He says that he is attending a training.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) He says that he is attending a training.

• Reporting verb: 'says' = PRESENT TENSE → NO tense backshift needed!
• Key rule: Reporting verb present tense ആണെങ്കിൽ (says/tells/asks) reported clause tense CHANGE ആകില്ല.
• 'I am attending' → 'he is attending' (tense same, pronoun changed only: I → he) ✓
• Pronoun change: 'I' → 'he' (speaker about himself).
• 'He says that he is attending a training.' = ✓ (present reporting verb → present reported clause)
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: Reporting verb PRESENT (says/tells) → NO tense change | Reporting verb PAST (said/told) → TENSE BACKSHIFT
• Tense backshift only when reporting verb is PAST:
  - says → said: am attending → was attending (backshift)
  - says → says: am attending → is attending (NO backshift) ✓
• Tense backshift table (used only when reporting verb = past):
  - Present Simple → Past Simple
  - Present Continuous → Past Continuous (am attending → was attending)
  - Present Perfect → Past Perfect
  - Will → Would
• Pronoun changes always happen regardless of tense:
  - I → he/she
  - my → his/her
  - we → they
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Present tense reporting verb (says) + no backshift = PSC classic trap!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He says, 'I am hungry.' → He says that he is hungry. (says → no change)
  2. He said, 'I am hungry.' → He said that he was hungry. (said → backshift)
  3. She says, 'I will come.' → She says that she will come. (says → no change)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He said that he was attending a training.
• 'Said' = past tense reporting verb. Original sentence uses 'says' (present) → 'said' wrong.
• Also 'was attending' = backshifted tense — but no backshift needed since reporting verb is present.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students memorise backshift rule (am→was) and apply it automatically, even when reporting verb is present tense. Two errors: said (wrong) + was attending (wrong).

❌ (B) He says that he was attending a training.
• 'Says' = correct ✓ (matches original). But 'was attending' = wrong tense backshift.
• Reporting verb 'says' (present) → NO backshift → 'is attending' needed, not 'was attending'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students know 'says' is correct but then still apply backshift automatically. Half correct, half wrong.

❌ (C) He said that he is attending a training.
• 'Said' = wrong (original uses 'says'). Tense is 'is attending' which is correct for present reporting verb, but 'said' is wrong.
• Inconsistency: past reporting verb 'said' + present reported clause 'is attending' = tense mismatch.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students mix up — correct tense (is attending) but wrong reporting verb (said). Another half-correct trap.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SAYS = STAYS — tense STAYS the same!' — When reporting verb is 'says' (present), the tense in the reported clause STAYS the same. SAYS = STAYS. Easy rhyme!
• Two-step check: (1) Is reporting verb present (says/tells) or past (said/told)? (2) Present → no backshift. Past → backshift.
• PSC exam strategy: First look at the reporting verb — 'says' or 'said'? If 'says' → choose option where tense is NOT changed ('is attending' not 'was attending'). If 'said' → choose option where tense IS changed. This 2-second check eliminates 3 wrong options instantly!

questionText: If I had money, I _____________ the poor.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) would help

• If clause: 'If I had money' = Past Simple (had) → ഇത് Second Conditional ആണ്.
• Second Conditional = present/future imaginary situation (ഇപ്പോൾ money ഇല്ല, പക്ഷേ ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ...).
• Pattern: If + Past Simple → would + V1 (bare infinitive)
• 'If I had money' → main clause: 'would help' ✓
• Meaning: 'എനിക്ക് പണം ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, ദരിദ്രരെ സഹായിക്കുമായിരുന്നു.' (actually doesn't have money now).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: If + Past Simple (had/knew/were) → would + V1 (Second Conditional)
• Three Conditional types comparison:
  1. First (Real future): If + Present Simple → will + V1
     'If I have money, I will help.' (possible future)
  2. Second (Imaginary present): If + Past Simple → would + V1
     'If I had money, I would help.' ✓ (imaginary — no money now)
  3. Third (Past unreal): If + Past Perfect → would have + V3
     'If I had had money, I would have helped.' (past — didn't have money)
• Key signal: 'had' in if-clause WITHOUT another verb = Past Simple of 'have' → Second Conditional
• 'Had had' in if-clause = Past Perfect of 'have' → Third Conditional
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Second vs Third Conditional distinction PSC-ൽ favourite!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If I were rich, I would travel the world. (Second)
  2. If she knew the answer, she would tell us. (Second)
  3. If it rained, I would stay at home. (Second)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) would have helped
• 'Would have helped' = Third Conditional main clause (needs 'had + V3' in if-clause).
• If-clause here: 'had money' = Past Simple → Second Conditional → 'would help' needed.
• 'Would have helped' needs: 'If I had had money' (Past Perfect) in if-clause.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Both Second and Third use 'would' → students confuse 'would help' vs 'would have helped'. 'Have' makes the critical difference!

❌ (B) will help
• 'Will help' = First Conditional (real future possibility). If-clause needs Present Simple: 'If I have money'.
• But if-clause here is 'had money' (Past Simple) = Second Conditional → 'would', not 'will'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will help' = optimistic/positive feel → students want to help the poor so choose 'will'. But tense rule overrides meaning!

❌ (D) will be helped
• 'Will be helped' = passive voice future. Subject 'I' is HELPING the poor (active), not being helped.
• Active: I would help the poor. ✓ Passive: The poor would be helped by me. ✓ — but 'will be helped' wrong tense and wrong voice for this context.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Students confuse active/passive and also use 'will' instead of 'would'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IF + HAD (alone) = WOULD. IF + HAD HAD = WOULD HAVE!' — Simple Past 'had' → Second Conditional → would. Past Perfect 'had had' → Third Conditional → would have. Count the 'hads'!
• Quick conditional identifier:
  - 'If I had money' (one 'had' = simple past) → WOULD help (Second)
  - 'If I had had money' (two 'hads' = past perfect) → WOULD HAVE helped (Third)
• PSC exam strategy: If-clause-ൽ 'had + noun' (had money, had time, had chance) = Second Conditional → 'would + V1'. If-clause-ൽ 'had + V3' (had known, had gone, had tried) = Third Conditional → 'would have + V3'. Count the pattern!

questionText: The doll belongs __________ the child.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) to

• 'Belong to' = fixed verb + preposition collocation — 'ആരുടേതോ ആണ്, ownership' indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'The doll belongs to the child' = 'ആ doll ആ കുട്ടിയുടേതാണ്.'
• 'Belong' + 'to' = inseparable pair. 'Belongs at / over / of' = completely wrong.
• This is a fixed collocation — 'to' ഒഴികെ മറ്റൊരു preposition 'belong'-ന് ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Meaning: 'ആ doll ആ കുട്ടിക്ക് സ്വന്തമാണ്.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 Quick Formula: belong TO = ownership/membership. No other preposition works with 'belong'.
• 'Belong to' usage contexts:
  - Ownership: 'This book belongs to me.'
  - Membership: 'She belongs to the chess club.'
  - Category: 'The whale belongs to the mammal family.'
  - Origin: 'He belongs to Kerala.'
• Verb + preposition fixed collocations (PSC must-know):
  - belong TO (ownership)
  - listen TO (music/radio)
  - refer TO (a source)
  - object TO (a proposal)
  - agree TO (a plan)
  - attend TO (needs/duties)
  - subscribe TO (a magazine)
  - contribute TO (a cause)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Belong to' preposition collocation PSC-ൽ classic question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. This house belongs to my grandmother.
  2. He belongs to a famous family.
  3. Kerala belongs to South India.
  4. The trophy belongs to the winner.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) at
• 'Belong at' = not a standard English collocation. 'At' = location (at school, at home) — ownership ഇതിൽ indicate ആകില്ല.
• 'The doll belongs at the child' = grammatically wrong and meaningless.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'At' + location possible ('belongs at this place') → students confuse location with ownership.

❌ (C) over
• 'Belong over' = not a standard English collocation. 'Over' = above/across/more than.
• No context where 'belong over' makes sense in standard English.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Rare/unfamiliar preposition → students choose it as possible answer when unsure.

❌ (D) of
• 'Belong of' = not standard English. 'Of' = possession in noun phrases (the child's doll / the doll of the child), but 'belong of' does not exist.
• Students confuse 'of' (possession in noun phrases) with verb 'belong' + preposition.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Of' = possession feel (the doll OF the child) → students transfer this to verb form. But 'belong' + 'of' = wrong combination.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BELONG TO = you give ownership TO someone!' — When something belongs TO you, you have ownership. 'TO' shows direction of ownership — the doll goes TO the child (it is theirs). Belong → TO → ownership!
• 'TO' collocation group — verbs that always use 'TO': belong TO, listen TO, refer TO, object TO, agree TO, speak TO, write TO, respond TO.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Belong ___' question-ൽ → automatically 'to' choose ചെയ്യുക — ഇത് fixed collocation ആണ്, ഒരു exception-ഉം ഇല്ല. 'Belong to' = one of the most tested preposition collocations in PSC. Memorize it permanently!

questionText: Identify the correct spelling.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Propriety

• 'Propriety' = ശരിയായ സ്പെല്ലിംഗ് — ഇതിന്റെ അർഥം: proper behaviour, social correctness — 'മര്യാദ, ഔചിത്യബോധം'.
• ഈ വാക്കിന്റെ spelling ശ്രദ്ധിക്കുക: P-R-O-P-R-I-E-T-Y — 'propri' + 'ety'.
• 'Proper' → 'propriety' — proper-ൽ നിന്ന് ഉണ്ടായ word ആണ്, 'proper + iety' pattern follow ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'She behaved with great propriety at the ceremony' = 'ചടങ്ങിൽ അവൾ വലിയ ഔചിത്യബോധത്തോടെ പെരുമാറി.'
• PSC-ൽ ഇത്തരം '-iety' ending words spelling questions-ൽ regular ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: PROPRI + ETY = PROPRIETY. 'Proper' എന്ന word-ൽ നിന്നാണ് 'propriety' ഉണ്ടായത്.
• ഈ word-ൽ ഉള്ള common spelling mistakes ഇവയാണ്:
  - 'Propreity' — 'e' തെറ്റായ position-ൽ ഇട്ടിരിക്കുന്നു ('ie' order തെറ്റ്)
  - 'Proprity' — 'ie' completely missing ആണ്
  - 'Propreiti' — ending 'ity' instead of 'ety' — wrong suffix
• '-iety' suffix ഉള്ള PSC important words:
  - propriety (ഔചിത്യം)
  - society (സമൂഹം)
  - anxiety (ഉത്ക്കണ്ഠ)
  - variety (വൈവിധ്യം)
  - sobriety (സംയമനം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Propriety' PSC spelling questions-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരുന്ന word ആണ്!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Propriety demands that we dress formally for the event. (ഔചിത്യം formal dress ആവശ്യപ്പെടുന്നു)
  2. He always observed the proprieties of the occasion. (അദ്ദേഹം എല്ലായ്‌പ്പോഴും ഔചിത്യം പാലിച്ചു)
  3. Social propriety is important in formal gatherings. (ഔദ്യോഗിക സ്ഥലങ്ങളിൽ സാമൂഹിക ഔചിത്യം പ്രധാനം)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Propreity
• ഇവിടെ 'e' തെറ്റായ position-ൽ ആണ് — 'propre-ity' ആണ് ഇവിടെ ഉള്ളത്, ശരി 'propri-ety' ആണ്.
• 'ie' → 'ei' order mistake — vowel sequence reversed ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Propreity' ഉച്ചരിക്കുമ്പോൾ 'Propriety' പോലെ sound ആകും → students write 'e' ആദ്യം ഇടും. Sound same, spelling different!

❌ (B) Proprity
• ഇവിടെ 'ie' completely missing ആണ് — 'propri-ety' ആകേണ്ടത് 'proprity' ആയി.
• ഒരു vowel combination (-ie-) drop ആയതിനാൽ word incomplete ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: വേഗത്തിൽ എഴുതുമ്പോൾ middle vowels miss ആകും — careful ആയി read ചെയ്‌താൽ letter missing ആണ് കാണാം.

❌ (C) Propreiti
• രണ്ട് errors ഉണ്ട്: (1) 'e' wrong position-ൽ, (2) ending 'iti' ആണ്, ശരി 'ety' ആണ്.
• '-iety' ending-ന് പകരം '-eiti' ഇട്ടിരിക്കുന്നു — sound-based guessing ആണ് ഈ error-ന്റെ കാരണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ety' sound-ഉം 'iti' sound-ഉം ഒരേ പോലെ feel ആകും — spelling rules ഓർക്കാതെ sound-ൽ depend ചെയ്‌ത് write ചെയ്‌താൽ ഈ തെറ്റ് സംഭവിക്കും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'PROPER + iety = PROPRIETY — proper ആളുകൾ propriety follow ചെയ്യുന്നു!' — 'Proper behaviour' = 'propriety'. Proper-ൽ നിന്ന് ഉണ്ടായ word ആണ് propriety — proper ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ propri-ety spelling ഓർക്കാം!
• '-iety' family ഒരുമിച്ച് memorize ചെയ്യുക: soci-ety, anxi-ety, vari-ety, propri-ety — ഇവ എല്ലാം '-iety' ending ആണ്.
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling questions-ൽ ഓരോ option-ഉം ശ്രദ്ധയോടെ letter-by-letter compare ചെയ്യുക. 'Propriety' = P-R-O-P-R-I-E-T-Y — ഈ sequence ഒരിക്കൽ memorize ചെയ്‌താൽ മതി. '-iety' suffix ഉള്ള option ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ ആദ്യം അത് consider ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: The idiom 'bone of contention' means

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) subject of dispute

• 'Bone of contention' = തർക്കവിഷയം, വിവാദ കാരണം — ഒരു dispute-ന് cause ആകുന്ന കാര്യം.
• Origin: നായ്ക്കൾ ഒരു bone-നുവേണ്ടി fight ചെയ്യുന്നത് imagine ചെയ്യുക — ആ bone ആണ് 'bone of contention'. രണ്ടു പേർ ഒരു കാര്യത്തിനുവേണ്ടി argue ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ ആ കാര്യം 'bone of contention' ആണ്.
• 'Contention' = disagreement, dispute, argument — 'തർക്കം, വിവാദം'.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'The property was the bone of contention between the two brothers.' = 'ആ സ്വത്ത് രണ്ട് സഹോദരന്മാർ തമ്മിലുള്ള തർക്കവിഷയമായിരുന്നു.'
• Meaning: Subject of dispute = ഒരു argument-ന്റെ കേന്ദ്ര വിഷയം.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Bone of contention = 'ആ ഒരു കാര്യം' — അതിനുവേണ്ടിയാണ് fight നടക്കുന്നത്.
• 'Bone' idioms PSC must-know:
  - bone of contention = subject of dispute (തർക്കവിഷയം)
  - bone-dry = completely dry (തികച്ചും ഉണങ്ങിയ)
  - make no bones about = to be direct/frank (നേരിട്ട് പറയുക)
  - feel it in one's bones = strong intuition (അനുഭവം കൊണ്ട് അറിയുക)
• 'Contention' ഉള്ള phrases:
  - bone of contention (തർക്കവിഷയം)
  - point of contention (തർക്കം ഉള്ള point)
  - in contention = competing ആണ് (ഒരു prize-ന് competition-ൽ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Bone of contention' idiom PSC-ൽ regularly ഉള്ള classic question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The border dispute has been a bone of contention for decades. (ദശകങ്ങളായി border dispute ഒരു തർക്കവിഷയമാണ്)
  2. Money is often the bone of contention in family disputes. (കുടുംബ തർക്കങ്ങളിൽ പണം പ്രധാന വിഷയം)
  3. The new policy became a bone of contention among the staff. (staff-ഇടയിൽ new policy തർക്കവിഷയമായി)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) quite worthless
• 'Quite worthless' = 'ഒരു വിലയും ഇല്ലാത്ത' — ഇത് 'bone of contention'-ന്റെ meaning അല്ല.
• ഒരു bone-ന് worthless feel ആകും — 'dog-ന്റെ bone = worthless' ആണ് ഇവിടെ confuse ചെയ്യുന്ന idea.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Bone' = കൊള്ളരുതാത്ത/worthless feeling → 'quite worthless' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ idioms literal ആയി interpret ചെയ്യരുത്!

❌ (B) man of nobility
• 'Man of nobility' = 'ഉന്നത കുലജാതൻ, കുലീനൻ' — bone of contention-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 'Noble' = high-class, dignified — tർക്കം, dispute ഒന്നും ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Contention' = 'noble contention / noble cause' ആണ് എന്ന് confuse ആകും. 'Contention' = argument/dispute ആണ്, nobility അല്ല.

❌ (D) very rarely
• 'Very rarely' = 'വളരെ അപൂർവ്വമായി' — frequency indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന phrase ആണ്.
• 'Bone of contention'-ന് frequency-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Once in a blue moon' = very rarely → students confuse moon/bone idioms. Random guess ആയി 'very rarely' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BONE OF CONTENTION — രണ്ടു നായ്ക്കൾ ഒരു bone-നുവേണ്ടി fight ചെയ്യുന്നത് imagine ചെയ്യൂ! ആ bone ആണ് cause of their fight — subject of dispute!' — ഈ picture ഒരിക്കൽ മനസ്സിൽ കണ്ടാൽ 'bone of contention = തർക്കവിഷയം' ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Dispute idioms family: bone of contention (subject of dispute) / at loggerheads (in strong disagreement) / cross swords (argue/fight) — ഇവ PSC-ൽ related topics ആണ്.
• PSC exam strategy: Idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning eliminate ചെയ്യുക. 'Bone = worthless' feel ആകുന്ന options ഉടനെ eliminate ചെയ്യുക. 'Contention' = dispute/argument keyword ഓർത്ത് 'subject of dispute' choose ചെയ്യുക!

questionText: The ______________ of wolves howled at the moon.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) pack

• 'A pack of wolves' = ചെന്നായ്ക്കളുടെ ഒരു കൂട്ടം — ഇത് fixed collective noun ആണ്.
• Wolves-ന്റെ collective noun = 'pack' — ഇത് fixed, മറ്റൊരു word ഉപയോഗിക്കാൻ പ്പറ്റില്ല.
• 'A pack of wolves howled at the moon' = 'ഒരു കൂട്ടം ചെന്നായ്ക്കൾ ചന്ദ്രനെ നോക്കി ഓരിയിട്ടു.'
• 'Pack' = wolves, dogs, cards-ന്റെ group indicate ചെയ്യാൻ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• Wolves group-ന് 'pack' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് ഒരു fixed rule ആണ് — animals-ന് ഓരോ specific collective noun ഉണ്ട്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: ഓരോ animal-ഉം ഒരു specific collective noun ഉപയോഗിക്കും — ഇവ memorize ചെയ്‌ത് വേണം!
• Animals-ന്റെ PSC must-know collective nouns:
  - wolves → pack (ചെന്നായ്ക്കൾ)
  - lions → pride (സിംഹങ്ങൾ)
  - sheep/birds → flock (ആടുകൾ/പക്ഷികൾ)
  - cattle/elephants → herd (കന്നുകാലി/ആനകൾ)
  - fish → shoal/school (മീനുകൾ)
  - bees → swarm (തേനീച്ചകൾ)
  - crows → murder (കാക്കകൾ)
  - ants → colony (ഉറുമ്പുകൾ)
  - dogs → pack/kennel (നായ്ക്കൾ)
  - horses → herd/stable (കുതിരകൾ)
  - geese → gaggle (താറാവ് ജാതി)
  - owls → parliament (മൂങ്ങകൾ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Animal collective nouns PSC-ൽ guaranteed question — ഓരോ exam-ലും ഉണ്ടാകും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A pack of wolves ran through the forest. (ഒരു കൂട്ടം ചെന്നായ്ക്കൾ)
  2. A pride of lions rested under the tree. (ഒരു കൂട്ടം സിംഹങ്ങൾ)
  3. A flock of birds flew overhead. (ഒരു കൂട്ടം പക്ഷികൾ)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) pride
• 'Pride' = lions-ന്റെ collective noun — 'a pride of lions' ആണ് correct usage.
• Wolves-ന് 'pride' ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — ഇത് exclusively lions-ന്റേതാണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Pride' familiar word + lion/wolf both wild animals → students confuse ചെയ്യും. ഓർക്കുക: Lions = pride, Wolves = pack. ഒരിക്കലും swap ആകരുത്!

❌ (C) flock
• 'Flock' = birds-ന്റെ അല്ലെങ്കിൽ sheep-ന്റെ collective noun — 'a flock of birds / a flock of sheep' ആണ് correct.
• Wolves-ന് 'flock' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — wolves wild predators ആണ്, birds/sheep-ന്റെ term wolves-ന് apply ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Flock' = group feel → general sense-ൽ students choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ ഓരോ animal-ഉം specific word ആണ്!

❌ (D) herd
• 'Herd' = cattle, elephants, horses പോലുള്ള large animals-ന്റെ collective noun — 'a herd of cattle / a herd of elephants' ആണ്.
• Wolves-ന് 'herd' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — herd = domesticated/large herbivores, wolves = wild predators.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Herd' = large group feel → wolves-ഉം large animals ആണ് → students confuse ചെയ്യും. പക്ഷേ herd = cattle/elephants only.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Wolves travel in a PACK like a backPACK — they carry their pack everywhere!' — ചെന്നായ്ക്കൾ together travel ചെയ്യുന്നു, like a packed backpack. Pack of wolves = backpack connection — ഒരിക്കലും forget ആകില്ല!
• Animal collective noun shortlist PSC top picks:
  - PACK = wolves / dogs / cards
  - PRIDE = lions
  - FLOCK = birds / sheep
  - HERD = cattle / elephants
  - SWARM = bees
  - SCHOOL = fish
• PSC exam strategy: 'Wolves' കണ്ടാൽ → ഉടൻ 'pack' choose ചെയ്യുക. ഈ list memorize ചെയ്‌തിരിക്കുക — collective noun questions PSC-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകരുത്. Exam-ൽ ഈ list ഓർത്ത് 5 seconds-ൽ answer ആക്കാം!

questionText: Identify the synonym for 'amendment'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) modification

• 'Amendment' = ഒരു law, document, system-ൽ വരുത്തുന്ന official change — 'ഭേദഗതി, മാറ്റം, തിരുത്ത്'.
• 'Modification' = ഒരു കാര്യം better ആക്കാൻ വരുത്തുന്ന change/adjustment — 'മാറ്റം, ക്രമീകരണം'.
• Amendment = Modification — രണ്ടും change indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു, synonyms ആണ്.
• Constitutional amendment, bill amendment-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന formal word.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'The amendment to the constitution was passed' = 'ഭരണഘടനയ്ക്ക് ഭേദഗതി pass ആയി.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Amendment = official change/modification — ഭേദഗതി
• 'Amend' word family:
  - amend (verb) = മാറ്റം വരുത്തുക, തിരുത്തുക
  - amendment (noun) = ഭേദഗതി, official change
  - amendable (adjective) = മാറ്റം വരുത്താൻ കഴിയുന്ന
• 'Amendment'-ന്റെ synonym family:
  - modification (ക്രമീകരണം, ഭേദഗതി)
  - revision (പുനരവലോകനം)
  - alteration (മാറ്റം)
  - correction (തിരുത്ത്)
  - reform (പരിഷ്‌കരണം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Amendment' PSC-ൽ frequently asked synonym — government/law context-ൽ common word!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The amendment improved the existing law. (ഭേദഗതി നിലവിലുള്ള നിയമം മെച്ചപ്പെടുത്തി)
  2. Several modifications were made to the original plan. (original plan-ൽ നിരവധി മാറ്റങ്ങൾ വരുത്തി)
  3. The constitutional amendment was debated in Parliament. (ഭരണഘടന ഭേദഗതി Parliament-ൽ ചർച്ച ചെയ്‌തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) reluctant
• 'Reluctant' = 'മടിയുള്ള, ഇഷ്ടമില്ലാതെ ചെയ്യുന്ന' — 'amendment'-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 'She was reluctant to speak' = 'അവൾക്ക് സംസാരിക്കാൻ മടി ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നു' — ഇത് attitude describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Reluctant' formal English word → familiar sound ഇല്ലാത്ത students guess ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ meaning completely different!

❌ (B) hesitant
• 'Hesitant' = 'മടിക്കുന്ന, uncertain-ആയ' — ഇതും attitude/behaviour describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjective ആണ്.
• 'Reluctant'-ഉം 'hesitant'-ഉം ഒരേ domain-ൽ ഉള്ളവ ആണ് — 'amendment'-ന്റെ synonyms അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Reluctant' and 'hesitant' രണ്ടും similar meaning ആണ് (both = unwilling/uncertain) → students ഒരെണ്ണം choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ amendment = change, ഇവ = attitude!

❌ (D) worsen
• 'Worsen' = 'കൂടുതൽ മോശമാകുക, deteriorate ആകുക' — ഇത് amendment-ന്റെ antonym direction-ൽ ആണ്.
• Amendment = improve/change something. Worsen = make it worse. Opposite direction!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Worsen' = change feel ആകാം (things change when they worsen). പക്ഷേ amendment = positive change, worsen = negative change — opposite ആണ്!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'AMEND = A MEND — ഒരു കേടുപാടു MEND ചെയ്യുക, repair ചെയ്യുക!' — 'Mend' = repair/fix. 'Amend' = fix/improve a law or document. AMEND = A + MEND = fixing something. Modification = another word for fixing/changing. ഒരിക്കൽ ഈ connection ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ forget ആകില്ല!
• Amendment synonyms ഒരുമിച്ച് memorize ചെയ്യുക: modification / revision / alteration / correction / reform — ഇവ ഏതു context-ലും 'amendment'-ന് replace ആകും.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Amendment' question-ൽ 'change / improve / correct' meaning ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക. Attitude words (reluctant, hesitant) — ഉടൻ eliminate. Negative words (worsen) — ഉടൻ eliminate. 'Modification' = change = amendment. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Identify the antonym for 'deficit'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) surplus

• 'Deficit' = കുറവ്, shortage — ആവശ്യമുള്ളതിലും കുറഞ്ഞ amount. 'Budget deficit' = spending > income.
• 'Surplus' = അധികം, excess — ആവശ്യമുള്ളതിലും കൂടുതൽ amount. 'Budget surplus' = income > spending.
• Deficit (കുറവ്) ↔ Surplus (അധികം) — perfect antonyms.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'The country has a trade deficit' = 'ആ രാജ്യത്തിന് വ്യാപാര കമ്മി ഉണ്ട്.' 'They have a surplus of food' = 'അവർക്ക് ഭക്ഷണം അധികം ഉണ്ട്.'
• Finance, economics context-ൽ ഈ pair PSC-ൽ very important ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Deficit = LESS than needed (കുറവ്) ↔ Surplus = MORE than needed (അധികം)
• 'Deficit' word family:
  - deficit (noun) = കമ്മി, shortage
  - deficient (adjective) = കുറവുള്ള, lacking
  - deficiency (noun) = കുറവ്, lack
  - Example: 'Vitamin deficiency' = 'വിറ്റാമിൻ കുറവ്'
• 'Surplus' word family:
  - surplus (noun/adjective) = അധിക്യം, excess
  - surpass (verb) = കവിഞ്ഞ് പോകുക (related root)
  - Example: 'Surplus food' = 'അധിക ഭക്ഷണം'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Deficit ↔ Surplus' antonym pair government exam-ൽ — especially Kerala PSC-ൽ — very frequently asked!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The government faced a budget deficit this year. (ഈ വർഷം സർക്കാർ ബജറ്റ് കമ്മി നേരിട്ടു)
  2. Good rainfall created a food surplus. (നല്ല മഴ ഭക്ഷ്യ അധിക്യം ഉണ്ടാക്കി)
  3. A trade surplus means exports exceed imports. (trade surplus = export > import)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) smudge
• 'Smudge' = 'ഒരു dirty mark അല്ലെങ്കിൽ blot, ink smear' — 'deficit'-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 'There was a smudge on the paper' = 'കടലാസ്സിൽ ഒരു ink-ന്റെ കറ ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നു.' — finance-മായി ഒരു connection-ഉം ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Smudge' unfamiliar word → students 'deficit'-ന്റെ antonym ആകും എന്ന് guess ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. Meaning check ചെയ്‌തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ ഉടൻ eliminate ആകും.

❌ (C) reprise
• 'Reprise' = 'ഒരു music piece-ന്റെ repetition' — musical term ആണ്, finance term അല്ല.
• 'A reprise of the chorus' = 'chorus-ന്റെ ആവർത്തനം' — 'deficit'-ന്റെ antonym ആകാൻ വഴിയേ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Reprise' uncommon word → students confuse ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. Unfamiliar options-ൽ guess ചെയ്യുന്നത് ഒഴിവാക്കണം.

❌ (D) sippet
• 'Sippet' = 'soup-ൽ ഇടുന്ന bread-ന്റെ small piece' — ഒരു culinary (cooking) term ആണ്.
• 'Deficit'-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല — deliberately confusing option ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Sippet' very uncommon word → students completely unfamiliar → guess ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. Unfamiliar word = distractor option — 'surplus' clearly correct ആണ്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DEFICIT = DE-FICIENT = deficient ആണ്, കുറവ് ഉണ്ട്! SURPLUS = SUPER PLUS = super extra, കൂടുതൽ ഉണ്ട്!' — Deficit = something is deficient/lacking. Surplus = super plus = more than enough. കുറവ് ↔ അധികം — രണ്ടും finance words ആണ്.
• Finance antonym pairs PSC-ൽ important:
  - deficit ↔ surplus (കമ്മി ↔ അധിക്യം)
  - loss ↔ profit (നഷ്ടം ↔ ലാഭം)
  - debit ↔ credit (ഡെബിറ്റ് ↔ ക്രെഡിറ്റ്)
  - expenditure ↔ income (ചെലവ് ↔ വരുമാനം)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Deficit' antonym question-ൽ finance/economics context ഓർക്കുക. 'Deficit = shortage/less' → antonym = 'excess/more'. 'Surplus' = excess. Unfamiliar words (smudge, reprise, sippet) ഉടൻ eliminate ചെയ്‌ത് 'surplus' choose ചെയ്യുക!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable preposition.
There was nothing importunate ————— him.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) about

• 'Importunate' = ശല്യപ്പെടുത്തുന്ന, നിരന്തരം demand ചെയ്യുന്ന — persistently demanding, annoying.
• 'Nothing importunate about him' = 'അദ്ദേഹത്തെക്കുറിച്ച് (about) ശല്യകരമായ ഒന്നും ഇല്ല' — ഇവിടെ 'about' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് ഒരു quality/characteristic describe ചെയ്യാൻ ആണ്.
• 'About' = ഒരാളുടെ character/nature describe ചെയ്യുന്ന adjectives-ന് ശേഷം വരുന്ന fixed preposition.
• Meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹത്തിൽ ശല്യകരമായ ഒന്നും ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നില്ല.'
• ഇതേ pattern: 'There is something strange about her' = 'അവളെക്കുറിച്ച് എന്തോ അസ്വാഭാവികം ഉണ്ട്.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'There is/was something/nothing + adjective + ABOUT + person' — ഒരാളുടെ character describe ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'about' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'About' preposition — character/quality describe ചെയ്യുന്ന contexts:
  - 'There is something suspicious about him.' (അദ്ദേഹത്തെക്കുറിച്ച് സംശയകരമായ എന്തോ ഉണ്ട്)
  - 'There is something charming about her.' (അവളെക്കുറിച്ച് ആകർഷണീയമായ എന്തോ ഉണ്ട്)
  - 'There is nothing unusual about this situation.' (ഈ സ്ഥിതിയിൽ അസാധാരണമായി ഒന്നുമില്ല)
• Adjective + preposition collocations 'about' ഉള്ളവ:
  - worried about (ആശങ്കയുള്ള)
  - careful about (ശ്രദ്ധയുള്ള)
  - curious about (ജിജ്ഞാസയുള്ള)
  - nervous about (ആശങ്കയ്ക്കു ഉള്ള)
  - enthusiastic about (ഉത്സാഹമുള്ള)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'There is something/nothing + adjective + about' pattern PSC-ൽ tricky preposition question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. There was nothing extraordinary about his appearance. (അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ രൂപത്തിൽ അസാധാരണമായി ഒന്നുമില്ലായിരുന്നു)
  2. There is something mysterious about that old house. (ആ പഴയ വീടിനെ കുറിച്ച് ദുഗൂഹമായ എന്തോ ഉണ്ട്)
  3. There was nothing unusual about her behaviour. (അവളുടെ പെരുമാറ്റത്തിൽ അസ്വാഭാവികമായി ഒന്നും ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നില്ല)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) on
• 'Nothing importunate on him' = ഈ combination standard English-ൽ ഇല്ല. 'On' = surface/topic indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു — character description-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'On' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നത് ഇങ്ങനെ: 'dependent on', 'insist on', 'focus on' — character trait description-ന് 'on' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'On him' = 'അദ്ദേഹത്തിൽ' natural feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ character describe ചെയ്യാൻ 'about' ആണ് ശരി.

❌ (B) at
• 'Nothing importunate at him' = grammatically incorrect combination. 'At' = specific point/direction indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'at school', 'at him' (angry at him).
• Character trait description-ൽ 'at' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Angry at him', 'look at him' patterns ഓർത്ത് 'at' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ 'importunate at' = wrong combination.

❌ (C) by
• 'Nothing importunate by him' = agent indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന passive voice pattern feel ആകും — 'done by him' like.
• Passive voice context-ൽ 'by' ഉപയോഗിക്കാം, character description-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'By him' = 'അദ്ദേഹത്താൽ' passive feel → students confuse ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'ABOUT = Around/surrounding a person — character ആ person-നെ SURROUND ചെയ്യുന്നു!' — ഒരാളിന്റെ character, quality describe ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'about' = around that person. 'Something about him' = his character surrounds him.
• 'About' character description pattern: 'There is [something/nothing] [adjective] ABOUT [person]' — ഈ formula memorize ചെയ്‌താൽ similar questions-ൽ ഒരിക്കലും confuse ആകില്ല.
• PSC exam strategy: Blank-ൽ ഒരു person-ന്റെ quality/character describe ആകുന്ന sentence-ൽ → 'about' choose ചെയ്യുക. 'There was nothing [adjective] ___ him' pattern-ൽ answer = 'about'. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Use an appropriate article.
In many countries, it is important to have ————— heir to carry on the family name.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) an

• 'Heir' pronunciation: /ɛər/ — ഇവിടെ 'h' silent ആണ്! Sound ആരംഭിക്കുന്നത് vowel sound /ɛ/ ആണ്.
• Article rule: SOUND-based, letter-based അല്ല — vowel sound ആദ്യം ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'H' silent ആയ words: heir, hour, honest, honour, herb (British) — ഇവ എല്ലാം 'an' ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• 'An heir' ✓ — vowel sound /ɛ/ ആദ്യം ആയതിനാൽ 'an' correct.
• Meaning: 'കുടുംബ name തുടരാൻ ഒരു അനന്തരാവകാശി (heir) ഉണ്ടാകണം.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: a/an = SOUND rule — letter നോക്കാതെ sound നോക്കുക!
  - Vowel SOUND (/a,e,i,o,u/ sound) → 'an'
  - Consonant SOUND → 'a'
• 'H' silent ആയ PSC important words (an ഉപയോഗിക്കും):
  - an heir (അനന്തരാവകാശി) — h silent
  - an hour (ഒരു മണിക്കൂർ) — h silent
  - an honest man (ഒരു സത്യസന്ധൻ) — h silent
  - an honour (ഒരു ബഹുമതി) — h silent
• 'H' ഉള്ളതും 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന words (h pronounced):
  - a house, a hat, a hotel, a hospital, a hero
• Vowel letter ഉണ്ടെങ്കിലും 'a' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്ന words (consonant sound):
  - a university (/juː/ sound), a European, a one-time offer, a year
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Silent 'h' article trap (an heir, an hour, an honest) PSC-ൽ favourite question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. He waited for an hour. (h silent → an)
  2. She is an honest worker. (h silent → an)
  3. The king has an heir to the throne. (h silent → an)
  4. It was an honour to meet him. (h silent → an)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) a
• 'A heir' — ഇത് ഏറ്റവും common error ആണ്! 'H' letter ആദ്യം കണ്ട് 'a' choose ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'Heir' letter 'H'-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്നു, പക്ഷേ sound /ɛ/ (vowel) ആണ് — rule: sound ആണ് matter, letter അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'H = consonant letter → a heir' ആകും എന്ന് students automatically think. പക്ഷേ 'heir'-ൽ H silent! ഏറ്റവും classic PSC article trap!

❌ (C) the
• 'The heir' = specific, already known heir-ഇനെ indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു. Sentence-ൽ 'an heir' = any heir (non-specific, first mention).
• 'The' = definite (specific/known). 'An' = indefinite (non-specific). ഇവിടെ general statement ആണ് — non-specific heir.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'The heir' formal/important feel → royal family association → 'the' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ non-specific → 'an'.

❌ (D) no article
• Article ഇല്ലാതെ 'have heir' = grammatically incomplete. Singular countable noun-ന് മുൻപ് article mandatory ആണ്.
• 'Heir' = singular countable noun → article (a/an/the) required.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ചിലർ article skip ചെയ്‌ത് 'have heir' correct ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകും. Singular nouns-ന് article mandatory!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HEIR = AIR — both sound the same, both start with vowel sound!' — 'Heir' sound ചെയ്‌താൽ 'air' പോലെ ആണ്! 'An air of mystery' = 'an heir to the throne' — 'an' use ചെയ്യുന്നു കാരണം /ɛ/ vowel sound ആദ്യം ആണ്!
• H-silent family (PSC must memorize): heir, hour, honest, honour, herb (British) — ഇവ 5 words PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ചോദിക്കും. ഈ 5 words 'an' ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ടത്.
• PSC exam strategy: 'H' letter ആദ്യം ഉള്ള words-ൽ article question വന്നാൽ — ആദ്യം sound check ചെയ്യുക. 'Heir, hour, honest, honour' = h silent = 'an'. 'House, hat, hero' = h pronounced = 'a'. Sound test = correct answer!

questionText: Which of the following is correctly spelt?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Quintessential

• 'Quintessential' = ഏറ്റവും perfect example ആയ, most typical — 'ഒരു കാര്യത്തിന്റെ ഏറ്റവും നല്ല ഉദാഹരണം'.
• ശരിയായ spelling: Q-U-I-N-T-E-S-S-E-N-T-I-A-L — 'quint' + 'ess' + 'ential'.
• Etymology: Latin 'quinta essentia' = fifth essence (ancient belief: earth, water, fire, air + fifth element = perfect substance).
• Middle-ൽ double 's' ഉണ്ടെന്ന് ഓർക്കുക: 'quintESSential' — 'ESS' ആണ് middle part.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'She is the quintessential professional' = 'അവൾ ഒരു professional-ന്റെ ഏറ്റവും perfect ഉദാഹരണമാണ്.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: QUINT + ESS + ENTIAL = QUINTESSENTIAL. 'Quint' (five) + 'ess' (double s) + 'ential' (suffix).
• ഓരോ wrong option-ലെ error:
  - Quitenssential: 'quint' → 'quite+n' ആയി — 'n' position wrong, 'ss' position wrong
  - Quintesential: middle-ൽ single 's' — double 'ss' missing (quintESsential ആണ് ശരി)
  - Quetenssential: 'quint' → 'queten' — 'i' missing, letters rearranged
• '-ential' suffix ഉള്ള PSC important words:
  - quintessential (ഏറ്റവും typical)
  - essential (അത്യാവശ്യം)
  - potential (സാധ്യത)
  - credential (യോഗ്യത)
  - existential (അസ്തിത്വ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Quintessential' spelling trap PSC-ൽ popular question — double 's' confuse ആക്കും!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Cricket is quintessential to Indian culture. (Cricket ഇന്ത്യൻ സംസ്‌കാരത്തിന്റെ ഏറ്റവും representative element ആണ്)
  2. She is the quintessential teacher — patient and knowledgeable. (അവൾ ഒരു teacher-ന്റെ perfect ഉദാഹരണം)
  3. The Taj Mahal is the quintessential symbol of love. (Taj Mahal സ്‌നേഹത്തിന്റെ ഏറ്റവും perfect symbol ആണ്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Quitenssential
• 'Quint' → 'Quiten' — 'n' wrong position-ലേക്ക് move ആയി. 'Quint' ആണ് ശരി (q-u-i-n-t order).
• 'ss' correct ആണ്, പക്ഷേ 'quint' section-ൽ error ഉണ്ട്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Double 'ss' ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'correct' feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ 'quiten' = wrong — 'quint' ആണ് ശരി.

❌ (C) Quintesential
• 'quintESential' — middle-ൽ single 's' മാത്രം. ശരി 'quintESSential' — double 'ss' ആണ്.
• 'ESS' part-ൽ ഒരു 's' missing ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: ഏറ്റവും common error! Single 's' vs double 'ss' — ഉച്ചാരണത്തിൽ difference ഇല്ലാത്തതിനാൽ students single 's' ഇടും. 'ESS' = double 's' ഓർക്കുക!

❌ (D) Quetenssential
• 'Queten' = completely wrong beginning. 'Quint' (q-u-i-n-t) ആണ് ശരി, 'queten' (q-u-e-t-e-n) wrong.
• Multiple errors: 'i' → 'e', letters rearranged.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'ss' correct ഉണ്ട്, പക്ഷേ beginning-ൽ 'queten' = completely wrong. Sound-based guessing ആണ് ഈ error ഉണ്ടാകുന്നത്.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'QUINT (5) + ESS (double S) + ENTIAL = QUINTESSENTIAL!' — 'Quint' = five (quintet = 5 singers, quintuplets = 5 babies). 'ESS' = like the letter S said twice. Middle-ൽ 'ESS' imagine ചെയ്‌താൽ double 's' ഒരിക്കലും miss ആകില്ല!
• Breaking it down ഓർക്കാൻ: QUINT-ESS-ENTIAL = (5)(SS)(suffix). 'Quintessential' = the FIVE-NESS of something = the most essential/perfect form.
• PSC exam strategy: Spelling questions-ൽ letter-by-letter compare ചെയ്യുക. 'Quintessential' = Q-U-I-N-T-E-S-S-E-N-T-I-A-L. Middle-ൽ double 'ss' ഉണ്ടോ confirm ചെയ്‌ത് (C) eliminate. 'Quint' ആദ്യം correct ആണോ confirm ചെയ്‌ത് (B)(D) eliminate. (A) = correct!

questionText:
Everybody has avoided discussing the critical issues during the meeting ——————?
Choose the most suitable question tag from the options given.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) haven't they?

• Subject: 'Everybody' — ഇത് singular noun ആണ്, പക്ഷേ question tag-ൽ 'they' pronoun ഉപയോഗിക്കണം (modern English rule).
• 'Everybody/everyone/someone/nobody' → pronoun in tag = 'they' (singular meaning, plural tag pronoun).
• Sentence positive ആണ് ('has avoided') → negative tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• Auxiliary 'has' → tag-ൽ 'have' (plural 'they'-ന് 'have' ആണ് auxiliary).
• Negative tag: 'haven't they?' ✓
• Meaning: 'Meeting-ൽ critical issues discuss ചെയ്യുന്നത് എല്ലാവരും ഒഴിവാക്കി, അല്ലേ?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Everybody/Everyone/Someone/Nobody → question tag pronoun = 'they'
• Indefinite pronouns → tag pronoun rules:
  - everybody/everyone → haven't they?
  - somebody/someone → haven't they?
  - nobody/no one → have they? (nobody = negative → positive tag)
  - anybody/anyone → have they? (hardly any = negative feel)
• Positive sentence → negative tag:
  - 'Everybody has...' (positive) → 'haven't they?' (negative) ✓
• Tag auxiliary agreement with 'they':
  - 'has' in sentence (singular) → 'have' in tag (because tag subject = 'they', plural)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Everybody → they' tag rule PSC-ൽ classic tricky question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Everybody loves a good story, don't they? (everybody → they)
  2. Someone has taken my pen, haven't they? (someone → they)
  3. Nobody came to the meeting, did they? (nobody = negative → positive tag, they)
  4. Everyone enjoyed the party, didn't they? (everyone → they)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) has anybody
• 'Has anybody' = question form ആണ്, tag form അല്ല. Question tags short auxiliary + pronoun ആണ് (hasn't it / haven't they).
• Plus 'anybody' = wrong pronoun for 'everybody' tag — 'they' ആണ് ഉപയോഗിക്കേണ്ടത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Everybody has...' → question form 'has anybody?' ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകും. പക്ഷേ tag = hasn't/haven't + pronoun.

❌ (B) hasn't it
• 'It' = neutral singular pronoun — 'everybody' ഒരിക്കലും 'it' ആകില്ല (people-ന് 'it' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല).
• Modern English-ൽ 'everybody' → 'they'. 'It' = things/animals (non-human).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Everybody' singular → 'it' (singular pronoun) choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ people = they, never it!

❌ (D) have they
• 'Have they' = positive tag. Sentence 'Everybody has avoided' = positive sentence → NEGATIVE tag ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• Positive sentence + positive tag = wrong rule.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'They' pronoun correct ആണ്! പക്ഷേ 'have they' positive tag → positive sentence-ന് negative tag ആണ് വേണ്ടത് → 'haven't they' ✓.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'EVERY-BODY has a BODY → they are PEOPLE → THEY! Everybody = many people together → THEY!' — 'Everybody' ഉള്ളിൽ many people ഉണ്ട് → tag-ൽ 'they'. Simple logic!
• Indefinite pronoun → they family: everybody, everyone, somebody, someone, nobody, no one, anybody, anyone — ഇവ എല്ലാം 'they' tag ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Everybody/Someone/Nobody' subject ആണെങ്കിൽ → tag pronoun = 'they'. Positive sentence → negative tag ('haven't they'). Negative sentence → positive tag ('have they'). Two-step check = guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable phrasal verb.
The sailor was ————— on a remote island after the storm wrecked his ship.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) cast away

• 'Cast away' = കടലിൽ shipwreck ആയ ശേഷം ഒരു remote island-ൽ തനിച്ചാകുക — stranded after a shipwreck.
• 'The sailor was cast away on a remote island' = 'കപ്പൽ തകർന്ന ശേഷം sailor ഒരു ഒറ്റപ്പെട്ട ദ്വീപിൽ stranded ആയി.'
