• Famous usage: 'Cast Away' (2000) Tom Hanks movie — ഒരാൾ island-ൽ stranded ആകുന്ന story.
• 'Castaways' = shipwreck-ൽ island-ൽ ഒറ്റപ്പെടുന്ന ആളുകൾ (noun form).
• Meaning: 'കൊടുങ്കാറ്റ് കപ്പൽ തകർത്ത ശേഷം sailor ഒരു ഒറ്റപ്പെട്ട island-ൽ ഉപേക്ഷിക്കപ്പെട്ടു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'Cast away' = island-ൽ stranded ആകുക (shipwreck context)
• 'Cast' phrasal verb family (PSC must-know):
  - cast away = shipwreck-ൽ island-ൽ ഒറ്റപ്പെടുക, strand (കടലിൽ ദുരന്തം)
  - cast aside = reject ചെയ്‌ത് വലിച്ചെറിയുക (discard, ignore)
  - cast off = കെട്ടഴിക്കുക (untie ropes of a ship) / shed (snake casts off skin)
  - cast out = exile/expel ചെയ്യുക (ഒരാളെ community-ൽ നിന്ന് പുറത്താക്കുക)
• 'Away' particle meaning: DISTANCE + SEPARATION — 'cast away' = thrown far away (abandoned on island).
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Cast' phrasal verb family PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ ചോദിക്കുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The survivors were cast away on a deserted island. (shipwreck survivors island-ൽ ഒറ്റപ്പെട്ടു)
  2. She cast aside her old clothes. (പഴയ വസ്ത്രങ്ങൾ discard ചെയ്‌തു)
  3. The ship cast off from the harbour. (harbour-ൽ നിന്ന് കെട്ടഴിച്ച് departure ആയി)
  4. He was cast out by his community. (community-ൽ നിന്ന് exile ആക്കി)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) cast aside
• 'Cast aside' = discard/reject ചെയ്‌ത് ignore ചെയ്യുക — 'cast aside her feelings' (feelings ignore ചെയ്‌തു).
• Island-ൽ stranded ആകുന്ന meaning ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല. Emotionally/socially reject ചെയ്യുക ആണ് meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Cast' same base verb → options similar feel → confuse ആകും. 'Aside' = to one side (discard), 'away' = far distance (stranded). Context = storm/island → 'away'.

❌ (C) cast off
• 'Cast off' = ship/boat-ന്റെ ropes untie ചെയ്‌ത് journey start ചെയ്യുക, അല്ലെങ്കിൽ skin/clothes shed ചെയ്യുക.
• 'The ship cast off at dawn' (dawn-ൽ journey start ആയി). Stranded ആകുന്ന meaning ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Nautical (ship) context ഉള്ളതിനാൽ 'cast off' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. Ship context ശരി, പക്ഷേ meaning = departure (not stranded).

❌ (D) cast out
• 'Cast out' = ഒരാളെ group/society-ൽ നിന്ന് expel/exile ചെയ്യുക — social exclusion.
• 'He was cast out of the village' (village-ൽ നിന്ন് exile ആക്കി). Island-ൽ ഒറ്റപ്പെടുക ≠ society-ൽ നിന്ന് expel ചെയ്യുക.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Cast out on island' = island-ൽ നിന്ന് exile ആക്കി feel → 'cast out' choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ storm+shipwreck context = 'cast away'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CAST AWAY = THROWN AWAY INTO THE OCEAN — storm throws the sailor FAR AWAY onto an island!' — Storm = cast + away (far distance). Sailor thrown far away = stranded. Tom Hanks movie 'Cast Away' ഓർക്കുക — exactly ഈ meaning!
• 'Cast' family quick guide:
  - cast AWAY = storm/shipwreck → island stranded (ദൂരെ കടലിൽ)
  - cast ASIDE = emotionally/socially reject ചെയ്യുക (ഒഴിവാക്കുക)
  - cast OFF = ship depart / shed skin (departure)
  - cast OUT = community exile ചെയ്യുക (പുറത്താക്കുക)
• PSC exam strategy: Shipwreck + island + storm context = always 'cast away'. Context keyword: storm wrecked + remote island = 'cast away'. Tom Hanks movie connection ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഒരിക്കലും wrong answer ആകില്ല!

questionText:
Choose the word that can substitute the given group of words.
'A student with highest rank in a class'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Valedictorian

• 'Valedictorian' = class-ൽ ഏറ്റവും ഉയർന്ന rank ഉള്ള student — graduation ceremony-ൽ farewell speech deliver ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.
• Etymology: Latin 'valedicere' (vale = farewell + dicere = to say) + '-orian' (person who does) = 'farewell speech ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ' — highest rank ഉള്ള student ആണ് ഈ ബഹുമതി ലഭിക്കുന്നത്.
• അർഥം: 'ക്ലാസ്സിൽ ഒന്നാം rank ഉള്ള student, graduation-ൽ farewell speech ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ.'
• American education system-ൽ ഈ term widely ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'She was the valedictorian of her graduating class.' = 'അവൾ ബിരുദ ക്ലാസ്സിലെ ഒന്നാം rank ആയിരുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: VALE (farewell) + DICT (say/speak) + ORIAN (person) = VALEDICTORIAN — 'farewell speech say ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ' = ക്ലാസ്സ് topper!
• '-arian / -orian' suffix ഉള്ള one word substitutes (PSC must-know):
  - valedictorian = highest rank student (ക്ലാസ്സ് topper)
  - veterinarian = animal doctor (മൃഗ ഡോക്ടർ)
  - agrarian = farming related (കൃഷി സംബന്ധിച്ച)
  - humanitarian = human welfare worker (മനുഷ്യസ്‌നേഹി)
  - authoritarian = strict ruler (ഏകാധിപതി)
  - librarian = library worker (ഗ്രന്ഥശാലാ ജോലിക്കാരൻ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: '-arian' suffix one word substitutes PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്കിടെ വരുന്ന topic!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The valedictorian delivered an inspiring speech at graduation. (ഒന്നാം rank student inspiring speech ചെയ്‌തു)
  2. A veterinarian treated the injured dog. (മൃഗ ഡോക്ടർ injured dog-നെ treat ചെയ്‌തു)
  3. The agrarian community depends on monsoon rains. (കൃഷിക്കാർ monsoon-ഉം ആശ്രയിക്കുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Veterinarian
• 'Veterinarian' = മൃഗ ഡോക്ടർ (animal doctor) — 'highest rank student'-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 'Vet' = veterinarian-ന്റെ short form — animals treat ചെയ്യുന്ന doctor.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Veterinarian' + 'Valedictorian' — spelling similar, both '-arian' ending → confuse ആകും! 'Veter' = animal related, 'Valedic' = farewell/speech related — ഇവ distinguish ചെയ്യണം.

❌ (C) Agrarian
• 'Agrarian' = കൃഷി സംബന്ധിച്ച, farming related — 'agrarian society' = farming community.
• ഇത് adjective ആണ്, student rank-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-arian' ending familiar → options confuse ആക്കും. 'Agri' = agriculture/farming ഓർത്താൽ ഇത് eliminate ആകും.

❌ (D) Presbyterian
• 'Presbyterian' = ഒരു Christian church denomination-ന്റെ member/follower — religious term ആണ്.
• Student rank-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-arian' ending → similar feel. 'Presb' = elder/church elder → religious context ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ ഉടൻ eliminate ആകും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'VALE-DICT-ORIAN = VALE (bye!) + DICT (speech) = the student who says BYE with a SPEECH at graduation — the TOP student!' — ഒന്നാം rank student graduation-ൽ goodbye speech ചെയ്യുന്നു. VALE (farewell) + DICT (say) = valedictorian!
• '-arian' family ഒരുമിച്ച് ഓർക്കുക: VALEdictorian (top student), VETERinarian (animal doctor), AGRarian (farming). ആദ്യ syllable ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ enough: VALE = school, VETER = animals, AGRI = farming.
• PSC exam strategy: 'Highest rank student' keyword കണ്ടാൽ → 'Valedictorian' choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Val' sound = value/farewell — class-ൽ most valuable student farewell speech ചെയ്യുന്നു. ഒരിക്കൽ etymology ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: Which of the following words is not a compound word?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) playful

• 'Playful' = 'play' (verb/noun) + '-ful' (suffix) — ഇവിടെ '-ful' ഒരു independent word അല്ല, ഒരു suffix ആണ്.
• Compound word = രണ്ട് independent words ചേർന്ന് ഉണ്ടാകുന്ന word. 'Ful' ഒരിക്കലും alone stand ചെയ്യില്ല.
• 'Playful' = 'play' + suffix '-ful' = derived word (suffix-formed word), compound word അല്ല.
• മറ്റ് options compound words ആണ്: week+end, back+pack, sun+shine — ഇവ രണ്ടും independent words.
• 'Playful' ഒരു suffix (-ful) ഉപയോഗിച്ച് ഉണ്ടാക്കിയ derived word ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Compound word = WORD + WORD (രണ്ടും independent). Derived word = WORD + SUFFIX/PREFIX (suffix/prefix alone stand ചെയ്യില്ല).
• Compound words — ഓരോ part-ഉം independent word ആണ്:
  - weekend = week (ആഴ്‌ച) + end (അവസാനം) ✓ compound
  - backpack = back (പുറം) + pack (bag) ✓ compound
  - sunshine = sun (സൂര്യൻ) + shine (തിളക്കം) ✓ compound
  - rainfall, notebook, football, bedroom, doorbell — ഇവ compound words ✓
• Derived words — suffix/prefix ഉള്ളവ (compound words അല്ല):
  - playful = play + '-ful' suffix (cheerful, hopeful, careful — all derived)
  - kindness = kind + '-ness' suffix
  - teacher = teach + '-er' suffix
  - unhappy = 'un-' prefix + happy
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Compound word vs derived word distinction PSC-ൽ word formation question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Weekend is a compound word — both 'week' and 'end' stand alone.
  2. 'Playful' is a derived word — 'ful' cannot stand alone as a word.
  3. Sunshine = sun + shine, both are independent words.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) weekend
• 'Weekend' = 'week' + 'end' — രണ്ടും independent words ആണ്. 'Week' alone = ആഴ്‌ച, 'end' alone = അവസാനം.
• ഇത് compound word ആണ്, question 'NOT a compound word' ചോദിക്കുന്നു → eliminate.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Weekend' one word ആയി write ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ compound word ആണ് എന്ന് students miss ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ week + end = compound ✓.

❌ (B) backpack
• 'Backpack' = 'back' + 'pack' — 'back' alone = പുറം, 'pack' alone = bag/bundle. രണ്ടും independent.
• ഇത് compound word ആണ് → question-ന്റെ answer അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Backpack' common everyday word → students analyse ചെയ്‌ത് നോക്കില്ല. 'Back' + 'pack' = two words = compound ✓.

❌ (C) sunshine
• 'Sunshine' = 'sun' + 'shine' — 'sun' alone = സൂര്യൻ, 'shine' alone = തിളക്കം. രണ്ടും independent.
• ഇത് compound word ആണ് → question-ന്റെ answer അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Shine' = verb ആണ്, compound-ൽ noun/verb mixing ആകുമോ എന്ന് students confuse ആകും. Compound words-ൽ verb + noun, noun + noun — ഏതു combination-ഉം possible.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Compound word = MARRIAGE of two INDEPENDENT words! Each can live ALONE!' — Weekend = week lives alone + end lives alone → they got MARRIED = compound word. Playful = play lives alone, but '-ful' CANNOT live alone → not a marriage, just a decoration (suffix)!
• Test: ഓരോ part-ഉം alone sentence-ൽ use ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയുമോ? Yes + Yes = compound. Yes + No = derived (not compound).
  - week ✓ + end ✓ = compound (weekend)
  - play ✓ + ful ✗ = derived (playful) — 'ful' alone ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല!
• PSC exam strategy: Options-ൽ '-ful, -ness, -less, -ly, -er, -tion' suffix ഉള്ള option = NOT compound word. ഉടൻ ആ option choose ചെയ്യുക. Suffix words = derived words, compound words അല്ല. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Choose the correct combination that shows noun to adjective transformation with a suffix.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Friend-Friendly

• 'Friend' = noun (സുഹൃത്ത്). 'Friendly' = adjective (സൗഹൃദപരമായ).
• Transformation: Friend (noun) + '-ly' suffix → Friendly (adjective) ✓
• Question: noun → adjective transformation. 'Friend' noun ആണ്, 'friendly' adjective ആണ് — ഇത് correct ആണ്.
• '-ly' suffix adjective ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന context: friend → friendly, love → lovely, cost → costly, live → lively, man → manly.
• Meaning: 'Friend' (noun — ഒരു വ്യക്തി) → 'Friendly' (adjective — that person's quality, 'സൗഹൃദപരമായ').
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Noun + '-ly' → Adjective (ചില words-ൽ). Adjective + '-ly' → Adverb (most common '-ly' use).
• '-ly' suffix = mainly Adjective → Adverb. പക്ഷേ ചില nouns-ൽ '-ly' ചേർത്താൽ Adjective ഉണ്ടാകും:
  - friend (N) → friendly (Adj) ✓
  - love (N) → lovely (Adj)
  - cost (N) → costly (Adj)
  - time (N) → timely (Adj)
  - man (N) → manly (Adj)
  - brother (N) → brotherly (Adj)
  - father (N) → fatherly (Adj)
• Other options-ൽ transformation type:
  - Cheap (Adj) → Cheaply (Adv) = adjective to adverb
  - Hopeful (Adj) → Hopefully (Adv) = adjective to adverb
  - Fearless (Adj) → Fearlessly (Adv) = adjective to adverb
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Word transformation (noun→adjective, adjective→adverb) PSC-ൽ word formation question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has many friends and is very friendly. (friend=noun, friendly=adjective)
  2. The lovely flowers attracted everyone. (love=noun, lovely=adjective)
  3. He is a manly person. (man=noun, manly=adjective)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Cheap-Cheaply
• 'Cheap' = adjective (വില കുറഞ്ഞ). 'Cheaply' = adverb (cheaply bought = കുറഞ്ഞ വിലയ്ക്ക് വാങ്ങി).
• Transformation: Adjective → Adverb (adjective to adverb ആണ്, noun to adjective അല്ല).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: '-ly' suffix ഉള്ളതിനാൽ transformation feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ question 'noun → adjective' ചോദിക്കുന്നു — 'cheap' noun അല്ല, adjective ആണ്!

❌ (B) Hopeful-Hopefully
• 'Hopeful' = adjective (പ്രതീക്ഷയുള്ള). 'Hopefully' = adverb (hopefully = പ്രതീക്ഷയോടെ).
• Transformation: Adjective → Adverb. Noun → Adjective അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Hopeful' sounds like a transformation from 'hope'. 'Hope' (noun) → 'hopeful' (adjective) = noun to adjective! പക്ഷേ question 'Hopeful → Hopefully' ആണ് ചോദിക്കുന്നത് — ഇത് Adj → Adv, noun → adjective അല്ല.

❌ (C) Fearless-Fearlessly
• 'Fearless' = adjective (ഭയമില്ലാത്ത). 'Fearlessly' = adverb (ഭയമില്ലാതെ).
• Transformation: Adjective → Adverb. Noun → Adjective അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Fear' (noun) → 'Fearless' (adj) = noun to adjective! പക്ഷേ ഇവിടെ 'Fearless → Fearlessly' ആണ് pair — Adj → Adv ആണ് transformation.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'FRIEND is a PERSON (noun) → FRIENDLY is that person's QUALITY (adjective)! A friend makes you feel friendly — person → quality!' — ഒരു 'friend' (person/noun) ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ 'friendly' (quality/adjective) feel ആകും. Person → Quality = Noun → Adjective!
• '-ly' ഉപയോഗം ഓർക്കൽ:
  - Most common: Adj + '-ly' = Adverb (quick→quickly, slow→slowly)
  - Special case: Noun + '-ly' = Adjective (friend→friendly, love→lovely)
  - Test: First word noun ആണോ adjective ആണോ identify ചെയ്യുക. Noun + ly = adjective. Adjective + ly = adverb.
• PSC exam strategy: Question 'noun → adjective' ആണ്, അതിനാൽ FIRST word NOUN ആയ option choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Friend' = noun, 'Cheap' = adjective, 'Hopeful' = adjective, 'Fearless' = adjective → 'Friend' alone noun ആണ് → (D) ✓!

questionText:
Identify the underlined word with correct parts of speech.
The factory discharged its waste __downstream__ causing pollution in the nearby communities.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Adverb

• 'Downstream' = 'discharge' verb-നെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നു — 'ഏത് direction-ൽ discharge ചെയ്‌തു?' → downstream (നദിയുടെ flow direction-ൽ, അഥവാ താഴോട്ട്).
• Adverb = verb, adjective, or another adverb-ഇനെ modify ചെയ്യുന്ന word.
• ഇവിടെ 'downstream' = verb 'discharged'-ഇനെ modify ചെയ്യുന്ന adverb of place/direction.
• Test: 'Where was the waste discharged?' → 'Downstream.' — 'Where' question-ന് answer ആകുന്ന word = adverb of place.
• Meaning: 'Factory അതിന്റെ waste നദിയിലേക്ക് (downstream) discharge ചെയ്‌ത് nearby communities-ൽ pollution ഉണ്ടാക്കി.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'Downstream' = where? (direction) → Adverb of place.
• Adverb identify ചെയ്യാനുള്ള tests:
  - Where? → Adverb of place (downstream, here, there, outside)
  - When? → Adverb of time (yesterday, soon, now)
  - How? → Adverb of manner (quickly, carefully, well)
  - How much? → Adverb of degree (very, quite, almost)
• 'Downstream' = direction/place indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന adverb:
  - 'The boat drifted downstream.' (boat എങ്ങോട്ടു drifted? → downstream = adverb)
  - Antonym: upstream (നദിക്കെതിരെ, up direction)
• 'Downstream' parts of speech ഒരേ word-ൽ:
  - Adverb: 'The fish swam downstream.' (verb modify)
  - Adjective: 'The downstream communities suffered.' (noun modify)
  - ഇവിടെ 'discharged' verb-ഇനെ modify → adverb ✓
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of speech (adverb identification) PSC-ൽ regular question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The logs floated downstream. (downstream = verb modify, adverb)
  2. Pollution flowed downstream affecting many villages. (adverb)
  3. The fish swam upstream against the current. (upstream = adverb)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Noun
• Noun = ഒരു person, place, thing, or idea-ഇന്റെ name. 'Downstream' ഒരു name അല്ല.
• 'Downstream' ഇവിടെ direction indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന word ആണ്, sentence-ൽ noun position-ൽ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Downstream' = place name ആണ് (ഒരു location) → noun feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ ഇവിടെ verb 'discharged'-ഇനെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ adverb ആണ്.

❌ (B) Adjective
• Adjective = noun-ഇനെ modify ചെയ്യുന്ന word. 'Downstream' ഇവിടെ ഒരു noun-ഇനെ modify ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• 'The downstream communities' ആണ് adjective use — ഇവിടെ 'discharged downstream' ആണ്, verb modify ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Downstream communities' ഉദാഹരണം ഓർത്ത് adjective choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ ഈ sentence-ൽ verb modify = adverb.

❌ (D) Verb
• Verb = action or state indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന word. 'Downstream' ഒരു action അല്ല.
• Sentence-ൽ verb = 'discharged' (action ചെയ്‌തു). 'Downstream' action describe ചെയ്യുന്നില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Down' = go down feeling → 'stream down' = water flowing → verb feel. പക്ഷേ 'downstream' ഒരൊറ്റ word ആണ്, verb അല്ല.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DOWNSTREAM answers WHERE → WHERE = ADVERB!' — 'Factory discharged WHERE? → Downstream.' WHERE-ന്റെ answer = adverb of place. ഈ simple question test ഉപയോഗിച്ച് parts of speech identify ചെയ്യാം.
• Quick parts of speech test:
  - WHO/WHAT = Noun (factory, waste)
  - WHAT KIND = Adjective (nearby communities)
  - WHERE/WHEN/HOW = Adverb (downstream = where)
  - ACTION = Verb (discharged, causing)
• PSC exam strategy: Underlined word-ന്റെ function identify ചെയ്യുക. 'Downstream' = where? → adverb. 'Nearby' = what kind of communities? → adjective. Function test = part of speech. ഈ method ഉപയോഗിച്ചാൽ parts of speech questions-ൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText: Choose the one word substitution for 'A person who prepares and sells coffee'.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) Barista

• 'Barista' = coffee prepare ചെയ്‌ത് serve ചെയ്യുന്ന professional — 'കോഫി specialist'.
• Italian word 'barista' = bartender (bar-ൽ ജോലി ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ) — Italian-ൽ നിന്ന് English-ൽ coffee specialist ആയി narrowed ആയി.
• Barista skills: espresso, cappuccino, latte prepare ചെയ്യൽ — coffee machine operate ചെയ്യൽ.
• Starbucks, Café Coffee Day-ൽ ഉള്ള coffee staff = baristas.
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'The barista prepared a perfect cappuccino.' = 'Barista ഒരു perfect cappuccino ഉണ്ടാക്കി.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: BARISTA = BAR (serving place) + ISTA (Italian specialist suffix) = coffee specialist.
• Occupation one word substitutes (PSC must-know):
  - barista = coffee preparer/server (കോഫി specialist)
  - baker = bread/cake baker (അപ്പക്കാരൻ)
  - bartender = bar-ൽ drinks serve ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ
  - bricklayer = bricks ഉപയോഗിച്ച് wall കെട്ടുന്ന ആൾ
  - sommelier = wine expert/server (wine specialist)
  - confectioner = sweets/candy maker (മിഠായി കച്ചവടക്കാരൻ)
  - cobbler = shoe repairer (ചെരിപ്പ് repair ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ)
  - fletcher = arrow maker (അമ്പ് ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന ആൾ)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Occupation one word substitutes PSC-ൽ popular question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The barista made the best espresso in town. (barista = best coffee maker)
  2. The baker baked fresh bread every morning. (baker = bread maker)
  3. The bartender served drinks at the party. (bartender = drinks server)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Baker
• 'Baker' = bread, cake, pastry ഉണ്ടാക്കി വിൽക്കുന്ന ആൾ — coffee-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല.
• 'The baker sells bread and cakes.' (baker = bread/cake specialist) — coffee ഉണ്ടാക്കുന്ന ആൾ അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Café-ൽ coffee + baked goods ഒരുമിച്ച് sell ചെയ്യുന്നതിനാൽ baker = coffee person ആകും എന്ന് confuse ആകും. Baker = bread specialist, barista = coffee specialist.

❌ (B) Bricklayer
• 'Bricklayer' = bricks ഉപയോഗിച്ച് walls, buildings construct ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — construction worker.
• Coffee-മായി ഒരു ബന്ധവുമില്ല — complete distractor option ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'B' letter-ൽ തുടങ്ങുന്ന 4 options-ഉം — students confuse ആകും. 'Brick' = construction keyword, coffee-മായി ഒരിക്കലും associate ആകരുത്.

❌ (C) Bartender
• 'Bartender' = bar-ൽ alcoholic drinks serve ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — pub/bar professional.
• Coffee bar ≠ drinking bar. Coffee ഒരിക്കലും bartender prepare ചെയ്യില്ല — barista prepare ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Bar' + 'ista' both have 'bar' root → 'bartender' and 'barista' confuse ആകും. Bar = alcohol bar (bartender). Coffee bar = barista. Different specializations!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'BARI-STA = BARI (sounds like Barry) makes STA (stays) at the coffee machine all day!' — ഒരു barista coffee machine-ൽ നിന്ന് move ആകില്ല — always there preparing coffee! Barista = coffee machine specialist who STAYS (sta) at the BAR (bari)!
• Occupation 'B' words quick guide:
  - BAKer = BAKE (bread/cake bake ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ)
  - BARtender = BAR (alcohol bar-ൽ)
  - BARista = BAR + coffee (coffee bar-ൽ)
  - BRICKlayer = BRICK (bricks lay ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Coffee prepare and sell' keyword = 'Barista' — ഇത് direct one-to-one match ആണ്. Bartender ≠ coffee (alcohol drinks). Baker ≠ coffee (bread). Bricklayer ≠ coffee (construction). Only Barista = coffee. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct form of verb given below.
The delicious aroma of freshly prepared meals ————— from the kitchen.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) wafts

• Subject: 'The aroma' = singular noun (ഒരു aroma ആണ്). Subject-ന് agree ആകുന്ന singular verb ആവശ്യം.
• 'Waft' = gentle-ആയി air-ൽ float ചെയ്‌ത് travel ചെയ്യുക — 'ഒഴുകി വരുക' (smell/sound/smoke).
• Simple Present tense, singular subject → 'wafts' (waft + s) ✓
• Trap: 'Meals' plural ആണ്, പക്ഷേ main subject 'aroma' ആണ് (singular) — 'of freshly prepared meals' = prepositional phrase, subject അല്ല.
• Meaning: 'Kitchen-ൽ നിന്ന് freshly prepared meals-ന്റെ രുചിയുള്ള aroma ഒഴുകി വരുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Subject find ചെയ്യൽ — 'of + noun' prepositional phrase = subject അല്ല, ignore ചെയ്യുക!
• Subject-Verb Agreement with prepositional phrases:
  - 'The aroma OF meals WAFTS' — subject = 'aroma' (singular) → wafts ✓
  - 'The quality OF these products IS good' — subject = 'quality' (singular) → is ✓
  - 'A bunch OF grapes IS on the table' — subject = 'bunch' (singular) → is ✓
  - 'The team OF players WAS ready' — subject = 'team' (singular) → was ✓
• 'Waft' verb ഉപയോഗം:
  - 'A smell wafts through the air.' (smell ഒഴുകി വരുന്നു)
  - 'Music wafted from the hall.' (music ഒഴുകി വന്നു)
  - 'The aroma wafts from the kitchen.' (aroma ഒഴുകി വരുന്നു)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Subject + of + noun' subject-verb agreement trap PSC-ൽ very common!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The smell of roses wafts through the garden. (smell = singular → wafts)
  2. The price of vegetables rises every day. (price = singular → rises)
  3. The sound of birds fills the morning. (sound = singular → fills)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) waver
• 'Waver' = uncertain ആകുക, fluctuate ചെയ്യുക — 'confidence waver ആകുന്നു' (ആത്മവിശ്വാസം ഇടിയുന്നു).
• Meaning completely wrong: aroma waver ആകില്ല — aroma 'waft' (ഒഴുകി വരുക) ആണ് ചെയ്യുന്നത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Waver' ഉം 'waft' ഉം similar sound ഉണ്ടെന്ന് feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ meanings totally different — waver = uncertain, waft = float gently.

❌ (B) wafting
• 'Wafting' = present participle. Present participle main verb ആകണമെങ്കിൽ 'is/was' auxiliary ആവശ്യം: 'is wafting' ✓.
• 'Wafting' alone (without auxiliary) = incomplete sentence — main verb ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Wafting' -ing form = active, progressive feel → natural sound ആകും. പക്ഷേ auxiliary 'is/are' ഇല്ലാതെ '-ing' form main verb ആകില്ല.

❌ (D) to waft
• 'To waft' = infinitive form. Infinitive main verb position-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — 'to + verb' = purpose/intention indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• 'The aroma to waft from the kitchen' = grammatically incomplete and wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'To waft' formal feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ infinitive = 'want to waft', 'begin to waft' — indirect object/purpose position-ൽ മാത്രം ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'OF = TRAP! Subject is BEFORE OF, not after!' — 'The aroma OF meals' — aroma = subject, meals = just extra info. 'OF' ഒരു trap ആണ്, ഇതിന് ശേഷം ഉള്ളത് subject അല്ല. Always find subject BEFORE 'of'.
• Subject identification shortcut: 'of + noun' phrase bracket ൽ ഇടുക: 'The aroma (of freshly prepared meals) ___ from the kitchen.' → 'The aroma ___ from the kitchen' → singular → 'wafts'.
• PSC exam strategy: 'The [X] of [Y] ___' pattern-ൽ → X = real subject, Y = ignore. X singular ആണോ plural ആണോ confirm ചെയ്‌ത് verb choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Aroma' singular → 'wafts' (singular verb). Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the given sentence using a suitable collective noun.
The tourists enjoyed watching the mesmerizing moment of the ————— of fish gliding through the water.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) school

• 'A school of fish' = മീനുകളുടെ ഒരു കൂട്ടം — ഇത് fixed collective noun ആണ്.
• 'School' ഇവിടെ education-ന്റെ school അല്ല! Fish-ന്റെ collective noun ആണ്.
• 'A school of fish gliding through the water' = 'വെള്ളത്തിലൂടെ ഒഴുകി നടക്കുന്ന ഒരു കൂട്ടം മീനുകൾ.'
• Etymology: Old Dutch 'schole' = group, multitude — ഇതേ word school (education) and school (fish group) രണ്ടിനും ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Shoal' ഉം fish-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കാം — 'a shoal of fish' ✓, 'a school of fish' ✓ — രണ്ടും correct.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Fish = SCHOOL (or SHOAL) — fish 'school'-ൽ ചേർന്ന് നടക്കുന്നു!
• Water animals collective nouns (PSC must-know):
  - fish → school / shoal (മീനുകൾ)
  - whales → pod (തിമിംഗലങ്ങൾ)
  - sharks → shiver (സ്രാവുകൾ)
  - dolphins → pod (ഡോൾഫിനുകൾ)
  - frogs → army (തവളകൾ)
• Other animals PSC list:
  - wolves → pack
  - lions → pride
  - birds/sheep → flock
  - cattle/elephants → herd
  - bees → swarm
  - ants → colony
  - crows → murder
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'School of fish' collective noun PSC-ൽ classic question — 'school' unexpected meaning confuse ആക്കുന്നു!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. A school of fish swam past the diver. (ഒരു കൂട്ടം മീൻ diver-ൽ നിന്ന് കടന്നുപോയി)
  2. We saw a school of colorful fish near the coral reef. (coral reef-ൽ colorful fish-ന്റെ ഒരു കൂട്ടം)
  3. A shoal of sardines moved together as one. (sardines ഒന്നായി move ചെയ്‌തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) stack
• 'Stack' = items ഒന്നിന് മേൽ ഒന്നായി pile ചെയ്‌തു വച്ചിരിക്കുന്ന group — 'a stack of books/papers' (pile).
• Fish glide ചെയ്‌ത് നടക്കുന്ന moving group-ന് 'stack' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — stack = stationary pile.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Stack' = large group feel → fish-ന് apply ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ stack = inanimate objects-ന്റെ pile, animals-ന്റെ group അല്ല.

❌ (C) squad
• 'Squad' = ഒരു specific task-ന് ഒരുമിച്ച് work ചെയ്യുന്ന people-ന്റെ group — military squad, sports squad.
• Fish-ന് 'squad' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — humans-ന്റെ organized team term ആണ്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Squad' = organized group → fish organized ആയി swim ചെയ്യുന്നു → squad feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ squad = human/organized team only.

❌ (D) suite
• 'Suite' = hotel rooms-ന്റെ set / furniture-ന്റെ set — 'a suite of rooms / a suite of furniture'.
• Fish-മായി ഒരിക്കലും ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Suite' = collection feel → group of fish = collection → suite feel ആകും. പക്ഷേ suite = rooms/furniture only.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Fish go to SCHOOL together — they learn to swim in groups!' — Fish school-ൽ ചേർന്ന് together swim ചെയ്യുന്നു, humans school-ൽ ചേർന്ന് together learn ചെയ്യുന്നു. SCHOOL = together learning/moving group!
• Water animals collective noun shortlist:
  - FISH → SCHOOL (or shoal)
  - WHALES/DOLPHINS → POD
  - SHARKS → SHIVER
  - FROGS → ARMY
• PSC exam strategy: 'Fish' + collective noun question-ൽ → automatically 'school' choose ചെയ്യുക. 'School' ഉം 'shoal' ഉം fish-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കാം. Options-ൽ 'school' ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ fish-ന്റെ collective noun question-ൽ അത് ആണ് answer. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the unreal conditional clause with suitable option given below.
————— if you had won the lottery.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) What would you have done

• If clause: 'if you had won the lottery' = Past Perfect (had won) → ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ്.
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഉണ്ടാകാതെ പോയ unreal situation.
• Pattern: If + had + V3 → would have + V3
• Main clause = 'What would you have done' = 'നിങ്ങൾ എന്ത് ചെയ്‌തേനേ?' ✓
• Question form: 'Would you have done what?' → 'What would you have done?' (inversion in question)
• Meaning: 'Lottery ജയിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ നിങ്ങൾ എന്ത് ചെയ്‌തേനേ?' (actually didn't win).
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'If + had + V3' കണ്ടാൽ → main clause = 'would have + V3' (Third Conditional)
• Third Conditional question form:
  - Statement: 'You would have done something.'
  - Question: 'What would you have done?' (would + subject + have + V3)
• Conditional question inversion:
  - Normal: Subject + would have + V3
  - Question: WH-word + would + subject + have + V3
  - 'What would you have done?' = What (WH) + would + you + have + done ✓
• 'Would have' vs 'will have':
  - would have = past unreal (Third Conditional) ✓
  - will have = future perfect (something completed in future) — conditional-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional question form PSC-ൽ tricky pattern!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. What would you have done if you had lost your passport? (passport നഷ്ടപ്പെട്ടിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ?)
  2. Where would you have gone if you had had the chance? (chance ഉണ്ടായിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ?)
  3. How would you have reacted if you had heard the news? (news കേട്ടിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ?)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) What you will have done
• 'Will have done' = Future Perfect (ഭാവിയിൽ complete ആകുന്ന action). Conditional-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• Plus 'you will have done' = statement word order, question inversion ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Will have' future perfect = 'would have' conditional — similar sound → confuse ആകും. 'If + had won' = past unreal → 'would have', never 'will have'.

❌ (C) What have you done
• 'Have you done' = Present Perfect question. 'Had won' (past perfect) ഉള്ള conditional-ന് present perfect main clause wrong ആണ്.
• Tense mismatch: if-clause = past perfect → main clause = would have + V3 (not present perfect).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'What have you done?' = familiar English question → choose ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ 'had won' = Third Conditional → 'would have done' ആണ് ആവശ്യം.

❌ (D) What will you have done
• 'Will you have done' = Future Perfect question (inversion correct). പക്ഷേ 'will have' = future, conditional-ൽ 'would have' ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• 'Will' vs 'would': 'Will' = real future. 'Would' = conditional/unreal → 'had won' unreal past → 'would have' ✓.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Inversion correct ('will you have done' = proper question form) → correct feel. പക്ഷേ 'will' instead of 'would' = wrong conditional form.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAD in if-clause = WOULD HAVE in main clause — HAD and WOULD HAVE are MARRIED!' — 'If + HAD won' → 'WOULD HAVE done'. HAD + WOULD HAVE = Third Conditional married pair. Will have, have done = wrong partners!
• Third Conditional question pattern:
  - IF + HAD + V3 → WHAT + WOULD + subject + HAVE + V3?
  - 'If had won' → 'What would you have done?'
• PSC exam strategy: 'If + had + V3' ഉള്ള sentence-ൽ → main clause-ൽ 'would have + V3' ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Will have' ഉള്ള options eliminate ചെയ്യുക (will ≠ would in conditionals). Simple check = guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct alternative from those given as options.
Bring a jacket in case ——————

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) it gets cold later

• 'In case' = ഒരു possible future situation-ന് precaution/preparation ആയി — 'ഒരുവേള, ആകസ്‌മികമായി ആ situation വന്നാൽ'.
• 'In case' + Present Simple = fixed rule. Future possibility indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു, 'will' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Bring a jacket in case it gets cold' = 'തണുക്കുന്ന ഒരുവേള ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ ഉപകരിക്കട്ടെ എന്ന് jacket കൊണ്ടുവരൂ.'
• Compare: 'if' vs 'in case':
  - 'If' = condition (that situation happen ആയാൽ ഉണ്ടാകുന്ന result)
  - 'In case' = precaution (that situation ഉണ്ടാകാം, preparation ആയി ഇപ്പോൾ ചെയ്യുക)
• ഉദ്ദേശ്യം: 'Jacket കൊണ്ടുവരൂ — later cold ആകുന്ന ഒരുവേള ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ (preparation).'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'In case' + Present Simple (future 'will' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല!)
• 'In case' + tense rules:
  - Future possibility: in case + Present Simple: 'Take an umbrella in case it rains.' ✓
  - Past possibility: in case + Past Simple: 'She took an umbrella in case it rained.' ✓
  - NEVER: in case + will/would: 'in case it will rain' ✗ (wrong!)
• 'In case' vs 'if' comparison:
  - IF: 'If it rains, take an umbrella.' (condition → result)
  - IN CASE: 'Take an umbrella in case it rains.' (precaution → possible situation)
• Other conjunctions + present simple (future meaning):
  - when, until, as soon as, after, before, unless + present simple = future meaning
  - 'I will call you when he arrives.' (arrives = present simple, future meaning)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'In case + present simple' rule PSC-ൽ tricky tense question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Take some water in case you get thirsty. (ദാഹിക്കുന്ന ഒരുവേള ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ)
  2. Write down the number in case you forget. (മറക്കുന്ന ഒരുവേള ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ)
  3. Bring a charger in case your phone dies. (phone battery ഒടുങ്ങുന്ന ഒരുവേള)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) it got cold later
• 'Got cold' = Past Simple. ഇവിടെ future possibility ആണ് ഉദ്ദേശ്യം — past tense wrong.
• 'In case it got cold' = past event-ന് precaution ആയി present-ൽ jacket bring ചെയ്യുക = illogical.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Got' = past form → wrong time reference. 'In case' + present simple ആണ് future-ൽ ഉള്ള rule.

❌ (B) it is getting cold later
• 'Is getting' = Present Continuous. Present Continuous = ഇപ്പോൾ നടക്കുന്ന action. 'Later' = future → Present Continuous + later = time conflict.
• 'In case' + present continuous = standard usage അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Is getting' present feel + 'later' future = mixed → students confuse ആകും. 'In case' + simple present ആണ് correct pattern.

❌ (D) it was getting cold later
• 'Was getting' = Past Continuous + 'later' = completely wrong time combination.
• Past Continuous = ഭൂതകാലത്ത് നടന്നുകൊണ്ടിരുന്ന action. Future precaution-ന് past continuous wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was getting' = polite/uncertain feel → students think it's tentative about future. പക്ഷേ 'in case' + present simple ആണ് fixed rule.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'IN CASE = I PREPARE NOW for what MIGHT happen LATER — Present Simple shows I'm ready NOW!' — 'In case it gets cold' = I bring jacket NOW because later cold MIGHT happen. Present Simple = my current preparation for a possible future event!
• 'In case' family rule: ALWAYS present simple after 'in case' (future context). 'Will, would, got, was getting' — all wrong after 'in case'.
• PSC exam strategy: 'In case' കണ്ടാൽ → Present Simple option (V1/V+s) choose ചെയ്യുക. Options-ൽ 'gets' (present simple) ഉണ്ടെങ്കിൽ → answer. 'Got, will get, was getting' → all eliminate. ഈ one rule ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Rewrite the sentence using 'so…that'.
The movie was too scary for me to watch it alone
optionA: The movie was so scary that I shouldn't watch it alone
optionB: The movie was so scary that I couldn't watch it alone
optionC: The movie was so scary that I should watch it alone
optionD: The movie was so scary that I could watch it alone

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) The movie was so scary that I couldn't watch it alone

• Original sentence: 'too scary for me to watch it alone' = 'ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് കാണാൻ കഴിയാത്ത അത്ര scary'.
• 'Too + adjective + to' → 'so + adjective + that + subject + couldn't + verb' (negative transformation).
• 'Too' = inability indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു (cannot do because of excess) → 'couldn't' (negative) ✓
• Pattern: too + adj + for sb + to + V → so + adj + that + sb + couldn't + V
• 'Too scary to watch' → 'so scary that I couldn't watch' ✓
• Meaning: 'ആ movie ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് കാണാൻ കഴിയാത്ത അത്ര ഭയാനകമായിരുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Too + adj + to = So + adj + that + couldn't
• 'Too...to' → 'so...that' transformation rules:
  - too + adj + to + V → so + adj + that + subject + couldn't + V
  - 'Too cold to swim' → 'So cold that I couldn't swim.' ✓
  - 'Too heavy to lift' → 'So heavy that I couldn't lift it.' ✓
  - 'Too tired to walk' → 'So tired that I couldn't walk.' ✓
• Key principle: 'Too' = negative meaning (unable to do) → transformation-ൽ 'couldn't' (negative) ഉപയോഗിക്കണം.
• 'Should' vs 'could':
  - could = ability (കഴിവ്) / couldn't = inability
  - should = obligation/advice (ചെയ്യണം) — 'too...to' transformation-ൽ 'should' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'too...to' → 'so...that' sentence transformation PSC-ൽ common question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Too young to drive → So young that he couldn't drive. (വണ്ടി ഓടിക്കാൻ age ഇല്ല)
  2. Too loud to hear → So loud that I couldn't hear. (കേൾക്കാൻ കഴിയാത്ത ഒച്ച)
  3. Too sick to work → So sick that she couldn't work. (ജോലി ചെയ്യാൻ കഴിയാത്ത ആരോഗ്യം)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) so scary that I shouldn't watch it alone
• 'Shouldn't' = advice/obligation — 'watch ചെയ്യരുത്' (advice). Original-ൽ 'unable to watch' (inability) ആണ്.
• Meaning changed: 'shouldn't' = 'I advise myself not to watch' ≠ 'I couldn't watch' (unable).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Shouldn't' negative feel → 'too scary' negative → match ആകും feel. പക്ഷേ 'too' = inability (couldn't), 'shouldn't' = advice — different meanings!

❌ (C) so scary that I should watch it alone
• 'Should watch it alone' = positive suggestion — 'ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് കാണണം' (advice to watch alone).
• Original meaning completely reversed! 'Too scary to watch' → 'so scary that I couldn't watch' — not 'should watch'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'So scary that I should watch alone' sounds dramatic/brave. പക്ഷേ original = unable, not advised to watch alone.

❌ (D) so scary that I could watch it alone
• 'Could watch' = positive ability — 'ഒറ്റയ്ക്ക് കാണാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞു' (was able to).
• Original: 'too scary to watch' = UNABLE. Option D: 'could watch' = ABLE — completely opposite meaning!
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Could' ഉം 'couldn't' ഉം look similar → students miss the 'n't'. 'Could' = ability, 'couldn't' = inability — one 't' difference, opposite meaning!
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'TOO = 2 Much = NEGATIVE → COULDN'T!' — 'Too' scary = 2 much scary = cannot watch = COULDN'T. 'Too' is always negative in meaning → 'so...that...COULDN'T'. 'Too much' = negative = couldn't!
• Transformation formula — ഒരിക്കൽ memorize ചെയ്‌താൽ മതി:
  TOO + adj + TO + V → SO + adj + THAT + subject + COULDN'T + V
  (negative) → (negative)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Too...to' → 'so...that' question-ൽ → 'couldn't' ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Should, could' ഉള്ള options eliminate ചെയ്യുക. 'Too' = negative = couldn't — ഈ one connection ഓർത്തിരുന്നാൽ guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the most appropriate option to complete the sentence.
When he returned home ——————
optionA: he finds that his cat has knocked over the vase
optionB: he found that his cat was knocking the vase over
optionC: he will find that his cat had knocked over the vase
optionD: he found that his cat had knocked over the vase

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) he found that his cat had knocked over the vase

• 'When he returned home' = Simple Past (returned) — ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഉണ്ടായ ഒരു action.
• ആ 'returning' action-ന് BEFORE ഉണ്ടായ action = 'the cat knocked over the vase' — ഈ earlier action-ന് Past Perfect (had knocked) ഉപയോഗിക്കും.
• Sequence: Cat knocks over vase (earlier) → He returns home (later) → He finds the vase fallen.
• 'He found' (simple past) + 'had knocked' (past perfect) = correct time sequence ✓
• Meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹം വീട്ടിൽ തിരിച്ചെത്തിയപ്പോൾ, അദ്ദേഹത്തിന്റെ പൂച്ച vase ഉരുട്ടി വീഴ്ത്തിയിട്ടുണ്ടെന്ന് കണ്ടു' — vase fall ചെയ്‌തത് തിരിച്ചുവരുന്നതിന് BEFORE ആണ്.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Past-ൽ രണ്ട് actions → Earlier action = Past Perfect (had + V3), Later action = Simple Past
• Sequence of events rule:
  - Action 1 (earlier): cat knocked over vase → Past Perfect: 'had knocked'
  - Action 2 (later): he returned home → Simple Past: 'returned'
  - Discovery: he found → Simple Past: 'found'
• 'When' clause + time sequence:
  - 'When he arrived, she had already left.' (she left = earlier, Past Perfect)
  - 'When they reached, the train had departed.' (train left = earlier, Past Perfect)
  - 'When I called, he had already eaten.' (eating = earlier, Past Perfect)
• Past Perfect signal words: already, just, before, by the time, when (showing earlier action)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'When + Simple Past' → connected clause Past Perfect — classic PSC sequence question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. When she woke up, the rain had stopped. (ഉണർന്നപ്പോൾ rain നിന്നിരുന്നു)
  2. When we arrived, the match had ended. (എത്തിയപ്പോൾ match കഴിഞ്ഞിരുന്നു)
  3. When he opened the box, someone had taken everything. (തുറന്നപ്പോൾ എല്ലാം കൊണ്ടുപോയിരുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) he finds that his cat has knocked over the vase
• 'Finds' = Present Simple, 'has knocked' = Present Perfect — രണ്ടും present tense.
• 'When he returned' = past context. Present tense main clause past context-ന് ചേർന്നതല്ല — tense mismatch.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has knocked' = recent action feel → correct sound. പക്ഷേ 'returned' (past) context-ൽ present tense wrong ആണ്.

❌ (B) he found that his cat was knocking the vase over
• 'Was knocking' = Past Continuous — ആ moment-ൽ നടക്കുന്ന action.
• 'Was knocking' implies: he returned AND AT THAT SAME MOMENT the cat was knocking — simultaneous action.
• Meaning changed: 'was knocking' = still in progress when he arrived. 'Had knocked' = completed before he arrived. Vase ഇതിനകം വീണിട്ടുണ്ടെങ്കിൽ 'had knocked' ✓, ഇപ്പോഴും നടക്കുകയാണെങ്കിൽ 'was knocking' ✓. Context: 'found the vase fallen' → completed action → had knocked ✓.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was knocking' = past feel → correct sound. പക്ഷേ 'had knocked over' (completed, vase already fallen) ആണ് logical.

❌ (C) he will find that his cat had knocked over the vase
• 'Will find' = Future tense. 'When he returned' = past → future main clause = illogical.
• 'Will find' future + 'returned' past = tense clash — completely wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Had knocked' (past perfect) ശരിയാണ്, പക്ഷേ 'will find' future = past context-ൽ wrong. 'Will' → 'found' ആക്കണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Past + BEFORE Past = HAD + V3 (Past Perfect)!' — 'When he returned' (past), cat knocked over vase BEFORE that (earlier past) → Past Perfect = had knocked. Timeline: [cat knocked] → [he returned] → had = 'already happened before.'
• Quick identification: 'When + Simple Past' + another past action before it → that earlier action = Past Perfect.
• PSC exam strategy: 'When he returned / arrived / came back' type sentences-ൽ → main clause-ൽ 'had + V3' (Past Perfect) ഉള്ള option choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Was doing' (simultaneous), 'finds/has' (wrong tense) → eliminate. 'Found that... had...' pattern = guaranteed Past Perfect question answer!

questionText:
He had scarcely received the message from headquarters when he started.
Choose the correct option if the sentence is begun with 'Scarcely'.
optionA: Scarcely he had received the message from headquarters when he started
optionB: Scarcely had he received the message from headquarters before he started
optionC: Scarcely had he received the message from headquarters when he started
optionD: Scarcely he received the message from headquarters when he started

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) Scarcely had he received the message from headquarters when he started

• 'Scarcely' = ഒരു negative adverb — 'വളരെ കുറച്ച്/ഏതാണ്ട് ഇല്ലേ' (hardly) അർഥം. Sentence-ന്റെ തുടക്കത്തിൽ വരുമ്പോൾ subject-auxiliary inversion ആവശ്യം.
• Inversion rule: Negative adverb (Scarcely/Hardly/Rarely/Never/Seldom) sentence-ന്റെ first position-ൽ → auxiliary + subject (inverted word order).
• Original: 'He had scarcely...' → Inverted: 'Scarcely HAD HE received...'
• 'Scarcely...when' = fixed pair (not 'before'!) → 'Scarcely had he received... WHEN he started.' ✓
• Meaning: 'Headquarters-ൽ നിന്ന് message കിട്ടിയ ഉടനേ അദ്ദേഹം പുറപ്പെട്ടു — ഒട്ടും delay ഇല്ലാതെ.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Scarcely/Hardly/No sooner sentence-ന്റെ beginning-ൽ → HAD + subject + V3 (inversion!) + WHEN/THAN
• Negative adverb inversion pattern:
  - Normal: He had scarcely received... when he started.
  - Inverted: Scarcely had he received... when he started. ✓
  - Normal: She had hardly finished... when it rang.
  - Inverted: Hardly had she finished... when it rang. ✓
• Paired connectors (fixed pairs — mix ചെയ്യരുത്!):
  - Scarcely/Hardly → WHEN (never 'before'!)
  - No sooner → THAN (never 'when'!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Scarcely/Hardly inversion + 'when' PSC-ൽ frequent question — two traps: (1) inversion miss ചെയ്യൽ, (2) 'before' vs 'when' confusion!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Scarcely had we sat down when the lights went off. (ഇരുന്ന ഉടനേ light പോയി)
  2. Hardly had he opened his mouth when she interrupted. (തുടങ്ങിയ ഉടനേ interrupt ചെയ്‌തു)
  3. No sooner had they left than it began to rain. (പോയ ഉടനേ മഴ തുടങ്ങി)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Scarcely he had received the message from headquarters when he started
• 'Scarcely he had received' = inversion ഇല്ല! Subject (he) auxiliary (had)-ന് ശേഷം വന്നിരിക്കുന്നു — wrong word order.
• Negative adverb beginning-ൽ വരുമ്പോൾ: 'Scarcely + HAD + HE + received' (inversion) ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Scarcely' beginning-ൽ ഉണ്ട് → feels half-correct. പക്ഷേ 'he had' (normal order) = wrong — 'had he' (inverted) ആണ് വേണ്ടത്.

❌ (B) Scarcely had he received the message from headquarters before he started
• Inversion correct: 'had he received' ✓. പക്ഷേ 'before' = wrong connector!
• 'Scarcely/Hardly' pair = 'WHEN' (not 'before'). 'Before' = 'No sooner'-ന്റെ equivalent അല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Before' temporal feel → Scarcely = 'before much time passed' → 'before' natural feel. പക്ഷേ fixed rule: Scarcely/Hardly + WHEN (always!).

❌ (D) Scarcely he received the message from headquarters when he started
• രണ്ട് errors: (1) Inversion ഇല്ല ('he received' instead of 'had he received'), (2) 'had' auxiliary missing.
• 'Scarcely he received' = inversion ഇല്ല + Past Perfect 'had' missing.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Simple Past 'received' vs Past Perfect 'had received' — inversion-ൽ auxiliary 'had' retain ചെയ്യണം: 'Scarcely HAD HE received.'
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'SCARCELY calls for INVERSION + WHEN — like a VIP entering a room, everything reverses!' — 'Scarcely' = VIP adverb. When VIP walks in first → everything else reverses (HAD + HE instead of HE + HAD). And VIP always pairs with WHEN, not 'before'!
• Golden pairs — ഈ 3 memorize ചെയ്‌താൽ മതി:
  - SCARCELY/HARDLY + had + subject + V3 + WHEN
  - NO SOONER + had + subject + V3 + THAN
  - NEVER/SELDOM/RARELY + auxiliary + subject + V1
• PSC exam strategy: 'Scarcely/Hardly' beginning-ൽ ഉള്ള question → (1) 'had + subject' (inversion) confirm ചെയ്യുക, (2) 'when' connector confirm ചെയ്യുക. 'Before' ഉള്ള options eliminate. 'He had' (no inversion) ഉള്ള options eliminate. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct reported speech.
"Did you watch the film after you finished reading the book?"
optionA: He asked me whether I watched the film after I finished reading the book
optionB: He asked me did I watch the film after I finished reading the book
optionC: He asked me if I had watched the film after I had finished reading the book
optionD: He asked me whether I have watched the film after I finished reading the book

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) He asked me if I had watched the film after I had finished reading the book

• Yes/No question-ന്റെ reported speech: 'if' അല്ലെങ്കിൽ 'whether' ഉപയോഗിക്കും (both correct). 'if' ഉപയോഗിക്കുന്നതാണ് option C.
• Backshift rule: Direct speech-ൽ Simple Past → Indirect speech-ൽ Past Perfect.
  - 'Did you watch' (simple past) → 'had watched' (past perfect) ✓
  - 'finished' (simple past) → 'had finished' (past perfect) ✓
• Question word order inverted: 'Did you watch?' → normal statement order: 'I had watched' (no inversion in reported speech)
• Meaning: 'Book reading കഴിഞ്ഞ ശേഷം film കണ്ടോ?' — reported as 'book reading finish ആയ ശേഷം film കണ്ടോ എന്ന് ചോദിച്ചു'.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Yes/No question → Reported: asked + if/whether + subject + Past Perfect (backshift)
• Direct/Indirect backshift table:
  - Simple Present → Simple Past
  - Simple Past → Past Perfect ✓ (ഇവിടെ relevant)
  - Present Perfect → Past Perfect
  - 'will' → 'would'
• Yes/No question reporting steps:
  1. 'Did you watch?' → question mark remove, inversion undo
  2. 'if/whether' add
  3. Backshift: 'watched' → 'had watched', 'finished' → 'had finished'
  4. Pronoun change: 'you' → 'I' (or appropriate pronoun)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Simple Past backshift → Past Perfect in reported speech — PSC-ൽ regular question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. 'Did you eat?' → He asked if I had eaten. (Simple Past → Past Perfect)
  2. 'Did she call you?' → She asked whether he had called me. (backshift ✓)
  3. 'Did they leave early?' → She asked if they had left early. (backshift ✓)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) He asked me whether I watched the film after I finished reading the book
• 'Whether' connector correct ✓. പക്ഷേ backshift ഇല്ല — 'watched' ഉം 'finished' ഉം simple past-ൽ remain ചെയ്‌തു.
• Simple Past → Past Perfect backshift mandatory. 'Watched' → 'had watched', 'finished' → 'had finished' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Whether' correct connector → feels half-right. പക്ഷേ backshift miss — 'watched'/'finished' → 'had watched'/'had finished' ആകണം.

❌ (B) He asked me did I watch the film after I finished reading the book
• 'Did I watch' = question word order (inversion) retained — wrong! Reported speech-ൽ inversion undo ചെയ്യണം.
• Reported speech = statement order: 'he asked if I had watched' (not 'did I watch').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Did I watch' = direct question feel → natural. പക്ഷേ indirect speech = statement order + backshift. Question form ഒരിക്കലും retain ചെയ്യില്ല.

❌ (D) He asked me whether I have watched the film after I finished reading the book
• 'Have watched' = Present Perfect — wrong backshift. Simple Past → Past Perfect ('had watched'), not Present Perfect ('have watched').
• 'finished' = backshift ഇല്ല (should be 'had finished').
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have watched' = perfect tense feel → correct sound. പക്ഷേ backshift = Past Perfect ('had watched'), not Present Perfect ('have watched'). 'Have' → 'had' ആകണം.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Reported Speech PUSHES EVERYTHING ONE STEP BACK in time! Simple Past goes BACK one more step = Past Perfect!' — Direct speech time machine: Present → Past, Past → Past Perfect. 'Did/watched/finished' (Simple Past) → 'had watched/had finished' (Past Perfect) — one step further back!
• Yes/No question reported speech formula:
  asked + IF/WHETHER + subject + HAD + V3 (backshift)
• PSC exam strategy: Reported speech Yes/No question-ൽ → (1) 'if' or 'whether' ✓, (2) 'had + V3' (past perfect backshift) ✓, (3) statement word order (no inversion) ✓. ഈ 3 check ചെയ്‌ത് option select ചെയ്യുക. 'Did I/you' (inversion) ഉള്ളതും, 'have watched' (present perfect) ഉള്ളതും eliminate. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Change the voice.
Did they lend him some money?
optionB: Was some money lent him by them?
optionD: Was he being lent some money?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) Was he lent some money?

• Active: 'Did they lend him some money?' — Subject: they, Verb: lend (Simple Past question), Objects: him (indirect), some money (direct).
• Passive of interrogative Simple Past: 'Did + subject + V1' → 'Was/Were + new subject + V3?'
• Double object passive: 'lend him some money' → indirect object ('him') ആണ് passive subject ആകുന്നത് (more natural in English).
• 'They' passive-ൽ omit ചെയ്‌തു (by them omit — agent optional).
• Result: 'Was he lent some money?' ✓ (Simple Past question passive — 'Did lend' → 'Was lent')
• Meaning: 'അദ്ദേഹത്തിന് കുറച്ച് പണം കടം കൊടുത്തോ?'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'Did + sub + lend?' → 'Was + indirect object + lent?' (Simple Past interrogative passive)
• Interrogative passive formula:
  - Active: Did + subject + V1 + object?
  - Passive: Was/Were + object + V3 + (by agent)?
• Simple Past 'did lend' → Passive 'was lent' (not 'were lent', not 'was being lent')
• Double object rule (lend, give, send, show, teach):
  - 'lend him some money' → TWO possible passives:
    1. 'He was lent some money.' (indirect object = subject) — MORE natural ✓
    2. 'Some money was lent to him.' (direct object = subject, 'to' added)
• Interrogative = question mark + Was/Were at beginning
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Double object passive + interrogative passive — PSC-ൽ tricky combination!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. Did she give him a gift? → Was he given a gift? ✓
  2. Did they send her a letter? → Was she sent a letter? ✓
  3. Did he show them the results? → Were they shown the results? ✓
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) Was some money lent him by them?
• 'Some money' = direct object passive subject. Grammatically possible but 'to him' ആണ് ആവശ്യം — 'lent him' → 'lent to him' (preposition missing).
• 'Was some money lent to him?' = correct form, പക്ഷേ 'by them' = unnecessary (agent omit ചെയ്‌തിരിക്കുന്നു option A-ൽ). 'lent him' (without 'to') incorrect.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Some money' direct object → passive subject feel. പക്ഷേ 'lent him' → 'lent to him' ആകണം, 'by them' retention awkward.

❌ (C) Were they lent some money?
• 'They' = active subject → passive-ൽ subject ആകരുത്! They = agent (by them), passive-ൽ object (him/some money) = subject ആകണം.
• 'Were they' = 'they' plural → 'were'. പക്ഷേ passive subject = 'he' (him) → 'was he' ✓.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'They' = plural → 'were' → 'Were they' plural agreement feel. പക്ഷേ passive subject = 'him' (he), 'they' = active subject (doer = by them, optional).

❌ (D) Was he being lent some money?
• 'Was being lent' = Past Continuous Passive. Active 'did lend' = Simple Past (not Past Continuous).
• Simple Past active ('did lend') → Simple Past passive ('was lent') — 'being' add ചെയ്യരുത്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Was being lent' = progressive feel → sophisticated sound. പക്ഷേ 'did lend' (Simple Past) → 'was lent' (Simple Past Passive), not 'was being lent' (Past Continuous Passive).
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'DID = WAS/WERE in passive — Simple Past question flips to Was/Were question!' — 'Did they lend?' (active) → 'Was he lent?' (passive). 'DID' disappears, 'WAS' takes its place. Simple swap: DID → WAS.
• Double object 'lend/give/send' passive shortcut:
  - Indirect object (him/her/them) = preferred passive subject → 'Was HE lent...' ✓
  - 'By them' = omit (agent usually optional in passive)
• PSC exam strategy: 'Did + subject + V1?' passive question-ൽ → 'Was/Were + indirect object + V3?' format choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Were they' (active subject) ഉള്ളതും, 'was being' (continuous) ഉള്ളതും eliminate. 'Was he + V3' = Simple Past interrogative passive answer. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Choose the correct meaning of the idiom.
The doctor said that her patient had turned the corner and was on the road to recovery.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) to pass the crisis

• 'Turned the corner' = ഒരു difficult situation-ന്റെ worst part കഴിഞ്ഞ് better ആകാൻ തുടങ്ങുക — crisis pass ചെയ്‌തു, recovery ആരംഭിച്ചു.
• Medical context: 'Patient had turned the corner' = patient-ന്റെ critical stage കഴിഞ്ഞ്, ഇനി better ആകും.
• Confirmation: 'was on the road to recovery' = sentence-ൽ ഇതേ meaning confirm ചെയ്യുന്നു — turn the corner = recovery begin.
• Origin: Corner ചുറ്റുമ്പോൾ direction മാറുന്നു — crisis-ൽ നിന്ന് improvement-ലേക്ക് 'direction change' = turned the corner.
• Meaning: 'Doctor പറഞ്ഞു — patient crisis കഴിഞ്ഞ് recovery-ലേക്ക് നീങ്ങുന്നു.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: 'Turn the corner' = Crisis point കടന്നുകഴിഞ്ഞ്, ഇനി better ആകാൻ തുടങ്ങുക (to begin to recover or improve after a difficult time)
• 'Turn the corner' usage contexts:
  - Medical: patient turned the corner (illness worst stage കഴിഞ്ഞ്)
  - Business: company turned the corner (loss period കഴിഞ്ഞ് profit ആകാൻ തുടങ്ങി)
  - Personal: she's turned the corner (difficulty കഴിഞ്ഞ് improve ചെയ്‌തു)
• Related idioms (crisis/recovery theme):
  - 'on the road to recovery' = recovery progress ചെയ്‌തുകൊണ്ടിരിക്കുന്നു
  - 'out of the woods' = danger/difficulty period കഴിഞ്ഞ് safe ആയി
  - 'over the worst' = worst part completed
  - 'light at the end of the tunnel' = hope visible after difficulty
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Turn the corner' idiom PSC-ൽ ഇടയ്ക്ക് appear ആകുന്ന classic phrase!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. After months of losses, the business has finally turned the corner. (loss period കഴിഞ്ഞ് profit)
  2. The doctors believe he has turned the corner and will recover soon. (critical period കഴിഞ്ഞ്)
  3. With the new policy, the economy seems to have turned the corner. (recession കഴിഞ്ഞ്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) to take a wrong turn
• 'Turn the corner' = literal corner ആയി confuse ചെയ്‌ത് 'wrong direction' take ചെയ്‌തു എന്ന് interpret ചെയ്‌തിരിക്കുന്നു.
• 'Turn the corner' = improvement/recovery idiom — literal road-ൽ turning-ന്റെ meaning ഇതിൽ ഇല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Turned the corner' = corner-ൽ turn ചെയ്‌തു → 'wrong turn' literal interpretation feel. PSC idiom questions-ൽ literal meaning = usually wrong!

❌ (C) to reach a destination
• 'Corner reach ചെയ്‌തു = destination reach ചെയ്‌തു' — literal interpretation.
• 'Turn the corner' = crisis pass ചെയ്‌തു (improvement begin), destination arrive ആയതല്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Corner = endpoint feel → destination arrive feel. പക്ഷേ idiom = crisis passing, not arriving somewhere.

❌ (D) to make progress slowly
• 'Slowly progress' = 'on the road to recovery' = gradual progress feel. 'Turned the corner' ≠ slow progress.
• 'Turn the corner' = specific moment of turning — crisis point PASSED (sudden change of direction), not gradual slowness.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Medical context + recovery = slow improvement feel. പക്ഷേ 'turned the corner' = definite turning point (crisis exceeded), not slow progress.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'TURN THE CORNER = You were walking in darkness (crisis), you turned the corner, and NOW you can SEE the light (recovery)!' — Corner ചുറ്റിയ ശേഷം perspective മാറുന്നു — crisis → hope. Dark street corner ചുറ്റുമ്പോൾ light street കാണുന്നു = crisis → recovery!
• Crisis/Recovery idiom family (PSC must-know):
  - Turn the corner = crisis point pass ചെയ്‌തു
  - Out of the woods = danger period ended
  - On the road to recovery = recovery in progress
  - Hit rock bottom = worst point reached (opposite)
• PSC exam strategy: Medical context-ൽ 'turn the corner' = always 'crisis pass/recovery begin'. Literal meanings (wrong turn, destination, slow progress) = eliminate. Context clue: 'was on the road to recovery' confirms B. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Complete the clause suitably.
If I had attended the wedding, I _________.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (A) could have met him

• 'If I had attended' = Past Perfect (had + V3) → ഇത് Third Conditional ആണ് — ഭൂതകാലത്ത് ഉണ്ടാകാതെ പോയ unreal situation.
• Third Conditional pattern: If + had + V3 → modal (would/could/might) + have + V3
• 'Could have met' = 'met' ശരിയായ Past Participle (V3) ✓ — 'wedding-ൽ attend ചെയ്‌തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ, അദ്ദേഹത്തെ കണ്ടുമുട്ടാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ.'
• 'Could have' = past unreal ability — 'കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ' (would have = ആകുമായിരുന്നു, could have = കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ — both correct in Third Conditional)
• Meaning: 'Wedding-ൽ ഞാൻ attend ചെയ്‌തിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ അദ്ദേഹത്തെ കണ്ടുമുട്ടാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ' — actually didn't attend.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: If + HAD + V3 → would/could/might + HAVE + V3 (Third Conditional)
• Third Conditional = ഭൂതകാലത്തിലെ unreal situation — reality-ൽ ഉണ്ടായില്ല, imagine ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Modal choices in main clause:
  - 'would have' = certainty (ആകുമായിരുന്നു)
  - 'could have' = possibility/ability (കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ)
  - 'might have' = less certain possibility (ആയേനേ)
• V3 (Past Participle) ഉപയോഗം:
  - meet → met (V2) → met (V3) ✓
  - 'could have met' ✓ (V3 form)
  - 'could have meet' ✗ (V1 form — wrong!)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Third Conditional + modal perfect (could have/would have + V3) PSC-ൽ frequent!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. If she had studied harder, she could have passed. (കഠിനമായി പഠിച്ചിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ pass ആകാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ)
  2. If they had left early, they would have caught the train. (നേരത്തെ പോയിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ ട്രെയിൻ കിട്ടിയേനേ)
  3. If I had known, I could have helped. (അറിഞ്ഞിരുന്നെങ്കിൽ സഹായിക്കാൻ കഴിഞ്ഞേനേ)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (B) could have meet him
• 'Meet' = V1 (base form) — 'have'-ന് ശേഷം V3 (Past Participle) ആവശ്യം.
• 'Could have' + V1 ✗ → 'could have' + V3 ✓ → 'could have met' ✓
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Could have meet' ഉം 'could have met' ഉം similar sound → students miss V3 form. Meet-ന്റെ V3 = 'met', V1 = 'meet' — one letter difference!

❌ (C) would meet him
• 'Would meet' = Second Conditional main clause form (present/future unreal).
• If-clause-ൽ 'had attended' (Past Perfect) ഉണ്ട് → Third Conditional → 'would have met' ആവശ്യം, 'would meet' (without 'have') wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Would' modal ഉണ്ട് → correct feel. പക്ഷേ 'have' missing — Third Conditional-ൽ 'would/could + HAVE + V3' ആണ് pattern.

❌ (D) could meet him
• 'Could meet' = Second Conditional form — present/future imaginary situation-ന്.
• 'Had attended' (Past Perfect if-clause) → Third Conditional → 'could have met' (with 'have') ആണ് ആവശ്യം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Could meet' = ability feel → natural sound. പക്ഷേ 'have' missing — Third Conditional-ൽ 'have' mandatory.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'HAD in if-clause → HAVE in main clause — HAD and HAVE are TWINS!' — 'If I HAD attended' → 'could HAVE met'. If-clause-ൽ HAD കണ്ടാൽ main clause-ൽ HAVE ഉണ്ടാകണം. HAD = HAVE twin!
• V3 check shortcut: 'could have ___' → blank-ൽ V3 (past participle) ആണ് വേണ്ടത്. meet (V1) → met (V3). 'Could have met' ✓, 'could have meet' ✗.
• PSC exam strategy: Third Conditional-ൽ options-ൽ 'have' ഇല്ലാത്തത് (would meet, could meet) → eliminate. 'Have' ഉള്ളതിൽ V3 confirm ചെയ്യുക (met ✓, meet ✗). Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Identify the correctly spelt word from the following.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) colleague

• 'Colleague' = ഒരേ office അല്ലെങ്കിൽ profession-ൽ ഒന്നിച്ച് ജോലി ചെയ്യുന്ന ആൾ — 'സഹ-പ്രവർത്തകൻ/സഹ-പ്രവർത്തക'.
• Correct spelling breakdown: col + lea + gue = c-o-l-l-e-a-g-u-e
• Key letters to remember: double 'l' (col-l), 'ea' combination, silent 'ue' at end.
• Etymology: French 'collègue' → Latin 'collega' (co + legare = together + delegate) — ഒന്നിച്ച് delegate ചെയ്യപ്പെട്ടവർ.
• PSC-ൽ 'colleague' spelling question ഇടക്കിടെ വരുന്ന classic trap word!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: c-o-l-l-e-a-g-u-e — ഈ 9 letters exact order-ൽ ഓർക്കുക!
• Breakdown method: COL + LEAGUE = colleague
  - 'COL' = col (column-ലെ col പോലെ)
  - 'LEAGUE' = league (sports league = ടീം, ഇതേ spelling)
  - COL + LEAGUE = COLLEAGUE ✓
• Common errors in this word:
  - Double 'l' miss: 'coleague' ✗ (one l only) → 'colleague' ✓ (two l's)
  - Extra 'e': 'colleegue' ✗ (ee wrong) → 'colleague' ✓ (ea)
  - Missing 'u': 'collegue' ✗ → 'colleague' ✓ (gue not ge)
• Related words (same French -ague pattern):
  - league, plague, vague, dialogue, catalogue
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Colleague' PSC spelling questions-ൽ very frequently appear ആകുന്ന word!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She is my colleague at the bank. (bank-ൽ എന്റെ സഹ-പ്രവർത്തക)
  2. My colleagues helped me finish the project. (സഹ-പ്രവർത്തകർ project complete ചെയ്യാൻ സഹായിച്ചു)
  3. He introduced me to his colleague from the other department. (മറ്റൊരു department-ൽ നിന്നുള്ള colleague)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) colleegue
• 'ee' wrong — 'ea' ആണ് correct. 'collee-' → 'colle-' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Colleague' pronounce ചെയ്യുമ്പോൾ 'ee' sound feel → 'ee' write ചെയ്‌തേക്കാം. പക്ഷേ spelling = 'ea' (French origin).

❌ (B) coleague
• Single 'l' — 'colleague'-ൽ double 'l' ഉണ്ട്. 'col-' → 'coll-' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Col' = one 'l' feel (like 'color'). പക്ഷേ 'colleague' = double 'l' — col + league.

❌ (D) collegue
• 'gue' → 'gue' correct, പക്ഷേ 'ea' missing — 'colle-gue' → 'colle-a-gue' ആകണം.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Collegue' sounds almost right when spoken. പക്ഷേ 'a' missing — col-l-e-a-g-u-e.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'Your colleague is in your LEAGUE — COL + LEAGUE = COLLEAGUE!' — Sports-ൽ 'league' = ടീം group. Office-ൽ 'colleague' = ഒരേ league-ൽ (ടീമിൽ) ഉള്ള ആൾ. LEAGUE spelling അറിയാം → COLLEAGUE spelling = COL + LEAGUE!
• Spelling visual: col-LEAGUE → colleague. 'LEAGUE' part exact ആയി keep ചെയ്‌ത് 'col' prefix add ചെയ്യുക.
• PSC exam strategy: 4 options-ൽ 'colleague' correct spelling കണ്ടാൽ → confirm: (1) double 'l' ✓, (2) 'ea' ✓, (3) 'gue' ✓. ഈ 3 check ചെയ്‌ത് answer ഉറപ്പാക്കുക. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using the appropriate phrasal verb.
The match has been __________ due to bad weather.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) called off

• 'Called off' = cancel ചെയ്‌തു — ഒരു planned event/activity ഉണ്ടാകാതിരിക്കാൻ decide ചെയ്യുക.
• 'The match has been called off due to bad weather' = 'കഠിനമായ കാലാവസ്ഥ കാരണം match cancel ചെയ്‌തു.'
• Context clue: 'bad weather' → match നടക്കാൻ കഴിയില്ല → cancel/call off ✓
• Passive form used: 'has been called off' = Present Perfect Passive (someone called off the match → match has been called off)
• 'Call off' PSC-ൽ ഏറ്റവും frequently tested phrasal verbs-ൽ ഒന്ന്!
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: CALL OFF = CANCEL (planned event ഉണ്ടാകാതിരിക്കാൻ ചെയ്യുക)
• 'Call' phrasal verb family — PSC must-know list:
  - call off = cancel (match called off = match cancelled)
  - call on = visit someone / request someone to speak (call on a friend = സന്ദർശിക്കുക)
  - call at = stop briefly at a place (train calls at Chennai = Chennai-ൽ brief stop)
  - call away = summon someone away (called away on urgent business = അടിയന്തിര കാര്യത്തിന് വിളിച്ചു)
  - call up = telephone / recall to mind (call up an old friend = പഴയ സുഹൃത്തിനെ വിളിക്കുക)
  - call for = require / demand (situation calls for action = action ആവശ്യം)
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Call off' = cancel — PSC-ൽ most tested phrasal verb among 'call' family!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The strike was called off after negotiations. (negotiations ശേഷം strike cancel ആയി)
  2. The wedding has been called off. (wedding cancel ആയി)
  3. They called off the search due to darkness. (darkness കാരണം search cancel ചെയ്‌തു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) called at
• 'Call at' = ഒരു place-ൽ briefly stop ചെയ്യുക — mainly trains/ships use ചെയ്യും. 'The train calls at Thrissur.' (Thrissur-ൽ brief stop)
• Match cancel ആകുന്നതിന് 'called at' ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല — wrong meaning.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Called at' ഉം 'called off' ഉം similar → 'at' vs 'off' confuse ആകും. 'At' = place stop, 'off' = cancel.

❌ (C) called on
• 'Call on' = ഒരാളെ സന്ദർശിക്കുക / ഒരാളോട് ചെയ്യാൻ request ചെയ്യുക.
• 'The match has been called on due to bad weather' = illogical — call on = visit, bad weather-ൽ match visit ആകില്ല.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Called on' official feel → match official context → correct feel. പക്ഷേ 'call on' = visit/request, cancel അല്ല.

❌ (D) called away
• 'Call away' = ഒരാളെ urgently മറ്റൊരിടത്തേക്ക് വിളിക്കുക (a person, not an event).
• Match (an event) 'called away' ആകില്ല — match cancel ആകും (called off). 'Called away' = person-നെ summon ചെയ്യുന്നതിന്.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Called away' = absence feel → match absent feel → match cancel feel. പക്ഷേ match = event → 'called off' (cancel), not 'called away' (person summoned).
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CALL OFF = Switch OFF — OFF ആക്കുന്നതുപോലെ event-ഉം OFF ആകുന്നു = CANCEL!' — Light switch OFF ആക്കുമ്പോൾ light ഉണ്ടാകുന്നില്ല. Event 'called off' ആകുമ്പോൾ event ഉണ്ടാകുന്നില്ല. OFF = ഇല്ലാതാകുക = cancel!
• Call family quick reference:
  - CALL OFF = cancel ❌ (event ends)
  - CALL ON = visit 🚪 (go to someone)
  - CALL AT = stop briefly 🚉 (train/ship)
  - CALL AWAY = summon 📢 (person leaves)
• PSC exam strategy: Match/event/meeting cancel → 'called off'. Person summoned → 'called away'. Place brief stop → 'called at'. Visit someone → 'called on'. Context = bad weather + match = 'called off'. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Use the correct tense form.
He usually ________ to office by bus.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) goes

• 'Usually' = Simple Present signal word — habitual/regular action indicate ചെയ്യുന്ന adverb.
• Subject 'He' = third person singular → Simple Present = verb + s/es → 'goes' ✓
• 'He usually goes to office by bus' = 'അദ്ദേഹം സാധാരണ bus-ൽ office-ലേക്ക് പോകുന്നു' — routine action.
• Simple Present habitual: 'He goes every day' = ദിനംതോറും ചെയ്യുന്ന regular action.
• Signal words for Simple Present: usually, always, often, sometimes, never, every day/week/month.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: USUALLY + He/She/It → VERB + S (Simple Present, 3rd person singular)
• Simple Present formation:
  - I/You/We/They + V1 (go, eat, come)
  - He/She/It + V1+s/es (goes, eats, comes)
• 'Go' → 'goes' (go + es — 'o' ending verbs + es)
• Habitual action signal words:
  - usually, always, often, sometimes, rarely, never
  - every day/week/month, once a week, on Sundays
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: 'Usually' + 3rd person singular = 'goes/does/has' — very common PSC tense question!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She usually wakes up at 6 am. (സാധാരണ 6 മണിക്ക് എഴുന്നേൽക്കുന്നു)
  2. He always takes the morning train. (morning train-ൽ always കയറുന്നു)
  3. They often visit the temple on Fridays. (Fridays-ൽ often temple-ൽ പോകുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) go
• 'Go' = base form — I/You/We/They-ന് ഉപയോഗിക്കും. 'He go' = grammatically wrong.
• Subject 'He' = singular → 'goes' (with 's') ✓, not 'go' (without 's') ✗.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Go' natural word → 'go to office' feels right. പക്ഷേ 'He go' = subject-verb agreement error — 'He goes' ✓.

❌ (B) going
• 'Going' = present participle. Present participle main verb ആകണമെങ്കിൽ 'is/are/was' auxiliary ആവശ്യം ('is going' ✓).
• 'He usually going' = wrong — auxiliary 'is' missing, plus 'usually' + continuous tense = rare combination.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Going' = active feel → habitual action feel. പക്ഷേ 'usually' = Simple Present signal, not Continuous.

❌ (D) has gone
• 'Has gone' = Present Perfect — completed action with present relevance. 'Usually' = habitual action, Present Perfect-ൽ ഉപയോഗിക്കില്ല.
• 'Has gone' = recently left (ഇപ്പോൾ പോയിരിക്കുന്നു) — 'usually goes' (daily habit) ≠ 'has gone' (recent departure).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Has gone' = 'He has gone to office' correct sentence → familiar feel. പക്ഷേ 'usually' = habit → Simple Present → 'goes', not 'has gone'.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'USUALLY = SIMPLE PRESENT. He/She/It → add S to verb!' — 'Usually' കണ്ടാൽ Simple Present. Subject He/She/It ആണ് → verb-ന് 'S' add ചെയ്യുക. go → goes, do → does, have → has. 'USUALLY + HE = GOES' — automatic!
• Signal word → tense shortcut:
  - usually/always/often → Simple Present
  - now/at the moment → Present Continuous
  - yesterday/last week → Simple Past
  - since/for/already/just → Present Perfect
• PSC exam strategy: 'Usually' കണ്ടാൽ → Simple Present answer. Subject He/She/It → verb + s/es option choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Going' (no auxiliary) ഉം 'has gone' (present perfect) ഉം eliminate. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Fill in the blank using the correct verb.
The cutlery I bought yesterday _________ quite expensive.

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (D) is

• 'Cutlery' = uncountable noun — knives, forks, spoons-ന്റെ collective set. Uncountable noun → singular verb.
• 'The cutlery... is expensive' = 'Cutlery (knife-fork-spoon set) വളരെ expensive ആണ്.' ✓
• Trap: 'I bought yesterday' = relative clause — ഇത് subject-ന്റെ extra information, main subject 'cutlery' ആണ്.
• 'Cutlery' plural ആണ് (knives, forks...) എന്ന് feel ആകും, പക്ഷേ English-ൽ 'cutlery' = always singular (uncountable).
• Meaning: 'ഞാൻ ഇന്നലെ വാങ്ങിയ cutlery (knife-fork-spoon set) നല്ലൊരു വിലയുള്ളതാണ്.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: CUTLERY = Uncountable → always SINGULAR verb (is/was)
• Common uncountable nouns (PSC must-know) — always singular:
  - cutlery (knife, fork, spoon set) → is
  - furniture (chairs, tables, beds) → is
  - luggage/baggage → is
  - information → is
  - advice → is
  - news → is
  - equipment → is
  - machinery → is
  - clothing → is
• Subject identification trick: 'The cutlery [I bought yesterday] IS' — bracket the relative clause, find real subject.
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Uncountable collective nouns + singular verb PSC-ൽ frequent trap!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. The furniture in the hall is very old. (hall-ലെ furniture വളരെ പഴക്കമുള്ളതാണ്)
  2. The luggage is already checked in. (luggage check-in ആയിക്കഴിഞ്ഞു)
  3. The news is very shocking. (news വളരെ shocking ആണ്)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) are
• 'Are' = plural verb. 'Cutlery' = uncountable/singular → 'are' wrong.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: Cutlery = knives + forks + spoons → multiple items → plural feel → 'are'. പക്ഷേ collective uncountable noun = singular verb 'is'.

❌ (B) were
• 'Were' = past plural. 'Cutlery' = singular + sentence present tense context → 'is' (present singular) ✓.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'I bought yesterday' = past → 'were' past feel. പക്ഷേ main verb tense = present ('quite expensive' = current state) → 'is' ✓.

❌ (C) have been
• 'Have been' = Present Perfect Plural (I/You/We/They have been). Cutlery = singular → 'has been' (not 'have been').
• Plus context: 'quite expensive' = current simple state → Simple Present 'is' ✓, not Present Perfect 'have been'.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Have been' sophisticated feel → complex sentence → sophisticated verb feel. പക്ഷേ simple present 'is' = straightforward state description.
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'CUTLERY is ONE SET — one set = IS!' — Cutlery = knife + fork + spoon = ഒരു SET. ONE set = singular = IS. പലതുണ്ടെങ്കിലും ഒരു SET ആകുമ്പോൾ singular!
• Uncountable noun shortlist (memorize these 8):
  CUTLERY / FURNITURE / LUGGAGE / INFORMATION / ADVICE / NEWS / EQUIPMENT / MACHINERY → all take IS/WAS
• PSC exam strategy: 'Cutlery/Furniture/Luggage/News' subject ആണ് → singular verb (is/was) choose ചെയ്യുക. 'Are/Were/Have been' eliminate ചെയ്യുക. Relative clause 'I bought yesterday' ignore ചെയ്‌ത് main subject 'cutlery' ആണ് verb decide ചെയ്യുന്നത്. Guaranteed mark!

questionText: Which among the following is an adjective?

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (B) Mundane

• 'Mundane' = adjective — 'സാധാരണ, ദൈനംദിന, boring' (ordinary and not interesting or exciting).
• Adjective = noun-ഓ pronoun-ഓ describe ചെയ്യുന്ന word. 'Mundane tasks' = boring/ordinary tasks (tasks describe ചെയ്യുന്നു).
• 'Mundane' usage: 'mundane chores' (ദൈനംദിന ജോലികൾ), 'mundane life' (സാധാരണ ജീവിതം).
• Etymology: Latin 'mundus' = world → 'mundane' = worldly/ordinary (ലൗകിക, ഭൗതിക).
• Parts of Speech confirm: 'The job is mundane' — mundane = predicate adjective ✓.
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Parts of Speech identification — suffix (ending) നോക്കി Part of Speech guess ചെയ്യുക!
• Common suffix clues:
  - Adjective endings: -ful, -less, -ous, -al, -ive, -ic, -ane, -ar, -ary, -ible, -able
  - Adverb endings: -ly (uncannily ✓)
  - Noun endings: -tion, -ness, -ment, -ity, -our/-or (rigour ✓)
  - Verb endings: -ate, -ise/-ize, -ify, -en (commence ✓)
• Word analysis for this question:
  - Uncannily → -ly ending = adverb (describing how) ✗
  - Mundane → no verb/adverb suffix = adjective ✓ (describes nouns)
  - Commence → -ence base = begin (verb) ✗
  - Rigour → -our = noun (strictness) ✗
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Parts of Speech identification suffix-based shortcuts PSC-ൽ very useful!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She leads a mundane life in the city. (adjective — life describe ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  2. Even mundane tasks require attention. (adjective — tasks describe ചെയ്യുന്നു)
  3. The mundane routine bored him. (adjective — routine describe ചെയ്യുന്നു)
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) Uncannily
• 'Uncannily' = adverb — '-ly' ending = adverb. 'Uncanny' (adjective) + '-ly' = 'uncannily' (adverb).
• Meaning: 'uncannily similar' = അസ്വാഭാവികമാം വിധം similar (how similar = adverb).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Uncannily' long word → complex feel → adjective feel. പക്ഷേ '-ly' = adverb marker. Always check ending!

❌ (C) Commence
• 'Commence' = verb — 'ആരംഭിക്കുക' (to begin). 'The meeting will commence at 9 am.'
• Verb = action/state word. 'Commence' action indicate ചെയ്യുന്നു → verb ✗ (adjective അല്ല).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Commence' formal word → formal adjective feel. പക്ഷേ 'the meeting commenced' = verb (action). Noun form = 'commencement' (-ment suffix).

❌ (D) Rigour
• 'Rigour' = noun — 'കർശനത, ക്രൂരത, precision' (strictness/severity). British spelling (American = 'rigor').
• 'The rigour of the training' = training-ന്റെ strictness (noun). '-our' ending = noun marker.
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Rigour' = quality/characteristic feel → adjective feel. പക്ഷേ quality-ഉടെ name = noun. Adjective form = 'rigorous' (-ous suffix).
explanation.tip:
💡 HOW COME Tip

• 🧠 Memory trick: 'LY = adverb, OUR/MENT/TION = noun, ATE/ISE = verb, OUS/AL/IVE = adjective — SUFFIX TELLS THE TRUTH!' — Word-ന്റെ ending നോക്കി Part of Speech identify ചെയ്യുക. Suffix = instant clue!
• Quick suffix chart:
  - -LY → adverb (quickly, uncannily)
  - -OUS/-AL/-IVE/-ANE → adjective (mundane, active, natural)
  - -TION/-MENT/-OUR/-ITY → noun (rigour, nation, moment)
  - -ATE/-ISE/-EN → verb (commence, organise, widen)
• PSC exam strategy: Parts of Speech question-ൽ → ഓരോ option-ന്റെ suffix നോക്കുക. '-ly' = adverb eliminate. '-our' = noun eliminate. '-verb feel' eliminate. '-adjective suffix' = answer. Mundane (-ane = adjective ending) ✓. Guaranteed mark!

questionText:
Change into passive voice.
Someone has asked John to make a speech at the meeting.
optionA: John is asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone
optionB: John is being asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone
optionC: John has been asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone
optionD: John has asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone

explanation.correct:
✅ ശരിയുത്തരം: (C) John has been asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone

• Active: 'Someone HAS ASKED John...' = Present Perfect Active (has + V3).
• Present Perfect Passive formula: has/have + been + V3.
• 'Has asked' (active) → 'has been asked' (passive) ✓
• Object 'John' → passive subject 'John'. Agent 'someone' → 'by someone' (retained at end).
• 'To make a speech at the meeting' = infinitive phrase — passive-ലും unchanged remain ചെയ്യുന്നു.
• Meaning: 'Meeting-ൽ ഒരു speech നടത്താൻ John-നോട് ആരോ ആവശ്യപ്പെട്ടിട്ടുണ്ട്.'
explanation.rule:
📌 വ്യാകരണ നിയമം

• 🔑 ഓർക്കാനുള്ള formula: Present Perfect Passive = has/have + BEEN + V3
• Tense-wise passive formula table:
  - Simple Present: is/are + V3 (is asked)
  - Present Continuous: is/are + being + V3 (is being asked)
  - Present Perfect: has/have + been + V3 (has been asked) ✓
  - Simple Past: was/were + V3 (was asked)
  - Past Perfect: had + been + V3 (had been asked)
• Active → Passive steps:
  1. Active object (John) → Passive subject
  2. Active verb 'has asked' → 'has been asked'
  3. Active subject (someone) → 'by someone' (agent)
  4. Remaining phrase unchanged: 'to make a speech at the meeting'
• ⭐ PSC ആവർത്തനം: Present Perfect passive (has been + V3) PSC-ൽ frequently tested!
• ഉദാഹരണ sentences:
  1. She has written the letter. → The letter has been written by her.
  2. They have completed the project. → The project has been completed by them.
  3. He has submitted the report. → The report has been submitted by him.
explanation.wrong:
❌ തെറ്റായ ഉത്തരങ്ങൾ

❌ (A) John is asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone
• 'Is asked' = Simple Present Passive. Active 'has asked' = Present Perfect, not Simple Present.
• Tense mismatch: 'has asked' (Present Perfect) → 'has been asked' (Present Perfect Passive), not 'is asked' (Simple Present Passive).
• 🪤 PSC Trap: 'Is asked' passive feel → correct sound. പക്ഷേ tense wrong — 'has asked' → 'has been asked' (been ആവശ്യം).

❌ (B) John is being asked to make a speech at the meeting by someone
• 'Is being asked' = Present Continuous Passive. Active 'has asked' = Present Perfect, not Present Continuous.
